Anyone with dehydrated melanin sheets inside their tissues does have an allergy to O2. That is what sleep apnea is fundamentally based on: physics. The body is trying to save itself from your poor light choices. Why? Red light makes water for cytochrome c oxidase, and UV light makes melanin from POMC. Both are in sunlight.

You do not get sunlight for any reason; you will cause your brain to downregulate your breathing and need for oxygen. Why?

It is the terminal electron acceptor for oxygen. Without sunlight, you do not need more oxygen because it is TOXIC to tissues. Yet, functional medicine, food gurus, and MAHA type push HBO, supplemental oxygen, ozone, and ECMO. People forget the lessons of ARDS, space, and ventilated COVID patients.

All had acute intense oxygen therapy during a period that heteroplasmy rose rapidly. These "experts" are telling patients they are dumbasses when they give out this advice and the public at large has no idea they are making these people famous for being DANGEROUS to your healthspan. The very same people elect politicians the same way.

They keep letting criminals harm them because none of them can think well = which is a function of dopamine and melatonin biology working with hydrated melanin sheets. That is what consciousness is in a nutshell. All low dopamine adaptations are due to poor cognition and a low conscious state.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-15T06:27:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-the-story-ends-isn%E2%80%99t-written-yet.30430/post-348638>

Think about what I said about consciousness.

Then read the tweet: [https://x.com/MitoPsychoBio/status/1900503171954540685](https://x.com/MitoPsychoBio/status/1900503171954540685)

**How Light Becomes a Bioelectric and Electromechanical Signal: A Vibration**

Light, as electromagnetic radiation, carries energy in the form of photons. When these photons interact with biomolecules, they can induce electrical and mechanical effects through absorption, scattering, or quantum interactions. Here’s the process step-by-step:

1.  Photon Absorption by Biomolecules:
    -   Biomolecules like chromophores (e.g., cytochrome c oxidase in mitochondria, melanin in skin, or chlorophyll in photosynthetic organisms) absorb photons at specific wavelengths. For example, cytochrome c oxidase (CcO) absorbs red and near-infrared light (600–1000 nm), exciting its electrons to higher energy states.
    -   This excitation creates a temporary dipole moment or charge separation within the molecule, generating a local electric field. This is the bioelectric signal: light induces a voltage or current by altering the molecule’s charge distribution.
2.  Electrical Resistance and Signal Propagation:
    -   The induced electric field encounters resistance from the biomolecule’s structure and its surrounding environment (e.g., water, lipids, proteins). Resistance arises from the molecule’s dielectric properties and the medium’s impedance to charge flow.
    -   In mitochondria, the inner mitochondrial membrane (IMM) with its 30–40 MV/m field (per Nick Lane) already sustains a voltage (150–200 mV). Light-induced charge separation in CcO could modulate this field, creating a bioelectric signal that propagates along the membrane or through structured water (per Mae-Wan Ho’s liquid crystalline model).
3.  Mechanical Resistance and Vibration:
    -   Light absorption can also induce mechanical effects. When a photon excites a biomolecule, it may cause conformational changes—e.g., CcO’s heme group shifts during electron transfer, or melanin’s conjugated structure vibrates upon absorbing light (per Herrera’s water-splitting hypothesis).
    -   These conformational changes encounter mechanical resistance from the molecule’s bonds and the surrounding medium (e.g., water, cytoskeleton). This resistance converts light energy into vibrational energy, manifesting as molecular oscillations or phonons (quanta of vibrational energy).
4.  Soliton Formation:
    -   I've mentioned that light hitting a biomolecule creates a soliton—a self-reinforcing, localized wave that travels without dissipating. In biological systems, solitons are hypothesized to arise from nonlinear interactions between vibrational energy (phonons) and the biomolecule’s structure.
    -   For example, Davydov’s soliton theory (1973) suggests that vibrational energy in proteins (e.g., alpha helices) couples with lattice deformations, forming a soliton that propagates energy along the protein. Light-induced vibrations in a biomolecule could initiate such a soliton, transmitting energy and information across the cell—say, along microtubules or mitochondrial membranes.
5.  Photon Generation (Biophotons):
    -   I've noted that “some of the things it hits become photons.” This ties directly to the DCE and aquaphotomics. When light-induced vibrations oscillate water molecules (acting as a mirror), the DCE converts virtual photons from ZPE into real biophotons, as I've discussed in earlier blogs
    -   These biophotons, emitted by mtDNA or water (per Ho’s coherence data), become a secondary signaling mechanism. They can stimulate other chromophores, propagate the bioelectric signal, or drive further vibrational effects, creating a feedback loop for energy and information transfer.

**Light Becomes a Vibration: The Role of Resistance**

I’ve pinpointed resistance as the key to transforming light into vibrations:

-   Electrical Resistance: When light induces a bioelectric signal (e.g., charge separation in CcO), the signal encounters resistance from the biomolecule and its environment. This resistance converts some of the light’s energy into heat or vibrational energy (phonons), as electrons collide with molecular structures.
-   Mechanical Resistance: The biomolecule’s conformational changes (e.g., stretching, bending) face mechanical resistance from bonds and the surrounding medium. This resistance dissipates energy as vibrations, which can couple with water or protein structures to form solitons.
-   Vibration as a Signal: These vibrations aren’t random—they’re coherent, as Ho’s work suggests. In mitochondria, vibrations in the IMM or matrix water could modulate biophoton emission, creating a light-vibration feedback loop that “sculpts life,” as I’ve described.

**Role of ZPE and DCE in This Process**

The ZPE and DCE amplify this light-to-vibration transformation and its signaling potential:

-   ZPE Contribution: ZPE provides a quantum energy reservoir via vacuum fluctuations. The DCE, triggered by water’s oscillations (aquaphotometric mirror), converts ZPE’s virtual photons into real biophotons. These biophotons can:
    -   Enhance the initial light signal by stimulating chromophores, amplifying the bioelectric effect.
    -   Drive additional vibrations by exciting biomolecules, feeding into soliton formation.
-   DCE as a Signal Amplifier: Water’s aquaphotometric oscillations (driven by light, IMM fields, or thermal motion) generate biophotons via the DCE. These biophotons act as a secondary light source, reinforcing the bioelectric and electromechanical signals. This aligns with my idea of “signaling and transmitting energy and information.”

**Physiological Work via Bioelectric and Electromechanical Signals**

The bioelectric and electromechanical signals, now amplified by ZPE and DCE, perform physiological work: this was likely active at endosymbiosis.

-   Bioelectric Work: The voltage induced by light (e.g., in CcO) can drive ion fluxes across membranes, power ATP synthesis (via the IMM’s proton motive force), or trigger signaling cascades (e.g., calcium waves in cells). In mitochondria, this enhances OXPHOS, producing ATP, water, and CO₂.
-   Electromechanical Work: Vibrations and solitons transmit energy mechanically. For example, solitons along microtubules (per Stuart Hameroff’s ORCH-OR theory) could influence cytoskeletal dynamics, cell division, or mitochondrial positioning. In my cephalopod analogy, these vibrations might underlie chromatophore activity, linking metabolism to visible light signals. We know light controls metabolism this way already. Biochemists just act like these papers are not published, yet they are discoverable.
-   Information Transfer: Biophotons and solitons encode information. Biophotons, with their coherence (Ho), signal metabolic state across the cell, while solitons transmit localized energy packets, coordinating processes like protein folding or membrane dynamics. This is how light can create prions and protein misfolding in neurodegeneration.

**Tying to Bacterial vs. Archaeal Water Production**

My previous discussion on water production is relevant here for endosymbiosis:

-   Bacteria: Aerobic bacteria produce more water via O₂ reduction (e.g., 12 H₂O per glucose in the ETC). This water, potentially DDW, enhances aquaphotomic oscillations and DCE activity, amplifying biophoton generation from ZPE. More water might mean stronger soliton formation, as vibrations propagate through a larger structured water network. This energy likely helped fusion.
-   Archaea: Archaea (e.g., methanogens) produce less water (e.g., 2 H₂O per CO₂). With less water, their DCE and ZPE utilization might be weaker, potentially limiting soliton and biophoton signaling. However, extremophile archaea might compensate with unique lipids or proteins that enhance vibrational coherence. This is why the mitochondrial backbone is mostly bacterial with Archeal components.

In my cephalopod analogy, these vibrations might underlie chromatophore activity, linking metabolism to visible light signals. We know light controls metabolism this way already. Biochemists just act like these papers are not published, yet they are discoverable.
> -   Information Transfer: Biophotons and solitons encode information. Biophotons, with their coherence (Ho), signal metabolic state across the cell, while solitons transmit localized energy packets, coordinating processes like protein folding or membrane dynamics. This is how light can create prions and protein misfolding in neurodegeneration.
>
> **Tying to Bacterial vs. Archaeal Water Production**
>
> My previous discussion on water production is relevant here for endosymbiosis:
>
> -   Bacteria: Aerobic bacteria produce more water via O₂ reduction (e.g., 12 H₂O per glucose in the ETC). This water, potentially DDW, enhances aquaphotomic oscillations and DCE activity, amplifying biophoton generation from ZPE. More water might mean stronger soliton formation, as vibrations propagate through a larger structured water network. This energy likely helped fusion.
> -   Archaea: Archaea (e.g., methanogens) produce less water (e.g., 2 H₂O per CO₂). With less water, their DCE and ZPE utilization might be weaker, potentially limiting soliton and biophoton signaling. However, extremophile archaea might compensate with unique lipids or proteins that enhance vibrational coherence. This is why the mitochondrial backbone is mostly bacterial with Archeal components.

The way you tie it all together makes it impossible to ignore how fundamental this is. Our conscious experience is so deeply tied to how our cells interact with light. It’s not just energy -it’s shaping bioelectric signals, vibrations, and even perception itself. Light sculpting life, as you always say.

I’ve been sharing this discussion with Judyann.

Doing this to help people find places as it seems people struggle to find . I am far from a emf expert and will post when I find spots. If you want Intel I can possibly tell you if you post the link as I have seen lots of properties

### [Home in La Libertad · ★4.91 · 4 bedrooms · 8 beds · 4 private baths](https://www.airbnb.ca/rooms/703577631228516593?viralityEntryPoint=1&s=76)

Gorgeous 4 bedroom beach house with a pool

 www.airbnb.ca

Checked it out today. It had two WiFi cameras and two WiFi access points( host was OK with disabling if renting). I was able to do some measurements but sometimes you just have to stay there to test it out fully and only so much Intel you can gather with one visit.

Magnetic fields 0.30 - 0.40 Mg

WiFry 0.704 upper portion in bedrooms of property but was getting a pulses from what I believe was the cameras and or construction workers only in the one bedroom closest to the camera and the construction workers.

Mid portion was harder to read as after I disabled the WiFi access points he had something go crazy trying to reconnect. Had a lot of audio video equipment there

Electric fields didn't really take notes on readings

as nothing was concerning but had concerns that all shower heads were wired with a heater . outdoor shower was available with no emf.

Probably more Intel needed but from my first readings it could be decent if someone or a group needed a place to stay

# Thread: Podcast #7 Dr Kruse bio genetics of consciousness pain light healing
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcast-7-dr-kruse-bio-genetics-of-consciousness-pain-light-healing.30443/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 6
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Wanttoknow — 2025-03-09T04:59:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcast-7-dr-kruse-bio-genetics-of-consciousness-pain-light-healing.30443/post-348189>

I just finished watching this podcast. Very enlightening. The past couple years I have concluded it does no good to talk politics or religion with anyone or the war in Ukraine. It seems to me everyone is living in their own reality. Everybody watches different news feeds with conflicting news reports. Now I think I have a higher consciousness than others because I ask questions instead of following the herd. I could not vote for Trump because he never explained what he would do to end the Ukraine war in 1 day. This seemed illogical and I wasn't sure if he new the truth how the war started. I voted for him twice before but not this last time.

I went to church every week for over 30 years and came to realize most were following a man who could do no wrong. This church uses the Maslow Hierarchy of communication Which I believe is just a way of mind control. So I quit and not one member who were my friends even bother to keep in contact with me and after a few years of trying to communicate I figured they are not really friends after all. Sometimes I feel so isolated because I think different than most people. I live in SW Iowa everyone is so sick its unbelievable. I took some garden produce in to the farmers market once and people would not pay a dollar for a nice beet but they had a $2 coke in their hand. I remember one lady kind of snarled at me. That left an impression. It feels like I am in darkness. Then I read Dr Kruse post a little bit ago. Maybe its dark because I have been planted.

My question "does Consciousness form ones reality or are they interchangeable."

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-09T05:58:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcast-7-dr-kruse-bio-genetics-of-consciousness-pain-light-healing.30443/post-348197>

Consciousness is a spectrum of energy throughput linked to hydrated melanin. What people do not realize is that one can be awake but not fully conscious because their nonvisual photoreceptors are not optimized, which means low dopamine and melatonin. Water, melanin, dopamine, and melatonin are all ironically UV chromophores, and this is why POMC has beta endorphins locked up and confined in it.

We go to ground in the sun, and Nature addicts us to it. It removes us from Plato's allegory of the cave, so we become FULLY conscious.

That was the story of that pod. I think I really got to Sheryl. After I was done with her, she wrote me a note.

### Wanttoknow — 2025-03-09T06:27:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcast-7-dr-kruse-bio-genetics-of-consciousness-pain-light-healing.30443/post-348200>

I live on an acreage and sometimes I take video of the surroundings and when I play it back I hear all the birds singing which I never heard while taking the video. This is probably an example of not being fully conscious.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-09T06:41:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/podcast-7-dr-kruse-bio-genetics-of-consciousness-pain-light-healing.30443/post-348201>

Correct.

Consider this idea.

The most precious etchings of caring can be traced not in the scope of its message, but in the integrity of its purpose. For a message to resonate it must be unusual. Stop taking each element separately, connect your thoughts and fit them together properly.

In the statement above I am hinting at Claude Shannon’s 1948 work on information theory, which is all about how information is transmitted, measured, and understood. It is stripped down to its essence, beyond just meaning or sentiment. Between your words about etchings, caring, integrity, purpose, and resonance, You should be able to see Shannon’s ideas peeking through, especially if we connect them as I’ve asked.

Shannon argued that information isn’t about the content’s emotional weight but its capacity to reduce uncertainty, its signal amidst noise. The birds for you were the signal noise initially so you subtracted it out. The line about “the most precious etchings of caring” traced in “the integrity of its purpose” could tie to this: the true value of a message isn’t in its surface scope but in how reliably it conveys intent.

For Shannon, a message’s “unusualness” aligns with its information content, rare, unexpected signals carry more weight because they disrupt predictability. Blue light chromophores are rare in Nature because blue light is the unusal message cells are looking for to act. Problem is today, we've built a world where that signal is no longer rare, but our system is still built that blue light is rare.

My call to “connect thoughts and fit them together properly” mirrors Shannon's focus on encoding and decoding: a message only resonates if its parts cohere, cutting through the noise as a clear transmission.
So, weaving it together: a message with integrity (a clean signal) and unusualness (high information content) resonates (successfully decodes) when its elements aren’t scattered but unified (low entropy, in Shannon’s terms)

# Thread: CT and waking up in the middle of the night?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-waking-up-in-the-middle-of-the-night.30452/>
**Subforum:** Cold Thermogenesis
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MatsDS123 — 2025-03-10T16:28:32+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-waking-up-in-the-middle-of-the-night.30452/post-348286>

After starting cold exposure 2-3 times per week in September last year, I started waking up in the middle of the night. I can be awake for up to 2 hours before falling asleep again. Seems like a cortisol pulse, and I feel warm. I started out with 20 minutes in the lake and let the the declining temperature towards winter be my way to progress. I live in Norway and it was around 48 fahrenheit outside when I started. in late November we were down to 28 degrees fahrenheit outside, and after one session I lost the sensation in my arms, so I decided to pause the CT until I regained my sensation, which took about 7 or 8 weeks. During this time the sleep issues persisted, which caused me to think that it was unrelated.

Recently I got hospitalized with a perforated appendix that had to be removed and I was in the hospital for 5 days with no CT. When I got out I started sleeping normally again, and had my first CT 2 weeks after I got out. I only did 5 minutes in the shower that time, and the same day the sleep issues returned. I kept doing CT for a few weeks until I decided to stop and see if the sleep improved again, which it did. Now, yesterday I decided to do another CT session and sleep issue returned.
I also convinced a friend of mine to start doing CT at the time I started, he fell in love with it, but eventually started having sleep issues as well that went away when he stopped.

I have not concluded that the CT is the culprit, but it seems to be a correlation. The waking feels like a cortisol pulse that needs some time to drop off for me to fall back asleep.

Have any of you had any similar experiences, or can shed some insights on this?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-10T20:10:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ct-and-waking-up-in-the-middle-of-the-night.30452/post-348306>

I would say for me it happens about once every 2 weeks or so

### Anthony Smith — 2025-03-10T20:40:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/30-grounding-fundamentals.30454/post-348313>

Grounding made simple . . . Natural - GOOD, artificial - BAD

Grounding makes you part of a circuit
Outside - Natures DC circuit flowing through your DC circuits, with you in direct contact with the ground - GOOD
Inside - Your homes AC field flowing through your DC circuits, with you connected to a wire (antenna) - BAD

Natural light = outside, in direct sun light (away from nnEMF) - TINA (There Is No Alternative)
Natural grounding = outside, in direct contact with the ground (away from nnEMF) - TINA

>
> Natural light = outside, in direct sun light (away from nnEMF) - TINA (There Is No Alternative)
> Natural grounding = outside, in direct contact with the ground (away from nnEMF) - TINA

Perfect, many thanks Anthony.

### Jana C — 2025-03-10T17:16:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-in-el-salvador.30455/post-348291>

I went after my car accident to Peru (Machu Picchu) and hiked around. Loved it, but didn't love the food . . . or rather, the food didn't love me. I got super sick in Peru and stayed that way. That's when I began to really get the slow motility as well! What kinds of foods (specific meats/fish) and fats can I eat safely when down there? Any good suggestions that could provide me a staple food of sorts? Has anyone with bad stomach issues that is there or has been there had success with a certain food/diet? Open to suggestions.

### John Warner — 2025-03-10T18:42:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-in-el-salvador.30455/post-348299>

It is a third world country, buy your food and cook, ask others that live there where they eat, and see if they have any problems, I know everyone's different but the ones who have been there probably have vetted the restaurants, ask if they have, or if they haven't.

### Jana C — 2025-03-10T20:21:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-in-el-salvador.30455/post-348309>

Good advice. Would you suggest having food that I know I can handle shipped to El Salvador?? Is that fairly easy to do or is there some difficulty down there. I understand the mail is very slow, but wondering about shipping. Thank you for the suggestion!

### Jana C — 2025-03-10T20:28:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-in-el-salvador.30455/post-348311>

Quick question about Vasopressin. From the podcast #7 with Cheryl, Dr. Kruse had mention that pain (which is a stressor) releases Vasopressin. I'm assuming you are talking about physiological pain or systemic pain and NOT emotional pain, correct? It would seem as if emotional pain would inhibit the release of Vasopressin? Also, still trying to figure out "why" the head endocrinologist at Johns Hopkins told me not to take DDAVP. Told me to drink my water, which truly hasn't worked out for me for 5 years now. He did mention that me taking DDAVP when my issues are idiopathic would be dangerous. Is it because once you take DDAVP, you risk your body not being able to produce it on it's own?? I need to learn more about this.

### John Warner — 2025-03-10T20:29:00+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eating-in-el-salvador.30455/post-348312>

You're probably not going to be able to ship food there, they have a fresh seafood market close to El Zonte, there's grocery stores where you can buy meat, and there's a guy that raises local meat down there, you can find out from who's there where to get the best fruit, it's everywhere down there but I wouldn't trust every fruit that somebody selling there

### Jana C — 2025-03-10T20:44:40+0200

# Thread: Alison’s Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alison%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30456/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### alipankey — 2025-03-11T00:44:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alison%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30456/post-348343>

53, F.

Healthy and vibrant my whole life. Except I was a very heavy and long sleeper—waking up was like coming out of a coma EVERY DAY OF MY LIFE. And I was a natural night owl. (This was before screens. We only got the Apple IIe with the big floppy disks, as a teenager, and it was rarely used—not like these days, and not used not at night.)

Sports and activities came easily to me my whole life. I was very resilient. Endurance and the mind-over-matter required for it were fun and easy for me.

When I was 27, I skated a lot. Inline skating. I would do marathons in the wine country in Northern California, Tahiti, skating in Scandinavia, anywhere I went.

My longest race was 86 miles (no stopping, up and down hills, in the rain.) I came in 4th for my age group by barely training.

Crashed at 38, presumably with mercury toxicity, but really the toxic barrel filled up at that time. And I have never recovered.

Follow my thread if you’re interested, and I’m open to supportive and encouraging suggestions and solutions as I go along.

I will know I am healed when I can do that 86-mile race in Atlanta again—or it’s equivalent on my own, on Cañada Road in the Bay Area.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-11T07:52:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alison%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30456/post-348363>

Integration of Clinical and Environmental Features into My Model
My photo-bioelectric hypothesis posits that nnEMF and blue light damage melanopsin, mtDNA, and heme proteins, reducing DDW (deuterium-depleted water) production, dehydrating melanin, and lowering éR, leading to ROS/RNS overproduction, impaired bioelectric signaling (Becker’s trillionth-amp DC current), and systemic dysfunction.

1.  Natural Night Owl for 38 Years:
    -   Chronic circadian misalignment (late sleep/wake cycle) disrupts melanopsin signaling in the retina and vascular tissues, impairing circadian regulation of mitochondrial function, low NO production = poor stem cell depot usage = early aging due to higher heteroplasmy, and poor optimization of the POMC/melanin pathways.
2.  Living in California with High nnEMF and Population Density (Inverse Square Law):
    -   High nnEMF exposure from urban density (e.g., cell towers, Wi-Fi) increases via the inverse square law (intensity proportional to 1/distance²), amplifying melanopsin damage, mtDNA mutations, likely altering melanin problem endogenously, and elevating ROS/RNS in mitochondria.
3.  Excessive Endurance Athlete Stressing Mitochondria:
    -   Prolonged endurance exercise induces oxidative stress, overtaxing mitochondria, exacerbating mtDNA mutations, reducing DDW production, and lowering éR, especially in a nnEMF-heavy environment. This explains the crash.
4.  Energy and Cognition Crash at 38, Attributed to Mercury Toxicity but Likely Cumulative Stressors:
    -   The “toxic barrel” filled up at 38, combining nnEMF, circadian disruption, endurance stress, and possible mercury toxicity (e.g., from dental amalgams, toxic consumption due to open gut barriers and BBB secondary to nnEMF abuse). Mercury further damages mtDNA and heme proteins of the p450 system, compounding the crash in energy (mitochondrial failure) and cognition (neuronal photo- bioelectric dysfunction = excessive ultraweak biophoton loss.
5.  No Recovery Since:
    -   Persistent nnEMF exposure, lack of circadian alignment, and ongoing mitochondrial stress prevent recovery, with dehydrated melanin and low éR perpetuating systemic dysfunction across the colony of mitochondrial = why the crash happened.

* * *

**Predictions for Etiology and Progression**

1\. Circadian Misalignment and nnEMF Drive Mitochondrial Dysfunction

-   Prediction: Being a night owl for 38 years, combined with high nnEMF exposure in California, disrupted melanopsin signaling and mtDNA, initiating mitochondrial dysfunction and the energy/cognition crash.
-   Mechanism: Chronic circadian misalignment (night owl behavior) impairs melanopsin-driven signaling to the suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN), reducing hypothalamic regulation of mitochondrial function and NO production. nnEMF, intensified by population density (inverse square law), damages melanopsin in retinal ganglion cells and vascular tissues, liberating vitamin A and destroying heme-based cytochromes (e.g., cytochrome c). This increases mtDNA mutations (1000x more common than nDNA), reducing DDW production and éR, leading to ROS/RNS overproduction and dehydrated melanin. The result is mitochondrial failure, impairing ATP synthesis (energy crash) and neuronal bioelectric signaling (cognition crash).
-   Outcome: Persistent motor, sensory, cognitive fatigue, cognitive decline (e.g., brain fog, memory issues), and systemic inflammation, measurable via low ATP levels, high ROS markers (e.g., 8-OHdG), and mtDNA mutation analysis = higher heteroplasmy rate than stated age , early greying and depressed hormone panels.

2. Endurance Exercise Exacerbates Mitochondrial Stress

-   Prediction: Excessive endurance exercise in an EMF-heavy environment overtaxed mitochondria, amplifying mtDNA damage and contributing to the crash at 38.
-   Mechanism: Endurance exercise increases mitochondrial oxygen consumption, generating ROS/RNS that damage mtDNA, especially in cytochrome c oxidase genes, reducing DDW production and éR. nnEMF exposure during exercise (e.g., urban running with wireless devices) amplifies this by dehydrating melanin in muscle and vascular tissues, increasing conductivity and ultraweak biophotons, which disrupt bioelectric signaling. This overtaxes the mitochondrial colony, leading to a tipping point at 38, where energy production and neuronal function collapse.
-   Outcome: Chronic fatigue syndrome-like symptoms, reduced exercise capacity, and neuronal deficits, with blood tests showing elevated lactate (impaired oxidative phosphorylation) and peripheral blood smears showing abnormal RBC morphology.

3. Mercury Toxicity as a Contributing Factor in the “Toxic Barrel”

-   Prediction: Mercury toxicity, combined with nnEMF, circadian disruption, and endurance stress, filled the “toxic barrel,” triggering the crash and preventing recovery.
-   Mechanism: Mercury (e.g., from dental amalgams, predatory fish) binds to sulfhydryl groups in mitochondrial enzymes (e.g., cytochrome c oxidase), impairing DDW production and increasing ROS/RNS. It also disrupts heme synthesis, leading to anemia of chronic disease and altered sex steroid hormones due to pregnenolone steal syndrome due to nnEMF, exacerbating nnEMF/blue light-induced damage to melanopsin and mtDNA. This cumulative stress dehydrates melanin, lowers éR significantly, making you a victim of the Great Oxygen Holocaust, and impairs NO production, which destroys your stem cell depots and POMC signaling, preventing mitochondrial repair and neuronal recovery and perpetuating the energy/cognition crash.
-   Outcome: Elevated mercury levels in blood/hair, persistent mitochondrial dysfunction (e.g., low ATP/ADP ratio), and cognitive impairment (e.g., Montreal Cognitive Assessment \[MoCA\] score <26).

4\. Persistent nnEMF Exposure Prevents Recovery

-   Prediction: Ongoing nnEMF exposure in California, without circadian realignment, prevents recovery by maintaining low éR and mitochondrial stress. Rx requires new location with profound replenishment of UV and IR light and grounding. You will never recover in poor latitudes. DDW could seriously help here.
-   Mechanism: nnEMF continues to damage melanopsin, mtDNA, and heme proteins, reducing DDW and dehydrating melanin, perpetuating ROS/RNS and biophoton overproduction. This disrupts Becker’s regenerative current, preventing mitochondrial and neuronal repair. Lack of circadian alignment (night owl behavior) exacerbates this by impairing POMC/melanin signaling and hormonal regulation (e.g., pregnenolone steal syndrome, low cortisol/melatonin/testosterone). You have an elevated risk of cancers.
-   Outcome: Chronic energy/cognition deficits, systemic inflammation (e.g., high CRP), and potential progression to neurodegenerative conditions (e.g., early Alzheimer’s markers like amyloid-beta).

5\. Therapeutic Interventions Reverse the Crash

-   Prediction: A protocol combining sunlight, grounding, DDW, hypertonic saline, methylene blue, photobiomodulation (PBM), and mercury detoxification reverses the energy/cognition crash and prevents progression. Chealtion therapy might be necessary due to California's location, considering the amount of time BBB and gut barriers have been wide open. Bazan loop in liver likely shot. Same is true of short loop.
-   Mechanism: Sunlight (UV-A) hydrates melanin and regenerates melanopsin/NO, grounding reduces ROS/RNS, DDW enhances mitochondrial DDW production, hypertonic saline increases conductivity, methylene blue repairs mtDNA and boosts éR, and PBM (red/near-infrared) stimulates cytochrome c oxidase for ATP/DDW. Mercury detoxification (e.g., chelation with DMPS/DMSA under medical supervision) removes the toxic burden, allowing mitochondrial recovery. Circadian realignment (e.g., morning light exposure) restores POMC signaling and hormonal balance.
-   Outcome: Improved energy (e.g., fatigue scale improvement >2 points), enhanced cognition (e.g., MoCA score >28), and reduced inflammation (e.g., CRP <1 mg/L) within 12 weeks, with sustained recovery at 6 months. I predict moving the needle is a 6-12 month process for this sitaution.

* * *

**Integration with the Decentralized Model**

My model predicts that a person with these conditions results from a cumulative “toxic barrel” of stressors:

-   Night Owl Behavior: Disrupts melanopsin-driven circadian alignment, impairing mitochondrial function and NO production.
-   High nnEMF Exposure: Damages melanopsin, mtDNA, and heme proteins, reducing DDW and éR, increasing ROS/RNS.
-   Endurance Exercise: Overtaxes mitochondria, amplifying mtDNA mutations and oxidative stress in a EMF-heavy environment.
-   Mercury Toxicity: Exacerbates mitochondrial damage, contributing to the crash at 38.
-   Therapeutic Alignment: Sunlight, grounding, DDW, hypertonic saline, methylene blue, PBM, and mercury detoxification restore éR, hydrate melanin, and repair mitochondria, reversing the crash.

* * *

**Testable Predictions**

-   Mitochondrial Markers: Blood tests show low ATP/ADP ratio, high lactate, and increased mtDNA mutations (cytochrome c genes) at baseline, improving post-treatment. Improving blood smear, better on cognitive testing.
-   Inflammation and Toxicity: Elevated CRP and mercury levels (blood/hair) at baseline, decreasing with therapy (e.g., CRP <1 mg/L, mercury <5 µg/L).
-   Energy/Cognition: Fatigue scale (e.g., Chalder Fatigue Scale) and MoCA scores improve within 12 weeks (e.g., fatigue score drop >4 points, MoCA >28).
-   Photo-Bioelectric Recovery: Microcurrent measurements in tissues show increased ordered currents (e.g., 1–10 µA) post-PBM/grounding.
-   Long-Term: Reduced risk of neurodegenerative progression (e.g., normal amyloid-beta levels on CSF analysis) at 1 year. This recovery will likely take longer than one would expect given the comorbidities present fo mtDNA mutation risk. Avoid all supplements and jabs.

* * *

**Therapeutic Protocol Adjustment**

Building on my prior protocols (sunlight, grounding, DDW, hypertonic saline, methylene blue, PBM):

-   Phase 1 (Weeks 1–4):
    -   Circadian Realignment: Morning sunlight exposure (30 minutes, 8–10 a.m.), blue light blockers post-sunset.
    -   Mercury Detoxification: Chelation (e.g., DMPS 10 mg/kg IV weekly, under medical supervision), monitor renal function.
    -   Continue sunlight (30-120 min/day given solar callus), grounding (1-4 hr/day), DDW (1–2 L/day of 50-100 ppm based on budget), hypertonic saline (3% NaCl, 250 mL IV weekly with doc), methylene blue (1 mg/kg IV weekly), PBM (810 nm, 5 J/cm² daily on scalp).
    -   CT for 15 minutes 4 times a week to 55-65 degrees.
-   Phase 2 (Weeks 5–12):
    -   Exercise Adjustment: Transition to low-intensity activities (e.g., yoga, walking) to minimize mitochondrial stress.
    -   Reduce chelation to biweekly, maintain other interventions, and increase PBM to 10 J/cm² if tolerated.
    -   Ramp up CT times. Focus on cranial CT with carotid cooling
-   Phase 3 (Months 4–12):
    -   Sustain circadian alignment, sunlight, grounding, DDW; reduce hypertonic saline/methylene blue to monthly; PBM biweekly.
    -   Monitor energy/cognition via standardized scales.

* * *

### alipankey — 2025-06-03T02:17:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alison%E2%80%99s-optimal-journal.30456/post-353811>

**THANK YOU:**
Thank you so much, [@JackKruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/5/), for this detailed reply and the time it took to write it! What a wonderful assessment.

**LIFE UPDATES:**
Sorry for my late reply. I was doing some 24/7 caregiving, bathed in WiFi, and just came up to breathe.

My night owl and Non-24 rears its ugly head under such intensity, to the point that I’m barely hanging on. So now I hopefully have a window to work on my health.

**GREAT ASSESSMENT:**
Anyway, in my post, you really read between the lines and hit a lot of things spot on!

Though I was probably less of an athlete than you gave me credit for… it was probably only 1.5-2 years of intensity. San Francisco was 1998 to 2010, so thankfully I missed the worst of cell towers and phones. The “No Recovery Since” sounds totally accurate. Along with the outcomes for the factors. I have no jabs.

**SENSITIVITY / MOBILIZING METALS**
There are some treatments I know I can‘t tolerate—at least not now. I may not be believed, but I literally cannot tolerate even tablespoons of DDW for even a few days without a crash that I can’t recover from unless I stop intake. I wasted a few hundred dollars on testing and DDW water. Anything that mobilizes metals absolutely destroys me and quickly. In El Salvador recently, I wasn’t sleeping well for a while, and took 2-3 days of just 1 capsule of magnesium glycinate each night. I knew better, but I was desperate to sleep. After 2-3 days I could barely walk, and cancelled a waterfall hike . When I have these crashes, I literally feel like the life force is being sucked out of me, and FAST! It’s quite intimidating, and has happened often. The crashes can last from days to weeks to months, and really years at a low level. So I feel like the sun / melanin might the only way to get the heavy metals out safely for now. With this level of personal sensitivity, I would decline injections of any kind.

**RECOVERY OPTIONS:**
So for the moment, I think the best (only?) thing I can successfully do is sunlight and grounding. What do you think?

**TINNITUS:**
Can sun and grounding cure tinnitus? I also have a horrific case of that, non-stop for 3+ years, post viral infection

**HOUSING:**
I’m basically becoming homeless on June 23 with few funds, as the house I was staying for free in just sold. El Salvador is not my jam. I gave it two tries, and it is not for me.

**CARIBBEAN:**
The Caribbean sounds like a great place to be! So I’m ideally looking at the Caribbean first, with plenty of volcanic islands; and other Central America countries second, maybe. Suggestions certainly welcome.

I found an old post (2014) on the forum from you, so it might not be relevant anymore. But may I ask how did you “test” the Caribbean? I have a full set of high quality meters from Safe Living Technologies—no Amazon crap . But I don’t have a magnetometer. Which islands tested the best?

2014 ”Well I have not tested Europe......but I did test the Caribbean and Mexico and the Northern part of South America. The Carribean is a great place to be. The Riveria Maya in Mexico is also quite good.”

———

Many thanks again! I was delighted to see so much great feedback from you, and I hope you are doing well!

# Thread: Optimal journey, starting early 2025
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journey-starting-early-2025.30459/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JulieBlueSky — 2025-03-11T19:16:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journey-starting-early-2025.30459/post-348392>

It has been eight weeks since my husband and I found Jack's info from his podcast with Robert Breedlove. It has caused major changes in our lifestyle which we are still working on.
We are in the southwest UK (latitude 50) and its March so we started in January.
We are software programmers so whole life on computers and pretty much night owls, husband a dreadful sleeper and I could drop off anywhere/anytime. 57 and no centralized meds or jabs, so starting from a fairly good position in normal health terms.
We have changed all the lightbulbs in the house back to incandescent, or daylight led, or red led. We have invested in blue light blocking glasses for when we have to go out at night.
We have got up EVERY day and been outside for the sunrise ( or cloud rise because its winter here and they are spraying a lot see hazy pic below) luckily we have a nice garden. 1.5 hrs of outside daylight before even looking at phone or computers. 2 hrs at midday outside, in shorts if sun out (we have a tan! woo hoo never thought I would have a tan in March in the UK. and outside for sunset. then NO computers or phones. that has been super hard. Computers during the day have IRIS to help mostly in programming mode.
Bonus we are both in bed by 9.30 and asleep really quickly and sleeping really well and solidly through the night. We have much more energy through the day, no daytime or after lunch naps. Feeling really light weight wise but the scales don't agree! No uvb here yet to turn off appetite.
We both gave up our glasses 2 years this june (hubby astigmatism since early 20's - now gone) me +2.15 in both eyes, now back to normal, well i dont wear glasses a tall i have learnt the art of breathing and patience and cannot believe how clear things are, they still swim around somewhat and i have to wait, and i have loads of swimmers which i am hoping the sunlight will fix,(also taking lots of dha to help hopefully) but have a magnifying glass for emergencies.
So this has been a massive change for us, especially reduction in blue light during the day. Our son is also taking an interest in what we are doing as he is a real nightime gamer and now a btc savage too!
Really enjoying this whole experience, but thinking if this is right, and feels right, then we need to retire in spain.

Question- with our winters being about 5 months of really low sunlight, are we better off having 1 month in deep winter away in the sun to top melanin production with uvb or are we better off having 3 or 4 one week holidays and splitting it up? bearing in 4 hour flight involved too. Any thoughts? Hubbie is having a hard time embracing the suck of cold, but trying really hard to expose his skin.

sunrise a couple of days ago.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" } [](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img_1424-jpeg.30276/)

### JulieBlueSky — 2025-03-11T19:38:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journey-starting-early-2025.30459/post-348395>

Also went wired in the house 2 years ago, no wifi. phones only on 4g, bluetooth when needed, and certainly not in the bedroom. Have an emf meter, found out next doors smart tv was zapping us, so moved a unit and installed tin foil on the back! worked a treat.
Lots of good grass fed meat and dairy, have now dropped the fruits and food from abroad due to the water biomarkers,. being inconsistent with our latitude and season.
Feel a bit intimated by the all the blood and tests everyone is posting....

### brianalfredeneuman — 2025-03-12T11:44:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journey-starting-early-2025.30459/post-348446>

I just joined the Gold membership. I have a very short attention span to read through the threads. Do you have any suggestions for a guy who has limited attention and wants to start learning fast. I am listening to all the podcasts and want to figure out how to set up my life correctly. I have been stuck to a computer screen for work but and don't know how to change that, i have two young kids (have to pay for them). Thinking of steps to start, light bulbs to change, waking and seeing the sun, stopping any cell phone use, I use MB but not even sure if that's a good idea at this point. Any help would be amazing. ### JulieBlueSky — 2025-03-12T18:31:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journey-starting-early-2025.30459/post-348462>

> [brianalfredeneuman said:](/goto/post?id=348446)
>
> I just joined the Gold membership. I have a very short attention span to read through the threads. Do you have any suggestions for a guy who has limited attention and wants to start learning fast. I am listening to all the podcasts and want to figure out how to set up my life correctly. I have been stuck to a computer screen for work but and don't know how to change that, i have two young kids (have to pay for them). Thinking of steps to start, light bulbs to change, waking and seeing the sun, stopping any cell phone use, I use MB but not even sure if that's a good idea at this point. Any help would be amazing. One beginner leading another...

Just do what you can do and tackle each thing that you can easily do first.

So first off set an alarm and get outside first thing. i try to at least 30 mins plus before coming in to eat and make a cuppa.
make a list whilst you are outside of all the things you have already picked up from listening to the podcasts, and see which are the easy low hanging fruit, and knock those off, get stuff ordered on its way to you, while you decide what else you can do.

Even if you are in an office you should be able to take breaks (pretend you have started smoking and join the fag breakers outside as much as you can getaway with!)

I have IRIS the screen app is excellent for easily changing computer screen settings and get some blue glasses.
so you already know the main things to do, start with that list and do what you can do.
Hardest thing for us was turning off the tech in evenings. we listen to podcasts now, downloaded.
good luck

this morning's sky woo hoo 10 oclock. [@Joseph LeBlanc](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26734/)

### JulieBlueSky — 2025-04-03T14:50:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journey-starting-early-2025.30459/post-350015>

So the couple of days in lanzarote resulted in a slip by the pool, full dump on coccyx and writing arm out (auto reflex) to stop the fall, resulting in shunt to the wrist and elbow. Still have full movement immediately after fall, gave it good poke to make sure nothing broken, checked in with paramedic daughter who said bend this poke that, pressure here type thing and agreed nothing broken.
An hour later completely locked arm no movement at all! it was very painful. Not much sleep but forced myself up for sunrise and then arm in the sun all day. Really pleased have that solar callous so no burning. Walked in the volcanic wet sand and really took it easy. Anyway long story short was all good until about 10 days later when it still wasn't stretching out fully. Quick xray to confirm nothing broken, so keep it moving.
Borrow friends infrared lamp, what joy, 4 x 20 mins a day (forgetting the glasses of course - i have to get rid of damage in eye and hoped this might help - JK's talk about his father-in laws broken leg and red light for the pain = dont forget to wear the glasses - he forgot, 4 weeks later macular degeneration much reduced, so thought hey ho will give it a try at the same time.
arm is now nearly straight after 4 days and no pain. so all good.
Son eased his back and turned his cold around by sitting in front of it too!
tan is looking amazing, OMG i have bought a kiniki (no photos!) and am in the garden at the end of march. Def. a crazy talking monkey. Haven't had a bikini since my early teens! .
Weekend took a seaweed foraging class, I am a convert, love the stuff. The chap who took the course brought loads of cooked and dried samples so we were able to appreciate immediately what it tasted like. Really lucky to live 20 mins from the beach. Only me in short sleeves and no sunglasses that day, everyone else had winter woolies on and sunglasses. sigh!

### JulieBlueSky — 2025-05-18T11:02:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journey-starting-early-2025.30459/post-352812>

So update on health. Still not missed a sunrise or sunset since January. Almost straight to bed after sunset here now in the UK. Mornings are gift and wonderful when we get to actually see the sun.
We have started supplementing a little bit of copper bisgycinate to help with getting iron out of spleen and back where is should be.
I still have my floaters they are slightly lighter but still really beaded sheets.
Hubbies foot fungus on big toe is healing for the first time in six years. The new nail is halfway up the nail bed and still looking great, so that is major. His hives has also gone which is a bonus. BUT since January he has been struggling with what originally looked like an eczema patch on lower leg but just has not healed and has doubled in size and now he is has individual spots all over chest, arms and back. These itch like crazy and weep and really hurt when in full sun, so he is keeping to early morning exposed sun, for the red light effect but keeping it in the shade during the day. Lots of reflected UV but not direct as too painful. Since starting the copper on the 9th may it has started to dry up a bit, but it's really slow.
I have suggested walks in the sea (local and easy to do) but he is being a chicken about this and I really think it will help - CT, salt and neg ions and all that.

### JulieBlueSky — 2025-06-26T10:18:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journey-starting-early-2025.30459/post-355300>

.So we have made it to six months of not missing a sunrise or sunset, really interesting to find it has been easy overall, nearly 'summer' in the Uk so have had many visible full sunrises, but mainly we just are sat in the grey, and this morning rain. but we are still sticking with it, Jack again said at BTC prague keynote speech this year that even if cloudy you still need to get out, so we have been.

Overall health is good, sleep is great, weight it static from my point of view. Not much improvement in the floaters but have been increasing daytime screen breaks for 45 mins work 15 mins outside during work hours.

Biggest change is husbands rashes, all the ones the back, chest have gone completely not a trace, the few on his forearms which were large have lost all their scabs and now are pink but appear to be tanning.

The largest one his leg did completely scab over and these have fallen off leaving some raised spots underneath still but these are not itching and not getting bigger but slowly receding, even has a completely clear patch in the middle which appears tanned. So whilst still needs more time it is going in the right direction.
Jack had said somewhere, get up every day and be consistant, nature will heal you in the way she has to, not the way you want, as far as husband is concerned she better know what she is doing because she is taking her sweet time about it from his point of view, from my side six months to clear something systemic is excellent, and maybe if my eyes were fixed sooner I wouldn't be structured with my tech breaks during the day, so in an odd way wonder whether she is keeping those floaters to fix other stuff, knowing that if they went I would be less strict about my breaks!

So all in all, pretty happy, our son is so much happier in himself and sleeping much better, his screen time has greatly reduced and the blue blockers come on for his last hour of use before sundown. It is good to see him taking responsibility for his own health, and future wealth!

And we are all really rocking our tans, never been so brown in my life!

My nephew 17 years old (in Moscow) got pneumothorax condition. He had high fever, then got better and did workout with kettlebells. His body composition is very thin and tall. So his muscle in rib cage are weak. Doctors took the air out of the tissues. He is in the hospital. But the lung tissue is not growing well together. So they suggest to do the surgery. But my nephew has very important IT competition that would take him to the desired university like next week. Doctors are suggesting to artificially glue tissues together for the time of competition and then to do the surgery.
I'm assuming that the tissues are not growing together because there is no substantial energy in mitochondria for generating inner electrical charge that will turn on the healing process. Of course, he is not outside ever, not in the sun (there is no sun there for more then 7-8 months), lots of blue light, especially at night from the screen.

Help me, please, to understand more what can be done in case of gluing the tissues temporary, how to generate and boost inner electrical charge? IR light, blocking blue light. Taking MB?

# Thread: Catherine's Optimal journal

**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 36
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Catherine Drapeau — 2025-03-12T00:22:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/catherines-optimal-journal.30462/post-348416>

« Remember; nature has been whispering in your ears, you already had three cancers » This is what Dr. Jack said to me on my January 23th 2025’s consultation.

My name is Catherine Drapeau. I am a 46 yrs old French Canadian presently living in British Columbia at the 54’ latitude. My husband and I have been building a homestead for the past 3 years and we taught we had picked the perfect place. But when my husband came accros Dr Jack at the beginning of January 2025, I knew what had picked the wrong place! Dr Jack made me understand that the reason my body keeps making tumors is because of an trans-generational epi-genetic problem. Presently, I have a solid mass that looks malignant on the cervix and two fibroids in the uterus.

Before my consultation with Jack, I was so puzzle. I could not understand what I was doing wrong. Even my 21 years old god-son said; « Cathou, I don’t understand! You have always been in Nature, lived an healthy life and eat healthy food, why Sophie and Dominic (my brother and sister) never have cancer and you do? » Now that I understand that my Mitochondria has never right in the first place, I could explain to him why it keeps on happening! Recently, I have learned that my mother had the Polio vaccine and had a cancer in the uterus when she was in her twenties. She didn't breast feed me.

Please, tell me if I am wrong Dr Jack, because my circadian has a miss-match, I am aging at a much faster rate then a woman born with a good mitochondria.

At age 23, I had a first breast cancer. Stage 3, 2 tumors, a lumpectomy, 8 chemo of 3 cocktails and 25 radiation treatments. I was told I had the BRCA1 mutation.

At age 37, I had a new breast cancer in the other breast. Stage 3, 1 tumor, 6 chemo, and too many surgeries to count.

Age 39, I agree to a prophylactic surgery of the ovaries because of the risk associated with the mutation of the BRCA1. The pathology came back with a positive diagnose of ovarian cancer (clear cell carcinoma) on one of the ovaries. More chemo and radiation was on the menu.

After the castration, I was put on HRT (Estradiol 17-B patch and the « medoxy-progesterone » /horse progesterone non bio-identical). Hence, my body had the hardest time to adjust to the removal of all my sexual hormones. I started having cognitive issues, vertigo, tinnitus, dizziness, balance issues, aches and pain, difficulty getting out of the house to go to public places and difficulty driving. I had a couple of zapping episodes in my brain that left my face funny looking for a while….it was absolute hell. I often thought that dying from ovarian cancer would have been easier. It lasted a couple years. Finally, one day I came across a better gynecologist outside of the BC Cancer Agency who told me that I needed the « real » bio-identical progesterone Prometrium. After finding the « right » dosage, my brain functions and mood improved tremendously. My dosage is presently; Estradiol 17-B 100 mg patch (twice a week) and 300 mg of Prometrium every night.

My first question to you Dr Jack, in your opinion, does that dosage of HRT seem ok. I feel good on that, the best I have felt since the castration. Is Estradiol 17-B (Sandoz Trans-derm) safe considering my present cancer?

My second question to you Dr Jack, would you recommend testosterone? I have tried a couple of compounded cream in Canada but they both had sometime toxic in them because one gave me headaches (like a chemical headache) and the other joint stiffness. The dosage was .5mg. Very low both times. During my consult with Dr Alexis Cowan on February 6th, she highly recommended some injection of Testosterone. I have yet to find someone here in El Salvador to help me with that. Please, tell me what you think.

I have been here in El Salvador since February 18th. I took your words and Alexis’s words very seriously. I will have to for the rest of my life. It hasn’t been easy emotionally and I don’t know what my future will be but I would like to live longer.

I haven’t missed a sunrise and I only missed one sunset because a friend was visiting at our house that evening. I am grounding, drinking DDW, eating a diet in low DDW for 99% of the time. I eat oysters every day. I am in bed between 6:45-7:15pm every night. I take 12 mg of Ivermectin with my breakfast on the beach after 30 minutes of the sunrise. I have a tan and I am keeping it. I sleep ok must of the time if my environment is « calm/quiet », if my mind is free of worries. I have had 3 lipomas (2 on each leg and 1 on my stomach) for many many years. I think, the first one appeared in 2008. They are all getting smaller!  I can believe that the ones in my belly must be getting smaller.

My third question to you Dr Jack, is should I take more than 12 mg/a day? Dr Alexis suggested that dosage.

As for the full hysterectomy you recommended to me during my consul because of the « SD », all I can say for now is that I rarely have belly pain since I have been in El Salvador. I haven’t had a bloated stomach either. My husband is coming for the last two weeks of April and will take me back home. I can update you then.

I will go back home to BC for May and the rest of the summer. I will most likely come back to the tropics in the fall. I have a MRI scan on May 21th. Although, I am wondering if another ultra sound would be better for my health instead of an MRI because of the contrast. What do you think Dr Jack ? I look forward to know if my body was able to melt those tumors away here in ESalvador.

If I make it, my next journey will be to convince my husband to buy a little piece of this El Salvadorian magical land so I can add time to my longevity.

Dr Kruse, please, if you think of anything else I would be doing, please tell me. I don’t know what is my purpose in life anymore other than surviving this new war. My mantra since the first cancer has always been: « What would RAMBO do? He would fight! » Perhaps, if I win this one, life will bring me a new sense of purpose so I can use a new Mantra for once.

Thank you for reading and helping me Dr Kruse.

Catherine

### Catherine Drapeau — 2025-03-13T23:51:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/catherines-optimal-journal.30462/post-348555>

Dr Jack, I have had a brown horizontal mark inside both of my thighs for the past couple of weeks. They are both at the same level on each side. Do you have an explanation? Why horizontal, why same location on both legs? One has been fading (on the left leg) but a small one is becoming apparent closer to the inside of the knee! Gracias!

The Decentralized model posits that nnEMF and blue light damage melanopsin, mtDNA, and heme proteins, reducing DDW production, dehydrating melanin, and lowering éR, leading to ROS/RNS overproduction.

1.  Transgenerational Epigenetic Mitochondrial Dysfunction:
    -   Her mother’s polio vaccine (likely SV40-contaminated, linked to cancer) and uterine cancer suggest mtDNA damage passed to her, compounded by no breastfeeding (missed maternal melatonin, microbiome support). BRCA1 mutation (DNA repair defect) and mitochondrial dysfunction (T2b-like haplogroup predisposition) increase éR, driving tumorigenesis (breast, ovarian, cervical, fibroids).
2.  Cancer History and Treatments:
    -   Breast cancer (23, 37), ovarian cancer (39), and current cervical mass/fibroids reflect high éR and reductive stress (high NADH/NAD⁺, lactate, GDF15). Chemo/radiation damaged mtDNA, increasing éR, while nnEMF exposure in British Columbia (54°N, urban) exacerbated this (low UV-A, high nnEMF).
3.  Post-Ovariectomy Symptoms:
    -   Cognitive issues, vertigo, tinnitus, dizziness, zapping episodes, and balance issues post-ovariectomy (39) reflect nnEMF-induced melanopsin damage, low NO, and éR disruption (dehydrated melanin). Non-bioidentical HRT (medroxyprogesterone) worsened this (pro-inflammatory), while bioidentical Prometrium (300 mg) and Estradiol 17-B (100 mg patch) improved symptoms by supporting redox and neuronal function.
4.  El Salvador (13°N) Improvements:
    -   High solar EMF (UV-A) at 13°N regenerates melanopsin, boosts NO/DDW, and lowers éR, explaining shrinking lipomas (reduced éR, improved fat metabolism), better sleep (melatonin/dopamine balance), and reduced belly pain (improved steroidogenesis, gut redox). Her tan reflects melanin hydration, enhancing éR toward a healthier colony of mtDNA.
5.  British Columbia (54°N) Transition:
    -   Moving to 54°N reduces UV-A (weaker sun, shorter days), increasing nnEMF/blue light exposure (urban British Columbia). This may elevate éR, reductive stress, and GDF15, risking tumor growth, symptom recurrence (tinnitus, cognitive issues), and aging acceleration.

* * *

**Predictions for Her Transition to British Columbia**

1\. Reduced Solar EMF Increases éR and Tumor Risk

-   Prediction: Moving to British Columbia (54°N) reduces UV-A exposure, elevating éR and reductive stress, risking growth of cervical mass/fibroids and symptom recurrence.
-   Mechanism: Lower UV-A at 54°N reduces melanopsin signaling, NO, and DDW production, increasing éR and NADH/NAD⁺ (reductive stress). This reverses Krebs cycle reactions, raising lactate and GDF15, impairing mitochondrial function. nnEMF/blue light exposure exacerbates this, dehydrating melanin and driving tumorigenesis (BRCA1 mutation, mtDNA damage). Reduced vitamin D lowers immune function (NK cells), increasing cancer risk.
-   Outcome: Potential tumor growth (MRI/ultrasound showing increased cervical mass/fibroid size), high GDF15 (>1000 pg/mL), and low vitamin D (<80 ng/mL), measurable via imaging and blood tests.

2\. Symptom Recurrence and Aging Acceleration

-   Prediction: nnEMF and low UV-A in British Columbia increase éR, causing the recurrence of tinnitus, cognitive issues, vertigo, and accelerated aging.
-   Mechanism: Reduced solar EMF lowers melatonin/dopamine balance (melanopsin damage), increasing éR and reductive stress, impairing neuronal bioelectric signaling (tinnitus, vertigo, cognitive issues). High éR suppresses anabolism (e.g., collagen, telomeres), accelerating aging (transgenerational mitochondrial dysfunction).
-   Outcome: Increased tinnitus severity (THI >30), cognitive decline (MoCA <26), and aging markers (e.g., telomere length reduction), measurable via clinical scales and genetic tests.

3\. AM Solar Exposure and Grounding Mitigate Risks

-   Prediction: AM solar exposure (20–30 minutes daily) and grounding (1 hour daily) in British Columbia reduce éR, maintain anti-cancer defenses, and slow aging.
-   Mechanism: AM sunlight (UV-A, latitude 54°N) regenerates melanopsin, boosts NO/DDW, and lowers éR, reducing NADH/NAD⁺ and GDF15. Grounding donates electrons, reducing ROS/RNS and stabilizing éR. This supports vitamin D (>120 ng/mL), enhances immune function, and slows aging (telomere preservation).
-   Outcome: Stable vitamin D (>120 ng/mL), reduced tinnitus (THI <20), improved cognition (MoCA >28), and slower aging (telomere length stable) within 12 weeks.

4. Comprehensive Redox Protocol Prevents Tumor Growth

-   Prediction: A redox-first protocol (AM sunlight, grounding, DDW, PBM, hypertonic saline, methylene blue, ivermectin) prevents tumor growth and resolves symptoms.
-   Mechanism: AM sunlight, PBM, and grounding lower éR, DDW enhances mitochondrial function, hypertonic saline improves perfusion, and methylene blue repairs mtDNA/NO. Ivermectin targets cancer cells (mitochondrial inhibition), while solar EMF supports immune function, preventing tumor growth.
-   Outcome: No tumor growth (MRI/ultrasound clear), resolved tinnitus, normal GDF15 (<1000 pg/mL), and improved sleep (melatonin >20 pg/mL) at 6 months.

* * *

**Answers to Questions and Recommendations**

Question 1: Is Her HRT Dosage (Estradiol 17-B 100 mg Patch, Prometrium 300 mg) Safe Given Cervical Cancer?

-   Assessment: Estradiol 17-B (100 mg patch, twice weekly) and Prometrium (300 mg nightly) are bioidentical, supporting redox potential and neuronal function, as evidenced by her improved mood and cognition. However, estradiol can stimulate estrogen receptor-positive (ER+) cancers, a concern with her cervical mass (possibly malignant) and BRCA1 mutation. Prometrium (progesterone) is protective, counteracting estrogen’s proliferative effects, but the balance must be monitored. It promotes hydration but sun is safer option. DDW next best option
-   Prediction: Estradiol may increase cervical tumor growth if ER+, but Prometrium mitigates this risk. Her current stability suggests the dosage is tolerable, but imaging is critical and her doctor needs to follow it.
-   Recommendation:
    -   Continue current dosage (Estradiol 17-B 100 mg patch, Prometrium 300 mg nightly) if imaging (MRI/ultrasound) shows no tumor growth.
    -   If tumor growth is detected, reduce Estradiol to 50 mg patch (or discontinue) and increase Prometrium to 400 mg nightly (consult gynecologist). Monitor tumor markers (e.g., CA-125, target 5 U/mL). The photo-bioelectric current should drop the level. If it rises on protocol, I speak to the oncologist about stopping it
    -   Test estrogen/progesterone levels (target E ratio 1:200–300) to ensure balance, minimizing cancer risk while supporting cognition.

Question 2: Should She Add Testosterone (Injection vs. Cream)?

-   Assessment: Low-dose testosterone cream (0.5 mg) caused side effects (headaches, joint stiffness), likely from toxic excipients (e.g., propylene glycol) in compounded formulations, not testosterone itself. Testosterone injections (recommended by Dr. Alexis Cowan who is not an MD very puzzling to me) can support anabolism, mitochondrial function, and mood, countering éR-driven catabolism (cancer, aging). Her BRCA1 mutation and cervical cancer history require caution, as testosterone can convert to estrogen via aromatase, potentially stimulating ER+ tumors. Your oncologist should be consulted for this, not a PhD in New Jersey.
-   Prediction: Testosterone injections (low dose, e.g., 10–20 mg/week) will improve anabolism, cognition, and energy without significant cancer risk if monitored, outperforming creams.
-   Recommendation:
    -   Discuss with the cancer team------> Start testosterone injections (10 mg/week, intramuscular, bioidentical testosterone cypionate) in El Salvador or British Columbia. Monitor for aromatization (estradiol levels, target 0 pg/mL) and tumor growth (CA-125, imaging).
    -   Avoid compounded creams due to excipient toxicity; if injections aren’t available, use a transdermal gel (e.g., AndroGel 1%, 12.5 mg/day) with clean ingredients.
    -   Pair with Prometrium (progesterone) to counter estrogen conversion, supporting anti-cancer effects. DDW use wise.

Question 3: Should She Increase Ivermectin Beyond 12 mg/Day?

-   Assessment: Ivermectin (12 mg/day, per Dr. Alexis Cowan again is concerning. Dr. Makis is a better expert) targets cancer cells via mitochondrial inhibition (increases éR in cancer cells, inducing apoptosis), as seen in shrinking lipomas. Your anxiety around reducing dosage reflects fear of recurrence, but 12 mg/day is a standard anti-cancer dose, and higher doses risk side effects (e.g., neurotoxicity).
-   Prediction: 12 mg/day is sufficient for cancer prevention, especially with solar EMF and redox support. Higher doses (e.g., 24 mg/day) may increase éR in healthy cells, causing reductive stress which would be in Canada.
-   Recommendation: Talk to Dr. Makis about this.
    -   Maintain ivermectin at 12 mg/day (taken with breakfast on the beach, post-sunrise). If imaging shows tumor growth, increase to 24 mg/day (split doses, 12 mg 2x/day) for 4 weeks, then revert to 12 mg/day.
    -   Monitor for side effects (e.g., dizziness, nausea); if present, reduce to 6 mg/day.
    -   Continue until imaging confirms tumor resolution, then taper to maintenance (12 mg weekly).

Question 4: MRI vs. Ultrasound for Imaging?

-   Assessment: MRI with contrast (e.g., gadolinium) increases nnEMF exposure and oxidative stress (gadolinium toxicity), elevating éR and cancer risk. Ultrasound avoids these risks, using sound waves, and is effective for assessing cervical masses and fibroids.
-   Prediction: Ultrasound will safely confirm tumor status, while MRI may increase éR, risking tumor growth and symptom recurrence.
-   Recommendation:
    -   Right now might be best to opt for ultrasound (pelvic, transvaginal) on May 21, 2025, instead of MRI. It’s safer for assessing cervical mass/fibroids and avoids nnEMF/gadolinium exposure. Your symptoms have improved. If they get worse in Canada MRI is wise.
    -   If the ultrasound is inconclusive, consider MRI without contrast as a secondary option, but minimize exposure (e.g., short scan duration).
    -   Pair with thermogram (infrared imaging) to assess metabolic activity of the mass (cancer vs. benign). Oncologist should make that call with you.

**Additional Considerations and Recommendations**

Phase 1: Redox Maintenance in El Salvador (Last 2 Weeks, April 2025)

-   Maximize Solar EMF: Continue AM sunrises (30 minutes, 6:30–7 a.m.), add PM sunsets (30 minutes, 5:30–6 p.m.) to further lower éR, boost NO, and support tumor regression before leaving.
-   Grounding: 3 hours daily, barefoot on beach sand (wet sand enhances electron transfer). Reduces éR and ROS/RNS.
-   DDW: Drink 1–2 liters daily (<100 ppm deuterium). Supports mitochondrial function, lowers lactate, and enhances cancer defense.
-   Photobiomodulation (PBM): 600-1000 nm, 5 J/cm² daily on pelvis (cervical mass/fibroids) and ears (tinnitus). Reduces éR, repairs mtDNA, and boosts NO to improve stem cell depots to stimulate recovery.
-   Discuss with oncologist: Hypertonic Saline: 3% NaCl, 250 mL IV weekly (if available, monitor sodium/pH). Improves perfusion for tumor regression.
-   Discuss with oncologist: Methylene Blue (Mb): 0.5 mg/kg orally daily (10 mg/day, pharmaceutical grade). Lowers éR, boosts NO, and supports mtDNA repair for cancer prevention.
-   Diet: Continue oysters (zinc, omega-3s for immune support), low-DDW diet (meat, seafood, eggs, local fruit). Avoid processed carbs (fuels Warburg redox shift).

Phase 2: Transition to British Columbia (Weeks 1–4, May 2025)

-   AM Sunlight Exposure: 30–60 minutes daily (8–9 a.m., latitude 54°N), direct skin/eye exposure. Maintains vitamin D (>120 ng/mL), NO, and éR.
-   Grounding: 3 hour daily, barefoot on grass/soil (British Columbia’s summer allows this).
-   DDW: Continue 1–2 liters daily. Prevents éR increase from nnEMF.
-   PBM: Continue , @ 5 J/cm² daily on pelvis, ears, and scalp (cognitive support). Reduces éR and supports anti-cancer effects.
-   Screen Mitigation:
    -   Use blue light blockers (e.g., Lucia Eyes or TrueDark Daylights) for any screen use.
    -   Limit screen time to <1 hour/day, and take 5-minute grounding breaks hourly.
    -   Move screens >3 feet away (inverse square law reduces nnEMF).
-   Ultrasound Imaging (May 21): Confirm tumor status (cervical mass/fibroids). If resolved, continue current protocol; if growth, escalate ivermectin to 24 mg/day and consider surgical consult.
-   Monitor Symptoms:
    -   Tinnitus: PBM on ears, monitor THI (target <20).
    -   Cognition: Assess MoCA (target >28), continue Estradiol/Prometrium.
    -   Sleep: Maintain circadian alignment (sleep by 10 p.m., wake by 6 a.m.), use red glasses post-sunset.

Phase 3: Long-Term Prevention in British Columbia (Months 2–6, June–October 2025)

-   Sustain sunlight, grounding, DDW, PBM biweekly, and saline/Mb monthly with MD assist.
-   Prepare for El Salvador Return (Fall 2025):
    -   Returning to 13°N will further lower éR, supporting tumor regression and longevity. Continue AM sunlight/grounding.
    -   Maintain ivermectin at 12 mg/day (or weekly maintenance if tumors resolved).
-   British Columbia Maintenance:
    -   Use full-spectrum light (e.g., Sperti Vitamin D Lamp, 10 minutes daily) if sunlight is limited (cloudy days).
    -   Add infrared sauna (20 minutes 3x/week) to boost thermogenesis and NO (supports aging mitigation).
-   Aging Mitigation:
    -   Monitor telomere length (target stable) and GDF15 (<1000 pg/mL) to track aging.
    -   Continue testosterone (10 mg/week injection) to support anabolism and slow aging.
-   Cancer Prevention:
    -   Regular imaging (ultrasound every 3 months if tumors persist, thermogram annually).
    -   Monitor CA-125 (target 5 U/mL), GDF15, and CRP (<1 mg/L).
-   Hysterectomy Consideration:
    -   If ultrasound shows tumor growth, proceed with hysterectomy (as recommended during your consult) to eliminate cancer risk. If resolved, delay surgery and monitor.

Additional Strategies

-   Mold/Toxin Exposure: Test for mold in Canada because you are at super high risk due to altered immunity (urine mycotoxins, alpha-MSH, TGF-beta1) in British Columbia (homestead may have mold). If positive, detox with itraconazole (200 mg daily) after alpha-MSH >40 ng/L.
-   Lipomas: Continue monitoring (shrinking reflects éR reduction); PBM on sites (5 J/cm² daily) to accelerate resolution.
-   El Salvador Land Purchase: Strongly support buying land in El Salvador (13°N). Long-term residence in a high-solar, low-nnEMF environment will lower éR, slow aging, reduce cancer risk, and align with your model’s principles.

Practical Tips: An Idiot’s Guide to Uncle Jack

-   Morning Routine: Wake with sunrise (6:30–7 a.m., British Columbia), step outside barefoot, face the sun, drink 1 liter DDW with sea salt. Ground for 3 hour.
-   Work Setup: Use blue blockers, limit screens to <2 hours/day, ground during breaks.
-   Hydration: Sip 1-liter bottle every hour, aim for 3–4 liters/day with DDW.
-   Methylene Blue: 10 mg/day (oral), increase to 20 mg if tolerated. Buy from a compounding pharmacy.
-   Diet: Continue oysters, meat, seafood, eggs, fruit. Avoid processed carbs, alcohol.
-   Sleep: Sleep by 10 p.m., use red glasses post-sunset. Ensure quiet environment (white noise if needed).
-   Cancer Protocol: Ivermectin (12 mg/day), testosterone (10 mg/week injection), monitor with ultrasound/thermogram.

Hola Dr Jack,

Here is a little update from week 5 in El Salvador.
My daily routine is very simple:
\- Up at 5:30 am and go to the beach for Sunrise. Within 30 mns I eat my protein breakfast with 12mg of IVermectin.
\- I stay at the beach and ground/swim for an average of 4-6 hours. Within that time, it's oysters fiesta.
\- I go home for lunch/dinner (high fat,protein, some veggies low in DW and grass fed greek yogourt) and I eat under the trees for chemo treatments : [https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1812138235750908392](https://x.com/DrJackKruse/status/1812138235750908392)
\- I do a naked sun bathing session almost everyday unless I can't have enough privacy.
\- Drink DDW.
\- I do some reading/researching online and ground if it has to be more than 1 hour.
\- I go back to the beach around 3:30-4:00pm for more grounding and swimming.
\- I go home as soon as the sun is no longer in my sight.
\- I am sleeping by 8pm maximum.

Results at the moment:
\-My Lipomas keeps on shrinking.
\- I have never burned badly.
\- I am keeping my tan.
\- I think I have more white hair.
\- My back pain is back and I think I have to stop eating that fabulous grass fed greek yogourt. Will try for a week :-(!
\- I don't have more energy to do more than what I do at the moment. Perhaps, my energy goes to repairing my body.
\- I lost fat that I have on my body since I was 16 yrs old.
\- Stomach bloating and pain down by 95%.
\- My ears cracks and experiences more issues than at home. I have some tinnitus, fullness in right ear and pressure around right eye and nose. Those uncomfortable sensation got better living in the bush and are getting worse here. Too often, in my opinion, I am around people with phone on, or funny ring that scan their body (bluetooth device) ect....

Dr Jack, I have received Dr Makis's protocol. Can you please tell me what you think of it? I have sent you the entire document in a private message. I am a little puzzle at the moment. I have two protocols for drugs to take on my hand and I am not sure which to follow. Could you please give me some directions?

Lastly, your long time member Steve Turner has offer me his Red light panel while I am here. Do you recommend I start the PBM sonner then the last two weeks of April?

Thank you Dr Jack.
