### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-28T23:34:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/catherines-optimal-journal.30462/post-349527>

**Deuterium Depletion as a Cancer Therapy:**

The new paper provides real-world evidence for deuterium depletion’s therapeutic potential: integrating DDW into conventional cancer therapy significantly enhances survival, with a 75-80% reduction in cancer-related mortality. This aligns with Somlyai framework in his book, as DDW breaks the cycle of mitochondrial dysfunction, inflammation, and DNA instability by reducing deuterium’s kinetic effects. The study’s finding that survival correlates with DDW duration (r = 0.476, p < 0.001) and timing (better outcomes with earlier DDW adoption) underscores the importance of deuterium depletion in cancer management, supporting the mechanistic links to pH, voltage, and metabolic health in our framework.

Stressors elevate mitochondrial deuterium, increasing water viscosity, slowing the ATP synthase nanomotor, and disrupting proton dynamics, leading to a pH drop and cellular depolarization. This triggers UCP amplification, collapsing the proton gradient, while COX-2 ramps up via inflammation from leaky barriers (gut, BBB, BRB) and high-deuterium metabolic pathways (e.g., glycolysis in cancer cells). The resulting acidity spreads to blood and CSF, distorting water networks and promoting DNA instability via deuterium-driven hydroxyl radicals.

Deuterium-depleted water (DDW), as shown in the new study, significantly enhances cancer survival (MST of 12.4 years vs. 2.4 years in the general Hungarian cancer population) by restoring mitochondrial function, stabilizing pH/voltage, reducing inflammation (e.g., COX-2), and protecting DNA.
Red light therapy (600-1000 nm) lowers mitochondrial water viscosity, enhances ATP production, and reduces inflammation, while ketogenic diets reduce deuterium intake, supporting NAD+ levels and mitochondrial respiration. Together, these strategies—DDW, red light, and ketogenic diets, can break the cycle of mitochondrial dysfunction, systemic inflammation, and cancer progression, aligning with Somlyai's deuterium depletion framework and offering a promising therapeutic approach. Replacing the sun for PBM would offer better results in my opinion.

**CITES**

[https://www.preprints.org/manuscript/202503.0500/v1](https://www.preprints.org/manuscript/202503.0500/v1)

This insightful quote paints a picture of wisdom born not from an innate desire for grandeur but from a humble journey of self-awareness and growth. A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. Their usefulness emerges from their centralized experience, mistakes made, lessons learned, and a quiet resolve to move beyond the foolishness of their centralized past. ### Catherine Drapeau — 2025-05-28T19:06:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/catherines-optimal-journal.30462/post-353474>

Hi Dr Jack,

I have been back in Canada for almost 4 weeks now. Here's a short update and some questions for you.

\- Had third Ultra Sound at the beginning of the month. The mass on the cervix is back to the size it was in November 2024. So it got a little smaller since the February's US.

\- The DS that we spoke about in January was 95% gone in El Salvador but as I was getting less sun on the belly, it did come back. I did vomit bile while having diareha with pain (scale of 6-7 t0 10) but it doesn't last as long as prior to ELSalvador. Only 3-4 mns compare to 20-30 minutes.

\- Since our last exchange Dr Jack, my brain cognition is back. I did try to stop the MB but every time I slow down or stop, my brain seems to have these kind of unusual headache/head pressure. It's not the typical headache where you wish to pop an Advil but more like a headache where you want to massage your skull to release pain and pressure. When I touch and message it's tender.

My question to you is; what those headache mean, should I be concern, should I keep taking MB and how much? Dr Makis didn't comment on this when I asked him. I think my brain is better taking it.

\- Since this cognition decline/brain issues started to happen in ELS, I have been feeling a weird sensation in my right foot and up in my right calf. It's kind of a numbness, pressure or cramping sensation. It comes and goes...

My second question to you is: What does this tell you? Should I be concern?

\- My Cnd Oncologist wants to do a PAP Smears test. Should I consider it or should I avoid it?

\- Since I have been back, I have started to follow Dr Makis's protocol. I have yet to get every med he suggested in the #1 part of the protocol (I have send it to you back in April in a private message). I am not sure if it's related but about the same time, I started to feel inflammation and pain in my joints and bones. I don't have all the supplements to take yet so maybe it's related. Dr Makis didn't gave me any reason.

Here's my routine and things I do, please comment or correct me if I am doing something wrong.

\- The house if low EMF/RF and MG. Everything was tested and we don't have BT and WIFI + we are in the bush far from towers (75km)
\- I drink DDW 10 ppm to get a dilution of about 100 ppm
\- I eat a low Deterium Diet (meat, fish, seafood, raw milk, raw butter and some green veggies from my garden/in season)
\- I get up for Sunrise and go ground outside on all 4 for 30-45 mns.
\- I make my high protein/fat breakfast and go back out to eat it outside.
\- I work outside 4 to 8 hours a day bare foot mostly and wearing as little I can.
\- On cloudy day, I use the PBM 3 x 20 mns/day and 10 mns of Vit D lamp.
\- I go to bed between 8:30 pm and 9 pm. Sleep like a log for 8:30 to 9 hours.
\- Cold plunges in tub or lake 3-4 times a week.
\- No screen time before sunrise and after sunset. Actually lest then 30 mns a day of screen time. I have not reading lots of your writing yet. I am waiting for my Daylight.
\- I try to be at ease, be calm, do breath work but it's very difficult. My mind is busy and I worry. I will keep pushing. I enjoy being alone outside and go for walk in the forest.

Thank you for your time Dr Jack.

### Catherine Drapeau — 2025-07-09T21:14:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/catherines-optimal-journal.30462/post-356080>

Updates:

\-In the past few weeks, I had a friend from ELS that contacted me to tell me that the person who lived in the room I have stayed in for almost two months in El Salvador started to develop brain issues similar to what happened to me back in March. I had contacted you Dr Jack regarding this and stopped my Iver and started MB. The roof of that room was leaking and mold was suspected. Back in El Salvador I was not so concerned but with this first email from my friend I started to doubt a mold toxicity.

\- A few days ago, another friend from ELS contacted me to tell me that this person who lived in my room HAD to move out and stay somewhere else in ELS because he got very sick and worse, the other occupant of the house, my old roomate also had to move out because of skin lesions and feeling unwell. This was the wakeup call that made me look into mold toxicity.

\- Observations since possible mold exposure in ELS:
\- Brain issues, brain feels like it's inflamed. Pressure around the forehead and side of the head. It's not like a headache with which you want an aspirin but like a very discomfort head pressure and weird pain.
\- When brain issues started in ELS, I had a sudden growth of gray hair.
\- Weird sensation in right feet that sometimes go up in my leg to the knee. Almost like a numbness. A neurological issue that started in March in ELS.
\- Major hair lost started in May when back home.
\- Skin rashes after eating lobster for the first time in my life in June.
\- Instant Diarrhea after eating fermented raw milk.cream and a few hours after raw milk kefir (started when I got home in May when I started drinking raw milk again).
\- Nausea that started when drinking/ eating raw milk quark, yogurt ect.... One day the brain and nausea was so bad that I didn't care about much and said yes to a small piece of caramel homemade cheesecake. Results, within 10 mins my Nausea and head cleared. We laughed because we thought it was such a big surge of good dopamine after being so strict for so long...anyhow symptoms went away! Any thoughts on that?
\- My most recent blood work showed a good decrease ( since a December blood work) in my Progesterone and Estradiol 17-B even thought, my dosage of HRT hasn't changed.

What I was doing up to today:
\- Morning sunrise and grounding.
\- Hours of naked sunbathing, gardening and sometimes I have to put clothles on because we have company :-( but I keep it minimal.
\- Sunset
\-In Bed between 7-9 pm.
\- DDW
\-Keto diet (with lots of raw milk, shells seafoods, fresh oysters and canned smoked local oysters fish, lamb, beef, pork, moose, bear/all local and normal (organic). Veggies from my garden only. Some macadamias nuts. I am faithful 95% of the time to this diet.
\- 15 mns of screen time a time
\- no EMF in the house and no towers in my village in the bush.
\- Exercise a bit; some Myn biking, swimming, hiking and dirt biking.
\- Cold plunge in lake.

Had an consultation regarding mold with my Naturopath this morning:
\- To her; 99% sure I have a mold toxicity. Perhaps I had it before but if so ELS got it worse or started it.
\- Diarrhea after Kefir milk and cream, rash after lobster and hair lost show that I've developed an Histamine intolerance.
\- neuropathy in the right foot and calf/brain/rashes/ ect... all signs according to her.

What she told me to do:
\- Binder like Zeolite for the moment
\- Organic acid test and another one to find out what type of mold and how bad.
\- Getting home assess and will fix what ever is necessary. Someone is coming next week.
\- Sweating a lot (sauna/hot bath with Epsom salt and magnesium)
\- Low Histamine diet

Dr Jack, what are your recommandations?
\- I will continue Makis's protocol for cervical cancer Iver-Meben + supplements for support . If not ok let me know.
\- Should I stop all seafood? Low histamine diet?
\- Sun sun sun and always of course and sweating.
\- Can mold toxicity be related to cancer?
\- Anything else?

Thank you again Dr Jack. I am searching the forum to read about other peoples that wrote about that. I now have a daylight Computer so I will allow myself more time on computer.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-12T15:01:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/missing-periods-for-10-months.30464/post-348450>

**BLUE LIGHT DESTROYS ALL HEME PROTEINS**

Cytochrome c oxidase is a heme protein that (Complex IV) uses oxygen to produce water, preventing ROS buildup. In Warburg metabolism, suppressed ETC activity leaves oxygen unused, increasing ROS and further damaging heme groups. This lack of water is most critical. Why? The inner mitochondrial membranes contain 30 million volts, and pure DDW is the best insulator on Earth to stop the internal electrocution of the cell. This is why Nature put cytochrome C there. All dysfunction of cytochrome C means internal or endogenous electrical currents run amuck in the cell, causing damage. When this happens, ROS and RNS also increase, and this damages the hypothalamus. nnEMF specifically blue light is how it happens

You need to understand how it affects heme proteins as photoreceptors and see every disease it is linked to. Then you'll see how light leads to every disease known to man.

Heme proteins contain a porphyrin ring with iron (Fe²⁺ or Fe³⁺). They are critical for oxygen transport, electron transfer, and enzymatic reactions—all vulnerable to the oxidative and bioelectric disruptions I’ve outlined for two decades.

The blue light man has made via the MKULTRA experiment, which is now global, will destroy this heme cytochrome by vitamin A liberation. NO = Nitric oxide and heme proteins are also destroyed. Recall that NO controls the behavior of our stem cell depots.

This is why every snake oil salesman now sells lemmings stem cell injections.

Moreover, I hope you remember the lesson I delivered in Patreon: this information carried in NO concentrations is also supplied to the POMC/melanin complex in cells when hemoglobin is in a specific oxidation state.

Melanin has to be well-hydrated to optimize any hormone panel at the brain and gonad level. Right now, nnEMF is causing a TBI in everyone's anterior and posterior pituitary (vasopressin) while causing mtDNA mutations to diminish DDW production at cytochrome c anther heme-based protein.

380 nm light also controls the regenerative state of your hormone panel. This was a tremendous optical arrangement for the survival of mammals 65 million years ago. Still, it is now a big problem when you realize that we are in the Great Oxygen Holocaust due to nnEMF and what dehydration of the heme-containing cytochrome P450scc is designed to do in modern mammals' insides under the power of ALAN, where POMC is boss.

**This enzyme carries out the so-called side chain cleavage reaction, consuming cholesterol to produce pregnenolone, the precursor of cortisol, and all other steroids.** If melanin is not well hydrated, this cleavage fails, and you get pregnenolone steal syndrome. This is why males are losing testosterone at record rates and why females' sex steroid hormones are being demolished too. That is why your period is gone. You have complete failure of this pathway.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-13T14:27:46+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/missing-periods-for-10-months.30464/post-348513>

**Mitochondrial Steroidogenesis For Female Infertility Links To My Decentralized Model**

My photo-bioelectric hypothesis posits that nnEMF and blue light damage melanopsin, mtDNA, and heme proteins, reducing DDW production, dehydrating melanin, and lowering éR, leading to ROS/RNS overproduction and bioelectric dysfunction. The data on steroidogenesis adds:

1.  Mitochondrial Steroidogenesis Pathway:
    -   Mitochondria synthesize steroid hormones from cholesterol via StAR (STARD1 gene) at the outer mitochondrial membrane (OMM), importing cholesterol through TOM/TIMM translocators. VDAC2 stabilizes StAR, delivering cholesterol to CYP450scc at the inner mitochondrial membrane (IMM), which converts cholesterol to pregnanolone (rate-limiting step). Pregnanolone is exported to the endoplasmic reticulum (ER) for progesterone synthesis, with cortisol produced via 11β-hydroxylase (CYP11B1) in the matrix, regulated by rapid, redox-sensitive protein import.
2.  nnEMF/Blue Light Impact:
    -   nnEMF activates VGCCs (voltage-gated calcium channels), increasing Ca²⁺ influx and ROS/RNS, damaging mtDNA and heme proteins (cytochromes, CYP450scc, 11β-hydroxylase). Blue light liberates free retinal, further disrupting photoreceptors and heme function, reducing DDW and éR, impairing StAR stabilization and cholesterol import.
3.  Melanin and Water Dynamics:
    -   Dehydrated melanin (from nnEMF/blue light) lowers éR, disrupting microtubule/microfilament dynamics and protein synthesis for StAR. Low DDW (mtDNA mutations) affects water networks, stalling steroidogenesis.
4.  Connection to Amenorrhea:
    -   Impaired steroidogenesis (low progesterone, cortisol) disrupts the hypothalamic-pituitary-ovarian (HPO) axis, causing amenorrhea and infertility, exacerbated by the patient’s likely nnEMF/blue light exposure (modern lifestyle).
5.  Testable Predictions
    -   Steroid Levels: Low progesterone (<0.5 ng/mL), cortisol (<5 µg/dL) at baseline, increasing with AM sunlight/PBM (progesterone >5 ng/mL, cortisol 5–15 µg/dL).
    -   pH/éR: Low pH (<7.35), low éR (microcurrent <1 µA) at baseline, normalizing (pH 7.35–7.45, éR >1 µA) post-therapy.
    -   StAR/CYP450scc: Reduced StAR mRNA and CYP450scc activity at baseline, improving with PBM/DDW (e.g., mRNA increase >50%).
    -   Redox vs. Detox: Redox-first restores menstruation vs. detox alone (no change) at 12 weeks.
    -   Fertility: Ovulation is detected within 3 months, and pregnancy is within 6–12 months (hCG positive).

However over the last couple of months I have significantly changed my life style. I sleep on a Magnetico mattress. ### Jeanny — 2025-03-12T19:26:35+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-from-the-ground-question.30466/post-348466>

Do the benefits of sunlight bounce back off the earth so that a person could modestly (in a skirt) stand over the lawn and have sunlight benefits to the area "where the sun don't shine?" I'm trying to treat a squamous cell in situ. Thank you.

### Joseph LeBlanc — 2025-03-12T19:50:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-from-the-ground-question.30466/post-348473>

Potentially. My understanding is that green plants reflect IR very well. They also do not actively use UV and so reflect that back as well.

Wouldn't be nearly as effective as direct rays though

### Rusty — 2025-03-13T07:54:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/uv-from-the-ground-question.30466/post-348505>

Gold is a great IR mirror.

Silver/Aluminium is a great UV mirror.

Aluminium reflects with over 90% efficiency from 250 to 450 nm

**Fresh Snow & Ice** reflects **up to 80-90%** of UV. This is why snow blindness is a thing.

You know what to do.

# Thread: Jackson's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacksons-optimal-journal.30468/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 13
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jackson K — 2025-03-12T21:02:28+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacksons-optimal-journal.30468/post-348484>

### **About Me**​

I’m a 6'2", 210 lb, 22-year-old male living in Central Pennsylvania (latitude 41) my whole life. When I was younger, I spent a lot of time outside and felt great—full of energy. However, I remember a shift at some point when I lost energy, though not completely. As a child, I was never anxious; I was a spitfire with tons of energy. But over time, I became more anxious, stressed more easily, and had lower energy levels.

Whenever I got sick, it would take me out for a few days before I recovered. I spent a lot of time on screens, first starting around age 10-11 but becoming significantly more involved at 15, continuing to the present. Played highschool and junior high sports outside with soccer and indoors with basketball. Before my worst symptoms appeared over the past year, I had been working remotely since June of last year and doing remote school since 2022. I was 100% a tech/screen abuser in my younger years on my phone, gaming systems, and computers. Now I am cutting back on phone time, and never watch TV. I only go on screens for work or communication.

* * *

### **History**​

#### **Health Timeline:**​

-   Had a tonsillectomy at age 5-6.
-   Experienced **(BFEP)/floaters** since 6th-8th grade, though I only noticed them when very stressed or anxious. Over the years, I forgot I had them and never noticed them. I only really noticed them on white walls or white screens
-   When very lightheaded or dehydrated I would see very shiny BFEP in my central vision, but then it would go away.
-   Developed **tinnitus** in my right ear sometime between ages 14-16, possibly from a gunshot, but I’m not entirely sure.
-   Underwent **two varicocelectomies**.
-   Varicose veins in right and left leg
-   Had **three migraines** (two with aura and one without), all triggered by stress or excessive screen time.
-   In June, I suffered a **lip laceration** and, despite my reservations, received a tetanus shot. I haven’t taken any other vaccines beyond the ones I was “required” to get as a child.

* * *

### **New Symptoms**​

I’ve always struggled with stress and anxiety, but things worsened significantly last year, especially between June and November after receiving the tetanus shot. I suffered a **panic attack** at a dermatology appointment while getting moles removed. That night, I came down with **a fever and chills** and woke up with my heart racing, experiencing another panic attack.

A week or two later, I had an **ocular migraine**, which severely worsened my visual symptoms and introduced new ones:

-   **Excessive BFEP and floaters** (by far the worst symptom) very intrusive and are all over the place when anxious or stressed.
-   **Visual snow** – a mild graininess, especially in dim or dark lighting.
-   **Afterimages, starbursts, halos, and glare**, especially noticeable at night.
-   **Tinnitus in my left ear**, which changes with movement, chin tucks, biting down, or an underbite.

I recently had a **neuro-ophthalmology evaluation** to investigate my visual symptoms, including floaters, BFEP, glare, and visual snow-like effects. My **intraocular pressure (IOP)** was within the normal range (goal of 16 for both eyes), and all standard eye exam findings were normal, including pupil response, motility, and anterior chamber assessment.

The **slit lamp and fundus exams** showed:
Clear vitreous
Normal optic nerves
Healthy retinal structures with no signs of edema, pallor, or pigmentary retinopathy

The **OCT scan** of the retina and optic nerve showed:
Normal retinal nerve fiber layer (RNFL) thickness
Normal macular structure
Normal cup-to-disc ratios

My neuro-ophthalmologist did not find any obvious **structural abnormalities, optic neuropathy, or signs of increased intracranial pressure** and, therefore, did not recommend an MRI. He essentially diagnosed me with **Visual Snow Syndrome (VSS)**, though not officially. However, most of my symptoms persist daily.
When I asked about **blue light**, he dismissed its effects, stating that it has no impact on the eyes.

* * *

### **Progress**​

-   **Anxiety and stress have improved.**
-   **Grounding more and getting more sun.**
-   **Floaters and BFEP improve when I’m not stressed or anxious but worsen when I am.**
-   **Mitigating nnEMF exposure.**
-   **Eating more DHA**

Since spring/summer is approaching, I am setting up an **outdoor workspace** for remote work and school. I’m also working on improving my finances so I can travel to **El Salvador this winter**.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-12T22:55:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacksons-optimal-journal.30468/post-348488>

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-12T23:18:20+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacksons-optimal-journal.30468/post-348489>

**Dencentralized Medicines Predictions for this Condition**

1. nnEMF/Blue Light Drives VSS via Free Retinal Toxicity

-   Prediction: Excessive nnEMF/blue light from screen use since age 10–11, intensified by remote work/school, caused free retinal toxicity from melanopsin actions, leading to VSS (floaters/BFEP, visual snow, afterimages, starbursts, halos, glare).
-   Mechanism: nnEMF (computers, phones, microwaves) and blue light liberate free retinal (vitamin A), damaging melanopsin, neuropsin, and RPE melanin, as seen in mold patients with VCS deficits. This disrupts visual signal transduction, causing VSS symptoms (e.g., visual snow from neuronal hyperexcitability, floaters/BFEP from photoreceptor stress). Free retinal also damages mitochondrial cytochromes, reducing DDW and éR, while low NO (UVA vasodilator) impairs optic nerve blood flow, exacerbating symptoms under stress/dehydration (e.g., shiny BFEP when lightheaded). The bad news for a 22-year-old is that NO also controls stem cell depots, so regenerating this problem requires good NO production. This brings in Becker's regenerative current story of that one trillionth of an ampere ---> Watch and Listen

-   Outcome: VCS score <6 at 18 cycles/degree, high retinal levels (blood/saliva), low melatonin (<10 pg/mL), and high ROS (8-OHdG), measurable via VCS testing and bloodwork. Your eye doctor has a lot to learn.

2\. Warburg Shift and Pseudohypoxia Cause Energy Loss and Anxiety: Amazing your eye doc does not realize fovea is analog of your case

-   Prediction: The nnEMF/blue light-induced Warburg redox shift, worsened by remote work, drives energy loss, anxiety, and the development of panic attacks likely due to habenular relays to limbic loops via pseudohypoxia and mitochondrial dysfunction there.
-   Mechanism: nnEMF activates VGCCs, increasing Ca²⁺ influx and ROS/RNS (peroxynitrite, ·HO), lowering NAD⁺/NADH and pH (<7.35, membrane potential >-90 mV). Blue light reduces cytochrome c oxidase activity, reducing DDW creation and allowing 30 million volt current to run rampant (pseudohypoxia), shifting metabolism to glycolysis and glutamine addiction. Usually, a healthy human fovea runs this way, but the rest of your eye does not, and it is now running this way, causing all your symptoms from the blue light addiction. The blue light causes free retinal toxicity that damages heme proteins, amplifies mtDNA heteroplasmy in the 11 genes that control the IMM, reduces DDW, dehydrates melanin, and lowers éR = your disease phenotype. This disrupts neuronal bioelectric signaling in eye and central retinal pathways. It will target other things distally in your neurovascular networks if you do not stop the light abuse = causing TBIs in your mesial frontal lobe leading to ADHD, OCD, anxiety/panic (e.g., post-tetanus shot inflammation), while mitochondrial failure reduces ATP (energy loss). Red light reduces blood glucose and offsets blue damage to a degree but does not get you to Becker's regenerative current. You need high-quality solar UVA to do that. Pennsylvania ain't that place.
-   Outcome: Low NAD⁺/NADH (<1), high lactate (>2 mmol/L), low pH (<7.35), and anxiety (e.g., GAD-7 score >10), measurable via metabolomics and clinical scales. It's an idea, but this is too easy a problem to solve with my model.

3\. Tinnitus Reflects Auditory Photoreceptor Damage

-   Prediction: nnEMF and blue light damage auditory photoreceptors (e.g., melanopsin in cochlear tissues), causing tinnitus, worsened by stress and jaw movements. Likely due to heavy earphone use, wired or not, and talking on the phone.
-   Mechanism: nnEMF/blue light disrupts melanopsin in the cochlea, increasing free retinal and ROS/RNS, damaging auditory neurons. Dehydrated melanin lowers éR, amplifying ultraweak biophotons and self-electrocution, leading to tinnitus (right ear since 14–16). It is possible from the noise trauma of gunshot noise but unlikely, given your history (left ear post-tetanus shot). Jaw movements (chin tucks, biting) alter tensor tympani tension, modulating tinnitus perception due to photo-bioelectric dysfunction. CN5 here. So this tells me the problem is already at a critical level since it is at pontine brainstem.
-   Outcome: Tinnitus severity (e.g., THI score >30), low NO (<20 µM), and high ROS, measurable via audiology and blood tests. Peripheral blood smear would likely show a lot of nastiness.

4\. Vascular Issues (Varicose Veins, Migraines) Stem from Low NO and éR

-   Prediction: Varicose veins and migraines (with/without aura) result from nnEMF-induced NO reduction and éR loss at mtDAN level in the affected area, exacerbated by screen time and stress. This is a telling sign because of the age. Means the toxic burden is big time.
-   Mechanism: nnEMF/blue light damage melanopsin and free retinal toxicity reduce NO (UVA vasodilator stem cell depots), impairing vascular tone (varicose veins, varicocelectomies). Melanopsin is also found in the walls of these vessels, so you are blue-irradiating your legs or being hit with surface RF to cause this. Low éR in mtDNA causes dehydrated melanin, which increases stray conductivity that leads to distal and distant damage in other organs, causing photo-bioelectric chaos in cerebral vasculature (pons and trigeminal regions likely) and triggering migraines (stress, screen time). Dura is inverted mostly by CN 5. The posterior fossa gets some from CN 9 and 10. Again, EMF toxicity is the driver. Ocular migraine post-tetanus shot reflects inflammation and pseudohypoxia (Warburg redox shift), worsening VSS.
-   Outcome: Low NO levels, high vascular resistance (Doppler ultrasound), and migraine frequency (e.g., > three episodes/year), measurable via plasma NO, peripheral blood smear, and clinical history.

5. Redox Restoration Reverses ALL Symptoms

-   Prediction: A redox-first protocol (AM sunlight, grounding, DDW, PBM, excessive oral salt use can replace hypertonic saline IV injections, and or methylene blue under MD care) this will reverse the stray voltages from dehydrated melanin sheets in eye and ear = VSS, tinnitus, anxiety, and energy loss, preventing progression can all be remedied.
-   Mechanism: AM sunlight (UV-A) reduces free retinal, regenerates melanopsin, and boosts cytochrome c oxidase for DDW production, raising NAD⁺/NADH and pH (7.35–7.45). Grounding reduces ROS/RNS, DDW enhances mitochondrial efficiency, PBM (600-1500 nm) repairs photoreceptors, hypertonic saline improves conductivity, and methylene blue restores mtDNA/NO. This counters nnEMF/blue light, restores éR, and mitigates the Warburg redox shift in the eye and ear, which hate using this pathways hence causing the brownouts, improving visual, auditory, and neuronal function.
-   Outcome: VCS score >7, THI <20, GAD-7 <5, MoCA >28, and fatigue <10 within 12 weeks, with reduced migraine frequency (<1/year) at 6 months.

Lol, your comment made me laugh because that was exactly the face he gave me when I asked about blue light being a problem. He just said, “There’s nothing you can do about it.” I walked out of there knowing that wasn’t true. I hate how closed-minded centralized doctors are—he wasn’t even curious about why I brought it up.

From your detailed breakdown, it’s clear that my environment—screens, nnEMF, and blue light—is the main issue.

What makes you think this is a pretty simple fix? I’m happy you think so, but I’d love to understand why.

Floaters and BFEP are by far my worst symptoms. Even in dim lighting or on random surfaces, I still see them. It’s almost like I’m seeing the outlines of the trails BFEP makes. And when I’m stressed or anxious, both floaters and BFEP explode everywhere.

I’m hoping to see real improvements by switching my work setup outdoors in the sun and sticking to your protocols.

### Jackson K — 2025-03-22T19:38:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacksons-optimal-journal.30468/post-349133>

Yesterday, I finally got my outdoor computer setup all situated. This was probably the first time I’ve spent this much time in the sun grounded since last year’s vacation—about 4–5 hours total, which was all I could really handle. I got burned pretty badly on my chest, stomach, and arms. My face was a little red, but not too bad. It was my fault because I didn’t get outside early enough, but I was just excited to finally be out in the sun.

I watched Jack’s podcast on Rumble with the Australian doctors, where they talked about using red light to build up your skin’s tolerance for longer sun exposure. It made me wish I’d watched it _before_ going outside for that long—I would’ve used my Firestorm beforehand, lol. Now I know for next time.

One thing I noticed while being outside for a good chunk of the day: time moved a lot slower. Indoors, especially on devices, time feels so fleeting.

**Setup:**

-   Lenovo Legion 5i, i9-14900HX, 4070
-   Wired keyboard and mouse
-   Ethernet
-   IETS GT600 cooler
-   RMAX insulation blacked out
-   f.lux set to 1900K
-   Standing desk

The laptop doesn’t give off any high electric readings except for the charger. The only high reading was the mag, which I expected because of the GPU, but I feel fine using it wired. I was grounded the whole time while using it outside. I’m really happy with how the setup turned out. The temperature was in the high 40s, but the wind made it feel more like the 30s.

My left ring finger turned completely white—basically Raynaud’s—which is another issue to add to the list. It’s only happened twice now, when I’ve been cold or after a cold shower followed by staying in a cold environment for a while. I remember from when I was younger until now, my hands and feet were mostly cold unless I was working out, being active, or when it was hot out. But my finger never turned completely white until recently, and only on that one finger. What helped was going inside and warming my hands with hot water—it went back to normal.

A good idea I had was to take breaks in our greenhouse and sit there for a couple of minutes. It helped with circulation and the cold. It was probably in the 70s or higher in there. This is where I felt the sun the best, and it made me think it’s the perfect place to be in the sun naked, secluded to an extent. Need to get a nice lawn chair. Felt really good at the end of the day working outside, shooting hoops, and running around.

My BFEP and floaters improved a bit while outside—noticeably, but still intrusive. Just feels like my filter for it is gone. Tinnitus is still there but doesn’t really bother me. Other visual symptoms are still present but also less bothersome. Varicose veins and varicocele don't cause any pain. Libido feels low. I need to be in the sun more and implement more of the protocols. I saw Jack write in someone else’s journal that a dead battery needs to be charged, and I related to that—I feel like my battery is drained.

Once the good weather comes to stay, I’d like to be outside all day—working, playing basketball, etc.

My next course of action is to get a DNA test and see what my haplotype is and other details. I also want to include my mom’s and grandmother’s health history in my journal next time. I still plan to go down to El Salvador in the winter for a couple of months and hopefully be able to do my work from there. I feel like I need to get to a point where I can delegate and automate enough so that I’m not tied to screens. Also, I read the melanin blog and was just blown away. Like Jack said—everything fits.

### Jackson K — 2025-04-02T19:34:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacksons-optimal-journal.30468/post-349988>

Dr. Kruse, I took a short course of Accutane in March of 2023. I forget exactly when I stopped, but I wasn’t on it for long.

I understand that vitamin A is essential for the retina, but after reading your work, it seems like context matters more than the compound. Could Accutane have increased my free retinal load while I was already blue light toxic, around nnEMF, and in a low redox state—quietly disrupting the melanopsin–retinol–melatonin axis over time? And then, did inflammatory events like emotional stress, getting sick, nnEMF, or the tetanus vaccine act as triggers, slowly pushing my system past a tipping point?

What’s also interesting is that my younger brother sees BFEP, floaters, and visual snow too. He doesn’t have tinnitus, and none of it really bothers him. That used to be the case for me as well—I never paid attention to it, and it disappeared. This made me think about transgenerational epigenetics, especially since my mom and maternal grandmother both had/have cataracts. Could this mitochondrial or epigenetic inheritance play a role here?

I’m just trying to piece together the puzzle, and would love to hear how you’d interpret this pattern.

### Jackson K — 2025-07-09T04:23:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacksons-optimal-journal.30468/post-356053>

The last couple of months since my last update have felt mostly the same overall, but it depends on the day. The weather has been super nice, and I’ve been trying to be outside as much as possible, even though I still end up inside some days. I will say I’m feeling a lot better than I did in the winter.

I can see how low dopamine makes it hard to stay on track and make good decisions. My focus still isn’t where I want it to be, and I can tell my dopamine is low because it’s hard to work for long periods or do things outside my comfort zone.

Recently, I’ve enjoyed picking blueberries in our garden. I like to ground and be as little clothed as possible while picking. During these moments, I can almost feel my symptoms fade, and everything feels smoother. The symptoms are still there, but much less intense. I also like laying out on a floatie in our pool, soaking up as much sun as possible.

At the same time, it feels like I’m hitting a brick wall. I don’t know how to see real, dramatic improvements in real time, and these symptoms feel tough to tackle on some days.

I don’t understand how I let my health get to this point. Some days, it feels like being on a runaway train with no brakes. From the outside, I look relatively fit and healthy, but on the inside, it must be mitochondrial chaos. I know I’m not alone, since some of my cousins who got the covid vax are also dealing with health issues (like one cousin whose legs go numb and tingly when running).

I’m grateful to be young, flexible, and aware enough to consider heading south. I just turned 23 in June, so I’m thinking I have two years to focus on healing and maximizing my myelin before it fully develops.

Currently, I am wearing blue blockers whenever on screens. My phone screen time is down to about an hour a day, which is a big improvement. My nnEMF environment isn’t the most optimal until I get my own place. On occasion, I add a teaspoon or two of Redmond salt to my water.

On a positive note, I am the tannest I have ever been in years. I used to get made fun of for burning easily and never tanning. This summer, I’ve given my brother (who tans easily) a run for his money—I’m actually a bit tanner than him now. When I was showing my cousin my tan, he even asked me, “How did that happen?” (lol) Looking back, I realized my worst burns happened when I wore sunscreen, sunglasses, and didn’t fully expose my skin. This summer, I’ve worn no sunscreen, no sunglasses, and I’m shirtless outside almost all the time. I didn’t burn—just built a solid tan. I am really surprised at how well I am tanning. I did notice that my face and neck are not tanning as much as my body.

Besides seeing the sunrise, what is something I can implement that would allow me to see improvements more drastically?

# Thread: Thanks for welcoming a noob like me,  Alfred E Newman
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thanks-for-welcoming-a-noob-like-me-alfred-e-newman.30469/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### brianalfredeneuman — 2025-03-13T10:53:58+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thanks-for-welcoming-a-noob-like-me-alfred-e-newman.30469/post-348508>

Like Bitcoin, I dismissed Uncle Jack as another elaborate internet hoax—just one more thing for the tinfoil crowd—until, at age 53, I finally realized I’d been living in the Matrix. Now, here I am on Day 3, stepping outside barefoot before dawn like some hopeful lunatic, praying for a shred of health. I’m a borderline simpleton who wants a clear, no-BS path to detox from this modern poison and stick around long enough to make a difference in my young kids' lives.

A little about me: I started researching the so-called "elite" in college, digging into the economics behind historical manipulation and propaganda. Then I worked in Hollywood and saw the machine up close—let’s say L.A. is a cesspool of soulless degeneracy. At one point, I even became a personal assistant (handmaid, really) to a wealthy princess-type, where I overheard all the things conspiracy theorists rant about—9/11, Princess Di, the Rothschild bankers—yeah, they talked about it like casual dinner gossip. I wanted a closer look, so I joined the Freemasons. What did I learn? The world is darker than most people can stomach, and we’re all walking around with a veil over our eyes.

My father, a plumber who worked in nuclear plants, died young from Alzheimer’s, toxic exposure, and stress. Watching him deteriorate pushed me to seek health early, but all I found were diet fads and supplement shills preying on desperate people. I barely scraped by in organic chemistry, but I can smell bullshit a mile away. The healthiest I’ve ever been? It wasn’t on some overpriced diet plan—it was after a midlife crisis when I spent four years waking up with the sun, fishing from my kayak, and living primarily off what I caught.

Now I sit in the Czech Republic, working security for one of the top cryptocurrency wallets, staring at a lightbox all day, rotting away behind a screen. Last year, I had a swollen lymph node scare—the doctors here couldn’t find cancer, but that was enough to snap me out of my stupor. It’s time to change.

Here’s the plan: Wake up, ground, move somewhere safer in a few years (maybe Central America or the Azores), and actually learn how to take care of myself. But I need answers. What kind of glasses block the right light? What shoes won’t ruin my body? Can I work on a daylight computer? Should I be dosing with iodine? Self-medicating with MB? There’s too much to learn and not enough time, but I refuse to keep sleepwalking through life.

Hit me over the head with your practical tips. Think, "an idiot's guide to Uncle Jack."

### brianalfredeneuman — 2025-03-13T12:19:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thanks-for-welcoming-a-noob-like-me-alfred-e-newman.30469/post-348511>

I forgot one thing. I have suffered for years with varicose vein issues in my legs. I used to wait around in cars for long hours as a servant to the elite. I also don't drink enough water. I have tried to change that. I have had three surgeries by the "top" varicose vein doctor in Boston. I always ask him if there is something to prevent this, and he always says, I'll see you in four years. He has been correct so far. I wear compression stockings. I don't want to see him again.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-13T14:57:57+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thanks-for-welcoming-a-noob-like-me-alfred-e-newman.30469/post-348516>

Alignment with My Photo-Bioelectric Model
My decentralized model posits that nnEMF and blue light damage melanopsin, mtDNA, and heme proteins, reducing DDW (deuterium-depleted water) production, dehydrating melanin, and lowering éR, leading to ROS/RNS overproduction and bioelectric dysfunction. Let’s map your history and current state:

1.  Healthy Period (Midlife Crisis, Outdoor Living):
    -   Four years of waking with the sun and fishing provided solar EMF (UV-A, visible light), grounding, and a natural diet (fish), optimizing melanopsin signaling, mitochondrial function (high DDW, éR), and NO production (UVA vasodilator). This aligns with my model’s emphasis on light-driven redox potential, explaining his peak health.
2.  Current Decline (Czech Republic, Screen Exposure):
    -   Working in security, staring at a lightbox all day, exposes him to nnEMF (computers, Wi-Fi) and blue light at latitude 50°N (low UV-A). This damages melanopsin, liberates free retinal, and disrupts heme proteins (cytochromes, catalase), reducing DDW and éR, driving a Warburg redox shift (glutamine addiction, pseudohypoxia low NAD+).
3.  Varicose Veins and Surgeries:
    -   Long hours in cars (sedentary, nnEMF exposure) and dehydration reduce NO and éR, impairing vascular tone (varicose veins). Three surgeries by a “top” doctor in Boston, with no preventive advice beyond compression stockings, reflect centralized medicine’s symptom-focused approach, missing my model’s photo-bioelectric insights.
4.  Swollen Lymph Node Scare:
    -   The lymph node issue (cancer ruled out) suggests systemic inflammation, likely from nnEMF-induced ROS/RNS, low alpha-MSH (potential mold exposure), and dehydration, impairing lymphatic drainage.
5.  Family History (Father’s Alzheimer’s):

### brianalfredeneuman — 2025-04-17T13:45:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thanks-for-welcoming-a-noob-like-me-alfred-e-newman.30469/post-350823>

After about two solid months of sunrise and sunset, I can say I do feel healthier. I have started to do the cold shower thing but it's just too much for me. I live in an apartment with no tub, and pond/lakes aren't available in the Czech Republic. Maybe they sell temporary baths, I have to look into it more. I have been eating mostly carnivorous for two years. I didn't do anything major to change my indoor life, I have the blue blocker and we light candles if we're up later than sunset. Mostly, I've been keeping the natural cycle.

I'm looking forward to BTC Prague to hear Dr Kruse in real life for the first time. I have never had an interview on my YouTube channel, which was focused on my life as an expat and BTC, but I have mostly taken them all down. I wondered if I could do an interview with Dr. Kruse and maybe have it translated afterward for Czech speakers? I'm unsure if I'd have to pay for that or how to do it.

I feel like anything that would open the eyes of people here would be great. I know many Czech friends who are already on board to change the unhealthy effects of blue light.

Unfortunately, I was very late in BTC and was a victim of a major hack that led me to get into what I do now, security. Every day, I deal with cybercrime victims, and it's really hard on my soul. I have to stare at a computer. I empathize with anyone (like myself) who lost their entire stack.
In a similar way to how Dr Kruse uncovered, or I should say unravelled, the story of MK/ JFK/ vaccines... I have led a similar unfolding of events around 9/11 and some other things, I mentioned. It is inspiring to see Dr. Kruse turn that knowledge into positive change. I have felt so angry inside for so long and did not know what to do with it. I have decided the interview thing is the way to go because I want a better world. I have two young kids, and I never want them to feel helpless against the monsters that have killed us.

The time to activate my strength is here now. Thanks to the cold and the sun. And special thanks to the guy who figured it out. Even though I grew up on the Cape, I especially love that he's got the NYC accent, or Queens, whatever. NYC was my favorite place to live, and I know it's horrible now, but it's the heart of my life. It makes me so fucking happy to listen to him.

### Horejsi00 — 2025-03-13T18:00:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-grant.30470/post-348526>

Heard Dr. Kruse say he provides direct feedback to your Optimal Journal on the PowWow.  

But if this is too much, please let me know the best way to schedule a consultation.

**OPTIMAL JOURNAL:** 
\-Maternal Haplogroup is T2b with mother, grandmother and great-grandmother all being born in Nebraska.
\-40yr Old Male
\-Born in Lincoln, NE lived until 32
\-Moved to Austin, TX in 2017
\-Work from home

**Jan. 1st 2013-**
Brain fog, disassociation, some dizziness, etc. but centralized and holistic doctors told me bloodwork is perfect so I tried various supplements and diets but nothing seemed to work. Daily drinker with excessive intake regularly.

**Jan. 1st 2019-**
Brute forced “in shape” down from 260lbs to 195lbs (weight has always fluctuated a lot) and had one of my worst spells in memory, so I effectively gave up on trying to feel well.  

**Jan. 1st 2020-**
Independently determined a histamine intolerance as the worst spells were after wine/beer or cured meats. Switched to liquor only and experimented with various healthy diets (Whole30, Low Histamine, Etc.). Started buying Bitcoin.

**April 1st 2021-Dec 1st 2021-**
Covid Jabs Moderna 2 shots and 1 Moderna booster (I know poor choice).

**March 1st 2024-**
Found you through Kyle Kingsburry podcast, signed up for Membership and began consuming personal and recommended material. Began living the blackswan lifestyle (sunrises, grounding, etc.) not perfect but in the right direction.

**July 1st 2024-**
Totaled car, not at fault, with broken thumb with no surgery needed but it knocked me off most positive behaviors. Work from desk, dealing with insurance, rehab, Dr. visits, etc.

First time ‘bad bloodwork’ across a handful of markers from a centralized standpoint:
\-High CRP, T3 Uptake, Apolipoprotein B, Triglycerides, Cholesterol
\-Low Testosterone

**September 10th 2024-**
Pulmonary embolism diagnosed as unprovoked but multiple doctors involved said likely due to Covid or Covid Vaccine. Placed on Eliquis blood thinner

**November 11th 2024-** 
Diagnosed with Sleep Apnea HST with moderate OSA (AHI 23) hold off on machine as I am traveling to New Zealand for a month deciding to hold off use until I return for CPAP.

**Dec. 13th 2024-**
Had an echo cardiogram was told all damage healed and minimal damage was healed cleared for travel:
• Left Ventricle: Normal cavity size. Mild concentric hypertrophy. The EF
is 60%. Systolic function is normal.
• Left Atrium: Cavity is mildly dilated. Volume index is moderately
increased.
• Right Ventricle: Systolic function is normal.
• Aortic Valve: Normal trileaflet valve. No regurgitation.
• Mitral Valve: Mitral valve structure is normal. Mild regurgitation.
• Tricuspid Valve: Tricuspid valve structure is normal. Mild
regurgitation.
• PA Pressure: No pulmonary hypertension.

Followed your travel protocol on trip but mentioned some tightness but they doubted blockage so just prescribed blood pressure medicine (Amlodipine Besylate 5mg) which I was told to take as needed.

**February 13th 2025-** 
Received CPAP and began nightly use.

**March 1st 2025-**
Felt spasms around heart and started taking blood pressure regularly when I mentioned this they moved to (Amlodipine Besylate 10mg). Started taking regular BP, averaging 144/96 with HR avg. 88. Before PE I was always around 130/80.  

**March 10th 2025-**
Consumed the last 3 PowWow’s.

Sleep Apnea was discussed and the CPAP is actually hurting me.

Covid or Covid Vaccine could have caused my PE.

And Low Redox is only exasperating past/current issues.

**Current State:**
Seeing how I can revert back to decentralized and begin to heal.

I am doing the basics (sunrise/set, hitting Dminder goals, grounding, begun eating Epi-Paleo again, etc) but curious with my unique experience what more needs to be done.

Please let me know if I did not do this correctly or what other information may be needed. Thanks!

* * *

Dehydrated melanin lowers éR, increasing conductivity and self-electrocution, driving brain fog and cardiovascular strain (mild left atrial dilation, heart spasms).

2\. Warburg Shift due Low Redox State will Exacerbate Sleep Apnea and PE: Great Oxygen Allergy

-   Prediction: nnEMF-induced Warburg redox shift, worsened by Covid/vaccine, lowers NAD⁺/NADH and pH, contributing to sleep apnea, PE, and low testosterone.
-   Mechanism: nnEMF/blue light reduces cytochrome c oxidase activity (pseudohypoxia), lowering NAD⁺/NADH and pH (<7.35), shifting metabolism to glycolysis and glutamine addiction. mtDNA heteroplasmy (T2b predisposition) amplifies this, reducing DDW and éR. Covid/vaccine spike protein increases inflammation, impairing NO and endothelial function, leading to PE and OSA (AHI 23). CPAP’s nnEMF worsens éR, disrupting sleep further. Oxygen will make all these things worse due to damage in IMM.
-   Outcome: Low NAD⁺/NADH (<1), high lactate (>2 mmol/L), low pH (<7.35), and low melatonin (<10 pg/mL), measurable via metabolomics and saliva tests. You can do them, but they are all a waste of money because the problem is obvious.

3\. Histamine Intolerance Reflects Gut and Redox Dysregulation

-   Prediction: Histamine intolerance stems from a serious lack of sun combined with toxicity from nnEMF-induced low alpha-MSH and gut dysbiosis, exacerbated by alcohol and Covid/vaccine inflammation.

Alcohol (2013–2020) and Covid/vaccine inflammation disrupt gut microbiota, increasing histamine production (wine, beer, cured meats). Dehydration (historical alcohol use) lowers pH, worsening symptoms.
-   Outcome: High histamine (plasma >1 ng/mL), low alpha-MSH (5 ng/L), and high TGF-beta1 (>5000 pg/mL), measurable via blood tests (all a waste)

4\. AM Solar Exposure and Grounding Reverse Symptoms

-   Prediction: AM solar exposure (30–120 minutes daily) and grounding (3 hour daily) restore NO, éR, and testosterone, improving brain fog, sleep apnea, and cardiovascular health.
-   Mechanism: AM sunlight (UV-A, latitude 30°N) regenerates melanopsin, reduces free retinal, and boosts cytochrome c oxidase for DDW production, raising NAD⁺/NADH and pH (7.35–7.45). This increases NO, improves endothelial function (PE, hypertension), and enhances steroidogenesis (testosterone). Grounding donates electrons, reducing ROS/RNS and stabilizing éR, mitigating sleep apnea and brain fog.

5. Comprehensive Redox Protocol Prevents Progression

-   Prediction: A redox-first protocol (AM sunlight, grounding, DDW, PBM, hypertonic saline, methylene blue) reverses symptoms, prevents further PEs, and supports long-term health. Daylight computer and 500 BPI tinted blue blockers a must.

**A Hypothetical Decentralized Healing Approach**

Phase 1: Redox Restoration (Weeks 1–4, March 2025)

-   Stop CPAP immediately: CPAP’s nnEMF (electronics) worsens éR and sleep quality. Focus on natural sleep apnea reversal (below).
-   AM Sunlight Exposure: 20–30 minutes daily (7–8 a.m., latitude 30°N), direct skin/eye exposure (no sunglasses, UV-A-permissive glasses if needed). Regenerates melanopsin, boosts NO, and restores DDW/éR.
-   Grounding: 3 hours daily, barefoot on grass/soil (Austin’s climate allows year-round). Use a grounding mat if indoors (connected to a grounded outlet). Reduces ROS/RNS and stabilizes éR. **Austin is a terrible place to do grounding due to stray currents.**
-   DDW: Drink 1–2 liters daily (<100 ppm deuterium, e.g., Qlarivia). Hydrates mitochondria, raises pH, and supports steroidogenesis (testosterone).
-   Photobiomodulation (PBM): 600-1000nm or you can find 810 nm, 5 J/cm² daily on chest (heart), pelvis (testosterone), and scalp (brain fog). Stimulates mitochondrial repair and NO production.
-   Hypertonic Saline: 3% NaCl, 250 mL IV weekly (under medical supervision, monitor sodium/pH). Improves perfusion for cardiovascular health and PE recovery.
-   Methylene Blue (Mb): 0.5 mg/kg orally daily (pharmaceutical grade, e.g., compounded, 10 mg/day). Boosts mtDNA repair, NO, and éR. Avoid MB use with Amlodipine if possible (monitor BP closely with doctor).
-   Hydration: Drink 3–4 liters water daily (mix with DDW), add a pinch of sea salt (electrolytes). Counteracts nnEMF dehydration, lowers NaCl concentration, and raises pH.
-   Screen Mitigation:
    -   Use blue light blockers (e.g., luciaeyes.com or TrueDark Daylights) during work.
    -   Limit screen time to <4 hours/day, and take 5-minute grounding breaks hourly.
    -   Move screens >3 feet away (inverse square law reduces nnEMF intensity). **_\----> Daylight computer smart mover_**
-   Sleep Apnea Reversal:
    -   Sleep on your side (reduces airway collapse), and elevate your head 30 degrees.
    -   Practice diaphragmatic breathing (5 minutes 3x/day) to improve oxygenation.
    -   Avoid alcohol and sedatives (e.g., Amlodipine at night if possible: MD assist).

Phase 2: Redox Maintenance and Cardiovascular Support (Weeks 5–12, April–June 2025)

-   Continue sunlight, grounding, DDW, PBM (increase to 10 J/cm² if tolerated), reduce saline/Mb to biweekly.
-   Wean Off Amlodipine: As BP improves with sunlight/grounding (target 130/80 mmHg), reduce Amlodipine to 5 mg, then discontinue (under medical supervision). Sunlight’s NO boost replaces its effect.
-   Eliquis Continuation: Continue Eliquis (per doctor’s guidance, typically 3–6 months post-PE), but monitor D-dimer (target <500 ng/mL) to confirm no clotting risk before stopping.
-   Testosterone Support: Increase cholesterol-rich foods (e.g., eggs, avocados) to support StAR and CYP450scc for steroidogenesis. Avoid processed carbs (fuels Warburg shift).
-   Histamine Management: More sunlight and continue low-histamine diet (e.g., avoid wine, beer, cured meats), add DAO enzyme supplement (e.g., Seeking Health Histamine Block, 1 capsule before meals) if needed. Not for me.
-   Monitor Markers: Weekly BP (target 130/80 mmHg), testosterone (target >500 ng/dL), CRP (target <1 mg/L), and AHI (target <10 via home sleep test).

Phase 3: Long-Term Prevention (Months 4–12, July 2025–March 2026)

-   Sustain sunlight, grounding, DDW, PBM biweekly, saline/Mb monthly.
-   Cardiovascular Health:
    -   Calf exercises (20 raises 3x/day) and leg elevation (15 minutes daily) to improve venous return.
    -   Monitor echocardiogram (target: normal left atrium volume, no regurgitation progression).
-   Sleep Apnea: Retest AHI (target <10 without CPAP), continue side sleeping and breathing exercises.
-   Mold/Environmental Toxins: Test for mold because you are at risk (urine mycotoxins, alpha-MSH, TGF-beta1); if positive, detox with itraconazole (200 mg daily) after alpha-MSH >40 ng/L.
-   Neuroprotection: Monitor cognitive function (MoCA, target >28) and NAD⁺/NADH (>2) to prevent neurodegenerative progression (e.g., Alzheimer’s, linked to Covid/vaccine).
-   Travel Protocol: For future travel (e.g., post-New Zealand), follow your protocol: hydrate (4 liters/day), ground at destination, wear compression socks, and take methylene blue (10 mg/day) to prevent clotting.

Practical Tips: An Idiot’s Guide to Decentralized Medicine

-   Morning Routine: Wake with sunrise (6:30–7 a.m.), step outside barefoot, face the sun, and drink 1 liter DDW with a pinch of sea salt. Ground for 1 hour (walk in a park).
-   Work Setup: Use blue light blockers, keep screens >3 feet away, and ground during breaks (touch grass or use a grounding mat). Limit screen time to <4 hours/day.
-   Hydration Hack: Keep a 1-liter bottle with you, sip every hour, aim for 3–4 liters/day. Add lemon & salt for taste, not sugar.
-   Methylene Blue: 10 mg/day (oral, dissolve in water), increase to 20 mg if tolerated. Buy from a compounding pharmacy (e.g., 1% solution).
-   Diet: Eat eggs, avocados, and fish (omega-3s for brain/heart). Avoid processed carbs, alcohol (stick to occasional liquor if needed).
-   Sleep: Sleep by 10 p.m., wake by 6 a.m., side sleep, elevate head 30 degrees. Use red-tinted glasses (e.g., TrueDark Twilights) post-sunset.
-   Monitor BP: Check BP daily (morning/evening), target 130/80 mmHg. If >140/90, ground longer (2 hours) and recheck.

Dehydration (historical alcohol use) lowers pH, worsening symptoms.

>

This increases NO, improves endothelial function (PE, hypertension), and enhances steroidogenesis (testosterone). Grounding donates electrons, reducing ROS/RNS and stabilizing éR, mitigating sleep apnea and brain fog.

>
> 5. Comprehensive Redox Protocol Prevents Progression
>
> -   Prediction: A redox-first protocol (AM sunlight, grounding, DDW, PBM, hypertonic saline, methylene blue) reverses symptoms, prevents further PEs, and supports long-term health. Daylight computer and 500 BPI tinted blue blockers a must.
> -   Mechanism: AM sunlight, PBM, and grounding restore mitochondrial function, DDW enhances water networks, hypertonic saline improves perfusion, and methylene blue repairs mtDNA/NO. This counters nnEMF/Covid effects, restores éR, and reduces inflammation, preventing recurrent PEs and cardiovascular progression (e.g., heart spasms). **_Sunlight best inside tropics._**
> -   Outcome: Normal CRP (<1 mg/L), AHI <10 (without CPAP), testosterone >500 ng/dL, and BP 130/80 mmHg at 6 months, with no recurrent PEs (D-dimer <500 ng/mL).
>
>
> **A Hypothetical Decentralized Healing Approach**
>
> Phase 1: Redox Restoration (Weeks 1–4, March 2025)
>
> -   Stop CPAP immediately: CPAP’s nnEMF (electronics) worsens éR and sleep quality. Focus on natural sleep apnea reversal (below).
> -   AM Sunlight Exposure: 20–30 minutes daily (7–8 a.m., latitude 30°N), direct skin/eye exposure (no sunglasses, UV-A-permissive glasses if needed). Regenerates melanopsin, boosts NO, and restores DDW/éR.
> -   Grounding: 3 hours daily, barefoot on grass/soil (Austin’s climate allows year-round). Use a grounding mat if indoors (connected to a grounded outlet). Reduces ROS/RNS and stabilizes éR. **Austin is a terrible place to do grounding due to stray currents.**
> -   DDW: Drink 1–2 liters daily (<100 ppm deuterium, e.g., Qlarivia). Hydrates mitochondria, raises pH, and supports steroidogenesis (testosterone).
> -   Photobiomodulation (PBM): 600-1000nm or you can find 810 nm, 5 J/cm² daily on chest (heart), pelvis (testosterone), and scalp (brain fog). Stimulates mitochondrial repair and NO production.
> -   Hypertonic Saline: 3% NaCl, 250 mL IV weekly (under medical supervision, monitor sodium/pH). Improves perfusion for cardiovascular health and PE recovery.
> -   Methylene Blue (Mb): 0.5 mg/kg orally daily (pharmaceutical grade, e.g., compounded, 10 mg/day). Boosts mtDNA repair, NO, and éR. Avoid MB use with Amlodipine if possible (monitor BP closely with doctor).
> -   Hydration: Drink 3–4 liters water daily (mix with DDW), add a pinch of sea salt (electrolytes). Counteracts nnEMF dehydration, lowers NaCl concentration, and raises pH.
> -   Screen Mitigation:
>     -   Use blue light blockers (e.g., luciaeyes.com or TrueDark Daylights) during work.
>     -   Limit screen time to <4 hours/day, and take 5-minute grounding breaks hourly.
>     -   Move screens >3 feet away (inverse square law reduces nnEMF intensity). **_\----> Daylight computer smart mover_**
> -   Sleep Apnea Reversal:
>     -   Sleep on your side (reduces airway collapse), and elevate your head 30 degrees.
>     -   Practice diaphragmatic breathing (5 minutes 3x/day) to improve oxygenation.
>     -   Avoid alcohol and sedatives (e.g., Amlodipine at night if possible: MD assist).
>
> Phase 2: Redox Maintenance and Cardiovascular Support (Weeks 5–12, April–June 2025)
>
> -   Continue sunlight, grounding, DDW, PBM (increase to 10 J/cm² if tolerated), reduce saline/Mb to biweekly.
> -   Wean Off Amlodipine: As BP improves with sunlight/grounding (target 130/80 mmHg), reduce Amlodipine to 5 mg, then discontinue (under medical supervision). Sunlight’s NO boost replaces its effect.
> -   Eliquis Continuation: Continue Eliquis (per doctor’s guidance, typically 3–6 months post-PE), but monitor D-dimer (target <500 ng/mL) to confirm no clotting risk before stopping.
> -   Testosterone Support: Increase cholesterol-rich foods (e.g., eggs, avocados) to support StAR and CYP450scc for steroidogenesis. Avoid processed carbs (fuels Warburg shift).
> -   Histamine Management: More sunlight and continue low-histamine diet (e.g., avoid wine, beer, cured meats), add DAO enzyme supplement (e.g., Seeking Health Histamine Block, 1 capsule before meals) if needed. Not for me.
> -   Monitor Markers: Weekly BP (target 130/80 mmHg), testosterone (target >500 ng/dL), CRP (target <1 mg/L), and AHI (target <10 via home sleep test).
>
> Phase 3: Long-Term Prevention (Months 4–12, July 2025–March 2026)
>
> -   Sustain sunlight, grounding, DDW, PBM biweekly, saline/Mb monthly.
> -   Cardiovascular Health:
>     -   Calf exercises (20 raises 3x/day) and leg elevation (15 minutes daily) to improve venous return.
>     -   Monitor echocardiogram (target: normal left atrium volume, no regurgitation progression).
> -   Sleep Apnea: Retest AHI (target <10 without CPAP), continue side sleeping and breathing exercises.
> -   Mold/Environmental Toxins: Test for mold because you are at risk (urine mycotoxins, alpha-MSH, TGF-beta1); if positive, detox with itraconazole (200 mg daily) after alpha-MSH >40 ng/L.
> -   Neuroprotection: Monitor cognitive function (MoCA, target >28) and NAD⁺/NADH (>2) to prevent neurodegenerative progression (e.g., Alzheimer’s, linked to Covid/vaccine).
> -   Travel Protocol: For future travel (e.g., post-New Zealand), follow your protocol: hydrate (4 liters/day), ground at destination, wear compression socks, and take methylene blue (10 mg/day) to prevent clotting.
>
> Practical Tips: An Idiot’s Guide to Decentralized Medicine
>
> -   Morning Routine: Wake with sunrise (6:30–7 a.m.), step outside barefoot, face the sun, and drink 1 liter DDW with a pinch of sea salt. Ground for 1 hour (walk in a park).
> -   Work Setup: Use blue light blockers, keep screens >3 feet away, and ground during breaks (touch grass or use a grounding mat). Limit screen time to <4 hours/day.
> -   Hydration Hack: Keep a 1-liter bottle with you, sip every hour, aim for 3–4 liters/day. Add lemon & salt for taste, not sugar.
> -   Methylene Blue: 10 mg/day (oral, dissolve in water), increase to 20 mg if tolerated. Buy from a compounding pharmacy (e.g., 1% solution).
> -   Diet: Eat eggs, avocados, and fish (omega-3s for brain/heart). Avoid processed carbs, alcohol (stick to occasional liquor if needed).
> -   Sleep: Sleep by 10 p.m., wake by 6 a.m., side sleep, elevate head 30 degrees. Use red-tinted glasses (e.g., TrueDark Twilights) post-sunset.
> -   Monitor BP: Check BP daily (morning/evening), target 130/80 mmHg. If >140/90, ground longer (2 hours) and recheck.
>
>
>
> * * *

Appreciative of such detail!

After visiting chiropractor last week, he suggested seeing a GP after noticing what sounded like a heart valve regurgitation through the stethoscope. Of course can't go directly to a cardiologist without a GP recommendation (centralized BS). Not sure what, if any, cardio tests the GP can/will run, but was luckily able to get into one next week.

This morning was the first day I've seemed to notice/feel something abnormal/irregular happening in the heart area. I'm sure I've noticed it before, but didn't have any indication it could actually be a leaky valve.

I'm also an office/screen worker as a video editor, but will do better wearing the red lenses during work when inside. Will load up the file cabinet with DHA-rich fish. Continue sunrise viewing while grounded. Order some DDW. I like the PBM suggestion directly on heart, would just have to vet/fird right machine.

Ohio-based my whole life. Looking forward to getting some tropical sun for a week in September.

Thank you again, Jack, for all you give of yourself and share for us to become decentralized savages.

# Thread: Hello from the SF Bay Area
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-sf-bay-area.30482/>

### AlbertW — 2025-03-14T23:34:15+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-sf-bay-area.30482/post-348609>

I live in Palo Alto, CA and work remotely as a software engineer.

I haven't had serious health issues (that I know of), but I do have moderate myopia and sleep apnea and have experienced chronic fatigue for 10-20+ years now. My kids also have some chronic health issues, so I'm trying to research health in my spare time.

A few years ago, I started optimizing via diet and exercise, but it wasn't until recently that I learned about nnEMFs and sunlight from Jack and others on X. I really subscribe to Jack's theories, though I have to say that his delivery is often a bit harsh. I totally subscribe to the theories that "nature knows best" and people evolved for real reasons. Everything in life (e.g. technology) is a tradeoff and there are rarely free lunches. I must say that I feel like an outsider in the tech community with my beliefs.

I've been working from my backyard using an rCLD monitor all day recently, trying to maximize UV + IR and hopefully improve my myopia this way.

I'm also looking to move to a new town/city in the US and build a custom EMF fortress house. Any recommendations? Unfortunately, I have a lot of requirements:
\- love California weather (I know I'll need to compromise here). not too hot. not too cold. hate humidity (mold?)
\- avoid states with vaccine mandates for school
\- don't want to live on flat land in the middle of nowhere with no connection to society. should be at least within 20-45 minutes of society
\- have a decently good public or private school nearby in case I don't have time to homeschool

I have to stay in the US for work. Any suggestions?

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-04-04T23:09:45+0300

The SF bay area is covered in 5G Stanford is covered in 5G too

### AlbertW — 2025-04-05T00:25:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-sf-bay-area.30482/post-350115>

I had a Building Biologist come out with the 200Mhz-8Ghz and 20-40 GHz meters and didn't seem to pick up too much RF nearby (likely 4G only, though need to verify). I heard a fixed-high-pitched sound indicating cell, but the RF was in the 50-250 uW/m2 range, which isn't ideal but also isn't sky-high. In some rooms in my house, with WiFi off, I can achieve 0-10 uW/m2, which seems decent for sleeping.

Yesterday was our first day of having the entire family sleeping in the basement with those breakers off. Body voltage 5-20 mV range (grounding via outlet ground). RF 0-10 uW/m2, < 0.2 mG. Seems as close to perfect as you can get for Bay Area, I'd think, right? The 2 toddlers still kept waking up at night, so no immediate changes. I'm hoping that this is just a healing process and they won't recover immediately.

We also started giving the toddlers DDW and more salt. More grounding, getting sun outside.

Considering buying a Magnetico. Would it help in my situation?

One concern I have is that body voltage (compared to outlet ground) is 400-800 mV just walking around my first floor (even when I'm not close to walls). I'll need to do a test to turn off all breakers to the house, but I wonder if I'm picking up electric fields from wiring for ceiling lights in the basement. I also heard that DE radiates a lot farther from walls than the 60 Hz signal. I saw 200-800 mV readings with a Greenwave EMI meter. My neighbor has solar, so maybe it's from that? Maybe I need to do a 10-minute consult with Anthony.

I don't live near downtown PA nor Stanford fortunately, to avoid the bulk of the 5G.

Nevertheless, I am planning to spend a month or two soon in ES at Jack's recommendation to see if my son's eczema, irritability, and anaphylactic reactions can clear up.

### SunHunter ☀️ — 2025-04-05T00:57:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-from-the-sf-bay-area.30482/post-350117>

I think you need to realize that this area is not a good place for your toddlers. My house here in El Salvador has RF below 1uW/m2 in all rooms, 0.0 mG magnetic fields. I started having dreams again. If you visit with your family you will feel the difference. Looking forward to welcome you here.

# Thread: Sunrise isn’t novel
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunrise-isn%E2%80%99t-novel.30485/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jessnet3 — 2025-03-15T14:45:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sunrise-isn%E2%80%99t-novel.30485/post-348644>

I chose this book as part of early American history for my daughter who is in fifth grade. Because we’ve been greeting the sunrise for just a year, she was under the impression that the concept is novel. She approached me in surprise yesterday after she read that Native Americans had this ritual as part of their every day, too. It offered an opportunity to discuss modern humanity’s disconnect to the earth and how our lack of reverence for Mother Nature has abundant consequences for human health.

What are your children learning in school? Are they teaching them to be more connected to nature or are they the wall that keeps them from it?

### Daulatwant — 2025-03-15T18:51:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/113ppm-ddw-from-snow.30486/post-348667>

I collected some snow this year. It was quite polluted from the air. I tossed a bunch of crushed charcoal into the snow water, left it to steep for like a week. Then filtered out the charcoal. The water felt great after the charcoal treatment.

Activated charcoal has worked good when all I had was tap water. I am not a fan of filtration, but activated charcoal is an intriguing substance.

I feel blessed any time it rains or snows. And try to make the best use of what's right there in front of me. If there was a particularly heavy snowfall, I'd fill the bathtub with it. The longer the snow stays on the ground the more pollution from the air will end up on it.

### Daulatwant — 2025-03-19T17:05:40+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/113ppm-ddw-from-snow.30486/post-348897>

Rain or snow can easily be one of the purest local sources of water for so many people.

In terms of rain. I would worry more about how the water is harvested and stored. A typical rain barrel will have all kinds of debris and crap settle in the tank overtime both from the rooftop/gutters and from the air. If the rain is captured on a clean catchment surface and stored in a clean closed container, it's an entirely different story.

Perhaps rain and snow would end up being much easier to filter than other local sources.

Where I live well water has tons of iron, and the water treatment system is large and expensive and still leaves quite a bit of iron in the water.

# Thread: WHY YOU MUST SAY NO THANKS TO SUPPLEMENTS/DRUGS/PROCESSED FOODS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-you-must-say-no-thanks-to-supplements-drugs-processed-foods.30487/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 53
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-15T19:10:10+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-you-must-say-no-thanks-to-supplements-drugs-processed-foods.30487/post-348676>

**WHY YOU MUST SAY NO THANKS TO SUPPLEMENTS/DRUGS/PROCESSED FOODS**

If you ran a semiconductor factory that was alive, as Robert O.Becker showed in his book amphibians, reptiles and mammals are.......would you add atoms and elements during your biological photolithography step?

Why does AMD and Intel build their semiconductors in clean rooms but people here continually shit the bed and think adding exogenous atoms that have no place in their semiconductuve phase makes any sense?

You know this book lays it out?

The semiconduction story right that occurs in each one of you right?

What happens when you add dopants to a semiconductor that is not supposed to be in the recipe? Anyone know?

When you add dopants to a semiconductor that aren’t part of the intended recipe, you’re essentially altering its electrical properties in ways that might not align with its original design. Dopants are impurities added deliberately to tune a semiconductor’s conductivity—n-type dopants (like phosphorus) donate extra electrons, while p-type dopants (like boron) create "holes" by accepting electrons.

If you introduce an unplanned dopant (supplement/peptide/drug/jab), a few things can happen depending on what you add, how much, and the base material (say, silicon or gallium arsenide).

First, the carrier concentration changes. If you accidentally dope an intrinsic (pure) semiconductor with an n-type dopant when it wasn’t meant to be, you’ll flood it with free electrons, making it conductive in a way it wasn’t supposed to be. If it’s already doped (say, p-type) and you add an opposite-type dopant (n-type), you could compensate the existing carriers—holes and electrons cancel each other out—reducing conductivity or even flipping the material’s type entirely if the new dopant overpowers the old one.

Second, the bandgap and electronic structure can shift. Dopants don’t just add carriers; they can mess with the lattice and energy levels. An unintended heavy dopant (like a rare earth element in silicon) might introduce deep-level traps—states in the bandgap that snag carriers and kill efficiency in devices like transistors or LEDs.

**_Third, crystal defects can pile up like mtDNA mutations do. Semiconductors are finicky; their performance hinges on a pristine lattice._**

Random dopants, especially at high concentrations or if they don’t fit well (wrong atomic size), can strain the crystal, creating dislocations or clusters. This tanks carrier mobility and reliability—think slower transistors or leaky diodes. Details matter in quantum biology and the marketers know you are all dumbasses.

What does the world of physics tell us about semiconductor contamination?

Real-world example: in silicon solar cells, iron contamination (an unintentional "dopant" from processing) acts as a recombination center, slashing efficiency by trapping electrons before they contribute to current.

Another case: if you’re making a p-n junction and sneak in an n-type dopant on the p-side, you’ll blur the junction, wrecking its ability to separate charge.

If you cannot charge them even going into the sun does not matter...........GOT IT? It is a big freaking deal.
