no i do not know with precision whats in the vitamin unless its looked at closely. but let me ask you this i dont know whats in the food i eat either with precision. and i dont think any of us can say that about food either.
theres some foods they put crap on for all kinds of reasons like in the fruit section i saw them spraying something on fresh organic produce to keep them looking good or whatever

i dont think anyone looks at what they ingest"with precision" unless they choose to and that rarely happens. we mainly go by the ingredient list on the item.

are you saying that natural foods you eat are not that important? or are you saying all any food you eat are not that important? because as said above theres some foods they put the same crap as they do in those supplements and more in them. so supplements are in a way like if anything crappier food.body takes the small good form them. and even with crappier food what if theres a small good side to them like the nugget guy story?

ive seen so many foods that have (added vitamin d) enriched foods etc im sure youve seen this everywhere when it says enriched" that means they are putting in the supplement in there also. so what the difference of taking the supplements separate then with that food? they put supplements in milk orange juice its really in almost everything. and even the organic stores have this. so when you say the food is not that important that "food\* you mention has those supplements in them right there no?

on the one hand you say food is not that important and on the other hand you say the supplements are important but in a bad way. but none of us can deny those supplements are already in the food you say is not.

so still trying to figure this one out.

from your readings i gathered that the main idea of ingesting foods etc was, food is not that important because the body handles the rest. now i did read and understand the difference you mention of supplements not having the hydration factor (water) but what if you drank extra water with the supplement or ate with them. isnt that why most supplements say"take these vitamins with food" the food probably has that water

when you said that guy winning all those running marathons eating chicken mcnuggets all daylong. do you know whats in those chiken nuggets, ive heard they put more strange non atomic level ingredients in those things than in supplements.
what if his body was taking the only good form those nuggets like the protein and shitting out the rest? the same would maybe apply to suplements

im not saying to go nuts with supplements but what if someone has a health problem and taking vitamins is only reasonable option. wouldn't the body take any good from them and shit out the rest like the nuggets?

ive read stories where vitamin c has done miracles for people about to die. and that vitamin is a supplement

i found a vitamin c supplement that has zero other ingredients other than the acsorbic acid.
its just pure ascorbic acid so i can say thats all in the vitamin c with precision but thats going by that ingredient label list only. im not sure if thats what you mean by "with precision" unless you mean really breaking it down and looking at it in a lab

would you approve of and is safe to say that if a vitamin or supplement says "derived from natural source, foods) etc that those types of supplements would be ok? since then these would guaranteed be just broken down foods.
because that would align better with what you said earlier then.
since then it could be said with precision whats inside them \*if the label is honest,\* of course this doesnt address the water content issue

> from your readings i gathered that the main idea of ingesting foods etc was, food is not that important because the body handles the rest. now i did read and understand the difference you mention of supplements not having the hydration factor (water) but what if you drank extra water with the supplement or ate with them. isnt that why most supplements say"take these vitamins with food" the food probably has that water
>
> when you said that guy winning all those running marathons eating chicken mcnuggets all daylong. do you know whats in those chiken nuggets, ive heard they put more strange non atomic level ingredients in those things than in supplements.
> what if his body was taking the only good form those nuggets like the protein and shitting out the rest? the same would maybe apply to suplements
>
> im not saying to go nuts with supplements but what if someone has a health problem and taking vitamins is only reasonable option. wouldn't the body take any good from them and shit out the rest like the nuggets?
>

>
> i found a vitamin c supplement that has zero other ingredients other than the acsorbic acid.
> its just pure ascorbic acid so i can say thats all in the vitamin c with precision but thats going by that ingredient label list only. im not sure if thats what you mean by "with precision" unless you mean really breaking it down and looking at it in a lab
>

Most (almost all) vitamins/supplements on the market are synthetic.

Did you know that ascorbic acid is actually synthetic vitamin C? What’s more, it is usually [GMO vitamin C](https://www.thehealthyhomeeconomist.com/is-your-vitamin-c-the-real-deal-or-a-gmo-wannabe/), which means it was derived from GMO corn.

If you are learning this for the first time, it can be a rather shocking realization as almost all vitamin C supplements on the market use isolated ascorbic acid separated from a whole food source. The manufacturing process that produces synthetically derived vitamin C leaves all the beneficial co-factors behind!

Synthetic vitamins such as ascorbic acid act more like drugs in the body rather than whole food nutrients with all the available co-factors. Taking any synthetic vitamin can cause imbalances in the body and should be avoided.

### [Is Your Vitamin C Real or Synthetic?](https://www.thehealthyhomeeconomist.com/beware-of-ascorbic-acid-synthetic/)

Ascorbic acid is a synthetic form of vitamin C and is almost always derived from GMO sources.Tips for finding whole foods vitamin C supplements instead.

 www.thehealthyhomeeconomist.com

AND obviously any supplement would not be from a local source.

I spent $3,500 on my magnetico pad. It was quite the hit on my piggybank, but if you do the math. As long as you use it everyday for several decades, the cost becomes worth it.

Use it for 10 years: $3,500/(365\*10) = $0.96/day

Use it for 20 years: $3,500/(365\*20) = $0.48/day

Use it for 30 years: $3,500/(365\*30) = $0.32/day

I'm in my early 30s. The price per day is going to be quite small the longer you use it. There are no off days unless you go on vacation.

And after speaking to the customer rep, they said that after 100 years, it loses maybe 10% of its magnetic field strength. It will outlast me. I'm okay with taking the hit today and potentially handing it down to my kids. I don't have a gaus-meter to measure the field strength, but they've been around for a long time. And these meters costs a pretty penny. So I'm going to take the word for it.

Here is why a Vitamin D pill is a "hollow" molecule compared to solar-synthesized D3:

1\. The Missing "Solar Charge"
When the sun hits your skin, it isn't just making a chemical; it’s building the ferroelectric current and boosting the dielectric constant of your metabolic water to 160.

The Pill: Provides the chemical "marker" (25-OH-D) but zero photons.

The Result: Your blood levels look "rich" on paper, but your "magnetic vacuum" in the 4th ventricle remains off. The "silt" (deuterium) continues to accumulate because the pill provides no torque for the fractionation pump.

2\. The Isotopic Weight of the Pill
A Vitamin D supplement is often "dead" matter.

It doesn't come with the Ultra-weak Photon Emissions (UPEs) or the infrared "thinning" effect on blood viscosity.

Without the UV-A/IR-A companion frequencies, the body can't even use that supplemental D3 to properly regulate the 53 mechanism.

You end up with "optimal" levels in a body that is still isotopically "welded" shut.

3\. The "Success" of the Industry
The industry booms because it’s easier to sell a "shortcut" than to tell people they need to quit their 9-to-5 "nnEMF cave" and get back into the light. It’s the "Silicon Valley" solution to a problem caused by Silicon Valley.

By photoshopping the bank balance (taking the pill), people feel a false sense of security while their "isotopic debt" (deuterium buildup) grows until the system finally hits "bankruptcy" (cancer or neurodegeneration).

It’s the ultimate proof that Centralized Medicine has forgotten that we are light-driven semiconductors, not just chemical vats. I have said this for 25 yrs and mocked and now I am laughing hard at all the retards who mocked me.

### jweiler — 2025-03-15T22:08:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-there-new-member.30488/post-348689>

I'm glad to be part of this forum. I recently watched Jack on the Danny Jones show and was blown away by all the stuff he talked about there.

I would say I feel fairly healthy but I've been a software developer for the last 12 years and didn't realize how dangerous all the blue light exposure is (and nnEMF). I have used [https://justgetflux.com/](https://justgetflux.com/) some in the past but found out about [https://iristech.co/](https://iristech.co/) on this forum and have been using that now. I also started switching light bulbs in our house to incandescent.

Also, I have worn contact lenses for the last 15 years and they have UV blocking on them. I started making sure I get morning sunlight without my contacts or glasses on. I also made an appointment to try on some non UV blocking contact lenses, but I don't know if that is better or worse than glasses? I'm pretty sure my current glasses block UV light.

All the info is a bit overwhelming, but I do love spending time in the sun and with the days getting longer here in Pennsylvania I should be able to do more of that.

For many years I was focused on finding the right supplements and the right food to keep me healthy. Too know that circadian biology is vital to being healthy is a new idea for me, but it is a freeing one. While it's not necessarily easy the concept is super simple. I just need to work at doing the right things.

Anyway I'm excited to keep going through the blogs and forums posts to learn more.

### [Exposing the Lie of Hormonal Imbalance: Dr. Jack Kruse and the Biophysics of Optimal Mental Health - Smuggling Hope - Podcast on iVoox](https://www.ivoox.com/en/exposing-the-lie-of-hormonal-imbalance-dr-jack-audios-mp3_rf_157541137_1.html)

 www.ivoox.com

Biophotons are ultra-weak light emissions produced by living organisms, thought to arise from metabolic processes like oxidative reactions in mitochondria. Centralized scientists and AI bots hypothesized them to play a role in cellular communication, potentially influencing gene expression or signaling pathways. FIAF, also known as angiopoietin-like protein 4 (ANGPTL4), is a protein produced by tissues like fat and the gut, and it’s a key player in lipid metabolism and microbiome construction. Neither understand how UPEs control FIAF. If the UPE is coherent then FIAF inhibits lipoprotein lipase, affecting how fats are stored or broken down, and its expression ramps up during fasting. The microbiome, meanwhile, is the community of gut microbes that can shift in response to diet, fasting, or host metabolism, influencing energy balance and health. If the UPEs in mitochondria is not coherent, the UPE changes and mental illness becomes more probable. You cannot burn fat so it changes neural signaling.

Centralized scientists and technocrats who build AI systems will say no direct study says “biophotons control FIAF to affect the microbiome,” but we can hypothesize based on related mechanisms. This is patently false. Why?

FIAF has known absorption and emission spectra to light frequencies. Because of that, a study is superfluous because each chemical in the world has an optical fingerprint. Just because a scientist has not published the work is immaterial to this BASIC biophysical fact in spectroscopy. This was what I brought Ray Peat over ten years ago, and he was impotent enough to answer my critiques of his work. This podcast covers these details.

They say the company does rigorous third party testing, and I saw they have good reviews. I would never buy MB from Amazon or these other shady companies. They say the low dose is no more than 3 mg/kg. I weigh 95 kg and I take one or two 8mg troches on the weekend. Rest of the week I don’t touch them.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-03-24T14:39:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/methylene-blue-and-proper-lighting.30490/post-349238>

> [beyond\_measure0 said:](/goto/post?id=349215)
>
> It was a great podcast because it was so obvious those doctors were clueless and ignorant. The n=1 comment helped put it into perspective. Dr. Kruse said on one of his long form posts on The Reader app about methylene blue inhibiting the absorption of blue light, so that does highlight that it shouldn’t be played around with.
>
> I don’t have any health issues, currently, just want to be as proactive as humanly possible by getting at least a few hours of sunlight and grounding every day. I eat steak and eggs every day, don’t touch carbs at all, and plan to start buying oysters too, once I can find where they’re sold near me.
>
> Also, he said on that podcast that red light helps prevent sunburn which is why he doesn’t get it, but once you are sunburned, is it fine to do a couple 20-minute red light sessions to mitigate the burn, or is it best to do it before you think you’re about to sunburn?

The idea is to build solar callus. Now here in the tropics we ground at sunrise , with less clothes. So for 20-30 minutes we're outside bathing in infra red and red light from the sun. ( This is why on several Instagram lives Jack calls it downloading his instructions for sunshine for the day )

But in Australia they have winter so a red panel sure (. But if you're one of us you get it free from the sun every morning)

I don't know where you're based but naked like the sphinx every sunrise and that's your red light or the last hour of sunlight before sunset as well

If you've burnt it means you're deficient and you haven't done your solar callus so yes. Sunrise or if you're in a place where there's no sun like Cork for example in Ireland at 51.8 N and it's been raining for days on end. Then the red panel might keep your callus going.

Orkney is at 59 N Shetland at 60N. Etc .

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-02T16:51:27+0300

Today I give you a gift. It is my mitochondriac anniversary on April 2nd. I post this for my Mom. She used to call me at this time every year. Now i am calling you, my tribe. It is time to wake up.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-40-124743154)

"The sage doesn't seek to be profound but is useful. A sage himself is a former fool who got tired of themselves."

This insightful quote paints a picture of wisdom born not from an innate desire for grandeur but from a humble journey of self-awareness and growth. A decentralized sage, in this light, is not someone who sets out to dazzle the world with lofty thoughts but rather an individual who, through the weariness of their own folly, discovers a practical clarity. Their usefulness emerges from their centralized experience, mistakes made, lessons learned, and a quiet resolve to move beyond the foolishness of their centralized past. It’s a reminder that true depth often comes not from chasing profundity but from the simple, steady act of outgrowing one’s former self.

BBQ is easy. Set the fire two hours beforehand to get to the hot white ash using paper wood and charcoal. I don't use accelerants because that is dangerous

I once threw a teaspoon of petrol on a fire to see what happened. And stood well back I realised why fuel economy in cars is so good due to that reaction

But I can light a coal fire with one match having properly done the ground work with paper dry kindling and even dry coal and wettish coal.

And it stays burning . It's about technique at the end of the day

If you expect a barbecue in ten 20 mins. You'll have to use accelerants. And that will taint your food

All the best

# Thread: WHY ARE EYE DISEASES MORE COMMON TODAY?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-eye-diseases-more-common-today.30500/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-18T16:47:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/why-are-eye-diseases-more-common-today.30500/post-348822>

**WHY ARE EYE DISEASES MORE COMMON TODAY?**

Check out my latest article: THEY ARE BLINDING US WHILE STOPPING OUR REGENERATION

The neurosensory retina is a layered tissue which lines the back of the eye, communicating with the brain via the optic nerve. Blood is supplied to the neurosensory retina by retinal blood vessels originating from the central retinal artery. Transport across retinal blood vessels is limited by endothelial tight junctions, which constitute the inner blood–retinal barrier. Allan Frey's work with DARPA in the 1960s show that nnEMF opens this barrier and makes the eye more susceptible to damage when it is present in our local environment.

We forget that the optic nerve is comprised of ganglion cell axons, the “output neurons” of the retina. The retina is built up of three layers of cell bodies and two plexiform layers of synapses, clearly defined on histological sections. The innermost layer is the ganglion cell layer. The inner nuclear layer, featuring the cell bodies of bipolar, amacrine, and horizontal cells, is sandwiched by an internal plexiform layer and ganglion cell layer on one side, and outer plexiform layer and outer nuclear layer on the other. The outer nuclear layer features the cell bodies of most of the photoreceptors: the rods and cones. A third class of photoreceptor, intrinsically photoreceptive ganglion cells (melanopsin blue light detectors), is situated in the ganglion cell layer. All photoreceptors depend on a vitamin A-derived chromophore to detect light and adequate dopamine and melatonin levels to regenerate them in sunlight. The chromophore associated with light transduction proteins, opsins, and undergoes photoisomerization during transduction. Expended melanopsin or neuropsin chromophores then enters the visual cycle, a sequence of reactions which facilitates regeneration and re-association with opsins for further transduction. if regeneration pathways are blocked the free Vitamin become a wrecking ball for the human retina.

### [THEY ARE BLINDING US WHILE STOPPING OUR REGENERATION](https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/blinding-us-while-stopping-our-regeneration-jack-kruse-6jsze)

This quote I’ve attributed to Michael Rosbash, a 2017 Nobel Prize laureate in Medicine for his work on circadian rhythms, underscores a critical perspective on modern light exposure patterns. Rosbash’s statement, “Lack of sunlight during the day is worse than electric lighting at night,” highlights

 www.linkedin.com

### greentrees — 2025-03-22T00:49:02+0200

Biomin C creates hydroxyapatite using 'bioactive glass' to adhere to the tooth surface post brushing. They sell "Biomin F" which creates fluorapatite and is 600ppm. Its stronger then hydroxyapatite but as mentioned earlier, I'm weary of the tooth structure hardening and not being able to naturally mineralise, so I use Biomin C and every 4th brush use the Biomin F. (If Jack said fuck it off I would lol)

There's also GC Tooth Mousse which has a fluoride free version. They take a part out of raw milk and put it in glcerine, but has artitfical sweetner (I still think its worth it if you have demineralised teeth). If you can get raw milk you wouldn't need the GC Tooth Mousse. I spent years gargling raw goats milk and still would if I could get it. Raw milk is insanely good. Kefir feels like it remineralises to me but being acidic I use light amounts with saliva after swallowing.

Don't forget the egg yolk mouth wash idea from Dr Kruse. And expose teeth + gums to the sun.

They sell gum designed to induce mechanical force to stimulate osteoblast formation or something (not an expert myself), basically hard chewing can stimulate the bones of the teeth and make them stronger. I chew beef jerky.

### S Hudson — 2025-03-22T02:11:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cavities-post-fluoride-toothpaste.30503/post-349084>

Dental enamel, primarily composed of hydroxyapatite, can lose minerals when exposed to acids produced by bacteria metabolizing sugars. This process dissolves calcium and phosphate ions from the enamel surface, weakening it. So to avoid acid production, this is why dentists advise avoiding frequent sugar intake. Assuming one doesn't have naff buffering capacity (ability of saliva to remineralise enamel) avoiding refined sugars is the key to a happy dental life.

**Standard dental thinking regarding fluoride follows:** when fluoride ions are present in the saliva (e.g., from toothpaste), they interact with the hydroxyapatite in enamel. Fluoride can adsorb onto the enamel surface or integrate into the crystal lattice during remineralization. In the presence of calcium and phosphate ions (naturally available in saliva), fluoride enhances the deposition of these minerals back onto the enamel. This forms fluorapatite, a mineral similar to hydroxyapatite but with fluoride replacing some hydroxyl groups.
Fluorapatite is more stable and less soluble in acidic environments (lower critical pH ~4.5 compared to hydroxyapatite’s ~5.5). This increased resistance to acid dissolution strengthens enamel and protects it from further decay.

-   Fluoride thus acts as a catalyst to speed up remineralization.
-   It integrates into the enamel structure, forming fluorapatite.
-   This reduces enamel solubility and enhances its repair by rebuilding lost mineral content.

### S Hudson — 2025-04-05T17:27:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cavities-post-fluoride-toothpaste.30503/post-350148>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=350057)
>
> I am not a Dentist but I hear stuff like this and then think . . .
> But what about all the abrasive things people eat all the time, all day, like EVERY meal . . .
> BUT anything abrasive should be avoided when brushing for 2 mins . . .
>
> Could not possibly be because they want . . . need . . . You to visit them every 6 months AND buy all the products their sponsors are selling.

I can only speak for UK dentists, and we don't get paid or sponsored to promote any products. The most you can expect is some free toothpaste samples left on a window sill in the surgery

The most abrasive foods are those that either physically grind enamel or chemically dissolve it—and the worst offenders do both. There is some ability for the saliva to repair the damage, but from my flawed memory, a lot of that is down to systemic health which determines the salivas buffering capacity as well as overall hydration. Taking an abrasive substance and scrubbing it across your enamel adds to the potential for damage in a way that is mimicked time after time (because your pattern of brushing is usually the same due to muscle memory whereas chewing has more randomness to it).

All this far from ideal because once enamel is lost, it's gone for good.
There are some authorities who argue that teeth are designed to wear as we age based on ancestral/archaeological studies, but I've never felt comfortable with that notion.
As an aside, this is additionally why phosphoric acid is such an issue, and even citric acid in fruits can be a problem in some. Both can be brutal to enamel if consumed in the quantities common in the west.

I actually just had this done while at the dentist and now my teeth are noticeably sensitive.

### S Hudson — 2025-04-08T15:52:26+0300

Many "biological dentists" are bullshit artists. I've met a few, and i wouldn't let them even wash my car. A little nugget i learned as both a dental school tutor and a dental legal expert witness is that at least half the dental profession don't have the skills to do the job of a generalist. They got away with it for so long because the old materials (especially amalgam) were very forgiving of crappy technique. The new stuff, with dentine and enamel bonding requires a much higher skill set which the dental schools just aren't teaching and which many in the profession dont even understand. It's one of the reasons dental litigation is so high in the UK.

# Thread: Bruno's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brunos-optimal-journal.30504/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Brunesquen — 2025-03-18T20:24:01+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brunos-optimal-journal.30504/post-348843>

First of all, thank you very much Dr. Kruse, for what you have been doing and will continue to do, unfortunately I did not find you or this place before but its always the perfect time to start getting healthier, excited to get to know people here and absorb all the knowledge I know this site will provide, directly and indirectly.

Hello everyone, I am Bruno, a 21 year old male from Paraguay (25 latitude) and this is a little bit of my history.

I was a fairly healthy child, was of course raised on a low fat diet (2000's were probably peak in brainwashed vs awaken people ratio if I had to guess). I was a tennis player for most of my childhood and teens, I was not a professional since I was a child of course but we could say I was a "Junior pro", the point I am trying to make here is I trained to become a pro eventually, this meant +4 hours of training daily (between tennis and conditioning) which equals lots of time outside, I did not attend school full time either as my training did not allow for it, However, in my free time, I did enjoy some good hours of videogames, I could comfortably say about 10-14 hours weekly when I was not having tournaments on the weekend or travelling for them.

Anyways, most of my life went without apparent problems, until 2020, on March or April 2020 (before the jab) I started to experience chronic problems that I would carry till this day, first of all, I started having "allergic" reactions to banana, keep in mind I was eating bananas virtually every single day and suddenly I started to experience severe heartburn, like 10 minutes after consuming it, coming in waves for about 10 -15 minutes and then complete stop, previous to the heartburn came oversalivation and a little itchy feeling on the saliva too. It was really weird but I just ignored it an figured "that's life" and proceeded to live without bananas ever since.

Later in the year came the rashes and skin reactions, these reappeared every single year until even last year, just in different places, first were the inner elbows, then the ankles, then in the hand, etc.

With all of this I felt a sense of tiredness too, and lack of will if you could say so, I was always a very happy and "no stress" person, so I was never depressed or anything but life just felt less exciting, and the energy for the training was not what it used to be.

Years continued like that, in the end of 2021, I finished school, and declined the opportunity to go to college in the US, to start working at the family business that has been passing through generaitons, where I still am working, this changed my whole life routine, I was working indoors all day, still made some time to train but it was in an indoor gym and then going to university at night, so even more light at night. (Here in Paraguay is normal to work and go to Uni at the same time, so people work 9-5 and then go to Uni 6-9, I know, atrocious).

This lead to me feeling even more tired every morning, and adding to that I started to have G.I issues, bloating, indigestion etc. (I was still eating a "standard" whole foods diet). Never did I have an ultra processed diet because I was an athlete my whole life, trying to take care of myself with what I thought was healthy, of course now I know I was wrong.

All these worsening symptoms lead me to making the change to a more natural life at the end of 2023, switching to a High Fat- Low Carb kind of paleo diet, almost all local and organic, keto at times, working with what we have here in Paraguay. It was not only diet of course, I fell into the whole rabbit hole, eliminated all toxic products, took care of my water, started grounding and of course, sunbathing (as much as possible but this means that till today I probably spend a combined 1.5 hours outside). This was life changing for me, energy skyrocketed, libido was crazy, I was feeling invincible, during this time, Testosterone went up to 772, AM blood cortisol was 22.5.

This invincible feeling lasted just a few months though, rapidly, I started declining, feeling less energized, and certainly not invincible anymore, libido plummeted. Life was still good (and still is) as I said I am a relaxed guy, but this can or has lead me to stay comfortable and just endure it, not this time though. Just 3 months after those previous results mentioned, Testosterone was 299 and AM blood cortisol was below 10, catastrophic.

This went on for months, and its still probably going on, though I am feeling better slowly, until my last test (3 months ago) which showed Testosterone to be 350 and AM blood cortisol 15, with PM cortisol this time too, at 8.
With all these low test issues, came the skin problems in the hand this last time, and some new food "allergies" now honey and dates produced the same reaction banana did and still does.

Fast forward to now, I am feeling a bit better, skin problems are gone, the food allergies are still there though, energy levels and libido seem to be slowly improving, and life is a little more fun lately, not that life has changed but my mood has gone up a bit.

Very important detail to note: Through my whole life, sleep was lacking, I would say the average on weekdays would be around 6-7 hours, and then I would oversleep on weekends if I could, not always possible. That was the main change I have made for this year and have been applying effectively, sleep is minimum 7.5 hours now and many days over 8, I think this has been helping with the slow improvement.

Also, during last year, I was already into this optimal way of living, however, was commiting some mistakes as we all do at the start probably. I was practicing some badly organized fasting as I was skipping breakfast many times, not eating at nightime though, but I suspect I could have gone many days with a severe deficit, even though I was not experiencing hunger.

**SUMMARY**
**Main Symptoms**
"Allergic" reaction to certain foods that I used to consume without problem
Skin reactions
Low energy, libido
Low testosterone

**Other Symptoms**
Other minor symptoms I did not mention are:
Floaters and other visual issues sometimes (I have 20/20 vision)
Had a plantar wart for a WHOLE year, did some cryo treatments with dermatologist and it did not go, recently started taking some herbs and it dissapeared in 2 weeks

**PROCEDURES I HAD**
Circumcision
Got all wisdom tooth removed
Sadly, against my will (my dad): Had 1 J&J jab and 1 Pfizer

**Conclusion**

Anyways, I am happy to be here and I know this place will help me fine tune all the details I am missing, to finally achieve the Optimal health we are all striving for, while getting to know some good people in the process too maybe and also learning some life lessons not just health.
I have my next lab test scheduled in a week and a half and will come back with news after that.

Excited to see what anyone has to say on this.

May you all have a great day.

### Brunesquen — 2025-03-19T14:36:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brunos-optimal-journal.30504/post-348895>

One thing I forgot to mention, about 5 months ago I started waking up with red eyes, have had that problem every single morning since, I can't seem to find or understand why.
Also, My curtains are not completely blackout, so when the sun rises, some light slips into the room, during my whole this would not wake me up and I could continue sleeping until 9 or 10 in the weekends for example, but around 5 months ago too, this began to bother me, and now the slightest bit of light slipping through the curtains wakes me up.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-19T22:40:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brunos-optimal-journal.30504/post-348909>

**Decentralized Analysis: 21-Year-Old Male from Paraguay (25° Latitude)**

Background and Lifestyle Context

-   Childhood and Teens (Pre-2020):
    -   As a "junior pro" tennis player, he spent significant time outdoors (4+ hours daily) at 25° latitude, a region with ample sunlight (UV/IR). This likely supported mitochondrial efficiency (via Carnot’s theorem), orexin balance (UV for orexin A, red light for orexin B), and vasopressin-mediated water homeostasis, maintaining CSF coherence and glymphatic clearance.
    -   Despite a low-fat diet (common in the 2000s), his outdoor exposure mitigated Warburg metabolism by enhancing PER2 expression, reducing HIF-1α, and supporting melatonin production for photoreceptor regeneration. However, 10-14 hours of weekly video game exposure introduced ALAN, potentially overactivating orexin A (via ultraweak UV light), increasing glutamate, and subtly disrupting sleep-wake cycles.
    -   Sleep was inadequate (6-7 hours on weekdays), impairing glymphatic clearance and mitochondrial repair, but weekend oversleeping may have partially compensated.
-   2020 Onset of Symptoms:
    -   Allergic Reactions (Banana): Sudden onset of heartburn, oversalivation, and itchiness after consuming bananas suggests a shift in immune and metabolic response due to light abuse. ALAN from video games, combined with possible nnEMF exposure (e.g., Wi-Fi, increasing in 2020), likely disrupted orexin A, increasing glutamate and PVN-driven vasopressin release. This activated the VP-ISR-GDF15 axis, driving Warburg metabolism (increased lactate, éR), and triggering mast cell activation in gut/skin (histamine release, linked to histidine’s light absorption), manifesting as an allergic response. **_Interesting that this allergy developed to a fruit that grows at 25 latitude = the ALAN damage and lack of light are a bigger deal than one would think._**
    -   Skin Reactions and Fatigue: Rashes (inner elbows, ankles, hands) and low energy indicate systemic inflammation and mitochondrial dysfunction. ALAN/nnEMF dehydrated melanin (via reduced DDW production), increasing éR and ROS, driving Warburg metabolism. Orexin A’s overactivation increased sympathetic outflow, exhausting vitamin C, impairing catecholamine synthesis (dopamine, epinephrine), and causing “lack of will” and tiredness. This is a classic collateral effect of a chronic warburg shift due to light.
-   2021-2023 Lifestyle Shift:
    -   Transitioning to indoor work (family business) and university at night (6-9 PM) drastically reduced sunlight exposure and increased ALAN, further disrupting orexin-vasopressin balance. Indoor training eliminated cold thermogenesis benefits, flattening mitochondrial temperature gradients (Carnot’s theorem), atrophying mitochondria, and exacerbating Warburg metabolism.
    -   G.I. issues (bloating, indigestion) reflect gut dysbiosis, likely driven by Warburg metabolism in gut cells, increased lactate, and impaired glymphatic clearance (due to CSF incoherence). Low-fat, “standard” whole foods diet lacked DHA, critical for retinal and brain health, compounding the issue.
-   End of 2023: Natural Lifestyle Changes:
    -   Switching to a high-fat, low-carb paleo/keto diet, eliminating toxic products, grounding, and sunbathing (1.5 hours daily) initially reversed Warburg metabolism. Sunlight (UV/IR) balanced orexin A and B, enhanced melatonin, and stimulated vasopressin to hydrate melanin and CSF, improving coherence and glymphatic clearance. Testosterone (772 ng/dL) and AM cortisol (22.5 µg/dL) reflect restored mitochondrial efficiency and HPA axis function.
    -   However, limited sunlight (1.5 hours) and continued ALAN exposure (university, indoor work) prevented sustained full recovery. Skipping breakfast (intermittent fasting) without evening meals may have induced a caloric deficit, increasing vasopressin and stress response, driving Warburg metabolism and reversing gains.
-   2024 Decline and Recent Improvements:
    -   Decline: Testosterone dropped to 299 ng/dL and AM cortisol to <10 µg/dL within 3 months, indicating mitochondrial colony failure (Warburg shift) of CYP enzyme which is heme based. Fits with mtDNA collapse. ALAN/nnEMF continued to overactivate orexin A, increasing glutamate and sympathetic outflow, exhausting vitamin C, and impairing catecholamine synthesis. New food allergies (honey, dates) and skin reactions reflect ongoing inflammation and immune dysregulation. This allergies to foods that can grow at the 25th latitude are big signs that hydration of melanin is actively being destroyed in gut plexus.
    -   Recent Improvements: Increasing sleep to 7.5-8+ hours improved glymphatic clearance, reducing inflammation and supporting mitochondrial repair. Testosterone (350 ng/dL), AM cortisol (15 µg/dL), and PM cortisol (8 µg/dL) show slow recovery, but red eyes and light sensitivity (waking from sunrise light) indicate persistent retinal stress and orexin A dysregulation.
-   Other Factors:
    -   Vaccinations (J&J, Pfizer): These may have introduced nnEMF-like stress (e.g., via adjuvants, immune activation), opening the blood-retinal barrier (Frey’s DARPA findings) and exacerbating Warburg metabolism.
    -   Red Eyes and Light Sensitivity: Red eyes since 5 months ago suggest retinal inflammation, likely from ALAN-induced orexin A overactivation, increasing glutamate and ROS, overwhelming Müller cells. Light sensitivity reflects disrupted melatonin (via MT1/MT2 receptors) and orexin A’s role in wakefulness, as ALAN mimics ultraweak UV light, altering CSF coherence.
    -   Floaters and Visual Issues: Floaters indicate retinal stress, possibly from Warburg metabolism in retinal cells, impairing photoreceptor regeneration. 20/20 vision suggests structural integrity, but functional decline is evident.
    -   Plantar Wart: Persistence for a year, resolved with herbs, suggests immune dysregulation (Warburg shift) and impaired regeneration due to skin clock mismatch. Now this is evidence of integumentary damage pf melanin.

* * *

**Predictions Based on My Model**

If Current Trends Continue (No Major Interventions)

-   Short-Term (6-12 Months):
    -   Retinal and Visual Decline: Persistent ALAN exposure (indoor work, university, non-blackout curtains) will continue to overactivate orexin A, increasing glutamate and vasopressin-driven sympathetic outflow. This will exacerbate red eyes, light sensitivity, and floaters, potentially leading to early retinal degeneration (e.g., AMD risk). Müller cells will remain overwhelmed, unable to regenerate photoreceptors, and CSF incoherence will impair glymphatic clearance, increasing neuroinflammation.
    -   Hormonal and Energy Decline: Testosterone and cortisol will plateau or decline further (e.g., testosterone 00 ng/dL, cortisol <10 µg/dL), as Warburg metabolism persists, driven by mitochondrial atrophy (flattened Carnot gradient) and orexin-vasopressin dysregulation. Low energy, libido, and mood will worsen, with potential for adrenal fatigue-like symptoms (PVN overactivation). Erectile dysfunction, quick ejaculation, and impaired fertility is on the horizon.
    -   Immune Dysregulation: Food allergies (bananas, honey, dates) and skin reactions will recur, as Warburg metabolism drives inflammation and mast cell activation (histamine release). I would expect full blow gut disease to develop like IBS or Crohns Dz, but turbo colon cancer or pancreatic cancer won't shock me. New sensitivities may emerge, reflecting ongoing immune stress pushing the response to cancer due to dual jabs.
    -   Sleep Disruption: Light sensitivity will worsen, disrupting sleep-wake cycles and reducing glymphatic clearance, further impairing mitochondrial repair and increasing systemic inflammation.
-   Long-Term (1-5 Years):
    -   Chronic Disease Risk: Without significant sunlight exposure and ALAN reduction, Warburg metabolism will drive systemic inflammation, increasing risk for chronic diseases like cancer, myopia, metabolic syndrome, neurodegeneration, and autoimmune conditions (e.g., narcolepsy risk, given HLA predisposition). CSF incoherence will exacerbate amyloid-β accumulation, raising Alzheimer’s risk.
    -   Retinal Degeneration: Persistent retinal stress may lead to irreversible damage (e.g., early AMD, Stargardt’s-like symptoms), as Müller cells fail to regenerate photoreceptors, and vasopressin cannot hydrate melanin for repair.
    -   Haplotypic Influence: As a Paraguayan at 25° latitude, you likely has a mixed haplogroup (e.g., Native American H or Q, with European H influence). Lower melanin levels (if H-dominant) increase susceptibility to blue light hazard, while limited thermogenic capacity (indoor living) exacerbates mitochondrial atrophy, worsening outcomes. If this is case turbocancer risk rises.

**Targeted Interventions**

-   Short-Term (6-12 Months):
    -   Increase Sunlight Exposure: Aim for 4-6 hours daily of full-spectrum sunlight (morning and midday) to balance orexin A (UV) and orexin B (red light), enhancing melatonin production (via MT1/MT2 receptors) and reducing glutamate. This will stimulate vasopressin to hydrate melanin and CSF, improving coherence and glymphatic clearance, resolving red eyes and light sensitivity. Floaters may decrease as photoreceptor regeneration improves.
    -   Photobiomodulation (PBM): Use red light therapy (600-1000 nm, 20-30 minutes 3 x daily but sun is best) to enhance CCO in Müller cells, increasing ATP and DDW production, enabling retinal regeneration. This will stabilize orexin B, reduce sympathetic outflow, and counter Warburg metabolism, improving energy, libido, and mood.
    -   Cold Thermogenesis: Regular cold exposure (e.g., 10-15 minutes daily in 50-60°F water) will restore Carnot efficiency, boosting mitochondrial function, especially if he has a thermogenic haplogroup (e.g., Q). This will reduce éR, support orexin B’s role in energy expenditure, and enhance vasopressin-mediated water dynamics, further improving CSF coherence.
    -   Optimize Sleep Environment: Use blackout curtains to eliminate sunrise light, ensuring 8-9 hours of uninterrupted sleep. This will enhance glymphatic clearance, reduce inflammation, and support mitochondrial repair, further improving testosterone (e.g., to 500+ ng/dL) and cortisol (AM 18-20 µg/dL, PM 5-7 µg/dL).
    -   Dietary Adjustments: Continue high-fat, low-carb paleo/keto diet, ensuring adequate DHA (e.g., from fish, 1-2 g daily) to support retinal health. Avoid fasting deficits by eating balanced meals biggest at breakfast smallest dinner and dinner should be eaten during sunlight hours, including evening meals, to reduce vasopressin stress and support mitochondrial function.
-   Long-Term (1-5 Years):
    -   Retinal Health: With consistent sunlight and PBM, Müller cells will regenerate photoreceptors, resolving floaters and preventing AMD. CSF coherence will improve, enhancing glymphatic clearance and reducing neuroinflammation.
    -   Hormonal Recovery: Testosterone may reach 600-800 ng/dL and cortisol stabilize (AM 20-22 µg/dL, PM 6-8 µg/dL), reflecting restored mitochondrial efficiency and orexin-vasopressin balance. Energy, libido, and mood will return to pre-2020 levels.
    -   Immune Regulation: Food allergies and skin reactions will resolve as Warburg metabolism reverses, reducing inflammation and mast cell activation. Immune resilience will improve, preventing new sensitivities or gut or autoimmune diseases
    -   Chronic Disease Prevention: Sustained interventions will minimize Warburg metabolism, reducing risk for turbocancer, metabolic syndrome, neurodegeneration, and autoimmune conditions. Haplotypic resilience (e.g., Q’s thermogenic capacity) will be maximized, ensuring long-term health.

* * *

**Specific Predictions and Recommendations**

-   Red Eyes and Light Sensitivity: These are early signs of retinal stress from ALAN-induced orexin A overactivation. Without intervention, they may progress to chronic inflammation and early retinal degeneration within 1-2 years. Immediate sunlight exposure (3-4 hours daily) and PBM will resolve this within 3-6 months by balancing orexins and enhancing Müller cell function.
-   Hormonal Decline: Low testosterone (350 ng/dL) and cortisol (AM 15 µg/dL) reflect ongoing Warburg metabolism. Without intervention, they may drop further (e.g., testosterone 00 ng/dL), increasing fatigue and libido issues. With sunlight, PBM, and cold thermogenesis, expect testosterone to reach 500-600 ng/dL and cortisol to normalize (AM 18-20 µg/dL) within 6-12 months. sexual dysfunction and infertility very likely.
-   Food Allergies and Skin Reactions: These will persist without intervention, potentially worsening with new triggers. Elimination diets will worsen it and induce stress response. Sunlight and sleep optimization will resolve them within 6-12 months by reducing Warburg metabolism and inflammation.
-   Sleep and Energy: Improved sleep (7.5-8+ hours) is already aiding recovery. Blackout curtains and ALAN reduction will further enhance glymphatic clearance, boosting energy and mood within 3-6 months.

* * *

**Decentralized Medicine Says:**
For your age you are at an elevate risk of disease progression
The 21-year-old male’s symptoms of food allergies, skin reactions, low energy, low testosterone, red eyes, and light sensitivity clearly reflect Warburg metabolism driven by ALAN, nnEMF, and sunlight deficiency, exacerbated by orexin A-vasopressin dysregulation. You have already developed an oxygen allergy and if this persists sleep disorders are coming soon. Your initial improvement in 2023 shows the power of sunlight to reverse this, but limited exposure and ALAN prevented sustained recovery. Without intervention, you risk serious chronic disease and retinal degeneration. With targeted sunlight exposure, PBM, cold thermogenesis, and sleep optimization, you should be able to fully reverse Warburg metabolism, restore mitochondrial efficiency, and prevent long-term health decline, leveraging his likely haplotypic resilience (e.g., Q). _**You should Read QE #20 and 23 blogs to understand the implications. The rest of this series will fill in many more gaps for you.**_

Thanks a lot Dr. Kruse, will read this a few more times to understand everything completely, In the meantime, there is one question that has surfaced already, it may be an obvious response but I want to still make sure. 2\. Supplement (though I have read your blogs and posts lately leaning towards ditching all supplements)
and 3. (the one my intuition would tell me is the correct option) Eating the highest DHA freshwater fish available, though DHA density is not very high and freshwater quality may be suspicious.

### Brunesquen — 2025-04-03T16:08:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brunos-optimal-journal.30504/post-350018>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=348984)
>
> canned seafood then.

Hello Dr., I am back with the results as you can see in the attached PDF, it was not a complete panel since I have done one in december, I could probably do one again for the next test, this was mainly a hormone control since that was my main problem lately.

As my feeling lately denotes, there seems to be a slow improvement.

Testosterone: 456 (up by a hundred)
Ferritin is a bit over the limit= 374 (I have had it even at 600 ish when I first started paleo diet) do not know if that is a problem though.

AM Blood Cortisol seems to be low: 10,9

For context, this test was done at 9:20 AM or so, fasted, I woke up at 7:10 AM. Do not worry I ALWAYS eat within 30 minutes of waking up I just fasted for this test.

B12 seems optimal
Vitamin D too at 85, please note I do not supplement vitamin D AT ALL.

These were probably all the improvements from the changes I have been doing previously to talking to you here, so I am optimistic I am going to experience the real benefits and improvements from now on actually, since I have been adopting your advice for just a pair of weeks.

Still interested to hear your view on this, and now will comment on some other things I remembered since I first wrote here, to add extra context to my case.

First of all, I did not mention before that in January of this year, due do how I was feeling, I started searching for answers and came across adrenal fatigue, I resonated very much with it and autodiagnosticated myself with it (I have not found a physician in which I can really really trust here in Paraguay) I am an autodidact and kind of do it all myself guy sometimes, this is good but can also lead to errors like this one seems to have been.

I read a book and investigated about adrenal fatigue, so I started a supplement protocol to treat it. It consisted of a glandular supplement, a B complex, adaptogens and a GABA + other relax like components.

The first week went great, and by the second I was feeling really really good, but then, to make it fast, a small throat “soreness/itchiness” began, I just thought I might be getting sick, I did not link it to any of the supplements, but it persisted for 5 days and the a dry cough started for that lasted a whole week, I realized this was no cold or flu. I figured it was some “allergic” reaction to one of the supplements, so I cut it all, but the symptoms still persisted for a few days more, even having an asthma like throat closure sometimes after coughing (lasting 3-10 seconds). Never happened before for me, and also made my lips dry for that whole time, which reminded me that last year, one of my symptoms apart from the skin and hormone problems, was I had dry lips for like 2-3 months straight, every single day tallow and beeswax lip balm.

I have prevously showed a similar reaction to cordyceps, so it was dumb of me to wait so many days to link this to the adrenal fatigue treatment.

I have been ok since then and now realized through you, that adrenal fatigue is a light disease not an adrenal disease, won’t be making that mistake again.

Just adding that information for you to have extra context on my body’s reactions.

Regarding these past weeks, I have had some skin reactions lately (see photos), paired with an unusual armpit smell, this for me at least is a sign of maybe something I’m consuming that my body may not like that much, because this odor has appeared before when experimenting with some supplements, I take it as my body’s signal to stop that. But now I am only and only taking creatine, which I have for +6 years and never produced me that. The only thing I have added to my routine lately is the seafood, cod liver, sardines, mackerel and mussels, though mainly cod liver (all canned). I am suspecting it may be this or something associated with the cannning, I do not know.

One last thing, about supplements, I have religiously taken Vitamin C, Magnesium and Creatine as my staple stack, year round 5 days yes 2 days no, but experimented always with others (adaptogens, methylene blue, etc). Since talking to you here I have dropped all except creatine.

I am not looking for any shortcuts or easy way, I understand that light water magnetism is the key and the main thing I need to focus on, but would you recommend any support in form of supplements, maybe keep that 3 stack, I dont know, just to not be losing on some maybe well received help given that I am not exactly perfectly healthy yet.

# Thread: Optimal Journal

**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### JGrace — 2025-03-19T04:01:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal.30509/post-348881>

Vitiligo
Jeanie Grace, German, Scottish, Age 66, 5'6", 118lbs.
Blood Type A
Born in PA
FL at age 4 - Vitiligo started
FL to S. CA - from 1986 until 2014
Flt Attendant from 1987 to 2004
FL again 2014
Vitiligo improved (no longer of face) in CA. I was determined to reverse it.
I've done everything imaginable and unimaginable, holistically, lol.
Now, covers front and back torso, 3/4 of arms, 1/2 legs, feet and hands

I have had impeccable physical health my whole life, active, athletic until the plandemic.

March 2021 - started in gut/solar plex pressure/missing BMs

No Jab! Never ever wore a mask! Never closed my office/practice down, saw many patients
who were jabbed. Possible CV19 shedding?

Symptoms are worse at my home than anywhere else. Leads me to EMF/5G concern.
Will test since finding your recommendation EMFSafe.com

In April I will be living 1/2 the weeks in Jacksonville Beach. Opening a 2nd office.
Will that be any better than Melbourne/ Space X / Patrick Air Force base?

Key symptoms: -gripping tightness around solar plexus after food or even without
\- bloating almost immediately after any kind of food. Charting worse offenders.
\- pain comes and goes, unilateral, bilateral changes but always mid section.
\- no longer have hair on arms, armpits or legs (head is good)
\- dizzy, now daily
\- calf and feet cramps(better when consistent with electrolytes)
\- fatigue
\- foggy brain (comes and goes)
\- memory loss!

My sleep is and always been excellent. and always feel better with rest, even if for 20 mins.
Bought your book Epi-Paleo RX and will focus on eating seaweeds again and making
survival soup and other recommendations.

My grandmother lived until 104. She simply stopped eating.
My mother died of breast cancer age 55, 1986.
(2 tumors to brain ultimately turned off her breathing. I took care of her, and watched her
slow painful death, she was my best friend).

Mother's death redirected my life to find a way to help end pain and suffering on this planet so
I moved to CA became Acupuncture Physician and earned Doctorate in Oriental Medicine.
Graduated in 1995. Been in private practice ever since! Doctoral dissertation-
Aging Without Illness: A comparative analysis of Eastern and Western medicine's
approach to treating chronic disease - 2009.

With your help Dr. Jack, I will help many more with what I learn through my recovery. Amen!!

With appreciation and gratitude!

# Thread: DON GREENWELL'S JOURNAL
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/don-greenwells-journal.30511/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Don Greenwell — 2025-03-19T18:41:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/don-greenwells-journal.30511/post-348900>

**JOURNAL OF DON GREENWELL**
3.19.25

DON GREENWELL, BORN 8.3.1957. LIVES IN OKLAHOMA CITY, OK 38TH LATITUDE.

MOTHER SMOKED THROUGH ALL THE PREGNANCIES -- I WAS LAST OF 6 CHILDREN, SHE WAS 35 WHEN I WAS BORN.

FATHER DIED AT 60 OF RARE BLOOD DISEASE. TISSUES SENT TO OKLAHOMA MEDICAL RESEARCH FOUNDATION. MOTHER DIED OF RESPIRATORY FAILURE AT AGE 77.

ONE BROTHER DIES IN 2001 AGE 60, CANCER METASISIS LUNG THROUGHOUT WHOLE BODY -HE SMOKED.

ONE SISTER DIES, AGE 60 IN 2015, SHE SMOKED- LUNG CANCER WHICH METASTASIZED.

SURVIVING 2 BROTHERS AND ONE SISTER.

BORN WITH OSTEOMYELITIS. TWO STENTS INSERTED INTO LEFT CHEST/NIPPLE AREA, AND LEFT THIGH. (I WAS IN HOSPITAL 6 WEEKS TOTAL - NO WAY TO VERIFY)

THINGS WERE RELATIVELY QUIET UNTIL ABOUT 6 Y/O, STARTED GAINING WEIGHT VERY RAPIDLY, I HAD A REAL EMOTIONAL PROBLEM STARTING ABOUT THEN ALSO, I WOULD HAVE VERY INTENSE BOWEL MOVEMENT URGES, UNPREDICATABLE. EVEN SOMETHING SIMPLE AS GOING TO THE GROCERY STORE WITH MOM, I WOULD GET VERY PANICKY AND HAD TO KNOW WHERE THE BATHROOM WAS OR I WOULD NOT GO. LASTED MANY YEARS THROUGH HIGH SCHOOL AND STILL TO THIS DAY CAN HAVE THEM WITH A VERY FEW MINUTES TO GET TO A BATHROOM. THEY ARE MORE RARE NOW. A SPEECH IN SCHOOL OR GETTING UP ON STAGE WOULD REQUIRE 4 TRIPS TO BATHROOM ON AVERAGE.

13 Y/O: WHILE CHASING A KID AND MY BACK WENT INTO TERRIFIC LOW BACK SPASMS WAS FIRST CLUE OF SEVERE BACK PROBLEMS TO COME. FIRST TIME I STAYED HOME FOR 2 DAYS WITH THE HEATING PAD GLUED TO MY BACK.

16 Y/O: (1974) DISLOCATED LEFT PATELLAE, THROWING KICKS WITHOUT KARATE LESSONS. X-RAYS SHOWED SPURS BEHIND PATELLAE { FIRST CLUE TO HAVING POOR CALCIUM REABSORPTION ABILITIES).

DURING AGES OF 14 TO 16 Y/O: FIRST CLUE THAT I HAD A RESPIRATORY PROBLEM. MY MOM ON SEVERAL OCCASIONS OVER A 2 YEAR PERIOD SAID SHE HEARD ME WHEEZING FOR LONG PERIODS OF TIMES DURING THE NIGHT. SHE SAID WHEEZE, NOT SNORE. THERE WAS NO DOOR BETWEEN MY BEDROOM AND HERS. SHE NEVER HEARD IT AFTER THAT TIME FRAME.

17.5 Y/O: FATHER DIED – (AGE 60) FROM A RARE BLOOD DISEASE, _NOT LEUKEMIA_. THIS WAS MONTHS AFTER I GRADUATED HIGH SCHOOL -NO ONE IN MY FAMILY WOULD TELL ME THE SPECIFICS. SAMPLES OF BLOOD AND TISSUES SENT TO THE MEDICAL RESEARCH FOUNDATION.

SOMETIME AFTER THIS, I WENT TO THE DOCTOR TO LOSE WEIGHT. HE PRESCRIBED TO ME AMPHETAMINE-LIKE PILLS AND IT WAS EFFORTLESS. STARTED HANGING OUT WITH WRONG CROWD DUE TO NO FATHER FIGURE. EXPERIMENTED WITH WEED. WEIGHT CAME BACK. DOCTOR COULD NOT PRESCRIBE THOSE PILLS ANYMORE DUE TO LAW CHANGE AND I USED OVERCOUNTER DIET PILLS PSEUDO-EPHEDRINE. THEY WORKED ALMOST AS WELL AND I LOST MY WEIGHT AGAIN.

21 Y/O: (1979) I WENT TO A JUNIOR COLLEGE SHORTLY AFTER HIGH SCHOOL AND ALMOST FINISHED A 2 YEAR DEGREE, THEN I LANDED MY FIRST REAL JOB. I WAS ABOUT 21, COLLEGE HELPED ME GET THE JOB. IT WAS AT THIS TIME; I BECAME SOCIALLY WITHDRAWN AND LIVED WITH MOM AND BOUGHT A HOME GYM TO TOUGHEN UP. THIS JOB LASTED UNTIL ABOUT 1983 AND IN ‘82’ I DEVELOPED A CALCIUM OXALATE KIDNEY STONE, YET ANOTHER CLUE OF POOR CALCIUM REABSORPTION.

25 TO 28 Y/O: (1983 – 1986) I WAS A POSTAL CARRIER. TWO SURGERIES DURING THIS TIME 1.) CORRECTED DEVIATED SEPTUM WHICH DID NOTHING EXCEPT TO LEAVE SCAR TISSUE AND ALLOWING MUCOUS TO DRY OUT OVER THE SCAR TO STOP UP MY NIGHTIME BREATHING DURING ALLERGY MONTHS- TO THIS DAY IT STILL HAPPENS 2.) THE SECOND SURGERY WAS REMOVING CALCIUM SPURS FROM MY INNER EAR, AGAIN YET ANOTHER CLUE ABOUT ME AND CALCIUM.

29 YO: BEGAN PHYSICAL THERAPY COLLEGE. APPROXIMATELY 9 TO 10 MONTHS IN, I HURT MY BACK. INSTRUCTOR USED ME TO TEACH A CLASS USING ROBIN MCKENZIE , (A FAMOUS NEW ZEALAND P.T.) EVALUATION TECHNIQUES. HE TOLD ME THAT I HAD A DISC DERANGEMENT NUMBER OR GRADE 3. 2ND BIG CLUE THAT I HAD A ‘BAD BACK’ AND 2 INSTRUCTORS GAVE ME THEIR CONDOLENCES. I DID A LOT OF JOB HOPPING AS I HAD TO FIND THE RIGHT PROGRAM FOR MY BACK. AFTER THE COLLEGE BACK EPISODE THE SPASMS BECAME MORE FREQUENT.

44Y/0: 2001, LET MYSELF BE TALKED INTO BRACES FOR CROOKED TEETH, I HATED THOSE. IT WAS A NEGATIVE EXPERIENCE FROM THE START. SEVERAL TEETH WERE EXTRACTED.

45Y/O: 2002: BREATHING PROBLEMS RETURNED WITH A VENGENCE; WE BOUGHT A HOUSE WITH BLACK MOLD IN IT. I WENT TO A PCP AND HE ORDERED A BREATHING CHAMBER TEST. RESPIRATORY TECH TOLD ME I HAD A BARREL CHEST INDICATIVE OF ASTHMA. THE TEST ITSELF SHOWED POOR READINGS AND HE DIAGNOSED ME WITH COPD AND GAVE ME SPIRIVA FOR 4 OR 5 YEARS WHICH REALLY DID NOTHING. I FOUGHT MANY YEARS TO GET THAT COPD DX REMOVED FROM RECORDS, BECAUSE I COULD DO 45 MINUTES ON MY NORDICTRAK SKI MACHINE WITHOUT PROBLEMS

48Y/O: 2005 LEFT KNEE ARTHROSCOPY FOR TORN MENISCUS THEN I READ A YEAR LATER DON’T GET THIS BECAUSE THE STATISTICS BEAR OUT YOU WILL GET A TOTAL KNEE IN 10 TO 15 YEARS. I ONLY HAD MEDIOCRE RESULTS WITH ARTHROSCOPY, SO A DECADE LATER WHEN IT WAS STARTING TO BOTHER ME AGAIN I WENT FOR A PLATLET RICH PLASMA (PRP) ABOUT 2014 AND THAT PUT AN END TO MY PROBLEMS.

50Y/O: IN 2007 I HAD A FEW LARGE MOLES REMOVED. THE DERMATOLOGIST BUTCHERED MY BACK AS MY WIFE AND OTHERS HAVE TOLD ME WHEN I TAKE OFF MY SHIRT.

52 Y/O: 2009 - SKIN CANCER RIGHT UPPER FOREHEAD. THEY DID A MOHS.

52 Y/O: SAME YEAR DID A COLONOSCOPY, FEW POLYPS, HEMORRHOIDS, DIVERTICULOSIS NOT DIVERTICULITIS……

56Y/O: 2013 WHILE LIFTING BOXES IN ATTIC, I PERFORMED THE TECHNIQUE WITH TERRIBLE BODY MECHANICS. BULDGED DISC ALL PARTS OF SPINE BUT CERVICAL WAS THE WORSE. VERY SERIOUS AND MADE MY RIGHT UPPER EXTREMITY GO VERY WEAK COULD NOT EVEN TURN A KEY IN A DOORKNOB, THE ARM AND HAND RECOVERED IN ABOUT 3 MONTHS. ( I INCLUDED THE MRI RESULTS FROM 2013.) A LATER X-RAY FOR MY THORACIC SHOWS FURTHER DETERIATION STRESS LIKE FRACTURE IN VERTEBRAE AND FACET JOINTS. THIS AREA, THE THORACIC LOCKED UP ON ME OFTEN AND A CHIROPRACTOR ADJUSTMENT WOULD ALWAYS GET ME THROUGH. REGARDING MY RIGHT UPPER EXTREMITY, IT STILL SHOWS REMNANAT SIGNS OF THAT TIME PERIOD. AS AN EXAMPLE, WHEN I WOULD WORK ON SOMETHING MANUALLY LIKE CHANGE SPARK PLUGS, WHICH REQUIRES AWKWARD BODY MECHANICS MY RIGHT-HAND THUMB ADDUCTORS WOULD CONTRACT AND I WOULD NEED MY LEFT HAND TO FREE THE RIGHT THUMB FROM THE INDEX FINGER. LAST SUMMER OF 2024, I HAD JUST FINISHED A FEW OF YOUR PODCAST HAVING DISCOVERED YOU IN MAY AND DECIDED TO GO SAVAGE. I WORKED ON MY DYI, LIFTING HEAVY PAVER STONES FOR A WALKWAY. I ALSO WAS SHIRT OFF (30 MINUTES AT A TIME) AND NO GLASSES FOR MOST OF THE TIME. I COULD SEE SLIGHT IMPROVEMENTS OVER THE COURSE OF THE SUMMER, EXCEPT THE CONTRACTURES IN THE THUMB ADDUCTORS WERE GETTING MORE AND MORE FREQUENT AND EVEN AFTER I FINISHED THE DIY PROJECTS IN OCTOBER IT WAS END OF NOVEMBER OR EARLY DECEMBER BEFORE THE CONTRACTURES SLOWED DOWN TO FEW AND FAR BETWEEN. THEY STILL HAPPEN ON OCCASION BUT 2 TO 3 WEEKS GO BY IN BETWEEN.

64YO: DECEMBER 2022 FELL AND HURT MY LEFT HIP AND IT WAS JUST BRUISED AND GOT BETTER, BUT A DEXA SCAN WAS PERFORMED AND IT SHOWED OSTEOPENIA (POOR CALCIUM UTILIZATION), PECULIARLY, I STARTED HURTING IN THE RIGHT KNEE AND HIP SHORTLY AFTER THIS.

65 YO: FEB 2023 CALCIUM SCORE OFF THE CHARTS. I WAS SENT TO A SPECIALIST AND HE DID A PARA-THYROIDECTOMY ON THE LEFT LOWER LOBE. POST SURGERY THE CALCIUM LEVEL WENT BACK TO NORMAL. (POOR CALCIUM UTILIZATION)

67 YO: (MARCH 2025) EYE DOCTOR TRIED TALKING ME INTO CATARACTS SURGERY ASAP. I SAID I WANT TO GIVE IT A YEAR. SHE TALKS JUST LIKE A CENTRALIZED MEDICINE DOCTOR. I AM HOPEFUL YOU CAN HELP BECAUSE YOU SAID IN A RECENT BLOG (3.17.25) THAT IN YOUR DECENTRALIZED MEDICINE SERIES COMING UP YOU MAY HAVE SOME COMMENTS.

**_I WANT TO SUMMARIZE THE MAJOR TOPICS AND ALSO ASK A QUESTION OR TWO_**.

BREATHING PROBLEMS: 1.) WHEEZING INTERMITTANT TO THIS DAY. 2) SOB EPISODE DESCRIBED BEST BY STATING THAT NO MATTER HOW DEEP I INHALE, I DON’T FEEL LIKE I AM GETTING NOURISHING AIR INTAKE. 3.) POSTURAL RESTORATION CLINICIAN TOLD ME I WAS STUCK IN EXTENSION; SHE SAW ME TWICE, BUT WE LIVE 150 MILES APART AND LITTLE BY LITTLE I QUIT THE EXERCISES AND GOING TO SEE HER, I STILL WOULD GET MILD SOB BOUTS DESPITE 5-6 WEEKS OF DAILY EXERCISE PRACTICES. ARE THERE ANY SUGGESTIONS THAT I COULD DO IN A DECENTRALIZED WORLD. (**_I JUST WANT TO ADD A LITTLE SIDE NOTE THAT MY FIRST 2 JOBS REQUIRED A CHEST X-RAY TO CATCH TB AND BOTH HOSPITALS SAID I HAD A FLAT , NOT DOME SHAPED DIAPHRAGM; PRI THERAPIST SAID I HAD FLARED RIBS, COINCIDES WITH RESPIRATORY TECH REMARKING ABOUT A BARREL CHEST_**.

MY SPINE IS DETERIORATING. SO, I WONDER IF I SHOULD GET A CERVICAL FUSION SURGERY OR HOLD OUT AS LONG AS I CAN --THE TRIGGER POINT IN MY RIGHT UPPER TRAPEZIUS IS GETTING TO BE MORE FREQUENT AND INTENSE, AND I GET BURNING SENSATIONS NEAR THE RHOMBOIDS ON THE LEFT AND ELSEWHERE. IS THERE ANYTHING I CAN DO ABOUT THIS FROM A DECENTRALIZED PERSPECTIVE?

CALCIUM REABSORPTION PROBLEMS. AFTER WATCHING SEVERAL OF YOUR PODCAST, I AM SURE THERE IS A SOLUTION PARTIAL OR COMPLETE REVERSAL?

**_MENTAL OR COGNITIVE DECLINE_** : WORRIED ABOUT GOING TO PHYSICAL THERAPY SCHOOL DUE TO MY LONG HISTORY OF MEDIOCRE CONCENTRATION/ DISTRACTING THOUGHTS. I HAD TO LISTEN TO SOFT MUSIC OR NO SOUND IN ORDER TO STUDY AND I WOULD READ AND RE-READ TO GET A SMALL PASSAGE INTEGRATED IN MY THOUGHTS – THIS KEPT ME FROM BEING A GREAT CLINICIAN, BECAUSE I WOULD NOT RETAIN THINGS LONG TERM AND HAVE TO GO BACK TO REVIEW THINGS FREQUENTLY AND WHEN I WAS CHALLENGED BY A PATIENT REGARDING WHAT I SAID OR FROM ANOTHER CLINICIAN, THEN I WOULD BECOME QUIET AND NOT ASSERT MYSELF. I HOPE TO FIX THIS, IF POSSIBLE, I WANT MY BRAIN TO WORK BETTER THAN ANY PREVIOUS PART OF MY LIFE- I WOULD LIKE TO GO BACK TO SCHOOL AND GET MY TRANSITIONAL PHD DEGREE IN P.T. OR SOMETHING ELSE THAT I HAVE BEEN THINKING ABOUT THAT WOULD ALSO NEED MY BRAIN FUNTIONING A MAXIMUM CAPACITY.

CATARACTS I HOPE THERE WOULD BE A SOLUTION , WE HAVE A YEAR UNTIL MY RETURN TO EYE DOCTOR. IF THIS CAN BE MADE BETTER, WOULD YOU HAVE A TIMELINE, OR IS EVERYONE DIFFERENT?

SKIN: PATCHY PSORIASIS OR ECZEMA WOULD I PUT ANYTHING ON THIS OR JUST KEEP IT WASHED AND BAKE IT IN THE MORNING AND AFTERNOON SUN. IS THERE AN EXPECTED TIMELINE OR IS EVERYONE DIFFERENT?

CHEST PAINS: AFTER LAST SUMMER’S IINTENSE LIFTING OF PAVERS TONES, I HAD MINOR CHEST PAINS THAT WOULD COME AND GO FOR MONTHS. NOW THEY ARE RETREATING, BUT I STILL HAVE INCREASED PAIN ON THE LEFT NIPPLE, IS THIS SOMETHING TO BE CONCERENED WITH?

I AM GOING TO PURCHASE A CONSULT, BUT I THINK YOU SAID IN TWO DIFFERENT POW WOWS THAT I NEED TO WRITE A JOURNEL THEN PURCHASE A CONSULT. THIS JOURNEL IS VERY LONG AND I HOPE I DON’T ANGER SOMEONE BY PUTTING THIS IN THE FORUM UNDER MY NAME.

I HAVE RANDOM ELECTRICAL SHOCKS THAT I GET AT NIGHT AND THEY CAN BE NIGHTLY FOR A WEEK OF A FEW DAYS OR WEEKS. THE PAINIS THE SIZE OF A IN HEAD AND VERY INTENSE PAIN OF ABOUT 7 ON 0 TO 10 SCALE AND ONLY LST FRO A SECOND. I INVESTIGATED SEVERAL TIMES IF IT WAS A BUG BITE, BUT NOTHING SHOWS UP ON SKIN. COULD THIS BE A PRECURSOR TO NEUROPATHY. I TAKE I TABLET OF GABAPENTIN AT BEDTIME FOR RESTLESS LEG SYNDROME, DESPITE READING THAT THIS MEDICATION IS NOT GOOD FOR YOU.

**_MEDICATIONS_**: 25 MG COREG (HTN); FLEXERIL 10MG (NECK SPASMS); GABAPENTIN 600 MG TABLET (RESTLESS LEG SYNDROME) MY ACUPUNCTURE DOCTOR SAYS GAVE ME HERBALL PILLS AND SAID HTN IS SOMETIMES A CNS PROBLEM AND THE PILLS ALLOWED ME TO QUIT LOSARTAN AND A 2ND COREG IN THE AFTERNOON. STOPPED TAKING LOSARTAN, OMEPRAZOLE, FLONASE. REPLACING FLONASE WITH XCLEAR (HAS XYLITOL) I TAKE VIT K AND D FROM LIFE EXTENSION AND MANY OTHER VITAMINS, WHICH I WILL DISCLOSE IN MY CONSULTATION

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-21T23:39:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/don-greenwells-journal.30511/post-349068>

This story screams environmental and circadian mismatch: prenatal smoke exposure, early osteomyelitis, blue light-heavy modern life, and a family legacy of chronic disease = transgenerational epigenetics causing a lot of mtDNA mutations. At 38°N, Oklahoma City gets decent sunlight (about 5-6 peak sun hours daily). Still, your indoor habits and tech exposure likely skew your light diet toward artificial blue, disrupting melatonin and leptin signaling. Your calcium issues (osteopenia, spurs, kidney stones) suggest poor vitamin D metabolism or parathyroid dysfunction (confirmed by your 2023 surgery). Spine and breathing problems hint at structural and autonomic nervous system stress (PVN = Brain Gut 16 blog), possibly from early trauma or mtDNA mutations in the MS system.

1.  Morning Sun + Protein Reset
    -   What: At sunrise (around 6:30-7 AM now in March 2025), go outside, shirtless if possible, no glasses, for 20-600 minutes. Face east, let UVB hit your eyes and skin GROUNDED. Then eat 50-75g protein (e.g., eggs, bacon, beef) with <50g carbs, high fat (butter, ghee) within 30 minutes of waking.
    -   Why: Resets circadian clock via melanopsin, boosts vitamin D, primes leptin sensitivity. Your latitude supports this year-round, though winter UVB is weaker.
    -   Your Twist: This counters your blue light overload, leaning into being with nature’s rhythm. You are a serious tech abuser.
2.  Dark Evenings
    -   What: After sunset (around 6:30-7 PM), block blue light—use red bulbs (e.g., G40 incandescent) or blue-blocker glasses. No screens 2-3 hours before bed (aim for 9-10 PM sleep).
    -   Why: Protects melatonin, syncs leptin, reduces CNS overstimulation. Your electric shocks and restless legs might ease here.
    -   Your Twist: A being practice—unplug from doing (tech) to rest.
3.  Meal Timing
    -   What: Two meals—breakfast post-sun, dinner 4-5 hours before bed (e.g., 4-5 PM). No snacking or supplements.
    -   Why: Stabilizes insulin and leptin, supports liver redox overnight due to apoptosis repair. Your bowel urgency might calm with less gut stress -----> nnEMF opens the Gut brain barrier.

* * *

**Specific Recommendations by Concern**

1\. Breathing Problems (Wheezing, SOB, Flat Diaphragm)

-   Circumstances: Wheezing since 14-16, SOB bouts, barrel chest, flat diaphragm, flared ribs—likely from prenatal smoke, mold exposure, and posture (stuck in extension).
-   Recs:
    -   Morning Grounding: Stand barefoot on grass during sun exposure for 20 minutes. Inhale deeply through your nose and exhale slowly—aim for 5-6 second cycles. This builds diaphragm strength and helps increase NO production which you lack. This blocks stem cells from Becker's current and it sets you up for eye disease and sexual issues and high BP.
    -   Cold Exposure: Post-sun, splash face/chest with cold water (30-60 seconds). Stimulates vagus nerve, may reduce wheezing.
    -   Posture Fix: Lie on back, knees bent, feet flat, 10 minutes daily. Place a small towel roll under mid-back to open chest, counter extension. Pair with nasal breathing Tape your mouth. You have an oxygen allergy ----->

    -   Decentralized Angle: Ditch centralized asthma meds (Spiriva didn’t help). More sun on head and neck and chest is a good swap for Flonase—keep it. Test raw dairy (if tolerated) for lung-supporting fats. If you cannot tolerate it you know gut barrier is wide open.
-   Why: Realigns autonomic tone at PVN, leverages sunlight’s anti-inflammatory effects. Your flat diaphragm suggests poor mechanics, not just COPD. COPD = Oxygen Allergy

2\. Spine Deterioration (Cervical, Thoracic, Back Spasms)

-   Circumstances: Disc derangement, bulging discs, thoracic stress fractures, trigger points—starting at 13, worsened by lifting and posture all point to life long low MS redox. Sun and grounding key.
-   Recs:
    -   Sun + Movement: Post-sun, walk barefoot 15-20 minutes, loose spine, no shoes. Add gentle McKenzie extensions (5 reps, hold 5 seconds) if pain-free.
    -   Magnesium: via a piece of 90-100% dark chocolate a night. Swap Flexeril (CNS depressant) for PBM reduces spasms, aids calcium metabolism.
    -   Cervical Fusion?: Hold off. Surgery locks you into centralized dogma. Try 6 months of this first—your 2013 recovery suggests resilience.
    -   Decentralized Angle: Red light therapy (10-15 min daily on neck/back) via a cheap 600-1000 nm panel. Boosts mitochondrial repair, eases trigger points.
-   Why: Sun and grounding reduce inflammation; magnesium and red light support tissue repair. Your being focus fits—less invasive, more natural.

3\. Calcium Reabsorption (Osteopenia, Stones, Spurs)

-   Circumstances: Lifelong issue, parathyroidectomy fixed blood levels, but utilization lags—tied to low D, poor K2, or gut absorption due to open gut barrier and vagus nerve is shot.
-   Recs:
    -   Sun + D3/K2: 30 min sun daily, supplement 5,000 IU D3 + 100-200 mcg K2 (MK-7) with fatty meals. Your Life Extension combo’s solid—keep it for now not foreever.
    -   Bone Broth via the Survivor soup Patreon blog: 1 cup daily—collagen and minerals for bones, gut healing for absorption.
    -   Avoid calcium pills: Focus on food (sardines, cheese) and sun pills, which risk stones.
    -   Decentralized Angle: Test serum 25(OH)D (aim for 50-80 ng/mL) via home kit, not doc. Adjust D3 based on results.
-   Why: Sun and K2 shuttle calcium to bones, not arteries/soft tissue. You might consider a Calcium index score because you have serious risk for MI. Partial reversal likely in 6-12 months; full depends on consistency.

4\. Mental/Cognitive Decline (Concentration, Memory)

-   Circumstances: Mediocre focus since youth, worsened by stress, meds, and blue light.
-   Recs:
    -   Sun + DHA: Morning sun, add 1-2g DHA (fish oil or cod liver oil) daily. Rewires brain via BDNF and omega-3s.
    -   No Blue Light: Strict evening cutoff. Read paper books under red light, daylight computer best—your being antidote to screen-driven doing.
    -   Cold + Silence: Post-sun cold exposure, then 10 min silence (no music). Retrains focus.
    -   Decentralized Angle: Drop gabapentin (neurotoxic long-term). PBM therapy is way better and not toxic.
-   Why: Sun and DHA rebuild neurons; silence cuts noise. PhD potential in 12-18 months if consistent—your brain’s not done yet.

5\. Cataracts: Big blog coming soon on that one.

-   Circumstances: Centralized Doc is pushing surgery, and you’ve got a year to stall.
-   Recs:
    -   Sun + Antioxidants: Morning sun (no glasses), 500-1000 mg vitamin C + survivor soup blog.
    -   Blue Light Block: Evening glasses mandatory—cataracts worsen with oxidative stress from screens.
    -   Decentralized Angle: You could try N-acetylcarnosine eye drops (1-2 drops, 2x daily)—some studies show lens clearing. Source online, not Rx.
-   Why: Sun boosts eye antioxidants; drops may dissolve glycation. Timeline: 6-12 months for stabilization, reversal rare but possible.

6\. Skin (Psoriasis/Eczema)

-   Circumstances: Patchy, likely inflammation-driven.
-   Recs:
    -   Sun: 20-30 min morning, 20 min afternoon (no burn). No soap on patches—just water.
    -   Fatty Acids: Increase ghee, fish, beef tallow in diet. Drop seed oils (canola, soy).
    -   Decentralized Angle: Test raw honey (dab on patches)—anti-inflammatory, no Rx needed.

7\. Chest Pains (Left Nipple, Post-Lifting)

-   Circumstances: Lingering since 2024 paver work, high calcium score history = PAD.

    -   Sun + K2: As above—K2 clears arterial calcium. Check with ECG if pain spikes (unlikely stent-related after 60+ years).
    -   Magnesium: Nightly dose—relaxes chest muscles, cuts angina risk. You might benefit from MB but cannot tell based on this journal
    -   Decentralized Angle: Grounding + slow breathing during pain. Avoid centralized “stent-first” panic.
-   Why: Likely musculoskeletal from lifting, not cardiac—stents were neonatal. Monitor, but don’t rush to ER.

8\. Electric Shocks (Nightly, Random)

-   Circumstances: Intense, fleeting, no skin marks—CNS or nerve irritation?
-   Recs:
    -   Dark + Grounding: Full dark sleep, barefoot grounding daily. Drop gabapentin—may paradoxically worsen nerve firing.
    -   B12: Test methylcobalamin because B12 deficiency mimics this and blue destroys B12.

-   Why: Circadian reset and B12 calm nerves. Not neuropathy yet—precursor possible. Test 3 months.

* * *

Meds and Supplements

-   Keep: Coreg (25 mg HTN)—taper if BP drops with acupuncture. But BP is up due to NO being gone. Fits with PAD and MS issues
-   Ditch: Flexeril (swap magnesium), gabapentin (swap B12 + grounding), omeprazole (gut heals with survivor soup broth and nnEMF cut).
