# Thread: Symbicort, Collagen Breakdown & Sun Sensitivity – Workarounds for my Asthma?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symbicort-collagen-breakdown-sun-sensitivity-%E2%80%93-workarounds-for-my-asthma.30522/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bennyhoffman — 2025-03-20T21:36:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symbicort-collagen-breakdown-sun-sensitivity-%E2%80%93-workarounds-for-my-asthma.30522/post-348979>

Hey everyone,

I recently developed new-onset asthma after inhaling construction dust/silica dust for about a month. My airways became inflamed, and a high-resolution CT (HRCT) showed minimal bronchial thickening and mild airways disease. My doctor prescribed Symbicort (Budesonide/Formoterol), and I’ve been researching its long-term effects, particularly how it may impact collagen breakdown and sun sensitivity.

Budesonide is a potent glucocorticoid steroid that reduces inflammation by suppressing immune responses and inhibiting cytokine and collagen production in tissues. Mechanistically, corticosteroids affect the skin by inhibiting fibroblast activity and collagen synthesis, leading to thinning of the dermal layer. Over time, this results in skin atrophy – making the skin thinner, less elastic, and more prone to tearing or bruising (Source 1). Biopsies have confirmed that inhaled budesonide reduces levels of procollagen after just a few weeks of use, raising concerns about its potential long-term effects on skin integrity and UV resistance.

Since sunlight is a huge part of the protocols Jack Kruse recommends, I’m trying to quantify how significant this issue might be and what potential workarounds exist. My main questions are:

1.  Does continued use of Symbicort weaken the skin’s natural resistance to sunburn, and is there a way to counteract this?
2.  Can building up melanin (or other UV adaptations) help offset the potential damage from the inhaler?
3.  If I need to stay on this medication long-term, how do I balance it with optimal sun exposure for mitochondrial health?

Would love to hear any insights from people who understand the intersection of corticosteroids, collagen degradation, and UV resilience. If I have to be on this medication for the rest of my life, I want to figure out the best strategy to mitigate any unintended consequences while still maximizing my sun exposure benefits.

Thanks in advance for any thoughts!

\---

Sources:
1) Rademaker, M. _“Inhaled Corticosteroids – Watch for Skin Atrophy.”_Prescriber Update 25(1):6-7, 2004. (Medsafe, NZ) – Discusses skin thinning from inhaled steroids and additive effects of sun exposure -> [https://www.medsafe.govt.nz/profs/puarticles/atrophy.htm#:~:text=A number of studies2 ,,800 mcg/day.6](https://www.medsafe.govt.nz/profs/puarticles/atrophy.htm#:~:text=A%20number%20of%20studies2%20%2C,800%20mcg%2Fday.6)

2) PDR (Physician’s Desk Reference) – _Advair Diskus (fluticasone/salmeterol) Drug Summary_ – Lists rare photosensitivity as an adverse effect (incidence unknown) for an ICS/LABA inhaler, implying steroid-related skin reactions. -> [https://www.pdr.net/drug-summary/?d...yed / Incidence,Delayed / Incidence not known](https://www.pdr.net/drug-summary/?drugLabelId=Advair-Diskus-fluticasone-propionate-salmeterol-166#:~:text=photosensitivity%20%2F%20Delayed%20%2F%20Incidence,Delayed%20%2F%20Incidence%20not%20known)

### bennyhoffman — 2025-03-20T21:44:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symbicort-collagen-breakdown-sun-sensitivity-%E2%80%93-workarounds-for-my-asthma.30522/post-348980>

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) would you be able to answer this kind of question in a consultation? I’m definitely going to be scheduling one at some point this summer, but I’d love to hear any preliminary thoughts on this topic if you have any. Is this a true concern?

I started taking Symbicort, and I’ve been building my solar callus fro about 2 weeks, but I burned my shoulder the other day after being outside from 3 PM to 4 PM in UV Index 5.5 in Pittsburgh, despite gradually increasing my exposure for weeks. Really strange. My skin also feels drier, and I’ve been experiencing fatigue/tiredness—possibly from the inhaler.

I really hope I can reverse this early-stage small airways disease with sunlight. You wouldn’t happen to have any blog posts on this particular disorder, would you? My pulmonologist diagnosed me with asthma, but my small airways disease is still in the early stages and not very advanced. Would love any additional insights you might have on this.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-20T22:14:06+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/symbicort-collagen-breakdown-sun-sensitivity-%E2%80%93-workarounds-for-my-asthma.30522/post-348983>

Yes. Asthma is always related to lack of sun and/or too much nnEMF.

# Thread: Perimenopause, breast cancer, dystonia
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/perimenopause-breast-cancer-dystonia.30524/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 18
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### michellejorge — 2025-03-21T12:28:42+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/perimenopause-breast-cancer-dystonia.30524/post-349037>

Help with Improving perimenopausal symptoms and I formerly had hormone dependent breast cancer-

Hi, I’m 46 and have been experiencing perimenopause and haven’t had a period since early January. I had breast cancer a couple years ago. Stage one, ERP positive
her two negative that was surgically removed and then radiation. I need something to help me with my perimenopause symptoms particularly bc it’s aggravating a condition I have called dystonia and it’s really declined affecting new parts of my body and the pain is keeping me up at night. ### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-30T23:39:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/perimenopause-breast-cancer-dystonia.30524/post-349695>

Modern Extinction Event is what is defining your life. That is why a loss or fecundity is tied to a breast cancer and why the next place you'll see damage is in the CNS. It is an energy hog and you are losing function there because your system is WARBURG shifted. But you are not reading the blogs much less putting the lessons together.

The combined effects of reduced UVA exposure, circadian disruption, and metabolic dysregulation suggest that humans may be in a modern extinction event, driven by environmental changes that undermine ancient light-dependent adaptations:

-   Decreased Fecundity and Fertility: The decline in sex steroid production (due to impaired CYP activity) and dopamine dysregulation (leading to ejaculatory dysfunction) directly reduces reproductive success. Global fertility rates are declining, with sperm counts dropping by over 50% in the past 50 years (studies up to 2023). _**This aligns with the evolutionary narrative: nature prioritizes survival over reproduction, as seen in menopause (mentioned in the thesis), and modern light environments are triggering a similar shift by suppressing fertility = perimenopause now. Why did you have a hormone sensitive Breast CA? The heme protein CYP controls sex steroid hormones and your environment destroyed all your heme proteins. Guess why? nnEMF. No sun.**_
-   Intracellular Hypoxia and mtDNA Damage: The thesis notes that circadian misalignment causes “intracellular hypoxia” and “quantum timing loss” in mtDNA, accelerating mutations. Reduced mTOR activity (from lack of UVA light) impairs mitochondrial biogenesis, exacerbating intracellular hypoxia. This damages mtDNA in gametes (sperm and oocytes), reducing their viability and contributing to infertility.
-   Neuropsin and mTOR Suppression: Neuropsin’s role in sensing UVA light (380 nm) is critical for mTOR activation and dopamine synthesis. Modern light environments (e.g., blue-heavy artificial light, lack of UVA) suppress neuropsin signaling, reducing mTOR activity and dopamine production. This disrupts not only ejaculation but also broader reproductive physiology, including libido, gamete quality, and embryonic development.
-   Evolutionary Parallel to K-T Extinction: The K-T event caused darkness, suppressing light-driven pathways and shifting metabolism to Warburg-like glycolysis. Modern sunlight avoidance mimics this darkness, reducing UVA exposure and reactivating glycolysis at the expense of OXPHOS. This metabolic regression, combined with dopamine dysregulation and impaired sex steroid synthesis, mirrors the stress responses of the K-T extinction, suggesting a modern “extinction event” driven by environmental light disruption breast CA and dystonia.
-   **Heme Proteins and Sex Steroids:** The slide below notes that dopamine synthesis is “highly oxygenated” and “augmented by hemoglobin oxidation state.” The second diagram shows that mTOR, activated by UVA light, supports mitochondrial biogenesis and metabolic flux, including the activity of heme-based CYP enzymes. CYP enzymes are critical for synthesizing sex steroids (e.g., testosterone, estrogen) from cholesterol. This is why your cancer had its hormone signal. I knew what it meant right away and your doctors just are drooling on themselves trying to make sense of this. Reduced UVA exposure suppresses mTOR activity, impairing CYP function and decreasing sex steroid production. This leads to reduced fertility (lower sperm quality, ovulatory dysfunction) and fecundity (decreased reproductive capacity). Hemoglobin oxidation state changes in CYP and that is why it happens.
-

-

-

**Integrated Narrative: From GOE to Modern Extinction**
Let’s weave the dopamine circuitry findings into the evolutionary framework, highlighting how environmental light changes are affecting sex, fertility, and fecundity:

-   GOE (2.4 Billion Years Ago): Hypoxia and cooling favored the Warburg metabolism, with melanin degradation into L-DOPA providing dopamine for stress responses. Early mTOR-like pathways regulated growth but were not yet light-sensitive.
-   Evolution of Complex Life (Post-GOE): Neuropsin evolved, linking UVA light (380 nm) to mTOR and dopamine synthesis. Dopamine began regulating reward and movement, setting the stage for sexual behavior in complex organisms.
-   K-T Extinction (66 Million Years Ago): Darkness suppressed neuropsin and mTOR, reducing dopamine synthesis and shifting metabolism to glycolysis. Hypoxia increased catecholamine production, prioritizing survival over reproduction.
-   Post-K-T Mammalian Evolution: UVA light reactivated mTOR and dopamine pathways, supporting mitochondrial efficiency, circadian rhythms, and sexual behavior. Rhythmic dopamine in the vsNAc (modulated by acetylcholine) optimized ejaculation timing, enhancing fertility and fecundity in diurnal environments.
-   Primates and Humans: Dopamine in the vsNAc, driven by UVA light via neuropsin and mTOR, fine-tuned sexual behavior and supported sex steroid synthesis via CYP enzymes. This underpinned the evolution of complex social and reproductive behaviors.
-   Modern Extinction Event (Today): Reduced UVA exposure (from ALAN, indoor living) suppresses neuropsin, mTOR, and dopamine synthesis, leading to:
    -   Quick Ejaculation: Dysregulated dopamine rhythmicity in the vsNAc causes premature ejaculation, reducing reproductive success.
    -   Decreased Fertility and Fecundity: Impaired mTOR activity reduces CYP function, lowering sex steroid production and gamete quality. Intracellular hypoxia and mtDNA damage further exacerbate infertility.
    -   Circadian Misalignment: Disrupted circadian clocks (via PER, CRY) impair dopamine and metabolic regulation, contributing to reproductive dysfunction.
    -   Metabolic Regression: A shift to Warburg metabolism reduces ATP efficiency in gametes, impairing sperm motility and oocyte maturation.

**Predictions and Implications**

-   Reproductive Health Crisis: The decline in fertility and fecundity, driven by light disruption, suggests a modern extinction event. That is why your period is OVER. Restoring natural light exposure (e.g., increasing UVA at 380 nm) could reactivate neuropsin, mTOR, and dopamine pathways, improving ejaculation timing, sex steroid synthesis, and gamete quality. You have not changed a damn thing in your life. If you did you would not have dystonia now.
-   The question of _why_ normal host cells in your CNS/PNS have dystonia is obvious. As your heteroplasmy in tissue already destroyed by CA and menopause increases it creates a pseudoinfection of piece and part of mitochondria affecting your immune reponse. Why? People with cancer and broken heme proteins (cytochrome c oxidase) cannot make water and they cannot particiipate in apoptosis to avoid cancer so they get it more easily than one would expect. Also explains why the cancer is hormone sensitive because CYP is another heme protein. ALl of these things are OBVIOUS SIGNS to a decentralized clinicians that you have been living in Chernobyl for decades blissfully ignorant. In patients with cancer (like you) ANY OTHER chronic inflammatory conditions (like mtDNA colony failure) cn adapt a pathological Warburg-like metabolism. This is ONLY a matter of debate in the centralized circles who are ignorant of the evolution of heme proteins in evolution that define all these findings.
-   This review/hypothesis piece explores how intracellular infection can contribute to this Warburg metabolism or related pathological metabolic states. They detail molecular mechanisms by which viral, bacterial, and protozoan intracellular pathogens can induce, or contribute to, a Warburg-like metabolism in infected host cells in order to meet their own replication and nutritional needs. It also discuss how host defense towards infection may impact cellular metabolic changes. That is you sister. [https://wap.hapres.com/htmls/IJ\_1341\_Detail.html](https://wap.hapres.com/htmls/IJ_1341_Detail.html)
-   Therapeutic Interventions: Light therapy at 380 nm could enhance mTOR activation and dopamine synthesis, addressing ejaculatory dysfunction and infertility. Targeting acetylcholine-dopamine interactions in the vsNAc (as shown in the research) may also offer treatments for sexual dysfunction.
-   Environmental Solutions: Reducing ALAN and promoting natural light exposure could re-synchronize circadian rhythms, mTOR activity, and dopamine signaling, mitigating the reproductive and metabolic consequences of modern light environments.

How do we know you cannot use the TCA cycle? Cause you live in a blue lit nnEMF shit hole that blocks you from using the TCA cycle. This is why this paper exists and none of the centralize food gurus or biochemists have a clue why you have the life you have.

Why? Sunrise is loaded with red light to regenerate the heme proteins you destroyed with your light choices in you life. YES, you dis this and you need to change the environment because you cannot get well in the same environment you got sick in. I've said it for 20 years but you and many like you DONT LISTEN.

Nature is giving you diseases to wake your ass up. But you want answer and you won't move your feet to move..........YOU NEED TO MOVE OR YOUR GOING TO DIE WITH A HORRIBLE functional neuro deficit from the dystonia.

Study the slide above. Since influenza is an electrical disease that should get many scientist thinking so endosymbiosis was an electrical disease whose force caused the first domains to join forces to create heme proteins to protect itself from the electical stimulus of oxygen infusion to the environment. I wonder when they will wake up to that reality? Oxygen changes the electrical resistance of everything with a membrane. And oxygen is paramagnetic. Big deal folks in the story of heme biology and how we built our wireless connection to the sun from our mitochondria. **So why do you have to see the sunrise and make like the SPHINX before you can use the TCA cycle for food? Because that is what happened in the GOE on Earth. Before it, nothing on Earth used the TCA.** You need to mimic your evolutionary history with your story of diseases.

When I tell you something listen, shut up, comprehend the lesson before speaking and asking another question.

YOU NEED TO MOVE.

That does not require a consult. It requires you read what I have written over 20 years and string the lessons together.

Thousands of people make this mistake. None of them read the fucking blogs or listen to the questions carefully enough.

Patients navigating the highways of centralized medicine are on the road to nowhere and trying to escape. The escape route is through their light environment. None of their centralized MDs know this. The decentralized truth is out there.

It is not to be found among the centralized experts in healthcare.
It cannot be found by reading the research or the guidelines.
But it is out there if you look hard enough. I put up with many years of them putting my science down and watching all of you bleed, now I'd like to see them bleed some back. It is time to bleed the freak. You need to cowboy up and do what Nature is telling you.

The GOE introduced a new player to Earth’s environment: molecular oxygen. For early anaerobic life forms, oxygen was a toxic electrical stressor. Oxygen, with its high electronegativity, changes the electrical resistance of biological membranes, disrupting the delicate balance of charge that early cells relied on for survival. Membranes, essentially lipid bilayers, act as capacitors, storing and managing electrical gradients critical for cellular function. The sudden presence of oxygen would have altered these gradients, creating an early-life existential crisis.

Without oxygen, UPE biophotons cannot be made. This explains the Cambrian explosion and what life became capable of. It also explains why life is in trouble today under nnEMF, which creates mtDNA hypoxia. That is where the dystonia is coming from.

I’m not recommending this just something I am looking into. I have read you can get a low interest loan secured with bitcoin. Let’s say the interest rate is 3% but the bitcoin grows at 10%, 20%, 30% - in 3 - 5 years your bitcoin has doubled in value. Pay off the loan. Now you have a payed off house AND the same value in bitcoin.

The house will go up in value but almost certainly not as much as the bitcoin. So the alternative is to keep all the bitcoin and rent. 3% on $800,000 = $24,000/year or $2,000/month. Not there yet, we shall see.

# Thread: My Journal: Publishing a Book on Leptin
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 78

### JHackett — 2025-03-21T19:25:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-349050>

Hi everyone,

Jack suggested that I write an optimal journal of sorts on my work from the last year, which has been writing a book on **leptin’s overarching effects on optimal health.**

The book is part of a series of approximately seven, that I’m calling the _Decentralized Health Rebellion_.

This first book on leptin, called _The Metabolic Scam: Leptin Unlocks the Truth_, is basically my way of paying homage to Jack’s earlier blogs, **the Leptin series**. I hope to shine my own little version of a flashlight on his work and with that, spread the word to other people.

I just wanted to introduce the work today but over time I’ll be using some excerpts within this journal that I hope will spark some conversation.

Please let me know if you’d be interested in reading the book, it’s going to be ready via PDF soon. I cannot say at this time when paperback will be available. Thanks!

Would be happy to read it!

### JHackett — 2025-03-23T15:23:02+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-349167>

Excerpt: “_Leptin crosses the blood-brain barrier and enters the hypothalamus to bind leptin receptors in the arcuate nucleus, where it can relay system wide energy status. This is not just a passive signal—leptin evaluates how much energy was used throughout the day and determines whether fat should be burned or stored.”_

That’s not something taught in any textbook I ever read in med school. These few sentences dismantle the entire calories-in, calories-out model, yet that is a thriving multi-billion dollar industry and growing.

### JHackett — 2025-03-25T00:44:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-349262>

_Excerpt: “If sunlight is the most critical metabolic regular, artificial light is the greatest metabolic disrupter. In fact, poor light environment is a greater driver of metabolic disease than diet.”_

Doing my best to also "light" matches and toss them onto current nutritional dogma.

### JHackett — 2025-03-27T15:32:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-349434>

Excerpt:

“Quantum tunneling, in essence, is a phenomenon where tiny particles, like electrons, can pass through energy barriers that they normally wouldn’t be able to cross—almost like a ghost walking through a wall. In your mitochondria, this allows electrons to move more efficiently, speeding up energy production and reducing waste.”

On this forum, we already know: mitochondria aren’t just ATP factories—they’re quantum processors. But articulating tunneling as the mechanism for metabolic elegance feels like pulling classical biology into the quantum firelight and saying, “You’ve served your time—now step aside.” I hope my future readers understand and appreciate the significance.

### JHackett — 2025-03-31T17:30:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-349743>

Excerpt:

“Obesity isn’t a calorie problem. It’s not even just about insulin. It’s a leptin hijacking—an attack on your brain’s ability to regulate energy. Big Food, artificial light, and 24/7 access to calories keep you in a constant state of fat storage.”

⸻

For years I have watched people blame themselves, obsess over food, and fail—because no one ever told them the war was being fought in the brain, not the mouth.

That's amazing. Aren't you curious what actually changed in her brain? Power of positivity and its effects on neuroinflammation, perhaps?

She actually changed her mind . . . So her brain changed . . . then her biology changed.
All the sub-conscious programs that were keeping her fat got over ridden with new “code” that said - be thin . . .
Epigenetics . . . DNA is subject to the environment AND the dominant belief system in the mind programming.
What “they” use against us (sub-conscious mind programming) we can, by becoming conscious, use to reprogram and become free.
Change your mind - change your world.

### JHackett — 2025-04-05T03:38:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-350126>

**Excerpt:**
_“Circadian biology doesn’t just affect sleep—it governs hormone release, mitochondrial efficiency, immune signaling, and fat metabolism. Break the light-dark cycle, and you break everything downstream.”_

This one came from my own place of clarity—and exhaustion. Clarity because once you understand circadian biology, the rest of medicine feels like noise. Exhaustion because I realized how far off-course we've gone—in a field that I dedicated my life to. Writing this felt like distilling a thousand papers and a decade of clinical frustration into one scalpel-sharp truth.

### JHackett — 2025-04-17T19:35:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-350843>

**"Leptin is made by your fat cells, but don’t mistake it for a passive byproduct. It's your fuel gauge, sending continuous updates to the hypothalamus—the command center of your brain's energy regulation. When leptin signaling is intact, your brain gets a clear message: energy is sufficient, burn calories freely, suppress appetite and stay lean. But when leptin resistance sets in, the brain goes blind. It reads scarcity where abundance exists. You have access to thousands of calories a day, yet hunger increases, metabolism slows, fat is hoarded and energy crashes."**
— _The Metabolic Scam_

### Anthony Smith — 2025-04-20T19:59:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-351007>

> [AngieM said:](/goto/post?id=350978)
>
> What an amazing experience! I’m curious, how did your wife change her mind? Did she simply make a decision? Did she do meditations/visualizations?

Who we are, what we think and feel comes from a life time of experiences, decisions made, emotional responses, programing (from parents, school, media, MKUltra etc). For most people, most of the time all these are subconscious “programs” running in the background, dictating our reactions, thoughts and feelings to what we experience today.

I believe the key to becoming who you want to be, not who you have been programed to be, is to become conscious and remove these old programs. Meditation and visualizations can help but just looking and becoming conscious of the programs there empowers you to cancel them or rewrite and replace them with new ones of your choosing.

The sub conscious mind is very powerful, take the time to do the work to have it work for you, not against you. I am healthy, wealthy and wise. I am successful. I am in the right place at the right time. When I need help, help will be there etc.

Yes, she “just” changed her mind one day but it was a core decision, she decided not to be heavy any more and the weight left. Watching the sun rise and being in the sun IS good for everyone but why do some get faster results than others? Multiple reasons, severity of underlying conditions etc BUT - I expect people who BELIEVE it works get better, faster results than those just giving it a try or who are skeptical.

Today is resurrection Sunday, I believe in resurrection, the “impossible”, life from death. Choose life.

### JHackett — 2025-04-20T23:29:09+0300

Sounds like a broken record, but I believe doing any version of introspection in a parasympathetic state while deeply immersed in nature (light/magnetism) will begin to erase this programming. Sometimes it can take years, depends on micro-variations of the individual. But, it works.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-07-07T16:04:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-355916>

> [moon77 said:](/goto/post?id=355909)
>
> if this is true then why do animal get cancer? my dog died of cancer, ar you telling me its form his light environment?
>
> im smelling somthing in the air

Dogs get cancer because they're kept inside fed crap and generally given too much food and they get vaccinated yearly.

sound familiar? Pampered dogs generally get cancer. I'll tell you a story of two dogs
When I was in London and I had a dog. We would go to the park and eventually about two years in we met an Airedale terrier. now this dog was kept in a house and given wheat biscuits with sugar. And was a complete nightmare as far as good was concerned. They used to have to keep him behind a barrier in the utility room that they had in their nice big Victorian end Semi detached House

But I don't think apart from the park that he got much sunlight and he was indulged.

Mine. I would let her go out as much as possible. I would even leave the door open so she could come and go at will, the back door to the garden

She did not steal food off the counters.

I had another dog walking friend deliver a food box to me who also had an Airedale terrier

She looked scandaliséd by the fact that the food bag was on the floor and open ish and she said. You can leave your food bag like that with your dog ? And I said yeah. What's the difficulty? She said her dog would have got into it and eaten the lot

So back to Freddie the Airedale terrier

And to my Doberman
I learnt later that Freddie had died of cancer at age 5

And then recently his mistress she also died. But that was 2 years ago and she was only 71

So , there was a huge plasma TV flat screen in that house which I'm sure the dog was watching

John his master was at that point wondering whether to get an Led TV

What did I have. An old Mitsubishi cathode ray TV which I barely watched

In fact so old that when I got burgled they didn't take the TV. They left that behind

So light environment was a total factor

There you go.

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-07-07T17:52:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-355925>

> [moon77 said:](/goto/post?id=355920)
>
> top of the line response and info.
>
> that i believe. that makes sense the food the environment and light and jbs
> but not just light
>
> thanks for sharing this.
> if i ever have a pet again i will remember

I agree with everything Dr. Robert Kapur said, and having dogs, I would post again that they are meant to eat their ancestral food, just like the people living in the "blue zones" who are removed from the Western diet, highly processed and convenient in abundance, yet never satisfying a person's true hunger.

I takes no effort to research what dogs eat in the wild, as they are predators or scavengers, eating everything except the sound the animals make. Internal organs, skin, muscle, etc.

They are pure examples of instinct, something humans abandoned when they became "civilized". They live outside, find shade when necessary, and unless they hunt at night, are asleep at sunset, or before, and arise at sunrise.

One would learn a great deal to learn how humans are meant to live if they would just observe animals/pets in their natural environment.

"The Dog Whisperer" show makes revenue from showing the owners are the source of the problem, if there is one, as Caesar does best when he takes a dog who has behavioral problems back around other healthy dogs who are given the opportunity to just be dogs.

Nature will heal humans if the person hasn't succumbed to the Western lifestyle for too long. Every sunrise I thank God for his Son, and our Sun. Manna turned to worms if it wasn't gathered early, when the people of Israel where forced to wander for 40 years. Morning light is the same way. The benefit is gone if not taken early.

>
> thes dam vets here always said we wont even see your pet without\\t a vax.

I have my dog as much outside time as she wanted. And I fed her decent food and id give her Christmas dinner. Turkey, gravy (gluten free of course because wheat for dogs is damaging to their guts) potatoes and sprouts. Ate it all up.

Used to give scraps on top of the dry food

If I couldn't finish it. She'd get it. Once bought a beef joint that had been marked down in Tesco and gave her that too
Would give her beef bones, Pig ears, and cow ears and no more than 3 treats a day.

I once left her at a friend's for a weekend with a week's worth of treats. He finished them all and I asked him. What the hell. That was a week's worth of treats ! He said oh but she was hungry. But then the thing is they have no impulse control around food. None whatsoever. Dogs are generally on a see food diet

### JHackett — 2025-07-08T20:43:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-356020>

> [Dr Rohen Kapur said:](/goto/post?id=355916)
>
> Dogs get cancer because they're kept inside fed crap and generally given too much food and they get vaccinated yearly.
>
> sound familiar? Pampered dogs generally get cancer. I'll tell you a story of two dogs
> When I was in London and I had a dog. We would go to the park and eventually about two years in we met an Airedale terrier. now this dog was kept in a house and given wheat biscuits with sugar. And was a complete nightmare as far as good was concerned. They used to have to keep him behind a barrier in the utility room that they had in their nice big Victorian end Semi detached House
>
> But I don't think apart from the park that he got much sunlight and he was indulged.
>
> Mine. I would let her go out as much as possible. I would even leave the door open so she could come and go at will, the back door to the garden
>
> She did not steal food off the counters.
>
> I had another dog walking friend deliver a food box to me who also had an Airedale terrier
>
> She looked scandaliséd by the fact that the food bag was on the floor and open ish and she said. You can leave your food bag like that with your dog ? And I said yeah. What's the difficulty? She said her dog would have got into it and eaten the lot
>
>
> So back to Freddie the Airedale terrier
>
> And to my Doberman
> I learnt later that Freddie had died of cancer at age 5
>
> And then recently his mistress she also died. But that was 2 years ago and she was only 71
>
> So , there was a huge plasma TV flat screen in that house which I'm sure the dog was watching
>
> John his master was at that point wondering whether to get an Led TV
>
> What did I have. An old Mitsubishi cathode ray TV which I barely watched
>
> In fact so old that when I got burgled they didn't take the TV. They left that behind
>
> So light environment was a total factor
>
> There you go.

I thought this was an interesting question as the dog lives in the environment with us. Doesn’t that prove the point only further? But anyway happy to answer Moon’s concerns.

### JHackett — 2025-07-30T20:35:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-publishing-a-book-on-leptin.30527/post-357222>

> [BiohackersTO said:](/goto/post?id=357157)
>
> I would love to read more about Leptin for those who do not have weight issues. I am not trained in biology and whenever the story of Leptin is presented as a weight issue, I start to tune out, because I don't have a weight issue and so it sounds not relevant to my situation. I also don't take stimulants (other than a cup of coffee maybe once every two weeks), I am not sleep deprived and I don't do a starvation diet or excessive cardio. I have had endometriosis and fibroids. So how does the Leptin story interact with conditions that result from a Warburg shifted metabolism? Sorry if that's obvious. Thank you in advance from a biology-retard.

Great question and extremely relevant to my book. Leptin is the energy accountant of the body. It deals with every tissue, connecting said tissue to the brain and assessing energetics. Do we need to down regulate production because this tissue is stressed? Can we continue with optimal production of a hormone because we have the energy inputs needed? Is the 'signal' throughout the body being presented more as 'noise', requiring protection/survival mode (very common with nnEMF exposure)? Etc.

Leptin is tightly regulated to the weight loss story as it is produced from adipose tissue and deals intimately with system-wide energy status. But it has its hands everywhere.

# Thread: My journey back to health
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-back-to-health.30531/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### petrichor — 2025-03-21T22:32:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-back-to-health.30531/post-349063>

### Myself​

-   Age: 49 years old, male.
-   Background: Grew up in the Bay Area, California.

#### Current Health Issues​

-   Insomnia: I go to bed early and fall asleep easily but wake up ~5 hours later to use the bathroom and struggle to fall back asleep for 45 minutes to 1.5 hours. Worse in winter. I'm not very alert or energetic when I wake, it's just a ruminating mind. It's not every night, seems related to stress, and when I was was in a relationship I always slept through the night when my girlfriend shared the bed with me.
-   Minor Fatigue: Directly tied to sleep quality. Generally, my energy levels are very high.
-   Tinea Versicolor: Minor, in areas that are prone to staying damp.
-   Tinnitus: Minor and not bothersome. Additional context: Played loud music in a band for years and drums prior to that.
-   Muscle Stiffness: No pain, but limited mobility, especially in back, neck, and hamstrings. I sometimes throw my back out. Usually stress related.
-   Mental Health: Overall amazing, though I'm currently processing significant grief accumulated over 30 years from a tumultuous life.

Otherwise, life feels very good. I have mental clarity, sense of well being, and have acquired some sort of magnetic affect that is drawing people towards me.

### My Mother​

-   Childhood: Diagnosed with a benign heart murmur.
-   Ages 5–8: Frequent ear infections.
-   Ages 16–18: Cystic acne.
-   Age 25: Developed lactose intolerance following a Giardia infection.
-   Age 47: Diagnosed with mitral valve prolapse.
-   Ages 54–60: Diagnosed with basal cell skin cancer (5 occurrences).
-   Undated: Diagnosed with ocular and facial rosacea.
-   Age 59: Diagnosed with interstitial cystitis.
-   Age 62: Posterior vitreous detachment in the right eye.
-   Age 65: Diagnosed with glaucoma.
-   Age 67: Underwent mitral valve repair surgery.
-   Age 74: Diagnosed with gluten intolerance.

### My Maternal Grandmother​

-   No known health issues until age 39.
-   Early Spring, Age 39: Appendectomy due to a nearly ruptured appendix. Doctor noted significant inflammation in her small bowel.
-   Summer, Age 39: Intermittent diarrhea or constipation, developed hemorrhoids.
-   Later in 39th Year: Began vomiting; diagnosed with bowel obstruction caused by cancer.
-   October, Age 39: Underwent surgery, but cancer had already spread.
-   December 31, Age 40: Passed away on New Year’s Eve, one week after her 40th birthday (born December 25).

My grandmother grew up on a farm in the Central Valley CA, born in 1920. My mom was raised on that farm, but left at age 18.

### petrichor — 2025-03-21T22:34:43+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-back-to-health.30531/post-349064>

### Health History - Significant Events​

-   Birth:
    -   Born 7 weeks premature; spent first week in an incubator.
-   Ages 5 & ~7–8:
    -   Experienced acute, non-violent physical/mental trauma (DM for details if needed).
    -   I struggled to go to sleep for years afterward. (Not fall asleep. Scared to go to bed)
    -   Bed wetting. Headaches.
-   Childhood in general: In general though, I was very robust and rarely got sick or injured. Never broke a bone despite many bad falls.
-   Age 16:
    -   Prescribed tetracycline for acne (3 months).
    -   Advised to avoid sun exposure.
-   Age 17:
    -   Became depressed, dropped out of high school.
    -   Inverted sleep schedule for computer science self-study.
-   Age 19:
    -   Traded computers for excessive alcohol use.
    -   Returned to outdoor activities.
-   Age 22:
    -   Struggles with sinus infections began.
    -   Back injury (L4-L5) with positive ANA (lupus test).
    -   Contracted mononucleosis.
    -   Developed sciatica and pain in feet from standing.
    -   Workers’ comp denied, returned to computer work.
-   Age 23:
    -   Mono relapsed (2–3 months).
    -   Taking Aleve for back pain, triggered severe GERD.
    -   Something triggered full-body rash with peeling skin.
-   Age 24:
    -   Moved to New Orleans (1999), most symptoms improved except GERD, triggered by certain foods and alcohol.
    -   Moved back to CA, started remote tech work.
    -   RSI in wrists (disabled 1.5 years), treated with cortisone.
    -   Brief period panic attacks due to high stress.
-   Age 29:
    -   Moved to Oregon.
    -   Sinus infections returned with full-body muscle pain and inflammation.
-   Age 33:
    -   Insomnia began (middle-of-night waking).
    -   Developed panic disorder.
    -   At ER, given Xanax.
    -   The primary doctor prescribed Ativan to bridge to Zoloft.
        -   Didn’t tolerate Zoloft (inability to sleep, nausea leading to vomiting).
        -   Switched to Celexa, sleep worsened, experienced brain zaps.
        -   Refused SSRIs after three days.
    -   ND diagnosed adrenal fatigue; treatment resolved panic.
    -   Unable to quit Ativan for 9 months, weaned off by switching to Klonopin.
-   Age 34:
    -   Diagnosed with candida.
    -   Adopted Weston A. Price diet, cut alcohol/coffee, health improved significantly.
    -   Diagnosed as functional hypothyroid.
        -   Given Armour Thyroid, caused panic attacks and severe hunger.
-   Age 37:
    -   RSI in wrists returned.
    -   Resolved with prolotherapy.
-   Age 38:
    -   Diagnosed with “ADHD”
    -   Skeptical, refused treatment. I do have time blindness though.
-   Age 41:
    -   Divorce with severely stressful aftermath (DM for details).
    -   Panic attacks stopped with separation though.
    -   Resumed Weston A. Price diet and weightlifting, health improved.
    -   Started melatonin dependency for jet lag from work travel.
-   Age 42:
    -   1 year of unrelenting stress + prescription-grade Advil and Mucinex for persistent sinus issues.
    -   Relationship with a woman diagnosed with Borderline personality disorder.
    -   Lead to physical breakdown (GERD, inflamed esophagous fatigue).
        -   Given antacids, which made me nauseous.
        -   Inability to breathe through nose at night (waking 10+ times).
-   Age 43:
    -   Worked in office for a company that developed WiFi/microwave devices. Dozens or more were always on.
    -   Underwent septoplasty/turbinate reduction.
    -   Anesthesia sensitivity led to 3–4 months of brain fog.
    -   Candida diagnosed again, treated with nystatin/diet.
    -   Quit job, spent time in sun and nature, started to heal.
    -   Diagnosed with C-PTSD.
        -   Suspicious of the diagnosis, but will provide details below.
-   Age 44:
    -   Returned to remote tech work.
    -   Methylcobalamin shots for MTHFR (homozygous C677T), but hypermethylation worsened symptoms.
    -   NAC resolved remaining panic/mood issues.
-   Age 45:
    -   Began full-body tanning regularly.
    -   Tan very easily but noticed a reddish tan tone compared to childhood.
-   Age 46:
    -   Received glutathione injections and liposomal glutathione.
-   Age 47:
    -   Discovered quantum biology.
    -   Stopped seeing doctors, weaned off supplements except magnesium.
-   Age 48:
    -   Discontinued magnesium.
    -   Began waking for sunrise consistently.
    -   Life got better

### petrichor — 2025-03-21T22:37:37+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-back-to-health.30531/post-349065>

**C-PTSD Notes**

These are symptoms that I wrote down at the time of the diagnoses. I never sought treatment and they are all completely resolved except for:

-   Exaggerated startle response.
-   Trouble maintaining (romantic) relationships
-   Suppressing emotions unconsciously (but it's getting better).

I did not include all the details of things considered "trauma" in my history. Too personal. But there is a not-insignificant amount, and there were both acute and chronic events. Perhaps my skepticism of the diagnosis is a symptom to the of the diagnosis, but it just doesn't feel like a big issue to me anymore.

**Mental Symptoms:**

-   Emotional detachment/disassociation. Pervasive sense of being defective or “different”, self-loathing.
-   Trouble maintaining relationships, and feeling disconnected or isolated, even when around others.
-   Emotional Flashbacks
-   Nightmares related to events.
-   Triggers causing sudden emotional reactions.
-   Constantly feeling on edge, or unable to relax. Coping by always “doing something”
-   Exaggerated startle response.
-   Sleep disturbances.
-   Avoiding thoughts, places, or people tied to the trauma.
-   Suppressing emotions to cope, often unconsciously.
-   Anhedonia, spiritual/existential disconnection.

**Physical Manifestations:**

-   Chronic fatigue/pain.
-   Gastrointestinal issues.
-   Headaches.
-   Shallow breathing

### petrichor — 2025-03-24T00:51:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journey-back-to-health.30531/post-349210>

**Insomnia notes**

Experiments and their effect on the middle of the night wake ups. None of these were interventions I stuck to for the long term. I just wanted to experiment and learn.

1\. Protein + carb snack before bed - No effect
2\. Benadryl - Effective at 25mg, but side effects (grogginess, low mood) are intolerable.
3\. Psylocibin - 100 mg in the morning. Effective at first. One dose got me several days of sleeping through the night. The next dose got me a week. Eventually it stopped working
4\. Procain therapy - 10 mg injected into lower back. Three days of sleeping through the night.
5\. Glycine - 3 grams. Negative effect. Had trouble falling asleep
6\. CBD - 10 mg. Negative effect. Unable to fall asleep for hours
7\. CBD + THC 1:1 ratio - 5 mg. Slept through the night, but didn't like it otherwise. Didn't wake up rested.
8\. Phenibut - 800mg. I was taking this inadvertently. It was one ingredient in a supplement I was taking. It was very effective, but when the FDA cracked down on it and the supplement was no longer available I had two weeks of terrible withdrawal effects.
9\. Melatonin - .5 mg. This helped shorten the duration of the wake up, but often lead to grogginess in the AM

# Thread: BIPEDALISM:  DECENTRALIZED BIOLOGY 101
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bipedalism-decentralized-biology-101.30532/>
**Subforum:** Factor X
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-22T02:53:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bipedalism-decentralized-biology-101.30532/post-349094>

**Key Points from My Decentralized Model**

1.  ****Bipedalism as the First Defining Change in Human Evolution**

    -   Context: Bipedalism preceded brain expansion in hominins (e.g., Lucy, Ardi), contradicting the idea that a larger brain or wider birth canal drove upright walking. Traditional evolutionary theories (e.g., Darwin) struggled to explain this, often assuming it was a natural progression.
    -   Mechanism: I've proposed environmental changes in the East African Rift—rapid forest loss, a cold and wet climate at higher elevations, and proximity to seawater forced bipedalism. This was driven by the need for a rigid foot/ankle to navigate the terrain, increased DHA from seafood, and changes in light and magnetic flux (ROS/RNS) and biophoton spectral change affecting the SCN and mitochondrial metabolism.
    -   Implication: Bipedalism was an epigenetic adaptation to environmental conditions, not a genetic or brain-driven change, aligning with my emphasis on light, water, and bioelectric signaling over nucleic acids.
    -   Connection to Warburg: The shift to bipedalism in a low-deuterium, high-DHA environment (seawater, sunlight) enhanced TCA/urea cycle efficiency (Kreb’s bicycle), countering Warburg metabolism. Modern nnEMF environments reverse this, driving Warburg metabolism which is why so many have foot ankle and spine problems.

    **
2.  ****Role of Eccrine Glands and Water Superconduction**

    -   Mechanism: Humans evolved eccrine glands in their feet to absorb seawater, desalinate it (removing NaCl), and create coherent, low-density water for superconduction in nerves, fascia, and spinal discs. This water lacks a blood supply in discs, relying on superconduction for energy transfer, explaining why back pain is prevalent in modern humans (dehydration from nnEMF).
    -   Implication: Eccrine glands facilitated bipedalism by providing water to hydrate and power the musculoskeletal system (e.g., lumbar discs, collagen semiconductors), enabling rapid skeletal changes. This aligns with my focus on water and grounding as a key bioenergetic medium.
    -   Connection to Warburg: Coherent water (low deuterium) supported mitochondrial function in early humans, countering Warburg metabolism. Modern dehydration (nnEMF, blue light) slows Kreb’s bicycle, driving glycolysis.

    **
3.  ****Environmental Drivers in the East African Rift**

    -   Mechanism: The Rift’s cold, wet climate at higher elevation (3,500 feet) forced a terrestrial diet (seafood, high DHA), altered light frequencies (more UV), and increased magnetic flux (from vulcanism), speeding up the TCA/urea cycle and ATPase despite lower oxygen tension. This turned off glycolysis/PPP, favoring biosynthesis for skeletal changes (e.g., HOX genes for legs).
    -   Implication: Bipedalism was a photo-bioenergetic adaptation to environmental stressors, not a brain-driven change, supporting my light-driven evolutionary model.
    -   Connection to Warburg: Early humans avoided Warburg metabolism by reducing deuterium (seawater, UV light) and enhancing mitochondrial efficiency (Z-Z pathway, Grotthuss mechanism). Modern nnEMF environments (high deuterium) drive Warburg metabolism, exacerbating back pain and systemic disease.

    **
4.  ****Skeletal and Physiological Adaptations**

    -   Mechanism: Bipedalism required massive skeletal changes (pelvis, lumbar vertebrae, discs, gluteus maximus), which were supported by water superconduction and hormones (e.g., from the pelvis). The spine shifted from a cantilever arch (quadrupeds) to a vertical tower, increasing mechanical stress and back pain in modern humans.
    -   Implication: The rapid evolutionary transition to bipedalism left humans with a “patchwork” disc annulus, prone to dehydration and injury, explaining the prevalence of modern back pain.
    -   Connection to Warburg: Dehydration from nnEMF slows water superconduction in discs, impairing mitochondrial function and driving Warburg metabolism in affected tissues (e.g., spinal cord, nerves).

    **
5.  ****Tie-In to Previous Blog Concepts**

    -   5-hmC and Brain Development: Bipedalism preceded brain expansion, but the low-deuterium environment (seawater, UV) supported 5-hmC’s H⁺-rich environment, enhancing bioelectric signaling for later brain growth.
    -   Noggin and Deuterium: High deuterium in noggin (chimp-like) slowed brain growth, but the Rift’s conditions (low deuterium, seawater) supported skeletal changes first, enabling bipedalism.
    -   Grotthuss Mechanism (Z-Z vs. E-Z-E): The Z-Z pathway dominated in early humans (low deuterium, seawater), enhancing H⁺ tunneling and mitochondrial function, countering Warburg metabolism. Modern nnEMF (high deuterium) shifts to E-Z-E, driving glycolysis.
    -   Modern Buildings and Life: nnEMF and blue light dehydrate tissues, impairing water superconduction (e.g., in discs), slowing Kreb’s bicycle, and driving Warburg metabolism = leading to disease.

    **

**CITES**

### [Quantum Biology 6: Bipedalism](https://jackkruse.com/quantum-biology-6-bipedalism/)

Readers Summary What is one aspect of our body that separates us from all other mammals? Is our two legs still forcing us to evolve? How does it affect modern spine disease? How is it directly tied to water? How can you use it to regain your health? ‘For any quadruped to get up on its

 jackkruse.com

# Thread: Mirrors and the sun

### Sweden — 2025-03-24T12:54:30+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mirrors-and-the-sun.30540/post-349233>

"Hello everyone,

I’ve been thinking about the sun, infrared light, and its potential role in managing pain. With all the devices and gadgets people use for conditions like osteoarthritis and similar problems, it’s clear that nothing truly matches the power of the sun.

This got me wondering—what if we could harness and enhance the sun’s natural effects? For example, by using a mirror to concentrate the morning sunlight on a specific area of the body, like the fingers. Could this approach potentially amplify the therapeutic benefits of the sun’s infrared and red light, delivering them in more focused doses whilst also beeing outside?

### **General Reflection Percentages by Wavelength:**​

-   **Visible Light (400–700 nm):** ~85–95% reflection for most mirrors.
-   **Near-Infrared (700–2500 nm):** ~70–90% reflection, depending on the mirror type.
-   **Ultraviolet (100–400 nm):** ~50–80% reflection; varies because glass absorbs much of the UV light.

# Thread: 23andme bankruptcy data delete
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-bankruptcy-data-delete.30541/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### DLO — 2025-03-24T15:24:26+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-bankruptcy-data-delete.30541/post-349241>

**23&ME users should delete their data as soon as possible, California’s Attorney General [has warned](https://nypost.com/2025/03/23/us-news/calif-ag-issues-stark-warning-to-15-million-23andme-users/), as the beleaguered genetic-testing company faces bankruptcy and sale.**

“Given 23andMe’s reported financial distress, I remind Californians to consider invoking their rights and directing 23andMe to delete their data and destroy any samples of genetic material held by the company,” AG Rob Bonta [said in a statement on Friday](https://oag.ca.gov/news/press-releases/attorney-general-bonta-urgently-issues-consumer-alert-23andme-customers).

Under California’s Genetic Information Privacy Act of 2022, companies must obtain explicit consent for the collection, use and disclosure of any genetic data. The law also guarantees consumers the right to access or delete their data at their choosing.

Of particular concern is the fate of the company’s massive bank of customer data, which could end up being used for purposes never envisaged by the company or by users themselves. The company has data on at least 15 million individuals, including a wide variety of information on personal health and ancestry.

Last year, there [was widespread concern](https://www.reuters.com/fact-check/article-us-genetics-company-selling-all-dna-assets-china-is-satire-2024-05-17/) over a satirical article that suggested the company was selling its genetic data to a Chinese company, in a $10 billion deal.

As recently as 2020, 23&Me was valued at $6 billion, but a botched expansion into research and pharmaceutical partnerships, and an embarrassing data breach, caused the company’s stock to tank. Its share price has fallen from $320 a share to less than $2.

The company has now filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy and its CEO, Anne Wojcicki, has resigned.

23andMe [**said**](https://investors.23andme.com/news-releases/news-release-details/23andme-initiates-voluntary-chapter-11-process-maximize) in a press release it plans to continue operating throughout the sale process and that there “are no changes to the way the company stores, manages, or protects customer data.”

### Marshall_Sponder — 2025-03-25T02:31:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/23andme-bankruptcy-data-delete.30541/post-349272>

> [DLO said:](/goto/post?id=349241)
>
> **23&ME users should delete their data as soon as possible, California’s Attorney General [has warned](https://nypost.com/2025/03/23/us-news/calif-ag-issues-stark-warning-to-15-million-23andme-users/), as the beleaguered genetic-testing company faces bankruptcy and sale.**
>
> “Given 23andMe’s reported financial distress, I remind Californians to consider invoking their rights and directing 23andMe to delete their data and destroy any samples of genetic material held by the company,” AG Rob Bonta [said in a statement on Friday](https://oag.ca.gov/news/press-releases/attorney-general-bonta-urgently-issues-consumer-alert-23andme-customers).
>
> Under California’s Genetic Information Privacy Act of 2022, companies must obtain explicit consent for the collection, use and disclosure of any genetic data. The law also guarantees consumers the right to access or delete their data at their choosing.
>
> Of particular concern is the fate of the company’s massive bank of customer data, which could end up being used for purposes never envisaged by the company or by users themselves. The company has data on at least 15 million individuals, including a wide variety of information on personal health and ancestry.
>
> Last year, there [was widespread concern](https://www.reuters.com/fact-check/article-us-genetics-company-selling-all-dna-assets-china-is-satire-2024-05-17/) over a satirical article that suggested the company was selling its genetic data to a Chinese company, in a $10 billion deal.
>
> As recently as 2020, 23&Me was valued at $6 billion, but a botched expansion into research and pharmaceutical partnerships, and an embarrassing data breach, caused the company’s stock to tank. Its share price has fallen from $320 a share to less than $2.
>
> The company has now filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy and its CEO, Anne Wojcicki, has resigned.
>
> 23andMe [**said**](https://investors.23andme.com/news-releases/news-release-details/23andme-initiates-voluntary-chapter-11-process-maximize) in a press release it plans to continue operating throughout the sale process and that there “are no changes to the way the company stores, manages, or protects customer data.”

Yes, I saw the news and requested my data be deleted within minutes of hearing about the bankruptcy!!~

### Mat.I — 2025-03-24T23:18:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-europe.30544/post-349258>

Buy a decent multimeter, that's all you need. Then you can make your own kit with an outdoor peg (for the ground) cable and some jumper cables..

### GHeisser — 2025-03-25T04:24:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-europe.30544/post-349276>

Maybe a good ohm meter will work for you. Knowing your normal ph (7.-7.35) and its mv (-20 to -40)
may help. normal ph vs abnormal ph. Get to know which are electron doners and electron stealers to your body. Consider reading "Healing is Voltage" Chapter 3 specifically.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-europe.30544/post-349307>

You can build your own, we recommend a TRMS meter that measures down to 3 decimal points on AC volts, a handle for good body contact, a long ground cord with the right connections each end, a circuit tester for safety -or- We ship to Europe, all you would need to get locally is a US to local plug adaptor with ground (3 pins). - [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

### Mat.I — 2025-07-09T08:17:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/body-voltage-meter-europe.30544/post-356060>

> [GHeisser said:](/goto/post?id=349276)
>
> Knowing your normal ph (7.-7.35) and its mv (-20 to -40)

I have not read the book or know anything about it, however, this statement (I assume from the book) appears to be a "blanket" link for pH to voltage.. As I understand pH is the negative log of proton concentration & the V (potential diff) depends on many ions (not just protons). Ties back to Nernst equation and cell mem potentials. This "voltage" potential would vary throughout the body, so, IMO I do not believe scientifically a global statement like 7 - 7.35 is -20 to -40 mV can be made with out compartment level specificity.

# Thread: I Stay Away...From nnEMF
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 235
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mr.AJ — 2025-03-24T23:52:07+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-349259>

Just turned 37 years old a few days ago. Came here because I was interested in circadian biology/decentralized health. I guess some info on my background:

\- Born and raised in Northern IL, moved to Southern IL around 8 years old and stayed there until ~ 25. Did normie things for the first 35 years or so of my life, broke a lot of nature's rules. Was overweight basically from the age of 5 until ~ 13, slimmed out until 25 or so and then started gaining more weight. Prob peaked around 280 at 6'2. The only times I was not pale was during the summer when we would go swimming or spend time at the lake. From ~20 to ~31 or so I was basically never outside getting sun. Lived indoors, played poker professionally in casinos, tons of wifi, laptop on body etc etc. All of the usual mistakes. Was fortunate to have stumbled into BTC in 2016 as a poker player. For the last 1.5 years or so I only play poker outdoors during daylight hours gro

\- Mother and maternal grandmother are from Poland. Deleted 23andMe info so don't have exact Mitochondrial haplotype info. Grandmother lived until 88 years old, died of a heart attack just a few days ago. She had basically no chronic disease burden up until her death, though they got her on BP meds which she did not like to take and I tried to explain to her how to lower her BP without them. She was a smoker but grew up on a farm in Southern Poland so definitely had buzzing mitochondria before modern day Chernobyl. She was in quite good health until the last couple of years where it was obvious that she was just withering away -> harder to walk around the house/walk the dogs etc. But she always smoked outside.

\- My mother has had chronic health problems most if not all of her life. She was obese as a child, leaned out but broke a lot of nature's rules in terms of staying out late/lots of blue light etc. She had me at 23 years old so eggs/mtDNA were probably still is reasonably well shape given this was before tons of nnEMF. As she's aged she'd developed osteoarthritis, would do cortisone shots over and over until they stopped working, she then had both knees replaced. (I have a lot of knee inflammation unsurprisingly). Currently her shoulder is starting to do the exact same problem. (Loss Becker's current, ROS/RNS problem). She lives in Northern CO right now so obviously not great sun and she does not embrace the cold. It's interesting to observe how my mother is ageing at a far more rapid rate than my grandmother did. She looks about her age, but in terms of chronic diseases, she has many more than my grandma ever had.

\-Since becoming an outdoor human in the last 2-4 years, and really embracing the cold during winter at (35N), I feel like I almost enjoy the cold more than I do high UV sunlight. My mood is improved, my sleep is deep, my cardio output is crazy better (walking my dog up hills is a breeze in 20F) Makes me wonder if some Mitochondrial haplotypes actually prefer or require cold to function optimally. Or maybe it's just improving ECT throughput for me.

Health Issues:

Sleep Apnea: The sleep apnea has actually improved a lot. I notice that it gets worse when I'm in higher nnEMF environments or if I've flown that day. I'm using my CPAP much less than I had been, basically only when I'm jerking awake at night so much that I can't sleep, or I wake up feeling very stressed from all of the paused breathing at night.

Hair Loss: Dad was bald, grandpa was bald. I started losing hair a lot in my late 20's and it's seemed to have stopped. Hard to tell if it's been actually growing back or not but i do think the falling out has ceased.

Mood: I used to have very bad depression/anxiety/addiction issues but those have mostly resolved after improving my light environment and keeping my clocks as aligned as possible.

Sexual Function: Had legit ED in my mid to late 20's. Tried all the usual supplements to improve it and nothing has worked except sunning the balls + oysters.

Current Goals:

Figure how a way to still be able to make money to support myself while living 3-6 mo of the year in an EMF shithole. Going to do some research into places like Mount Charleston NV which is up in the mountains further away from downtown LV and has much lower EMF yield and hopefully even higher UV given the altitude increase.

My other home is in rural TN where there is 1 tower ~ 2 miles from me and 4 tiny antennas also about 2 miles from me. I can feel the difference between both locations. Highly populated NV has a constant buzzing/stress feeling and rural TN is much more calming and relaxing. The only downside to where I'm at in TN currently is the geoengineering. Been in contact with my state rep about this and hoping to introduce the same legislation Florida's house just passed to make it a felony for pilots to spray.

If and when I do decide to retire and leave NV I'm debating between going to Destin FL or El Salvador. I have a wife and child on the way and she is not to keen on ES and "dropping dead weight" as Jack likes to say is not as simple when you have a kid coming. Will probably take a trip to Destin next month and see what the prices are / how the area is.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-03-27T21:17:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-349454>

Observation:

When I developed sleep apnea in my late 20's, I would have constant high BP and pulse throughout the day, which I attributed to the apnea episodes at night. (Totally possible my light environment was the cause). After becoming an outdoor human for the last year or so, and especially after experiencing something much more closely resembling a real winter for a human, I've noticed my sleep has gradually been getting better. So I decided to drop the sleep apnea machine for a few days and see how my sleep was and how my pulse/BP were.

So far after about a week of not using the machine, my pulse upon waking is in a pretty normal range (60--80 bpm), certainly not fast paced or elevated like it had been in the past. I have not taken any BP measurements, but I am not waking up with that constant "stressed" feeling that I was when I first realized I had sleep apnea. My wife has also told me I've been snoring less and I am recalling fewer instances of waking up at night.

I do think EMF plays a major roll in this though. When I am living in a higher EMF environment, I notice my sleep almost immediately starts to become worse.

Helios on X has been posting a fair bit about sleep apnea and I may run some similar tests to him over the next few months to get a baseline of how I sleep in a fairly low EMF environment and then compare it to when I have to travel to Chenobyl for a few months for work.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-04-12T23:36:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-350568>

Finally spending most nights with no CPAP. I think instances of waking in the night are higher than when I wear my CPAP, but I don't notice jolting awake or high BP/stressed symptoms during the day that I used to get from not wearing my CPAP. Think AM sunrise and improved redox is helping me here. Unfortunately I'm making the decision to head back to Chernobyl in May for 2-3 months of work. I expect things to get worse, even with UV 10+ available essentially every day.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-04-12T23:49:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-350571>

Along with more sun light sleeping in an EMF mitigated sleep sanctuary is a big +.
It is not just RF, don’t just unplug, turn off the bedroom circuits.
Measure and make sure you get them all.
The EMF’s (electric and magnetic fields) from a CPAP are significant. IF you use it make sure it is at least 6ft from you. Get a 6ft hose extension.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-04-13T17:44:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-350610>

Ya I can feel the sleep apnea fatigue building up on me. Wife said I was jolting awake/snoring a lot the last few nights and I do feel less rested/more fatigued when I do not wear my mask.

I do wonder, if I'm doing all of the other things correctly, sunny days, dark nights, shutting off EMF at night, sleeping grounded, if I'm still getting better sleep using my CPAP I should just use it and create distance. Because not using it for longer streaks has noticeable detrimental affects to me during the day.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-04-13T20:15:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-350623>

Sleeping grounded works for some but hinders others. Many other factors come into play.
IF you are in a 60Hz (50Hz) electric field being grounded can increase your exposure because now you are part of the circuit and that field is going to ground thru you.
Try it both ways for a few days each and do what is best for you.
IF your CPAP has the heater in the hose mouth piece replace the hose with an all plastic one. The wire brings the electric field into your face/head - VERY BAD.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-04-22T16:45:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-351084>

The last two weeks for some reason I have been extremely lethargic. Work has been slow and when I'm offered the choice of doing low ROI work vs laying grounded in the sun and vegging, I've been choosing the latter. I've never been like this. For the last decade, I'd always choose the work side just to get the reps in, even if it was not critically important work. But more and more I feel like my body is just telling me to go lay in sun and veg and do nothing. I wonder if this a normal part of the process?

### Mr.AJ — 2025-05-02T01:47:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-351682>

I'm starting to understand why Uncle Jack is the way he is. It's wild how you can have people come to you complaining about ailments or injuries or health problems, tell them "hey your environment might be messed up, try X,Y,Z", show them a few papers or blogs on these topics, and they won't even TRY. Or if they do try it's a half-assed effort followed by "Sunlight can't fix this, I tried." or "Well you have to be able to live your life, you can't escape EMF" So many bullshit excuses without even giving it an honest effort. ### Mr.AJ — 2025-06-07T00:25:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-354145>

Jet Lag Rx Takeaways:

1\. Drink lots of non fluoridated water, Reverse osmosis water, or spring water
2\. Avoid all alcohol 3 days before during and 3 days after your trip.
3\. Always sit in the aisle so you can get up and move.
5\. Always connect your foot to the metal framework in front of you to ground yourself to the plane.
6\. Always use noise reduction ear plugs.
7\. Never sit in the emergency row. It has all the avionics of the wings close to it and has higher EMF risks.
8\. Wear blue blockers during flight to preserve your DHA loss in your eye and skin.
9\. Always eat a large seafood meal before getting on a flight. Dose yourself with ubiquinol before during and after the flight. Try to fast during the flight and after you land. blue blockers during flight to preserve your DHA loss in your eye and skin.
10\. If you’re heading east, aim for an evening arrival to minimize the circadian disruption.
11\. Before your trip, ease your transition to the new time zone by moving your bedtime. The American Academy of Sleep Medicine guidelines suggest shifting your sleep schedule an hour earlier each night, starting three days before you leave on an eastbound trip.
12\. If you’re headed west, expose yourself to natural light at sunset in the evening before you leave 3 days before the flight.
13\. Swimming in cold water is one of the best hacks for long eastward trips.

Other note:

"Ohmmm's" to stimulate the vagus nerve have been insanely helpful for me. Every time I feel stressed I do a few minutes and immediately start feeling more relaxed.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-06-07T02:51:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-354152>

I was a flight attendant for British Airways (long haul 747’s, average 8-14 hr flights) for 5 years. One million miles +. I know about this.
As a flight attendant we were moving all the time. Most of my flights were trans-Atlantic and I basically lived on East coast USA time, even when in the UK. I was young in my early 20’s but “jet lag” did not affect me.

The advice in the post above is good. Adjust to the destination time zone several days ahead. Move as much as you can on the plane, get up often, stretch in your seat. IMHO sitting and not moving is the #1 problem.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-06-27T17:52:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-355371>

On Regeneration:

I, like a few people on here, have often thought to myself "man, how long do I have to keep doing this light/water/magnetism stuff before I start to see the big improvements? It's been over a year."

Well, about idk 9-10 months ago, I was opening a Topo Chico and dropped the bottle. It fell on my foot and broke my big toenail. Blood shooting out everywhere, lost the nail completely. Since then I've been watching it slowly heal and grow back. After 10 months it's about 85-90% of the way regrown but still looks a little jacked up.

Now, I'm not saying all tissues regrow or heal or regenerate at the same rate. But if it takes over a year for a single toenail to come back, it's not unreasonable to expect areas in our bodies that have huge electrical scars take longer than a year to recover. In fact I would argue a lot of those internally damaged areas have lost a lot of their healing capacities to begin with. My toe probably had all of that wiring intact and working properly. My brain stem might not even have the full healing capacity working at the moment. The first 12-18 months of following circadian principals might have been used to just get that regenerative pathway functional and back online. And lord knows how much more tissue would have to be repaired in that region of my body.

The main takeaway for me from this whole thing is to just enjoy the process. Being outside all day, especially now in the summer where it's warm and UV10+ all day long is really a treat. I would still choose to follow the protocols even if I would not completely fix whatever remaining ailments I have simply because it feels good to be outside and connected to nature. I sleep better than I did in my 20's. My body and mind function better than they did in my 20's. I completely eliminated serious depression and mental health problems I had from living an indoor lifestyle. Living a life at 71 degrees F and constant artificial lighting year round is such a dull and disconnected way of life. I look forward to being outside in shorts now whether it's 112F or 10F outside. Amazing what improving your dopamine levels can do you for.

> I, like a few people on here, have often thought to myself "man, how long do I have to keep doing this light/water/magnetism stuff before I start to see the big improvements? It's been over a year."
>
> Well, about idk 9-10 months ago, I was opening a Topo Chico and dropped the bottle. It fell on my foot and broke my big toenail. Blood shooting out everywhere, lost the nail completely. Since then I've been watching it slowly heal and grow back. After 10 months it's about 85-90% of the way regrown but still looks a little jacked up.

That is far from healthy, you shouldn't be losing a toenail from that. Normal bruising should be healed within a week if not days.

When I lost a fingernail as a kid after it got cut off jumping and falling on a very sharp street barricade in Japan it grew back completely in less than three months. And I was far from a healthy child in the poor environments I was in.

Ozzy is retiring after 57 years of being in a rock band. Watching this video of presumably his last performance of Crazy Train, I saw a person who did not want to quit, but time was forcing him to put it down. If you watch closely, you can see the desire in him to put on a great show. He smiles and gets excited every time the crows sings the chorus of the song for him. He's feeding off their energy even at 77 years old. He wants to give them a great show so badly. The will and drive and heart and love of music is still there.

You only have one life. And that life only has so much time remaining. You have to live your life the way that you want to live it. For me, it's the way that brings the happiness and excitement and meaning that Ozzy feels on stage. There's just not enough time to do anything else. Sooner or later nature is going to take the light back from you.

I think if you can pair that with some wisdom that puts time back into your bank account, I think it can be a pretty fun ride.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-07-12T17:59:58+0300

"Not everyone’s ready to break free. You’ll pour your heart out, trying to pull family, friends, teammates along. You’ll share the map, show the exit, beg them to see the cage they’re in. But some won’t budge. They’re cozy in their chains. They’ll curse the system but cling to it. Freedom’s too heavy—responsibility scares them. They’d rather stay in familiar misery than chase uncertain dreams. Your job isn’t to drag them. It’s to live free. Blaze the trail. Leave the door cracked. Some will follow. Most won’t. And that’s okay for you, not everyone’s meant to roam free."

Funny that this came up on X today. My mom, who has had both knees replaced and is on track to getting her shoulders replaced, send a photo to me and my brothers of her on a golf course wearing a smart watch. I text her and told her she should throw that watch in the trash, and leave where she's living and move inside the tropics because her environment is killing her. (2 jabs btw). Melatonin drives the RQ shift from ~1 to ~0.7 during sleep by inhibiting CI via cardiolipin, promoting fat oxidation through FADH₂ and CII. This supports mitochondrial repair, links to DEC2 and thanatotranscriptomic genes’ chaos management, and moderates UPE emission.

Life’s default daytime metabolism relies on glucose (RQ ~1), driven by high energy demand and sunlight-mediated insulin signaling. This aligns with glycolysis, providing rapid ATP via substrate-level phosphorylation.

AM Sunrise Trigger: Morning sunlight, rich in red and UV with blue light, activates non-visual photoreceptors (e.g., melanopsin, neuropsin), syncing circadian rhythms and boosting mitochondrial function. This shift favors the TCA cycle (citric acid cycle) and urea cycle, optimizing oxygen use (RQ <1) and fat metabolism, as I’ve linked to melatonin’s nighttime role above.

Metabolic Implications: The TCA cycle enhances ATP production via OXPHOS, while the urea cycle detoxifies ammonia from protein catabolism, reducing oxidative stress. This daytime pivot, initiated by sunrise, extend lifespan further from life's GOE design by improving mitochondrial efficiency and sleep quality by balancing energy reserves. This cycle builds more myelin and more myelin means we need less sleep to become alive the next day after the entropy dump of thantotristic genes of vibrational level UPEs. This is why melatonin absorption spectra is what it is and why 95% of melatonin is found in mitochondria.
