**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-358378>

Can you appreciate why this stuff is a bit confusing when reading these photos?

Red light = heme protein = spinning Fo head = more ATP production. UVA = mTOR signaling/NAD+/melanin/POMC.

Still, if you are outside during the winter (IR/NIR) + cold (UV + NIR + increased solar UV absorption) I still don't really grasp what's missing from the photo repair recipe. You can still build internal melanin from cold exposure which tells me there is some way for the body to convert internally generated UV -> UVA -> POMC pathway.

The only negative is that the amount of available sunlight in the winter is just not very good the higher up you go. But mammals had to survive for quite a while with basically zero sunlight, so the mechanism must exist.

Maybe if you are already really fucked up you just have a much higher success rate being in the tropics than trying to fix it with meh sunlight especially in today's electrified world?

### Zeke — 2025-08-17T19:32:10+0300

> [Anthony S said:](/goto/post?id=354145)
>
> Jet Lag Rx Takeaways:
>
> 1\. Drink lots of non fluoridated water, Reverse osmosis water, or spring water
> 2\. Avoid all alcohol 3 days before during and 3 days after your trip.
> 3\. Always sit in the aisle so you can get up and move.
> 5\. Always connect your foot to the metal framework in front of you to ground yourself to the plane.
> 6\. Always use noise reduction ear plugs.
> 7\. Never sit in the emergency row. It has all the avionics of the wings close to it and has higher EMF risks.
> 8\. Wear blue blockers during flight to preserve your DHA loss in your eye and skin.
> 9\. Always eat a large seafood meal before getting on a flight. Dose yourself with ubiquinol before during and after the flight. Try to fast during the flight and after you land. blue blockers during flight to preserve your DHA loss in your eye and skin.
> 10\. If you’re heading east, aim for an evening arrival to minimize the circadian disruption.
> 11\. Before your trip, ease your transition to the new time zone by moving your bedtime. The American Academy of Sleep Medicine guidelines suggest shifting your sleep schedule an hour earlier each night, starting three days before you leave on an eastbound trip.
> 12\. If you’re headed west, expose yourself to natural light at sunset in the evening before you leave 3 days before the flight.
> 13\. Swimming in cold water is one of the best hacks for long eastward trips.
>
> Other note:
>
> "Ohmmm's" to stimulate the vagus nerve have been insanely helpful for me. Every time I feel stressed I do a few minutes and immediately start feeling more relaxed.
>

I was an airline pilot for 42 years before I retired.

I always chose a window seat when riding in the cabin so I can seat undisturbed.

I had an old gauss meter with a needle back then.

The spot where my head was located in the flight deck pegged the needle at over 10 milliguass.

I do find that opening the circuit breakers in my bedroom definitely improves my sleep even though it measures low using the meters that Anthony recommends.

Have been off of my cpap for 4 months now. I still snore but I do not wake up nearly as often as I used to. Most days are good to very good (sunrise absolutely critical) but I can tell if I stay in a bad environment for too long I start to have higher stress/faster respiratory rate/borderline anxiety, which I would imagine is hypoxia/+3 shift related. I've basically turned into a human nnEMF detector just based on symptoms alone. Every time I get that response and measure, it's there.

Grounding is insanely beneficial though. Couple of minutes of putting my feet in a lake and by entire body relaxes/breathing rate slows down.

Returning to CT and Photo repair. Unc if you're out there, I'd love to know what I'm missing.

I read DM# 66 and 69.

My original question was "is CT + IR (outdoors) sufficient for photo repair" That was a hard "no".

You need specifically 380nm light -> neuropsin -> down regulates mTOR signaling -> allows autophagy and apoptosis and cellular repair. + repairing vit A liberation from tech.

Downstream UPE release is also providing you additional 600-1k nm light for ATP production/CCO water/insulation.

\-----------------------------------

So it sounds like to me that CT is not providing that spectrum of light internally (directly or downstream).

If that is the case then CT alone can not photo repair, now can it fix vit-A liberation via nnEMF/blue light.

Hence why Jack says you can not bio hack the inverse square law. And to move to better sun when you're really messed up.

\--------------------------------------

The question still lingers:

A) Why would nature not provide a photo repair mechanism for winter months? It does not seem challenging to do, you would just need to take UVC production from cold and emit 380nm somehow.

OR

B) If cold does in fact allow you to absorb more UV light when there is less of it coming from the sun during winter months. Is this process still not sufficient to stimulate healing even if you are outside virtually naked during the winter?

### Mr.AJ — 2025-09-30T16:43:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-360188>

Someone had a heart attack last week. Drove themselves to the hospital after it was over and checked in bc they didn't feel right. 2 arteries 95% and 99% blocked, 3 stints put in. A month ago this person got a CAC score back of almost 800 and Dr's told them it was nothing to worry about until it was around 2000. I told this person the truth and was very....Dr.Jack about it. I said the doctors need to look where that plaque build up is in your body because if it's not evenly distributed it could be as serious problem. The fact that this CAC number is going up is a problem in itself. I said time is your most valuable asset and the longer you stay where you got the sick less time you have. I said to get on a plane to ES/tropics, call a relator when you get there and put the house up for sale, and cut the anchor if they aren't going to come with. I then showed this person a lot of stuff about UV/IR light, ALAN etc. Explained why the plague build up was occurring and how it could not be fixed in their current location. Ultimately, they didn't act. A week after the CAC, a stress test was done (blue lit nnEMF retardation) and there were issues with it. While they were waiting for the follow up (1 mo later) the heart attack happened.

Another person is now getting both shoulders replaced after getting both knees replaced 1-2 years ago. I've explained to this person that the problem was not solved with the first two knee surgeries and so this is not going to solve it either. I've explained to them what was likely causing their problem. They did not change, they're getting the next surgery in a couple of months.

Another person is peak low dopamine (And this is coming from someone Uncle Jack has told many times is low dopamine.) And I love this person very much. But they are for lack of a better term, a tech addicted moron/pale fuck (whichever you prefer). They will throw any excuse in the book to not change their behavior even though it's hurting themselves and the people around them. Textbook addict mentality. "I have all the answers. I'm right. Why should I have to feel uncomfortable?" I am always the bad guy. Anemic. Zero beta oxidation. Can't get basic tasks accomplished. Zero tolerance to any temperature deviations. Hypersensitivity to sound. The list goes on.

This person and I have a lot of tension and anger towards each other lately because of this. I am trying to drag this person to freedom. They are kicking and screaming. It leads me to becoming angrier and angrier at them.

Anyways, this tweet is on my fridge now.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-11-09T02:46:45+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-362378>

Put your money/assets into a Wyoming LLC “Limited Liability Company” which has its own legal identity (not attached to your name) . . .
You own nothing . . . But control everything . . . So are VERY happy.
Someone looks you up and would find little to nothing so chances are would move on. No profit in going after or suing someone with nothing.
Put the LLC into a Trust for even more protection and a clean pathway to pass it all on.

Off CPAP for 7 months now.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-12-09T21:30:18+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-363736>

First day of sun this afternoon in about a week. Definitely makes it feel 20 degrees warmer. I was starting to get some SAD from the last week of darkness, even with regular CT, so glad sun is out today. Got to say this winter has been a tough adaptation to cold. I still find myself shivering quite often at 40 or lower. Upped the fat intake quite a lot by eating copious amounts of pork, bacon, and 80/20 ground chuck. Still lots of DHA. Big breakfast+ med lunch + big dinner seems to be working the best given my room is quite cold at night. It really didn't get cold until Thanksgiving so maybe I need 1-2 more weeks of below 40 to get fully adapted.

Sleep has been good. 90%+ I sleep through the entire night with zero issues and mostly wake up feeling anywhere from pretty good to very well rested. There have been a small number of nights where I've had some waking episodes. Never full blown sleep apnea panic mode, but just waking up fairly alert. I usually test EMF levels if I think I forgot to shut something off and they've been good (<25uw/m2). Most nights it only takes 15-30 min to fall back asleep. There were a couple of nights I'd find myself waking up really cold, probably due to not eating enough during the day. I found eating a larger dinner around an hour before sunset helps with this.

I do think some of the poor sleep nights were tied to post-sunset routine. If I have a phone near my face before bed, even with dark red blockers on and brightness at zero for too long my sleep is a lot less deep. The nights where I've had only candle light or a small laptop screen far away / shut off early I've slept very well. You definitely pay a higher toll in the winter for breaking rules than in the summer.

Going to read the photoreceptor evolution blogs and then go back to the 2011/12 stuff I've been working on. There's so much stuff to read and often current blogs reference older blogs that I'm not sure how to do this other than start from the beginning and build to present day, which at the rate Jack releases blogs, will be somewhere around 2030.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-12-18T17:20:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-364028>

**Sleep:**

Sleep is so much more sensitive lately to light/dark cycles and the absence of UV.

3 nights in a row I was very in line with my post sunset light environment. Computers turned off an hour before sunset. Candle or red light low and far away after dark. If I watched a movie it was on a small laptop screen with IRIS running at a distance. On each of these nights I was having trouble staying awake past 7pm. Sleep quality was very good, woke up feeling rested.

Last night I worked past sunset because had some very good opportunities. Finished about 2 hours post sunset. I could tell cortisol was high even with iris running and blue light blockers on. After getting into bed I had a really hard time relaxing. I kept wanting to be on my phone or to get stimulation before bed. Finally shut it all off around 8:45 but I remember waking up and tossing and turning more than usual. Woke up today tired and more groggy/slower cognitively.

**Decentralization:**

I am in the market for solar powered house boats that are as low nnEMF as possible. The marketplaces seem pretty insanely priced and mostly containing older boats, not many solar powered. Some of the boat builders that I've looked at have been underwhelming and pricey. I did find this company in the Germany which seemed kind of cool. I liked the aesthetics of the project.

### [24 Meter Waterloft](https://www.wohnschiffmanufaktur.de/24-meter-waterloft?lang=en)

 www.wohnschiffmanufaktur.de

Also looking for some some passports to certain countries in the tropics. Vietnam/Thailand area. Maybe go visit Rusty in India. I have a feeling some pacific islands might also be a viable option.

If anyone out there reads this and knows anyone, let me know.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-12-21T16:32:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-364110>

**SAD:**
Ya I realized I've got seasonal affective disorder still. It's really bad when I use any form of artificial light post sunset during the winter. I think this is due to circadian clock disruption which is easier to disrupt because solar yield is lower and BRIGHT mornings are a lot less bright during the winter (cloudier) months. When the AM sunrise "START" signal is weaker, it makes maintaining coherence harder later on in the 24 hour cycle. And I suspect lower UV = less melatonin production during the day. I basically have to go forward with ending my day when the sun goes down and minimal lighting before bed. Candles and reading a book seem like a pretty good approach. Maybe try the daylight too.

**Sleep:**
It's not been great the last week or so. I keep my room in the low 50's with a window open right next to me. I fall asleep fine but feel like I toss and turn and wake up more than usual. I was told I was snoring really "loud and dry" last night. Not gasping for air or waking up in panic mode, but very loud snoring. I also feel the urge to sleep longer, like 9-9.5 hours, which could also just be a seasonal thing when we currently have ~14 hours of darkness.

**Cold:**
Maybe I'm misremembering last winter, but I've got to say the cold adaptation this year has been really slow and arduous. Usually 2 weeks post thanksgiving I am pretty comfortable at 30 degrees and above, and can tolerate 20's alright with a little movement. But from idk 1 hour to 3 hours post sunrise on Dec 21 I'm really struggling, especially with my hands. I'm still getting muscle shivers even in high 30's low 40's and generally just having thoughts of wanting to go back inside. I'm better at dealing with cold than I was around thanksgiving but it's not that really hot furnace feeling that I recall from prior years. Limbs are cold, body doesn't feel cold but doesn't feel crazy warm either. It's odd because my nnEMF environment has improved a lot since last winter so if this is a redox issue I do wonder where the problem is coming from.

**Misc:**
Biggest white pill I've seen in the last few days is formal impeachment process for Pam Bondi was submitted by Massie + Khanna for the DOJ not following the law in releasing the Epstein files fully redacted (minus victim names). Still think the likelihood we get someone to do what needs to be done is <25%, it's at least a shot at an improvement.

### Mr.AJ — 2025-12-31T17:30:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-364406>

Had an interesting CT experience yesterday. Was 24 degrees during sunrise and in the high 20's/ low 30's most of the day. Did the usual AM sunrise stuff outside in a t shirt and shorts. Then had to drive about 3 hours so kept the windows down and sun roof open for the drive. Only turned on the seat warmer for a few min to warm my hands back up bc I had to drive. By the time this was over I basically could not stop shivering for most of the rest of the day. Was debating taking a warm shower at night because I was so cold. Was wondering if I pushed it too hard. Ended up doing a cold shower anyways. Went to bed that night and had probably the best night sleep of the last two weeks and woke up today (25 degrees) and feel a lot more comfortable in it than did just the day before. The cold I've been dealing with last 3 days finally broke last night into this morning too.

Not exactly sure what the takeaway here is, but I definitely appears that the body has gears it can turn on and off as the cold gets more extreme. What I thought was past my limit yesterday seems a lot easier today.

These were good. Decent amount was over my head. But I think the idea is information can be transferred even at low power densities, which increases the S:N ratio = loss of coherence.

Mitigation I've done so far:

Hardwire all devices
wifi off
bed >6ft from walls to have electric/magnetic fields below the building biology recommended levels.
wrapped smart meter in mesh, reading decreased by >90% (could still be an issue given your 1st post above but I can not replace it with an analogue one)
unplugged or killed any other RF emitting devices in the house

I live in a rural area with a lot of space between neighbors. Pop density is tiny. 2 towers within 2 miles, the most I've ever measured off of one of them was 200uW/m2 but it does not get nearly that high in my bedroom.

I don't really know what other mitigation things I can do. I'm in probably one of the better locations available in terms of population density, very far from major cities, airports, military bases.

Main problem currently:

There is an unknown source of RF that I can pick up in my bedroom. I can tell the general direction but have not been able to identify the source (opposite direction of tower).

### Mr.AJ — 2026-01-28T23:38:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-366067>

nnEMF update:

Primary culprit seems to be the tower, with most likely secondary option being someone placing trail cameras in the woods near my property but I think that's a decent bit less likely. All other avenue's I've checked have come back negative.

WRT the tower I'm not sure what to do about this because it's going to be a massive pain in the ass trying to shield. Need like 2-3 story tall, dense trees, which ruins a beautiful view.. Can not paint the walls bc they're primarily windows. And I would rather not use a canopy.

The best approach may be just choosing a different bedroom to sleep in. I've found two that are <10 uW/m2.

I do want to add that the sleep disturbances seem to be more connected to my light choices post sunset. The nights I do not break the rules I generally wake up 0-1 times and for shorter durations, and feel rested when I wake in the morning. If I am bad with a phone in my face even with all the blockers going sleep gets worse/lighter, more tired upon waking. When I do wake up it takes longer to fall back to sleep. So tower could be a factor here, it could also just be me cheating post sunset.

Winter:

We got smashed w snow over the weekend and it's been pretty shitty out and in the 10's and 20's. My cold tolerance this winter has been not as great as prior years. Definitely getting enough time out there and eating easily >4k cal per day and sleeping with a cold room but it's uncomfortable most days. Today was 35 and sunny which feels like 65 so I'm basically grateful for being able to sun my nuts in January.

### Mr.AJ — 2026-01-29T23:43:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-366104>

Wanted to flush out a more detailed report of the sleep issues as of late:

Sleep Disturbances:

"Bad sleep comes from bad solar redox and nnEMF/blue light hazard"

Coming from a sleep apnea background, have not used CPAP in 10 months. Subjective sleep score was like 6-9 most nights after discontinuing.

Over the last 2-3 weeks (late winter) I've been experiencing this maybe 5/7 nights:

Symptoms:
\-Waking up once a night between 11pm and 3am, thoughts racing, elevated pulse and heart beat. Will go to the bathroom sometimes but not every time. Takes time to fall back asleep. Have not tested my BP recently.
\-Waking up not feeling as rested or restored when this happens. (When it does not, or is a short wake up, I do feel rested and restored)
\-Constant tight muscles in thoracic spine area that moves the rib cage up and down, out and in. They also feel like they need to be popped constantly. This has gotten worse since winter which I would guess is tied to shivering/tighter muscles. When I do wake up at night, if I can use deep breathing and get my thoracic spine to pop, I almost immediately fall back to sleep with little issues after.
\-Very little thirst/water craving ever. I have to remind myself to drink water throughout the day.
\-Constipation and irregular bathroom times (seems like maybe gut clock is out of whack?).
\-Memory and recall not very good. Often lose train of thought, especially the first couple hours of the morning.

Current protocol:
\-Wake up 5:30am, outside sunrise 7:05am.
\-BAB ~8am. Outside as much as possible with as little clothes on as possible, if I do have to be inside for a time, windows open + iris.
\-Med lunch around noon
\-Dinner around 4pm, total daily cal ~4k give or take a couple hundred, dinner is in the 700-900 cal region.
\-Beef, pork, eggs, lard, butter, salmon, oysters, goat Keifer from farmer near me is basically all I eat.
\-Sunset 5:10, inside 5:20-30. Cold shower, relax. Candle light and/or red lit screen + BLB. Lights out by 8pm at the absolute latest and bed. (Could the polarized light/cortisol be the culprit?) Been trying to do more podcasts where I can have the phone hardwired and not looking at it.
\-8pm everything off and I fall asleep quickly and with no problems. I've usually already fallen asleep once to the podcast. Bedroom has windows open so it gets cold at night. I'll shiver a bit as I try to fall asleep when it's really cold out. Often times when I do wake up at night I feel like I'm roasting/not cold at all.

If I exercise it's capped at 35-40 min. Will say the last few days exercise has been harder.

Other notes:

Wifi disabled, phones off and out of the room, bed >6ft from all walls. I do have RF readings that peak around 60uW/m2 coming I believe from a tower, but this has been here since I've owned the place and I haven't been doing this wake up thing at night as often or as intensely, and no waking up to go to the bathroom (could this just be affecting me more due to less sunlight available in winter?)

Outside of this, solar yield has been lower, obviously, and we've had some weather and more cloudy days. So curious if this is just a low redox problem, is my red light use after sunset/tower nnEMF the problem, or if I possibly need to move my last meal maybe 30 minutes later and add a couple hundred more calories before bed. (Really not that hungry by dinner truthfully).

Was gonna post this in ask [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) but figured mb this is a better place for it.

### Woodstock — 2026-01-31T19:14:10+0200

I measured a house with zero average RFs yesterday. But the poor woman had pain in her brain. She mentioned using a microwave oven, and when I showed her that her nearly zero exposure went up to 3 million, she immediately unplugged it and said she was getting rid of it. She can’t do online at all she is hurting so bad so I doubt she will get on this forum. She was feeling too bad to even cook so she thought she was just making it easier with the microwave. She lives near an ocean but she hasn’t even been to it in months. But she does eat oysters. I told her to swim in the ocean every day, ground in the black volcanic sand and eat more oysters. That’s all she has the energy for right now. I know I can’t explain the science properly and there’s so much about the meters I still have to learn BUT I helped someone in desperate need. And that is a blessing. Just praying her boyfriend can help her beyond that and get her more info.

Even a little knowledge can go a long way. I did what I could. I hope her life takes a healing turn.

Slept fine until 5:30 after falling back asleep.

### Anthony Smith — 2026-03-02T01:24:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-367891>

> [Mr.AJ said:](/goto/post?id=367885)
>
> Was it quarts glass? Or what kind of glass did they use back then?

OK, there was “some” filtration but nothing like modern glass.

Tolkien was a professor at Oxford 1925-1959. All the windows at Oxford have small pane clear glass. All his homes had the same. The hotel in Bournemouth where he spent weeks/months at a time was built in the early 1900’s and also has big windows made up of small pane clear glass panels. Very energy inefficient ie they let through MOST of the IR and UV light. Almost everyone was up at dawn.

Also remember windows were open most of the time, even in winter, I grew up in England. We wore layers. Notice Tolkien wearing a wool suit and waist coat, cotton shirt and almost certainly an undershirt. Unless it was raining that window would have been open (if only to let the smoke from his pipe out).

Even modern clear float glass (invented 1959) a crystal-clear piece of glass with no coatings, no special treatments, and no tricks up its sleeve. It’s the purest form of glass, giving you a clean, undistorted view and letting in nearly all the natural light. But while it might look flawless, clear glass has a weakness: it doesn’t know how to control heat. In the summer, sunlight (IR) pours through it, heating up your interiors like a greenhouse. In winter, the warmth inside your room escapes right out through the glass. It also allows nearly all UV rays to pass, which means your carpets, curtains, and wooden floors are constantly exposed to fading and damage over time.

In short, clear glass is honest and beautiful — but it’s terrible at saving energy . . . It lets MOST of the full spectrum light through, including IR and UV.

The UV transmittance of an uncoated (modern) clear glass is around 0.6 (60%) and the UV rejection of it is around 0.4 (40%). Is that 40% important, sure but many PVB interlayer or window film products (low e glass introduced in the 1970’s) can effectively block “all” UV radiation, with less than 0.01 (1%) of UV transmittance or greater than 0.99 (99%) of UV rejection and also a lot of IR.

We live in different times and all the differences add up.

### Mr.AJ — 2026-03-02T19:16:23+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-367924>

Does BTC make 401k's and IRA's obsolete?

Option 1: 70k invested into Vanguard's Total US Markets Index assume (5% real returns after inflation/taxes) over 30 years = 313,718.23.

\-You are locked out until retirement (60-65)
\-Not your keys not your coins
\-"The Great Taking" risks

Option 2: Pay Taxes on the 70k (using highest tax bracket) = 41,900 remaining. You would need 7% YoY returns on BTC over the next 30 years to be indifferent on the dollar side.

\-Can use whenever
\-Fully self custodied
\-Historical returns have been 60% YoY+, and even if they are decreasing YoY it still seems reasonable to assume >7% is attainable.

What incentive does anyone have to invest money into retirement accounts anymore?

One argument I can make is automation and AI will lead to increased productivity, which in fucked up fiat world may lead to higher stock valuations. But as Jeff Booth says, "The Free Market is naturally deflationary." And if BTC is the free-est market, because it can not be fucked with my humans, you would expect all of those gains to cause deflation inside of BTC (prices fall relative to BTC) because is it the purest representation of the free market we have. So I think even in an AI/robotics world you are still likely to see BTC out compete simply because it's closer to the truth than fiat.

### Mr.AJ — 2026-03-03T16:31:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-367990>

How do you think the geoengineering is being done?

I've tracked a lot of the planes flying over my house and the majority of them are either commercial airliners or private jets, most of which have public destinations and tracking numbers written on them. I've heard of cases where non-public planes have been spraying but I haven't seen evidence of it.

Also Anthony has mentioned contrails occurring due to certain atmospheric conditions at 30k-40k ft. (cold air below −40°C, low pressure (~30–55 kPa), and RHᵢ above ~70%). I've looked at daily atmospheric weather reports and on days the trails are sticky the weather does meet every single one of these requirements (small sample so far).

I'm not ruling out potential additives to commercial airliner fuel to do it -> it's probably the cheapest and most logical way for the feds to spray. And the skies were not this sprayed 10-20 years ago (fewer planes?). But atmospheric conditions do seem to play a roll in whether they stick around or not.

I think a spectrometer may add more data to see if it's just frozen water spreading across the sky, or if it's actually altering solar yield beyond a normal cloudy day. Also rain samples. But to be fair with the amount of other shit in the environment it's difficult to tell where exactly the rain water pollution is coming from.

### Mr.AJ — 2026-03-04T00:09:40+0200

Trying to figure out why UVA can be detected on a spectrometer during higher latitude winter but Dr Jack says "is not available" many months out of the year at high latitude. Is this a power or intensity problem? Ie: the frequency is there it's just not strong enough to elicit the commands we want?

[https://www.mdpi.com/2072-4292/13/4/752](https://www.mdpi.com/2072-4292/13/4/752)

[https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S2352938524002878](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S2352938524002878)

[https://gml.noaa.gov/grad/neubrew/docs/publications/Fioletov\_JGR2004.pdf](https://gml.noaa.gov/grad/neubrew/docs/publications/Fioletov_JGR2004.pdf)

This last one was probably the closest thing I could find. It did not detect 380nm, but 286-363nm. January UV levels suck basically above 30N. There's a slight snow refractory benefit, maybe adding 20- 25% benefit (altitude dependent) in the most northern states where snow happens.

Interestingly on a few of the studies, cloudy days has higher 380nm detection than sunny days.

Try again tomorrow.

### Mr.AJ — 2026-03-20T17:48:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-369284>

People are very often concerned with their kids not socializing enough. While that is a valid concern, I believe it's possible to be over socialized as well. To the point where you have no self-identity or original thoughts. Your entire being is of the group, which IMO can be as bad, if not worse, than being the knuckle dragging-grunting, unsocialized person.

Trying to imagine back in the day, you had your tribe. Maybe 50-150 people. Within that group there would be sub groups, hunters, farmers, chef's, seamstress/tailor, etc that you would primarily spend your time with. Along with times where the entire tribe would get together. So there were enough people, and time spent with people, to develop social skills and relationships. But also sufficient alone time connected to nature while doing chores, waiting on a hunt, fishing, etc. And while you do not talk to each other during the hunt, there is a bond/connection being built through the silence of a shared directive or goal with your tribe members.

I think if those times of active socialization and interactions coupled with solitude in nature are not balanced properly, you wind up with under developed or pathologically over developed aspects of yourself. And in today's modern age, the alone time connected to nature has basically been removed from most people's lives. It's been replaced with siting in a bedroom in front of a blue lit screen, talking to people on Xbox or w/e. You really have to make a deliberate effort to get that reconnection time to nature. I think parents really need to be mindful of this going forward. The solution is not found sitting in your bedroom, nor socializing in major cities or suburbs, nor by going away to college. But all of it can be found through reconnecting with nature.

### Mr.AJ — 2026-03-26T02:01:05+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-369643>

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tech-addiction-living-post-sunset.32455/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/tech-addiction-living-post-sunset.32455/)

Going to parse out this addiction thread:

Tyrosine (AAA) -> L-DOPA -> can be made into either melanin or dopamine via Tyrosinase or DOPA decarboxylase

Sounds like deuterium in the wrong places, coupled with poor removal of waste/mass from CSF.

\---------------------

**2\. Withdrawal/Negative Affect Stage (Stress and Dysphoria)**
When substance use stops, the brain's stress systems become overactive while reward sensitivity declines.

**Extended Amygdala:** This group of structures, including the **Central Nucleus of the Amygdala (CeA)** and the **Bed Nucleus of the Stria Terminalis (BNST)**, regulates reactions to stress. It is responsible for the feelings of anxiety, irritability, and unease during withdrawal.

**Hypothalamus:** Interacts with the extended amygdala to control hormone-producing glands that manage the body's physiological stress response.

**3\. Preoccupation/Anticipation Stage (Executive Function and Craving)**
This stage involves the "hijacking" of executive control, making it difficult to resist triggers and maintain abstinence.

**Prefrontal Cortex (PFC):** Responsible for complex cognitive processes like decision-making, impulse control, and prioritizing tasks. In addiction, "Go" circuits in the PFC can become overactive (promoting drug-seeking), while "Stop" circuits become underactive (reducing self-control).

**Hippocampus and Basolateral Amygdala (BLA):** These regions process memories of drug-associated cues (people, places, things), which can trigger intense cravings even after long periods of abstinence.

**Insula:** Involved in interoceptive awareness and conscious perception of drug cravings.

**Lateral Habenula (LHb):** Increasingly recognized for its role in signaling aversive states and inhibiting dopamine firing, which can contribute to the "dark side" of addiction and relapse.

Ya I think this is probably a thing for me. I would say during the day when UV is good (I would even say UV 4 is enough) I can feel the beta endorphin release and a lot of this stuff is not there. When the sun is bad or it goes down, I do feel like I spend a lot of my time in the stress and dysphoria state.

I would say even during the winter when it's really cold, I did not get the same beta endorphin feeling. It was mostly like "fuck it's cold adapt already" and basically never got better than that all winter.

And I've 100% noticed the quality and type of thoughts I have when I'm outside vs inside are very different. Outdoor thoughts are so much more positive than indoor thoughts.

\------------------------

**8\. Lack of Solar Exposure**
When a mammal (including humans) is not in the sun enough, the drop in beta-endorphin can lead to specific behavioral and physiological changes:

**Vitamin D Feedback:** Evolutionarily, this system may have developed to ensure humans sought enough sun to synthesize Vitamin D. Research indicates that **Vitamin D deficiency** can actually _increase_ the drive to seek UV light, as the brain tries to "re-up" the beta-endorphin levels it associates with solar exposure.

**Anhedonia and Low Mood:** Without the regular "boost" of beta-endorphin from the sun, some individuals experience a drop in mood or increased sensitivity to pain. This is often a component of **Seasonal Affective Disorder (SAD)**, where the lack of solar-triggered endorphins contributes to lethargy and low motivation.

**Withdrawal Symptoms:** In individuals who have become "addicted" to UV exposure (often seen in chronic tanners), a sudden lack of sun can trigger physical **withdrawal symptoms**—such as anxiety and tremors—similar to those seen in opioid withdrawal, because the addiction nuclei (like the **Extended Amygdala**) are no longer receiving their regular "fix".

This connection completes the "addiction Lagrangian." In my framework, addiction isn't a moral failure or a simple "receptors" problem; it is a mass-loading error in the brain's quantum fluid.

Yup. I think I get the UV withdrawal big time and definitely have SAD. I would say Thanksgiving until idk Valentines day is tough at my latitude.

\---------------

**10\. Melanin Destruction & Low Dopamine**
I’ve identified neuromelanin (NM) as the stabilizer. When NM is destroyed, either by the high-energy "M1 supernova" of oxidative stress or the physical disruption of deuterium, the system collapses:
Loss of the Quencher: Neuromelanin normally sequesters toxic metals and reactive dopamine species. When it is "bleached" or destroyed, these toxins leak into the cytosol.

**Dopamine Depletion:** The brain compensates for the energy exhaust by shutting down dopamine production to save power. This leads to the low-dopamine state characteristic of chronic addiction, where the patient feels "flat" and requires higher and higher "external shocks" (drugs/tech) just to feel a baseline signal.

Wow. I never thought of dopamine depletion as a protective mechanism. I always thought there was just a set level = health, high or low level = disease. But I guess that makes sense if it can be a form of inflammation and diseases like schizophrenia exist.

\---------

Because melanin is a **Chiral Organic Semiconductor**, it acts as a "traffic controller" for electron spin. This chirality allows it to filter electrons based on their spin state (CISS effect). If the spin is not filtered, the "software" (the electromagnetic signaling in the brain) becomes noisy. In addiction, this "noise" manifests as the loss of executive control in the **Prefrontal Cortex**.

So your environment dictates not only how well you can think, but your self control and executive function. Kind of reminds me of that squirrel eating another squirrel in an nnEMF shithole.
\----------------------------------

So it sounds like nature built beta endorphin into POMC from the sun to actually use this pathway to addict us to the sun in order to maintain optics, structure and coherence. I need to read more about spin filtering (CISS). It's been in some of the blogs but need to get a better understanding there.

### Mr.AJ — 2026-04-03T17:07:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-370168>

In the movie Ghostbusters, the primary villain, Gozer the Gozerian, manifests itself in our reality and presents to the Ghostbusters/humanity itself a choice: I am going to destroy your world, you may choose the form that the destructor manifests in your reality. Ray imagines the state puff marshmallow guy and all of a sudden you see a giant marshmallow man destroying NYC. The Ghostbusters decide, in a point of peak desperation, to cross the streams, knowing full well they may risk destroying the entire universe in doing so. Given the circumstances, if they do nothing, they are all destroyed, if they cross the streams, there is at least some % probability that they can rip open the fabric of spacetime and send Gozer out of their reality, thereby saving the universe. So game theory suggests you choose the option that has the highest total game value, all probabilities summed. We've all seen the movie and know how it ends.

I want to focus on the choice presented to the Ghostbusters by Gozer. It was essentially not a real choice. You were just given the option to choose the aesthetics of how you were destroyed. I see parallels between this movie and the current world we find ourselves living in today. The 2024 election was very similar to the choice the Ghostbusters had. There was always going to be some sort of "event." Something that triggered the economic reset. Trump offered the "peak America" 1980's nostalgia version. You get good old fashioned American imperialism. CIA/spec ops doing CIA/spec ops type things. Governments overthrown. Military might. Kamala was the climate change, SJW, progressive aesthetic. Just as Trump's administration reeks of incompetence, you'd see a Biden/Obama 4.0 presidency, and some other flavor of "reset" event.

I think back to the interview Bill Gates did where he said "we're either going to have a war or another pandemic." I believe he understands exactly what Trump/Kamala represented, and the reason he couldn't tell you which one, is because he did not know which aesthetic choice the people would make. The other possibility is that Trump blunders this Iran thing massively, tries to get a 3rd term, the left and potentially some factions of the military try to stop him, and then we get Venezuela 2028 followed by another viral lab leak/covid 2.0

I think politics in America today is essentially the same "choice" Gozer presented to the Ghostbusters. You're going to get your reset. You're going to get your NWO. You're going to get your surveillance state. But we will allow you to choose the aesthetics of how it's done. Anyone I speak to today who gets politically involved in the "left/right" dichotomy, I immediately dismiss. They are not even conscious enough to see the trap they're stuck in so I do not know how anything they say following that can be useful.

There is a lesson, that personally I do not have the precise answer to, that we can learn from the Ghostbuster's response. You have to do something out of the box, unconventional, potentially dangerous and extreme, to break yourself out of the frame they're trying to keep you in. Maybe that's "decentralizing your life" as Jack says. Maybe that's "exiting the system" like Jeff Boothe says. Whatever it is, we as individuals, and collectively, need to find our way to "cross our streams" in order to destroy/exit/free ourselves from the system built around us. You cannot trust a politician to do it. You cannot play their game in any way shape or form.

How do they survive? What are you doing to continually give them air to breathe? How can you live a good life while bleeding the beast at the same time?

\------------------------------------------

Semi unrelated topic: I think the purpose of the surveillance system may have been a way to fix what was broken during the Vietnam war. I am not super well read on the war and the political environment in the US during it, other than simply knowing the way was a scam and no one wanted it to happen and there was a ton of protesting/draft dodging. I do wonder if the reason Palantir/etc is being built is to make sure that can not happen during the next war. We really have not had a large scale boots on the ground/military draft since Vietnam. I think that is telling. Even Iraq/Afghanistan was mostly voluntary and 9/11 was used to preemptively promote political will for the war/improve recruitment numbers. I think if/when Palantir is fully functional and Iran escalates, it's first use case is going to be to prevent draft dodging and real time/full time location tracking.

### Mr.AJ — 2026-04-08T19:00:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-370488>

[http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cpap-vs-redox-why-the-body-rejects-the-air-pump-fix.32512/](http://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cpap-vs-redox-why-the-body-rejects-the-air-pump-fix.32512/)

Just to explain my n=1 for having sleep apnea and dumping the CPAP machine. The entire process was actually really easy and painless. I was following the protocols of AM sunrise + sun maxxing all day + dark nights. for close to a year while still using my CPAP. I DID NOT have my nnEMF environment is very good shape during or even for a while after stopping the CPAP. But I think the constant AM sunrise and overall increase in sun time allowed me to handle oxygen better.

When I did decide to kick the CPAP fully, I focused on a few things: 1) Head/mouth positioning. I laid on my side and hugged a pillow below my chin to keep my mouth as closed as I could. 2) I used cold, either chewing on crushed ice and/or an ice bath or cold shower before bed. 3) I made sure to practice good tongue position (pushing and holding tongue to the roof of my mouth) regularly and especially when going to sleep. 4) I tracked and monitored both number of waking times I remembered and overall quality of how rested I felt in the morning.

For a while I felt kind of sleepy/groggy uponm waking but after an hour of AM sunrise I felt completely awake and good. Over time this grogginess lessened, especially once I improved the nnEMF environment.

I think the main idea when starting to kick the CPAP is to just make sure you're focusing on good head/tongue position and be persistent, give it time.

Train your body to use ALL the lungs not just middle.

### Mr.AJ — 2026-04-27T16:00:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-372196>

Reading QE 47-48? I think on ipRGC's really opened my eyes to the mental health/mood stuff. Looking at the illustration showing depression in winter, mania peaks in the summer was odd to me. I was aware of the depression side, I just assumed the other side was "not depressed." I wouldn't say I get manic, at least by the technical definition, but I am much more "up" I guess you could say, during the summer time.

But I did notice that when I use tech post sunset, even in the summer, there is a toll. I wake up much more lethargic and brain foggy than when I do not. It recovers more quicky because the better AM sun, but I don't think I realized the effect before reading those blogs and going through what I did last winter.

Two nights ago I was sitting in my back yard watching the sunset and thought to myself "man I could fall asleep right here right now and be out like a rock, why is it that 1-2 hours from now I'll feel stimulated and awake?" The answer pretty obviously was too much ALAN. So the last two nights I just turned everything off 1.5 hours before sunset and just went to bed when it got dark outside. I have to say it was the best 2 nights of sleep I've had in a while. And I woke up feeling good and refreshed.

I'm going to monitor the lethargy more closely. I don't know if this is an ALAN problem or just a repair mechanism from now living in a low nnEMF environment after a decade of nnEMF induced excitotoxicity, but it's definitely been more noticeable since the weather has gotten better. The norepinephrine buzz from cold weather I think was either hiding it or reducing it.

But I will say I have 2 bumblebees, 2 bats (Bruce and Wayne), rabbits, lizards, and a ton of different birds hanging out around my house. Not the worst environment to be in.

### Mr.AJ — 2026-04-30T19:02:34+0300

### [Historical Magnetic Declination Viewer](https://www.ncei.noaa.gov/maps/historical-declination/)

 www.ncei.noaa.gov

I really have no idea where to start with this. I do not have a physics or engineering background and understand very little about magnetism outside of what I've read on the forum.

I can see the Minneapolis to Mexico City line Jack was referring to. Is the entire midwest, east coast, and global south (TN/GA/AL/FL/NC/SC) all in trouble now? Is west of the Mississippi actually improving it's magnetism as a result of this? (Death Valley Rain maxing?)

As far as I understand, the goal is to select locations on the planet where you are experiencing inclination where the new magnetic north lies, or at least not declination. You need your shield to protect from heavy isotopes + run CCO. And there's also a "proton pinning" aspect I don't fully understand yet. Either way If this is correct, this seems pretty challenging given the rate at which the poll is moving. Locations chosen today could definitely change in the next 5-10 years.

I guess I'm picturing it as a plasma field going around the entire planet. When one poll shifts a direction, the field going from one poll closer to the other will expand/incline/wide on one side, and decline/shrink on the other side. And you would prefer to be on the side that is experiencing the incline not the decline. The SAA I guess would be seen as a weaking/almost collapse of the field entirely?

Will keep exploring.

But my problem is I live on a corner and the local overhead street distribution lines run along the road on both the south side and west side of my home. So the AC magnetic field strength from those two interacting make my house crap to live in without having a magnetically shielded room.

### Mr.AJ — 2026-05-10T18:06:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-stay-away-from-nnemf.30545/post-373654>

**Hardening, Hardness of Heart**
In the Scriptures various aspects of human anatomy are used to define the whole person, but the most frequently used is heart. The heart is the seat of emotion ( [Psalm 25:1](https://www.biblestudytools.com/psalms/25-1.html) ; [Prov 14:10](https://www.biblestudytools.com/proverbs/14-10.html) ; [Isa 66:14](https://www.biblestudytools.com/isaiah/66-14.html) ; [John 14:1](https://www.biblestudytools.com/john/14-1.html) ; [Rom 9:2](https://www.biblestudytools.com/romans/9-2.html) ), intelligence ( [Prov 16:1](https://www.biblestudytools.com/proverbs/16-1.html) ; [Luke 9:47](https://www.biblestudytools.com/luke/9-47.html) ), morality ( [Psalm 58:2](https://www.biblestudytools.com/psalms/58-2.html) ; [Rom 1:24](https://www.biblestudytools.com/romans/1-24.html) ), human choice ( [Deut 8:2](https://www.biblestudytools.com/deuteronomy/8-2.html) ; [Luke 21:34](https://www.biblestudytools.com/luke/21-34.html) ; [Acts 11:23](https://www.biblestudytools.com/acts/11-23.html) ), and one's religious life ( [Deut 6:5](https://www.biblestudytools.com/deuteronomy/6-5.html) ; [Jer 31:33](https://www.biblestudytools.com/jeremiah/31-33.html) ; [Rom 10:9-10](https://www.biblestudytools.com/passage/?q=Romans+10:9-10) ; [Gal 4:6](https://www.biblestudytools.com/galatians/4-6.html) ). The heart, in effect, is the whole person in all of his or her distinctive human activity as a thinking, planning, willing, feeling, worshiping, socially interacting being. And, of course, when the person is not living according to God's will, it is the heart that is described as darkened, rebellious, callous, unfeeling, or idolatrous. It is within the heart that God works; hence the human heart may be tender and soft or as hard as stone ( [Eze 11:19](https://www.biblestudytools.com/ezekiel/11-19.html) ). It is in this context that hardening or hardness of the heart must be understood. The heart represents the total response of a person to life around him or her and to the religious and moral demands of God. Hardness of heart thus describes a negative condition in which the person ignores, spurns, or rejects the gracious offer of God to be a part of his or her life.

Jesus speaks of a general condition of human hardness (_sklerokardian_ \[[sklhrokardiva](https://www.biblestudytools.com/lexicons/greek/nas/sklerokardia.html)\]) that God takes into consideration when dealing with us ( [Matt 19:7-8](https://www.biblestudytools.com/passage/?q=Matthew+19:7-8) ; [Mark 10:4-5](https://www.biblestudytools.com/passage/?q=Mark+10:4-5) ). The heart is deceitful above all things and beyond cure ( [Jer 17:9](https://www.biblestudytools.com/jeremiah/17-9.html) ); it is hard, but not necessarily hardened. Hardening of the heart goes beyond the tragic obtuseness of our inherited condition. So, working upon the fertile ground of our innately hard hearts, sin may harden them further ( [Dan 5:20](https://www.biblestudytools.com/daniel/5-20.html) ; [Eph 4:18](https://www.biblestudytools.com/ephesians/4-18.html) ; [Heb 3:12](https://www.biblestudytools.com/hebrews/3-12.html) ). People may harden their own hearts, in sinful rebellion, in bitterness over circumstances, or in sheer self-will ( [Exod 9:34-35](https://www.biblestudytools.com/passage/?q=Exodus+9:34-35) ; [2 Chron 36:13](https://www.biblestudytools.com/2-chronicles/36-13.html) ; [Zech 7:12](https://www.biblestudytools.com/zechariah/7-12.html) ; [Heb 3:15](https://www.biblestudytools.com/hebrews/3-15.html) ). In a few instances, such as Pharaoh and the Egyptians ( [Exod 7:3](https://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/7-3.html) ; [9:12](https://www.biblestudytools.com/exodus/9-12.html) ), Sihon, king of Heshbon ( [Deut 2:30](https://www.biblestudytools.com/deuteronomy/2-30.html) ), and the Hivites living in Gibeon ( [Jos 11:19-20](https://www.biblestudytools.com/passage/?q=Joshua+11:19-20) ), it is said that God hardened their hearts. There is something of a mystery here, but apparently these people were so irremediable in their rebellion against God that God entered into the hardening process so that he could accomplish his purposes in spite of, and yet in and through, that hardness. It is God's prerogative, as God, to do this ( [Rom 9:18-21](https://www.biblestudytools.com/passage/?q=Romans+9:18-21) ). That they were morally responsible for their condition is a theological given, and we are warned not to harden our hearts as they did, a command that would make no sense if hardening were simply God's act ( [1 Sam 6:6](https://www.biblestudytools.com/1-samuel/6-6.html) ).

Israel's hardening as a nation represents a special set of circumstances. In the psalms, the wicked are described as having a calloused heart ( [17:10](https://www.biblestudytools.com/psalms/17-10.html) ; [73:7](https://www.biblestudytools.com/psalms/73-7.html) ; [119:70](https://www.biblestudytools.com/psalms/119-70.html) ). God tells Isaiah that Israel, with its calloused heart, will reject him as God's messenger when he goes to them ( [Isa 6:9-10](https://www.biblestudytools.com/passage/?q=Isaiah+6:9-10) ). This event was taken as prophetic by Jesus ( [Matt 13:14-15](https://www.biblestudytools.com/passage/?q=Matthew+13:14-15) ) and Paul ( [Acts 28:25-27](https://www.biblestudytools.com/passage/?q=Acts+28:25-27) ) as referring to Israel's rejection of Jesus as God's Messiah. For Paul, Israel's hardening paved the way to a ministry to the Gentiles ( [Acts 28:28](https://www.biblestudytools.com/acts/28-28.html) ) and was not intended by God to be final, but only until the full number of the Gentiles had come in; then all Israel will be saved ( [Rom 11:25-27](https://www.biblestudytools.com/passage/?q=Romans+11:25-27) ).

For the believers of both the Old Testament ( [Psalm 95:8](https://www.biblestudytools.com/psalms/95-8.html) ) and the New Testament ( [Hebrews 3:8](https://www.biblestudytools.com/hebrews/3-8.html) [Hebrews 3:15](https://www.biblestudytools.com/hebrews/3-15.html) ; [4:7](https://www.biblestudytools.com/hebrews/4-7.html) ) the hardness of Israel's heart served as a warning and a challenge not to react in the same way. Jesus, at one point, alludes to this by rebuking his disciples for the hardness of their hearts ( [Mark 8:17-21](https://www.biblestudytools.com/passage/?q=Mark+8:17-21) ).

Ultimately the hardness of the human heart can only be repaired by the grace of God. It is he who can restore us, by taking away our heart of stone and giving us a heart of flesh ( [Eze 11:18-21](https://www.biblestudytools.com/passage/?q=Ezekiel+11:18-21) ).

\-------------------

A lot of religious historical researchers claim that God is a representation of the sun. Even the "He died and on the 3rd day was resurrected" could be considered a nod to the movement of the sun through the winter solstice. And Easter "He is risen" (the sun is starting to cook after a long winter) is considered the true New Year if you used a 13 month/28 day day calendar. Whether you buy that or not, DARPA certainly likes to "harden people's hearts" whether through mRNA or technology or propagation of vice and sin or through financial means. And coincidentally, the way to protect yourself/heal yourself from that is through grounded sunlight.

I dunno what it all means. I just see some parallels there.

# Thread: Cowgirl Michelle's Journal 2025
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cowgirl-michelles-journal-2025.30549/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michelle Blair — 2025-03-25T21:40:41+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cowgirl-michelles-journal-2025.30549/post-349337>

I intentionally named my optimal journal _Cowgirl Michelle_. Why? Because the authentic cowgirl is connected with nature, land and animals. She lives through her senses and her instincts—the language of her soul. She is the archetypal image of the goddess, the feminine quality of life.

In November 2023, I completed my PhD in Philosophy and Wisdom Studies. This academic pilgrimage was not just a scholarly pursuit—it was a soul-calling, prompted by a rupture: a divorce after over 20 years with a sociopath. I realized that unless I took responsibility for my choices, true change would never come.

My university was not ordinary. It demanded deep engagement with earth wisdom and first principles found in ancient cultures, especially those of Native peoples. Along the way, I discovered that the “good woman” I’d strived to be was a social construct—a mechanistic, monotheistic distortion that had cut me off from my instincts. I’ve been untying the corset ever since.

I live in Cowtown, where the West begins. During my doctoral work, I began working deeply with my imaginal life and dreams. My dissertation, _Cowgirl as Goddess: An Inquiry into an Archetypal Image of the Instinctual Feminine and Texas Culture_, marked a reckoning. I know longer believed in the authoritarian Papa God. My salvation comes from within and the universe tinkers people and knowledge onto my path. Dr. Jack Kruse’s work arrived into my sphere last year; his language of light resonates like an old friend. My past craft was an architectural lighting designer in New York City.

After studying many blogs and listening to Dr. Kruse’s lectures, I now see the Cowgirl through a quantum lens. Now, I wonder if her instinctual life is BECAUSE of her lifestyle wearing leather boots, grounded through her horse and living according to the rhythms of life.

There is much work ahead for me—the restoration of my health and understanding decentralized wealth. I end this first entry with a poem I wrote in 2017 that continues to reveal itself to me.

**This willful shouting identity is dissolving
a deep wild redeeming voice is rising.
I am listening.
The standards of others applied,
yet it is fakery, a trick.
I find my truth in my bones and blood.
It is the only valuable I can offer,
there is no worth in the nobility
of others' mediocrity.
My shameless life
flames without apologies.**

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-25T23:12:50+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cowgirl-michelles-journal-2025.30549/post-349344>

Welcome.

I like this declaration of self-discovery and defiance against imposed norms. It feels like a shedding of external expectations, a reclaiming of something primal and authentic. The "deep wild redeeming voice" rising within you suggests a powerful inner shift, one that drowns out the "willful shouting identity" of conformity or pretense. I hear a rejection of fakery, a refusal to bend to the "standards of others," and an embrace of something truer, rooted in your very "bones and blood."

This reflection strikes at the heart of living authentically and purposefully. Continue.

### Michelle Blair — 2025-03-27T00:37:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cowgirl-michelles-journal-2025.30549/post-349409>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=349344)
>
> Welcome.
>
> I like this declaration of self-discovery and defiance against imposed norms. It feels like a shedding of external expectations, a reclaiming of something primal and authentic. The "deep wild redeeming voice" rising within you suggests a powerful inner shift, one that drowns out the "willful shouting identity" of conformity or pretense. I hear a rejection of fakery, a refusal to bend to the "standards of others," and an embrace of something truer, rooted in your very "bones and blood."
>
> This reflection strikes at the heart of living authentically and purposefully. Continue.

Thank you for reading. I am resolute and willing! Here we go...

Mary

I fast 2 days before my rescans, promoting autophagy and trying to mitigate the negative effects of the scans, and drink 2 liters of water each day, to flush my kidneys.

If there is anything else I can do, I would do it.

# Thread: SAB3 Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sab3-optimal-journal.30558/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 32
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-03-26T17:31:44+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sab3-optimal-journal.30558/post-349387>

I was born August 3, 1950 in Brookhaven, MS. I have 2 older sisters, Barbara (Briscoe) Kohnert, 3 years older, passed away from Lung cancer with the EGFR mutation on March 30.2022, just a few days from 3 years ago. Since mitochondria are inherited from the maternal side, our mother had lung cancer in the 70's, but was years in remission before her passing from heart issues a few years ago, also.

Susan (Briscoe) Schaeffer is 4 years older, and got her lungs scanned after Barbara's involvement with cancer, and is healthy, and living in Colorado presently. Preemptively had bilateral breast removal out of caution from our mother's breast cancer years earlier. Susan is a retired hospice nurse, so we can discuss medical and science stuff freely.

My father ended his career with Anheuser-Busch in St. Louis. as director of Quality Control, obtaining his engineering background at St. Louis University, from the GI Bill, settling in St. Louis, my mom's birthplace.

We moved from St. Louis in 1964, to Houston, TX, where I went to Memorial High School, choosing Centenary College for my undergraduate degree in Biology, with a minor in Chemistry, attending the University of Missouri Medical School, in Columbia, MO in 1972 as a Ph.D. candidate in Pharmacology, having done a year of research my Senior year at Centenary under Dr. Jamison, in the Dept. of Virology at LSU Med School, being told I could continue my virology research at U.M.MS, which was a ruse to get me to enroll in their graduate program. Once there, I was told they had no funds for my research, so I left at the end of '72 to go back to Shreveport, where I had made life long connections, eventually working at Charity Hospital, later becoming LSUMC, in Pathology, helping with autopsies, while working with all the pathologists, attracting a head-hunter with Coulter Electronics, who was looking for someone like me to sell their blood chemistry analyzers to pathology departments at all hospitals.

I did that for a few years, which ended shortly after a serious car crash in East Texas, which, by that time, I had become interested in holistic medicine, and decided to start with earning a paying degree, which Biology was not, and entered the first Respiratory Therapy class at LSUMC. I did the first year, but not the clinical second year, having decided to go back and retake classes at LSU Shreveport, with the plan of going to Medical School, as allied health was not challenging for me.

Even though I excelled in retaking all the classes I felt I needed to pass the MCAT, I had to settle for the highest score on the GRE exam and was accepted into the Dept. of Physiology at LSUMC, where I was still able to take some of the first year medical school classes, to begin my goal of pursuing both a Ph.D. and eventual MD degree.

Plans, no matter how hard fought they took to achieve, ended when my marriage took a u-turn, and involved me getting full custody of my 3 precious children, a mostly unheard of event for a male at that time.

I was already working for 3 different medically-related companies while carrying a full load at graduate school, so after my unscheduled departure from grad school, I put all my energies into my work and taking care of my children, who were 5, 8, and 11 at the time of the divorce.

Life was very good for those years, as my bond continued to strengthen with the 3 most important people in my life, and remarriage was not even on my radar, as I expected no one would want any part of my life. In spite of my not looking to establish another relationship, close friends wanted me to share their misery of being married, I jest, and introduced me to Jan, who after a long 7-year courtship, would become my wife in 2005.

My life was very fulfilling, and I became a Gentleman Racer, as it's called, competing in a semi-pro level with the Trophy East Porsche Series, and the Pro-Am Pirelli World Challenge SRO series, co-driving a Porsche GT4 with my coach, and good friend, Andrew Davis, separately competing in other series, such as HSR's Classic-24, a 24-hour race at Daytona.

I mention my racing career as a set-up into my thinking that my conditioning was good enough to avoid being run over while walking my dog, 2 doors down from our house in August 2021. It was not. Security video footage clearly shows me picking up my precious Phoebe, indicating to me that I might have seen the errant driving of the approaching car, which would run over me, but spared my miniature poodle.

I mention the wreck, as the trauma of breaking my left tibia in 5 places, right hand, fracturing my spine, and inflicting a severe head wound and resulting concussion, would necessitate an 18-month rehab, only worsened by the discovery of a slightly elevated PSA, just as I was finished with my rehab.

How much we learn only after going through things, is my thought now, as my urologist ordered a PET scan for my prostate, which a year later would be looked at by a pulmonologist friend, only after I chose robotic RP at Mayo to remove the Gleason 7 prostate, where my pulmonologist saw a note by the radiologist from my prostate PET scan, indicating an upper left lobe nodule, which I should have been notified of by my local urologist. CT scans showed no cancer in my spine, at that time, but 7 months later, MD Anderson would diagnose S4 lung adenoma with the same EGFR mutation my older sister passed away with, which had metastasized to several places in my spine, still classified as an oligometastatic cancer, only slight improving my Overall Survival (OS), from 3% to 25% in 5 years.

Not expecting S4 lung cancer, I immediately sought out scientific articles on alternate ways besides SOC to deal with it. That led me to Dr. Seyfried, and others, where I attempted to follow the strict therapeutic keto diet, checking my blood ketones and blood sugar with a Keto-Mojo meter, and doing 40 HBOT sessions, with some of those sessions being used to mitigate the radiation necrosis of receiving SBRT to 5 spinal tumors in 1 MDA visit in June '24 and another 2 spinal tumors in Sept. '24.

I refused the suggestion of chemo offered by my medcon at MDA last summer, and again, with more fervor by the same medcon in Feb. '25. I read the success rate with Ivermectin and mebendazole/febendazole by Dr.s' William Makis and more thoroughly by Dr.Paul Marik, who offered the additional insight that cancer stem cells (CSC) were the more acute need to address by blocking their metabolic pathways with additional supplements, while maintaining a therapeutic dose of IVM, MBZ, and/or FBZ.

Concurrently, I started reading Dr. Kruse's material/podcast (especially helped by reading the transcripts), and after requesting an appointment with Jack, and receiving no reply from support, I felt it incumbent to write this descriptive medical bio., in the hope that I would hear from Uncle Jack.

It is with the most profound respect of Jack's journey himself, leaving Centralized Medicine, to become a decentralized person, retaining all his life experiences and skills, that I hope our paths will cross at some time, apart from my present medical situation, as I understand the loneliness of living with thoughts that independent thinking, and the faith it takes to step into them, can result in.

I added DDW a few months ago, and am considering molecular hydrogen inhalation combining DDW with the Brown gas generated by H2 machines, as 1-2 L/day of DDW is difficult enough, so if adding bubbled H2 gas to the
DDW is an added benefit, great.

I added MB for a couple weeks recently, but have withdrawn it after reading jack's caution that each case has to be evaluated before using.

I get my rescan MRI's, PET/CT scan at MDA on March 22-24, and hope the 2 months on IVM, MBZ/FBZ will show some positive results, and hope I can connect with Jack in the near future, as I understand Dr. Seryfried's work with Glio patients, and his success with applying low carb to restrict cancer-loving glucose, and also Dr. Cahill, et al. work with fasting obese patients and medically seeing no adverse effects when given insulin injections, showing that if insulin potentiated chemo is valid, it can be improved.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-28T23:33:14+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sab3-optimal-journal.30558/post-349526>

**Deuterium Depletion as a Cancer Therapy:**

The new paper provides real-world evidence for deuterium depletion’s therapeutic potential: integrating DDW into conventional cancer therapy significantly enhances survival, with a 75-80% reduction in cancer-related mortality. This aligns with Somlyai framework in his book, as DDW breaks the cycle of mitochondrial dysfunction, inflammation, and DNA instability by reducing deuterium’s kinetic effects. The study’s finding that survival correlates with DDW duration (r = 0.476, p < 0.001) and timing (better outcomes with earlier DDW adoption) underscores the importance of deuterium depletion in cancer management, supporting the mechanistic links to pH, voltage, and metabolic health in our framework.

Stressors elevate mitochondrial deuterium, increasing water viscosity, slowing the ATP synthase nanomotor, and disrupting proton dynamics, leading to a pH drop and cellular depolarization. This triggers UCP amplification, collapsing the proton gradient, while COX-2 ramps up via inflammation from leaky barriers (gut, BBB, BRB) and high-deuterium metabolic pathways (e.g., glycolysis in cancer cells). The resulting acidity spreads to blood and CSF, distorting water networks and promoting DNA instability via deuterium-driven hydroxyl radicals.

Deuterium-depleted water (DDW), as shown in the new study, significantly enhances cancer survival (MST of 12.4 years vs. 2.4 years in the general Hungarian cancer population) by restoring mitochondrial function, stabilizing pH/voltage, reducing inflammation (e.g., COX-2), and protecting DNA.
Red light therapy (600-1000 nm) lowers mitochondrial water viscosity, enhances ATP production, and reduces inflammation, while ketogenic diets reduce deuterium intake, supporting NAD+ levels and mitochondrial respiration. Together, these strategies—DDW, red light, and ketogenic diets, can break the cycle of mitochondrial dysfunction, systemic inflammation, and cancer progression, aligning with Somlyai's deuterium depletion framework and offering a promising therapeutic approach. Replacing the sun for PBM would offer better results in my opinion.

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-04-13T17:28:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sab3-optimal-journal.30558/post-350606>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=349526)
>
> **Deuterium Depletion as a Cancer Therapy:**
>
> The new paper provides real-world evidence for deuterium depletion’s therapeutic potential: integrating DDW into conventional cancer therapy significantly enhances survival, with a 75-80% reduction in cancer-related mortality. This aligns with Somlyai framework in his book, as DDW breaks the cycle of mitochondrial dysfunction, inflammation, and DNA instability by reducing deuterium’s kinetic effects. The study’s finding that survival correlates with DDW duration (r = 0.476, p < 0.001) and timing (better outcomes with earlier DDW adoption) underscores the importance of deuterium depletion in cancer management, supporting the mechanistic links to pH, voltage, and metabolic health in our framework.
>
>
> Stressors elevate mitochondrial deuterium, increasing water viscosity, slowing the ATP synthase nanomotor, and disrupting proton dynamics, leading to a pH drop and cellular depolarization. This triggers UCP amplification, collapsing the proton gradient, while COX-2 ramps up via inflammation from leaky barriers (gut, BBB, BRB) and high-deuterium metabolic pathways (e.g., glycolysis in cancer cells). The resulting acidity spreads to blood and CSF, distorting water networks and promoting DNA instability via deuterium-driven hydroxyl radicals.
>
> Deuterium-depleted water (DDW), as shown in the new study, significantly enhances cancer survival (MST of 12.4 years vs. 2.4 years in the general Hungarian cancer population) by restoring mitochondrial function, stabilizing pH/voltage, reducing inflammation (e.g., COX-2), and protecting DNA.
> Red light therapy (600-1000 nm) lowers mitochondrial water viscosity, enhances ATP production, and reduces inflammation, while ketogenic diets reduce deuterium intake, supporting NAD+ levels and mitochondrial respiration. Together, these strategies—DDW, red light, and ketogenic diets, can break the cycle of mitochondrial dysfunction, systemic inflammation, and cancer progression, aligning with Somlyai's deuterium depletion framework and offering a promising therapeutic approach. Replacing the sun for PBM would offer better results in my opinion.
>
> **CITES**
>
> [https://www.preprints.org/manuscript/202503.0500/v1](https://www.preprints.org/manuscript/202503.0500/v1)

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-05T04:45:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sab3-optimal-journal.30558/post-353978>

SBRT makes your tissues a desert. It destroys heme proteins and the VDR and you lose the GOE braking mechanism and makes it more likely to develop heteroplasmy

Most of Colorado is a desert too. People think because it gets good light that is enough. IT IS NOT. It is a state loaded with military bases and SPACE FORCE IT which is the worse of the worse nnEMF.

The Colorado Desert is a [subregion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Subregion) of the larger [Sonoran Desert](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sonoran_Desert),[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Colorado_Desert#cite_note-1) covering about 7 million acres (2.8 million ha; 28,000 km2).[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Colorado_Desert#cite_note-2) The desert occupies [Imperial County](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Imperial_County,_California), parts of [San Diego](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Diego_County,_California)and [Riverside](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Riverside_County,_California) counties, and a small part of [San Bernardino County](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Bernardino_County,_California) in [California](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/California), United States,[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Colorado_Desert#cite_note-3) as well as the northern part of [Mexicali Municipality](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mexicali_Municipality) in [Baja California](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Baja_California), Mexico.

Most of the Colorado Desert lies at a relatively low elevation, below 1,000 ft, with the lowest point of the desert floor at 275 ft [below sea level](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Height_above_mean_sea_level), at the [Salton Sea](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Salton_Sea). Although the highest peaks of the [Peninsular Ranges](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peninsular_Ranges) reach elevations of nearly 10,000 ft, most of the region's mountains do not exceed 3,000 ft

In this region, the [geology](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Geology) is dominated by the transition of the [tectonic plate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tectonic_plate) boundary from [rift](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rift) to [fault](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fault_\(geology\)). Normally a good thing but not good here because the water here is horrendous. The southernmost strands of the [San Andreas Fault](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/San_Andreas_Fault) connect to the northernmost extensions of the [East Pacific Rise](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Pacific_Rise). Consequently, the region is subject to [earthquakes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Earthquake), and the crust is being stretched swollowing the mountains right now, which will result in a sinking of the terrain over time. Many peiople do not realize the Rockies are being unzipped from south to north right now actively. The tectonics is very active as the magnetic pole is super mobile. When it gets close to the supervolcano watch out.

What does a desert or SBRT MEAN to a living being? -----> [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-flux-of-deserts.30717/page-2](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-flux-of-deserts.30717/page-2)

Once I discovered your work, I have been on "Lite Water" DDW (10ppm), but after reading recent posts, I will order DDW from Preventa, starting with 105 ppm. I have been around many intelligent, and even well-meaning personal friend physicians, but no one has parsed the quantum physics/biology as you have, and I am learning and applying your principles- as if my life depended on it, and it is, with S4 NSCLC.
