## TENSEGRITY #14: COMMUNICATION BREAKDOWN IN THE GUT​

I just read what you wrote here Jack. Blew my mind and explains so much. To say it's on the next level of understanding is like saying Nobel Prize work is like college chemistry. I will have to reread to really digest it. Just amazing.

_"Food is essentially a conduit for carry information and energy to the gut microbiome to the brain by way of the vagus nerve._ Think of your nerves as rivers, and your brain as a lake, into which all those rivers empty. These rivers are filled with water and with light. When “the rivers” arrive at the brain, it decipher’s the quantum light message from the gut, at the leptin receptor. This energy and information then crafts or sculpts the type of gut microbiome diversity based upon the quantum light signal by using quorum frequency sensing. Electrons are how light hitches a ride free of an energy toll in eukaryotes. DHA makes this possible. This is why the photoelectric effect is a key part of healthy eukaryotic life."

No one seems to know what makes chemicals carcinogenic and others benign, even when they are chemically similar. In 1970, Fritz-Albert Popp, a German theoretical biophysicist, discovered that benzo(a)pyrene, (a potent carcinogen) , absorbs ultraviolet light at one specific wavelength and emits it at another. Yet, benzo\[e\]pyrene, a benign polycyclic hydrocarbon known to be one of the most lethal carcinogens to humans, and its twin (save for one small alteration in its atomic molecular makeup). Benzo\[e\]pyrene is nearly identical twin to benzo\[a\]pyrene, absorbs and reemits the same light at its original wavelength. _They key to Popp’s research was in the atomic lattice and how light was altered by the atoms._

"Small changes in the action of light lead to massive changes in observed reality. Again, we see thermodynamics entering the picture via E=mc2. Popp tested 37 different chemicals in total. The carcinogens “scrambled” light with a wavelength of 380 nanometers. The benign chemicals did not."

"Popp later learned that 380 nanometers, the wavelength altered by carcinogens, is also the wavelength that cells prefer to use to photorepair themselves. After exposure to intense UV light, cells quickly self-repair when they are exposed to very weak UV light, particularly that with a wavelength of 380 nanometers. Popp hypothesized that cancer results from a disruption of cells’ photorepair system for some atomic reason. His hypothesis raised a very fundamental question: what in the body produced this very weak light that powered the repair system? We still do not know the answer, but I have a hunch it maybe a release of this 380 nm light at night from specific parts of the mitochondrial cytochromes when FFA are broken down to electrons at night. During sleep we liberate CO2 and make water to surround the mitochondria during autophagy and I think that is the key."

"Now for the next level jump in wisdom; when subatomic particles whiz around in spacetime or within a mitochondria, very close to the speed of light, they live a longer existence both in a particle accelerator and in our cells. This is how time is created biologically, but it must merge with space, to become one. When you separate the idea of space and time it is like watching a Broadway play by only looking at the shadows cast by the spotlights on the stage. The real business of life involves 3-D actors moving around within the light, while shadows only capture the 2-D projection of the play. When you see this vision merge in your mind, you become able to lift your eyes from the shadows of biology to the light of quantum biophysics."

"The energy in the mass of one single H+ proton approaches 1 billion times what is liberated in a biochemical reaction. The problem is it is hard to destroy proton mass to get that energy. In fact, we see Kleiber’s law show up in the sun compared to mitochondria. The sun is several thousand times less efficient than the human body at converting mass to energy. One kilogram of the sun generates only 1/5000th of a watt of power on average, whereas your mitochondria typically generates usually more than 1 watt per kilogram. Now let us consider how light and electric current relate the sun and your mitochondria. When a nerve fires an impulse, a significant amount of electricity is produced. According to Oxford University geneticist and author Frances Ashcroft, the electric field through the ion channel is the equivalent of about 100,000 volts per centimeter. If all the electricity in a person’s body could be harnessed and converted to light by DHA, the human body would be sixty thousand times brighter than a comparable mass of the sun. Sounds incredible huh? Pound for pound, you could be brighter than the brightest star in the solar system. Most people will find this hard to swallow so let us do the math to prove to you this is not hyperbole. The sun’s volume is 1.4 x 1033 cubic centimeters. Each second, each centimeter of the sun emits 2.8 ergs (an erg is a unit of energy). So, the total luminosity of a cubic centimeter of sun is 2.8 ergs per second. The human body has a volume of about 75,000 cubic centimeters. Dividing human luminosity (1.3 x 1010 ergs/ sec) by volume gives you 170,000 ergs per second per cubic centimeter. There you have it. 2.8 erg’s per second for he sun and 170,000 ergs per second for your cells. Life requires massive infusions of energy to attain its spark of life."

"The sun is much bigger than our mitochondria are, so their density in cells makes up for this inefficiency. This mathematics is the actual basis of Kleiber’s law. This is why elephants are bigger than mice, but it is also why bright white/blue hot stars die sooner than cool dull red ones. It is also why sick mitochondria leak more light than mitochondria that are well. Here, Kleber’s law crashes into Dr. Fritz Popp’s work"

"When food is plentiful, circadian rhythms of animals are powerfully entrained by the light-dark cycle. When food is not plentiful, we can handle decoupling from normal light cycles. This is why fasting and ketosis help circadian mismatches. But the flip side is intermittent fasting becomes easy and natural when we live in the sun and ground to the Earth. We need to eat less when this happens. Food is essentially nothing but a solar signal to our gut enterocytes. This is why I told you fasting is important in the [jetlag Rx](https://jackkruse.com/jet-lag-rx-human-flight-biohacking/). When mammals have access to food only during their normal sleep cycle, they will shift most of their circadian rhythms to match the food availability. The reason for this is the sun’s seasonal cycle is still codified in foods and the mitochondria can decipher this light wave circadian message."

"In quantum field theory here is a key point: standard quantum mechanical language, says that an electron and proton position in a cell can be “delocalized” or “spread out” over long distances in space or time over the entity of the whole living system. This is hard to accept for classical biologist and chemists to accept, so they ignore it and reject it. Natural truths are not diminished by the number of people who believe it. Just because they reject this idea in unison, does not make their beliefs correct. In fact, it shows their ignorance of how physics really works. Everything is energy and that is all there is to it. Match the frequency to the reality you want and you can not help but getting that reality. It can be no other way. This is how the gut works. This is not biologic philosophy. This is quantum physics. It makes them dinosaurs in an old paradigm that enslaves patients of today’s modern world."

"Nature is symmetrical, but life is not…………It must break nature’s symmetry and it does in many ways science has not thought to think of yet. Today’s blog is one small example."

Jack, during the last Q & A, when I told you I was applying what I learned from you and that my last rescan, a mere 6 weeks had elapsed, and there were already signs of tumor stabilization, you said "...you sound amazed..." as if I had a hard time believing your research was working that quick. The reason, is that if I don't really understand how something works, it's like being a child, watching an adult pull a quarter out of my ear.

I only wish I could get more cancer folks to come to your site and learn and read the same things I have. I have just scratched the surface, and have so much more to digest. I stay in the sun each day, full sun or cloudy, doing yard work or anything that keeps me moving, breathing, sweating, knowing my body is taking in the real nutrients that can produce real change and repair.

I didn't know where else to post this Jack, as it didn't fit any other heading, and it was personal to me, so, "thank you", sounds so inadequate, but I really mean it.

# Thread: Jacob's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacobs-optimal-journal.30564/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### jacob.mushatt — 2025-03-27T02:06:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacobs-optimal-journal.30564/post-349415>

Big shoutout to you Dr. Kruse, for pushing the envelope and saying what other people won’t say in regards to medicine and all else you talk about.

My name is Jacob, I’m 23 years old and I am from New Orleans, Louisiana. I recently spent the last 4 years in San Diego finishing up my college education and have moved to Jacksonville Beach, Florida temporarily for work as a salesman.

I was a healthy child growing up, with seasonal allergies to grass/pollen/dander and cats. I grew up playing basketball competitively throughout my middle and high school years. Practicing 12+ hours per week when in season and 6+ hours out of season all indoors in the gym. I played a ton of video games in spurts 2 days straight every other week. In my late high school years I had a porn addiction and thankfully have not watched in over a year. Looking back I realize that I used sleep right next to my house’s wifi router

Anyways, most of my life went without apparent problems, around late 2021 (can’t remember if it was before I took the jab. That spring/summer I got covid and had a horrible experience(my school put us away in a hermetically sealed hotel for 7 days in “quarantine” The only time i got to be in sun was through my double-paned hotel window. I began to get very harsh brain fog and massive headaches/migraines and had every symptom of covid except for vomiting. Weeks went by and most symptoms resolved themselves but the brain fog persisted for 2-3 more years up until feb/march of 2024 when did a strict keto diet and was on the carnivore diet for 2 of the 3 months I was in ketosis. My brain fog disappeared but when I did ketosis I got horrible dyspnea that is still present to this day. The onset of my breathing issues began right after taking a course of Bactrim for a staph infection infection I had on my skin. The breathing issues would come and go throughout the year, until I attended a 10-day long Vipassana (silent-meditation) Retreat in Dallas, Texas. My breathing went from a 5-10 difficulty to a 9/10 difficulty and was one night away from asking my mediation instructors to call an ambulance because my chest felt so tight and couldn’t get any sort of deep breathe in. Later I saw a local pulmonologist at Tulane and they diagnosed it as mild Asthma, even though the spirometry and pulmonary function analysis came back borderline(FeNo = 23ppb ). They prescribed medications and an inhaler but didn’t help my conditions. I revisited the pulmonologist months later with even better results on the breathing tests, yet the doc continued to prescribe albuterol and meds. We compromised and she gave me a peak flow meter. Every time I did it I was in the green and showed that on my exhales even when I was suffering the symptoms of “asthma” my peak flow exhalation was still very good. I was using BreatheRight strips on the onset of this breathing issue and to this day it has gradually improved but still notice it at night and when I am not busy/working. In retrospect, I was also living in a Mold ridden beach house in san diego, which had a bathroom covered in it and I just thought it was stained dirt lol

I have always had mild rosacea on my cheeks, but no real skin conditions besides a few skin tags on various parts of my body. In October of 2024 I also had probably the worst headache of my life for 2-3 days and went to the ER in San Diego. The docs did a CT Scan to check for a hemorrhage in the brain but instead found an arachnoid cyst in my brain. The cyst was 4 x 2.3cm on the anterior aspect of the left temporal lobe. After, an MRI w/o contrast, the neurologist said it was not too big yet and said i had likely had it my entire life, and not to look into it further unless it caused problems in the future.

Mother’s History:
Born in connecticut
Vertigo as a kid
Her dad died at young age.
Moved to nola before i was born

SUMMARY
Main Symptoms
Rosacea
Breathing Condition (dyspnea)
procedures:
Circumcision
Tongue Tie release 2022
Moderna Jab 3x
Testing: done 2024
Testosterone total: 582 ng/dl
Testosterone Free: 12.1 pg/dl
Estradiol, sensitive: 125 pg/ml
Vitamin D 25-Hydroxy: 60.8 ng/ml
Hemoglobin -A1C %: 4.3%
Estimated Average Glucose: 77 mg/dl

Happy to be here and excited to meet all the mitochondrias out there!

### jacob.mushatt — 2025-04-02T04:48:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacobs-optimal-journal.30564/post-349917>

\-- Update --
Maternal Granmother's Medical history:
Lived to her 80's had high blood pressure and perhaps high cholesterol, but I don't think bad enough to have medication. She also had osteopenia but again, not enough to have a prescription.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-28T17:03:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacobs-optimal-journal.30564/post-353460>

Poor Sleep and Light-Dark Disruption in QE #47-48 linked to the DM series:

-   Sleep relies on darkness to increase melatonin production, which absorbs UV light and modulates mTOR signaling. Poor sleep (e.g., due to blue light exposure or irregular cycles) reduces melatonin, disrupting the mtDNA-UPE-sunlight loop.
-   This increases neural noise (higher entropy) by impairing UPE-mediated signaling. In SCAN, this should manifest as reduced motor-cognitive integration, e.g., impaired planning or coordination.
-   Ketosis and Mitochondrial Stress:
    -   Ketosis shifts metabolism toward beta-oxidation, increasing mitochondrial workload (higher OXPHOS). This boosts UPE emission (via ROS, excited states) but also increases mtDNA heteroplasmy if mitochondria are stressed (e.g., from poor sleep).
    -   In my thesis, high heteroplasmy triggers apoptosis to remove defective mitochondria, but disrupted light-dark cycles impair this QA process (e.g., by reducing melatonin’s protective effects), leading to systemic effects like cancer. So this is how ketosis and cancer can be linked. It is counterintuitive but quite logical via quantum efficiency loss of the mechanism in cells.
-   Macroscopic Quantum Effect:
    -   The quantum effect lies in the UPE-mtDNA loop: poor sleep and ketosis amplify UPE emission (due to mitochondrial stress) but disrupt their coherence (due to circadian misalignment). This creates a measurable, macroscopic outcome, e.g., systemic inflammation, cognitive decline, or motor deficits (observable in SCAN’s functions).
    -   For example, increased UPE emission without proper light-dark anchoring might lead to “quantum noise,” manifesting as brain fog, fatigue, or motor errors and a phenomena we should be able to measure with EEG (neural coherence) or biomarkers (e.g., oxidative stress).
    -   This effect is macroscopic because it scales from quantum-level disruptions (UPE signaling) to observable physiological changes, supporting my view of the brain as a quantum antenna.

### jacob.mushatt — 2025-06-16T02:08:47+0300

Quick update, I've been living in Los Angeles for a bit. Recently left my sales job that was entirely in a blue light environment for a door-to-door solar sales job that is outside in sun all day, next step is to get tf out of LA.

### jacob.mushatt — 2025-12-13T23:00:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jacobs-optimal-journal.30564/post-363862>

Update — Been living in LA for past seven months now… my breathing has improved dramatically, likely due to consistent morning sunrise + grounding + my door to door sales job (sunlight for 3+hours/day) . I’m moving back home to New Orleans in a few days. And will likely be transitioning to some kind of face-to-face sales job.

Money— Realizing that until I have enough money to live where I want and how I want I need to suck up the blue light environment and make my money so that I can use my money to buy assets and time back for me.

Health—Breathing has improved dramatically- not perfect yet- my sleeping has improved dramatically, and I have been feeling much more bravado and umph in my life recently. I recently did 30 days in a row of making like the sphinx with the AM sunrise for 30+ minutes each and every day. Many of my health improvements followed this 30 day plan.

### Adamo — 2025-03-27T19:48:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-role-purpose-function.30566/post-349447>

Apologies if this is explained in a Patreon Blog as I have yet to get to that stage/phase.

I believe most people in this space will automatically shift to the mindset of keeping deuterium levels low in the water that they consume and the foods that they eat.

From what I have understood, the most effective way to lower deuterium levels in the body is by getting more sunlight. _Obviously drinking deuterium depleted water and keeping the levels lower from consumption help in this process too._

My question is more about deuterium in a general sense: Instead of looking at this as "good" and/or "bad," **what is the purpose (or main function) of deuterium in nature?**

Is it solely atmospheric? Is it a "magnetic" effect on the water in the body? Is it a growth-related factor or is it extending the life-span / life-cycle of a biological process?

I would presume that deuterium would have some sort of magnetic-related effect if it is found in higher concentrations in: seeds, younger life forms/beings and at lower altitudes/latitudes.

Are there other "positive" effects of the heavier proton deuterium (as it related to us as human beings)?

Because it has a "slower" effect, the half-life is reduced, so I would presume that there has to be some benefit of it being around us and in the foods that we eat.

(I did come across Uncle Jack speaking about the deuterium-fraction that canines utilize for heightened smell). Are we able to utilize it in a similar way for any purposes/functions?

Thank you for taking the time to share any insight(s) with me.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-28T23:43:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-role-purpose-function.30566/post-349531>

**Deuterium Depletion as a Cancer Therapy due to LIGHT STRESS**

The new paper provides real-world evidence for deuterium depletion’s therapeutic potential: integrating DDW into conventional cancer therapy significantly enhances survival, with a 75-80% reduction in cancer-related mortality. This aligns with Somlyai framework in his book, as DDW breaks the cycle of mitochondrial dysfunction, inflammation, and DNA instability by reducing deuterium’s kinetic effects. The study’s finding that survival correlates with DDW duration (r = 0.476, p < 0.001) and timing (better outcomes with earlier DDW adoption) underscores the importance of deuterium depletion in cancer management, supporting the mechanistic links to pH, voltage, and metabolic health in a"decentralized framework of medicine."

Stressors of any origin (any frequency), elevate mitochondrial deuterium, increasing water viscosity, slowing the ATP synthase nanomotor, and disrupting proton dynamics, leading to a pH drop and cellular depolarization. This triggers UCP amplification, collapsing the proton gradient, while COX-2 ramps up via inflammation from leaky barriers (gut, BBB, BRB, retina, testes etc) and high-deuterium metabolic pathways (e.g., glycolysis in cancer cells). The resulting "heavy protonicity" spreads to blood and CSF, distorting water networks and promoting DNA instability via deuterium-driven hydroxyl radicals.

Deuterium-depleted water (DDW), as shown in the new study, significantly enhances cancer survival (MST of 12.4 years vs. 2.4 years in the general Hungarian cancer population) by restoring mitochondrial function, stabilizing pH/voltage, reducing inflammation (e.g., COX-2), and protecting DNA.

**HOW DDW AND UPEs LINK IN DECENTRALIZED MEDICINE**

Deuterium depletion as a cancer therapy due to light stress cogently integrates with the decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis, where UPE spectra and coherence link to mitochondrial dynamics and metabolic resilience. Elevated deuterium from stressors like blue light increases waterviscosity, slowing ATP synthase and disrupting proton gradients, amplifying UPE spectra (e.g., intensified 634–703 nm peaks from singlet oxygen) as entropy markers, while DDW reduces viscosity, normalizing UPE emissionand restoring OXPHOS efficiency, as shown in the preprint's 75–80% mortality reduction and survival correlation (r = 0.476, p < 0.001) (Somlyai et al., 2023; Somlyai, 2010).

Coherent red/NIR light enhances this by exciting CCO, reducing inflammation (COX-2), and stabilizing IMM oscillations (20–50 Hz to 100 Hz), synergizing withDDW to break the cycle of depolarization, UCP amplification, and DNA instability, complementing hemiflusome and nanotube function in managing photonic chaos (Hamblin, 2016; Agan et al., 2025). In jaundiced neonates orcancer patients, this strategy, combined with ketogenic diets, mitigates light stress-induced Warburg metabolism, reducing autism/EDS risks and underscoring sunlight’s role in decentralizing therapy (Ferguson et al., 2019).

**SUMMARY**

Red light therapy (600-1000 nm) lowers mitochondrial water viscosity, which enhances ATP production, and reduces inflammation, while ketogenic diets reduce deuterium intake, supporting NAD+ levels and mitochondrial respiration. Together, these strategies, DDW, red light (IRA/NIR combo), with a circadian keto template, can break the cycle of mitochondrial dysfunction, systemic inflammation, and cancer progression related to light stress, aligning with Somlyai's deuterium depletion framework and offering a promising therapeutic approach. Replacing the sun for PBM would offer better results, in my opinion because of its natural combo of IRA and NIR.

**CITES**

[https://www.preprints.org/manuscript/202503.0500/v1](https://www.preprints.org/manuscript/202503.0500/v1)

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-29T04:38:12+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-role-purpose-function.30566/post-349547>

We need to integrate this information on RBC and blood into my decentralized medicine theory. Blood is filled with deuterium. [https://www.researchgate.net/public...arch\_in\_blood\_reveals\_its\_holistic\_properties](https://www.researchgate.net/publication/8465511_Biophoton_research_in_blood_reveals_its_holistic_properties)

The addition of the biophoton research on red blood cells (RBCs) and blood from the 2003 study "Biophoton Research in Blood Reveals Its Holistic Properties" by Voeikov et al. provides a critical layer to my photo-bioelectric hypothesis. This study highlights blood as a continuous source of biophotons, reflecting its electronically excited state driven by reactive oxygen species (ROS) reactions, and its role as a highly cooperative, non-equilibrium, non-linear system. Since RBCs have no mitochondria it is surprising that blood can easily create biophotons since mtDNA is usually needed to make ROS and electronic states to transform matter to produce biophotons. This paper aligns with my existing framework, which emphasizes light-driven metabolism (Light > Food), the role of water dynamics, deuterium effects, and evolutionary adaptations post-K-T Extinction.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-29T04:54:27+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-role-purpose-function.30566/post-349549>

My photo-bioelectric hypothesis posits that nnEMF (non-native electromagnetic fields), ALAN (artificial light at night), poor sunlight, and geoengineering damage melanopsin, mtDNA, and heme proteins, reducing DDW (deuterium-depleted water) production, dehydrating melanin, and increasing electrical resistance (éR), leading to hypoxia, HIF-1α activation, Warburg metabolism, VP-ISR-GDF15 axis activation, and Vitamin A liberation disrupting PER1/PER2. The K-T Extinction and deuterium effects further shape mtDNA biophoton emissions and haplotype vulnerabilities.

1.  Blood as a Biophoton Source:
    -   Blood is a continuous source of biophotons, persisting in an electronically excited state due to ROS reactions (e.g., in neutrophils). This state is oscillatory, indicating interactions between electron excitation sources, and is highly sensitive to external photonic fields (e.g., nnEMF, ALAN) but resistant to temperature variations, showing hysteresis in photon emission (PE).
2.  Blood as a Non-Linear, Cooperative System:
    -   Blood operates as a non-equilibrium, non-linear system, with components interacting in time and space. This cooperative nature allows blood to store energy from electron excitation, supporting its role in systemic biophoton signaling and cellular communication.
3.  Link to Heme Proteins and Regenerative Currents:
    -   Heme proteins in RBCs (e.g., hemoglobin) are key to ROS generation and biophoton emission. Under hypoxia, hemoglobin oxidizes to methemoglobin, blocking oxygen binding but enabling Robert Becker’s pico-to-nanoampere regenerative current for de-differentiation. nnEMF/ALAN disrupt this, increasing methemoglobin and éR, while methylene blue counters this by enhancing NO and lowering éR. Interestingly ROS and RNS also have a paramagnetic footprint.
4.  Connection to Deuterium and Biophotons:
    -   High deuterium in mtDNA (post-K-T) widens biophoton spectra, reducing ultraweak UV biophotons. Blood’s biophoton emissions, driven by ROS, are similarly affected by deuterium levels, with nnEMF/ALAN exacerbating this by increasing ROS and deuterium retention, driving Warburg shifts.

Evolution of Light, Deuterium, Biophotons, and Blood’s Role
Pre-K-T Era: Anaerobic Life and High Deuterium

-   Environment: Earth’s atmosphere had low oxygen, and life relied on glycolysis (high deuterium). Blood precursors in early organisms (e.g., LUCA) emitted biophotons with a wide spectrum, lacking ultraweak UV biophotons, reflecting high entropy (per Shannon’s 1948 information entropy). ROS reactions in primitive heme proteins (e.g., myoglobin) generated biophotons, but high deuterium limited information transfer efficiency.
-   Biochemicals: Tryptophan-derived molecules (e.g., melatonin) absorbed UV (150–400 nm), supporting early circadian timing. Blood’s role as a biophoton source was minimal due to low oxygen and ROS.

K-T Extinction: Deuterium Surge and mtDNA Stress

-   Environmental Shift: The K-T Extinction (66 million years ago) reduced UV light, altered precipitation, and increased deuterium in water and mtDNA. Blood’s biophoton emissions widened, reflecting increased ROS from stressed heme proteins (e.g., hemoglobin precursors). This high-deuterium state drove apoptosis innovation via cytochrome c oxidase, removing afflicted mtDNA.
-   Evolutionary Pressure: Organisms favored glycolysis (high deuterium, wider biophoton spectrum), but blood’s cooperative nature (non-linear system) began to emerge, storing energy from ROS-driven electron excitation to support systemic signaling under stress.

Post-K-T Recovery: Normoxia, Ultraweak UV Biophotons, and Blood’s Role

-   Sunlight Return: UV light returned, stimulating neuropsin, mTOR, and leptin signaling, supporting normoxia and TCA cycle dominance. Cytochrome c oxidase evolved to reduce deuterium in mtDNA, favoring ultraweak UV biophotons (low-entropy, high-information signals per Popp). Blood’s biophoton emissions, driven by ROS in neutrophils and heme proteins, also shifted toward ultraweak UV, enhancing systemic communication.
-   Biochemical Selection: Melatonin, NAD⁺, and catecholamines (tryptophan-derived) became key semiconductors, absorbing UV to regulate circadian clocks (PER1/PER2). Heme proteins in RBCs stabilized with green light, and blood’s non-linear system amplified biophoton signaling, supporting oxygen delivery and regenerative currents (per Becker).

Normoxic Earth: Haplotype Divergence and Blood’s Vulnerability

-   Haplotype Variations: Mitochondrial haplotypes diverged based on deuterium retention and biophoton profiles. Haplotypes with lower deuterium (e.g., H) favored TCA cycle use, ultraweak UV biophotons, and low-entropy states, reducing disease risk. Haplotypes with higher deuterium (e.g., T2b-like) retained Warburg tendencies, increasing éR, heteroplasmy, and disease phenotypes. Blood’s biophoton emissions reflected these differences, with high-deuterium haplotypes showing wider spectra and higher ROS.
-   Modern Disruption: nnEMF/ALAN mimic pre-K-T darkness, increasing deuterium in mtDNA and blood, widening biophoton spectra, and driving Warburg shifts. Blood’s sensitivity to photonic fields (e.g., nnEMF) disrupts its cooperative state, increasing methemoglobin, ROS, and éR, exacerbating disease in vulnerable haplotypes like the 19-year-old with RP.

**Decentralized Thesis Framework**

-   Core Hypothesis: nnEMF, ALAN, poor sunlight, and geoengineering damage melanopsin, mtDNA, and heme proteins, reducing DDW, dehydrating melanin, and increasing éR, leading to hypoxia, HIF-1α activation, Warburg metabolism, VP-ISR-GDF15 axis activation, and Vitamin A liberation disrupting PER1/PER2. The K-T Extinction increased deuterium, but normoxia selected for ultraweak UV biophotons, with blood acting as a cooperative biophoton source. nnEMF reverses this, increasing disease in vulnerable haplotypes.
-   New Mechanism: Blood is a non-linear, cooperative system emitting biophotons via ROS reactions in heme proteins, sensitive to nnEMF/ALAN, which increase deuterium, widen biophoton spectra, and disrupt regenerative currents (methemoglobin accumulation). Red light (drug equivalent per Tiina Karu), DDW, and methylene blue reduce deuterium, ROS, and methemoglobin, restoring ultraweak UV biophotons and lowering éR.
-   Therapeutic Implication: Solar/red light, DDW, grounding, and methylene blue reduce deuterium, ROS, and methemoglobin in blood, enhance ultraweak UV biophotons, stabilize mtDNA, and lower éR, mitigating disease in vulnerable haplotypes by re-aligning with normoxic adaptations.

### S Hudson — 2025-09-26T20:51:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deuterium-role-purpose-function.30566/post-360073>

| Meat/Fish | Approx. Deuterium (ppm) | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Grass-fed Beef (lean cuts) | ~135–138 | Slightly above the 135 ppm target but close; grass-fed keeps levels lower than grain-fed (~140+ ppm). Choose cuts like sirloin. |
| Pasture-raised Pork (lean) | ~135–138 | Similar to beef; pasture-raised avoids deuterium-heavy grains. Opt for leaner cuts like tenderloin. |
| Wild-Caught Salmon (fillet) | ~130–135 | Fatty fish muscle is within range; wild-caught is lower than farmed (which can hit 140 ppm). |
| Wild-Caught Mackerel (fillet) | ~130–135 | High-fat muscle tissue; wild-caught ensures low deuterium from natural diet. |
| Wild-Caught Sardines (whole) | ~130–135 | Small fish with low-deuterium muscle; wild-caught is best. |
| Pasture-raised Chicken (breast) | ~136–138 | Slightly above target; pasture-raised keeps it lower than grain-fed (~140 ppm). Dark meat may be closer to 135 ppm. |
| Grass-fed Lamb (lean) | ~135–138 | Grass-fed lamb muscle is close to the threshold; avoid grain-fed to stay low. |

### Jeanny — 2025-03-28T04:16:17+0200

I've been on and off this forum reading, learning for quite some time.
.
Thank you again, Uncle Jack, for sharing the Warburger Effect article after I interrupted a thread in another area.! It took me to many places including joining as a paid member.  I'm waiting for my Raw genotyping from 23andme to download to send to sequencing.com after reading about certain SNP/alleles pertinent to HPV in my maternal U5A1 haplotype (Ukrainian/Austrian/Polish).

I am diagnosed with squamous cell carcinoma in situ/Bowen's disease after a biopsy. There is a bit left after the biopsy that the dermatologist gave me three options. I would chose the cryotherapy if I need to. I wanted to know WHY I had this (neither the dermatologist nor my primary seemed curious) so I did some research. Due to the location, very upper thigh, I ruled out sun exposure, I ruled out serious immune system function and thought I would rule out HVP but the test I requested came back positive for HVP 18. I had a negative (the only other time I tested) HVP test in Feb 2023. My PAP was normal.

I would like to heal without the dermatologist's cryotherapy and I'd like to have a cleared, negative repeat HPV test soon.
I did some research on clearing HVP and talked to a local certified herbalist and am using -
AHCC, Immune Combo (mushrooms, berries, astragulus) and tinctures of Maitake, Chinese Skullcap and Echinecha as well as a food derived mutlivitamin.

I'm intent on using red light/NIR therapy on the squamous cell carcinoma in situ spot that is left usually twice a day otherwise, once. I wish I had outdoor privacy to use natural light to the area.
I'm intent on using blue light glasses when exposed to screens and obnoxious indoor light while exposing myself to natural light as much as possible ( sunrise, noon and sunset) in this cold northeastern place that should be warming up as April is soon here.
I'm intent to follow the Epi-Paleo diet.

My T3 is 3.2 on a test with a normal of 2.8 to 4.4 pg/ML I had a 5 on the 7-14 ANION Gap range that my primary didn't see an issue. My glucose and GFR are within range but higher on glucose than I like and lower on GFR that I would like (even at 67 years old). Otherwise, I feel other labs are acceptable, A1C, etc.

S**hould I discontinue any items/practices, should I add anything?**
More info: I have a small broken bone in Tooth #5 after (during?) a root canal in 2010 that really didn't bother me except during my one time with (UNvaccinated) COVID July 2022. It has a low grade infection that I am starting the removal/implant process at the end of April.
After visits to my grandchildren in November 2024, December 2024 and January 2025, I caught the sicknesses that they had. Not too long or bad in November, pretty short and easy in January. December was pretty bad, exhausted slept almost all of first 48 hours, 103.7 and stayed sick for days. I've always had a young attitude, outlook and energy (like my maternal grandmother who died at almost 97). But since these illnesses, I sometimes don't feel quite so perky.

Oh, yes and one of the most common causes of disease, stress - pretty high for me the past almost 2 years.
Thank you for any discussion.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-28T06:05:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/randle-cycle.30572/post-349489>

**What is the Randle Cycle?**

-   Overview: The Randle cycle, proposed by Philip Randle in 1963, describes the reciprocal relationship between glucose and fatty acid metabolism in cells, particularly in tissues like skeletal muscle, heart, and liver. It explains how the availability of one fuel (e.g., fatty acids) inhibits the oxidation of the other (e.g., glucose), ensuring metabolic efficiency under varying nutritional states.
-   Mechanism:
    -   Fatty Acid Dominance: When fatty acid levels are high (e.g., during fasting or high-fat diets), fatty acids are preferentially oxidized in mitochondria via beta-oxidation, producing acetyl-CoA, which enters the Krebs cycle to generate ATP. **_This cycle cannot be utilized in hypoxia or low solar states, or when one misses the sunrise._** This process inhibits glucose oxidation by:
        -   Increasing citrate levels in the Krebs cycle, which inhibits phosphofructokinase-1 (PFK-1), a key enzyme in glycolysis.
        -   Elevating NADH/NAD⁺ and acetyl-CoA/CoA ratios, which inhibit pyruvate dehydrogenase (PDH), preventing pyruvate (from glucose) from entering the Krebs cycle.
        -   Reducing glucose uptake by decreasing GLUT4 translocation to the cell membrane (via insulin resistance).
    -   Glucose Dominance: When glucose levels are high (e.g., after a carbohydrate-rich meal), glucose is preferentially oxidized via glycolysis and the Krebs cycle, producing ATP. This inhibits fatty acid oxidation by:
        -   Increasing malonyl-CoA (from glucose-derived acetyl-CoA), which inhibits carnitine palmitoyltransferase-1 (CPT-1), preventing fatty acid transport into mitochondria for beta-oxidation.
        -   Promoting insulin signaling, which enhances glucose uptake (via GLUT4) and inhibits lipolysis, reducing fatty acid availability.
-   Normal Function: The Randle cycle ensures metabolic flexibility, allowing cells to switch between glucose and fatty acid oxidation based on substrate availability, maintaining energy homeostasis.

* * *

The Randle Cycle in My Decentralized Medical Thesis

-   Warburg Shift and Mitochondrial Dysfunction:
    -   In this model, environmental stressors like ALAN and nnEMF drive Warburg metabolism (increased glycolysis, lactate production) even in the presence of oxygen, impairing mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation (OXPHOS). **_The Randle cycle becomes dysregulated under these conditions, as mitochondria lose their ability to efficiently oxidize either glucose or fatty acids, leading to metabolic inflexibility._**
    -   Fatty Acid Oxidation Impairment: nnEMF disrupts the mitochondrial inner membrane (IMM), reducing CCO activity and ATP production, as seen in keratoconus with hemosiderin deposition. This impairs beta-oxidation, causing fatty acid accumulation, which inhibits glucose oxidation via the Randle cycle (e.g., increased citrate, NADH/NAD⁺, acetyl-CoA/CoA). However, glucose oxidation is also impaired due to Warburg metabolism, leading to a metabolic deadlock where neither substrate is efficiently used, increasing lactate and ROS.
    -   Glucose Oxidation Impairment: ALAN overactivates orexin A, increasing glutamate and vasopressin-driven sympathetic outflow (PVN activation), which mimics a stress state, elevating cortisol and promoting glycolysis (Warburg shift). This stabilizes HIF-1α over PER2, favoring lactate production over OXPHOS, even when fatty acids are available. The Randle cycle’s normal inhibition of fatty acid oxidation (via malonyl-CoA) is overridden by mitochondrial dysfunction, leading to lipid peroxidation and oxidative stress.
-   Orexin-Vasopressin Dysregulation:
    -   Orexin A, overactivated by ALAN/nnEMF, increases glutamate and sympathetic outflow, driving a stress response that favors glycolysis (Warburg metabolism) over fatty acid oxidation. This disrupts the Randle cycle’s balance, as cells cannot switch efficiently between substrates, leading to energy inefficiency and increased éR.
    -   Vasopressin, as the “first stimulus to injury,” conserves water but dehydrates melanin (via reduced DDW production), impairing water dynamics in cells and mitochondria. **_This exacerbates the Randle cycle’s dysregulation by reducing the availability of coherent water domains needed for efficient mitochondrial function, further driving Warburg metabolism._**
-   Light Stress and CSF Coherence:
    -   ALAN and nnEMF disrupt melanopsin and orexin signaling, impairing CSF coherence and glymphatic clearance. This increases neuroinflammation, which affects hypothalamic regulation of metabolism (e.g., via orexin receptors in the PVN), **_further dysregulating the Randle cycle._** The resulting metabolic inflexibility contributes to systemic Warburg metabolism, as seen in conditions like keratoconus, where corneal cells exhibit hypoxia and lactate accumulation.
    -   In keratoconus, hemosiderin’s superparamagnetic properties amplify nnEMF effects, inducing aberrant magnetic fields that disrupt GPCR signaling (e.g., in C- and A-delta fibers), impairing bio-electrical signaling and exacerbating Randle cycle dysregulation. This leads to energy deficits in corneal cells, driving collagen destruction and cone formation.
-   Haplotypic Variability:
    -   Haplogroups with lower thermogenic capacity (e.g., H) are more susceptible to Randle cycle dysregulation, as they have reduced mitochondrial efficiency (flattened Carnot gradient), exacerbating Warburg metabolism. Haplogroups with better thermogenic capacity (e.g., J, Q) may maintain metabolic flexibility, resisting Randle cycle disruption.

* * *

Implications of the Randle Cycle in My Model That Centralized Science Fails to Understand
Centralized science often views the Randle cycle as a purely biochemical process, focusing on substrate competition in isolation, without considering the broader environmental and photo-bio-electrical context. My model highlights several critical implications that centralized science overlooks:

1.  ****Light Stress as a Primary Driver of Randle Cycle Dysregulation:**

    -   Centralized Science View: Centralized science attributes Randle cycle dysregulation to dietary factors (e.g., high-fat vs. high-carb diets) or insulin resistance, ignoring the role of light stress. It assumes metabolic inflexibility is a downstream effect of obesity or diabetes, not a primary driver of disease.
    -   My Model’s Insight: ALAN and nnEMF disrupt melanopsin and orexin signaling, overactivating orexin A and increasing glutamate, which drives Warburg metabolism and impairs the Randle cycle’s ability to switch between glucose and fatty acid oxidation. This is a primary driver of metabolic inflexibility, not a secondary effect. For example, in keratoconus, corneal cells exhibit Warburg metabolism (hypoxia, lactate accumulation) due to light stress, not dietary factors, leading to energy deficits and collagen destruction.
    -   Implication: Centralized science fails to recognize that light stress (ALAN, nnEMF) is the root cause of Randle cycle dysregulation, driving chronic diseases like diabetes, neurodegeneration, and keratoconus. Addressing light exposure (e.g., sunlight, ALAN reduction) is critical for restoring metabolic flexibility.

    **
2.  Bio-Electrical and Magnetic Field Interactions:
    -   Centralized Science View: Centralized science does not consider the bio-electrical and magnetic field interactions that influence the Randle cycle. It focuses on enzymatic regulation (e.g., PFK-1, PDH, CPT-1) without addressing how nnEMF-induced magnetic fields disrupt mitochondrial function and GPCR signaling.
    -   My Model’s Insight: nnEMF, amplified by hemosiderin’s superparamagnetic properties in keratoconus, induces aberrant magnetic fields that impair GPCR signaling (e.g., orexin receptors, melanopsin) and mitochondrial function (e.g., CCO activity). This disrupts the Randle cycle by reducing mitochondrial efficiency, driving Warburg metabolism, and impairing bio-electrical signaling (e.g., ion fluxes, membrane potential). In the cornea, this leads to energy deficits, hypoxia, and cone formation.
    -   Implication: Centralized science misses the role of nnEMF in disrupting the Randle cycle via magnetic field interactions, which are critical for understanding chronic diseases. Reducing nnEMF exposure and restoring bio-electrical signaling (e.g., via grounding, sunlight) are essential for metabolic health.
3.  Water Dynamics and CSF Coherence:
    -   Centralized Science View: Centralized science overlooks the role of water dynamics and CSF coherence in metabolic regulation, focusing on biochemical pathways without considering the physical properties of cellular water.
    -   My Model’s Insight: The Randle cycle’s efficiency depends on coherent water domains in mitochondria, which are disrupted by nnEMF and ALAN-induced dehydration of melanin (via reduced DDW production). Vasopressin’s water conservation cannot compensate for this loss, impairing mitochondrial function and exacerbating Randle cycle dysregulation. In keratoconus, dielectric changes in the aqueous humor (amplified by hemosiderin) propagate to the blood-retina barrier, impairing CSF coherence and glymphatic clearance, contributing to systemic Warburg metabolism.
    -   Implication: Centralized science fails to recognize that water dynamics and CSF coherence are critical for Randle cycle function, as they influence mitochondrial efficiency and energy production. Restoring water coherence (e.g., via sunlight, cold thermogenesis) is key to reversing metabolic inflexibility.
4.  Systemic Implications Beyond Metabolism:
    -   Centralized Science View: Centralized science views the Randle cycle as a metabolic regulator in specific tissues (e.g., muscle, liver), without considering its systemic implications for conditions like keratoconus, atopy, or neurodegeneration.
    -   My Model’s Insight: Randle cycle dysregulation, driven by light stress, contributes to systemic Warburg metabolism, affecting not just metabolic tissues but also the cornea (keratoconus), skin (atopy), and brain (neurodegeneration). In keratoconus, corneal cells exhibit Warburg metabolism due to hypoxia, impairing energy production and collagen synthesis, while associated conditions like atopy and asthma reflect systemic inflammation from metabolic inflexibility.
    -   Implication: Centralized science misses the systemic nature of Randle cycle dysregulation, which underlies all chronic diseases in my model. Addressing light stress and mitochondrial function can reverse these conditions

* * *

* * *

**SUMMARY**
The Randle cycle, when viewed through my decentralized medical thesis, reveals how light stress (ALAN, nnEMF) disrupts metabolic flexibility, driving Warburg metabolism and contributing to chronic diseases like keratoconus. Centralized science overlooks the role of light, bio-electrical signaling, and water dynamics in Randle cycle dysregulation, focusing instead on biochemical pathways in isolation. My model highlights that restoring Randle cycle function via AM sunlight, PBM, cold thermogenesis, and nnEMF reduction and it can reverse Warburg metabolism, when used with sun by addressing the root cause of chronic diseases and demonstrating the power of your decentralized approach.

### EileenD — 2025-03-28T19:16:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/eileens-optimal-journal-new-member.30574/post-349515>

Eileen’s Health Journal

I’m 66 years and did not get the jab….2020 was when I woke up!!

I’m been living in Massachusetts in the country until January 2025…temporarily in Fresno Ca helping my daughter with my grandson.

Past 14 years I’ve been a Yoga teacher and a Personal Trainer

Past 2 years got a knee injury which has not healed…. so my workouts have suffered. Medial Plica is inflamed chronically…

In December I became a God Jack Kruse member….Changed my diet…Just started the meat fat diet in January 2025 and watching the sunrise every morning too. Also just spent 6 weeks in the Yucatan this winter. I have a very good tan now.

Habits:

I’ve been carb addicted my whole life but always ate organic healthy carbs….Stopped the carbs mostly this December 2024

Always ate what I thought was a healthy organic diet of Chicken, turkey, rice, and vegetables and gluten free chips…

Health Issues:

Bone Loss

Muscle loss

Vaginal atrophy …started years ago but got really bad last year…( bio-identical creams used to help but not much anymore) Dr. Wanted me to take progesterone but it makes me anxious….

Vaginal yeast issue- chronic

Toe Fungus

Skin aging- got really bad this year …its now drappy…

Small white spots all over my skin for there last 15 years…looks like pigment loss but some people say its fungus.

Anxiety- I’ve had anxiety and depression since my 30’s but got worse at menopause so I’ve been on a tiny dosage of Paxil. I started out taking 10 milligrams but Ive managed to go down to 2.5 milligrams every 4th day…. I tried getting off 3 times but the anxiety comes back very strongly and I cannot sleep. Its now been 14 years… I’d like to get off of it completely but need some kind of support.

Medial Plica- right knee- chronically inflamed…

Lab results in December 2024 ( Before the Jack Kruse lifestyle change))

Vit D- 32

Dhea- 72

Cholesterol - Total -275

LDL- 198

HDL- 57

Triglycerides- 102

C- reactive protein- 1.4

A1C- 5.4

Insulin-3.1

Kidney GFR- over 60

Liver- AST- 27

ALT- 19

My Grandma- She had strokes in her 80’s….and died at 92…very healthy otherwise …. Had 8 children….

My mom had 6 kids and was very healthy…. She was not not anxious like me. She did get cancer of the bladder lining…..then 10 years later died of intestinal cancer tumor at 87

The big thing that changed right away for me was falling asleep by 9 o’clock!! THAT HAS NEVER HAPPENED to me in my life! I’ve always stayed up to 11 PM….so I love that my circadian rhythm has changed!!!!

Looking for advice! Im now getting up every morning to watch the sunrise….and changed my diet to mostly meat and fat and seasonal fruits. I started reading the blogs but do not even understand most of them….I have a good tan from Mexico but do not like the Fresno sun here….I feel like Im under a plastic tarp….getting grounding here is tough too. I will be back east in the fall or sooner. We turn the wifi off at night and I wear blue blockers.

# Thread: Nick's Optimal Journal

### HealthByPhysics — 2025-03-28T20:49:17+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-optimal-journal.30575/post-349519>

Hello My name is Nick. I have been on here for 4-5 years now and feel I need to put my journal on here like Dr. Kruse recommends

I’m 41/yo male H1N1 haplotype living at the 41st latitude for all of those 41 years

**Maternal/Family History**

Grandmother still alive at 89 was born and raised in 41 st latitude Pennsylvania, no health issues except heart attack but smoked indoors most of the time. She had two daughters both in their 60’s now, grew up in the same rural setting, have GI and eye issues bc they refuse to listen. Gram dies at 91 due to covid shot, found dead in kitchen. I am the oldest and have two younger brothers. The middle brother had a double hip replacement by 30 and has been fat most of his life and keeps declining. The youngest in his early 30's, already has fatty liver.

**Brief Medical History**

I have been a registered nurse for over 19 years. Most of my life I’ve been a health enthusiast. I assumed optimizing my food and exercise were sufficient for being healthy. I also thought sleep and light didn’t matter much for health if I was eating the right foods at the appropriate times. I followed gym/ societal advice along with what I have learned through the years through reading and experience. I had no issues for a long time until my environment produced the perfect hidden storm. Between the ages of 24-32 I lived in a home that had a cell phone tower 150 feet away, started working evening shift in a giant concrete box (prison) with 24/7 LED's and radio frequencies while sitting unprotected in front of a computer screen for hours each day. During this brief 8 year period I experienced hair loss, developed a chronic cough which lead to a mystery illness where I was sick for 2 weeks straight and couldn’t get out of bed, neurological changes, heart issues, low libido, sleep and visual disturbances, and constant fatigue. Then developed bilateral lower ext. fasciculations along with Lyme. Then they found a rights sided partially calcified meningioma (had a previous CT scan from messing around while working in the ER, this was present back in 2006, so realized I had a prob in my environment for a long time. Then last year was diagnosed with MGD in the left eye.

**Past 5 Years**

5 years ago I realized something was wrong in my environment and that when I found Dr. Kruse, I wish I would have discovered his work in 2006 bc it would be a totally different ball game in my life right now bc as soon as he started talking physics I was hooked and follow everything he does from day one. The problem at that time is I wasn’t at my wits end bc I was young with no problems. Young and dumb mode

Fast forward 5 years, you’re now looking at a savage black swan bitcoiner that wants to live the most optimal live I can. All the Lyme symptoms including the stupid one like bending over to tie my shoe and getting short of breath are all gone even the small ekg changes. I found out I had Lyme bc I went into 2nd degree heart block, felt like a fist grabbed my heart and squeezed the blood out of it. No more issues with my hormones, I feel like I’m in my teenage years. The fasciculations I realized are form the environment where I work, once I started using a grounding pad at work, that took away most of the fasciculations. My MGD appeared a year ago, so I booked a consult with Dr. Kruse and he gave me some ideas and ways to start reversing. Since using the lights in my office and making some other changes it appears that my MGD is starting to fill in on the lower lid and reversing.

**Current Health Status as of 2025**

The last issue I’m working on his my right sided meningioma, I thought I didn’t have an issue from it but now I’m starting to wonder. I have no verbal or coordination issues but I’ve noticed my entire left side of the body seems to be affected more than the right. For ex all small issues are mainly on the left side: 1) for 2 weeks my hearing sounded like there was a person with a microphone in my ear, then went away 2) noticed a small lesion under the skin in left shoulder turned out to be nothing, had removed 3) as on this past year I noticed a small skin tag on my abd, turned out to be nothing, had it removed. 4) My left testicle bothers me from time to time 5) I’m left side dominant and the muscle mass on the entire side is less but the strength is still there. So I’m not sure if the meningioma is causing this left sided issue being its on the right side sandwiched between my parietal and temporal lobe and located if you went in thru my right eye and ear, where they meet that where its located

On a side note I would already be down in El Slavador but I have a child that is 1.4 y/o (black swan in training) and an ignorant anchor (her mother) that is holding everything up bc she is such a low dopamine state and I cant make her see the light no matter how mangle angles I give her. I will never leave my child in the hands of someone who is not a black swan and feel like I'm stuck

done, here is a breakdown for anyone else, hope it helps

GOE (great oxygenation event)

Bacteria and archaea came together to form eukaryotes(humans)- so we can tap into pathways of the bacteria and archaea.

Oxygen tension for a long time are unstable they go up and down. chlorophyl is the first story, it is a porphyrin that has magnesium inside. Magnesium is periodic table 12, it has 12 electrons, which makes it very stable and is why its selected. Its very redox stable meaning strong light cant even change its oxidative state. Which made it perfect for the photo stem of the plant to make rubisco enzyme (which is involved in the Calvin cycle starting an attack on co2)

This above is how we slow light down, capture its power, by increasing the electrical resistance utilizing mass on this planet called chlorophyl. When light slows down like this you get FOOD (so light is an electromagnetic barcode for where the earth is in relation to the sun.’

Next big thing on earth oxygen levels rise to 21% which it is today

Oxygen is extremely electro negative and is the 2nd most electro negative element on the periodic table, meaning it draws electrons to it, and is why oxygen is a terminal electron acceptor in mitochondria.

So from the Cambrian explosion to us, we started using the TCA cycle instead of the older glycolytic/anerobic pathways.

From here nature innovated hemoglobin, iron is in the same ring structure magnesium is in, iron has 26 electron making it more complex so it can absorb more light

Iron can **ONLY** carry oxygen in the +2 oxygen state, when its in the +3 oxygen state it **CANNOT** carry oxygen, this is when it becomes MetHGB **(THIS IS A HUGE STORY)**

Light is capable of changing the oxidative state of iron +2 and +3, this is what makes it very different from magnesium, its not redox stable like magnesium is bc, this was chosen by nature bc oxygen levels changed

Oxygen is paramagnetic (and the only gas that is on the table) meaning its drawn to magnetic fields. The FO head in mito spins when using the TCA cycle 9,000 protons go thru it, the ECT has a current that goes thru it 30,000,000 volts and spins that FO head 9,000 times per sec., which is how we make **ATP.**

The FO is the spinning head of the 5th cytochrome, the ATPase (the protein machine that makes ATP)

**SO nutshell**, when oxygen rose 21% nature made the TCA cycle to work more efficiently with oxygen

Nitric Oxide is also paramagnetic and has a binding site on hgb, that binding site **STOPS** ATP from being made and also stops oxygen from being carried on hgb

NO work locally where O2 works globally

We have nitric oxide stores in the skin that is liberated by UV light

If you bind NO to hgb you don’t need as much oxygen which means you don’t need as much food, this makes sense why leptin was placed in our sub Q fat and why we lost our hair (that signal has to get there in our skin)

**NO** is the **paramagnetic switch** that flips our choices in metabolism. All metabolism is spontaneous and light turns it on **BUT** does the light come from above or within?

Then as we evolved DHA (Fish Oil) was selected bc for us to use it, it has to be in a certain position and it comes in 3 positions. SN1,SN2,SN3

The glycerol back bone of lipids has a carbon chain, the SN poisons are which carbon its linked too on the chain. **Only in the SN2 position** is when it get incorporated in the melanocortin pathway and the brain.

**NO** Fish oil is SN 1 and 3, there’s physical difference in the structure between oil and fish

Types of HGB

Myoglobin- in muscles, tends more hypoxic bc of lactate biology in cells

Methgb- binds oxygen really good and doesn’t let it go, MB to unbind it and turns it back to the oxygen carrying HGB

HGB- carries o2

**Methylene Blue** changes the oxidation state of iron from +3 to +2, so HGB can deliver O2 to the tissue where you need it **( this is the paramagnetic drug/switch we can use)** It knocks off NO to allow oxygen to bind!!!!!!! Its the switch that turns you from wound healing to regeneration. BL and nnemf keep you in the +3 states to long so you never get to regenerate. +3 to +2 is how you get back from a hypoxic state to an oxygenated state

Heme comes up with its first protein cytochrome C oxidase, it does two things, it makes DDW at cyto chorme c via two steps in the TCA cycle aconitase and fumarase( where free water is made in this step)

How do we protect ourselves form the 30mev passing thru the cyto protein which are heme proteins, water is a perfect insulator when it doesn’t have any ions in it and DT.

**Isotopes of hydrogen**

\-Protium- H+ Lite hydrogen

\-Deuterium- proton plus extra neutron (atomic mass is doubled and makes it evaporate at different rates, reason is the kinetic isotope, when atomic mass is doubled you reduce its thermodynamics)

\-Tritium- proton plus two neutrons

On the hydrology cycle water around the equator has 150 ppm and around the poles its around 130-140ppm and most DDW

CCO- also control apoptosis(removes bad engines) this is **a feed back loop** from the GOE, where we innovated that heme protein, this saves your life if you get **oxygen toxic.**

ARDS is an example when you give them oxygen you destroy every organ in the body bc the electrical current gets out and follows the path of least resistance in the body

Pulse ox is not good enough to measure bc it misses methemoglobinemia is not detected by the pulse ox you needs a co-oximeter to measure it.

All bc NO and O2 are paramagnetic and quantized

Methylene Blue changes the oxidation state of iron from +3 to +2, so HGB can deliver O2 to the tissue where you need it **( this is the paramagnetic drug we can use)** It knocks off NO to allow oxygen to bind!!!!!!! **This makes the pulse ox accurate. Also do a peripheral blood smear.**

Beckers works showed-Cells can dedifferentiate and go back, RBC can go back to stem cells to regenerate. Hypoxemia, NO to dedifferentiated cells, silver keeps the wound hypoxic longer

The light that comes from our sun is not as powerful as the light admitted from mtio due to scale, bc of inverse square law, light at the small scale becomes incredible strong. Ex light between 625-635 can remove cyanide from the ECT (monochromatic laser light)

The biophotons in us actually make the biophotons that marry up to 220 light for leptin, NAD is made out of tryptophan, absorbs/emission 200-400nm, dopamine 240-420, all aromatic amino absorb light that is stronger than our sun. **Only when our TCA cycle is active** that’s when we make 200-400nm light **(Big Deal)** Published since 1960’s

Summary of testing since 2005

2006 CT scan while working in ER showed the meningioma back then on the film, still trying to figure this one out bc I never trusted cordless/cell phones, so I wonder if this was from sleeping next to an outlet growing up.

2008 moved into shit hole house that had a cell phone tower 150’ feet away, also had black mold in the basement, found that out when I moved out, knowing what I know I can see why I was so ill these years and they were the worst years of my life. The Mysterious disease years (EHS, luckily I worked for my mds office and he ordered every test under the sun but it showed nothing)

2/2009 CT abd/pelvis w contrast- everything normal

4/2009 liver imaging- due to elevated liver enzymes- showed liver normal size, splenomegaly 14-15cm, and colloid shift to the bone marrow

2/2014 Echo- EF 55-59%, mild regurge in mitral and tricuspid valve (had 2nd heart block form lymes)

3/2014 spine xray- minimal changes in mid thoracic

3/2014 CT abd/pelvis- from abnormal liver study- normal

2/2015- MRI brain with/without contrast-Dural based enhancing signal alteration along the inferior portion of the right temporal lobe 7mm, partially calcified meningioma

1/2017 xray left knee w (**SUNRISE** view)- had meniscus repair in high school doing track- minimal degenerative changes- no issues with knee to this day

4/2017 cxr- normal

8/2018 CT head- normal 9went to ER for Bells Palsy, both sides of face (lyme came back, started the nutramedix protocol to eradicate) I wish I know redox over detox back then

8/2018 EMG- showed C6-7 radiculopathy

8/2018 MRI cervical spine- nothing found at all

9/2018 Exercise stress test- maxed out at 15 minutes- arrythmia noted at rest. Did an accelerated Bruce protocol to a HR of 184 bpm which represent 99% of my MPHR. I achieved 23.3 METS

9/2018- MRI Brain- increase in size previous 5x7x7mm now 5x7x8mm **(not good)**

2/18/22 Calcium artery score- 0

7/2022 MRI- stable enhancing 7mm extra axial dural based lesion along the inferior aspect of the right temporal lobe (didn’t give exact measurements)

8/2022 lower emg- normal despite bil lower ext

3/2023 Telomere test 9.83
11/30/23- Had first child at 40, wife and I had no issues which is surprising bc she went of birth control and within 1 month she was pregnant. Her body failed her at 8 months bc she had preeclampsia. Didn't listen to any advice but did watch around tech and light at night. Had Vag delivery and no jaundice, we were out in 2 days due to her dropping weight slightly. To this day no issues and is hopefully developing normally

8/2024-lower ext doppler- 0% occlusive disease bil

4/2025- us testicles due to pain- found left varicocele which has increased in size size 10 years ago (anything increasing in size is losing energy)

My biggest concern right now is moving out of this hell hole. I can see everything is increasing in size meaning the shit Im doing is not enough and I do everything to the T, I don't screwy around with my life, I only have one vessel this go around. ### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-28T16:53:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-optimal-journal.30575/post-351436>

Steiner’s description of “giant oysters” emitting slime that sustained life, with the Earth and Moon conjoined, seems to be a metaphorical or esoteric narrative rather than a literal scientific account. His lectures often used vivid imagery to convey spiritual ideas about Earth’s evolution, drawing from his clairvoyant visions rather than physical evidence. The mention of oysters the size of France, a thick sauce-like slime, and the Earth as an egg with the Moon as a pearl suggests a cosmological mythos, not a testable hypothesis. The idea that this slime could be DHA, a key omega-3 fatty acid critical for brain and eye development in modern organisms, is intriguing but speculative.

Time, Light, the JAB, COVID, and Mitochondrial Hypoxia. The GIFT THAT KEEPS ON GIVING. It is worse than Herpes because it is DEADLY.

Time theft is irreplaceable nature, which directly extends to how light impacts mitochondrial function at the mtDNA level, where ultraweak photon emissions (UPEs) are generated in mammals. Mitochondria rely on circadian timing to optimize energy transformation via the electron transport chain (ETC), using sunlight to drive photobioelectric currents. Sunlight, rich in red and near-infrared wavelengths (600–1000 nm), penetrates tissues and enhances cytochrome c oxidase (CCO) activity, boosting ATP production and reducing reactive oxygen species (ROS). This process is disrupted by hypoxia at the mtDNA level, where UPE spectra shift, often narrowing and weakening, mimicing the current solar cycle and that manifests in us as a chronic disease.

Light stress caused all 5 extinction events on earth because it lead to a metabolic stress in living things they could not adapt to fast enough.

Artificial light, like LEDs, lacks the full spectrum of sunlight, particularly red/infrared wavelengths, and emits high levels of blue light (400–500 nm). Blue light inhibits CCO by increasing ROS, leading to hypoxia and mitochondrial heteroplasmy. This “subtracts time” from our life spans by accelerating cellular aging: disrupted circadian rhythms (melanopsin in the retina/SCN), impairing mitochondrial UPE production. Over time, this manifests as chronic diseases. [https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1930629376795627528.html](https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1930629376795627528.html)

### Mat.I — 2025-09-25T02:55:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-optimal-journal.30575/post-360018>

O2 & NO have unpaired electrons (they are paramagnetic). At CCO normally O2>H2O. NO competes with O2 for the binding site at CCO. When NO binds it blocks O2 reduction (slowing respiration).

By throttling O2 use NO maintains low mito oxygen consumption (hypoxic like) + higher NADH/NAD+ ratio (more reduced environment) - this is what stem cells would need to be atavistic. The elavated NADH would tune the ROS and biophoton output

Switch could be on (high NO) = stemness/atavistic state or off (low NO) = differentiation/more ordered

E.g. disease states (cancer/chronic inflammation) - CCO is permanatley throttled (aka offline) + NADH/NAD+ ratio is high and shifts to a warburg metabolism - results in ROS + biophoton signaling becoming noisy and incoherent (aka cells lose differentiation guidance).

Health = NO on as needed
Disease = NO on is prolonged or stuck on (which send you back in time to fetal program)

### HealthByPhysics — 2025-11-05T18:01:08+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-optimal-journal.30575/post-362208>

WTF happened 11/2/25, I need some advice on this one and if one more person tells me I inflamed a disc or slept on my arm wrong I'm going to lose it bc that's all I been hearing

Symptoms started at 11 am. Prior to them I was working on sugar shack roof, vacuuming downstairs and pulled the plug on my tanning bed from the wall, that's it nothing strenuous. At that time started with left forearm cramping/tightness from elbow to hand, with pain radiating up to shoulder, across trap and into the start of the deltoid (not the whole muscle, just one small area head the front head of the delt attaches)

Went to get lunch but within an hour my wife had to drive the car, I was unable to move my left arm without pain, even the slightest movement hurt. My left shoulder in that one area hurt so bad I was unable to turn the steering wheel. Left shoulder was painful but not like the arm or other shoulder.

At 1:05 the forearm was so stiff and painful. It hurt to lower or raise my arm, every way I moved it even rotational hurt. Very difficult to open fingers, when pulled back felt in my forearm. Felt like the carpal tendons what a vice on them. Tried Motrin and Flexeril which did nothing

I went to urgent care for steroids, had none at the house but needed something bc this pain I have never experienced before in my hand. After wards while driving, I just let the phone roll off my fingers bc I couldn't really manipulate it and I nearly cried. It was so painful for another 2 hours, steroids maybe were kicking in. Hot baths did help w the pain and did loosen while in the water

That night after another bath which really helped, the very hot water broke the tightness and was able to more more but not much, cancelled the pain pill order from a friend

That night my left shoulder was the worst and just in that one spot, unable to move the arm without severe pain. right arm still hurt but no where like it did.

# Thread: Istanbul
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/istanbul.30578/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Michael Allred — 2025-03-28T23:36:13+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/istanbul.30578/post-349528>

My experience - if you book a roundtrip on Turkish Air the price was cheaper if you book a stopover in Istanbul. If you book business class their lounge is nice. Take advantage of a refreshing shower, but get on the list as soon as you arrive. My advice is book the stopover on the way back to the US.
Old Town is a 45-60 minute drive from the airport, IST. I hate getting screwed by airport taxis so we went into town with the locals. They have coaches leaving IST continuously just outside baggage claim. Each coach has a couple of drop off points all over town. Find the coach to Old Town or cab it.
We stayed at the Radisson Blue in Old Town, across the street from the water, a good location. You can also book a boat cruise from the dock across the street from the hotel.
Turkish baths - if you are like me and prefer not to get bathed by some Turkish dude there is 1 place, close to Old Town where it is staffed with ladies from Indonesia, we also got a massage afterwards. If anyone is interested, I”m sure I can fine out the name of the place.
The food is good, our favorite restaurant was close to the hotel. But it was own by Arabs = no wine. Again, with a little effort I can probably find the name of the place.

Istanbul is a great place to visit!

2\. Even more for my referring physicians who know almost zero
3\. Does anyone have a way to monetize educating patients and being a resource so my practice doesn’t go under trying to bring health to my patients.

I tried just giving paperwork and resources but those patient never start

Was thinking of a concierge approach.
Help
Andy

# Thread: Is the vernal equinox causing puffy eyes and infection/ immune system crash?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-vernal-equinox-causing-puffy-eyes-and-infection-immune-system-crash.30584/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Shanti — 2025-03-29T23:10:22+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/is-the-vernal-equinox-causing-puffy-eyes-and-infection-immune-system-crash.30584/post-349601>

Jack our consult raised a lot of questions and I am still searching for useful solutions, although it was interesting to hear so much of the girl in the Dallas airport on the spring equinox. I am wondering what you think:

My gums above my canine on the right side has been a little inflamed the past few weeks alongside my conjunctivitis symptoms and skin eruptions. Is this another symptom of the same thing?

Last year I also had conjunctivitis for several months on and off starting in January and subsiding in November. (This is my second year, and so it's 3 months. ) The eyes create mucus with the initial onset but continue to be inflamed, puffy and watery without mucus. Still the optometrist (from my May visit last year ) said it tests convincingly at conjunctivitis. Yes the situation returns after even a month of antibiotics for the eyes. You suggested saline solution. I am wondering if you might suggest Ivermectin (how many treatments?) or something else?

I eat all local, mostly fresh vacuum packed organic ribeyes and seafood raw with some fruit. I cook the shellfish. Could these raw meats be causing the problem? (To optimize the biophotons)

If I am in the northern hemisphere then I experience the sun's light the most intensely and coming from the highest angle in the sky at the summer solstice, yes? Not at the spring equinox. Yes? Or am I confused?

Or could my immune system struggle with this weird infection that created a few pussy cysts be a bacterial related challenge unrelated to the equinox?

Is my immune system really "old"" or is it just currently taxed with bacteria\\microbes here in Costa Rica?

If I am turning 60 and still have my natural blonde hair color without graying, is it possible that I haven't run out of stem cells but rather my chronic dry skin is a result of my environment and reversible if I just crack the code?

My daughter appreciated your feedback. We are currently reading Jason Lowry's book and Jaws. But both books seem to be misguiding as they point to diet as both the change agents. Jason is missing the DHA (light) component and instead pointing to cooked food and our access to more calories as pivotal to our brain development. Jaws points to sleeping with our mouths open and chewing methods instead of light environment as the cause of malocclusions. Yet my daughter is wondering if you--as a neurosurgeon and dentist-- see any validity to these authors' arguments?

# Thread: Ready to join the revolution and spread the word!
