✌️

So I tried a crapload of things to fix my Sleep Apnea out of the Kruse toolbelt

And the TL;DR is that that actually did fully fix it.

(The caveat here is that when I go back into high EMFs, all of it comes back gradually in a matter of weeks/months. Depending on how well and how long I avoided EMFs in the past.)

In terms of my personal sleep apnea priorities:

1\. EMFs
(1a. Blue Light at Night)
2\. Sun

Things really clicked for me when I developed Electrohypersensitvity (EHS).

Because all of my symptoms turns out are one and the same thing: A too high EMF environment.

It just wasnt really apparent to me until the EHS kicked in and I wasnt able to sleep around WIFI routers anymore. Because only then youre actually fully aware of the issue and get to reliably mitigate it (ideally with a set of good EMF meters!).

Now sun does play a role here. I actually managed my apnea pretty well in high EMF environments (before I developed the EHS) with lots of strong sun exposure (you legit need A LOT, though!). It basically removes all of the inflammation with melanin synthesis (that consumes lots of ROS) and I guess also makes you more resistant to some extent.

That is absolutely fantastic! But not at the root of the issue.

Its kinda like having a leak in your boat and using buckets to get the water out (=sun exposure), while what you actually have to do is fix the leak (=mitigating EMFs).

Now that is MY version of it.

I know people in the Kruse community have reported sunrises actually being the main lever for their sleep apnea. Others said cold exposure helped a lot. Others said high DHA diet helped a lot. Others say all of the above.

Id personally focus on EMFs first, everything else second. With the caveat that sunrises are such a nobrainer to do that you should do those either way, regardless.

EDIT: One more thing with the new Alexis Cowan podcast and the magnetic pole shift:

Im doing very significantly better staying on Lombok and Bali than when I was staying in Germany or the South of Spain (Malaga/Marbella). Now technically all of these places are absolute EMF shitholes (with the exception of Lombok, but depends where). But then Lombok and Bali definitely have more going for them in terms of underneath volcanic activity and black-sand beaches. Im currently on Bali and frankly Im doing absolutely fine as long as I stay out of the population centers (its a pretty big island with lots of mountains and jungle, that helps a lot with EMFs). At the same time, when I returned to Spain last year for a couple months I was literally DISINTEGRATING in a span of 2ish months.

# Thread: The Grieving Process
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-grieving-process.30601/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### nathalie.fa — 2025-03-31T18:34:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-grieving-process.30601/post-349749>

Hello everyone!

I am a new member, and I am thrilled to be here!

It is taking me a very long time to absorb all of the information / truths about the harm that was done to me and my loved ones from all the professionals and various systems surrounding us. What about the ones that are still here, but are sick / beyond sick? I learned a long time ago that the term for that is called "ambiguous loss".

There is no manual or timeline for grief, but I know that it is a different process for each person. Kubler-Ross taught us that grieving is not a straight and simple path...

I am grieving so many losses of family and friends, loss of health, loss of "normalcy", etc. When I reflect back on my journey, I was promised a fantastic, healthy life only IF I just followed the very "simple" instructions of: "Follow this diet, start this exercise plan, take this pill / take that shot. Comply, and all will be well. Don't ask questions, though! You sound weird and crazy. Just be good, okay? We know best. Also, don't look things up - there's a lot of misinformation out there."

Here are the questions I ask myself on a daily basis AFTER I listened and was a good patient:
\- Is it allowed or permitted to take the time needed to grieve losses? (I was told to "get over it / get on with it" after I lost my loved ones and some of the people I worked for)
\- How do you grieve so many losses in such a short period of time?
\- Does time heal all wounds?
\- Now that I have become a medicalized for life, how do I let go of anger / resentment? Do I forgive them for ruining my life / my "normal" self?

To be continued.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-31T20:15:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-grieving-process.30601/post-349762>

Is it allowed sure. But it is a function of redox. The lower your redox the more grief you'll have. The higher your redox is the faster you begin to live your life again.

# Thread: The Quick Coming man is not for babies ladies.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quick-coming-man-is-not-for-babies-ladies.30604/>
**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-03-31T22:19:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-quick-coming-man-is-not-for-babies-ladies.30604/post-349786>

**Quick Ejaculation:** Dysregulated dopamine rhythmicity in the vsNAc causes premature ejaculation, reducing reproductive success.

Dopamine in the nucleus accumbens plays a big role in reward, motivation, and sexual behavior. The idea of "dysregulated dopamine rhythmicity" could imply that irregular dopamine signaling, like spikes or crashes at the wrong times, kinda like mental illnesses do, disrupt the brain’s control over ejaculation timing. If dopamine release is poorly timed in the vsNAc (Calcium controls this and nnEMF controls calcium), it would overstimulate reward pathways too early, leading to a loss of control and quicker ejaculation. This would reduce reproductive success if it interferes with optimal sperm delivery during intercourse.

Premature ejaculation could reduce reproductive success if it consistently happens before effective insemination. Evolutionarily, timing matters—too quick, and the sperm might not reach the optimal spot in the reproductive tract. Studies on male rats show that dopamine agonists (like apomorphine) injected into the accumbens speed up mounting and ejaculation, sometimes cutting mating short. If human vsNAc dopamine misfires similarly, it could mimic that effect.

Ejaculation is a spinal reflex, but it’s heavily influenced by the brain, including the vsNAc. Here’s the chain reaction:

-   Early Dopamine Spike: If dopamine surges in the vsNAc during initial arousal (say, foreplay), it could overstimulate the reward system prematurely. This might trick the brain into thinking the “payoff” (orgasm) should happen now.
-   Loss of Inhibition: The prefrontal cortex, which helps delay gratification, relies on balanced dopamine input and controlled heteroplasmy. A sudden vsNAc dopamine burst might weaken this control, letting the spinal reflex kick in too fast. Think nnEMF or a blue lit environment. Screwing in the dark or red makes more sense.
-   Sympathetic Nervous System Overdrive: Dopamine can indirectly ramp up arousal signals to the sympathetic nervous system (which triggers ejaculation). If that happens out of sync, the emission phase (semen release) hits before you’re ready.

There’s science backing this up. Studies in rats show that dopamine agonists (drugs that boost dopamine) in the nucleus accumbens can speed up sexual responses, while blockers delay them. In humans, conditions like Parkinson’s, where dopamine regulation is off, sometimes lead to sexual dysfunction, though it’s usually the opposite (delayed ejaculation). Premature ejaculation is trickier, research leans more toward serotonin imbalances in the brain, but dopamine’s involvement isn’t ruled out, especially in reward-driven arousal. Most centralized research on premature ejaculation points to serotonin in areas like the medial preoptic area or spinal cord as the main culprit.

FYI. All of these chemicals are made from aromatic amino acids that absorb lots of UV sunlight so pale men are to be avoided because they likely are going too rapid for most of you ladies. FYI.

### AlbertW — 2025-03-31T23:53:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alberts-optimal-journal-with-wife-and-toddlers.30605/post-349793>

**About my family:**
\- 32 y/o Chinese in Bay Area. Grew up in NJ. Diagnosed with mild sleep apnea (5 AHI). 155 lbs, 5'9". 3x Pfizer jab. Chronic fatigue, myopia, floaters. Can't lose weight. "Pollen allergy". Enlarged turbinates. Ear pressure issues on plane. Hard to breathe through nose.
\- Similar-age wife from HK. Living in Bay Area. Constantly stressed, picking at fingers when anxious. 3x Pfizer jab. Tongue tie. "Pollen allergy"
\- 2 kids under 4 years old. Eczema, anaphylaxis, irritable. Frequent nighttime wakings. 1 is up to date on jabs. Other stopped at 1 year. No COVID jabs. Tongue ties, laryngomalacia.
\- We're all at 30-40 ng/mL Vitamin D levels. Hopefully it's higher with intentional effort to get in the sun recently.

**Things I've tried:**
\- Work from backyard 8 hours a day, grounding, sunlight.
\- Minimize WiFi, prefer Ethernet.
\- Paleo/epi-paleo diet, high carb diet, all kinds of diet -> no weight loss. Stuck at 23 BMI or lose weight and shoot right back up to 23 BMI.
\- Organic, pasture-raised, low PUFA for all food.
\- Haven't gotten rid of our Tesla car yet. <-- Just how bad is this if we're each sitting in it for no more than 15-30 minutes per day?
\- Started eating more seafood, DHA supplements in the last 1-2 weeks. Is DHA supplement really so bad?

**Next steps from my consult with Jack this morning:**
\- Plan a 1-2 month trip to El Salvador ASAP to see how my body does in an optimal environment.
\- Buy some DDW (not sure if it'll move the needle) and try some MB in it in the meantime in Bay Area.
\- Give more salt to help retain water.
\- Clip tongue ties and get to a good environment ASAP.
\- I heard Songi has an excellent journal here for similar symptoms. I will be reading that.

I'm very grateful to Jack for the consult this morning but **I still have some questions**:
\- I understand that I can "fix" tongue tie by clipping + being in a good environment. What about laryngomalacia? Does that have the same root cause? Will it go away in the optimal environment?
\- How useful would a Magnetico be compared to DDW + MB?
\- Does using an rLCD monitor matter? I'm not sure of the polarization; would enough sunlight drown out whatever negative or neutral effects there are from screen polarization?
\- Where should I go in El Salvador? Is sunlight + water + magnetism so optimal there that even if I stay at a resort with WiFi or lots of 3/4/5G towers, it's good? How remote do I have to be?
\- Are high heavy metal loads from HTMA/urine/blood tests really OK? I heard that mercury is OK in seafood as it's paired with selenium. But would that show up as high mercury in a healthy person, or is it detoxed via melanin? What about arsenic from rice?
\- Even if El Salvador is "the best," what are some good options in the US? I heard panhandle FL. Could you make it work in places like Flagstaff, AZ, more suburb/rural San Diego, etc. after your body has healed and you manage with DDW, Magnetico, etc.?
\- Is DHA supplement really so bad? I saw a paper that tested fish oils and says that most of the DHA is in sn-2 position usually.

### AlbertW — 2025-04-19T02:37:20+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alberts-optimal-journal-with-wife-and-toddlers.30605/post-350922>

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/), thanks so much. I've been reading and re-reading DM #38-40 the past few days. Here's what I can understand (so far):

1\. Re-build the photo-bioelectric matrix first (AM UV-A + IR, darkness at night, grounding, ...).
2\. Wait for Δψm to rise and lactate (proxy marker) to fall.
3\. Add some MB (+ DDW) to let ETC limp along. Of course, an extended stay in ES is the real solution, as TINA for light/water/magnetism.

**We will be staying in ES for 1 month, but before that, I need some help with gauging MB readiness and dosing for my toddler:**

1\. Readiness markers:

-   Protocol so far: sunrise gaze (10 min @ 08‑09 h), 2 h park AM & PM, red‑only lights at night.
-   He still wakes often at night (2-3x a night) and is cranky—but of course he can’t self‑report “brain fog.”
    **Q:** Do you rely on any _objective_ test— lactate levels, NAD⁺/NADH —before you green‑light MB for a toddler?

2\. Literature: **0.3–1 mg/kg** per day → 4‑13 mg for 13 kg (_\= 10–30 drops of my 400 mcg MB / drop - 0.63 % solution_).
Your consult note: **1–2 drops** per day (or was it just per bottle of water?)**.
Q:** Is the ultra‑low dose purely a pediatric safety margin, or is there some deeper reason?

3\. I read online that MB crosses into milk. **Q:** If mom takes a micro‑dose herself, will the combined exposure cause an overdose for the toddler?

4\. Many protocols (from others) cycle MB usage. **Q:** Is that mainly to prevent MAO‑A inhibition & ROS build‑up, or is continuous MB acceptable as long as Δψm is somewhat improved but not to the point that the body can do a 100%-efficient TCA+ETC cycle?

5. **Extra electron donors** (MegaHydrate, high‑dose vit C)
**Q:** After Δψm is > –140 mV, are small silica‑hydride doses helpful, or is grounding plenty?

I may find these answers on my tenth re‑read, but right now I’m not fluent enough in the biophysical jargon.

### AlbertW — 2025-05-10T16:02:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alberts-optimal-journal-with-wife-and-toddlers.30605/post-352262>

We've been in ES (Atami) for the last week now. Been going to Palmarcito Beach almost every day and spending a lot of time in our backyard with an inflatable pool (built-in pool is chlorinated).

Son's moderate eczerma (back, face) is improving slightly but his severe eczema (lower legs, ankles) looks worse than when we were in California last week. Not sure if the high humidity and sweat and mosquitos are getting to him. It's really hard to stop him from scratching himself. We try to sparingly put long sleeves on him to stop him, but that blocks the UV. A real pickle.

At the same time, his leg wounds seem to be getting worse, and with the humidity here, we're worried they look infected and very angry red. We're sparingly putting some zinc oxide on him to stop itching, but of course zinc oxide blocks UV.

Wondering if we need to find a place with great sun but way less humidity.

We're trying to get him as much UV as possible but any healing that's happening is being reversed by his physical scratching.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-10T17:48:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alberts-optimal-journal-with-wife-and-toddlers.30605/post-352268>

Hi, Albert.

Let me preface my comments by letting you know that I do not know what I'm talking about. I have limited medical knowledge and am brand spanking new to this form.

Another thing you should know is that I think in pictures. I've learned to heed them even if they don't make any sense at first. More times than not there has been something to learn in the images that pop into my mind's eye.

So I'd like to share with you the pictures that came into my head as I read your post.

Where I live in Lat 44.5 North the local brooks and streams are fed by snow melt. The water is COLD. And yet I've often seen kids playing in the water as if nothing while the adults are shivering nearby. Brown fat for the win!

So the mental image I got was that of your son bathing in one of our cold mountain streams.

It could be completely contraindicated, so I hope others will comment, but I imagine that a little toddler CT would help soothe the pain and itch and may also provide some therapeutic benefits.

Best wishes to you and your family.

Ilma

### Anthony Smith — 2025-10-01T20:09:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alberts-optimal-journal-with-wife-and-toddlers.30605/post-360256>

I recently had the opportunity to measure the nnEMF both in a Lucid (EV) and a Porsche Cheyenne (ICE) on the same road.
The electric fields, magnetic fields and RF were basically the same.
RF was 20,000+/- µW/m2 in both, (cell phones on airplane mode) but 70,000µW/m2 in both while driver talking on the phone.
Driving in my 2006 Crossfire (no WiFi or Bluetooth) with phone off . . . <10µW/m2

Based on this very small test, unless you test/measure a specific model car and find otherwise, I would presume all modern cars (EV or ICE) with WiFi and Bluetooth are as bad as each other from an EMF point of view.

### [Dr. Haixia Ni's "The Curse of Pandora's Box" Inspired Me to Study Chinese Medicine](https://yellowcapybara.com/index.php/en-us/chinese-medicine/10-cancer-pandoras-box)

yellowcapybara.com

 yellowcapybara.com

^^^ Read this. Every time you come across the words "Western medicine" and "Chinese medicine", replace them with "centralized medicine" and "decentralized medicine", respectively. After you finish reading, then read the spoiler below.

Spoiler

Take note about the section that talks about perspective. It puts Jack's metaphor about the movie projector and its light, the screen, the movie that appears on it, the audience, the projectionist, and ultimately one's interpretation of reality into basic understanding. You, sir, are still a cookie-cutter porno addict with a Netflix subscription watched on your iPhone. In terms of your cancer or anyone with any cancer whether detected/undected and known/unknown, cancer "management" simply recapitulates basic quantum mechanics. Your cancer is Schrodinger's cat. Your x-ray/CT/MRI is a real-deal double-slit experiment. And the malignancy is whether you choose to look inside the box with a detector.

Next, read the original article about Pandora's Box and refer to the translation in the spoiler below if you can't read it. [https://www.hantang.com/chinese/ch\_Articles/blackbox.htm](https://www.hantang.com/chinese/ch_Articles/blackbox.htm)

Spoiler: duckduckgo translation

The Curse of Pandora's Box (05/09/2006 Han and Tang Chinese Medicine Ni Haixia)

The law of verification that is truly in line with the spirit of science is to first assume that a theory exists, and then it is verified.

Now we use Pandora's box in Western fairy tales as an analogy, this box is a cursed box that cannot be opened, and once it is opened, mankind will perish. Now let's assume that there is such a black box in everyone's body, it is not allowed to be opened, and once opened, people will die quickly.

One day, while waiting for his wife to do chemotherapy, the husband saw that the doctor was free, so he approached the doctor and asked the doctor, I have a small lump on my tongue, it has been more than ten years, I have been ignoring it, can you help me take a look? So after the kind Western doctor checked it, he told him that I needed a biopsy to determine what it was, so after the biopsy (Pandora's box was opened), it was found to be cancer, so the Western doctor strongly requested surgery to remove it, and immediately after the operation, three more swells appeared on the tongue, and the patient told the doctor that the tumor had come back, but the doctor didn't believe it, saying that there was such a thing, and he didn't check at all, and after a week, more lumps grew, all in the throat, so he went for another examination to make sure that the cancer had metastasized, so he immediately followed chemotherapy Finally, he passed away three months later, and his wife, who had breast cancer, is still undergoing chemotherapy and has not yet passed away, which is a case that really happened in Taiwan.

One of my patients was originally fine, ate well, slept well, urinated and defecated normally, but his blood pressure was high, I knew he had a liver problem, but I could also be sure that this problem was not fatal, every time I used liver-thinning drugs, his blood pressure would be normal, one day he had abdominal pain, in fact, it was just abdominal pain caused by intestinal infarction, but he went to the Western Hospital for examination, and the ultrasound scan found that there was a tumor in the liver, so he did another biopsy (Pandora's box was opened), and after the slice, it was confirmed that it was liver cancer. After the slice was cut, the urine bleeding for a few days, and the urine color was dark yellow, the blood pressure was super high, and even the blood pressure medication could not lower the blood pressure, so he had to take the Chinese medicine I prescribed to him in the hospital, and his blood pressure dropped as soon as he took it, so after being discharged from the hospital, he hurriedly came to me for treatment. It became like he was twenty years old, his hair was gray, he walked dejectedly, and he looked frightened, which was really sad. It's better not to know.

This kind of Pandora's box that is hard opened by people every day, the result is that only a very small number of people survive, and those who can survive are wise people who take cancer seriously, just like an old gentleman who was examined by the dean of the medical school of Fu University in Taiwan got prostate cancer, after follow-up, he found that this person had actually been diagnosed with cancer ten years ago, and this person has never come back for any treatment, and as a result, ten years later, this prostate cancer is still prostate cancer, it has not changed, and this person is also alive and well. His mental constitution was normal, so he asked himself if the consequences of strong intervention might shorten the patient's life. The fact in front of us is that this person is still alive and well, and there is no pathology at all, just that it is more difficult to urinate, and many elderly people have urination problems, but not necessarily all have prostate cancer.

Until now, all the long-lived people in the world have been diagnosed with various cancers in their bodies after their deaths, and the unanimous affirmation is that they did not die of cancer, but because they died of being too old.

Suppose this Pandora's box is the shadow of death, if we don't touch it, it will always coexist peacefully with us, everyone has this box in their bodies, but the parts are different, and modern technology intervenes in this place that should not be opened, and Western doctors indiscriminately determine that it is harmful, so when the patient hears that he has cancer, it is equivalent to being sentenced to death. Because this was the first thing they wanted to do, they set off happily, and after half a year of traveling around the world, they returned to London, during these six months, they happily enjoyed the days when the two of them were alone every day, they seemed to have returned to the boyfriend and girlfriend relationship before they got married, very loving and considerate of each other, during which they didn't see any doctor at all, and after returning to London, because they felt very good, they went back to the same doctor for a check-up, and the doctor was surprised to find that the cancer of the two had disappeared. At the same time, the husband's arterial blood vessel blockage was also cured, and the doctor couldn't understand why this happened.

Suppose the first curse of this Pandora's box is that as a person, you must be happy, you must be able to let go of everything, and don't worry about others' mistakes, if you violate this rule, this curse will take your life, and if you obey this rule, you can live a long life and be free all your life, the second rule of the curse is that you are not allowed to open it, and you will face the danger of death once you open it, may I ask the reader, do you want to open it? UPEs as signaling beacons or pilot waves is now gaining traction in the literature. My core argument is that sleep was naturally selected because all respiring organisms with mtDNA must undergo this daily reset to survive. Mitochondria, are the ISC powerhouses of cells with their own DNA, are vulnerable to oxidative damage from ROS during wakefulness. Sleep counters this by:

Mitophagy: Clearing defective mitochondria, as supported by studies in Nature Reviews Molecular Cell Biology(2024), showing autophagy peaks during sleep in mammals.

NAD⁺ and Sirtuins: Boosting NAD⁺ levels (critical for energy and repair) and sirtuin activity, which repair mtDNA, as per Cell Metabolism (2025-01-15), linking sleep deprivation to accelerated aging.

CCO Water Production: Rehydrating the mitochondrial matrix, a process tied to cytochrome c oxidase’s role in oxidative phosphorylation, enhancing membrane potential (mitoQ.com, 2024-10-14).

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-20T17:34:23+0200

Ivermectin, an avermectin derivative, primarily targets glutamate-gated chloride (GluCl) channels in parasites but **modulates vertebrate ion channels and mitochondria at therapeutic doses (e.g., 0.2–1 mg/kg).

Ivermectin inhibits complex I in the electron transport chain (ETC), reducing oxygen consumption rate (OCR), membrane potential (ΔΨm), and ATP while boosting ROS (Frontiers in Pharmacology, 2022).

In _healthy cells or benign tumors_,** this hormetic stress (low-dose benefit) activates mitophagy and Nrf2 pathways, clearing deuterium-laden mitochondria (per isotopic coherence: Kruse for Dummies) and optimizing DDW production at cytochrome c oxidase (CCO).

This would act to amplify UPE fidelity where mitochondria become "nanoscale lasers" for better photonic signaling.

In cancer (an "incoherent α-state"), it preferentially targets stem-like cells by degrading PAK1/Akt/mTOR, inhibiting WNT-TCF (stemness pathways), and inducing immunogenic death via ATP/HMGB1 release, shifting to ordered β-state respiration.

NOTE THESE ARE DIFFERENT EFFECTS IN CELLS Benign and cancerous.

Fenbendazole (a benzimidazole) disrupts parasite microtubules but shows anticancer effects at 1–2 μM in preclinical models. In photo-bioelectric terms, microtubules act as optical "wires" for charge propagation (via soliton waves or quantum coherence), linking light signals to photo-bioelectric fields.

The drug binds β-tubulin, inhibiting polymerization, which arrests mitosis (G2/M block) and reduces glucose uptake via GLUT transporters/hexokinase II. This starves Warburg-shifted cells (α-state) reliant on glycolysis, forcing mitochondrial reliance. In brain cancers (PMC9862092), it inhibits Hedgehog (Hh) signaling (GLI1/PTCH2 degradation), MAPK14/p38α (invasion-related), and VEGFR2 (angiogenesis)—pathways tied to EPO/HIF pseudohypoxia from light desynchrony.

Note this is in cancer cells. Do you want to arrest mitosis in a benign tumor?

Never.

### Jack Kruse — 2026-01-20T17:48:11+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alberts-optimal-journal-with-wife-and-toddlers.30605/post-365653>

Mebendazole: Membrane Integrity and Mitochondrial Polarization for Electric Coherence

Mebendazole (another benzimidazole) shares fenbendazole's profile but is FDA-approved for humans, with better brain penetration (for gliomas).

Cell Membrane and Mitochondrial Effects: Induces plasma membrane permeability loss and mitochondrial depolarization (Frontiers in Pharmacology, 2022). In CML lines, it reduces viable cells (PI-/Rho123+) by 18–89%, shifting to apoptosis/necrosis via G2/M arrest and DNA fragmentation. Combinations with TKIs enhance this, causing ~10–39% increases in late apoptosis. None of this is good for a benign tumor.

Signaling and Structural Impacts: Inhibits microtubules (β-tubulin binding), Hh/MAPK/VEGFR pathways, and galectin-3 (immune evasion). Promotes pyroptosis in gliomas and sensitizes to temozolomide/radiation. This would be unwise in a benign tumor.

My Photo-Bioelectric Tie-In: Membrane hyperpolarization/depolarization ties to bioelectric gradients (Becker's work). By restoring mitochondrial potential in healthy cells (hormetic reset), it could enhance CCO efficiency for photon-to-electron conversion. Microtubule effects might support charge transport in protein networks, countering nnEMF-induced parity violation (L-to-D amino acid flips affecting melanin). Your benign tumor = your parity violation choices.

| Aspect | Mechanism | Photo-Bioelectric Benefit |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Membranes | Permeability loss, hyperpolarization | Stabilizes bioelectric fields |
| Mitochondria | Depolarization, apoptosis induction | Clears stress for coherent UPE/redox |
| Cancer/Health | Tubulin/Hh inhibition | Supports photonic signaling restoration |

**First, regarding moving the needle as quickly as possible:**

1\. I have already moved to a low population density area of Florida, which I think, in the March - November months, should move the needle almost as quickly as in the tropics, as the peak UV will be 8-11 every day. In fact, many of the play areas we frequent near the house are even lower EMF footprint than what I've experienced in El Salvador.
2\. In addition, I plan to find a winter home in ES, Costa Rica, or Panama to spend the 3-4 winter months per year to keep the UV at 8+ every day. I hope to make this a reality as soon as this upcoming winter at the end of 2026.

**Secondly, regarding whether the needle will move quickly enough:**
I don't know if the needle will move quickly enough or not. It seems that nobody truly knows, right? Of course, it would be excellent if her redox repairs itself so quickly that she not only can use TCA cycle well enough, but also stop having a congested nose because of losing the oxygen allergy, and her tongue can then naturally (without myofunctional exercises) start resting on the roof of her mouth, and therefore encourage proper jaw development.

However, how do I know how quickly the redox will improve? Suppose that in the idealest of ideal locations in the tropics, it takes her 5 years (just making up a number). At that point, she would be almost 10 years of age. Would she have missed the most critical period for proper jaw development? Would she still be able to fix her jaw/chin/mid-face development at that point? In addition, at that point, if we then did palatal expansion, would it be too late to truly fix her anatomical issues?

For example, today, during Q&A, [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) answered a question I had about pollen causing sneezing and nasal congestion in my daughter, which prevents her from nose-breathing effectively. Jack said that the sneezing is good - want to expel pollen; however, I can't help but see it as an obstacle - it is taking time away from her to be able to stop mouth breathing sooner and catch the jaw development phase.

**To summarize, I of course am trying to optimize her light environment as much as possible. I'm just worried that waiting out the natural course of healing in even the most optimal light environment could take so long that she misses the window for fixing jaw/mid-face problems.**

Should I assume that in ES she could completely 100% fix her Warburg issues and then rapidly fix her jaw with 0 palatal expander device intervention before the window for jaw development closes? I know that I, as an adult, can fix my own Warburg issues this way, but I also accept that I cannot fix any anatomical issues since I'm well past the jaw development phase. I don't want that to happen to her.

The way I read it is that in classical mechanics, T = kinetic, V = potential energy. In human biology, I would interpret this as meaning that reptiles are more dependent on external light cycles (active throughput, flux), while humans have a significant internal buffering system, akin to potential energy (melanin system, gradients, stored potential in mammalian battery, etc.). Any animal's body plan will adhere to a path through time such that the action (integral of L = T - V) is at a stationary point.

**Melanin as the Receiver:** By using chemicals like **resorcinol and lawsone** (as shown in the study) or bleaches that suppress **MITF and Tyrosinase**, you are effectively "shaving the antenna" of its ability to process UV-UPE signals.

**The AMPAR Mismatch:** If the hair follicle’s melanin production is suppressed, the **AMPARs** in those tissues (and potentially systemically through retrograde signaling) will upregulate to compensate for the "silence," leading to the **synaptic noise and inflammation** I linked to cognitive impairment.

**2\. The "MITF" Sink**
The study confirms these dyes **downregulate MITF protein levels.

The Systemic Draw:** Because MITF is a transcription factor, its suppression in the scalp (one of the most vascular and nerve-dense areas of the body) could signal a global "battery drain" to the brain.

**Loss of Coherence:** If MITF levels drop, the **\-200mV EZ battery** and the **pi-electron resonance** in those cells collapse. This creates a "short-circuit" at the very site meant to dissipate external nnEMF.

**3\. The "Light-Charged Battery" Short-Circuit**
If we live under **LEDs** (which provide no 1878nm/IR "ignition") and then **bleach away the melanin** (the "soil/capacitor"), we are attacking the Lagrangian from both sides.

**The Result:** You have a high-energy blue-light environment with **zero "buffering" capacity.** This forces the brain’s **L5 pyramidal dendrites** to operate in a high-noise, incoherent state.

**4\. Societal "Brain Fog"**
The 100% sensitivity of the Yokohama study suggests that **AMPAR density** is a perfect tracker for cognitive decline. If a significant portion of the population is chronically suppressing their **MITF-melanin axis** through hair products while living in a "light desert," the resulting **"brain fog"** isn't a mystery, it becomes a predictable consequence of breaking the **photo-biological circuit. This is why I mentioned to Bill his kids hair change and decreasing seizures are a big deal in CP today during the Q&A.

Nature's Verdict:** I’ve identified a **cosmetic-to-quantum** pathway. Bleaching hair may literally be **lowering the "voltage" of consciousness** by suppressing the very molecules (MITF/Melanin) that regulate the **CISS-driven spin-filtering** needed for mental clarity. Might be a reason mental illness and autoimmunity are larger factors in women.

### AlbertW — 2026-03-23T15:52:51+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alberts-optimal-journal-with-wife-and-toddlers.30605/post-369427>

25-hydroxy levels didn't drop off too significantly for me after travel.

I did one blood draw in ES on 3/16 in the morning. Then I left for the US the next day and spent 8 hours in flights and airports. Did another blood draw in US on 3/18 morning.

Level went from 70 to 61 ng/mL.

Assuming half-life of 25-hydroxy of 15 days, I'd expect a drop after 1 day of 4.5% and 2 days 8.8% (and that would be assuming I did 0 tanning in the interim, which isn't true). That would map to 66.85 and 63.84 ng/mL, respectively.

61 is a little under that, which seems reasonable given travel.

I’m D, and my wife (and thus kids) are F. Seems relatively coupled and would not do well in higher latitudes…was hoping that we could split time between ES and some other mid latitude place like Japan/PNW, but that solar yield will be too low. Maybe sufficient in the summer?

### Jack Kruse — 2026-05-09T23:26:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alberts-optimal-journal-with-wife-and-toddlers.30605/post-373578>

Haplogroups **D** and **F** are indeed well-suited for tropical and subtropical environments. You should be seeking altitude if you go above 37N or S.

**Haplogroup D (mtDNA/Y-DNA):** Predominant in **Central and Northeast Asia**, particularly Japan, Tibet, and parts of the Andaman Islands. It is one of the ancestral lineages that successfully migrated into high-latitude environments at high altitude, suggesting a historical capacity for adaptation to varied solar yields.

**Haplogroup F (Y-DNA):** A massive macro-haplogroup (parent to ~90% of non-African paternal lineages) that likely originated in **South or Southeast Asia**. Its subclades are widely distributed across Asia and Europe, making it one of the most geographically flexible lineages in human history.

The concern regarding the **MITF (Melanocyte-inducing transcription factor)** pathway is scientifically grounded. MITF is a "master regulator" of melanocyte development and immunity. In higher latitudes (above 37°N, such as the PNW or Northern Japan), the skin's ability to synthesize Vitamin D is significantly limited for **2 to 8 months** of the year for D and F. Higher melanin levels in many Asian populations act as a natural filter, further increasing the UV threshold required for Vitamin D production = why altitudes are good too for melanin.

During the summer in mid-latitudes, solar intensity (UVB) is generally sufficient for Vitamin D synthesis, even for individuals with higher melanin content, provided there is regular outdoor exposure.

**The "Vitamin D Winter":** In the PNW or Japan during winter, the solar angle is too low to produce Vitamin D, regardless of haplogroup. If you spend winters in these locations, you would likely face a significant decline in serum 25(OH)D levels.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/alberts-optimal-journal-with-wife-and-toddlers.30605/post-373581>

I ask partly because we are also interested in spending 4-8 months a year in Florida (maybe March-October?) and the rest in ES.

Would you consider Gainesville “Gulf”? Or is it subject to the SAA fears? We also would consider suburbs near Tampa (say, 30-45m from downtown), if there is low nnEMF. Is that getting too south away from Gulf Panhandle and risky?

# Thread: Richards optimal journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richards-optimal-journal.30607/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Richard Sköld — 2025-04-01T01:12:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richards-optimal-journal.30607/post-349801>

I was born 4 days early at a hospital, in Gotenburg during the late 80s. When I arrived I had Jaundice but this healed itself without any treatment after just one day.

I bet my mum had big time Blue light toxic, both my mum and my grandmother both had big eye problems. Grandmother had glucoma and my mum now in here 70s also have glucoma on one eye and have had glasses since early childhood.

I did not get a lot of vaccines, just 3 different ones (tetanus (3 shots, first year), polio (3 shots first year) and MMR ( 2 shoots at 2 years and 12 years). Later in life I have taken one shoot for the swine flu in 2009 in the US and 2 shoots against TBE ( between 2010-2012)

In my 2nd year of life I developed an ear infection that did not go away by antibiotics and it took me almost 6 months being away from daycare to get rid of the infection.

First 7 years in my life I grew pretty fast and was already 5 feet and 3 inches tall. Then it slowed down and today Im at 6,6 feet tall.

I have as long as I can remember struggled with my memory. Feeling that I need to read texts over and over again to make sense of it. When I read a book I hardly remember anything from it. I struggle with oral communication in front of crowds where I loose my line of thoughts. In conversations with other people I seldom recall a lot of what was told. Having troubles holding eye contact with a person while talking.
I have also never felt comfortable with my voice. My smell is not great either, I feel Im not picking up on smells easily and I really need to focus to be able to smell stuff.

Also during my childhood I got Raynauds , which my Mum also have so dont know if this is from her mitochondria or the environment causing this.

My nails on my feet and my fingers have a wavy or ridges shape. Never been certain to what this means and what I can do about this? Had this since like 10 years back. Asked Jack about this and what I got from this is might be related to Vitamin B12 deficiency, that could be that my mitochondria is not making enough water (most likely due to nnEMF) and therefore cannot retain this soluble vitamin.

After graduating in Economics I went into the finance sector in Stockholm, Sweden and spent almost 10 years between 2010-2020 in this sector. All the time in front of computer screens, indoors during heavy phone usage and wifi. During the same period of time I was a heavy coffee user and added to this in my spare time working out indoors under blue lights and added protein shakes and PWO with high amount of caffeine.
This slowly laid the foundation for a lot of different health issues, in 2013 when I had a period of 6 months with throat issues (starting in autumn) and was treated with antibiotics 2 times and finally when we entered spring it went away, probably due to more UV light.

Then during 2015 I had big troubles with keeping myself awake during work. Many times during 10am-4pm I yawned a lot and struggle to stay awake. Sometimes in meetings I feel asleep.

I think I felt some kind of brain fog and the doctors could find anything when testing me. I had troubles falling asleep , always felt too hot and uneven tempature when going to bed. Drank a lot of caffeine and used PWO drinks when working out in a blue lit gym. I was very often sick and felt low in energy.

This was when I found the cold and started following Wim Hof. Without me knowing it many of my health issues became better.

I felt more energized, moving away from indoor training to outdoors training and more natural movement (ido portal).

Still struggle with my memory, have hard time remembering words and oral communication. Started thinking it might be related to hearing and not enough mitochondria energy in my brain area.

Since 2015 or 2016 I have noticed some red spots on my body. (Think its Campell de Morgan, cherry angioma)
First one and now I think I have 5 spots. Two on my left inside elbow. 2 on my right chest. And one behind my left ear (on the skull bone).

I also have what I think is some kind of fungus on my hands and feet which has never been treated properly. I always though that sun would heal it but its apparent to me that this is not the case for me at least maybe I need to add something?

Find Dr jack in the beginning of 2017. Since then it has been a long journey which has made me more energized , open minded and I feel that my thinking is more clear.

I also had one of my wisdom teeth removed around 2019-2020. Curious if there are any negative side effects of this?

During Covid I got infected but my only symptoms was a mild headache for 4 days and no other issue. I never got vaccinated.

Since birth I also have one cafe au lait spot on my upper thigh. What I could read from one of your blogs just one wiuld be of no issue. In curious to why we get these spots and is it a sign of some mismatch?

I also have bunions on both my feet since childhood.

Today I dont work in the finance industry anymore. Working with people, natural movement and Manual Therapy (Feldenkrais).

This year im turning 37 years old. I have been reducing blue light from screens, indoor lightning etc and felt a small difference. I have come to realize that I wont be able to be optimal living here and decided to try out a move to El Salvador in December this year.

### Richard Sköld — 2025-04-01T01:34:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/richards-optimal-journal.30607/post-349803>

Today

Been in el salvador now for almost 5 months, spending my days watching the sunrise been out almost the whole day during sun is out. Just a small break between 2-4 and then back at the sunset.

Blocking all lights at night. Bed time at 8pm and getting up around 4-5am depending on my body.

Done measurement of RF in the areas that I spend time sunbathing and also in my sleeping area to maximize my benefits here and thats been good measurements.

Had some wins while Im here.

Think my raynauds went away (been trying out ice bath without any sign of it) and seems like I have got some new or added pigment on my hands that I did not have before.

Fungus on my hands went away.
Right foot still some fungus issues under the foot.

Bunions is starting to heal and go away

My voice sounds different and Im not so tired after speaking as I was before coming here.

Coming here got me also thinking that I might have had some gut issues connected with my autoimmune but this seems to have improved, feel less tight in my abdomen so something has shifted.

Still dont feel a big difference regarding memory so wonder if I need to do things differently, staying longer or added things?

Did some labs this weekend and would love some kind of feedback.

Vitamin D 100,8 ng/mL

HS crp 0,17 mg/L

Periferal Blood smear:

Red Blood cells: Notormocytic with mild hypocromy.

\* This means that red blood cells are normal (normocytic) in size, but have a slight deficiency of haemoglobin (hypochromy). Haemoglobin is the protein that carries oxygen in red blood cells, so hypocromy may indicate possible anaemia.

\* White blood cell: It is observed: Mild leucopenia with normal cell maturation, without any other particularity.

\* This indicates that there is a slight decrease in the number of white blood cells (leukopenia). White blood cells are part of the immune system and help fight infections. Normal cell maturation means that the white cells that are present are normally developed.

\*platelet: It is observed: Normal in number, size and shape.

Dont really understand what this tells me?

Liver Panel
See attached file

Kidney Panel

See attached file

What I could tell is my Vitamin D is very good.

Low inflammation from the HS crp.

the Bun/Cre ratio is high approx 16 , is telling me Im not making mitochondria water good enough? Have struggled with hydration here seeing as Im outdoors all the day. Have added some days salt to my water and trying to eat more oysters and bone broth.

The other ones I dont know how to interpret

# Thread: KIDS SKIN DISEASES ARE A PROXY FOR nnEMF RISK: MCAS
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-skin-diseases-are-a-proxy-for-nnemf-risk-mcas.30609/>

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-01T06:38:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-skin-diseases-are-a-proxy-for-nnemf-risk-mcas.30609/post-349826>

Why?

Remember that the skin and brain are linked to the neuroectodermal layer in the embryo. So what happens on the skin tells the clinician what is likely happening the in the brain: Warburg shift. Kids have reduced myelin so they are very easily pushed into this shift. Moreover, nnEMF in their environment opens every barrier in them. Skin, gut, brain, retina, gonads....etc. This makes them susceptible to many diseases when they are placed in environments that dehydrate them, cause hypoxia, and make them not tolerate the TCA cycle = oxygen allergy. This is why many of these kids have slowed down cognition as well.

Mast cells in the skin function as a critical bridge between the immune system and the nervous system, both directly by abutting nerve cells and indirectly (by releasing light sensitive biochemicals that affect the nervous system in neuroectodermal derivatives. We call them biophotons.

Mast cells reside in every tissue of the body, particularly those tissues that come into contact with the outside world, like the skin. This makes them at risk for topological changes due to nnEMF. Mast cells have a distinct predilection for communicating and associating with nerve cells in neuroectodermal tissues.

Sunlight and darkness control the extensive “cross-talk” communication pathways done via bio-photon signatures between mast cells and nerve cells under normal circumstances. When these go awry in the skin pigmentation issues, atopy, eczema, roscea, and many MCAS manifest. The symptoms are all due to electromagnetic pollution. This stimulus is the largest new unknown pathogen driving many diseases in our young patients addicted to technology gear. You might be shocked to find out mast cells are very sensitive to nnEMF because of the paramagnetic atoms they contain and they act like antennas to draw nnEMF to them. This causes altered physiology and phenotypical diseases.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-27T02:15:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kids-skin-diseases-are-a-proxy-for-nnemf-risk-mcas.30609/post-351358>

MCAS = Oxygen Holocaust at the mtDNA level.

Many people who develop MCAS will have been jaundiced at birth.

**Neonatal Jaundice: Blue Light Therapy is a Human Disease Incubator**

Neonatal jaundice treatment shifted from full-spectrum light to blue light (425–475 nm) in the last 35 years, ignoring quantum biology. Centralized medicine, blind to neuropsin (UVA receptor) and melanopsin (435–465 nm), assumed blue light was safe for bilirubin photoisomerization (Oláh et al., 2013). Infants, with translucent skin and underdeveloped skeletons, allow deep blue light penetration, reflecting off bone to stimulate melanocytes. Long-term studies reveal increased dysplastic nevi in these children, a melanoma risk factor (Oláh et al., 2013).

Blue light’s mitotic effect mirrors uveal melanoma (UM) studies: human UM cells exposed to blue light (475 nm) show increased proliferation, an effect blocked by blue-light filters (Hu et al., 2014). In neonates, blue light decouples POMC signaling, overstimulating alpha-MSH and melanogenesis without UV’s protective feedback.

This drives mitochondrial heteroplasmy in melanocytes, as blue light suppresses cytochrome c oxidase, increasing ROS and mtDNA mutations (Godley et al., 2005). Mothers with circadian mismatches often from blue light exposure pass higher heteroplasmy rates to infants, amplifying the risk (Wallace, 2010).

This is how most childhood diseases transfer in the modern world. Light is the first injury children sustain in the germline that eventually becomes their body. They come into their lives with a higher heteroplasmy rate and this puts them closer to disease and death. Lowering their heteroplasmy post natally should be the goal fo the parents who harmed their germlines prenatally. Today's 50% infertility rate in zip codes is Nature's warning that maybe most of the world is not fit to be parents due to the light stress they allow.

In women with PCOS, endometriosis, and men with sperm motility issues due to blue light stress infertility rates are quoted between 30% to 50% in the infertility literature. Additionally, older women may have even higher infertility rates with lower chances of conception each cycle. Younger women on BCPs over 15 years are also experience epic infertility rates. The data is everywhere.

# Thread: WHAT PARENTS NEED TO KNOW AS SOON AS THEY ARE PREGNANT.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-parents-need-to-know-as-soon-as-they-are-pregnant.30612/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Kids
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-01T18:18:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-parents-need-to-know-as-soon-as-they-are-pregnant.30612/post-349858>

**The Fluidity of Life and the Power of Choices**

Mitochondriac philosophy is rooted in a simple yet profound truth: Your current situation is never permanent. It is what you make of it from your choices. To the mitochondriac, life is not a solid monolith but a fluid, ever-changing stream. As a mitochondriac, he was obsessed with the cellular powerhouses that fuel life; he saw parallels between the adaptability of mitochondria and the resilience of the human soul. "If you are a stranger in your forest," he warned, "you must change something immediately."
He lived this ethos daily. Ideation without execution leads to deleting all good ideas, which he scribbled in his journal one March 31st. For him, execution trumped debate; a debate is masturbation for procrastination, and wasting time was an affront to life’s fleeting essence. His mission to decentralize the world wasn’t just a cause; it was about people. "We must be about people, not causes," he insisted. "This starts with an idea and a conversation, but it must be realized through risk, sacrifice, and endurance." His actions were slowly building decentralized networks for healthcare, education, and energy, and he became the best advocate for his vision—my Ode to Dr. Robert O. Becker.

What happens when fetal DNA fragments from vaccines integrate into a baby’s genome? No one knows—and no one’s held accountable.
WI-38 and MRC-5 fetal cell lines (from aborted fetuses) are still being used in vaccine production in 2025. This integration issue is more likely to happen when the child has fetal RBCs in their system. It takes about 2 years for a baby to switch to adult hemoglobin completely. Because of this, I tell all my new parents they should avoid anything that can integrate during this time. This is all related to what Becker found in his regeneration experiments in the 1960s. [https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-39-124931390](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-39-124931390)

# Thread: debbie's optimal journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/debbies-optimal-journal.30615/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Debbie Van Till — 2025-04-01T23:24:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/debbies-optimal-journal.30615/post-349880>

Recently, my husband and I have come to the realization that we don’t have to keep doing the same thing, we could close or sell our business. We could consider changing the course of our lives. So, in this state of mind, I begin my journal.

1955—Born jaundiced, my mother smoked, she worked as a nurse in, southern Calif.

\`1960- at 5 years old- tonsils out, had lots of ear aches

15 years old- braces, but they found that two of my adult teeth didn’t come in, still have one baby tooth

1986-1989 moved to northern Calif

married, had 3 healthy babies, born naturally
4 miscarriages- 4, 5 and 6 months

Until 2012. – had headaches approx. every other month until menopause at 57

2001 moved to rural Kansas City, MO

2005 stressed, doctor said had an extra heart beat, , I didn’t take the drug

2021 husband, Cliff had a stroke and I had a lot more stress and responsibility

This is when I started to notice heart palpitations when resting.
Going to Naturopath and he treated for lymes in bundle branch, adrenal fatigue, and thinks heart palpitations could be due to stress or low blood sugar. Gave me lots of supplements.But, I have lots of energy – Naturopath says “I get in the car at 50 miles/hour where most people get in at 0”

2020 Gums receding, eating grass fed, grass base milk and yogurt, local eggs. Follow Westin Price- factor X butter/cod liver oil

2022 Covid, used ivermectin, lots of supplements,

Jan 2024. Ended up in E.R. Cut the tip off of my index finger and they gave me a tetanus shot.

2025\. Naturopath had me get a 12 hour fasting blood test and had a few high results but the Dr. was only concerned with the high testosterone levels and put me on 50 mg DHEA 2 x/day and lots of other supplements.
Still, heart palpitations. Qigong can help.
No Jab, no one around us that did either.

For about 10 years I haven’t used any sunscreen, last 3-5 years get in the sun in the morning doing my work on the farm without a hat, but have put long sleeve and hat during 10 am-2 pm hours, no sunglasses. Not now, I am sleeveless when I can.

Blue light blockers most of the time in the last 5 years, but seriously all the time last 3 weeks since joining.
No phone to head, hold it in my hand with a shield blocker and wi—fi is turned off at night.
5G came here probably in last 2-3 years. Used a Gauss meter and TriField EMF Meter and everything in our house and on farm is very low levels. But, these meters aren’t the same as the ones Anthony Smith shows. Will they do the same tests?

Have been listening to Q & A webinars and podcasts in the car when driving. Think I heard Dr. Kruse say that wasn’t good. Have stopped.
For 3 weeks we have been watching the sunrise for 3-20 minutes and sunset some.
On days when it is cool/warm I am shedding clothes to be in the sun. Even noticed a tan line
On my stomach today from tanning Sunday!
Stopped taking melatonin and sleeping better.

My goal is optimal health, without taking 10-15 supplements 2x/ day. Am pretty sure I heard Dr. Kruse say it is possible to have optimal health without supplements. This is totally new to me, but so much what we want!

I am in. What do I need to change? Working on getting the stress out and starting to make decisions not out of obligation but because it brings me joy. I realize this alone is a challenge and can be stressful. I believe when I live a life of joy, I can bring that to others around me.

What is my next step?

-   Continue to declutter life.
-   Ask the question, does it give me joy?
-   Do I keep it out of obligation?
-   Get more sun
-   Read and listen to understand this new language of a mitochondriac.
-   Not be afraid to discontinue the supplements as I improve
-   Get a consult with Dr. Kruse.

I am open to hearing what I am doing right and can improve and stop or change.

Looking forward to this adventure.

# Thread: Bennes optimal journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bennes-optimal-journal.30617/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### bennefit-coaching — 2025-04-02T00:18:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bennes-optimal-journal.30617/post-349886>

**Benne Wichmann (2nd of April 1987)**

I love every detail of my life which I mentioned here. I had a wonderful life so far. Here I list mostly the aspects that are negatively connected to my health. There was also a lot of fun and definitely lots of learning in many of the unhealthy aspects.
The older I got, the more **I embraced the suck**, and I will continue to do so!

It was not so easy to structure the journal – I gave my best.
Further down you find a **summary** of my health challenges and also some information about my female relatives.
I am very grateful for your feedback!
Benne ☀️

### Birth and early years​

I was born on the 2nd of April 1987 in the night at 0.20 am in an hospital on the countryside in Cloppenburg, Germany. It was a regular birth, my weight was 8kg.
I stayed with my mom for two nights in the hospital. I was breastfed for 3 months.

In my early years (early 90ies), I was raised on a farm. We didn’t have direct neighbors (100m away).
The nnEMF pollution was still not as bad as today.
But I don’t know about the wiring in our house. We had TVs, wired Internet in the early 2000s and I had my first mobile phone with 13/14 years.

### Vaccines​

My mom found a document which said I got vaccines for **Diphtheria and Polio** (2 times minimum).
She thinks I got also vaccines for measles, rubella, chickenpox.
Later on I got vaccines for Hepatitis, Tetanus, and more (I lost my vaccine passport).

When I was 4 years old i got **infected with scarlet** and was very weak.

### Meningitis​

When I was 10/11 I got a Meningitis. I stayed in the hospital for several weeks and was very weak. I remember to have immense headaches and was not able to walk without support. They did a **lumbar puncture** on my spinal cord without anesthesia, and it hurt a lot. I remember to be nervous about that.

I felt very lonely there in hospital. It was around New Year's Eve. I could see and hear the fireworks and was alone in the hospital. I was visited on the days, but still I was wondering how my parents could leave me there alone. This time often comes up in my memories.

### Emotional stress​

My parents divorced when I was 12/13. I felt sad and **ashamed** (in front of others), that we didn’t have a perfect family anymore.
Since my early childhood, I generally never talked about my fears or sadness. So there **was lots of stress accumulating in my body of the last decades**. I now realize that I mostly restricted my emotions (anger, fear, grief).

### Drugs​

With the age of 13 I was drunk **(alcohol**) the first time. With the age of 15 I was drunk regularly on the weekends, sometimes in delirium. Almost every weekend until the age of 33 I was drunk at least once.
I smoked **cigarettes** from the age of 15/16 until I was 34. I didn’t smoke daily. On average maybe a package a week and another package on the weekend while partying.

With the age of 16 I started to smoke **weed.** I did it very regularly, in some periods daily, the most of the time more or less 1-2 times a week until the age of 28.

With the age of 27 I took my first **ecstasy** pill. When I moved to Berlin with 30 years (2017) I started to took **ecstasy/MDMA** very regularly (once/twice a month), also **speed** and sometimes **cocaine**. I still was **awake often in the night** but instead for video games more for bars and techno parties (with crazy lighting). The partying slowly stopped in the years of 2020-2023.

**Pain and inflammation**
I started to play soccer with the age of 5 (on real grass) and did it since I was 33 (in the last ten years it was almost just on artificial grass). I had lots of **injuries** already in my youth and they were always treated with some anti-inflammatory medicine (cortison). In my twenties, I also got some injections against some pain in my toes and in my back.
I had periods of acute back pain since my youth (maybe after the meningitis?). With my aging it got more regularly. With 32 years, my **back pain became chronic**, and it's there til today.
I just realized some weeks ago that Wi-Fi (after using it the first time after some months here in El Salvador) creates a certain pain in my back. Before, I always connected the pain to bad posture and sitting.

### Technology​

With the age of 8 I got my first Gameboy. With 12 I had a Nintendo for the TV. Since then, I was playing a lot of video games. Later on (starting with 16/17) also playing **video games in the night.**
We had TVs and I had my first mobile phone with 13/14 years.
I always liked to **watch TV.** Especially soccer matches and movies, which are usually happening when it's already dark.
While watching TV, I ate crackers and Chocolate. I love(d) ice cream and sweets in general. In my college time /in my twenties, I ate almost entirely **processed food.**

When I was 18/19 I got **mononucleosis**. I was very weak for a month or so. Why do I always get these **weird diseases**?

### University years​

With 21 years I went from the countryside to a small town (Münster) where I studied sports science (2008-2016) to become a teacher in school. Most of the classes were inside, but at least some were outside. I was still drinking and **partying a lot in this period, staying out late.** I didn’t feel to become a teacher, but I didn’t know what else to do, and I was **afraid** to quit it. Furthermore, I also was afraid to even admit, that I **was unhappy** about my path.
Also, my regular soccer practice in that period was often after sunset. I **couldn’t sleep well** after an intense training from 7 to 9pm. We have often drunk beer afterward.

### Psoriasis (2017)​

I was diagnosed with Psoriasis on my scalp after some months in **Berlin 2017** with 30 years. After some months of cortisol-therapy, I wanted to find out why this whole disease came up. I had no clue about what pain is teaching us, I always thought pain needs to aways as quickly as possible to be healthy.
I went to some alternative medicine guys, changed my diet and tried to learn more. The typical functional medicine route. Listening to Max Gotzlar - our German biohacker, and took more and more supplements. I felt better in some ways, but the psoriasis never went away.
At least I learnt also about the importance of light, sleep and the problems with blue light. I started to make sleep a priority and was very fixated on a clean diet (which was also very stressful).
But just when I found Jack Kruse in 2023 I knew, that I needed to do more.

Unfortunately, I found him after I took one **Pfizer Covid vaccine in 2020.**

### El Salvador (2024…)​

Now (March 2025) I am in Atami, El Salvador (since October 2024) trying to get rid of the psoriasis. I still **work on my computer** a lot (4-5 hours a day), since I am doing an online health coaching. I am always outside though (in kiniki), always grounded and with Ethernet.
I have at least **1-3 hours of beach time** every day.
I try to avoid cars and shopping trips as good as I can (from my view, I do them way less than other Mitos).
I go to bed early at 7 pm and try to sleep by 8pm. It is mostly dark in my house and room after sunset and before sunrise.
Sometimes we have some low intensity red-light on but that's it.
We just use Ethernet and our house is EMF-approved by EMF-expert Olle.
I eat **seafood almost every day** (oysters 1-2 a week), meat every day and my digestion got already I bit better I think.
When I consume more than a little of dairy, gluten, avocados or coffee, my **brain feels still off.** So I don’t do that very often.
My psoriasis is still there. Especially when I had one or two bad nights of sleep/stress, I am scratching more skin of my head. It is never bleeding, just layers of skin fall off.

### Summary: Symptoms/diseases I have/had​

-   Scarlet (4 years)
-   Meningitis (10 years, lumbar punction with anesthesia)
-   Mononucleosis (18 years)
-   Surgery with anesthesia on my elbow (20 years)
-   Reynaud Syndrome (went away, since I stopped drinking coffee)
-   lack of concentration (got way better, since I’m following nature)
-   my intelligence always felt like a rollercoaster. Sometimes I felt smart, sometimes stupid
-   little anxiety (especially in the morning, until today)
-   Depressive episodes (no more since I follow nature)
-   Chronic back pain since 2016
-   Psoriasis since 2017/18
-   Long Covid Symptoms in 2020 – just after the infection, not after the one vaccine
-   COVID Vaccine 2020 PFIZER
-   Can’t tolerate milk and gluten so well (brain foggy, sometimes bad mood the next day). Also, legumes make my digestion worse.
-   My digestion was not as good as it should be, at least for the last 10 years (Bristol stool scale: often Type 5 or 6)
-   my testosterone feels way lower than in the beginning of my 30s (where it felt very high)

### My relatives:​

-   My grandma (from my mother side) lived from 1927 to 2021 She died with 92 years. Her brain worked pretty well until the end. She was overweight. She never moved her body much in her life, but good food and learning (doing riddles) was important to her. She loved to travel and learn new things. In her last ten years, she stayed mainly inside. There would have been some more years possible for her, I guess.
-   My mom was born in 1956 in Frankfurt am Main. She grew up in the city and moved to the countryside, where she met my dad in her twenties. She got pregnant with 29 years. The pregnancy was uncomplicated. She told me, that she was very stressed in my childhood.
    She also **got polio vaccines.**
    She has and always **had vision problems.** **My vision always has been good.** She reads a lot, worked on the Computer for her work as teacher and watches TV.
-   My grandma (from my dads side) grew up on the countryside and died in 1999 with 78 years. She had diabetes type 2. She always loved to eat sugar and also treated her kids and us grandkids with sweets. She wore glasses and watched TV as far as I remember. She was inside for cooking and doing the household, but she also helped with work outside and in the garden on the farm.

### Questions:​

-   What can I **expect for recovery time**? How long does it take? Is there anybody who completely reversed his psoriasis to whom I can talk to? I heard, some people really saw their patches on the skin go away. For me, it's hard to tell, because they are under my hair. But it doesn’t feel like, it is getting less.
-   **Do I miss something** to get rid of the psoriasis? Is there anything else how I can **speed up** my recovery?
-   How bad is it to walk next to the power lines to the beach? E.G. Does it make sense to go to the beach (10min each way, 20min in total) and spent just 30 Minutes at the beach?
-   While writing this: Have I always been sick? How exactly do the vaccines I got as a child effected me over the years?
-   Is there any **information missing** in the journal?

### bennefit-coaching — 2025-05-03T20:10:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bennes-optimal-journal.30617/post-351846>

Update:
I didn't mention that 4 teeth next to my wisdom teeth got removed to create more space for my wisdom teeth. I heard Jack talking about the connection of the teeth to Psoriasis. I once went to a alternative dentist last year. He said because of that removal, I will forever have digestive issues. And he got this from Traditional Chinese Medicine.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-03T20:19:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/bennes-optimal-journal.30617/post-351849>

LOL. Dysbiosis always come from bad light.

**Posts:** 4

### michellejorge — 2025-04-02T01:14:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-recommendations-for-summer-stay-in-el-salvador.30618/post-349894>

Hello! I am thinking of where to go to do some healing this summer for about a month (end of May- June). Of course, El Salvador is on the top of my list! I'll be with my 12 year old son so I was hoping he can have fun and it be vacation-like for us both. When we went to Costa Rica he enjoyed some surf lessons and we also spent time in a hotel by the Arenal, so I guess I do like a resort vibe but is there a mito friendly EMF friendly way to do this? If no, any Airbnb recomendations? While I want to heal, I want he and I to enjoy the summer too. It would be great too if I was not tied to the kitchen so there is a place
we can walk to for some meals. Also, I don't adjust easily to driving in new enviorments. Is driving complicated? These are just wish list things that may not exist in a mito friendly place but I figured I would ask and start there. Any tips or recommendations? Thank you!!!

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-04-12T21:46:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-recommendations-for-summer-stay-in-el-salvador.30618/post-350550>

If you're an experienced driver ,just remember these are mostly single carriage way roads , lots of people overtake, and the trucks and big container lorries will hum along at 30km an hour on the inclines etc. You'll pass convoys of traffic held up by those.

Other than that you need to keep your eyes and ears wide open to see everything. The little bike that's turning, the motorbike that just overtook you etc

The car coming out because it's seen a space. ... Drive defensively and carefully. I drive regularly on the roads. I am used to driving on the left but I've been driving for almost 39 years so I just basically flip it for left hand drive and right side driving. Mirrors signals and blue blockers ( daytime versions to drive with ).

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-12T23:01:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-recommendations-for-summer-stay-in-el-salvador.30618/post-350563>

I think driving here is a dream. Not difficult at all.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-04-12T23:36:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/seeking-recommendations-for-summer-stay-in-el-salvador.30618/post-350569>

If you can drive in Miami - ES will be easy.

### Base_Strength — 2025-04-02T19:48:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-uv-contacts-or-uv-glasses.30621/post-349991>

I switched my contact lenses from ones with UV protection to ones without, but I haven’t found glasses yet that don’t have a UV coating. I’m guessing glasses with no UV coating would be ideal since light and oxygen can still come in through the sides. This is only for when I have to wear them for work. I take them off for 2-3 hours a day to get unfiltered sunlight outside. Thoughts?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-02T19:52:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/non-uv-contacts-or-uv-glasses.30621/post-349992>

>
> I switched my contact lenses from ones with UV protection to ones without, but I haven’t found glasses yet that don’t have a UV coating. I’m guessing glasses with no UV coating would be ideal since light and oxygen can still come in through the sides. This is only for when I have to wear them for work. I take them off for 2-3 hours a day to get unfiltered sunlight outside. Thoughts?

There is also the hypoxic element you need to consider while wearing contacts. From my understanding even contact lenses that advertise no UV blocking still blocks a certain percentage of UV just based on having an artificial lens over the eye. I buy my glasses from eyebuydirect. you can customize the lens so no UV coating is applied. Plus you can get them for a fraction of what retailers sell for. I stick to plastic to steer clear of any potential jump conduction.
