# Thread: Ivun's Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ivuns-journal.30625/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 12
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### IvunS — 2025-04-03T23:28:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ivuns-journal.30625/post-350032>

It seems like this is the place to start my journal so I will proceed. If this is not the place any feedback is welcome.
I live in Bluffdale Utah, I did not nor do I like Mitt Romney. I grew up usual, public school, vaccines, but we didn't really go to the doctor. My family raised our own beef and pork. my mom made all out meals from scratch until i turned 6, then the story changed.

My profession now is we own an agency that deals with Health insurance polices and Medicare transitions. I was in financial planning for a few years and after reading the creature from Jekyll island and connecting some dots I left that part of the career behind. I have watched the health of my clients decline over the years. i have watched infertility rise, most clients over 36 are now on HRT, kids have anxiety....a gigantic cluster F has shown up before my eyes over the last 22 years. When I heard Dr Kruse he was saying what I have believed for the last 2 decades. What a mess.

My maternal grandmother passed away at the age of 69 from uterine cancer and I don't think she really had any other health problems before that. My mother is 75, very active, lots of energy and happy, despite caring for my dad who was diagnoses with multiple myeloma 6 years ago. She does not take any medications and does not have any known health problems. Neither her or my dad took the jab. She was diagnosed with cervical cancer at 54 and had a total hysterectomy with success. No chemo, radiation or other treatment. My mother and grandmother have/did not have other health problems or take medications.

I have lived a very decentralized life for the past 15 years. Home school with kids, Automating money in my life to stay out of the "corporate churches" of business. I have owned bitcoin for quite a while, out last child was born at home, no vaccines, we use doctors for emergencies, our granddaughter was born on the side of the road august of 2023 and is not vaccinated so the tradition is carrying on.
I have struggled to feel good for the last 26 years. I lived in Korea as a missionary from 1996 to 1998 and after living there for 4 months, felt better than I ever had. As soon as I came home at the age of 21 the brain fog, fatigue, anxiety, low patience all set in. I weighed 160 pounds, and I'm 5'11. I struggled with weight gain and losing weight. I have tried, GAPS, keto, carnivore, epi paleo...etc. Exercise for the most part has made me more tired than when I didn't do it recently. Walking is ok and I do ride dirt bikes with my kids in the desert in my kiniki.

My family and I did not take the jab. Starting in 2020, my otherwise healthy last two sons ages 17,and 14, started to get sick with headaches, fatigue and body aches for 1 week of every month from September to April. In October of last year the cycle started again. I put my youngest son on a whole food diet. no processed food. He started doing better. Then started pulling him from school during the highest UV part of the day to be in the sun for 90 minutes or so. We had a very mild start to winter and both of us were able to get some tan. For the last 15 years I have not used sunscreen, I started getting in the sun more the last 3 years, no sunglasses for 2 years, no air pods since June. I do not believe normal evolutionary exposure to the sun is bad for me in any way. From 2000-2003 I was a pilot and as soon I was hired by an airline, what I now know to probably be radar and nnEMF sickness made it so that I could not stop obsessing about crashing a plane and killing people so I quit flying and staring selling life insurance.

In the fall of last year, I still suffered with terrible anxiety, a cough that showed up two years ago that would not go away, waking up at 2-5 am sweating and obsessing, inability to function in my business for several years. I set it up for reoccurring revenue though so I had the flexibility to focus on my health. I have always felt better not being in my house that we have lived in for the last 11 years. I weigh 215 or so and struggle to drop weight. Anytime I do keto or fast I struggle to sleep and get grumpy. My brother sent my the Danny Jones podcast on November 7th. 4 days later I started making huge changes that have helped almost all of my struggles.

Over the last few months since joining I have change my environment. I use my cell phone more carefully, moved my computer/office to the garage so I can sit in the sun while I work, changed light use after dark, CT in my bathtub, a 3 week trip to El Salvador in February with my wife, two sons and one of their friends to get as tan as possible, think chocolate cake or chocolate princess. Within 3 days of being at our place in Lake Ilopango, my anxiety became manageable and my older son who is on the spectrum a bit, was able to start managing his emotions much better as well. The youngest was along for the ride and has not been sick since we started in October and still has his tan from the trip. We spent 11 days at the crater, watching sunrise/sunset eating great food and then we spent 8 days in a beach house in costa del sol. I had my meters from Anthony Smith and the owners of each house we very accommodating of my requests to turn off breakers and Wi-Fi/ lights after dark etc. My son and I slept without AC or outside the entire time because the EMF from the AC was too uncomfortable. The trip to the sun sold us completely on what I already felt like I suspected. More sun, more darkness and issues with nnEMF. My wife also had many things change that she has been dealing with for years, cracked heals, rough skin patches on her face the went away, more patience in dealing with the changes we have made that are a bit inconvenient just to mention a couple. Since the trip I don't have anxiety at all, My motivation has returned, I sleep great, I felt the power of the sun and my family is converted for sure. We are just looking to chat with Dr Kruse to make sure we are on the right track. I just looked today and airfare is only $334 from Salt lake so a trip to El Salvador is in the works. Listening and studying has been more or less my full time job. We implement as quickly as we can and have already made some mistakes that we corrected as soon as we found out. (grounding sheets.....). Both of our boys have been pulled from school and we are working that all out.

I have listened to all the Q&As back to 2022 and read many of the blogs and listened to most of the podcasts. Neither my wife or I have a current diagnosis of anything and we do not take any medications. I sleep better when I eat carbs and I heard Dr Kruse tell a lady on one of the calls that the need to eat carbs before bed for her to sleep well was a concern for him. I wonder if that applies to me?

I have a couple of lingering issues that I would like to work out.
1\. Doing the epi paleo diet has still been a huge struggle so I am continuing to search and learn about why that may be. (Maybe my stress is still high and my redox low so a few more weeks in El Salvador could be the answer? or just muscle through it? i turn into a bit of a dick.)
2\. My wife and I are figuring out what to do about where we live and making sure that we don't jump out of the frying pan into the fire and get into a worse nnEMF situation than we are in now. One of our sons graduates high school this year and our youngest starts 9th grade and is flexible.
3\. My wife is probably in the beginnings of menopause and struggles with bloating at certain times of her cycle and has struggled with weight loss most of her life. Our daughter is in nursing school and really wants her to get blood work done and all that, but we do not want to jump on the HRT/GLP1 wagon. I think she may need more personalized information.

We love to study and know that following the mainstream is the path to the sausage grinder. We have been pissing in the wind with the lack of sunlight and are feeling improvement at the same time now that we have been to El Salvador.

We have a cell tower 1200 feet from our house and I have realized that sleeping in our room on the second floor has been detrimental to my health. The RF readings in our bedroom are 0.4-2500 mv/m all the time. I can shut breakers off to get the body voltage to 0.020, but the RF from the tower, even with some shielding still sucks so I sleep in the basement under a sleep canopy I made from reflective insulation and my wife still sleeps in our room. We did shield a wall that cuts the rf in half but I'm not sure that she believes it is a problem. According to Anthony the reading is too high for a sleeping space. I sleep like a baby now in my hut but I am afraid we need to move.

My wife Heidi's maternal history:
Her mom is 72 and has migraines and other health issues for many years. Mild OCD, constant worry etc. She had a cancer spot removed from her intestines a few years ago, she has a couple fused vertebrae in her back. Heidi's parents are very tight lipped about their health and i think she has something major she is dealing with now but they are not talking to the family about it. I think it may be neurological but I don't know, Parkinson's? Heidi's maternal grandmother suffered a back issue when she was 40. The surgery went south and she suffered paralysis for a while and chronic pain and other bowel issues for the rest of her life. She passed at 83 from a UTI and dementia was setting in. She had to use a catheter after her botched back surgery for the rest of her life. She did have breast cancer that was resolved with surgery in her 60s.
We have a consult scheduled for Sunday the 6th to talk about the best way for us to proceed and also for Heidi to take care of herself.

>
> My profession now is we own an agency that deals with Health insurance polices and Medicare transitions. >
> My maternal grandmother passed away at the age of 69 from uterine cancer and I don't think she really had any other health problems before that. My mother is 75, very active, lots of energy and happy, despite caring for my dad who was diagnoses with multiple myeloma 6 years ago. She does not take any medications and does not have any known health problems. Neither her or my dad took the jab. She was diagnosed with cervical cancer at 54 and had a total hysterectomy with success. No chemo, radiation or other treatment. My mother and grandmother have/did not have other health problems or take medications.
>
> I have lived a very decentralized life for the past 15 years. Home school with kids, Automating money in my life to stay out of the "corporate churches" of business. I have owned bitcoin for quite a while, out last child was born at home, no vaccines, we use doctors for emergencies, our granddaughter was born on the side of the road august of 2023 and is not vaccinated so the tradition is carrying on.
> I have struggled to feel good for the last 26 years. I lived in Korea as a missionary from 1996 to 1998 and after living there for 4 months, felt better than I ever had. As soon as I came home at the age of 21 the brain fog, fatigue, anxiety, low patience all set in. I weighed 160 pounds, and I'm 5'11. I struggled with weight gain and losing weight. I have tried, GAPS, keto, carnivore, epi paleo...etc. Exercise for the most part has made me more tired than when I didn't do it recently. Walking is ok and I do ride dirt bikes with my kids in the desert in my kiniki.
>
> My family and I did not take the jab. Starting in 2020, my otherwise healthy last two sons ages 17,and 14, started to get sick with headaches, fatigue and body aches for 1 week of every month from September to April. In October of last year the cycle started again. I put my youngest son on a whole food diet. no processed food. He started doing better. Then started pulling him from school during the highest UV part of the day to be in the sun for 90 minutes or so. We had a very mild start to winter and both of us were able to get some tan. For the last 15 years I have not used sunscreen, I started getting in the sun more the last 3 years, no sunglasses for 2 years, no air pods since June. I do not believe normal evolutionary exposure to the sun is bad for me in any way. From 2000-2003 I was a pilot and as soon I was hired by an airline, what I now know to probably be radar and nnEMF sickness made it so that I could not stop obsessing about crashing a plane and killing people so I quit flying and staring selling life insurance.
>
> In the fall of last year, I still suffered with terrible anxiety, a cough that showed up two years ago that would not go away, waking up at 2-5 am sweating and obsessing, inability to function in my business for several years. I set it up for reoccurring revenue though so I had the flexibility to focus on my health. I have always felt better not being in my house that we have lived in for the last 11 years. I weigh 215 or so and struggle to drop weight. Anytime I do keto or fast I struggle to sleep and get grumpy. My brother sent my the Danny Jones podcast on November 7th. 4 days later I started making huge changes that have helped almost all of my struggles.
>
> Over the last few months since joining I have change my environment. I use my cell phone more carefully, moved my computer/office to the garage so I can sit in the sun while I work, changed light use after dark, CT in my bathtub, a 3 week trip to El Salvador in February with my wife, two sons and one of their friends to get as tan as possible, think chocolate cake or chocolate princess. Within 3 days of being at our place in Lake Ilopango, my anxiety became manageable and my older son who is on the spectrum a bit, was able to start managing his emotions much better as well. The youngest was along for the ride and has not been sick since we started in October and still has his tan from the trip. We spent 11 days at the crater, watching sunrise/sunset eating great food and then we spent 8 days in a beach house in costa del sol. I had my meters from Anthony Smith and the owners of each house we very accommodating of my requests to turn off breakers and Wi-Fi/ lights after dark etc. My son and I slept without AC or outside the entire time because the EMF from the AC was too uncomfortable. The trip to the sun sold us completely on what I already felt like I suspected. More sun, more darkness and issues with nnEMF. My wife also had many things change that she has been dealing with for years, cracked heals, rough skin patches on her face the went away, more patience in dealing with the changes we have made that are a bit inconvenient just to mention a couple. ### billytheadult — 2025-04-04T07:55:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-savages-look-forward-to-learning-from-you.30626/post-350054>

I'm new here, just started to poke around and update my About Me.

I'm 45 y/o redhead in Brooklyn who came here to learn how to lower my Hypertension / blood pressure without meds, get deeper sleep, reverse my vitiligo, reverse my keratoconus, learn about longevity, become a savage and help others including my niece with autism 2 and having seizures and some worrisome bio-markers. And also to inform myself appropriately ahead of having a consult with Uncle Jack.

I've been an open minded, truth seeking conspiracy theorist my whole life. And hope I've found my tribe.

I know I have a lot to learn and am taking it one step at a time. (I KNOW I NEED TO MOVE)

Since discovering Uncle Jack in October '24, I've made an effort to...
\- See the (brooklyn) sunrise every morning, usually not until 7:30-8am currently
\- Take multiple walks without glasses (although I often wear a glass contact in left eye) with all the stupid e-bikes these days.
\- Take my shirt off in the backyard once a day when possible to build solar callus.
\- Wear blue-light blocking glasses after 8-9pm
\- Got rid of a repeated negative thought process concerning an old co-worker thanks to Joe Dispenza neuro plasticity videos and replaced with thoughts of gratitude
\- Now meditate every morning in front of an UV/IR panel about 8 inches from me
\- Eat more DHA rich foods. Eat salmon, canned tuna, sardines etc
\- End showers with cold water
\- My wife doesn't seem to be with me on this journey yet, but she bought all the ingredients for Survivor's Soup (for our anniversary) and we're making it tonight!
\- Moved my phone into another room at night instead of charging it by my freakin' head like I used to.
\- Worried how addicted to her phone my wife is
\- Do some grounding in the backyard or walk to the Brooklyn Botanic Gardens and go barefoot there, but I'm worried about jump conducting in my 5G location
\- Listened to the Saylor Series and have a deeper understanding of BTC and currently buying the dips
\- Become more irate at the turbo breast cancer / chemo that killed my mom a year ago
\- Use the word savage more

My highlight so far in this journey is going to El Salvador the week before starting a new job, not wearing sunglasses or sunscreen (pictured) and not burning or peeling for the first time in my life. And would consider buying land or a future home there.

My only frustrations are, while I FEEL better, more motivated etc. My deep sleep (often wake up around 3am) and my blood pressure have not improved, even with all those circadian changes.

Any advice or encouragement on how to proceed is appreciated!

Thanks Savages!

### WildForWesties — 2025-06-16T22:42:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-savages-look-forward-to-learning-from-you.30626/post-354726>

You are doing great so far. Your post is a couple of months old so I'll look for your updates. Fwiw, my advice is:

1\. Move. I can't emphasize enough how much difference it makes. I'm not in an ideal situation yet but it is light years ahead of where I was. In 2021 I moved from a condo in the densely populated tech corridor of the Wash DC area to a single family house in a new sprawling suburban neighborhood in North Texas. Still has lots of open land. My long list of chronic health problems are about 100x better now just from moving. A lower latitude, hotter/drier/sunnier climate, and lower population density has done more than the $100k+ I spent on functional medicine protocols the past 15 years. I moved here to care for my 96yo mother when my stepfather "died suddenly" and until she passes this is home.
2\. You have probably already done this, but I'll say it anyway. Until you can move, do every single thing possible to reduce nnEMF and RF in your environment. Ditch Hue bulbs, Sonos and hardwire everything, turn off bluetooth and LTE on your phone. Go old school and get devices down to the bare minimum. Phone and computer, maybe iPad. Stop wearables, wireless earbuds if you use them and anything else connected via wifi and bluetooth. This will help both sleep and BP.
3\. Keep doing Joe Dispenza meditations to heal and create a new future, along with adding HeartMath if you haven't already. Calming your autonomic nervous system and increasing HRV will help sleep and BP.

# Thread: Private E-mail and text app and video-call providers?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/private-e-mail-and-text-app-and-video-call-providers.30628/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 9
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### eve001 — 2025-04-04T10:55:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/private-e-mail-and-text-app-and-video-call-providers.30628/post-350063>

Hi people,

What communication software do you use over-all for privacy and data protection?

I am looking into e-mail providers doing end-to-end encryption. Like Protonmail and tuta.
Seems tuta does extra protection, of meta-data.
Seems more expensive also.

I don't mind paying, put monthly lifelong: not a business model I like. Bc I probably won't use all the options anyway.
I'd rather pay extra for extra space if needed.

And recommendation regarding text apps?
Why is everybody still using whatsapp?

And how about software for video-conferences respecting privacy?
Does it exist? Or is it too challenging to encrypt live call data?

Thanks.

### Adamo — 2025-04-04T16:58:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/private-e-mail-and-text-app-and-video-call-providers.30628/post-350074>

There are "better" email providers. But you have to consider whether or not they are adding extra security, as well as not selling or collecting data for certain purposes.

Email is likely one of the LEAST secure ways of communication in general, unless you are using some sort of customized service, running your own servers and protcols. _I am sure that there is a security-guru on these forums that can better speak to this._

Lots of people love Signal for messaging, but Telegram is also a great alternative to Whatsapp (Meta/Facebook). _People do not like changing their software and a lot of the world is already fixed on Whatsapp._

There are MANY different approaches to encrypting your communications, but if you are using centralized services (gmail, skype, zoom, etc.), how secure can it really be? Your device could also have other software pulling data and sharing it back to Google, Apple, Microsoft, etc.

VPNs are great, **USE ONE**. But everyone thinks that using a VPN is the end-all and be-all for protection. Your device has a built-in identification. Your ISP likely has your address, name, ID, and more data on you.

Is your router using TOR? What are all of your encryption points? Are you using sandbox environments or keeping certain actions (browsing, communicating, etc) within specific softwares and trying to limit the cross-contamination?

Digital security is a multi-facited thing and has been reduced down to a VPN and a secure email or app. The layers are so much deeper. Any real security person could likely penetrate most people's devices and comminications with relative ease. I am certain there are developers out there looking for block-chain version of these softwares and apps for a more secure and less-centralized ability.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-04T17:23:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/private-e-mail-and-text-app-and-video-call-providers.30628/post-350076>

Ironic this is posted on this site.

### Jeni — 2025-04-04T17:31:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/private-e-mail-and-text-app-and-video-call-providers.30628/post-350079>

### Jacks — 2025-04-04T21:33:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/private-e-mail-and-text-app-and-video-call-providers.30628/post-350109>

My bro who works in online security says all the security folks he knows use GPGtools to send encrypted emails and documents. It uses a public key and a private key. Free for PCs but costs a little bit for Macs. Not sure about any Linux OS. Have tried it for sending sensitive information to him a few times.

### GavinH — 2025-04-05T03:09:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/private-e-mail-and-text-app-and-video-call-providers.30628/post-350124>

Nostr and Keet are decentralised, uncensorable messaging applications. Not widely used yet but Nostr is growing. Keet in early stages. Nostr is actually a protocol so apps are built on top. Damus and Primal are good. Protonmail and tuta a good email clients. I lean towards Protonmail due to being bitcoin supporters.

### urko — 2025-12-09T17:52:55+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/private-e-mail-and-text-app-and-video-call-providers.30628/post-363728>

You can use xmpp protocol [https://xmpp.org/](https://xmpp.org/) with some client that has OMEMO encryption by default and use an instance of a trusted prosody server [https://prosody.im/](https://prosody.im/)
It also handles phone calls with video 1to1, for multi video calls I recommend jitsi [https://jitsi-club.gitlab.io/](https://jitsi-club.gitlab.io/)

All of them are decentralized open source, some free software, code that you can install like if it was your bitcoin node

### urko — 2025-12-09T17:56:24+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/private-e-mail-and-text-app-and-video-call-providers.30628/post-363729>

> [GavinH said:](/goto/post?id=350124)
>
> Nostr and Keet are decentralised, uncensorable messaging applications. Not widely used yet but Nostr is growing. Keet in early stages. Nostr is actually a protocol so apps are built on top. Damus and Primal are good. Protonmail and tuta a good email clients. I lean towards Protonmail due to being bitcoin supporters.

Protonmail is still not private [https://techcrunch.com/2021/09/06/p...ch-activist-after-order-by-swiss-authorities/](https://techcrunch.com/2021/09/06/protonmail-logged-ip-address-of-french-activist-after-order-by-swiss-authorities/) I would recommend to use an email server installed on njallah [https://njal.la/](https://njal.la/)

### urko — 2025-12-09T18:00:31+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/private-e-mail-and-text-app-and-video-call-providers.30628/post-363730>

But also you can be monitored by the hardware, so in the end there is not many way to escape from surveillance rather than building your own hardware and using your own networks  [https://www.fsf.org/blogs/sysadmin/the-management-engine-an-attack-on-computer-users-freedom](https://www.fsf.org/blogs/sysadmin/the-management-engine-an-attack-on-computer-users-freedom)

# Thread: Kidney stone and dehydration
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-stone-and-dehydration.30630/>
**Subforum:** Feedback/Suggestions
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### 0.66eV Eva — 2025-04-04T16:05:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-stone-and-dehydration.30630/post-350069>

I'm writing to ask for help with my mother's situation. She is 74 years old and does not take any medication. She went to the emergency room last Monday with excruciating abdominal and back pain on her right side (the pain had started abruptly during Sunday noon).

After an ultrasound, X-ray and low-dose CT scan without contrast injection, they found a stone in the ureter (2mm x 4mm). She was shaking from pain and dehydration.

They rehydrated her with salt water (about 1300 ml; I wish they'd given her more as she never drinks 2 liters a day) and gave her tylenol 650 mg twice during her ~9-hour stay in the ER.
They gave her toradol (Ketorolac) intravenously.

She has since returned home, and was prescribed naproxen with pantoloc (pantaprazole), tylenol and morphine, which I'm worried about.

She still has trouble drinking enough water, not even 1,500 ml a day.

I want to get her to eat fish, oysters and pasture-raised meat, get her outside, ground by the house, do infrared sessions with my little EMR-tek panel, put lemon in her water to get citrate, but she doesn't feel capable of those things.
I keep her away from her cell phone whenever possible and help her remember to use her Ra Optics glasses.
I've bought her some chanca piedra capsules, but she doesn't take enough either. She hasn't tried the magnesium citrate capsules yet.

I don't know where to put the emphasis to help her get better asap. What else can I do for her ?
Any help would be greatly appreciated, thank you.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-04T18:55:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-stone-and-dehydration.30630/post-350088>

Answered on the blogs. Intracellular dehydration is covered in detail in the last 3 blogs.

### 0.66eV Eva — 2025-04-04T19:07:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kidney-stone-and-dehydration.30630/post-350092>

Thank you Dr Kruse. Thank you for everything you do from the bottom of my heart. I'll read them today.

### sunres — 2025-04-04T18:40:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-journal-dr-help-needed.30632/post-350082>

35 yrs/
Had no real problems aware of.
Health was ok.

Before I lived a low stress decent life.

One day about 4 years ago I was robbed at gunpoint. The robber was extremely aggressive, even though I was complying It was also the way he was talking to me that got to me also. The way he was talking was with the most aggression and rage. At one point he tried to shoot but the gun malfunctioned.
I knew he wanted to shoot me in my head,,he wanted to see someone suffer. I could feel that but he didn't get to.
after the incident i started having thoughts, and guilt feelings, mainly because i knew i should have acted & done something at one point which could have saved my life (if gun did not jam) but i just did not. There were a few brief moments i knew i could have hit the gun away from my head before he tried to shoot, but i did not do anything.
That started to bother me as well as what happened.
I knew I could be dead right now, and failed to act properly.

There were nights i would wake up reliving this and angry that i did not act correctly and felt guilt ashamed.
A couple weeks after the incident my eyes got very blurry, i started to lose my vision, dr said it is cataracts. there is NO doubt i got cataracts from this incident, it has taken a toll on my mind and it is continuing.
I was hoping my vision would get better/ back to normal once time has passed but here i am 4 yrs later and vision is worse.
no matter how hard i have tried to get over this, i cannot, this is the first time in my life i can't control my thoughts, several times during every day and night for the last 4 years I relive this over and over its not stopping.

I've tried almost everything i can. I've tried relaxation techniques , and much more. Nothing works.

These are the worst days /years of my life. i can't stop thinking about that night. every night and day non stop. i'm more on guard.
Please help me. Tell me what if anything can be done.

the robber was never caught.

The piezoelectric limestone and sacred geometry amplify this quotient, linking human consciousness to the cosmos. As modern science rediscovers photonics’ role in biology, the Sphinx stands as a timeless guide, urging us to realign with natural light and reclaim the quantum efficiency of life.

# Thread: European moving to El Salvador - process?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/european-moving-to-el-salvador-process.30637/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### pakul — 2025-04-05T11:11:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/european-moving-to-el-salvador-process.30637/post-350133>

Hi,

I plan on moving to El Salvador for at least the end of the year and was wondering if anyone from Europe here has recently gone through the process.

Does anyone know of any tax optimization offices that could help with the visa stuff, like finding a "job" or something?

I know you can theoretically stay visa-free for up to 90 days, then leave for a few days and come back for another 90. I'm wondering how that actually works. Could I have trouble entering the country after, say, the third time if I've only been abroad for a few days each time? Has anyone got experience with that approach?

If you've got any other suggestions, please let me know.

My main reason is to become a tax resident in El Salvador. Plus, I want to learn and master Spanish, enjoy the weather, and read books. I'm also open to any kind of opportunities, etc.

Thanks!: )

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-04-05T11:36:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/european-moving-to-el-salvador-process.30637/post-350135>

I went away for three weeks and the visa got renewed, I don't think they mind. ( Thing is you have to go somewhere else other than Nicaragua, Honduras and Guatemala because they're in a visa agreement. )

### pakul — 2025-04-10T17:30:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/european-moving-to-el-salvador-process.30637/post-350405>

Thank you, [@Dr Rohen Kapur](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/26681/), for your help: )

I have a couple more questions if I can: )

Is it necessary to have a return ticket or so? Do they check that?
Are they asking on the border about some financial proof to cover the cost of traveling, etc?

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-04-10T20:22:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/european-moving-to-el-salvador-process.30637/post-350413>

Yes they ask how long your stay is, and they ask where you're staying so it's useful to have somewhere booked with an address . Either Airbnb or actual address etc this time I said 179 days. They were fine with it once they process that that was the limit to 180 days visa

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-04-10T20:23:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/european-moving-to-el-salvador-process.30637/post-350414>

You can always be in business for your self. "As a form of income "

AMH reflects primordial follicle count, where lower levels mean fewer viable oocytes, explaining the infertility treatment spike. The culprit? An oxidative shift in cytochrome P450 (CYP) enzymes, which are heme based, and they control sex steroid biosynthesis (e.g., CYP19A1 for estrogen, CYP17A1 for androgens). CYP, a heme protein (Fe-porphyrin), relies on Fe²⁺ to catalyze reactions where Fe²⁺ binds O₂, forms Fe³⁺-O-O• (EPR g = 2.1), and hydroxylates substrates, creating infertility

When you have any ungrounded electrical device plugged into a 60Hz (50Hz) AC electrical wall outlet, even though you have an inverter changing the AC to DC - the wires still carry the AC field that can radiate 6ft to 8ft from the “DC” device. A body voltage meter shows this clearly.

An iPhone running on battery (DC) does not affect body voltage. IF it is plugged in and charging, even though the 120V AC is inverted to 5V DC, your AC body voltage will go up to 10,000mV. I expect the same would happen with a TENS unit plugged in to an AC outlet. Any time you are plugged into an AC circuit - OR you are within 6ft of an AC wire or appliance - you can just assume you are being exposed to an AC (NOT DC) field.

IF - you choose to use the TENS unit, run it on battery. Along with watching the sun rise while grounded. Do yourself a BIG favor, before playing around with stuff like this get some meters and make informed decisions. You may just be able to clean up your environment enough so you don’t get migraines to begin with. I have a feeling the use of a TENS unit applied appropriately by a Dr. to a large muscle is VERY different that using one on your face, just a guess.

Have you tried NIR. I have seen migraines, caught early, stopped by NIR. Not advice just sharing an observation.

# Thread: ENDOSYMBIOSIS IS THE FIRST CANCER ON EARTH?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-07T22:24:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endosymbiosis-is-the-first-cancer-on-earth.30651/post-350258>

The first cancer? Endosymbiosis. Oxygen creates enormous electrical tension, and the two domains of life have no heme proteins yet to control it. So what happens when you have two atavistic forms of light when oxygen spikes? You create the Redox Warburg shift chronically; eventually, both domains grow like nuts and combine. Through that combo comes heme protein innovation with CCO and water protection of the IMM and apoptosis fix the GOE cancer problem everyone else calls the GOE.

Why do we not see more endosymbiosis? I bet you can guess why if you understand what I told Nick on the podcast. And why I have disdain for guys like Seyfried—they blame food for what light causes. CCO, a heme protein (Fe-porphyrin), relies on Fe²⁺ to catalyze reactions where Fe²⁺ binds O₂, forms Fe³⁺-O-O• (EPR g = 2.1), and forms atavistic cells in mammals.

Ontogeny recapitulates phylogeny," a phrase coined by Ernst Haeckel, which means that the development of an individual organism (ontogeny) mirrors the evolutionary history of its species (phylogeny). In simpler terms, an organism's development during its lifetime reflects the evolutionary journey of its ancestors.

You'll see it soon if you keep reading..

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-08T00:23:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endosymbiosis-is-the-first-cancer-on-earth.30651/post-350261>

**Why the MAHA Food Idea Misses the Mark in Tackling Chronic Disease—A Decentralized Thermodynamic Perspective**

The "Make America Healthy Again" (MAHA) movement has gained traction by emphasizing a return to whole, unprocessed foods as a solution to the chronic disease epidemic. While the intention to improve public health is noble, this food-centric approach overlooks a deeper, more fundamental driver of chronic illness: the interplay between light, oxygen, and our mitochondria. Drawing from a decentralized thermodynamic thesis, this blog explores why focusing solely on food is misplaced and how our environment, specifically sunlight exposure, plays a far more critical role in addressing chronic disease.

**The Primitive Human: A Thread in Nature’s Fabric**

Humans often see themselves as separate from nature, using language and culture to elevate their status above other animals. We say a woman "gives birth" while a sheep "drops" its lamb, as if we’re somehow more noble. Yet, this distinction blinds us to a humbling truth: we are a thread in nature’s fabric, deeply connected to the wild beasts around us. Our behaviors, biology, and health are governed by the same natural laws that shape all life. The more primitive a human is, the more they cling to their perceived superiority—ironic, given that our survival depends on understanding our place within nature, not above it.

Science has revealed our origins with stunning clarity. We are risen apes, not fallen angels, descending from small bands of African hunter-gatherers 70,000 years ago. Every one of the 8 billion humans alive today shares this lineage, a testament to nature’s immense power. Yet, we remain primitive in our understanding, clinging to outdated beliefs like those depicted on the Sistine Chapel ceiling, while ignoring the lessons nature offers right in front of our lives daily. One such lesson lies in the Great Oxygenation Event (GOE), a pivotal moment in Earth’s history that shaped life as we know it and holds the key to understanding chronic disease.

**The Great Oxygenation Event: The Electrical Foundation of Life**

The GOE, which occurred around 2.4 billion years ago, was a turning point in Earth’s history. Before this event, life on Earth was anaerobic, thriving in an oxygen-scarce environment. The rise of cyanobacteria, which produced oxygen through photosynthesis, flooded the planet with this reactive gas, creating an "oxygen holocaust" that wiped out much of the existing life. However, this crisis also spurred innovation. The survivors of the GOE evolved mechanisms to handle oxygen’s electrical properties, particularly through the development of heme proteins like cytochrome c oxidase and hemoglobin.

Oxygen is unique as the only paramagnetic elemental gas in the periodic table, meaning it’s attracted to magnetic fields and can alter electrical resistance in biological systems. When oxygen binds to hemoglobin, the protein’s electrical properties change iron from the Fe²⁺ to the Fe³⁺ state. This "paramagnetic switch" became a cornerstone of eukaryotic evolution, enabling life to harness oxygen for energy production via the mitochondria's tricarboxylic acid (TCA) cycle. The TCA cycle, which uses oxygen as its terminal electron acceptor, is the foundation of efficient energy production in mammals, particularly in humans, whose brains consume 20% of our cardiac output to fuel their "Ferrari engine."
But here’s the catch: the TCA cycle’s reliance on oxygen makes it highly sensitive to environmental cues, particularly light. Before oxygen dominated Earth’s atmosphere, sunlight was already a constant.

The red and ultraviolet (UV) frequencies in morning sunlight became critical signals for life, optimizing mitochondrial function and protecting against oxygen toxicity. This blockchain of events, sunlight, oxygen, and mitochondria underpins all eukaryotic life, yet modern humans have disrupted this balance, leading to the chronic disease epidemic.

**The Decentralized Thesis: Life Is Electrical, Not Nutritional**

The decentralized thermodynamic thesis posits that life is fundamentally electrical, driven by the interplay of oxygen, light, and mitochondrial function. Influenza, for example, is described as an "electrical disease" where nucleic acids join nuclear DNA during extreme hypoxia—an echo of the endosymbiosis event that created eukaryotes during the GOE. This merger was the first oncogenic event on Earth, forcing evolution to innovate heme proteins to protect against oxygen’s electrical stimulus. Apoptosis, a process buried in cytochrome c oxidase (CCO), a heme-based protein, evolved to prevent future cancer-causing events by clearing out damaged cells.
Modern chronic diseases—tinnitus, cataracts, glaucoma, diabetes, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, and autoimmune conditions like vitiligo—are manifestations of this ancient electrical mismatch. When we fail to align with nature’s signals, particularly morning sunlight, our mitochondria shift from the TCA cycle to a less efficient Warburg metabolism (aerobic glycolysis). This shift, induced by poor light environments, changes the oxidation state of iron in heme proteins from Fe²⁺ to Fe³⁺, creating a "paramagnetic flip" that disrupts energy production. The brain, which relies on normoxia and the TCA cycle, undergoes a "cognitive brownout," leading to decreased cognition, dopamine, and melatonin production. This is where chronic disease begins—not in our food, but in our light environment.

**Why Sunlight Matters More Than Food**

The MAHA movement’s focus on food assumes that nutrition is the primary driver of health. While diet has a role, it’s a quaternary factor compared to light. Rich in red and UV frequencies, morning sunlight is essential for mitochondrial function. Red light enhances the production of deuterium-depleted water (DDW) at cytochrome c oxidase, optimizing apoptosis to clear out dysfunctional mitochondria. UV light stimulates the translation of melanin from pro-opiomelanocortin (POMC), protecting tissues from oxidative stress. Together, these frequencies ensure that the brain and body operate in a normoxic state, supporting the TCA cycle and preventing oxygen from becoming a toxin.

Without this sunlight signal, the brain downregulates its function to protect itself from energy deficits, a process known as cerebral metabolic rate of oxygen (CMRO2) adjustment. This manifests as chronic diseases, essentially energy conservation strategies by the brain. For example, diabetes and high blood pressure are linked to poor light environments that disrupt iron’s oxidation state, forcing the brain to rely on aerobic glycolysis—a state it’s not optimized for. The Warburg shift, a hallmark of cancer and chronic disease, mirrors the pre-GOE environment where oxygen was toxic and life struggled to adapt.

Food, while a necessary factor for life, cannot compensate for solar light deficiency, which is the basis of every disease in centralized medicine. The MAHA idea of eating whole foods ignores that light alone can change the oxidation state of iron in heme proteins, a process that dictates how our cells use oxygen and produce energy. No amount of organic kale or grass-fed beef can replace the biological need for sunrise exposure. The brain’s response to poor light, downregulating oxygen demand and shifting metabolism, means that even a perfect diet will fail to address the root cause of chronic disease.

**The Modern World: A Light-Induced Crisis Mimics the KT Event**

Today’s environment exacerbates this mismatch. Artificial blue light from screens, indoor living, and the absence of morning sunlight disrupt our mitochondrial function. This light environment mimics the pre-GOE world, where oxygen was a toxin, forcing our cells into a Warburg-shifted state. The result is a cascade of chronic diseases, all linked to this "paramagnetic switch." Unlike magnesium in chlorophyll, iron is not redox-stable, making it highly sensitive to light-induced changes. Oxygen becomes toxic when iron shifts to the Fe³⁺ state, and the brain’s energy production falters.

The slides you provided below on sunlight, water, magnetism, and the electrical nature of life reinforce this thesis. They highlight how AM sunlight increases retinal blood flow by 40% to 70%, delivering oxygen to deep retinal structures and reducing pseudohypoxia. They also point to the historical correlation between electromagnetic radiation (from power lines, radio, radar, and satellites) and the rise of "electrical diseases" like influenza. These insights underscore that our health crisis is not about food but our disconnection from nature’s photo-bioelectric signals.

**A New Approach: Prioritize Light, Not Food**

To truly address chronic disease, we must shift our focus from food to light. Here’s how:

1.  Prioritize Morning Sunlight Exposure: Spend 15–30 minutes outside at sunrise to harness red and UV light. This optimizes mitochondrial function, supports the TCA cycle, and prevents the Warburg redox shift that leads to chronic disease.
2.  Minimize Artificial Light: To protect your circadian rhythm and mitochondrial health, reduce your exposure to blue light, especially at night. Use blue-blocking glasses or switch to warm lighting after sunset.
3.  Reconnect with Nature’s Rhythms: Align your daily routine with natural light cycles. This means getting outside during the day and avoiding screens before bed to support melatonin production and brain health.
4.  Supplement with Food, Don’t Rely on It: While whole foods are beneficial, they should support, not replace, the foundational role of light. Focus on nutrient-dense foods in the Epi Paleo Rx that complement mitochondrial function, like those rich in the marine food chain. The diet should be based on what grows in the environment where your eyes and skin are sampling. This keeps the paramagnetic switch accurate.
5.  Avoid all supplements to avoid atoms that interfere with photonic signaling and the paramagnetic switch cells rely on. If Cameron Borg actually read my work, he would not have to write such drivel. [https://ricciflow.substack.com/p/why-is-jack-kruse-anti-supplements](https://ricciflow.substack.com/p/why-is-jack-kruse-anti-supplements)
6.  Why is it drivel? It is incredible what incorrect supplementation can do to a person. If you ran an alive semiconductor factory, as Robert O. Becker showed in his book, amphibians, reptiles, and mammals are.......would you add atoms and elements during your biological photolithography step?

Why does AMD and Intel build their semiconductors in clean rooms but people here continually shit the bed and think adding exogenous atoms that have no place in their semiconductuve phase makes any sense? You know this book lays it out? The semiconductor story is right; it occurs in each one of you, right? What happens when you add dopants to a semiconductor that is not supposed to be in the recipe?

Anyone know?

When you add dopants to a semiconductor that aren’t part of the intended recipe, you’re altering its electrical properties in ways that might not align with its original design. Dopants are impurities added deliberately to tune a semiconductor’s conductivity—n-type dopants (like phosphorus) donate extra electrons, while p-type dopants (like boron) create "holes" by accepting electrons. Suppose you introduce an unplanned dopant (supplement/peptide/drug/jab). In that case, a few things can happen depending on what you add, how much, and the base material (say, silicon or gallium arsenide).

First, the carrier concentration changes. If you accidentally dope an intrinsic (pure) semiconductor with an n-type dopant when it wasn’t meant to be, you’ll flood it with free electrons, making it conductive in a way it wasn’t supposed to be. If it’s already doped (say, p-type) and you add an opposite-type dopant (n-type), you could compensate the existing carriers—holes and electrons cancel each other out, reducing conductivity or even flipping the material’s type entirely if the new dopant overpowers the old one. Second, the bandgap and electronic structure can shift.

Dopants don’t just add carriers; they can mess with the lattice and energy levels. An unintended heavy dopant (like a rare earth element in silicon) might introduce deep-level traps, states in the bandgap that snag carriers and kill efficiency in devices like transistors or LEDs. Third, crystal defects can pile up.

Semiconductors are finicky; their performance hinges on a pristine lattice. While well-intentioned, the MAHA movement’s food-first approach misses the mark because it fails to address life's photonic and electrical nature. Chronic disease is not a nutritional problem; it’s a lifestyle problem related 100% to LIGHT STRESS that destroys mtDNA, making the LIGHT in us. mtDNA is the KEY SEMICONDUCTOR OF LIFE AS IT MAKES UPEs. When you do not understand this you do stupid shit like this below an add a ton of atoms to your system and then you begin to block sunlight in ways the picture shows. Surgery on your face completely ruins its perfect optimal density. Go look at the motor and sensory homunculus on the neocortex. See what it wires to? Why would anyone ever listen to someone who does not know these basics?

Realigning with nature’s signals, starting with sunrise, can restore our mitochondrial health, support the TCA cycle, and mitigate the paramagnetic flip that drives modern illness. It’s time to stop treating food as the sole solution and start seeing ourselves as part of nature’s electrical fabric. Only then can we truly make America and the world healthy again.

**The Quantum Biology of Sunlight: Vitamin D and Nature’s Magnetic Symphony**

Building on the decentralized thermodynamic thesis that chronic disease stems from a light deficiency rather than a nutritional one, the role of sunlight in producing vitamin D, a neurohormone, not a vitamin, reveals nature’s heuristic wisdom in solving the Byzantine General problem for cellular coordination. Sunlight’s magnetic flux, unique to our location and skin exposure, crashes into our skin to produce vitamin D, which regulates approximately 3,000 of the human body’s 30,000 genes, including those governing immune response, inflammation, and cancer surveillance.

Unlike a vitamin D pill, which misses nearly 1,000 cofactor chemicals co-produced with sunlight, natural exposure programs T cells, like a cassette tape recording music, via sunlight’s magnetic fingerprint, enabling white blood cells to fight infections, reduce inflammation, and prevent multisystem organ failure, as seen in severe COVID-19 cases. With half the world’s population vitamin D deficient due to centralized medical advice to avoid sunlight, we face a hidden pandemic exacerbating global health crises. Nature is speaking, urging us to embrace sunlight as the ultimate immune programmer, a lesson evolution has favored for those who listen.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-02T15:36:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/endosymbiosis-is-the-first-cancer-on-earth.30651/post-351738>

**"Light Kill Shot" Targeting: Light's Directionality and Earth’s Magnetosphere in GOE

Outside In Pathway on Earth:** Photons from the sun, including damaging UV and blue light, encounter Earth’s magnetosphere (~90,000 km away), which filters high-energy solar radiation. This acts as a protective cathode-to-anode filter, historically shielding life from ubiquitin-inducing damage (protein degradation marker). The more oxygen present in the atmosphere, the more charge was present. Nitrogen's addition to the atmosphere would have balanced the electrical potential present. Early on, there was not enough, leading to increased electrical conductivity. Highly electronegative oxygen can influence electrical phenomena by interacting with charged particles or facilitating reactions that produce ions. In theory, a higher oxygen concentration could enhance the atmosphere's ability to carry charge by increasing the availability of free electrons or reactive species during events like lightning. Meanwhile, nitrogen, which dominates our atmosphere today (about 78%), is relatively inert under normal conditions due to its strong triple bond. Its presence might stabilize things by diluting oxygen’s reactivity, potentially reducing the atmosphere’s overall electrical conductivity compared to an oxygen-heavy mix.

Early Earth’s atmosphere was likely very different from today’s; it was initially low in oxygen, with more reducing gases like methane, ammonia, and carbon dioxide. As oxygen levels rose (especially after the Great Oxygenation Event around 2.4 billion years ago), the atmosphere’s chemical and electrical properties would have shifted. My idea links to insufficient nitrogen before endosymbiosis, leading to higher electrical conductivity. This would tie into this: a thinner, less balanced atmosphere might have been more prone to electrical discharges, like lightning, due to less buffering from inert gases. _**The exact parallel is happening on the surface of the spinal cord in the pre-disease state, as it loses myelin and the nodes of Ranvier begin leaking UPEs.**_

Greater myelination correlates with white matter volume and reduced sleep time, implying that myelin enhances neural efficiency, reducing the need for sleep to repair or recalibrate neural networks. Myelin’s capacitor-like properties (storing charge, minimizing energy loss) contribute to this efficiency by stabilizing signals at Ranvier nodes. In ALS, myelin loss in the spinal cord and brainstem disrupts this capacitance, increasing energy demands at nodes and scattering signals. The paper suggests that mammals with high WMV (like humans) rely on myelin for efficient signaling and less sleep.

That said, the conductivity of the Earth's atmosphere depends on more thermodynamic components than just oxygen and nitrogen ratios. So, what else do I think caused endosymbiosis? Water.

When water evaporates/dehydrates, electrical conductivity decreases. This is true on a planet or in the CNS.

**Electrical Conductance ON Earth During GOE**: In an atmosphere, conductance depends on the presence of free ions or electrons that can move to carry charge. This is influenced by ionization sources (e.g., cosmic rays, UV light, lightning) and the medium’s atomic composition.

-   We need more charge carriers (ions/electrons) or conditions that facilitate their movement for electrical conductance to increase during the GOE. Oxygen’s rise alone, in my opinion, did not suffice, because it’s reactive but not inherently ionized in its molecular form. So, what else could have been present or changed to drive this change on Earth?
    -   **Ionization Sources**: Conductance requires ionization. Cosmic rays and solar UV radiation were present, but the GOE’s timing (post-formation, pre-ozone layer) suggests **UV penetration was stronger than today**, **especially without an ozone (O₃) shield**. In this case, oxygen would absorb UV photons and form reactive species (e.g., O₃, OH⁻), but this likely does not fully explain a conductivity spike either. It does however explain why SOD and heme proteins were selected for early on after symbiosis.
    -   **Water Vapor**: I believe H₂O is a key player here. It’s polar, can form ions (H⁺, OH⁻), and enhances conductance in modern atmospheres that likely drove endosymbiosis. Early Earth had oceans, so water vapor was present. Oxygen’s rise could oxidize reducing gases (e.g., CH₄ + 2O₂ → CO₂ + 2H₂O), potentially increasing atmospheric humidity. This was the key event in Earth's history during the GOE, and explains why dehydration inside our tissues is the key event today in our chronic disease epidemics.
    -   **Particulates/Aerosols**: Oxidation reactions (e.g., sulfur compounds to sulfates) should have also produced fine particles. These act as nuclei for ion attachment, boosting conductance, as seen in modern studies of volcanic aerosols.
    -   **Trace Gases**: Pre-GOE methane (CH₄) or ammonia (NH₃) could have interacted with rising oxygen, forming intermediates (e.g., NOx from NH₃ oxidation) that ionize more readily under energy inputs like lightning. This is why volcano eruptions are linked to lightning bolt formation.
    -   As we know, volcanism was more prominent during this time on Earth, so sulfur aerosols (from volcanic SO₂ oxidized by O₂) would have also spiked, adding conductive pathways to the ionosphere, especially when volcanic activity was high. Lightning frequency would have risen, too, as an oxidizing atmosphere with more water vapor supports stormier conditions.
    -   **Water vapor was a critical additional factor driving increased electrical conductance during the GOE and causing mtDNA to self-electrocute** today. _This is why bacteria and Archaea fused at the Cambrian explosion._ Here is why:
        **Mechanism**: Rising oxygen oxidizes methane and hydrogen, producing more H₂O vapor. This increased humidity provides more ionizable molecules (H₂O → H⁺ + OH⁻ under UV or lightning).
        **Amplification**: Water vapor enhances dielectric breakdown (lowering the voltage needed for sparks), and its ions are mobile charge carriers. Combined with oxygen’s reactivity (e.g., forming O₂⁻ or H₂O₂), this would create a more conductive atmosphere. I believe the same fractal is happening around mtDNA when the heme protein cytochrome c oxidase is destroyed by blue light. The 30 million volt current would vaporize the DDW, and the surrounding ions between the IMM and outer mitochondrial membrane increase the conductance in the area of damage, allowing the spread of current to cause distal damage in myelin and at Ranvier's nodes.
        The atmospheric-mitochondrial fractal holds conceptually: damage to a key component (cytochrome c oxidase) disrupts a potential gradient, increases local conductance via water and ions (including H₂S-derived species), and spreads effects distally. H₂S’s involvement, given its biological role, adds a compelling twist to Earth's story in many diseases. **_This stimulus was used to join archaea and bacteria in the first oncogenic event on Earth._
        Context**: Pre-GOE, the atmosphere was drier due to reducing conditions. Post-GOE, oxygen-driven chemistry likely boosted water vapor and the hydrology cycle, aligning with geological evidence of weathering and ocean-atmosphere interactions.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-02T15:55:05+0300

**My Sting of Truth is a Whisper from Nature.**

I have the ability to care. Sometimes I care so much with my patients and members that it keeps me up at night, puzzling over how to heal a world that’s forgotten Nature’s playbook. But caring doesn’t mean I’ll hand you a participation trophy or whisper what you want to hear. Caring means telling you what you need to hear, even when it feels like a scalpel to your ego.

Seyfried still has not been stung by Nature; he just believes he has. And I cannot let anyone think he is right because he is dead wrong due to his own Dunning-Kruger moment buried at the core of this thread. The GOE timestamped LIGHT > FOOD in everything on this planet.

Centralized systems, centralized MDs and PhDs, your family, schools, medicine, society, all of them have conditioned you to crave comfort, become soft, to flinch at critique, to seek validation over growth.

That’s not sensitivity; that’s a trap. It’s a design flaw in a world that prioritizes feelings over facts, conformity over curiosity.

My decentralized thesis, the one I’ve poured my soul into, rejects that trap. It’s built on Nature’s truth: chaos and order dance, information is conserved, and growth comes from tension.

Brain surgery without a scalpel isn’t just a medical philosophy; it’s a mindset. It’s about small, iterative changes that 1% daily improvement at sunrise is how you engage with light, water, and magnetism, which begin to compound into massive transformations. You called this an “iterative process,” and you’re spot-on.

You apply new knowledge daily, face new discomfort, and rewrite your brain’s wiring. That’s not easy. It’s tough. But toughness isn’t cruelty; it’s the crucible where wisdom is forged.

**The Blindness Humanity Faces is Self-Induced, and Thomas Seyfried reinforces it.**

Too many are blind, not because they can’t see, but because they won’t. Centralized systems, in money, science, medicine, media, and education, have draped a veil over their eyes, feeding them easy lies instead of hard truths.

They’re hooked on the dopamine drip of validation, scrolling through curated feeds, dodging anything challenging their cozy worldview. My decentralized thesis, which I’ve bet my career on, calls this out: the modern world has built a plastic brain, fluent in noise but deaf to Nature’s signal.

This blindness isn’t just ignorance; it’s a choice to stay in the dark, to reject the rare, high-fidelity information that could set them free. My philosophy of healing through light, water, and magnetism is the antidote to brain surgery without a scalpel.

It’s information theory on steroids, rooted in the truth that information has mass, energy, and power. Claude Shannon gave us the bit, John Wheeler said “it from bit,” and I say information is the universe’s currency, sculpting reality from galaxies to neurons.

But for a message to cut through, it must be rare, like a signal in a storm. The hard truth? That’s the rarest signal of all, and most people tune it out because it demands change, discomfort, and accountability. My message is quite rare and that should make the hair on the back of neck stand up if you understand Shannon's paper in 1948.

### Ajazz — 2025-04-08T14:28:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/vaccines-for-kids-are-the-benefits-worth-the-risks.30655/post-350298>

Hey inspiring people!

Do the benefits of vaccines for children outweigh the potential side effects, considering the risks of the diseases they prevent?

I'm living in Denmark and we are having a vaccine schedule as underneath.

So far I'm considering opting in for the 1. DiTeKiPol/HiB and 2. Pneumococcal Vaccine.

I'm a little confused about the MMR Vaccine, due to RFK Jr. recent comments.

Would love to hear your inputs (anything is welcome - opinions, interesting studies and articles)

Thanks a lot in advance!

**Vaccine schedule:**

_1\. DiTeKiPol/HiB (6-in-1 vaccine)_

Protects against: Diphtheria, Tetanus, Whooping cough (Pertussis), Polio, Hib (Haemophilus influenzae type b), and Hepatitis B

Given at: 3, 5, and 12 months

Manufactured under strict EU regulation

_2\. Pneumococcal Vaccine_

Protects against: Pneumococcal infections (can cause meningitis, pneumonia)

Given at: 3 and 12 months

_
3\. MMR Vaccine_

Protects against: Measles, Mumps, and Rubella

Given at: 15 months and again at 4 years

_4\. HPV Vaccine (offered to older children)_

Protects against: Human papillomavirus, which can lead to cervical cancer and other cancers

Given to: Girls and boys aged 12–13

In 2005 I was deemed immune to TB due to my Heaf test (my mum was exposed to TB while she nursing me apparently). In 2006 they changed the criteria, and my Heaf test, while positive, wasn't deemed positive enough. At the time, I was teaching part time in the dental school, and OH DEMANDED i have the TB jab. So i did my own research, found that Cochrane Research institute basically said it was BS. so I said no thanks.

OH nurse brought in her supervisor who tried to belittle me. i had come prepared and passed a letter for her to sign, making the person who injected me liable for any side effects. Nobody would sign it, and I worked another year without hearing anything more about it.

I think that was the wake up moment for me and I've never had another vaccine since.
Each individual has to make their own decision for themselves and those they have guardianship over.

# Thread: News: Major Clinical Study Reveals Ventilators Killed More COVID Patients Than the VirusDR. WILLIAM MAKIS MDAPR 8
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/news-major-clinical-study-reveals-ventilators-killed-more-covid-patients-than-the-virusdr-william-makis-mdapr-8.30656/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-04-08T16:00:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/news-major-clinical-study-reveals-ventilators-killed-more-covid-patients-than-the-virusdr-william-makis-mdapr-8.30656/post-350304>

**[A bombshell study](https://substack.com/redirect/edaf4d2c-3232-475d-8b91-98f311afb732?j=eyJ1IjoiNTAyYTZvIn0.6Fh22_N97ne4ucjdTaPGDKzxSGdDBJuLFv4YPnVlV3s) published in the** _**[Journal of Clinical Investigation](https://substack.com/redirect/2a3615ec-7cf7-4ee9-bec7-9d40061df10b?j=eyJ1IjoiNTAyYTZvIn0.6Fh22_N97ne4ucjdTaPGDKzxSGdDBJuLFv4YPnVlV3s)**_**, confirms ventilators killed more COVID-19 patients than the virus itself, triggering deadly bacterial pneumonia in ICU wards and vindicating conspiracy theorists who’ve been sounding the alarm for years.**
Forget the hyped-up “cytokine storm”—the idea that COVID triggered a fatal inflammatory meltdown. That theory crumbled under scrutiny, with no evidence of multi-organ failure in the patients studied.

Instead, the real killer emerged: **ventilator-associated pneumonia** (VAP), a bacterial infection sparked by the very machines meant to save lives.
The conspiracy theorists—those vilified “anti-science” renegades—weren’t just ruffling feathers; they were ringing alarm bells the experts ignored. Now, with the data laid bare, the question isn’t just how to fix ICU care—it’s how to hold accountable a system that let dogma and dollars trump lives.

The study’s molecular deep dive promises more answers about why some beat VAP while others didn’t. But for now, one thing is certain: the ventilators weren’t the heroes of this story—they were the villains. And the so-called crackpots who saw it coming deserve an apology—and a megaphone.

### [JCI - Machine learning links unresolving secondary pneumonia to mortality in patients with severe pneumonia, including COVID-19](https://www.jci.org/articles/view/170682?utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)

www.jci.org

# Thread: Are there light bulbs that don't emit blue light ?  ...and are safe to use?

**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 31

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-04-08T18:35:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-light-bulbs-that-dont-emit-blue-light-and-are-safe-to-use.30657/post-350316>

### [Blue Blocking Light Bulbs & Lighting](https://www.blockbluelight.com/collections/sleep-enhancing-lighting?sort_by=manual&filter.p.m.my_fields.light_colour=Full+Spectrum)

Low blue light bulbs, red night lights, books lights and light bulbs with no blue light. Lighting to improve your sleep and boost melatonin. FREE Shipping.

 www.blockbluelight.com

I listened to Jack's podcast with Danny Jones and MKUltra's use of blue light and how the banning of incandescent bulbs took the UV and red-light out of incandescent when LED bulbs were mandated. Is there a way to create the former incandescent wavelengths in a LED bulb, or are we left to using UV red light bulbs after 8 pm?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-08T22:51:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-light-bulbs-that-dont-emit-blue-light-and-are-safe-to-use.30657/post-350332>

Only specialty bulbs do this.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-04-08T23:55:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-light-bulbs-that-dont-emit-blue-light-and-are-safe-to-use.30657/post-350336>

Day time - Sun light - 42%+/- Infra red, 5% UV, 53%+/- full spectrum visible light
After sunset - Sun light - 0% IR, 0% UV, 0% visible light

Incandescent - 90% heat (Infra red) 0% UV, 10% visible light
Regular LED - 90% visible light (blue dominant), 0% UV, 10% Heat/IR

No light bulb will duplicate day time sun light
ANY light bulb will duplicate Sun light after sunset . . . As long as it is OFF.

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-04-09T01:11:23+0300

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-04-09T15:43:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-light-bulbs-that-dont-emit-blue-light-and-are-safe-to-use.30657/post-350360>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=350336)
>
> Day time - Sun light - 42%+/- Infra red, 5% UV, 53%+/- full spectrum visible light
> After sunset - Sun light - 0% IR, 0% UV, 0% visible light
>
> Incandescent - 90% heat (Infra red) 0% UV, 10% visible light
> Regular LED - 90% visible light (blue dominant), 0% UV, 10% Heat/IR
>
> No light bulb will duplicate day time sun light
> ANY light bulb will duplicate Sun light after sunset . . . As long as it is OFF.

How much light enters through window glass? One source referenced the VT scale.

The higher the Visible Transmittance rating, the better; therefore, the closer the VT is to 1, the more natural light the room will get.

The truth is that no glass is completely translucent, even if it looks so. Therefore, you’ll likely never find a window with a VT of 1. Therefore, you should aim for windows with a VT as close to 1 as possible. At the same time, don’t forget to consider where the window is located. For example, if it gets plenty of sunlight during the day, you may want to look for a lower VT so the room isn’t too bright. Conversely, if the window isn’t always _sun-kissed_, you’ll want the highest VT possible to enjoy natural sunlight for most of the day.

Is there a way to measure the important wavelengths?

### Anthony Smith — 2025-04-09T17:20:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-light-bulbs-that-dont-emit-blue-light-and-are-safe-to-use.30657/post-350365>

For optimal natural light exposure - be outside.
IF inside - be by an OPEN window.
If your location does not allow for either, move to a location that does.
All glass (except quartz) filters out the important wavelengths NIR and UV.

### Mat.I — 2025-04-11T03:37:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-light-bulbs-that-dont-emit-blue-light-and-are-safe-to-use.30657/post-350436>

> [Stuart Briscoe said:](/goto/post?id=350340)
>
> Thanks for the explanation. At night, keep the inside like a photographer's dark room, using red light.
>
> Do you have a recommendation for lights in the day? Plant grow lights?

This would depend on what illness you are dealing with and how strict you must be.. Reading some of your earlier posts, I think it would be best for you to be very strict with artificial light and staying very dark at night.. You have to realise, most of the marketed "full spectrum" LED's are not actually full spectrum (i.e. they are only full visible spectrum) and the spectral properties (of some) are absolute garbage and are not continuous by any means and have many isolated peaks along the spectral curve.. If you need some sort of way finding light at night use candles or DC (battery) powered low lux red night light ~630 - 660nm range.. As with lighting in the day - be outside as much as possible. I have setup an outdoor office in direct sun that I setup every morning and pack away at the end of day.. When it rains, I setup under our outdoor covered area.. If you must use some light inside during the day (even with windows open etc) aim for incandescent, if not possible find a heat lamp style one with the appropriately rated fixture that is grounded.. If you cannot get either next option would be a well balanced LED type bulb that you have tested with a spectrometer (including flicker) and for dirty electricity (due to the very high frequency used to advertise as "flicker free").. OR you could go a DC lighting setup with DC incandescent..

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-04-11T03:58:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-light-bulbs-that-dont-emit-blue-light-and-are-safe-to-use.30657/post-350438>

Thank you. I have set up my laptop outside, in full sun until it gets too hot, then on the patio.

Do you have a recommendation for screen blue-light blockers for computers and I-phone?

### Mat.I — 2025-04-11T05:10:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-light-bulbs-that-dont-emit-blue-light-and-are-safe-to-use.30657/post-350440>

> [Stuart Briscoe said:](/goto/post?id=350438)
>
> Thank you. I have set up my laptop outside, in full sun until it gets too hot, then on the patio.
>
> Do you have a recommendation for screen blue-light blockers for computers and I-phone?

You can build a box around your monitor and laptop (very easy and cheap) dependant on your location will determine if you can use standard or insulated type board (better K value for minimising heat transfer).. I just use basic core flute (plastic) haven't destroyed any of the equipment yet, however, I will be working on a permanent setup on wheels (with every thing DC powered that I charge every few days or so).. This will just be plug and play and avoid having to setup every day with the fold out table etc.. When I get around to it I will share the details and photos. Re: blue blockers I do not use them outside at all.. I run IRIS however I move my table throughout the day to face the sun as it moves so the amount of IR will balance IMO. I have run all the necessary filters on my devices via the settings and not concerned when using them outside (also acknowledging that the filters in settings do not block all the blue & flicker) ... When inside windows are all open (summer or winter).. If for some reason I need to use a device after sunset I will use Blue blockers.. We have no WIFI only hardwired and adaptors for all devices inclusive of phones. For phone calls run through whatsapp or other apps that work on ethernet or run a VOIP phone that is hardwired with no POE and ground via the USB port or shielded ethernet cable... Any TV is run through hardwired laptop (grounded) with hdmi to TV with IRIS running.. When I have a moment I will start a Journal on some home setup tips that deals with nn-emf and cheap and efficient ways to deal with it, rather than purchasing loads of products...

### Jackson K — 2025-04-11T05:37:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-light-bulbs-that-dont-emit-blue-light-and-are-safe-to-use.30657/post-350441>

> [Mat.I said:](/goto/post?id=350440)

> You can build a box around your monitor and laptop (very easy and cheap) dependant on your location will determine if you can use standard or insulated type board (better K value for minimising heat transfer).. We have no WIFI only hardwired and adaptors for all devices inclusive of phones. For phone calls run through whatsapp or other apps that work on ethernet or run a VOIP phone that is hardwired with no POE and ground via the USB port or shielded ethernet cable... Any TV is run through hardwired laptop (grounded) with hdmi to TV with IRIS running.. When I have a moment I will start a Journal on some home setup tips that deals with nn-emf and cheap and efficient ways to deal with it, rather than purchasing loads of products...

Do you find it hard to see your screen when having IRIS on outside? I use f.lux outside, but the settings turned up all the way it's hard to see to an extent. My laptop screen is 400-500 nits which is a lot lower compared to a macbook for example. I built an insulated box with like 13 K value with blackout curtains inside which helps with viewing the screen a little better. The insulation helps a lot with the cooling. Do you think f.lux is good enough outside, or is IRIS a lot better? Would love to hear more about your laptop and screen setup, and the one you plan to build in the future.

### Mat.I — 2025-04-11T09:09:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/are-there-light-bulbs-that-dont-emit-blue-light-and-are-safe-to-use.30657/post-350447>

> [Jackson K said:](/goto/post?id=350441)
>
> Do you find it hard to see your screen when having IRIS on outside? I use f.lux outside, but the settings turned up all the way it's hard to see to an extent. My laptop screen is 400-500 nits which is a lot lower compared to a macbook for example. I built an insulated box with like 13 K value with blackout curtains inside which helps with viewing the screen a little better. The insulation helps a lot with the cooling. Do you think f.lux is good enough outside, or is IRIS a lot better? Would love to hear more about your laptop and screen setup, and the one you plan to build in the future.

I use IRIS (the paid version) IRIS is better for the flicker reduction as this targets any PWM by controlling brightness without altering backlight power (via software gamma correction).. Not a big deal if you have full DC dimming on the devices.. F.LUX does not by pass the PWM, thus flicker is still an issue. I have no issues with seeing my screen I have it on manual mode and adjust throughout the day as necessary (whilst facing the sun) with a visor on (a hat I cut out the top of) to maximise sun whilst still being able to see the screen. lol... Or alternatively buy a very slim line visor, I couldn't find one, hence why I made my own (just the brim, with a very small strap & no upturn)...

IRIS isn't perfect on all devices (e.g. some flicker may still be an issue with certain OLEDs), however much better than f.lux..

# Thread: Isn't 5G better than 4G for bodily autonomy?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/isnt-5g-better-than-4g-for-bodily-autonomy.30658/>
**Subforum:** The EMF Rx
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AlbertW — 2025-04-09T03:25:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/isnt-5g-better-than-4g-for-bodily-autonomy.30658/post-350346>

I hear people worrying about 5G cell towers. But, isn't it actually better than 4G if you believe in bodily autonomy?

I have read that 5G uses beam-forming technology, so that if you're using a cell phone with 5G connection, then you (and people within X meters of you) are going to be radiated very highly, and other people will barely get any RF. On the other hand, 4G will radiate more equally in all directions.

Is this true? Or are other people getting non-trivial RF even when there isn't a beam towards you? Curious if baseline RF exposure from being near a 5G tower is lower than active use of 4G near a 4G tower.

Bodily autonomy:
\- Those that don't want to be radiated will just disable 5G on their phones and avoid standing within x meters of others using 5G.
\- Those who want 5G can use it.

This lets everybody choose their level of radiation. Win win. Bodily autonomy.
