### YaMan — 2025-05-08T13:03:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/mitochondrial-atavism-is-real.30659/post-352149>

Arthritis and hip dysplasia resulted in two artificial hips for me already at the age of 53. Based on your thesis and my study of your work for then last year, I conclude I am Warburg shifted and as you explain O2 is toxic for me.
Environmental changes (light, nnEMF etc) are proceeding as you propose but I am now wondering about short and long term manifestation of this O2 toxicity based on O2 exposure duration.

I am a closed-circuit-rebreather (CCR) diver in the summer time.
My CCR dives go 60-90 min. and the breathing mix ranges from 21% (depending on depth) - 100% (at 6m decompression stop for 15-30min). The O2 partial pressure (ppO2) is set fix to 1.3bar. At 6m i manually inject more O2 to the breathing mix pushing the ppO2 to 1.6bar.
I do around 20-30 such dives per year but only one such dive within 24h window.

So
#1: Warburg shift (as per my chronic disease „arthritis, hip dysplasia“)
vs
#2: O2 exposure during dives
vs
#3: electron exchange with the environment during the dive (sun and sea) and during the day (the only time i am indoors in the summer is when i sleep - Greece 38 lat)
EDIT (just came to my mind) #4: CCR electronics (dive computer, EM solenoid for O2 injection, head up display etc) for monitoring and mixing are battery operated (2x 9V 2x 1.5V alkaline)

Is such O2 dose enough to manifest itself in short time or is it too isolated to „matter“ given my working assumption of Warburg shift? Is it more of long term effect?

# Thread: Windows to the Soul - My Optimal Journal

**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 37

### solsearcher — 2025-04-09T17:20:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-to-the-soul-my-optimal-journal.30662/post-350364>

Hi all,

I’m a new mitochondriac. I’m 24 yrs old, living in Phoenix, AZ.
Very happy to be learning with you all. I’m in healthcare, and relieved to have found Dr Kruse.
I’ll be brief. Here goes.

Past medical history:
\- Bipolar II. Big mood swings, with a seasonal arc. Not normal. Winters are horrendous.
\- Extreme fatigue, not all the time. Correlated to many things, including the weather. For example, I'll wake up exhausted, then go outside and see it's cloudy.
\- Awful anxiety attacks. Very, very bad, triggered by: nicotine, fish oil in the AM, fish oil without enough EPA, lion’s mane & reishi, high dose B vitamins, high carb load.
\- Insomnia
\- Some other histamine-like reactions: itching, dermatographia.
\- Intense teeth grinding, seasonally. This started since I moved to AZ. It appears in December and then quickly disappears. Related, I do have kind of a chewing fixation. My whole life I've chewed sunflower seeds, carrots, that kind of thing. Chewing is relaxing.
\- Unexplained anemia (ferritin of 8)
\- Cold intolerance, and extreme heat tolerance. All day in Arizona 100F is good with me.

It’s taken me many years to learn about myself. And test stupid supplements.

However, I’ve always known my environment was key. That's why I moved further south, to sunny Arizona.
Few understand how good I felt when I left West Coast and came out here.

I'm the family redhead and burnt a ton as a kid. I grew up outside in southern OR — and never wore sunglasses, funny enough.

Family history:
\- Mother: M.S., ovarian cancer (passed away at 52)
\- Father: cardiovascular disease & heart attack (passed away). He was a surfer and had this eternally youthful look and spirit.
\- Grandmother: I don't know much, but she was from a tougher generation. Also a genius. She hardly ate anything and lived to 92.
\- My mom and her siblings were all creative geniuses. Siblings had lots of substance use issues.
\- Maternal aunt and cousin with breast cancer.
\- Father's twin, two maternal uncles, and my brother all have bipolar disorder.

Substances: zero. I don't drink, smoke, anything.

Jab status: multiple manufacturers. I know.

Current treatments:
\- lithium orotate, thiamine (TTFD), high-EPA fish oil. These have helped stabilize both mood and energy.
\- ketovore. Big breakfast. Occasional cheating hasn't given me issues.
\- blue blockers
\- blackout curtain. Unbelievable difference in my energy and mood, overnight.
\- sunrise, of course.

I have lots of hope. Even with these few steps, my life is already changed.
People are commenting — "where did you go? You're super tan!" I guess redheads can tan.
I’m looking forward to reading steadily through the blogs.
My only hopes are to help my condition, help others' conditions, and… ask the right questions.

But as you can see: I'm a total biologic trainwreck. Small offsets in sleep, a few days inside, or a late dinner... Etc... has me running on empty. And mood flipping way up or down.
As I write this, my blackout curtain unstuck from the tape and fell off. That about sums it up.

I have a ticket to El Salvador booked. I'm way behind on info, but taking notes. Tropical sunlight and "EXTREME darkness." Thoracic and abdominal melanin renovation.

I guess my big question how to pay off my student debt. Because I have something lined up in AZ. But I can't do it if the "power shuts off."

More to come....

"Look into my eyes,
Eyes are the windows to the soul"

I'm about to graduate PA school — nervous as hell. This system is so broken, and no one gets better. Here I am in debt for a job where I'll be stuck in blue light, selling pharma.

### solsearcher — 2025-05-05T06:46:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-to-the-soul-my-optimal-journal.30662/post-351920>

"This is the hammer that killed John Henry
But it won't kill me, it won't kill me"

It's funny to see the Sisyphean quest all mitochondria newbies undergo, myself included.
"How can I stay in \_\_\_\_\_?"
My seasonal patterns cement one fact: I will not get truly better here.
ES is the goal. So the question is whether I can - medically - stay here & work for 2 years, or whether I need to move on this year.

Amazingly enough, after my last post, I found a job. It's home health visits & making my own schedule. I could buy a convertible & get 10mins of sun in between patient visits.

However, winter is my obstacle. That's when health issues rear their head to make minimum functioning impossible. Very variable and dependent on invisble planetary motions. QE 47 is right - you get awful cognitive deficits even during euthymic periods.

Possible solutions:
1\. Move
I'm in a 5G city. I may move to rural AZ, 262 residents per square mile. Since nnEMF destroys photoreceptors (melatonin, dopamine, heme), I think this is necessary to endure winter.

2\. Magnetico
[@TaylorJamesTJ](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/31387/) 's report of Magnetico having drastic effects suggests that it'll affect me. He is also a desert-dweller (no native magnetism). I’m thinking he might have not done it for long enough, like the other commenter who initially had his sleep destroyed, then improved.
If bipolar is a thalamic mass overload that lithium stabilizes…. could sleeping with my head on a magnet stabilize it too?
But, from QE 48 comments:
"The Earths magnetic field is weakest at the equator and if one has bipolar disorder this is the optimal place to live to limit seasonal changes."
So, I actually don't understand magnetism, or whether this will help me. I could FAFO but I’m getting a consult.

I'm really hoping these first two can “suffice.”

All I know is — I’m up against technology plus nature’s rhythms. Listen to the song ^^. I am so grateful to not be trapped in a hospital - as either patient or provider.

3\. Indoor UV & IR lamps when the photoperiod shrinks.
I don't have these, and I don’t really want them.

4\. CT?
I know this is beneficial, and I've loved cold plunges in the past.
And here in Arizona summer, it feels wonderful.
But some major issues: when I plunge in the afternoon, I wake up at 1, 2am overheating and sweating like crazy. It destroys my sleep. So I probably need to do it in the morning. But that creates problems.
With a ferritin of 8, in the winter, in the cold morning, with major cognitive and mood issues... I will need LOTS of willpower to do this.
On the other hand, I'm sure my transformation into a radiator is a major clue that I need CT.
(Tetragrammaton with Bill Gifford, 1:43:00)

I have many exciting hopes for the future. Living in the light and I'm not going back.

### solsearcher — 2025-05-14T07:12:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-to-the-soul-my-optimal-journal.30662/post-352472>

Excited for my trip to ES. I wasn't aware of how affordable car rentals are.

I'll reach out to some forum people regarding where to stay. I've seen Maria and India mentioned for places to stay, so I might message them.

As for journal, here are my supplements (for criticism).

I'm only beginning to understand how these are working in synergy to help/damage me. But some I think I need.

\- ketovore diet
\- lithium orotate 5mg twice a day
\- high EPA fish oil
\- methylene blue PO 25mg 1-2x per week
\- boron
\- magnesium (sucrosomial)
\- colostrum
\- thiamine (TTFD)
\- small dose of B complex
\- KCl in my food, to balance the salt

A few things I've learned about these:

Ketovore w/ lots of saturated fat
\=> Needs sunrise for TCA cycle.
Ketosis should be in its environmental context, specifically winter (cold / hibernation). It's about superoxide, ROS, RNS signaling, which are destroyed in some medical conditions. So, it can be re-engineered to handle, but not treat, quantum diseases (DM 9, Sept 2024 Q&A).
Sleep is another time for ketosis (QE 31).
PUFAs "increase proton leakage in mitochondrial matrix and allows more deuterium in matrix than should be there and this raised heteroplasmy rates to cause disease. " (DM 15)
Grains have folate which is "the real reason to avoid" them (DM 4).
"When the environment steals this ability from cells (nnEMF) cells are at the mercy of food and exercise." (DM 22)
Conclusion: It's delicious and doesn't seem harmful.

Lithium:
Scavenges UV (200-300nm) biophoton leakage from damaged neurons in demyelinating disorders (DM 47). Helpful in bipolar disorder, because it balances out deuterium-laden thalamus (DM35, QE47/48). Very hard to understand.
"Getting the right amount of lithium is tricky" and dangerous because of its effects on POMC utilization of light (DM 35). Especially dangerous in the manic phase (May 2025 Q&A).
Jack “likes” it—meaning that he sees treatment potential and has mapped out a Li+ experiment for the jabbed (DM 35). But, the isotope matters (7Li vs 6Li, like deterium vs protium). I obviously don't have isotope control in my supplement. Maybe isotopic content differs by brand.
Heavy lithium and the jab can worsen bipolar.
I think DM 35 confirms the theory that lithium orotate has superior CSF distribution to traditional, toxic lithium carbonate ([https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8413749/](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC8413749/)). Centralized medicine needs to investigate this yesterday. I cannot emphasize this enough. Millions of people could get better overnight.
Lithium orotate was one of the best things that ever happened to me.

EPA:
"Photon trap"
I know Michael Crawford is the expert here. I atill don't know the difference between EPA & DHA.
DHA traps 200-700nm light and converts to DC (DM 35).
There's also the issue of only Sn-2 getting into the brain. I’m not worried about it, because its a food derivative. Also, fish omega 3s have well-known data for many mental and physical conditions. I know real fish is better.

MB:
"Main job of MB is to change +3 metHb to +2 HbO2." (DM 46) Basically, it reverses Warburg shifting. But you have to know why the tissue was Warburg shifted in the first place.
Important for vax injured (Oct 2024 Q&A). IV has different biophysics than oral.
Can cause cancer by reintroducing oxygen too soon in the dedifferentiation and Becker regeneration process (DM 39, 40). This is because CCO, a heme based protein, needs to be in circadian operation first to ensure apoptosis is functional. If not, unchecked proliferation of defective mitochondria becomes metastasis (DM 38, 46). MB effects change when taken before PBM (DM 41).
I think Ehlrich found out it had high neuronal affinity. I'm taking it PRN on days where I'm anxious or down, combined with sunlight. I notice a huge positive switch.
I need to reread DM 38-42 again because they are so dense. The CO-oximetry piece was powerful... One of those "how does medicine not know this" moments. Embarrassing.

Boron:
Abnormal p-type dopant that creates "holes" for electron acception ([https://www.instagram.com/drjackkruse/p/DHOk3vVu-iY](https://www.instagram.com/drjackkruse/p/DHOk3vVu-iY)). I'm worried about this one. DM 35 reiterates the danger of introducing foreign atoms, and the body doesn't use boron.
I tried stopping it and my energy went down, so I started again.

Colostrum:
Jack liked it back when he wrote his book. I remember him saying it closes the gut barrier of athletes.
Again, I'm not worried about it because it's a food derivative.

I don't understand much yet, but at least I can show a little "proof of work."

### solsearcher — 2025-05-17T17:24:29+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-to-the-soul-my-optimal-journal.30662/post-352734>

Alright, I’ve restrained myself in the last few posts. Now, time to yap.

My #1 priority is my mental and physical health. I have many exciting ideas, but I cannot pursue them unless I am well.

A major insight from my Kruse consult was that cold isn’t good for my n=1 at the moment. This is a relief because I can’t stand the cold. But I’m looking forward to enjoying it once I get my anemia fixed.

I have 3 options coming out of PA school:

1\. Stay in Phoenix
This is already crossed out in my mind. I have nothing keeping me here.
City humans and rural humans have diverged as species.
I took a drive yesterday to a beautiful rural place, behind the McDowell mountains. I could tell the moment that the EMF dropped out. It was like a weight being lifted off my mind.
Plus, I already know I’m nonfunctional in winter here. Time to move.

2\. South Florida
Jack suggested this to me. Key West is 24N and Phoenix is 33N, so it’s a significant gain in light and temperature stability (see pics).

Someone mentioned Key West to me yesterday, unprompted. And there’s a city down there called “Homestead.” Maybe some synchronicity.

Cochise County has a population density of 20 per square mile. No one wants to live here. That means its perfect. Land is cheap. Very easy to build here. Downside: it’s a little colder in the winter.

### solsearcher — 2025-05-21T03:16:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-to-the-soul-my-optimal-journal.30662/post-353040>

Health care is a mess.
Across the past two years, I've seen iatrogenic injuries, where both patients and providers are clueless that the meds are making them worse. I've seen providers more concerned with legal risk than properly addressing a condition. Passing stories of turbo cancers, followed by shrugs. I've seen what I expected.

But I've also seen what I came into medicine for. Amazing connections, made possible by a provider that listens. People that recover despite the system. And Dr Kruse has shown me the most hope: people can get better. Starting with myself.

I want to help psychiatric patients. This is the highest thing I can aspire to in medicine. It's my condition, it's my family's condition, it's my generation's condition.

The worst thing about psychiatry is that it doesn't work. All of my classmates know this. It never has. I've learned only a little of the history of psychiatry. But I already agree with Tom Cruise in that one interview. It is dystopia, and has been for many, many years. In 1949 it was lobotomy, involuntary commitment, abuse. Today it's involuntary commitment, abuse, gaslighting, affective blunting, tardive dyskinesia, sexual dysfunction, withdrawals, and even neuro-hormonal changes from ozempic and birth control. It's hard to fathom how much medicine has damaged our minds.

But that also creates opportunity. The best thing about psychiatry is that it doesn't work. So no one wants to do it. What a beautiful thing it would be: to create a place where these patients can get better.

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) mentioned to me that some other members are discussing a center of some sort. It sounded like a week-to-monthlong place for mitos filling up their battery or establishing in El Salvador. He said I should talk to [@Laudy Cincotta](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/24082/) and [@India Rose Hopper](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29690/) about this, and also Tiffany (I don't know her username to tag her).

(Provided I can medically stay, I may work for a while in the US, gaining a footing out of school. Hoping on telemeds or home health to control my environment.
My \*number one goal\* is staying stable. As I discussed above, my two options are Southeast AZ and South Florida. Like Jack said, I really need to stay in FL first to know whether I can handle EMF. I have these three places floating around in my mind.
Southeast AZ - beautiful, \*cheap,\* close, sunny.... Maybe an unmerciful winter.
Florida - beautiful, chaotic, swampy, supposedly overcrowded & EMF'd, I've never set foot in it.
Straight to El Salvador - again, I haven't yet been, and moving here would maybe create obstacles for a PA career right out of school.)

I would be very eager to discuss the creation of such a psychiatric/mito center with those who are like-minded.

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-05-21T03:58:08+0300

I've seen providers more concerned with legal risk than properly addressing a condition. Passing stories of turbo cancers, followed by shrugs. I've seen what I expected.
>
> But I've also seen what I came into medicine for. Amazing connections, made possible by a provider that listens. People that recover despite the system. And Dr Kruse has shown me the most hope: people can get better. Starting with myself.
>
> I want to help psychiatric patients. This is the highest thing I can aspire to in medicine. It's my condition, it's my family's condition, it's my generation's condition.
>
> The worst thing about psychiatry is that it doesn't work. All of my classmates know this. It never has. I've learned only a little of the history of psychiatry. But I already agree with Tom Cruise in that one interview. It is dystopia, and has been for many, many years. In 1949 it was lobotomy, involuntary commitment, abuse. Today it's involuntary commitment, abuse, gaslighting, affective blunting, tardive dyskinesia, sexual dysfunction, withdrawals, and even neuro-hormonal changes from ozempic and birth control. It's hard to fathom how much medicine has damaged our minds.
>
> But that also creates opportunity. The best thing about psychiatry is that it doesn't work. So no one wants to do it. What a beautiful thing it would be: to create a place where these patients can get better.
>
> [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) mentioned to me that some other members are discussing a center of some sort. It sounded like a week-to-monthlong place for mitos filling up their battery or establishing in El Salvador. He said I should talk to [@Laudy Cincotta](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/24082/) and [@India Rose Hopper](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29690/) about this, and also Tiffany (I don't know her username to tag her).
>

> Southeast AZ - beautiful, \*cheap,\* close, sunny.... Maybe an unmerciful winter.
> Florida - beautiful, chaotic, swampy, supposedly overcrowded & EMF'd, I've never set foot in it.
> Straight to El Salvador - again, I haven't yet been, and moving here would maybe create obstacles for a PA career right out of school.)
>
>
> I would be very eager to discuss the creation of such a psychiatric/mito center with those who are like-minded.

You are very bright, and I am sorry about your mental health obstacles, but it has also given you an insight into the human condition.

When I was investigating a while ago for alternative places to live in the West away from CA, Flagstaff was definitely on my list. It is at a high elevation compared to much of Arizona so more rain and snow in winter (and colder too), much of the area is forested, and it's a university town so would have reasonable amenities. I've not looked at it recently, but I'm sure there is 5G now, but there are plenty of rural areas nearby in the forests that would shield and have low population density. The light should be good (UV), although ubiquitination speeds up at elevation (7,000 ft above SL), however, I believe the geology (volcanic) would be good magnetism-wise (has a positive magnetic anomaly - see below).

[https://mrdata.usgs.gov/magnetic/map-us.html#home](https://mrdata.usgs.gov/magnetic/map-us.html#home)

I was searching back when I did not know of Uncle Jacks work, but I love the western US because of the wild, wide-open spaces, and scenery (red rocks and mountains). I lived in SLC, UT for a few years as a student and loved that location for the mountains, snow sports, and red rock desert. I spent time in New Mexico while working in the 1990s which I liked but that is very dry and dusty unless you live in the mountains. I just installed a NOS conventional router and took about 15 minutes to disable the wifi completely from the admin settings. When I went into the room the router was in to measure if the wifi was off or not my ear facing the router from about seven feet away immediately started hurting a level of 4 to 5 on a scale of 10 which surprised even me.

You can purchase a 100 or 200 foot ethernet cable to use internet without wifi outdoors.

I honestly don't know how anyone lives much less survives using wifi.

### solsearcher — 2025-06-30T21:44:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-to-the-soul-my-optimal-journal.30662/post-355553>

“I’d fly to you tomorrow, I’m not fighting in this war
I wanna drop my arms and take your arms and walk you to the shore”

At the beginning of this month, I graduated from PA school.
It was a nice celebration, with family near. But what really was that piece of paper that they handed me?
It was a means of entry into a strange place: a place that treats patients like numbers, a place where a pandemic stripped away a thin film, revealed true priorities — a place that still pretends otherwise. A healthcare without healing. A simulacrum of care.

How often I wanted to tear patients out of their hospital room or office visit. To let them know, “this isn’t all there is; there’s something you’re not being told. There exists healing for you.” Mito clinicians must feel this way daily. But we’re dealing with reality.

In my graduation robes, I carried two papers, one on my left, one on my right. They symbolize for me the two faces of medicine: harm and promise.

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img_20250604_090007271-jpg.31436/)
Left: [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9905027/](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC9905027/)
The harm. Protracted benzodiazepine withdrawal is one of the worst ways medicine can afflict someone. It can send someone to living hell. There is no treatment; in fact, there is no recognition that this condition even exists. There are no side effects counseling or black box warning (not that the patient would be told of those). Yet, these patients are blamed for the destruction of their very lives by the same psychiatrists who prescribed this harm.
We all have blinders - we only know what we see. Medicine only sees illness; then, it iatrogenically imposes its diagnosis back onto the patient: incurable, broken, other. Nowhere is this more common than psychiatry. Medication harms are brushed under the rug as new symptoms of a forged original diagnosis. “Oh well.”
Who are you to believe in healing?

[](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/img_20250604_085642421-jpg.31437/)
Right: [https://www.jidonline.org/article/S0022-202X(24)00280-X/fulltext](https://www.jidonline.org/article/S0022-202X\(24\)00280-X/fulltext)
The promise. There is a fraction of scientists and doctors waking up, putting their reputations on the line. Dr Weller lays out the sun’s benefits and the “sunlight quartet.” The paper pleads that this is way beyond vitamin D. It’s published literature - the language doctors should be able to hear. Yet, no centralized players, despite their integrity, will be heard. We just heard RFK promise wearables for every American, something \*he himself knows\* is harmful. Like Dr Kruse said: mitochondriac wisdom will be spread from decentralized clinicians to centralized patients. Those are the players with skin in the game. It will take an almost psychotic level of determination to go against this current.

Right after graduating, I boarded a flight to El Salvador, where I’ve been for 2 weeks. It’s been an amazing time - talking to mitos and meeting the people of this beautiful country. This weekend, I hiked the Volcano of Saint Michael. Shirtless all the way. I already want to do it again, just to sit on that magnificent slope.

I need to let nature teach me. I’m a slow learner, but I know it’s the only way.
My life’s next chapter will be defined by my mistakes and how I adapt.
I walked across the stage with these two papers. Someday I hope to honor their message.

### solsearcher — 2025-08-16T16:32:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-to-the-soul-my-optimal-journal.30662/post-358337>

Good morning from Lago de Ilopango.

I’ve really enjoyed my time in El Salvador. The conversations I’ve had already with the Mitos have been amazing, yes, even miraculous.

My purpose and intent to help psychiatric patients has been strengthened by my time here.

I’m still wrestling with nature, and finding my correct spot in it. I’m finding what works— not for everyone else, for me.

I’m here,
To be healed,
To help others heal.

Specifically for me, I had trouble sleeping during the rainstorms in Atami. Not from the noise. I’m weather-sensitive on the whole; like Rick Rubin says in that book, I can feel cloudy mornings before I look out the window. Maybe something to be healed, maybe something to be lived in. Time will tell.

Thankfully, El Salvador has a dry season for half the year. So my journey leads me to Peru for now, to a city of eternal sun. Arequipa. I have an idea of what I’ll find, but I’ll only know if I go.

To be continued

### solsearcher — 2026-02-07T00:24:52+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-to-the-soul-my-optimal-journal.30662/post-366455>

**Hypothesis of Healing**

Everybody’s question:
Ok, I’m in the tropics…
Why haven’t I gotten better?
I’ll lead you through my answer.

My issues:
Bipolar 2, severe
Fatigue/long COVID/vax injury, severe
MCAS, severe
Food/pill sensitivities, severe
Weather sensitivities, severe
Something messed up with the maxillary nerve

**Phase 1:
El Salvador**
2 months

Why didn’t I get better? **Sleep**. I was racked by insomnia nearly every night. I believe this was due to the strong thunderstorms affecting the lithium orotate medication in my body. I can’t understate how tired I was during nearly all of my time in El Salvador. To be clear: it was not from noise, it was from electric atmospheric changes.

I had whole-body itching during these thunderstorms as well. I believe this was MCAS exacerbation, due to either
\- storm -> lithium -> histamine
\- Or, the storm affecting histamine directly.

It makes me think of Jacks “FM antenna” from QE 47 \[1\]. He mentions how thunderstorms can disrupt FM radios. Well, I’m the radio. I’m extremely sensitive to things that should be harmless.

Also:
I was eating hardly any fatty fish. This was a big mistake that I delayed for too long.

Instead of waiting out the rainy season in this horrible insomnia, I left for:

**Phase 2:
Peru,** mountain peace
4 months

I moved to Arequipa, Peru, an incredibly sunny and beautiful volcanic city at 2,300m elevation, 16S latitude.

I chose Peru since it is one of the only tropical countries without intense rainstorms. (Or so I thought.)

I studied Spanish, had some huge setbacks with food, but eventually got well stabilized. Eventually I got to the best I’ve ever felt in my life. I had days where I said, I want every day of my life to feel exactly like this. I said, this is how I’m meant to feel.

However, I was still incredibly vulnerable to setbacks when they did happen.
I started out ignorant of my EMF exposure. Eventually, I asked our question: Why haven’t I gotten better?
So I started really trying to understand my EMF exposure. From electrohypersensitivity sufferers, I understand how big of a problem even small exposures are, and why Jack emphasizes it so hard \[2\]. What have we done to this planet and the life on it…

RF in my apartment was good, and magnetic fields were low.

Unfortunately, I believe the apartment has
\- high electric fields
\- high dirty electricity
Which have prevented and slowed my healing progress.
I only recently learned how big of a deal dirty electricity is \[3\] and how it’s linked to MCAS \[4\].
I don’t have meters to test it, either.

However, I was able to sleep very well here.
This confirms that the storms really were what affected me in El Salvador, even if EMFs were present.
It also means I can spend the dry season in tropical countries. But I don’t know how to handle the storms yet.

My peace came to a close when January rolled around, and the pristine weather of Arequipa became cloudy and stormy. Just like that, I was “back” in El Salvador. Sleeping horribly, getting whole body itching, extreme agitation, tightly clenched teeth, inability to concentrate. It was awful, and destroying my sleep, without which I just cannot function.

I feel for EHS people, because I’m avoiding the weather like they avoid EMFs.
In fact, Jack says EHS and MCAS are linked \[5\].

So I moved again, to:

**Phase 3:
Peru**, cracking down
3 weeks

I moved to the beach, where
\- it’s sunny
\- storms are tiny
\- there’s no hidden DE.
\- Sleeping on the sand keeps RF down.

Initially, this was like a godsend. A last resort rescue from the weather.

However, for a week, my sleep has been destroyed. I think it’s from messing with my lithium dose, plus some high RF exposure at bedtime, plus eating too much seedy fruit, plus….?

Via Negativa progression:
\- subtract winter: tropics
\- subtract storms: Peru
\- subtract a room and electricity: the beach.

“Lithium creates huge electric currents in cells. … The problem with lithium is that it can never reverse the disease.” \[1\]
“Lithium can be excitotoxic in demyelinating diseases … Use the lowest effective lithium dose to minimize UPE disruption” \[6\]

Now, I have been trying to “subtract” lithium. I’ve had some whole-body itching, right at bedtime. This further makes me believe that lithium was exacerbating, or controlling, histamine in my body. I don’t believe I can get to zero safely.

Is this what’s destroying my sleep? Can I safely decrease from 5mg to 2.5mg? Can I get way lower than that? Is there some way to both take lithium and sleep in these storms? Is lithium even the storm issue, or would I have insomnia regardless?
I don’t know.

I’ve increased my fatty fish a lot, and now have a metallic taste in my mouth from the cans. Omega 3 improves EHS, heavy metals worsen it. Am I getting heavy metal toxicity from eating mainly fish for 3 weeks? Is that affecting me?
I don’t know.

Why is my face so red and peeling even after so long? Is it EMF, or because of phenomelanin being phototoxic like Jacks been writing about? How long until I really tan? Have I failed to tan for months because I’ve been eating chocolate every day? \[8\]
I don’t know.

I know all of us ask these questions. A dizzying retrace of tiny triggers, pacing, wondering what we did wrong.

My improvements so far:
\- more omega 3, from fish not pills
\- Sun and grounding in the ocean
\- much lower EMF and better habits, awareness of ground currents, DE, RF exposure
\- 3 months of incredible stability and peace.

But I haven’t gained hardly any resilience.
I’m still confused about the invisible things that affect me.

My biggest concerns:
\- **mood and fatigue.** They fluctuate extremely with not just low UV winter, but also lunar phases and minor disturbances in sleep.
\- lithium stabilizes my bipolar 2, but probably “electrosensitizes” me to weather and EMF \[1, 7\]. Bad trade off. I don’t know how much to take, and I seem to pay a price when I change the dosage. Not in mood, but in fatigue and sleep. Jack notes that dosage is very hard to get right.
\- Ability to find a true EMF sanctuary, anywhere.
\- Some new problems, like fragile skin, chest pain.
\- extreme sensitivities and lack of resilience.

**How do I heal when I’m controlled by not just the sun, but clouds and the moon?**

The only hopes I see:
\- strict deuterium depletion \[1\]. I’ll start going more keto.
\- **strict, extreme EMF avoidance** \[1\]. Very hard. \[2\] **I know how important this is**. I want to get it locked in, but it’s going to take lots of planning and work. I don’t know how I could do it in Peru.
\- cold exposure
\- sleeping grounded
\- Cut out chocolate, since it might be hindering both melanin renovation \[see 1, 8\] and thiamine \[9\]. Easy.
\- Some medication out there. Something that has helped me as much as:
\- saturated fat
\- fatty fish
\- chocolate
\- lithium
\- thiamine (TTFD). (Jack notes that thiamine is great for EHS: \[10\].)
For example, something from **\[6\]:**
\- Valproic acid
\- T3
\- retinoic acid
\- antioxidants
\- clemastine
\- metformin
\- vaptans….
Clemastine is an antihistamine, which Jack says to avoid for melanin renovation. Vaptans are expensive and hard to get.

And my last hope…
Time.

I need to know what will work.

I’d be eager to hear from [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) on this, or anyone else who’s had similar issues or healing.

### solsearcher — 2026-02-07T13:32:34+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-to-the-soul-my-optimal-journal.30662/post-366469>

**A timely example:**
Last night, after I wrote the above, the sun set. I watched it in the ocean. It was so peaceful.
Then, while trying to fall asleep, I went into whole-body itching again. Not torturous, but bad enough that I couldn’t fall asleep. It was lasting too long also… after 40 minutes I’d had enough. So I walked over to the ocean and put my feet and hands in.
It disappeared immediately!
I held them in for 20 minutes more.
The alleviation lasted for a while. Then, itching came back — but less intense. I was able to sleep. I woke up groggy — not bone-tired, but not good.

Why did this happen?
Storms? No…
ALAN / EMF past sunset? No…
Delayed effects of RF exposure from everyone in the bus? Maybe…
Delayed effects (3d later) of messing with lithium dose? Maybe…
Not enough sun yesterday (~3.5hrs)? Maybe…

I won’t always have an ocean nearby.

Hi mate,

Low UV, cold, grey clouds, rain etc = I feel terrible mentally
Consistent warmer weather, sunny without drastic pressure changes etc and I feel much better.

What I'm not 100% certain on yet is the thunderstorms during the wet season in a tropical area. Rain/clouds/pressure changes always affect me but seem much worst in winter, I haven't lived in the tropics yet and am unsure how it would affect me with the higher UV, humidity.

### solsearcher — 2026-02-23T20:06:16+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-to-the-soul-my-optimal-journal.30662/post-367479>

**A Break in the Clouds:**

Praise God, I'm back in Arequipa now. I have slept through two week of storms, loud ones with lightning, without any of the previous symptoms. Zero. None at all. Sleeping like a baby.
Why? Why can I handle it now when I couldn't only one month ago? Why don't I have whole body itching, insomnia, that sense of unexplainable "tightness." What did I do differently?

Four main things:
1\. Slept on the sand for 1mo, away from the electrical grid. When I returned, I turned off the breaker = lower E and DE fields (bad for MCAS \[1\]). I can definitely feel the difference between on vs off, within a few minutes. This aligns with what others say about how acutely toxic dirty electricity is \[2\].
2\. Ate many sardines for 1mo (good for MCAS \[3\] and bipolar).
3\. Lowered lithium from 5mg to 2.5mg (see previous posts).
4\. Stopped chocolate and fruit 1 week before returning (deuterium, \[4\]).

I feel that all of these played a part, especially lowering lithium. If it worsens MCAS & other issues like Jack has said, I probably had been taking too much. Like I mentioned above, I got some whole-body itching on the taper as well.

I take this as a major victory. I prayed and prayed for this crazy symptom to go away, and it has. Being afraid of the weather itself.... It has given me a new sympathy for people in similarly fragile states. I am overjoyed to be restored. I will not take this for granted again, nor the gift of having a roof over my head.

The same view above, the next morning:

### solsearcher — 2026-03-26T19:09:21+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/windows-to-the-soul-my-optimal-journal.30662/post-369680>

**Quantum Surf n Turf
AKA
The Sardine Prescription**

Let’s be honest, you are not eating enough seafood.

Maybe it’s too expensive, or hard to get, or you got bad food poisoning that one time. Seafood is a deep healing tool that many of us have ignored. And our omega 3 indexes have a long way to go to max out.

Thankfully, sardines are one of the best seafood options. Tons of omega-3. Cheap. Everywhere.

Just one problem: they’re a little… room temperature. Maybe we can make them a little more exciting.

Quantum Surf ‘n’ Turf:
1\. Turn pan on. Add butter.
2\. Add aromatics and veggies. Stir.
3\. Add small piece of steak. Flip. Set aside.
4\. Add water or broth - deglaze; add a little extra liquid. Lower heat.
5\. Add sardines, cover. Gently warm them in the liquid.
6\. Add finishing touches. A pad of compound butter to the steak. Remove from heat and serve.

Why this way?
\- Gently warming sardines in deglazing liquid prevents delicate omega 3 destruction.
\- More sardines, less steak lowers expenses and glyphosate exposure through beef.
\- Compound butter gives you flavor freedom and variety.

You can now eat sardines to your heart’s content.

Comment if you made it.

For more info see:

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-10T00:22:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nafld-is-a-warburg-shifted-disease-linked-to-nnemf-and-blue-light.30663/post-350389>

New podcast out with Dr. Bronson an internal medicine MD member of mine.

The Universe Is Indifferent: Your Will Isn’t, and Centralized Medicine’s Lies Must End
The MAHA movement’s food-first approach, while well-intentioned, misses the mark because it fails to address the photo bioelectrical nature of life a truth buried by the Rockefeller-driven centralized medical system that turned sickness into a subscription model, hospitals into businesses, and doctors into salesmen, all while banning natural cures like sunlight exposure.

Chronic disease is not a nutritional problem; it’s a light problem. Conditions like pterygium, dry macular degeneration, and NAFLD driven by the Bazan Effect where DHA destruction in the retina leads to systemic lipid dysregulation stem from our failure to align with nature’s signals, starting with sunrise. By realigning with these rhythms, we can restore our mitochondrial health, support the TCA cycle, and mitigate the paramagnetic flip that drives modern illness.

Yet, the most damning indictment of centralized medicine lies in its insidious indoctrination of both patients and doctors, who believe they’re following “science” when, as the slide starkly reveals, they’re actually listening to paid promotional ads by trillion-dollar industries that make up their own science—science that benefits them, not you. This deception runs so deep that even educated MDs think they’re being objective, when nothing could be further from the truth: take non-alcoholic fatty liver disease (NAFLD), where blue light and nnEMF shift heme proteins in the liver to the Fe³⁺ oxidation state, shutting down the TCA cycle and preventing fat burning, leaving fats sequestered in the liver.

Yet Big Pharma pushes drugs for this condition, knowing full well that no MD will ever tell you how light affects iron’s oxidation state, because this fundamental truth was deliberately excluded from medical school curriculums by the very industries that profit from your ignorance. The universe is indifferent; your will isn’t. That’s the wisdom that turns thorns into a throne. Time to decide what you’ll settle for. Mitochondriacs—those who prioritize their mitochondrial health by embracing sunlight and rejecting centralized dogma—never settle for less than they deserve. They understand that nature’s electrical fabric is the key to thriving, not just surviving. So, step into the sunrise, reclaim your health, and build your throne from the thorns of a world that’s forgotten nature’s wisdom, casting aside the lies of a centralized medical system that has betrayed us all. Only then can we truly make America—and the world—healthy again, free from the control of a system that has buried nature’s recipes and led to at least 50 catastrophic errors in modern healthcare. That will be covered soon on my blog.

Got myself some 5ppm Litewater Brand DDW. Have been using GPT to help me titrate the dose.

Current context: Brain fog, leaky gut, autoimmune-like symptoms, EMF sensitivity, low vitamin D (25 ng/mL), prior prediabetes, mitochondrial redox dysfunction, and a carnivore diet — consuming **Litewater (5 ppm deuterium-depleted water)** can be a powerful adjunct if used strategically.

Here’s how to optimize its use for **redox restoration, mitochondrial health, and gut repair**:

* * *

### ✅ **Dosing Guidelines**​

Because **Litewater is expensive**, the goal is not to drink it exclusively, but to **lower your body's deuterium burden over time**, especially during your **mitochondrial repair window (nighttime)**.

#### **1\. Dilution Protocol**​

You don’t need to drink it straight unless you’re targeting extreme depletion. Instead, mix to achieve these target deuterium levels:

| Desired Deuterium Level | Ratio (Litewater : Regular Water) |
| --- | --- |
| ~80–90 ppm (therapeutic) | 1 : 1.5 or 1 : 2 |
| ~105 ppm (mild support) | 1 : 4 or 1 : 5 |

**Example (1:2 ratio)**:
Mix 1 liter Litewater with 2 liters of spring water (or low-DDW mineral water like Icelandic Glacial or Pürica).

* * *

### **Timing Strategy**​

#### 1\. **Morning**​

-   Drink **250–500 ml** of your diluted DDW first thing in the morning **before food**.
-   This supports **mitochondrial uncoupling and autophagy** when your NAD+/redox status is highest.

#### 2\. **Evening (Pre-Sleep)**​

-   Another **250–500 ml** before bed.
-   This is when **mitochondrial repair and melatonin-driven uncoupling** peak. Drinking DDW here may enhance **deep sleep, glymphatic drainage, and DNA repair**.

* * *

### ⚙️ **Stack With These Protocols for Synergy**​

Since you already use some of these:

| Protocol | How It Helps | Stack With DDW |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **Morning Sunlight** | Increases exclusion zone (EZ) water, enhances ATP synthase function | Drink DDW before/after sun |
| **Red/NIR Light** | Activates cytochrome c oxidase, promotes DDW clearance | Drink 15–30 min before |
| **Cold Exposure** | Enhances fat-burning, lowers deuterium, boosts mitochondrial uncoupling | Stack before/after CT |
| **Ketosis (Carnivore)** | Produces more DDW endogenously, lowers metabolic water deuterium | Amplifies DDW impact |
| **Magnetico Sleep Pad** | Increases mitochondrial spin rate, supports H+ tunneling | Use with bedtime DDW dose |

* * *

### ⚠️ Cautions​

-   **Don’t use chlorinated tap water** to mix with DDW. Use spring or distilled.
-   **Avoid excessive dilution** if your symptoms worsen; DDW can mobilize toxins and ROS during repair.
-   **Monitor changes in sleep, cognition, and gut** — positive shifts indicate mitochondrial response.

* * *

### Suggested Plan (First 4 Weeks)​

| Week | Litewater Usage | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 1–2 | 1:2 dilution, ~500 ml/day (split AM/PM) | Monitor gut, sleep, energy |
| 3–4 | 1:1.5 dilution, up to 750–1000 ml/day | Use with red light and cold |

If you notice improvement (especially in **sleep depth, neurological clarity, gut function**), consider a **more aggressive cycle** with lower ppm (closer to pure Litewater) for 7–10 days every few months.

Been using GPT mode in project mode, dissecting blogs. Any thoughts or suggestions?

I have decided to do 1 : 3 ratio at start (115PPM)

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-07-08T03:36:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deterium-depleted-water.30664/post-355970>

Did you do a saliva test before and after, trying to get an objective measurement of deuterium content in your body?

90 bottles sounds like a lot, and maybe they were liter bottles, maybe not, but it takes 3-4 months for red blood cells to be replaced, other cells much longer.

I recall when I interviewed Dr Seyfried for a podcast I recall telling him that DDW increased OS (Overall Survival) time in cancer patients by 400%, and his response was they got some "Tritium water" (doesn't sound like he knows the difference between radioactive tritium and non-radioactive deuterium) and drank some and didn't tell a difference, either.

For those with S4 cancer, being told your OS can go from a few months to a few years is a big difference, and when combined with more sunshine, a Paleo diet, and maybe some reposed drugs like IVM & FBZ, one could potentially see even greater OS.

### Kolo — 2025-07-10T04:52:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deterium-depleted-water.30664/post-356099>

I drink it all day in my Nalgene bottle, about 3:1 and sometimes 2.5:1. Have noticed an increase in general energy and clarity of thought, especially in the evening. I follow a Ketovore diet. I was battling with Adrenal Fatigue(issues in the PVN area) and have noticed that it is harder to sleep when drinking DDW. I am also doing about 15minutes per day CT at 13degrees , full submersion up to the neck.

Will try to not drink it before bed, and switch to regular filtered water and report results, but I and my girlfriend are both noticing an increase an energy while sipping on it.

To be continued....

Thanks Anthony. I've been on Jack's protocol about 5 1/2 months, adding Dr. Marik's IVM & FBZ as the only re-purposed drugs, at 2x the normal recommendations of 2.5 mg/kg body weight (IVM) and 1000 mg liquid FBZ (as per Joe Tippens), along with DDW, max sunlight exposure, morning, noon, and late day, Paleo diet with 2-3 dozen oysters/week, only seafood for protein, otherwise, low carb, locally-sourced vegetables from farmer's market, green tea, and with family and my 2 dogs, which I very much count as family.

Being close to God has been the greatest part of the past 3 years, as I have repeated the oft quoted saying "there aren't any atheists in a foxhole during war".

Always going for Jack's "A" recommendations, with deep consideration, always, which includes studying, listening to podcasts and following PubMed links, taking notes, and passing on what I learn to other cancer patients. Doing my best to treat other cancer patients as I would my own family.

Being so very grateful for so many who tell me they are praying for me, such as yourself Anthony. Realizing we are all "brothers by different mothers."

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-10T22:27:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deterium-depleted-water.30664/post-356125>

HOW DOES DDW AND KERATOCONUS MINGLE?

A new blog post is available today on keratoconus. I promise that what you'll read here is not available anywhere else on this disease, which ophthalmologists still struggle to understand and solve. I used light to do it.

In this blog, you'll see how I cured two of my patients of this disease.

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-61-124956764](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-61-124956764)

How did I come up with new ideas to solve Keratoconus? It was through my studies on narcolepsy in 2005. The discovery that an orexin receptor mutation causes the sleep disorder canine narcolepsy in Doberman Pinschers indicated a significant role for this system in sleep regulation. Narcolepsy is a REM sleep disorder. Additionally, genetic knockout mice lacking the orexin gene were also reported to exhibit narcolepsy.

In humans, narcolepsy is associated with a specific variant of the human leukocyte antigen (HLA) complex.

A lack of either red light or UV light is believed to be a contributing factor to the onset of narcolepsy. Does the same principle apply to eye diseases and collagen vascular diseases?

Today, all of them begin with poor light signaling in the prism of the retina. Light changes the charge density of the retina. The proof is found in genome-wide analysis studies that show that, in addition to the HLA variant, people with narcolepsy also exhibit a specific genetic mutation in the T-cell receptor alpha locus. I spoke about the HLA complex in the brain-gut series when I explained how the chimp brain evolved into the homo sapien brain using the HLA complex. Orexins helped fuel the transition. They are active in this eye disease, too.

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-07-18T03:18:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deterium-depleted-water.30664/post-356518>

Results

### Impression​

Multicentric disease with numerous increasing sites as annotated above, favor progressive disease comparing current study to 04/22/2025. However also noted are several stable to slightly improving lesions with this mixed response. No leptomeningeal tumor and no intramedullary cord tumor. ACTIONABLE ITEMS/RECOMMENDATIONS\*: None. \*An Actionable Finding is a finding that may be unrelated to the original reason for imaging but potentially actionable, meaning further investigation may be necessary. The Actionable Findings Vigilance Unit (AFVU) assists medical providers with responding to additional radiologic findings that are unexpected and potentially actionable.

### Narrative​

FULL RESULT: Examination: MRI CERVICAL THORACIC LUMBAR SPINE W WO CONTRAST on 7/14/2025 5:30 PM. CLINICAL HISTORY: Metastatic malignant neoplasm to vertebral column, adenocarcinoma lung INDICATION: eval for disease, radiotherapy S1-S2 radiotherapy T2 01/17/2025, radiotherapy L2 6-14- 2024, radiotherapy T7-T8 - T11-T12 06/14/2024, COMPARISON: MR spine 04/22/2025 MR spine 12/17/2024 CT chest abdomen pelvis 07/14/2025 PET/CT 04/22/2025. TECHNIQUE: MRI of the cervical, thoracic and lumbosacral spine without and with IV contrast was performed. FINDINGS: SPINE COUNT: No transitional anatomy. POSTTREATMENT FINDINGS: None. NEOPLASTIC FINDINGS: Intradural neoplastic findings: T2 signal defects in the left cerebellum series 14 image 1 stable and unchanged from prior study going back to 02/07/2025 most consistent with prior injury or infarct. No evidence of malignancy, no leptomeningeal tumor and no intramedullary cord tumor. Extradural neoplastic findings: Multiple lesions throughout the cervical thoracic lumbosacral spine both ilia with the site of dated radiotherapy as noted above consistent with the multicentric marrow-based disease and metastasis. When comparing current study to 04/22/2025 there are multiple sites of increasing enhancement. C2 series 41 image 16, C5 series 41 image 41, T6 series 39 image 52 measures 27 mm current study compared to 17 mm 04/22/2025, T12 several new small nodules series 38 image 67, L3 current measurement 17 x 10 mm series 36 image 52 compared to 6 x 7 mm 04/22/2025, L5 increasing involvement measuring 18 x 45 mm current study series 36 image 87 compared to 2 smaller nodules 04/22/2025, increasing right posterior ilium current study series 36 image 77, new midline S1-S2 lesion series 36 image 81 and new/increasing marrow-based lesions in both the right and left acetabulum series 36 image 97. In addition there are multiple lesions are stable to slightly improved S3 T1, T2, T4, T7, T8, T11, L1 and right S1. With multiplicity of lesions many are increasing as annotated above others are stable to also has been some tumor treatment response but the overall effect is progression in many of these sites as noted. Slightly improved but with the dominant feature being increasing, favor progression when comparing current study to 04/22/2025. Multiple endplate irregularities disease with endplate remodeling and pathologic response but no significant anterior wedging or retrolisthesis. No epidural disease. NON-NEOPLASTIC FINDINGS: Alignment: Maintained. Degenerative: No significant degenerative spinal canal or neural foraminal narrowing. Other fractures: No acute non-pathological fracture. Visualized spinal cord: Unremarkable. Location of conus medullaris: L1. OTHER PERTINENT POSITIVE AND NEGATIVE FINDINGS: None.

## Results​

### Impression​

1\. Stable paramediastinal lower lobe post radiation scarring changes. No acute disease. 2. Progression in size of the primary malignancy in the medial left upper lobe. Increased size of additional left upper lobe nodule concerning for active metastatic growth. 3.Progression of of nodal disease suspected in the left prevascular station, left lower paratracheal station, left hilum and porta hepatis region 4. Progression of multiple liver metastases. Part of the areas of hypoattenuation in the liver parenchyma could be explained by radiation therapy to the nearby spine. 5. Overall the multiple predominantly sclerotic but also some lytic lesions throughout the skeleton have not significantly changed. ACTIONABLE ITEMS/RECOMMENDATIONS\*: None. \*An Actionable Finding is a finding that may be unrelated to the original reason for imaging but potentially actionable, meaning further investigation may be necessary. The Actionable Findings Vigilance Unit (AFVU) assists medical providers with responding to additional radiologic findings that are unexpected and potentially actionable.

### Narrative​

FULL RESULT: Examination: CT CHEST ABDOMEN PELVIS W CONTRAST on 7/14/2025 8:34 AM. Clinical History: Adenocarcinoma of lung <Unspecified side>. Indication: Cancer staging or restaging, Adenocarcinoma of the lung. Comparison: PET/CT 4/22/2025, 2/13/2025. Outside CT chest abdomen and pelvis 9/15/2023 Technique: CT CHEST ABDOMEN PELVIS W CONTRAST. Findings: Lungs/Airways/Pleura: The lung volumes are within normal limits. There are moderate irregular patchy airspace opacities in the paramediastinal lower lobes. The irregular lobulated nodule in the medial left upper lobe abutting the mediastinal pleura, is currently about 2.5 x 2.3 cm, previously 1.6 x 2.2 cm There is an increased 9 mm nodule image 38 series 302, previously less than 5 mm. Occasional additional less than 4 mm nodules annotated on series 302, not significantly changed since 9/15/2023. Lymph Nodes: Increased ill-defined left lower paratracheal node up to 1.5 cm in short axis, previously not visualized. One of the left prevascular nodes on image 31 series 301 appears to be larger up to 11 mm, previously subcentimeter. The rest of the adjacent nodes up to 9-2 mm, are stable. Increased conspicuity of left hilar nodes up to 11 mm in short axis. Development of lymphadenopathy in the porta hepatis region up to 1.5 cm in short axis Subcentimeter multi-compartmental retroperitoneal nodes, not significantly changed. There fat-containing external iliac nodes, also stable Neck/Mediastinum/Heart: No thyroid lesions detected. The central airways are unremarkable. The esophagus is normal in appearance. The heart size is normal. Unremarkable pericardium. Moderate to severe calcified predominant atherosclerotic plaque of the aorta including the iliac branches. Moderate to severe atherosclerotic calcifications of the coronary vessels. Abdomen and Pelvis: Liver: Heterogeneous attenuation of the liver parenchyma, in the background especially in segments 4A and 4B. There are also multiple additional and/or larger multi segmental right liver lobe lesions, significantly more prominent and more numerous than in the previous study. Gallbladder: No lesions detected Pancreas: No lesions identified Spleen: Normal size. No focal lesions. Adrenal glands: Grossly unchanged right adrenal nodule measuring about 1.7 x 1.2 cm, probably an adenoma given lack of FDG activity on prior PET/CT studies. Mild nodularity of the left adrenal gland is grossly anatomically stable. Kidneys: No focal lesions. No hydronephrosis Bowel: No lesions identified. No ascites. Pelvic organs: Partially distended bladder. Post-prostatectomy changes Bones/Soft Tissues: Multiple sclerotic predominant lesions throughout the axial and appendicular skeleton. The T7-T8 lesions are anatomically stable. The T7 vertebroplasty changes appear stable. Increased conspicuity of the cement at the T8 vertebroplasty site noted in the interval. Additional scattered spinal lesions especially at T2, T11 including posterior elements, T12, and essentially all of the lumbar spine vertebral bodies except perhaps for L3 Lytic and sclerotic lesions in the pelvic ring are also again seen, some of them or FDG avid, anatomically stable. A few focal lucencies are also noted, apparently not FDG avid, for example in the left iliac wing, and right scapula, nonspecific. Sclerotic lesion at the lateral 7-9 left ribs, as noted in prior studies as far back as 2023 Mild right greater than left gynecomastia

## Results​

Echocardiographic Report Interpretation Summary A complete two-dimensional transthoracic echocardiogram was performed (2D, M-mode, Spectral and color Doppler). The study was technically adequate. Compared to prior study, there is no significant change. Normal left ventricular size and systolic function. LV ejection fraction calculated using the bi-plane method of disks is 65 %. The right ventricle is normal in size and function. Right ventricular systolic pressure is normal. There is no pericardial effusion. Left Ventricle: Normal left ventricular size and systolic function. There is normal left ventricular wall thickness. LV ejection fraction calculated using the bi-plane method of disks is 65 %. No regional wall motion abnormalities noted. I WMSI = 1.00 % Normal = 100 Normal GLS (-20.9%) X - Cannot 1 - Normal 2 - 3 - Akinetic 4 - Dyskinetic Interpret Hypokinetic 5 - Aneurysmal 3D imaging: 3D volumes were not performed in this study. Cardiac Mechanics/Speckle Tracking Imaging: Normal global longitudinal peak systolic value. Strain Imaging was performed; GLPS avg = -20.9%. Diastology: LV relaxation appears normal. Right Ventricle: The right ventricle is normal in size and function. Atria: Atria are normal in size. Mitral Valve: Mitral annular calcification is present. There is no mitral valve stenosis. There is trace mitral regurgitation. Tricuspid Valve: There is trace tricuspid regurgitation. Estimated RVSP is 25-30mmHg. Right ventricular systolic pressure is normal. Aortic Valve: The aortic valve is trileaflet. No valvular aortic stenosis. No aortic regurgitation is present. Pulmonic Valve: The pulmonic valve is not well visualized. Great Vessels: The aortic root is normal size. The inferior vena cava demonstrates normal size and normal respiratory variation. Pericardium/Pleural: There is no pericardial effusion. My medcon had a singular, forceful recommendation for me to get back on Osimerniytab (Tagrisso), or chem + immunotherapy. I refused both. My long term labs show Tagrisso lowered my platelet, compromised my immune system which caused ne to get off Tagrisso months ago, seeing my platelets go back to low normal tells me all I need to know. I askw=ed he to help me raise my ALC (Absolute Lymphocyte Count). Using Anktiva. She refused. Returning to Shreveport, I decided to continue as I was with the addition of a 5% DMSO solution to mix with my liquid IVM and FBZ, and apply same to newly identified tumors. especially cervical, 9th left rib, Lumbar area, thoracic area. Ding that for 2 days, I already have less psin

I have been trying to find a physician that can get Anktiva, as I meet the conditions for Lympopenia'

[https://ir.immunitybio.com/news-releases/news-release-details/connecting-dots-anktivas-triangle-offense-deep-dive-dr-patrick?field\_nir\_news\_date\_value%5Bmin%5D=](https://ir.immunitybio.com/news-releases/news-release-details/connecting-dots-anktivas-triangle-offense-deep-dive-dr-patrick?field_nir_news_date_value%5Bmin%5D=)

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-07-18T03:20:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/deterium-depleted-water.30664/post-356519>

> [Stuart Briscoe said:](/goto/post?id=356518)
>
> Results
>
>
> ### Impression​
>
> Multicentric disease with numerous increasing sites as annotated above, favor progressive disease comparing current study to 04/22/2025. However also noted are several stable to slightly improving lesions with this mixed response. No leptomeningeal tumor and no intramedullary cord tumor. ACTIONABLE ITEMS/RECOMMENDATIONS\*: None. \*An Actionable Finding is a finding that may be unrelated to the original reason for imaging but potentially actionable, meaning further investigation may be necessary. The Actionable Findings Vigilance Unit (AFVU) assists medical providers with responding to additional radiologic findings that are unexpected and potentially actionable.
>
> ### Narrative​
>
> FULL RESULT: Examination: MRI CERVICAL THORACIC LUMBAR SPINE W WO CONTRAST on 7/14/2025 5:30 PM. CLINICAL HISTORY: Metastatic malignant neoplasm to vertebral column, adenocarcinoma lung INDICATION: eval for disease, radiotherapy S1-S2 radiotherapy T2 01/17/2025, radiotherapy L2 6-14- 2024, radiotherapy T7-T8 - T11-T12 06/14/2024, COMPARISON: MR spine 04/22/2025 MR spine 12/17/2024 CT chest abdomen pelvis 07/14/2025 PET/CT 04/22/2025. TECHNIQUE: MRI of the cervical, thoracic and lumbosacral spine without and with IV contrast was performed. FINDINGS: SPINE COUNT: No transitional anatomy. POSTTREATMENT FINDINGS: None. NEOPLASTIC FINDINGS: Intradural neoplastic findings: T2 signal defects in the left cerebellum series 14 image 1 stable and unchanged from prior study going back to 02/07/2025 most consistent with prior injury or infarct. No evidence of malignancy, no leptomeningeal tumor and no intramedullary cord tumor. Extradural neoplastic findings: Multiple lesions throughout the cervical thoracic lumbosacral spine both ilia with the site of dated radiotherapy as noted above consistent with the multicentric marrow-based disease and metastasis. When comparing current study to 04/22/2025 there are multiple sites of increasing enhancement. C2 series 41 image 16, C5 series 41 image 41, T6 series 39 image 52 measures 27 mm current study compared to 17 mm 04/22/2025, T12 several new small nodules series 38 image 67, L3 current measurement 17 x 10 mm series 36 image 52 compared to 6 x 7 mm 04/22/2025, L5 increasing involvement measuring 18 x 45 mm current study series 36 image 87 compared to 2 smaller nodules 04/22/2025, increasing right posterior ilium current study series 36 image 77, new midline S1-S2 lesion series 36 image 81 and new/increasing marrow-based lesions in both the right and left acetabulum series 36 image 97. In addition there are multiple lesions are stable to slightly improved S3 T1, T2, T4, T7, T8, T11, L1 and right S1. With multiplicity of lesions many are increasing as annotated above others are stable to also has been some tumor treatment response but the overall effect is progression in many of these sites as noted. Slightly improved but with the dominant feature being increasing, favor progression when comparing current study to 04/22/2025. Multiple endplate irregularities disease with endplate remodeling and pathologic response but no significant anterior wedging or retrolisthesis. No epidural disease. NON-NEOPLASTIC FINDINGS: Alignment: Maintained. Degenerative: No significant degenerative spinal canal or neural foraminal narrowing. Other fractures: No acute non-pathological fracture. Visualized spinal cord: Unremarkable. Location of conus medullaris: L1. OTHER PERTINENT POSITIVE AND NEGATIVE FINDINGS: None.
>
>
> ## Results​
>
> ### Impression​
>
> 1\. Stable paramediastinal lower lobe post radiation scarring changes. No acute disease. 2. Progression in size of the primary malignancy in the medial left upper lobe. Increased size of additional left upper lobe nodule concerning for active metastatic growth. 3.Progression of of nodal disease suspected in the left prevascular station, left lower paratracheal station, left hilum and porta hepatis region 4. Progression of multiple liver metastases. Part of the areas of hypoattenuation in the liver parenchyma could be explained by radiation therapy to the nearby spine. 5. Overall the multiple predominantly sclerotic but also some lytic lesions throughout the skeleton have not significantly changed. ACTIONABLE ITEMS/RECOMMENDATIONS\*: None. \*An Actionable Finding is a finding that may be unrelated to the original reason for imaging but potentially actionable, meaning further investigation may be necessary. The Actionable Findings Vigilance Unit (AFVU) assists medical providers with responding to additional radiologic findings that are unexpected and potentially actionable.

Value

90

The eGFRcr is calculated with the 2021 CKD-EPI creatinine equation using creatinine, patient's age, and sex for adults 18 years of age and older. Other factors, especially muscle mass, may affect accuracy and need to be considered. According to the Kidney Disease: Improving Global Outcomes (KDIGO) CKD Work Group 2012 Clinical Practice Guideline, chronic kidney disease (CKD) is defined as the abnormalities of kidney structure or function, present for more than 3 months, with implications for health. CKD should be classified by cause, GFR category, and albuminuria category. KDIGO guidelines provide the following GFR categories. Stage / Description / GFR mL/min/1.73 m2: G1\* / Normal or high / >= 90 G2\* / Mildly decreased / 60-89 G3a / Mildly to moderately decreased / 45-59 G3b / Moderately to severely decreased / 30-44 G4 / Severely decreased / 15-29 G5 / Kidney failure / <15 \*In the absence of evidence of kidney damage, neither G1 nor G2 fulfill criteria for CKD.

Within the past ten days, I have been diagnosed with lung cancer (complete biopsy results still pending) with a second mass in the bone at the T9 vertebrae. This is all coming at my very quickly and I have a lot of radicular pain from the vertebral mass pressing on nerve root leaving the spine. I've had severe back and radicular pain for a month or so, which is getting worse.

It sounds like you have a lot of support for what you're going through and how you're managing it. I feel I am at a crossroad between taking the centralized approach to my cancer, which is strongly recommended by my husband, a centralized hospitalist, or pursuing a decentralized path. Pain is a real issue right now, but I've discovered that opiates really don't alleviate pain for what I've got.

# Thread: The Human Viral Spark
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-viral-spark.30670/>

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-11T06:04:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-viral-spark.30670/post-350442>

**The Viral Spark: How Decentralized Biophysics and Viruses Shaped Human Evolution**
What if viruses aren’t just threats but the raw materials of life itself? My photo-bioelectric hypothesis posits that humans evolved not despite viruses but because of them, orchestrated by the decentralized dance of mitochondrial thermodynamics and environmental light. Let’s explore how viral pandemics, far from mere scourges, signal nature’s ancient blueprint for genetic novelty and why humans were built to harness this chaos.

**Viruses as Life’s Building Blocks**
Forget the old dogma that genes evolve solely from ancestral templates. Recent research reveals “orphan genes”, with no clear predecessors emerge from junk DNA, often virally derived. A single drop of seawater teems with 10 million viruses, nature’s spare parts waiting to be shuffled. I’ve long argued viruses are life’s raw material, integrated into DNA via mitochondrial quantum probability. When the K-T asteroid slammed the Yucatan 66 million years ago (Factor X), it didn’t just wipe out dinosaurs, it reset Earth’s stage, amplifying viral assimilation in surviving primates.

In the East African Rift Zone, 2.5 million years ago, tectonic shifts isolated apes in a viral-rich coastal crucible. Here, transitional hominids like Lucy and Ardi didn’t just survive they thrived by absorbing viruses without succumbing. Why? Their mitochondria, acting as thermodynamic casinos, used reactive oxygen species (ROS) via the perforin/granzyme pathway to epigenetically rewire DNA. This wasn’t random luck but a decentralized program: sunlight (UV-A/IR) optimized cytochrome c oxidase, hydrating melanin and producing deuterium-depleted water (DDW), keeping electrical resistance (éR) low and mtDNA stable. Viruses, far from foes, supplied genetic novelty—new genes for brain expansion, bipedalism, and resilience.

**The Perforin Pathway: Mitochondria’s Viral Choreographer**
Mitochondria don’t just power cells they orchestrate viral integration. The perforin/granzyme system, a mitochondrial ROS-driven apoptosis pathway, decides which cells live or die during viral encounters. Granzyme B activates pro-apoptotic proteins (Bid, Bax/Bak), releasing cytochrome c to trigger caspase cascades, fragmenting DNA or reshaping it. In healthy mitochondria, bathed in solar EMF, this process is precise—viral DNA integrates constructively, birthing genes for adaptation. But in modern humans, nnEMF and blue light dehydrate melanin, spike éR, and disrupt this dance, turning ROS into a cytokine storm. COVID-19’s heme damage high LDH, ferritin, low procalcitonin reveals this: viruses still target cytochrome c oxidase, but our tech-soaked cells can’t cope, fueling pandemics.

**Why Humans Embrace Viruses**
Nature built us virus-friendly not to suffer but to evolve. The K-T event’s climate and light shifts, amplified by Rift Zone geology, forced primates to lean on viral “marketing”—not today’s social media hype, but DNA’s original ad campaign. Mitochondria, using light-driven thermodynamics, sorted viral spare parts into new genes, sculpting Homo sapiens from ape stock in a blink (evolutionarily speaking). Our impotence against pandemics isn’t failure of human biology, instead it’s a legacy of openness to genetic flux, now misfiring in nnEMF-saturated cities where mtDNA mutates 15–20 times faster than nuclear DNA.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

**Decentralized Biophysics: The Real Hero**
This isn’t a centralized tale of mutations trudging forward. Human evolution is decentralized, mitochondria, guided by sunlight, water, and viral chaos, call the shots. The same system that birthed us makes pandemics a grim reminder: stray from nature’s light (UV-A/IR), and éR rises, Warburg metabolism kicks in, and viruses turn deadly. Yet, there is always a seed of hope in tragedy. By realigning with solar EMF, sunrise gazing, red light therapy, nnEMF avoidance, we can restore DDW, tame ROS, and maybe even harness viruses again for health, not harm.

A Good Sign in Pandemics? They’re nature’s nudge to return to our roots. Viruses aren’t mistakes, they’re evolution’s spark, and our mitochondria know how to wield them, if only we let the sun lead.
