### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-11T06:16:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-viral-spark.30670/post-350443>

Is there a good sign to viral pandemics? Have you ever thought about why evolution/GOD innovated viruses? Might it be how the building blocks of genes are created?
Have you ever asked yourself where do new genes come from?

In their search for sources of genetic novelty, researchers find that some “orphan genes” with no obvious ancestors evolve out of junk DNA, contrary to old paradigm assumptions.

I wrote a blog long ago that I believed human genes came from viral marketing. I believe viruses are the raw material of life. They just need to be organized and shuffled by native and nnEMF on Earth. In this analogy Earth should be seen as the EMF casino. Genes are the deck of cards. It is in the casino deck of mitochondrial thermodynamics to get their marching orders.

People forget Earth has been loaded with nnEMF terrestrially for 4 billion years. This is why ancients still got cateracts and Egyptian mummies got modern chronic disease in their temples when they did not spend time in the sun.

Things you do not know? In 2015, Cleo Loi, an undergraduate at the University of Sydney, used the Murchison Widefield Array radio telescope to provide the first visual proof of giant plasma tubes in Earth's magnetosphere. They have been present for 4 billion years delivering nnEMF to Earth below changing what is possible in biology.

Key Details of the Discovery Location: The tubes were identified approximately 600 kilometers above Earth in the upper ionosphere, extending into the plasmasphere. Technique: Loi split the radio telescope's signal into "left" and "right" sets, mimicking human stereoscopic vision to map the structures in 3D for the first time.

Significance: While theorized for over 60 years, confirming these structures is vital because they cause signal distortions that interfere with civilian and military satellite-based navigation systems. For this research, Loi was awarded the 2015 Bok Prize by the Astronomical Society of Australia. While the giant plasma tubes discovered by Cleo Loi primarily interfere with satellite-based navigation and radio communications, their indirect biological impact is linked to how they modulate Earth's magnetosphere and geomagnetic field (GMF).

The biological effects associated with these magnetospheric irregularities include:

Cardiovascular Sensitivity: Fluctuations in the geomagnetic field, often exacerbated by magnetospheric plasma structures, are linked to changes in heart rate variability (HRV), blood pressure, and increased risks of myocardial infarction or stroke in vulnerable populations.

Circadian Disruption: Intense geomagnetic disturbances can reduce the synthesis of melatonin, the hormone regulating sleep-wake cycles, particularly in high-latitude regions.

Cellular and Molecular Impacts: Low-frequency EMF variations cause cellular hypoxia and trigger the production of excessive reactive oxygen species (ROS), leading to oxidative stress and cellular damage. Some studies suggest these fields cause irregular gating of voltage-gated ion channels in cell membranes.

Navigation in Animals: Many species (e.g., migratory birds and sea turtles) rely on the Earth's "trickle" of magnetic information for navigation; irregularities in plasma distribution can distort the magnetic cues used for orientation.

Neurological Effects: High-intensity disturbances are associated with increased reports of fatigue, memory issues, and in extreme cases, higher frequencies of psychiatric incidents or suicides occur wear the plasma tubes flow. Plasma is drawn to charges. More people more nnEMF use more charges more damage in populated cities on Earth.

HISTORY LESSON OF 66 million years ago is instructive of what happened to life by a change in extraterrestrial light to the living.

See CPC #77.

When a 6 mile asteroid struck the Yucatan things changed. We call the KT event Factor X in Black Swan lingo.

Factor X was made actionable by setting the stage of the Earth environment to assist in creating man from other primates. This change was quick and radical.

Most of you Black Swans know, I believe the rapid changes to the brain and body plan was caused by a series of events that made change more likely due to epigenetics. The epigenetic plan was speed up due to the assimilation of virus that altered the mammalian body plan by controlling the mitochondrial epigenetic program.

Why were humans built to be so easily afflicted by viruses? Why are humans still impotent to stop pandemics? Nature needs viruses to sculpt life.
What most do not know our creation as a species was the result of viral marketing. This is not the viral marketing of today’s social media platform.
This is where viral marketing was truly invented. First created in our oceans, and eventually incorporated into the DNA of life. Do you think this hyperbole?

One drop of sea water has a million bacteria and 10 million viruses.

Nature does not use her resources unwisely. Somebody had to ask the question why nature would do this. Genes had to have an origin...........and I believe they are all made from viral spare parts put together using quantum probability to control thermodynamics in a cell.
When a certain group of viruses met the primate group in the East Africa Rift Zone, which became isolated from the forrest because of the action of three tectonic plates, in equatorial sun, the world of biology changed suddenly.

Moreover, when the world the apes were trapped in changed because of local geology, it seems the quantum biologic programs in the primates epigenetic toolbox were ready to take full advantage of the environmental change using viruses in seawater where their new food source was located.

Their previous circumstances of acquiring the ability to absorb viruses into their DNA without getting sick was the prerequisite for making a human from a primate. It appears Lucy and Ardi (transitional apes) did not triumph over their adversity then when they lived, but their ancestors found that a VIRAL adversity turned into tragedy & then into evolutionary opportunity when the world finally adapted to them with climate change that happened in the East African Rift Zone 2.5 million years ago. Hominids used many viral part to leave Africa using their new brains and body.

People ask how does a change in mitochondrial signaling (ROS) lead to these changes? This is when I introduce them to the perforin pathway which mitochondria play a central role in. Perforin is a pore-forming protein and also known as cytoplasmic granule toxins. Granzymes are a family of structurally related serine proteases stored within the cytotoxic granules of cytotoxic lymphocytes (CLs) that stimulate mitochondria to make ROS to begin the process.

Perforin and granzyme induce target-cell apoptosis cooperatively. When the circadian mechanism is defective, as it is in many humans, due to their poor environment (light/nnEMF), this system is what creates the bad side of the viral story.......the cytokine storm. That is what kills most people in any viral pandemic. If you read my Patreon blog Hypoxia #7, you'd know that this virus damages heme proteins as a collateral effect. The evidence of heme damage is the high LDH and ferritin levels in C-19 cases with LOW procalcitonin levels. This is a unique finger print that hemoglobin, and cytochrome C oxidase in mitochondria are under attack by cytotoxic T cells.

Why do I say this? You need to understand the process and regulation of perforin/granzyme apoptosis pathway used in your colony of mitochondria.

Once secreted by cytotoxic lymphocytes, granzymes enter into target cells, which is a vital step in cell death. The major lytic proteins packaged within the granules are different granzymes and the pore-forming protein perforin, which facilitates the internalization of granzymes by cells.
Granzyme B mainly triggers caspase activation indirectly, rather than by direct caspase processing. It achieves this by directly activating pro-apoptotic ‘BH3-only’ members of the BCL-2 family, such as BH3-interacting domain death agonist (Bid). Bid together with pro-apoptotic BCL-2 family Bax and/or Bak proteins result in the leakage of pro-apoptotic mitochondrial mediators, such as cytochrome c, into the cytosol. Cytochrome c release activates pro caspase-9, and by binding to apoptotic protease activating factor 1(Apaf-1), pro caspase-9 becomes mature caspase-9, which continues to form the apoptosome and activates downstream caspase-3.

Activated caspase-3 is able to cleave specific substrates like ICAD (inhibitor of the caspase-activated DNase, CAD), allowing the CAD to translocate to the nucleus to fragment DNA. Besides Bid, granzyme B can inactivate Mcl-1 which is a member of the anti-apoptotic Bcl-2 family to release the pro-anti-apoptotic Bcl-2 family protein Bim on the outer mitochondrial membrane. And granzyme B can also mediate the effector caspase-3 and initiator caspase-8 to regulate the apoptosis pathway.

It is as if there is a huge electrical resistance scar in the neural and vascular networks in the CRP to the habenular nucleus and into their frontal lobes that never seems to heal. The ditch gets larger as they stay in the environments that force those tracts to live and +3 existence. Mental illness was not common in the Rift zone. That is where we come from. That is not where we are today.

Why would you assume tomorrow will be better than today in the same places the electrical scar happens? That’s a trap. Nature doesn’t hand out free futures. Do you prepare your life or repair it when it cracks? Daily ownership is your only power. Tough to hear when your brain is fucked up. But Nature does not care. Your focus today delivers tomorrow’s results. Your decisions, the tiniest, small, gritty, deliberate things, set your trajectory. Stop exaggerating yesterday’s glories in evolution or banking on a magical tomorrow in Nirvana. The world underestimates the raw, blazing power of today. This is why the sick must heed the words........you cannot get well in the same environment you sick in. And when you do not get better on your timetable, realize that the depth of your electrical burn can go from a ditch to a hole and then to a prison. The depth of the resistance pattern will determine the fortitude you will need. If you read the neurotic farmer's post in someone else's journal today, she reported that after 24 years, she got her cycle back. 24 years. It happened in Costa Rica. It did not occur where she lost her fertility.

Learn the lesson. Too many do not. Repair happens on Nature's schedule, not yours. You must persist. If you do not, you won't get better.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-07-31T22:10:57+0300

Modern human birth practices, particularly for jaundiced children, recreate GOE-era stressors, driving an atavistic state that increases disease risk: Neonatal jaundice, exacerbated by blue light therapy and the vitamin K shot, generates ROS/UPEs akin to porphyrin-mediated stress during the GOE, impairing OXPHOS, raising heteroplasmy, and forcing a Warburg-like shift to glycolysis, as seen in hypermetabolic states (Picard et al., 2024). This lowers mtDNA redox power (ΔΨm), slowing IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz) and neural oscillations (40 Hz to 20–30 Hz), disrupting myelination (Agan et al., 2025), and causing sensory overload, mirroring autism phenotypes (1/27 births).

Reduced DC electric currents (per Becker) and photo-bioelectric power impair neural development, collagen piezoelectricity (leading to EDS), and autonomic function (POTS), while altered hemoglobin (+3 Hb, fetal Hb retention) exacerbates mtDNA hypoxia, increasing vulnerability to modern mitochondrial toxins (blue light, nnEMF, seed oils). UV light and melanin play a critical role in restoring mitochondrial function in jaundiced children, counteracting the atavistic stressors reminiscent of the GOE:

Jaundiced neonates, with impaired heme synthesis and immature oxygen sensors (neuropsin, mTOR), exhibit a disrupted TCA cycle topology, as UV-A light (380 nm) regulates POMC and melanin production to chelate toxins and support OXPHOS, reducing the Warburg-like state induced by blue light therapy (Ferguson et al., 2019; Tan et al., 2023).

This light-driven oxygen sensing, evolved post-GOE, is mirrored in COVID-19, where SARS-CoV-2 attacks hemoglobin’s porphyrin, exacerbating hypoxia and ROS, akin to jaundice (Wenzhong & Li, 2020; Al-Kindi et al., 2021). Diagnostic tools like UPE measurement, showing increased emissions from bilirubin-induced ROS (Van Wijk et al., 2008), and mtDNA oscillation analysis, revealing a drop from 100 Hz to 20–50 Hz due to desynchronized cristae (Kurz et al., 2010), can confirm mitochondrial stress, guiding interventions like UV-A exposure and melanin supplementation to mitigate autism risk and systemic effects like EDS and dysautonomia. Rh-negative babies, more prone to jaundice due to Rh incompatibility, face amplified mitochondrial stress from RhoGAM, exacerbating the GOE-like atavistic state at birth: RhoGAM’s immune activation induces low-grade inflammation, increasing ROS and mtDNA heteroplasmy, while hemolysis elevates bilirubin and free heme, generating additional ROS/UPEs under blue light therapy, further impairing OXPHOS and forcing a Warburg-like metabolic shift (Picard et al., 2014; Kruse, LinkedIn).

This synergistic stress slows IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), desynchronizes cristae, and damages oligodendrocyte mitochondria, thinning myelin and contributing to sensory overload, autism risk, and systemic effects like EDS and dysautonomia (Agan et al., 2025). The Vitamin K shot, often mislabeled as a vaccine, introduces an additional mitochondrial stressor in jaundiced newborns, as its package insert explicitly lists jaundice as a side effect due to hemolysis, particularly in vulnerable neonates: Hemolysis from the shot increases bilirubin and free heme, generating ROS/UPEs under blue light therapy, further damaging mtDNA, raising heteroplasmy, and impairing OXPHOS, which reinforces the Warburg-like metabolic shift and slows IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), desynchronizing cristae and contributing to myelin thinning, sensory overload, and autism risk (Agan et al., 2025).

Archimedes' principle and maternal light environment further exacerbate mitochondrial stress in jaundiced babies, influencing birth timing and long-term health outcomes: Fatter babies, often from sunlight-deficient mothers with gestational diabetes, exhibit negative buoyancy due to increased fat mass, delaying pelvic engagement and prolonging gestation, which increases hemolysis, bilirubin levels, and jaundice risk, while maternal blue light exposure disrupts EZ water and accelerates ubiquitination rates, amplifying mtDNA stress and heteroplasmy transgenerationally via blood UPE signaling (Sohns et al., 2004; Wallace, 2010). This circadian mismatch, reflected in the mother’s SCN clock speed and impaired DHA levels, slows IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), desynchronizes cristae, and contributes to autism, obesity, and skin cancer risks, underscoring the need for sunlight exposure to restore mitochondrial function and buoyancy dynamics, mitigating these GOE-like stressors (Oláh et al., 2013; Agan et al., 2025).

The evolutionary divergence of Rh-negative blood types, lacking the RhD antigen, reflects a trade-off that increases jaundice risk and mitochondrial stress in modern newborns, amplifying the GOE-like atavistic state: Rh- babies, more prone to hemolysis from Rh incompatibility, face heightened bilirubin and ROS/UPE production, exacerbated by RhoGAM, the Vitamin K shot, and blue light therapy, which impair OXPHOS, raise heteroplasmy, and slow IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), contributing to myelin thinning, sensory overload, and risks of autism, EDS, and dysautonomia (Agan et al., 2025; Flegr et al., 2015). The Rh- mutation, likely selected for pathogen resistance or hypoxic adaptation, does not involve copper-based blood, a common myth, but underscores the need for careful management of environmental stressors like blue light, advocating for sunlight exposure to mitigate these effects (Ferguson et al., 2019). The decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis is reinforced by red blood cells donating electrons to methylene blue-mediated reduction of methemoglobin compartmentalized in liposomes, enhancing the hemiiflusome’s role in mitochondrial function and oxygen delivery in jaundiced neonates:

This electron transfer, amplified by red/NIR light (660–700 nm), reduces MetHb to functional hemoglobin, stabilizing heme metabolism, restoring TCA cycle oxygen utilization, and increasing IMM oscillations (20–50 Hz to 100 Hz), countering the Warburg shift and ROS/UPE production from blue light therapy (Yang et al., 2014; Sohns et al., 2004). In Rh-negative babies, where hemolysis and RhoGAM exacerbate mitochondrial stress, this process mitigates mtDNA hypoxia, supports myelination, and boosts bioelectric currents, reducing autism, EDS, and dysautonomia risks (Agan et al., 2025). The hemiiflusome, a light-sensitive hub for proton flux and heme synthesis, evolves from GOE-era adaptations, with UPEs from MB reduction serving as a diagnostic marker to guide decentralized interventions like sunlight exposure (Ferguson et al., 2019). The confirmed existence of hemiflusomes, newly identified recycling hubs within mitochondria, and the role of thanatotranscriptomic genes enrich the decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis, linking light-driven bioenergetics to neonatal health:

Hemiflusomes, integrating proton flux, heme synthesis, and TCA cycle activity, suppress thanatotranscriptomic gene expression during the day to manage photonic/metabolic chaos and minimize UPE over-emission, while at night, low-level expression complements melatonin’s repair, reflecting an evolutionary adaptation to diurnal cycles (Ferreira et al., 2018; Nunnari et al., 2019). In jaundiced neonates, hemiflusome dysfunction from blue light therapy and hemolysis (e.g., RhoGAM, Vitamin K shot) impairs IMM oscillations (100 Hz to 20–50 Hz), increases ROS/UPEs, and activates thanatotranscriptomic genes, exacerbating autism and EDS risks, whereas melanin and red/NIR light (e.g., via methylene blue) restore hemiiflusome function, supporting myelination and bioelectric currents (Yang et al., 2014; Agan et al., 2025).

The origin of carbon through the rare triple-alpha process in collapsing stars, where three helium nuclei collide to form a threshold where chaos meets meaning, underpins the decentralized photo-bioelectric thesis, reflecting life’s asymmetric choice: Carbon’s unique ability to bend light into structure (via melanin and porphyrins), hold spin without collapse (enabling quantum coherence in mitochondria), and shape time into memory (through DNA and epigenetic light signals) drives hemiflusome function, TCA cycle regulation, and thanatotranscriptomic suppression, countering the ROS/UPE chaos from blue light therapy in jaundiced neonates (Nunnari et al., 2019; Van Wijk et al., 2008). This stellar legacy, fine-tuned by the Hoyle state, enhances red/NIR light’s role in restoring IMM oscillations (20–50 Hz to 100 Hz), supporting myelination and bioelectric currents, and mitigating autism and EDS risks in Rh-negative babies stressed by RhoGAM and hemolysis (Agan et al., 2025; Ferguson et al., 2019). Sunlight exposure, rich in UV-A/B and red/NIR, offers a decentralized solution by upregulating POMC and melanin to chelate toxins, restore CCO/VDR functionon the IMM, and support mitochondrial health, mitigating the long-term consequences of blue light therapy (Ferguson et al., 2019; Oláh et al., 2013).

### MITpowered26 — 2025-08-05T04:34:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/the-human-viral-spark.30670/post-357565>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=350442)
>
> **The Viral Spark: How Decentralized Biophysics and Viruses Shaped Human Evolution**
> What if viruses aren’t just threats but the raw materials of life itself? My photo-bioelectric hypothesis posits that humans evolved not despite viruses but because of them, orchestrated by the decentralized dance of mitochondrial thermodynamics and environmental light. Let’s explore how viral pandemics, far from mere scourges, signal nature’s ancient blueprint for genetic novelty and why humans were built to harness this chaos.
>
> **Viruses as Life’s Building Blocks**
> Forget the old dogma that genes evolve solely from ancestral templates. Recent research reveals “orphan genes”, with no clear predecessors emerge from junk DNA, often virally derived. A single drop of seawater teems with 10 million viruses, nature’s spare parts waiting to be shuffled. I’ve long argued viruses are life’s raw material, integrated into DNA via mitochondrial quantum probability. When the K-T asteroid slammed the Yucatan 66 million years ago (Factor X), it didn’t just wipe out dinosaurs, it reset Earth’s stage, amplifying viral assimilation in surviving primates.
>
> In the East African Rift Zone, 2.5 million years ago, tectonic shifts isolated apes in a viral-rich coastal crucible. Here, transitional hominids like Lucy and Ardi didn’t just survive they thrived by absorbing viruses without succumbing. Why? Their mitochondria, acting as thermodynamic casinos, used reactive oxygen species (ROS) via the perforin/granzyme pathway to epigenetically rewire DNA. This wasn’t random luck but a decentralized program: sunlight (UV-A/IR) optimized cytochrome c oxidase, hydrating melanin and producing deuterium-depleted water (DDW), keeping electrical resistance (éR) low and mtDNA stable. Viruses, far from foes, supplied genetic novelty—new genes for brain expansion, bipedalism, and resilience.
>
> **The Perforin Pathway: Mitochondria’s Viral Choreographer**
> Mitochondria don’t just power cells they orchestrate viral integration. The perforin/granzyme system, a mitochondrial ROS-driven apoptosis pathway, decides which cells live or die during viral encounters. Granzyme B activates pro-apoptotic proteins (Bid, Bax/Bak), releasing cytochrome c to trigger caspase cascades, fragmenting DNA or reshaping it. In healthy mitochondria, bathed in solar EMF, this process is precise—viral DNA integrates constructively, birthing genes for adaptation. But in modern humans, nnEMF and blue light dehydrate melanin, spike éR, and disrupt this dance, turning ROS into a cytokine storm. COVID-19’s heme damage high LDH, ferritin, low procalcitonin reveals this: viruses still target cytochrome c oxidase, but our tech-soaked cells can’t cope, fueling pandemics.
>
> **Why Humans Embrace Viruses**
> Nature built us virus-friendly not to suffer but to evolve. The K-T event’s climate and light shifts, amplified by Rift Zone geology, forced primates to lean on viral “marketing”—not today’s social media hype, but DNA’s original ad campaign. Mitochondria, using light-driven thermodynamics, sorted viral spare parts into new genes, sculpting Homo sapiens from ape stock in a blink (evolutionarily speaking). Our impotence against pandemics isn’t failure of human biology, instead it’s a legacy of openness to genetic flux, now misfiring in nnEMF-saturated cities where mtDNA mutates 15–20 times faster than nuclear DNA.
> [View attachment 30628](https://forum.jackkruse.com/attachments/30628/)
>
> **Decentralized Biophysics: The Real Hero**
> This isn’t a centralized tale of mutations trudging forward. Human evolution is decentralized, mitochondria, guided by sunlight, water, and viral chaos, call the shots. The same system that birthed us makes pandemics a grim reminder: stray from nature’s light (UV-A/IR), and éR rises, Warburg metabolism kicks in, and viruses turn deadly. Yet, there is always a seed of hope in tragedy. By realigning with solar EMF, sunrise gazing, red light therapy, nnEMF avoidance, we can restore DDW, tame ROS, and maybe even harness viruses again for health, not harm.
>
> A Good Sign in Pandemics? They’re nature’s nudge to return to our roots. Viruses aren’t mistakes, they’re evolution’s spark, and our mitochondria know how to wield them, if only we let the sun lead.
>
> **CITES**
> 1\. [https://www.ted.com/talks/craig\_venter\_sampling\_the\_ocean\_s\_dna/up-next#](https://www.ted.com/talks/craig_venter_sampling_the_ocean_s_dna/up-next#)
> 2\. [https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2013/10/131021162657.htm](https://www.sciencedaily.com/releases/2013/10/131021162657.htm)

Biophotons are ultra-weak light emissions produced by living organisms, thought to arise from metabolic processes like oxidative reactions in mitochondria. Centralized scientists and AI bots hypothesized them to play a role in cellular communication, potentially influencing gene expression or signaling pathways. FIAF, also known as angiopoietin-like protein 4 (ANGPTL4), is a protein produced by tissues like fat and the gut, and it’s a key player in lipid metabolism and microbiome construction. Neither understand how UPEs control FIAF. If the UPE is coherent then FIAF inhibits lipoprotein lipase, affecting how fats are stored or broken down, and its expression ramps up during fasting. The microbiome, meanwhile, is the community of gut microbes that can shift in response to diet, fasting, or host metabolism, influencing energy balance and health. If the UPEs in mitochondria is not coherent, the UPE changes and mental illness becomes more probable. You cannot burn fat so it changes neural signaling.

Centralized scientists and technocrats who build AI systems will say no direct study says “biophotons control FIAF to affect the microbiome,” but we can hypothesize based on related mechanisms. This is patently false. Why?

FIAF has known absorption and emission spectra to light frequencies. Because of that, a study is superfluous because each chemical in the world has an optical fingerprint. Just because a scientist has not published the work is immaterial to this BASIC biophysical fact in spectroscopy. This was what I brought Ray Peat over ten years ago, and he was impotent enough to answer my critiques of his work. This podcast covers these details.

# Thread: 30 million volts on the IMM
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/30-million-volts-on-the-imm.30671/>
**Subforum:** Mitochondrial Rx
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-11T07:13:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/30-million-volts-on-the-imm.30671/post-350445>

Mitochondrial Membrane Potential and the 30 Million Volt Field

Nick Lane often highlights the staggering electric field across the inner mitochondrial membrane, estimated at around 30 million volts per meter. This figure arises from the membrane potential, typically 150–200 millivolts (mV), across a membrane that’s about 5–7 nanometers thick. To put this in perspective:

-   Calculation: If the potential difference is 150 mV (0.15 V) across 5 nm (5 × 10⁻⁹ m), the electric field strength is: { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

-   This is comparable to the electric field in a lightning bolt, but it’s confined to a nanoscale distance, so the actual voltage drop is tiny.

This electrical field resistance is what drives ATP synthesis via the proton-motive force, where protons (H⁺) are pumped across the membrane by the electron transport chain (ETC), creating both a voltage (Δψ) and a pH gradient (ΔpH). Cytochrome c oxidase (Complex IV, or CCO) is the terminal enzyme in the ETC, reducing oxygen to water while pumping protons to maintain this gradient.

I have mentioned the production of “deuterium-free water” surrounding the current is one of the key innovations of the CCO heme protein. In the context of mitochondria, water is indeed critical, as CCO catalyzes the formation of water from oxygen and protons:

Some research, particularly in bioenergetics and quantum biology, suggests that the structured water within mitochondria (sometimes called “coherent domain” water or interfacial water) may have unique properties, potentially influenced by isotopic composition (e.g., lower deuterium content). Deuterium, being heavier than protium (¹H), can affect reaction kinetics or hydrogen bonding in confined spaces like the mitochondrial matrix. Structured water plays a role in proton conductance or redox reactions.

Regarding CCO’s “built-in system” to protect against loss of electrical resistance, I am lreferring to its role in maintaining membrane integrity and preventing oxidative damage. If the membrane potential collapses or reactive oxygen species (ROS) accumulate due to ETC dysfunction, CCO can contribute to signaling pathways that trigger apoptosis (programmed cell death). For example:

-   Cytochrome c release: If the membrane potential is disrupted, cytochrome c can leak from the intermembrane space into the cytosol, activating caspases and initiating apoptosis.
-   ROS production: CCO dysfunction can increase ROS, which, if unchecked, signals mitochondrial outer membrane permeabilization (MOMP), further promoting apoptosis.

Apoptosis acts as a failsafe to eliminate damaged cells, preserving tissue-level function. It also limits the chance of oncogensis if atavistic cells are made from hypoxia induced chances. However, I have described the CCO innovation post GOE as a protection mechanism protecting the organism itself from “loss of electrical resistance” so it can propagate to regions who have a low resistance of current to destroy distal tissues, is a new decentralized idea, because the biochemists believe apoptosis is more about cellular quality control than CCO self-preservation.

Voltage, Current, and the Needle Analogy
Now, let’s address the statement about voltage and current, which uses the analogy of a needle piercing the body to explain electrical concepts. The core claim is that voltage is about “speed” or “pressure at a given point,” while the danger lies in the size of the current, not the voltage. Let’s break it down:

1.  Voltage (Potential Difference):
    -   Voltage is the electric potential difference between two points, measured in volts (V). It represents the “push” or energy per unit charge available to move electrons through a circuit.
    -   In the mitochondrial context, the membrane potential (Δψ) is the voltage driving proton movement. The 30 million V/m field reflects the intensity of this push across a tiny distance, not the total energy involved.
2.  Current (Flow of Charge):
    -   Current is the rate of charge flow, measured in amperes (A). In biological systems, this could be likened to the flow of protons or electrons through the ETC.
    -   High current through the body is dangerous because it disrupts physiological processes (e.g., nerve signaling, muscle contraction). For example, 100 mA across the heart can cause ventricular fibrillation.
3.  The Needle Analogy:
    -   The analogy compares a needle’s penetration to voltage, suggesting that a slow needle (low voltage) causes less harm than a fast one (high voltage), but ultimately, the “size” of the current determines danger.
    -   This is misleading. Voltage and speed aren’t directly equivalent. A needle’s speed might correlate to kinetic energy or penetration force, but voltage is about electric potential, not mechanical motion.
    -   Electrically, a high-voltage, low-current shock (e.g., from a static discharge) can be harmless because the total charge delivered is small. Conversely, a low-voltage, high-current shock (e.g., from a car battery) can be deadly if sustained, as it drives more charge through tissue.
4.  High Voltage and Safety:
    -   The statement claims a million volts “doesn’t mean much under proper conditions.” This is partially true. For example, a Van de Graaff generator produces high voltage (thousands to millions of volts) but low current, so the shock feels like a spark rather than a lethal jolt.
    -   However, high voltage can still be dangerous if it drives sufficient current through the body, especially if the source can sustain it (e.g., power lines).
5.  AC vs. DC and Rapid Switching:
    -   The claim that a current “jerked back and forth” rapidly (like high-frequency AC) wouldn’t kill, while a continuous current would “burn you up,” oversimplifies things.
    -   Alternating current (AC) at 50–60 Hz is more dangerous than direct current (DC) at the same voltage because AC disrupts nerve and muscle function more effectively. However, very high-frequency AC (e.g., >100 kHz) can be less harmful because it doesn’t penetrate deeply, primarily causing surface burns (as in diathermy or Tesla coils).
    -   A continuous DC current can still kill by causing thermal damage or electrolysis in tissues if the current is high enough.
    -   The statement captures some truths about electricity—current is the main killer, and high voltage isn’t always dangerous—but it muddies the waters with an oversimplified analogy. In the mitochondrial context, the electric field is a marvel of biology, but it’s not about raw power; it’s about precise control.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-11T07:31:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/30-million-volts-on-the-imm.30671/post-350446>

**Biophysical Framework: Setting the Stage**
Let’s start with the mitochondrial inner membrane’s 30 MV/m electric field, which I’ve highlighted via Nick Lane’s work. This field, arising from a ~150–150 mV potential across ~5–5 nm, is a dynamic electromagnetic system driving proton dynamics, not a static biochemical pump. CCO, in my model, generates DDW to maintain this field’s coherence, using apoptosis as a failsafe to prevent field collapse, a mechanism I tie to the GOE when eukaryotes evolved to handle oxygen’s new risks to cells. DDW, produced exclusively by mtDNA-regulated processes, optimizes energy transfer, akin to its role in boosting plant yields by 40% using photosynthesis and/or stabilizing nuclear reactions by favoring light hydrogen. Hypoxia, as an environmental light stressor, disrupts this field, and I believe we should be asking how DDW’s interaction with Cherenkov radiation might influence mtDNA-linked biophoton spectra, reflecting cellular health or dysfunction.

**DDW and Cherenkov Radiation**
Cherenkov radiation is electromagnetic radiation emitted when a charged particle, like an electron, travels through a dielectric medium (e.g., water) faster than the medium’s phase velocity of light. In water, light’s speed is ~0.75c (c = speed of light in vacuum), so particles exceeding this threshold emit a blue glow, as seen in nuclear reactors. I am suggesting now that DDW plays a role in this process within mitochondria, so let’s explore biophysically:

-   DDW’s Dielectric Properties:
    -   DDW, with lower deuterium (~25–25 ppm vs. 150 ppm in normal water), has a slightly altered dielectric constant and refractive index due to protium’s lighter mass. Deuterium’s heavier nucleus (²H) strengthens hydrogen bonds, making normal water denser electromagnetically. DDW, being “lighter,” reduces the refractive index marginally, raising the speed of light in the medium (v = c/n, where n is the refractive index). This has been experimentally shown by Pollack.
    -   In mitochondria, where CCO produces DDW, this could lower the Cherenkov threshold, allowing slower electrons to emit radiation in the form of ultraweak biophtons. This potential could be a second source of biophtons in humans. The 30 MV/m field accelerates electrons during redox reactions (e.g., in CCO’s heme groups), and some may approach relativistic speeds in the matrix’s DDW-rich environment, triggering faint Cherenkov-like emissions. These would be ultraweak. Popp has found these. We just do not know where they come from.
-   Mitochondrial Context:
    -   Mitochondria aren’t nuclear reactors, but their intense electromagnetic field and high-energy electron transfers (via the electron transport chain, ETC) create conditions for microscale electromagnetic effects. CCO’s water production, if DDW, forms a low-deuterium matrix, potentially acting as a dielectric medium optimized for field coherence. Remember as scale shrinks the electromagnetic force gets strong because of the inverse square law.
    -   Cherenkov radiation in this context would be ultra-weak, possibly overlapping with biophoton emissions (photons from redox or field dynamics). DDW’s role is to enhance this process by reducing isotopic damping, ensuring electrons move freely in the field without deuterium’s mass slowing quantum tunneling or scattering light.
-   Biological Implications:
    -   If CCO produces DDW, it creates a medium where Cherenkov radiation could occur at lower electron energies, amplifying the mitochondrial field’s electromagnetic signature. This would likely act to stabilize proton tunneling or signal cellular health, reinforcing my GOE feedback loop I discussed with Nick Jikomes in his podcast with me where DDW protects against energy loss or ROS spikes.
    -   During hypoxia, oxygen scarcity disrupts CCO, reducing DDW output and weakening the field. This could raise the Cherenkov threshold (as deuterium accumulates in mtDNA), dimming or altering any radiation emitted, which ties to your biophoton spectra point.

**mtDNA, Biophotons, and Hypoxia**
I’ve connected mtDNA to DDW production via CCO function and suggested biophoton spectra vary in hypoxia, potentially linked to Cherenkov effects. Biophotons, which are ultra-weak photon emissions from cells, are thought to reflect coherent electromagnetic activity, especially in mitochondria. Let’s explore how mtDNA, DDW, and Cherenkov radiation shape these spectra biophysically:

-   mtDNA as a Field Regulator:
    -   I’ve stated mtDNA “only makes DDW,” implying it governs CCO’s water output. Biophysically, mtDNA, embedded in the inner membrane, could sense the 30 MV/m field’s oscillations, modulating CCO activity to favor protium over deuterium. This might involve mtDNA’s supercoiled structure acting as an electromagnetic antenna, tuning proton dynamics or electron transfers.
    -   mtDNA mutations or stress (e.g., hypoxia) could detune this antenna, reducing DDW production and disrupting field coherence, altering biophoton emissions.
-   Biophoton Spectra and DDW:
    -   Biophotons, often in the UV-visible range (~200–200 nm), arise from redox reactions, excited states, or field fluctuations. In healthy mitochondria, CCO’s DDW creates a low-entropy medium, enhancing biophoton coherence—sharp, narrow spectra signaling robust field dynamics.
    -   DDW’s role in Cherenkov radiation could contribute to biophotons. If high-energy electrons in CCO’s field emit Cherenkov light in DDW, these photons (blue-UV heavy) might overlap with biophoton bands, amplifying their intensity or shifting their spectra. DDW’s low deuterium minimizes scattering, preserving photon coherence.
-   Hypoxia’s Impact:
    -   Hypoxia starves CCO of oxygen, stalling electron transfer and reducing DDW production. The field weakens, and deuterium accumulation (from normal water) could dampen electron speeds, raising the Cherenkov threshold and dimming any radiation. This disrupts biophoton coherence, broadening or shifting spectra to longer wavelengths (e.g., redder, less energetic). This might be key in the human sleep cycle and regeneration programs.
    -   mtDNA, sensing this field collapse, might signal apoptosis via altered biophoton patterns become chaotic or dim emissions triggering CCO to release cytochrome c, as I've described above. This fits my GOE loop: where hypoxia mimics pre-eukaryotic oxygen scarcity, and apoptosis culls cells to prevent oncogenesis-like runaway growth.
-   Spectral Variations:
    -   In normoxia, biophoton spectra might peak in UV-blue (300–300 nm), reflecting DDW’s role in Cherenkov-like emissions and CCO’s coherent redox. Hypoxia due to light stress in our environments could shift peaks to visible (500–500 nm) or infrared, indicating field decoherence or ROS-driven luminescence. These shifts could serve as a biophysical diagnostic for mitochondrial health, tying to my oncogenesis protection model.

**Tying to the GOE Feedback Loop**
My decentralized thesis links CCO, DDW, and apoptosis to a post-GOE feedback loop protecting eukaryotes from oxygen toxicity and endosymbiotic risks. Let’s integrate Cherenkov radiation and biophotons:

-   Post-GOE Adaptation:
    -   The GOE (~2.4 billion years ago) introduced oxygen, enabling CCO’s high-energy redox and the 30 MV/m field in proto-mitochondria. Archaea and bacteria lacked CCO, relying on weak fields, making them vulnerable to ROS. Eukaryotes evolved DDW production to stabilize this field, with mtDNA as the control hub.
    -   Cherenkov radiation, if present, would have been a byproduct of this field’s intensity, with DDW lowering the emission threshold to signal mitochondrial efficiency. Biophotons, possibly including Cherenkov light, became a communication system, broadcasting field health across cells.
-   Apoptosis as Protection:
    -   Hypoxia or ROS spikes mimic GOE-era stress, disrupting DDW and the field. Reduced Cherenkov/biophoton output could signal this failure, with mtDNA triggering apoptosis to eliminate defective cells, preventing oncogenesis-like states where mitochondria outgrow host control.
    -   This loop ensured eukaryotes balanced energy gains with stability, unlike primitive domains prone to oxidative chaos.
-   Oncogenesis Link:
    -   In cancer, mtDNA mutations or hypoxia disrupt DDW, weakening the field and biophoton coherence. Aberrant spectra (e.g., red-shifted) might bypass apoptosis, letting cells evade culling, akin to endosymbiotic overreach. DDW’s role in restoring coherence could explain its anti-cancer effects. This aligning with my plant yield analogy where optimized energy transfer prevents chaos.

Addressing Tesla’s Voltage
In Tesla’s 1894 view, where he saw voltage as pressure, not power to the mitochondrial field. Biophysically:

-   The 30 MV/m field is a “pressure” driving proton tunneling, not a massive current, mirroring Tesla’s idea that high voltage (field strength) is safe if flow (proton leaks) is controlled. DDW enhances this by reducing deuterium’s drag, potentially enabling Cherenkov-like emissions without energy loss.
-   Hypoxia’s field collapse is like Tesla’s “continuous current” burning tissue which is uncontrolled proton flow triggers apoptosis, not biophoton coherence.

**Why Cherenkov and Biophotons Matter**
My focus on Cherenkov radiation suggests mitochondria might emit detectable electromagnetic signals beyond biophotons, with DDW as the medium of this communicating field. No centralized biologist even knows this field is possible. The physics predict it is possible. It needs to be studied. While mainstream biophysics doesn’t yet confirm Cherenkov in cells (it’s studied in radiotherapy, e.g., tumor imaging in dogs), my hypothesis is plausible because the laws of physics do not forbid it, they infact, support it:

-   Feasibility: The 30 MV/m field and CCO’s electron transfers could push electrons near the Cherenkov threshold in DDW’s low-refractive medium, producing faint UV-blue light overlapping biophoton ranges.
-   Tryptophan residues are often found in transmembrane proteins situated at the lipid–water interface. Multitryptophan proteins have been widely studied, including the heme proteins myoglobin, hemoglobin, cytochrome-_c_ oxidase, and cytochrome _P_4̅50. This implies that the long-range couplings between Tryptophan chromophores should greatly enhance the robustness of the network inside of cells.
-   This leads to a counterintuitive consequence. Quantum cooperativity increases the robustness of a system to disorder and robustness can increase with the system size. This effect, known as cooperative robustness in the literature.
-   Why does this interest me? The origin of this "robustness effect" can be qualitatively explained as a very large decay width, which can strongly couple the system in question with the electromagnetic field. That field effect is the biophoton field effect inside a cell.
-   Hypoxia Signature: Reduced DDW in hypoxia raises this threshold, dimming Cherenkov/biophoton output and shifting spectra, a biophysical marker for stress or oncogenesis risk.
-   We now know without a doubt that published data exists revealing that coherent quantum optical information transfer at ambient temperatures in the presence of static and/or dynamical disorder is a real measurable effect.
-   Tryptophan chromophores have been identified for their unique role in UV light sensing in the UVR8 plant photoreceptor, which is believed to be the first UV light perception system discovered to use a network of Trp chromophores as a funnel to enhance its quantum efficiency. I believe neuropsin is another such detector used in mammals.
-   Exceptionally bright superradiant states in these biocomplexes may facilitate the absorption and energy transfer of UV photoexcitations (UPE) even in an intensely oxidative environment, where electronically excited molecular species emit light quanta in this wavelength regime.

_IMPLICATIONS OF THIS IDEA?_

**The Perforin Pathway: Mitochondria’s Viral Choreographer**
Mitochondria don’t just power cells, they orchestrate viral integration that made us a silly talking monkey from our tree dwelling cousins. The perforin/granzyme system, a mitochondrial ROS-driven apoptosis pathway, decides which cells live or die during viral encounters. Granzyme B activates pro-apoptotic proteins (Bid, Bax/Bak), releasing cytochrome c to trigger caspase cascades, fragmenting DNA or reshaping it. Biophtons are likely critical signaling information carriers in this processIn healthy mitochondria. This mtDNA bathed in solar EMF, would optimized this process is precise because it is quantized by UPE release and information transfer allowing viral DNA integration accretively and constructively, birthing genes for adaptation. But in modern humans, nnEMF and blue light dehydrate CCO via DDW loss and affect the electrical resistance of melanin, spiking éR, and disrupt this quantum evolutionary dance, turning ROS into a cytokine storm. COVID-19’s heme damage leading to high LDH, ferritin, low procalcitonin reveals this: viruses still target cytochrome c oxidase to this day to sculpt life, but our tech-soaked cells can’t cope, fueling pandemics. The key is to remember prokaryotes which mitochondria come from have been producing light for billions of years. They produces massive amounts of it over evolutionary timescales but as we go further out on the evolutionary tree of life on Earth UPE gets more focused by the electrical resistance networks built into eukaryotes. This has huge implication for information entropy as Shannon taught us in 1948.

**Why Humans Embrace Viruses**
Nature built us virus-friendly not to suffer but to evolve. The K-T event’s climate shifts, amplified by Rift Zone geology, forced primates to lean on viral “marketing”, not today’s social media hype on screen technology, but DNA’s original ad campaign to sculpt life. Mitochondria, using light-driven thermodynamics, sorted viral spare parts into new genes, sculpting Homo sapiens from ape stock in a blink (evolutionarily speaking). Our impotence against pandemics isn’t failure, it’s a legacy of openness to genetic flux, now misfiring in nnEMF-saturated cities where mtDNA mutates 15–20 times faster than nuclear DNA. This implies as fast as UPE sculpted primates to man it could extinct man from himself.

**My Current Gaps and Open Questions**

I’m treating DDW’s mitochondrial role as fact, but biophysics hasn’t yet fully mapped Cherenkov radiation in cells. Studies on biophotons (e.g., 2024 Journal of Biophotonics on mitochondrial emissions) show hypoxia alters spectra, supporting my idea, but don’t mention Cherenkov explicitly. DDW’s anti-cancer effects (e.g., 2024 PMC review) hint at field stabilization, but the mtDNA-DDW-Cherenkov link is a frontier hypothesis I’ll explore further in my work for you.

**Next Steps**

I’ve framed DDW as enabling Cherenkov-like emissions in mitochondria, amplifying biophoton coherence, with hypoxia disrupting both, triggering apoptosis to protect against oncogenesis, per my GOE model. Where does centralized biophysics need to go next?

-   Cherenkov Deep Dive: Model how DDW lowers the Cherenkov threshold biophysically, calculating electron speeds in the 30 MV/m field.
-   Biophoton Spectra: Explore specific wavelength shifts in hypoxia, linking to mtDNA mutations, neurodegeneration, or cancer.
-   GOE Evolution: Flesh out how DDW and Cherenkov/biophotons gave eukaryotes an edge over archaea/bacteria.
-   Therapeutic Angles: Discuss DDW or biophoton-based diagnostics/therapies for hypoxia or oncogenesis.

Spend as much time in the sun as possible . . . While grounded (direct physical contact with the ground) and no EMF - be at least 8ft away from buildings, no WiFi Bluetooth etc. If online be hard wired (ethernet) and never on a power cord, device should be on battery And away from your belly.
Be sure you are sleeping in an EMF mitigated room, see drop down menu for pages, lots of info: [www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse](http://www.LiveEMFSafe.com/kruse)

# Thread: Blue Light Damage to Retina
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-damage-to-retina.30690/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Alain Dwight — 2025-04-13T17:40:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-damage-to-retina.30690/post-350609>

Have any studies been done, testing if blue light causes damage to the retina under ecologically valid conditions (chronic exposure at levels typical of indoor lighting and screens)?

Most of the studies I’ve found use exaggerated exposure protocols (eg. high-intensity blue light, continuous exposure for days), maybe because regulators frame the risk as acute rather than chronic despite the fact that modern health concerns are clearly centered on long term, chronic disease states.

I see studies that artificial light is bad for circadian health too, but nothing specific to blue light and retinal damage. It seems like a catch-22 similar to the questions and concerns about prescription opiate addiction that were ignored and underfunded in the 90s and 00s: funds for longer term, more detailed studies are not released despite reasonable calls for further investigation because there are no studies showing harm at real-world levels of chronic exposures.

Is there a body of evidence or synthesis that I’m missing, or are regulators and institutions successfully avoiding the creation of studies that would generate decisive data about retinal damage from real-world blue light exposure levels?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-13T18:15:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-damage-to-retina.30690/post-350613>

You actually think the NIH wants to fund studies to uncover the MKULTRA story? You must smoke some good herb LOL

### Alain Dwight — 2025-04-13T21:44:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/blue-light-damage-to-retina.30690/post-350627>

Lol, no I would not assume good faith from NIH, especially in regards to MKULTRA, just wanted a sanity check that I didn't miss anything, much appreciated... seems that alternative funding yielding decisive evidence here could really damage the credibility of Rockefeller Medicine Inc, even something simple like handing out daylight computers and e ink phones to a cohort to reduce blue exposure

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/saturated-fat-ldl-cholesterol.30693/post-350653>

I’ve been trying to connect a few dots lately and would love to hear your take on this.

Before discovering uncle Jack I followed Peter Attia, and he wrote and talked a lot about cholesterol.

In the centralized literature there's a clear connection between saturated fat intake and LDL cholesterol. I never understood why from the quantum biology lens.

Would this make sense?

Light stress elevates LDL cholesterol (too much bad light, too little good light)

Saturated fat also presumable elevates it, and this would be the case when:
1\. People don't see the sunrise and have a broken circadian rhythm.
2\. They can't utilize the TCA cycle for proper fat oxidation
3\. Saturated fats oxide and cause inflammation
4\. Liver increases cholesterol to fight the inflammation

Looking forward to your thoughts!

# Thread: Lloyd’s ALS Journey: A Family Path Toward Light, Nature, and Hope
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lloyd%E2%80%99s-als-journey-a-family-path-toward-light-nature-and-hope.30696/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 28
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mdharbin — 2025-04-15T16:08:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lloyd%E2%80%99s-als-journey-a-family-path-toward-light-nature-and-hope.30696/post-350729>

Hi everyone, Hi Uncle [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/),

I’m starting this journal on behalf of my father-in-law, Lloyd, who was diagnosed with ALS in March 2024. He doesn’t use technology very much anymore, so I’ll be the one sharing his journey here. We made this choice together, and I’m doing my best to be his voice in this space.

Lloyd is actually the reason I found Jack's work in the first place. A dear friend sent me the Rick Rubin podcast when I was desperately searching for ways to help Lloyd. That one podcast opened the door, and from there I dove into Jack’s world: webinars, blogs, podcasts, and more. When I kept hearing Jack recommend starting an Optimal Journal, it felt right to begin one here.

In my next post, I’ll share a full breakdown of Lloyd’s symptom timeline, lifestyle, and interventions. (Please excuse the AI for helping me organize that data.)

For now, here’s some background.

#### Lloyd's Life​

Lloyd is 63. He was born and raised in Moose Jaw, Saskatchewan, and lived his entire life there. He spent many years working in his family’s dry-cleaning business before shifting to truck driving, and eventually ended up managing the city’s event center.

He’s lived a life filled with shift work, late nights, and, like most of us, heavy tech exposure once smartphones came around. But despite that, he’s always been physically active. He ran marathons, played sports, and stayed strong into his 60s. His ancestry is at least 25-50% Middle Eastern (I'm not sure of the exact region), and he has dark olive skin. He basically never sunburns.

#### Coming to Costa Rica​

After his ALS diagnosis in late March 2024, my wife and I convinced him to come live with us and his grandchildren in Costa Rica. He stayed a couple months, went home for summer, and by October 2024, we got him to return and stay here long-term.

He still wants to go back to Moose Jaw for the summers, they have a nice warm summer evidently, but he now knows he can’t live there full-time anymore.

#### Our Current Protocol​

Since January 2025, I’ve had him on a Jack-inspired lifestyle program:

-   5:30am sunrise sessions outside, lying on boulders
-   Wi-Fi limited to 1 hour max/day (often less)
-   No artificial lights after sunset
-   Mostly outside lifestyle
-   2-4+ hrs/day strong direct sunlight on skin
-   Spring water from artesian well on site
-   Several hours/day barefoot walking
-   Zero cell towers nearby as we live deep in the Costa Rican jungle
-   Very low-tech home, just a fridge, simple kitchen gear, and the router (off 23+ hours a day)

On top of this, he’s growing his own garden and eating from it. He makes fresh herbal teas from the land each day. Every 3 weeks we do yage (ayahuasca) ceremonies together, something he genuinely looks forward to. (I’ve been a shamanic healer for 10 years and grow + prepare the medicine myself.) We also work with other plants like ortiga, chiricaspi, chichaha, and more.

Just two weeks ago, I was able to convince him to come off his long list of 20+ supplements. Pills, powders, all of it. He’s now supplement-free and focusing entirely on light, water, magnetism, and nature. He never took any pharmaceuticals or other mainstream drugs, just supplements.

#### Where We're At​

The progression seems to have only very slightly slowed since leaving Canada, but it hasn’t stopped. I’ve tried everything I possibly know how to do, and still, the symptoms continue. I feel like I’ve reached the edge of what I can offer, and I’m not sure what’s next.

We plan to book a consult with Jack this week, just been waiting for the 25% off surcharge to not be in effect. I’m hoping Uncle [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) can help us see what we’re missing.

Lloyd wants to return to Moose Jaw in June to be with his wife and sons for the summer. I worry that if he goes, he might not come back. Whether it’s his choice, or because his body won’t let him, I don’t know.

We’re doing our best. Thank you for reading, and please feel free to share any thoughts, questions, etc. Anything is welcome.

With gratitude,
Michael

### mdharbin — 2025-04-15T16:12:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lloyd%E2%80%99s-als-journey-a-family-path-toward-light-nature-and-hope.30696/post-350730>

## Lloyds Health History​

### **Vaccination & COVID History**​

-   **Sept 9, 2021** – Pfizer vaccine (Batch FD7206)
-   **Oct 8, 2021** – Pfizer vaccine (Batch FD7204)
-   **Feb 2022 & Oct 2022** – Contracted COVID (confirmed by positive tests, presumed)

### **Imaging & Diagnostics**​

-   **Dec 2021** – 1 brain MRI (no contrast): _Results clear_
-   **Aug 2024** – ALS-related follow-up MRI: _No changes from 2021_
-   **March 31, 2024** – Diagnosed with ALS by second neurologist

### **Physical Trauma & Injury History**​

-   **2006–2024** – Repeated head and spinal impacts (truck driving, accidents)
-   **2008** – Fell off trailer, damaged L5; advised to strengthen abs
-   **2019** – Fell and crushed T4–T5 vertebrae
-   **2022** – Severe spinal incident with audible "crunch"
-   Chronic: Tight neck, stiff muscles upon waking

* * *

## **ALS Symptom Timeline**​

### E**arly Indicators (2022)**​

-   **Late 2022**– Likely symptom onset
    -   Fatigue while brushing teeth in shower
    -   Head fog, headaches
    -   Muscle cramps & spasms begin (mainly left side)
    -   Constant need to stretch

### **Progression Through 2023**​

-   **Early 2023**
    -   Whole-body aches 1–2 days/month
    -   **Feb:** Quit alcohol
-   **Aug 2023** – Noticed back muscle atrophy (middle back hollow when flexing)
-   **Oct 2023**
    -   Week-long full-body aches
    -   Severe knee pain (trouble climbing stairs)
-   **Nov 2023**
    -   Quit sugar → significant pain reduction
    -   Wrist drooping while curling – loss of motor control

### **Winter 2023 – Early 2024**​

-   Left hand numb/non-functional when working in cold weather
-   Visible weakening of left arm and back muscles
-   Reduced thumb/forefinger dexterity begins
-   ALS testing underway

### **2024 Onward – Rapid Progression**​

-   **Feb 2024**– Switched to cleaner diet (high protein, vegetables, low sugar/carbs)
    -   Unable to do buttons on clothes
-   **April 2024**
    -   Severe cramps (left side: arches, calves, triceps)
    -   Random nerve pain ("ant bites") including ears
    -   Getting 2 hrs+ direct sunlight/day
    -   Slight reduction in tech use
    -   Awake for 95% of sunrises
-   **Oct 22, 2024**– Moved to Costa Rican Jungle
    -   Off-grid lifestyle: minimal tech use (<1hr/day), no Wi-Fi or phone outside this time
    -   Mostly outdoors
    -   Somatic meditation practice daily
    -   Daily devotionals
    -   Yoga or stretching each day
    -   Dry mouth in morning, episodes of slobbering

### **2025 – Major Decline**​

-   **Jan 2025**
    -   Still able to seal a zip-lock bag
    -   Jaw sore and tired upon waking
-   **April 2025**
    -   Can no longer seal bag or hold fork in left hand
    -   Thumb now barely touches index finger (was pinky in 2024)
    -   Left arm nearly unusable, visible atrophy in arm & back
    -   Spasms, cramps now bilateral (both sides, but worse on left)
    -   Random nerve spasms and cramps can last for days or go away for days
    -   Cramps/spasms/nerve pain have spread to both legs (started **Feb 2025**, left worse)
    -   Right Hand: End of thumb feels numb + palm feels sore & stiff

* * *

## **Symptom Summary (by Type)**​

### **Cognitive**​

-   Head fog
-   Headaches

### **Motor & Muscular**​

-   Atrophy (left arm, back, middle back)
-   Muscle cramps: arches, calves, triceps, chest, biceps, thighs, diaphragm, stomach
-   Muscle spasms: widespread, worse on left, now bilateral
-   Loss of grip strength: can’t hold fork, seal bag, limited thumb movement
-   Stiffness, chronic tightness

### **Nerve-Related**​

-   Random nerve pain (ant bite feeling) all over, including ears
-   Numbness: Left hand in cold
-   Loss of fine motor control (thumb/fingers, wrist droop)

### **Whole Body**​

-   Fatigue
-   Whole-day body aches (intermittent)
-   Knee pain

### **Oral & Jaw**​

-   Waking with dry mouth and episodes of slobbering
-   Jaw often feels sore and tired upon waking

### Laudy Cincotta — 2025-04-15T18:27:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lloyd%E2%80%99s-als-journey-a-family-path-toward-light-nature-and-hope.30696/post-350735>

**Michael, I am so glad your father in law has you on this journey!**

You are definitely in the place to find answers.

I guess you guys have already worked out that ALS is brutal.

Brutal on the patient for everything it takes from them and brutal on the family to have to watch the suffering.

Epigenetics is definitely what got him here and I believe the vaccines sped things up for Lloyd.

I am sure in your research you found that his life/lifestyle starting in the dry cleaning business with the chemical exposures to harsh hydrocarbons and percethylene are without a doubt damaging to the body followed by diesel exposure as a truck driver while exposed to very high magnetic fields in his truck also did not do him any favours and lastly blue light and circadian mismatched timing doing event work with the vaccines pushing his body over the edge.

It is wonderful you have already gotten him into a low latitude equatorial climate and are already so remote.

You have given him the best chances to fight this disease by the changes you have made.

I understand his wanting to go home but Canada can no longer be home. That environment will not support him.

‘You can never get better in the place where you got sick.’

ALS is a disease that disrupts your life and turns your whole world upside down, and how you deal with it depends on how flexible you are and how you manage moving forward. Mindset is critical as is a positive 'glass half full' attitude.

Sounds like Lloyd is sensible and open minded knowing already that he cannot live in Canada anymore, I am not the doctor here but in my opinion the risk of exposure to radiation while flying and returning to a high latitude even though its summer is not a wise move and your concerns are very justified.

I am saying what I say to you from experience and with all good intentions for you all so maybe the family discussion after your consult with Jack should focus on the wife and children relocating to Costa Rica for the immediate future to support him and you & your family because if you cannot halt it then as the disease progresses you will both require a lot of help if it cannot be stabilised and as an ALS patient declines they ‘need a village’ to support them.

If you get to this point, and I am cheering you on that you do not, you will all need to decide where he will live out the rest of his days that will make him happy.

In my husbands case we saw a slowing of disease progression and extended periods of stability when we arrived in El Salvador and there is no doubt in my mind that the environment worked in his favour, we were just too late in getting him here.

My husband did not want to return to Australia and was happiest here in El Salvador until he passed.

Truly wishing you all the best of luck to beat this.

I believe you can, hoping you got him to CR in time.

### MTA321 — 2025-04-15T18:56:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lloyd%E2%80%99s-als-journey-a-family-path-toward-light-nature-and-hope.30696/post-350738>

Don't have much to add but thank you for the detailed journal and I wish you the best of luck.

Can you hardwire the router when it is on? (every little bit may help. there are adapters to run ethernet and connect to cellphone, granted your location may prove a challenge for getting this quickly)

How is the light environment controlled when they go back home for the summer (wifi/towers, mis-matched light-to-food environment, etc)?

Supplements - Noticed you mentioned they were recently removed but if they were taking 20+ supplements (depending on the supplement) it could have significant impact on Rev-erbα - disrupting circadian signaling in the gut - opening mPTP leading to MPP+ (neurotoxin) wreaking havoc on mitochondria and could create a cascading infectious like state speeding up motor neuron destruction.

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lloyd%E2%80%99s-als-journey-a-family-path-toward-light-nature-and-hope.30696/post-350746>

>

Thank you [@Laudy Cincotta](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/24082/). I appreciate your kind words and your insights.

Unfortunately, Lloyds wife is unwilling to relocate to Costa Rica. She wants to continue working until retirement in 4-5 years. She has worked in a dental office for 30+ years and will undoubtedly be next in line for disease, but she remains unwilling. She does come visit 1x a year for 2 months straight, so that helps.

His sons are grown and content at home and I don't think they are as big of a pull for Lloyd. It's mainly his wife of 40 years that he misses.

Thanks again.

### mdharbin — 2025-04-15T21:34:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lloyd%E2%80%99s-als-journey-a-family-path-toward-light-nature-and-hope.30696/post-350748>

> [MTA321 said:](/goto/post?id=350738)
>
> Don't have much to add but thank you for the detailed journal and I wish you the best of luck.
>
> Can you hardwire the router when it is on? (every little bit may help. there are adapters to run ethernet and connect to cellphone, granted your location may prove a challenge for getting this quickly)
>
> How is the light environment controlled when they go back home for the summer (wifi/towers, mis-matched light-to-food environment, etc)?
>
> Supplements - Noticed you mentioned they were recently removed but if they were taking 20+ supplements (depending on the supplement) it could have significant impact on Rev-erbα - disrupting circadian signaling in the gut - opening mPTP leading to MPP+ (neurotoxin) wreaking havoc on mitochondria and could create a cascading infectious like state speeding up motor neuron destruction.

The router could probably be hardwired and connect to phone. Usually though he turns on the router and goes and sits in the sunlight far away when he wants to use his phone. The ethernet cable would make this more difficult. There is a separate building he can go to about 50ft away from the router that has no electricity and is open to sun.

When he goes home to Moose Jaw there is of course cell towers and wifi everywhere. It's a city of only like 30,000, but densely packed. I recall seeing at least 10 wifi networks to join, and that's just the visible ones. All the food comes from far away as Moose Jaw is basically frozen tundra plains for the majority of the year (neg 30-40C in the winter). Mostly eating frozen veggies and meat.

Regarding the supplements, I'm afraid I don't really understand this. **Is there a blog or podcast you could refer me to so I can study it in a little more detail?** I have only heard Uncle Jack talk about how bad supplements are, never any protocols for stopping them.
It was basically an amalgamation of 2-3 different new age "doctors'" recommendations for what he needed, and he bought everything. The kind of doctors where everything can be fixed with their supplements that they conveniently sell at the counter when you leave. They don't speak about light or circadian biology at all.

There was a bit of overlap between them all as well. Like a separate vitamin D supplement, but also some of the others had their own vitamin D. Everything looked like garbage to me, but I do have a list if you think its worth investigating more.

### MTA321 — 2025-04-15T22:53:38+0300

Yeah, to be honest I was just inferring the possibility of this disruption and downstream effects based on some of the blogs/posts I've read on this forum. I don't think there is a specific blog or post regarding supplements. I would assume it is dose dependent but even things like vitamin D, melatonin, and fish oil (all in pill form) could impact this system if overdone.

All the food comes from far away as Moose Jaw is basically frozen tundra plains for the majority of the year (neg 30-40C in the winter). Mostly eating frozen veggies and meat.

Nick lane discusses this in Power Sex Suicide highly recommend you read it.

[https://www.amazon.com.au/Power-Sex-Suicide-Mitochondria-meaning/dp/0199205647](https://www.amazon.com.au/Power-Sex-Suicide-Mitochondria-meaning/dp/0199205647)

In a nutshell Antioxidants can interfere with mitochondrial signaling.

**"We have already said that leakiness of free radicals causes us to amplify more mitochondria, so it also stands that to live longer requires us to have a more sensitive detection system to pick up that leakiness signal. Many diet gurus tout us taking antioxidants in large doses for health. We now can see this advice runs completely counter to how mitochondria really work. The intracellular environment requires less antioxidants to make sure the signal is heard and mitochondria can be made when the tissue requires it. So taking a ton of antioxidants every day may not be the correct thing to do. This may explain why the studies on exogenous antioxidant use has been so disappointing to so many."** [https://www.jackkruse.com/why-we-die-and-why-we-live/](https://www.jackkruse.com/why-we-die-and-why-we-live/)

ALS is an atavistic onslaught, unraveling the evolutionary triumphs of complex life forged during the Great Oxygenation Event (GOE) by plunging motor neurons into a primal chaos of light and oxidative stress. It reverses the GOE’s oxygen-driven stability, amplifying blue light-induced biophotons and ROS via OPN3, which cascade through CSF to devastate anterior horn cells and NMJs, while myelin loss and dysbiosis (linked to hnRNP A1) shatter neural efficiency. To combat this, patients must shield against blue light with blockers, bolster myelination with DHA-rich diets, and counter ROS with antioxidants, reclaiming control through decentralized, patient-driven strategies that defy the disease’s ancient, destructive pull.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-02T16:10:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/lloyd%E2%80%99s-als-journey-a-family-path-toward-light-nature-and-hope.30696/post-351746>

Is it really understood by you Mat? I do not think you have seen it yet.

**A New Approach that is the oldest approach on Earth, that TIME STAMPED LIFE TO Prioritize Light, Not Food**

To truly address chronic disease, we must shift our focus from food to light. Here’s how:

1.  Prioritize Morning Sunlight Exposure: Spend 15–30 minutes outside at sunrise to harness red and UV light. This optimizes mitochondrial function, supports the TCA cycle, and prevents the Warburg redox shift that leads to chronic disease.
2.  Minimize Artificial Light: To protect your circadian rhythm and mitochondrial health, reduce your exposure to blue light, especially at night. Use blue-blocking glasses or switch to warm lighting after sunset.
3.  Reconnect with Nature’s Rhythms: Align your daily routine with natural light cycles. This means getting outside during the day and avoiding screens before bed to support melatonin production and brain health.
4.  Supplement with Food, Don’t Rely on It: While whole foods are beneficial, they should support, not replace, the foundational role of light. Focus on nutrient-dense foods in the Epi Paleo Rx that complement mitochondrial function, like those rich in the marine food chain. The diet should be based on what grows in the environment where your eyes and skin are sampling. This keeps the paramagnetic switch accurate.
5.  Avoid all supplements to avoid atoms that interfere with photonic signaling and the paramagnetic switch cells rely on. If Cameron Borg actually read my work, he would not have to write such drivel. [https://ricciflow.substack.com/p/why-is-jack-kruse-anti-supplements](https://ricciflow.substack.com/p/why-is-jack-kruse-anti-supplements)
6.  Why is it drivel?

It is incredible what incorrect supplementation can do to a person. If you ran an alive semiconductor factory, as Robert O. Becker showed in his book, amphibians, reptiles, and mammals are.......would you add atoms and elements during your biological photolithography step?

Why does AMD and Intel build their semiconductors in clean rooms but people here continually shit the bed and think adding exogenous atoms that have no place in their semiconductuve phase makes any sense? You know this book lays it out? The semiconductor story is right; it occurs in each one of you, right? What happens when you add dopants to a semiconductor that is not supposed to be in the recipe?

Anyone know?

When you add dopants to a semiconductor that aren’t part of the intended recipe, you’re altering its electrical properties in ways that might not align with its original design. Dopants are impurities added deliberately to tune a semiconductor’s conductivity—n-type dopants (like phosphorus) donate extra electrons, while p-type dopants (like boron) create "holes" by accepting electrons. Suppose you introduce an unplanned dopant (supplement/peptide/drug/jab). In that case, a few things can happen depending on what you add, how much, and the base material (say, silicon or gallium arsenide).

First, the carrier concentration changes. If you accidentally dope an intrinsic (pure) semiconductor with an n-type dopant when it wasn’t meant to be, you’ll flood it with free electrons, making it conductive in a way it wasn’t supposed to be. If it’s already doped (say, p-type) and you add an opposite-type dopant (n-type), you could compensate the existing carriers—holes and electrons cancel each other out, reducing conductivity or even flipping the material’s type entirely if the new dopant overpowers the old one. Second, the bandgap and electronic structure can shift. Do you know what the bandgap of Lithium is and what it implies?

Dopants don’t just add carriers; they can mess with the lattice and energy levels. An unintended heavy dopant (like a rare earth element in silicon) might introduce deep-level traps, states in the bandgap that snag carriers and kill efficiency in devices like transistors or LEDs. Third, crystal defects can pile up.

Semiconductors are finicky; their performance hinges on a pristine lattice. While well-intentioned, the MAHA movement’s food-first approach misses the mark because it fails to address life's photonic and electrical nature. Chronic disease is not a nutritional problem; it’s a lifestyle problem related 100% to LIGHT STRESS that destroys mtDNA, making the LIGHT in us. mtDNA is the KEY SEMICONDUCTOR OF LIFE AS IT MAKES UPEs. What do you think surgery/tattoos do to the optical density of the tissue below? You think there is no consequence for altering the electronic state of tissues in an nnEMF world?

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Realigning with nature’s signals, starting with sunrise, can restore our mitochondrial health, support the TCA cycle, and mitigate the paramagnetic flip that drives modern illness. It’s time to stop treating food as the sole solution and start seeing ourselves as part of nature’s electrical fabric. Only then can we truly make America and the world healthy again.
