Jack, I'm on board and understand the point you are getting across here. Re: bandgap & of elemental Li - based on my understanding BG is the energy difference between the valence band (i.e. where electrons are bound) and the conduction band (i.e. where electrons are free to move).. Li is a conductor with a BG of ~0 as it has free moving electrons, (that's why it's highly reactive and conductive).. However, Li compounds have larger band gaps e.g. (LiOR) ~>4 eV or (Li2CO3) ~ 5.0 eV. In the compounds this (i.e. BG) would be dependant on the crystal structure of the compound not so much the elemental Li as the band gap is not limited to elemental atoms alone, but the crystal structure of the bonding environment of the entire compound..

Band structures emerge from collective atomic orbitals, thus Li properties as a "metal" do not dominate in say Li2CO3 as once it is ionized and bonded to CO3 2- in a 3D lattice the electronic environment changes..

Crystal structure dictates electron delocalisation e.g Li metal electrons are delocalized and free = no band gap (good conductor) -- however in Li2CO3, the lattice is tightly bound, thus valence electrons are localized ~ O2- & C4+ = little overlap between valence and conduction orbitals = large band gap ~ 5 eV (more of an insulator now)..

Li+ is fully ionised - it exists as a Li+ ion that has lost its outer most electron (individual Li+ cation doesn't contribute metallic conductivity or free electron behaviour of its own..

Re: quantum thermodynamics and biological field effects, isn’t it possible that some atomic 'dopants’ Li + - might act as local stabilizers or symmetry-breakers in otherwise decoherent tissues, especially under high entropy load (like in demyelination)..

From a solid-state physics standpoint, dopants aren’t always contaminants - in some lattices they’re essential to enable function (e.g., Josephson junctions, topological insulators, or memristive synapse analogs). Li + has a narrow ionic radius (~ 0.76 A), and in biological water-structured lattices, could it not act like a weak paramagnetic ‘phase lock’ in disrupted myelin or astroglial gap junctions - especially if the surrounding quantum field (resonance, natural light, magnetism) is properly restored?

In other words, are we throwing out the idea of selective coherence-stabilizing atoms too fast, assuming all dopants degrade bio-semiconductor fidelity, when in some cases - especially under light/circadian repair protocols - they may briefly function as training wheels to guide the lattice back toward coherence? Would love to hear your thoughts on adaptive phase engineering vs long-term decoherence risk in lithium's case." Do we fully understand this yet to say this is a bad idea or is the baby getting thrown out with the bath water? I’m new to this field so very grateful for your guidance and input here as always.

# Thread: Sam's Optimal Journal: Phoenix Rising
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sams-optimal-journal-phoenix-rising.30703/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### samantha_jordan — 2025-04-16T21:06:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sams-optimal-journal-phoenix-rising.30703/post-350798>

I'm so grateful to Dr. Kruse and to be here in this moment. I felt close to leaving this plane and found the needed trailhead in the nick of time.

Me: 36 years old, live in Rural Oregon, mother to a 3 year old beautiful soul, artist, lover of life

I found Dr. Kruse's work through Danny Jones. Hope expanded for me after this. My intuitive belief that the Sun was essential and would not cause harm was affirmed. My belief that we are creature's of light had a physical map of understanding laid out.

Before I start my history of struggles, I want to tell myself I am good. I am enough. I am a phoenix rising. I am beautiful. I am strong. I've always pushed and worked so hard. I have survived so much, to make it to this moment. There is hope for me to improve, to grow to FLOURISH. I've spent countless days/hours/months camping, hiking and soaking in hotsprings, feeling love and light all around me.

Physical battles to have overcome:

2003 (age 15) weird "viral" response. Tested positive for Epstein Barr. Symptoms: extremely painful migraine lasting 10+ hours, at some point temperature read 104. Took to hospital.
Fever broke, but was unable to respond/slept almost non stop for 4 days.
Frequent streptococcus and tonsillitis through middle school/highscool/young 20's
2009 sadly had an abortion that led to symptoms of depression/anxiety/derealization. Started fluoxetine and was on and off (average 3 years, on 3 years off) since my final quit after finding Dr Kruse's work 2024.
Hairstylist from 2008-2022 (chemical exposure/heavy metals)
Became severely responsive to any common virus and would indefinitely turn into ear infections/tonsillitis/sinus infection/bronchitis and two boughts of pneumonia. Bronchitis/sinus infections would repeatedly prolong for up to 3 months. Would become so ill would be unable to work and desperately sought medical help. Was given countless series of antibiotics, prednisone and diagnosed with mild asthma.
Found out was living in heavy black mold (floors caved in bathroom).
HSV II diagnosis 2014
Tonsillectomy 2016ish after 8 month long tonsillitis that ended up in fatigue so heavy I could hardly stay awake.
Had some relief after tonsillectomy and was chasing the sun for two years with the best tan of my life.
Carpal tunnel syndrome diagnosis
Developed severe pain in base of spine, hips, glutes, sciatica that wouldn't quit for months.
Had always had extremely tight muscles, neck pain.
Frequent stomach pain, nausea and diarrhea.
Started breaking out in hives.
No solid bowel movements for long periods up to 2 years.
Felt extremely tired again.
Fibromyalgia diagnosis 2017
Stomach pain and nausea became increasingly worse. Horrible stomach pain in the morning.
Saw gastroentonoligist 2018. Was told likely an ulcer and they wanted to scope and put me on protonpump inhibitors, I declined.
Went keto for 3 months, closely monitored my diet. This did not resolve stomach pain and nausea.
Blood testing for allergies. Found mild Ig allergy to eggs. Had definite relief when removing eggs as they were a primary food source. Still struggled with some pain and nausea.
Bilateral osteoarthritis knee diagnosis 2019
2022 pregnancy. Vomiting and nausea to the extreme for the entire gestation. Lived in the toilet bowl. Couldn't handle eating anything but carbs. Meat smelled like it was rotting. Had relief of chronic pain during this time. Naturally went into labor after being pregnant for exactly 43 weeks, refusing fear mongering to induce. Was committed to natural birth, but after 44+ hours of labor she wouldn't descend and ended up having a c section.

Was able to eat again after pregnancy. Had good digestive for awhile, but life events led to a place of extreme stress. The chronic nausea returned and eventually led to daily vomiting. I felt extremely unwell. Began having strong dizziness spells where I felt like I was going to the sky. Developed tremors that went on for months and knew something was very wrong.

Then I found Dr Kruse's work and everything has begun to change ☀️

Now:

I've had complete relief of stomach pain, nausea, vomiting. I intuitively believe I had an ulcer that healed. Not living with daily nausea after so many years is INCREDIBLE.

Tremors are gone. I no longer feel nearness of death/neurological symptoms

Unspeakably grateful for the time and relief gifted to me by this incredible knowledge.

Habits:

Wake up with the sunrise. Barefoot/least clothes possible outside every morning, accomplished through the winter. Sun salutations in the mud and now the spring sun finally warms me. Minimum 30 minutes morning sun.

Asleep by 10pm. Committed to this, but still in training

Reducing screen use and artificial light significantly. I acknowledge I still have room for improvement here, but I'm far from the place I was.

Grounding sheet on bed, walking barefoot everywhere possible.

Methylene blue: have consumed over 800mg since October (troscriptions). Incredible feeling of life after using.

Maximum time spent outside possible.

Hormone testing done in December after feelings of so much improvement (Note testing was done at optimal time in cycle, just forgot to write date down):

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Improvement Needed: Though I have had miraculous physical relief, I feel my hormones are still trashed. Intellectually I believe in my resiliency and power to heal. Mentally, I'm struggling. I'm blessed to say my life circumstances are wonderful. I've manifested my dreams into reality. Yet I feel an emotionally battle that must be primarily physical. Though I fall asleep easily, I wake up too early often (530AM), with so much anxiety (high cortisol probably). I feel a constant mental struggle to encourage myself and know that I'm successful. I do not feel I've reached my highest potential of optimal health. I'm willing to make bigger changes, but not sure the best direction. I've always been a workhorse and consistent. My goal is to maintain consistency in these life improvements and see what may come. To be patient and know that I am enough.

Quitting vaping nicotine. I am going to do this and had 5 years with no nicotine previously. It is a crutch for relieving physical anxiety. I'm two months off cannabis which I'm very proud of. I was an extremely heavy concentrate smoker for almost 20 years and now I'm done.

Looking forward to improvement of my hormone function. So encouraged after reviewing my progress here. I can see my journey a bit clearer after writing it out.

I want to know I'm at optimal health levels. When I review this I know I will see more progress. I'm open to growing and learning and that journey is not always smooth. I'm opening myself up to hear what I need to change and how I can create the feelings of wellness that I crave.

Love & Light

### samantha_jordan — 2025-04-25T20:34:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sams-optimal-journal-phoenix-rising.30703/post-351277>

Last night was my 4th day cutting out all artificial light after sundown/powering off wifi. I'm so proud of breaking these addictions. The silence at night is so relieving, soothing to me. I become tired as the sun goes down.
I'm deeply aware of the escapism from reality I've relied on for so long. I feel things are much clearer. When I lay onto the earth at sunrise, my vision snaps clear.
I've always been a perceptive individual, intuitively knowing truth. I've known many actions by the general public are folly. I've protected myself from disgusting vaccinations and trash food and lies, by sheer will and belief in my truth. Yet the transformation I'm experiencing now is radical and humbling. All of the escapism, the pacifiers that have misaligned me, are falling away.
Yet it is painful. I woke last night at 2, unable to fall back asleep as irrational concerns chatted incessantly in my mind. I can feel the front part of my brain, where my third eye is, pulsing me awake when I wish to sleep. Maybe this is my time for contemplation and not for rest yet. Maybe it is hormones out of balance and begging for the right care.
I'm going to rise up. I'm going to differentiate the duality within myself.
I incessantly dream of the beach and moving somewhere better. I worry that I can't reach my healthy goals here, despite incredible gains over the last 6 months of morning sun gazing and dirt hugging. There is hair regrowing all over my head I didn't even know I'd lost. There is light rebuilding inside me, creating a new glow more radiant than ever. There is passion to change resonating from me. There is courage growing that has allowed me to throw the crutches away. Without the crutches I'm limping, dragging, but I believe I will run. I believe I will be able to give so much to a community and to feel the warm hug of communion with other beautiful individuals. The happiness will grow inside of me and create beauty all around me.
I will cast aside the doubt, the shame. Shining, I will hold my daughter in the light and triumph.

### mfawski — 2025-04-25T21:01:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sams-optimal-journal-phoenix-rising.30703/post-351280>

Amazing Samantha! Thank you for sharing

### samantha_jordan — 2025-04-25T22:58:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sams-optimal-journal-phoenix-rising.30703/post-351286>

> [mfawski said:](/goto/post?id=351280)
>
> Amazing Samantha! Thank you for sharing

Thank you for the care and encouragement, it means so much

Dare to bleed for your own truth, or beg no aid from others’ hands.

Stake your soul, your sweat, your fire, skin in the game demands your stand.

Embrace the crucible’s cruel bite; let suffering forge your iron core.

You’ll never grasp your boundless might until you shatter the timid door.

Sometimes, in straying, you uncover what you sought, or it finds you, unasked.

Fools think power’s won through secret maps, yet shun the thorns of their own way.

True might is carved on clandestine trails, forge yours, or kneel to others’ sway.

# Thread: Morning routine question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-routine-question.30712/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 10
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### AntonisK — 2025-04-18T16:32:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-routine-question.30712/post-350888>

Sorry if answered before (I tried to search but found no direct answer), but I would like to know a) why have breakfast within 30 mins after waking and b) why eating (breakfast or other meal) before sun exposure.
Thanks in advance!
Happy Easter to all!!

### JHackett — 2025-04-18T23:21:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-routine-question.30712/post-350912>

Leptin Rx 101.

**A**. Because that’s when you're primed to receive food as a time signal. If you delay food too long, especially in a chronically inflamed or LR state, you force your body into an stress-dominant state, pushing adrenaline and cortisol higher than they need to be. This blunts leptin signaling, which leads to LR and IR and glucose instability.

**B**. Sunrise is first. Bottom line that Jack mentioned recently? Sunrise to turn on TCA cycle --> Food to utilize it properly.

### MTA321 — 2025-04-19T04:10:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-routine-question.30712/post-350924>

There are a few factors as mentioned above. Another potential reason would be eating before UV shows up as NO will put a damper on CCO slowing ETC and reducing ATP production.

### AntonisK — 2025-04-19T08:38:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-routine-question.30712/post-350926>

Thank you all for the replies!
I'm just curious as why Jack sometimes suggests eating before sun exposure and sometimes suggests the opposite. Perhaps it depends on the current situation every time?
I mean, as far as I can remember it seems he suggests eating before sun exposure for the general population (preventive) and eating after sun exposure for people on certain disorders (cancer, autoimmune etc.) as a "cure", am I right?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-19T16:39:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-routine-question.30712/post-350933>

Jack never says that. The voices in your head must be telling that. The Leptin Rx is CLEAR on this point. The voices in your head are confused.

### JHackett — 2025-04-19T17:16:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-routine-question.30712/post-350935>

My only thought is that maybe you wake up really early and still thinking you should eat within 30 minutes?

Eating within 30 minutes of waking assumes you wake with the sunrise. Eating within 60 minutes is ok for those with leptin sensitivity.

If you wake up early the lights should stay off and sunrise should still be the first light you see. Once you see that light, your 30-60 minute “timer” begins.

As an example, I will admit I’m not perfect about this from a light perspective and really enjoy pre-sunrise sauna with red light. I’ll wake at 5:30 for sauna/red light, get out around 6:15 for a cold plunge and sunrise gazing, then slowly start making breakfast (typically a 5 egg cheese omelette +/- sardines).

> [AntonisK said:](/goto/post?id=350926)
>
> Thank you all for the replies!
> I'm just curious as why Jack sometimes suggests eating before sun exposure and sometimes suggests the opposite. Perhaps it depends on the current situation every time?
> I mean, as far as I can remember it seems he suggests eating before sun exposure for the general population (preventive) and eating after sun exposure for people on certain disorders (cancer, autoimmune etc.) as a "cure", am I right?

With all the respect and no intention to question you or try to be wise-guy.

In the "Don Greewell's journal" [https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/don-greenwells-journal.30511/#post-349068](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/don-greenwells-journal.30511/#post-349068) you asnwered "What: At sunrise (around 6:30-7 AM now in March 2025), go outside, shirtless if possible, no glasses, for 20-600 minutes. Face east, let UVB hit your eyes and skin GROUNDED. Then eat 50-75g protein (e.g., eggs, bacon, beef) with <50g carbs, high fat (butter, ghee) within 30 minutes of waking."
From that, I concluded that he has to eat after sun exposure (underline and color mine). So, I supposed that it might be a specific advice for Don's specific problems, that's all!

Glad you made it clear though, now the voices in my head vanished!
Thanks again!

In ES I stayed in multiple locations, hung out with dozens of people and this is what everyone does. Get up, watch the sun rise, eat protein within 30 mins. (+/-)

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-05T00:28:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-flux-of-deserts.30717/post-353919>

Earth’s Geophysical Responses: Electromagnetic Coupling is the Key

Plasma cosmology suggests that Earth’s geophysical processes are tightly coupled to the heliosphere’s electric dynamics, with implications for the magnetosphere, climate, and tectonic activity.

Magnetospheric Modulation: Birkeland currents link the Sun to Earth, driving auroras and modulating the magnetosphere (Egeland & Burke, 2010). A declining solar electric output weakens Earth’s magnetic field (5% per decade, SWARM data, 2019), mirroring Mars’ magnetic collapse 4.1 billion years ago. This increases cosmic radiation penetration, which interacts with Earth’s iron-rich core, inducing currents that mimic stellar processes (iron absorbing radiation, as in a dying star).

Schumann Resonance Shifts: The Schumann Resonance (SR, peaking at 7.83 Hz) is a global electromagnetic resonance modulated by solar activity. Plasma cosmology predicts that SR shifts reflect changes in the heliosphere’s electric field. Recent SR frequency increases correlate with global temperature rises (Sekiguchi & Hayakawa, 2006), suggesting enhanced energy transfer from the core to the surface, driven by electromagnetic interactions rather than purely thermal processes.

Tectonic and Volcanic Activity: The ESM and plasma cosmology link a weakening solar electric field to a slowing geodynamo, reducing Earth’s rotation and increasing seismic/volcanic activity. This energy transfer heats the oceans (15% current acceleration over two decades), disrupts marine ecosystems, and parallels the core-magnetosphere dynamics seen in plasma simulations (Peratt, 2014).

Biological Implications: Quantum Electromagnetic Interactions of living in a Desert

Plasma cosmology’s emphasis on electromagnetic forces extends to the biosphere, where living organisms are influenced by the Sun’s electric output through quantum biological mechanisms.

Iron as a Cosmic Mediator: Iron, central to plasma-driven transmutation on the Sun’s surface (SAFIRE, 2020), is also critical for life. During the Great Oxygenation Event (GOE, 2.4 billion years ago), iron enabled oxygen-based energy production in mitochondria. The Sun’s electric field, via UV light (250–3100 nm), excites iron-containing proteins like cytochrome c oxidase (CCO), producing deuterium-depleted water (DDW) essential for cellular energy. A weakening solar electric field disrupts this process, increasing heteroplasmy and mitochondrial dysfunction.

UPEs and Leptin Resistance: Ultraweak photon emissions (UPEs, 100–300 nm) from mitochondrial ROS encode quantum information. Plasma cosmology predicts that solar UV variability, driven by galactic currents, affects UPE fidelity. A declining solar electric output (e.g., 2025 coronal holes) impairs UPEs at 220 nm, disrupting leptin signaling and leading to “quantum failure”—a driver of metabolic diseases like obesity (Toilekis, PhD thesis).

Circadian Disruption by nnEMF: The heliosphere’s electric field synchronizes circadian rhythms via the suprachiasmatic nucleus (SCN). Non-native EMF (nnEMF) from human sources (e.g., power lines, FCC regulations) decouples this resonance (Helliwell, 1975), increasing ROS and impairing mitochondrial coherence, mirroring the electromagnetic chaos seen in plasma experiments (SAFIRE, 2020).

Planetary Evolution: Mars as a Plasma Cosmology Case Study does not have to take a long time to occur.

Plasma cosmology provides a new lens for understanding Mars’ collapse 4.1 billion years ago. The ESM suggests that Mars lost its magnetic field due to a prolonged reduction in solar electric input, halting the geodynamo via a massive electric burst from the sun hitting Mars. It happened rapidly. Without a magnetic field, solar wind stripped Mars’ atmosphere, collapsing its energy cycles and leaving iron oxides on the surface—a relic of electromagnetic depletion. Deserts are the scar of previous electromagnetic destruction. How many times have I told you all deserts on Earth are at the 28 latitude. Why? The corriolis effect due ultimately to the sun DO It. The Ancients knew it and now the same thing is true in your heme proteins and nobody seems to be awake to it yet..........

Earth’s current trajectory, with a weakening magnetic field and nnEMF interference, risks a similar fate, as plasma cosmology highlights the primacy of electromagnetic forces in sustaining planetary habitability. It also underpins human survival.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-05T02:09:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/magnetic-flux-of-deserts.30717/post-353945>

**The Five Major Extinction Events Through the Lens of Plasma Cosmology and the Electric Sun Model**

The integration of plasma cosmology and the Electric Sun Model (ESM) into Earth’s historical narrative fundamentally reframes the mechanisms driving the five major extinction events: the Ordovician-Silurian (443 Ma), Late Devonian (372 Ma), Permian-Triassic (252 Ma), Triassic-Jurassic (201 Ma), and Cretaceous-Paleogene (66 Ma). Traditional explanations often focus on volcanic activity, asteroid impacts, or climate shifts driven by greenhouse gases, rooted in gravity-centric models of the Solar System. Plasma cosmology, however, emphasizes electromagnetic forces, galactic currents, and solar electric variability as dominant drivers of planetary and biological change. This section reinterprets these extinction events through this lens, drawing on first-principles from the provided bibliography (e.g., Birkeland, Alfvén, SAFIRE project), and offers probabilistic predictions for future events based on current trends as of June 2025.

**Interpreting the Five Extinction Events with an electric universe perspective**

1.1 Ordovician-Silurian Extinction (443 Ma): Galactic Current Surge and Magnetic Disruption

Traditional View: Two pulses of extinction, linked to glaciation and sea level changes, killed ~85% of marine species. Gamma-ray bursts or volcanic activity are often cited as triggers in the lamestream science.

Plasma Cosmology Perspective: A surge in galactic Birkeland currents, as proposed by Alfvén (1937), might have altered the Sun’s electric output, amplifying its UV/X-ray emissions (SAFIRE, 2020). This would weaken Earth’s magnetosphere, allowing cosmic radiation to penetrate and interact with the iron-rich core, inducing geomagnetic reversals. Increased radiation would disrupt marine ecosystems by impairing photosynthesis in plankton, a base of the food chain, while core heating would drive ocean anoxia, as seen in the 90% species loss.

Evidence Reframed: Sedimentary records show anoxic events and isotopic anomalies (e.g., δ13C shifts). Plasma cosmology interprets these as electromagnetic-induced heating and radiation effects, not solely volcanic or climatic. I am with the EU Model.

1.2 Late Devonian Extinction (372 Ma): Solar Electric Minimum and Core Inactivity

Traditional View: Multiple events over ~20 million years, attributed to global cooling, anoxia, and possibly asteroid impacts, killed ~70% of species, particularly reef builders.

Plasma Cosmology Perspective: A prolonged solar electric minimum, akin to the 2025 coronal holes, reduced the Sun’s electric field, slowing Earth’s geodynamo. This weakened the magnetic field, increasing cosmic radiation exposure, which stressed photosynthetic organisms like corals and algae. Reduced core activity would lower volcanic outgassing, cooling the planet, while electromagnetic decoupling of ocean currents (Birkeland currents’ influence on Earth) would lead to widespread anoxia. This is why evolution built HIF 1 alpha and linked it to PER clock genes.

Evidence Reframed: Black shales (anoxia indicators) and mass die-offs of shallow-water species align with electromagnetic disruption of ocean circulation and UV-driven stress on marine life.

1.3 Permian-Triassic Extinction (252 Ma): Galactic Plasma Shock and Core Overload know as the GREAT DYING

Traditional View: The “Great Dying,” killing ~90% of species, is linked to Siberian Traps volcanism, methane release, and extreme warming.

Plasma Cosmology Perspective: A galactic plasma shockwave, possibly from a nearby supernova interacting with the heliosphere (Peratt, 2014), would have overloaded the Sun’s photo-electric circuit, triggering intense solar flares and CMEs (Scott, 2015). This would induce massive currents in Earth’s core, driving unprecedented volcanic activity (e.g., Siberian Traps) and heating oceans, as seen in the 15% ocean current acceleration today. Anything using a photo bioelectric current would have massive electrical scars in tissues. It would mimic MARS or what happens in some LASIK patients. They kill them selves due to the photoelectric injuries they sustaned and their doctors are impotent to help them. The magnetic field would collapse temporarily, exposing life to lethal radiation levels, while electromagnetic-induced heating would release methane, exacerbating warming. Iron in marine ecosystems would catalyze ROS, leading to widespread ferroptosis in living things causing electrical scars. This was a real kill shot. People living in 5G cities are experiencing this right now. TROPICS heal this.

Evidence Reframed: The rapid onset of anoxia, δ13C anomalies, and fungal spikes suggest a sudden electromagnetic catastrophe, not a gradual volcanic process.

1.4 Triassic-Jurassic Extinction (201 Ma): Solar Electric Instability and Tectonic Uplift

Traditional View: Central Atlantic Magmatic Province (CAMP) volcanism, CO2-driven warming, and ocean acidification killed ~80% of species.

Plasma Cosmology Perspective: Instability in the Sun’s electric output, driven by fluctuating galactic currents (Birkeland, 1902-1903), would induce core currents, accelerating tectonic activity. This would trigger CAMP volcanism, while a weakened magnetosphere increases cosmic radiation, stressing terrestrial and marine ecosystems. UV variability would disrupt photosynthesis, and electromagnetic decoupling of ocean currents would lead to acidification and anoxia. Note all extinction events have hypoxia and link to the GOE and evolution of the opsin and heme proteins.

Evidence Reframed: CAMP flood basalts and rapid CO2 spikes align with electromagnetically induced tectonic activity, while mass die-offs reflect radiation and UV stress.

1.5 Cretaceous-Paleogene Extinction (66 Ma): Heliospheric Plasma Event and Impact Amplification: FACTOR X

Traditional View: The Chicxulub asteroid impact, combined with Deccan Traps volcanism, killed ~75% of species, including non-avian dinosaurs.

Plasma Cosmology Perspective: A heliospheric plasma event, possibly a plasmoid (Bostick, 1956), could have destabilized the Sun’s electric field, attracting charged asteroids like Chicxulub via electromagnetic forces (Peratt, 2014). The impact would amplify an already stressed system, where a weakened solar electric output reduced Earth’s magnetic shield, exposing life to cosmic radiation. Core heating would trigger Deccan volcanism, while radiation and UV variability would disrupt photosynthesis and food chains. This heliospheric event is mimicking the modern world since 1893 invention of the TESLA power grid. We are making EARTH a great target for a CME kill shot.

Evidence Reframed: The iridium layer and shocked quartz from Chicxulub are consistent with an impact, but global fern spikes and rapid climate shifts suggest a broader electromagnetic catastrophe ------> Auxin brake. Links to the mammalian VDR and CCO break on the IMM.

2\. Most Probable Predictions for Future Extinction Events (as of June 2025)

Plasma cosmology, combined with current trends in solar activity, geomagnetic weakening, and human-induced disruptions, allows for probabilistic predictions of future extinction risks.

2.1 Near-Term Risk: Solar Electric Minimum and nnEMF Amplification (2025–2050) ALL 5 previous one allow me to predict we are in the 6th one since 1893.

Probability: High (~70%)

2.2 Medium-Term Risk: Galactic Current Surge and Core Overload (2050–2100)

This would induce massive core currents, driving volcanic activity (similar to the Permian-Triassic event) and ocean anoxia. The magnetic field would weaken further, exposing life to lethal radiation levels, while iron-driven ROS in ecosystems would cause ferroptosis on a global scale.

2.3 Long-Term Risk: Mars-Like Magnetic Collapse (2100–2500)

Without a magnetic field, solar wind would strip the atmosphere, collapsing water’s electromagnetic resonance and energy cycles. Iron oxides would accumulate on the surface, signaling a dead planet.

Did you know weight lifting is beneficial in lowering all-cause of mortality in CVD and cancer until you hit approximately 140 minutes a week...So exercise/weight training has a context. Did your doctor tell you this? More than 140 minutes a week, then your risk of death actually increases. The whole truth and nothing but the fucking truth even it offends your ancestors. That is what we tell guys who just came back from death.

### John Warner — 2025-05-04T17:20:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-tell-those-who-just-died-but-came-back-from-the-abyss-about-exercise.30723/post-351892>

Optimal exercise, walking movement, meaning just moving through nature, at what speed nature dictates, tribes run there food down tracking pace, sprints have to be involved sometimes. sometimes climbing, sometimes crawling. Do what nature requires. Weight lifting is hot garbage, especially in a gym. Just move in nature, Quit being zoo animals, Yes I have a job, and drive, and live in home, working on getting rid of all that,
we drive down our food in a car in a blue light grocery store.

### Rachel Martin — 2025-05-05T16:14:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-tell-those-who-just-died-but-came-back-from-the-abyss-about-exercise.30723/post-351932>

I notice that we humans love to put things into nice, neat little algorithms with tables, charts, and timetables for everything that is both good or bad.

There's such a vibe change for me when I just consider that exercise is made up garbage and if I simply DO more of the things instead of OUTSOURCE most of the things, then I will have movement naturally built into my day.

I've been gardening (Virginia is in its sweet, Spring season), watering all of my flowers, cooking, tinkering with herbalism (taking a class locally at a farm), playing with the kids, staying on top of laundry and chores, and it's just a very active life. I pick up my 4 year old who loves to be held and tote her around. I'm up and down the stairs all day. I'm outside as often as possible and weeding the garden. I'll take a lunchtime stroll around my neighborhood.

It's not complicated. We love to make it complicated. It's so elegant it's laughable. We waste so much precious energy trying to fix problems that aren't really problems that we're missing the whole point! There is so much abundance to be enjoyed if we can get out of our own way and connect with nature.

Jack brought me in with the science but that's not what keeps me here. It's the essence of it. It's the return to nature - the wonder and awe that I feel watching a sunrise each morning while enjoying my tea and reading a book. It's the way the light hits the particles floating in the air during that magical evening hour when my kids are frolicking in the front yard and hanging on trees, while I enjoy my feet in the grass and my husband's company. All I did was go outside more, and everything changed.

They want us to be too busy to notice that there is magic everywhere. That we have everything we need - we don't need bigger, faster, better, stronger. It makes me so angry. We are already new and improved. Why do we think we aren't intelligently designed? How much energy have I wasted over the years chasing exercise programs or other nonsense? I'll be 40 in a few months and it just hits differently. Time is so precious.

Sending good vibes to all! I would apologize for the rant, but I know someone somewhere will resonate with me.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-11T21:54:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-tell-those-who-just-died-but-came-back-from-the-abyss-about-exercise.30723/post-352328>

I have spent a lot of time looking at what the “alternatives” are vs what the world says. Looking at the actual results people were getting and applying what resonated as truth to me at that time of my life. Always be a truth seeker not the defender of a position.

Light - the sun is the best there is, everything else is “sub” - stick to Nature.
Food - the more natural and local the better, avoid eating things you can’t pronounce - stick to Nature.
Exercise - I just spent a week measuring and mitigating two houses, one was three floors, People do NOT need a gym they need movement.
nnEMF - one of those houses was “smart” - the more technology you have the more nnEMF you have. Want to avoid nnEMF - avoid technology.
For most of us the #1 nnEMF concern is not the local cell tower or “smart” meter it is the phone in your hand and/or computer in front of you and the 60Hz (50Hz) AC field from the wires in your walls, floor and ceiling.

### GavinH — 2025-05-12T07:44:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-tell-those-who-just-died-but-came-back-from-the-abyss-about-exercise.30723/post-352359>

### Mat.I — 2025-05-12T09:37:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-tell-those-who-just-died-but-came-back-from-the-abyss-about-exercise.30723/post-352360>

You didn't escape death to follow the same centralised script that put you there.. You came back to remember - that your body is a solar antenna (not a gym project ) and that healing is electric. Time isn't money it is light..

Wake with the sun, barefoot, eyes to the light, set your clocks and ignite your redox engine..
Face the cold, cold sharpens and uncouples you - mito do not need comfort..
Touch the earth - your body is electric, ground it and let electrons flow.
Bathe in the sun - our ancestors built collagen, melanin and dopamine under it.
Move like nature, but only under the sky, not under LED slavery..
Catch the sunset, kill the lights, let the melatonin rise, sleep isn't rest it is resurrection
Sleep deep.
Still your mind - don't be a slave to dopamine and distraction..
Light charges you, cold hardens you, magnetism orients you.
Cells are thirsty for coherence.
Food is light, you do not burn calories you decode photons.
Be a decentralised savage to destroy illusions.
Be curious enough to rebuild from light.
First principles are the fire.
A vain society is easy to control. A coherent one in sync with nature is impossible to cage..
A body that chases praise is easy to manipulate. A body that chases the sun is free..

### Pete Collins — 2025-05-15T06:50:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-tell-those-who-just-died-but-came-back-from-the-abyss-about-exercise.30723/post-352526>

> [greentrees said:](/goto/post?id=352423)
>
> Body By Science by Doug McGuff might be the closest to a mitochondriac weight lifting program
>
> I've been building an outdoor gym with free weights [https://blas.com/body-by-science/](https://blas.com/body-by-science/)
>
> If you hit muscular failure within 2 minutes of each movement, you only work out for 10 minutes every 4-10 days (depends on recovery of fast twitch muscle fibers). Strength > hypertrophy imo

I am extremely familiar with Dr Doug McGuff's work. I do like the guy and how he moved people away from exercise madness. I take issue with the concept of muscular failure, is it hypoxia, is it the exercise machine, is it internal mechanical stress and on and on, as a Mito, we should now understand that if we could achieve this so called 'true muscular failure' during a set of lifting, you would have bypassed natures protective systems and drop dead on the spot, it really is a fallacy & idiots like Drew Baye and the HIT crowd, steal money from their legions of followers, lying & taking testosterone boosters & roids to support their bullshit. Doug I think is a guy who has the right intent, I like his broader attitude to life, he just needs to get out of the centralised medical cluster fuck.

### greentrees — 2025-05-19T07:17:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-tell-those-who-just-died-but-came-back-from-the-abyss-about-exercise.30723/post-352873>

True muscular failure is sequentially recruiting the slow twitch, in-between twitch and finally exhausting the fast twitch fibers of their stored sugar. It's recruitment of the full range of muscle fibers through mechanical stress you would encounter in nature. You could do a body weight version in a forest applying the principles. It's just hard to progressively load week by week thus the free weights (more controlled and defined)

You haven't bypassed natures protective systems, you're engaging and activating them. You won't drop dead on the spot, you'll deplete fast twitch of glycogen and send a nervous system signal that you need to develop more slow and fast twitch fibers. The way I look at it is its a controlled, once a week nervous system signal being sent and our ancestors at least had that. The craziest thing is I've never felt this connected to my body, doing compound exercises barefoot under the sun has really improved my ability to move in general and its only been 3 weeks, 3 workout sessions (10-15mins)

As for the roids, we have the ace up our sleeve in nature  It's a missing piece for body builders, the natural roids

I also have this theory I'll raise my testosterone in general by having muscle. I'm not looking for hypertrophy, just strength. I'm only just inside the healthy range of BMI and need to do something, I was very weak just a month ago

First principle thinking asks to define muscular failure, nobody not even Ken Hutchins has done this. Getting strong is a solid approach along with an activated TCA cycle. Well under the 140 mins a week too.

### greentrees — 2025-05-19T09:42:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-tell-those-who-just-died-but-came-back-from-the-abyss-about-exercise.30723/post-352883>

Not sure about the others, I used to follow Drew Baye too. But muscular failure should just be when the muscle fails? It can no longer exert its full force in that range of motion. Technically its when all muscle fibers have been recruited, including fast twitch (no fast twitch activation = you won't hit muscular failure, your muscles are just tired). You can do that by performing a 1 rep max lift that can't be done for a 2nd rep. No glycogen and the fast fibers are damaged after 1 rep. Try a week later and with replenished stores + repaired fibers, should be able to perform a higher 1 rep max as more fast twitch muscle fibers have developed (bigger muscle). Muscular failure ends when fast twitch is repaired + is full of glycogen which is 4-10 days on average based off the book (I wonder if a mito lifestyle would move someone from a 7 day recovery to a 5 day recovery etc.)

### MJ Vango — 2025-07-25T04:15:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-tell-those-who-just-died-but-came-back-from-the-abyss-about-exercise.30723/post-356931>

> [Rachel Martin said:](/goto/post?id=351932)
>
> I notice that we humans love to put things into nice, neat little algorithms with tables, charts, and timetables for everything that is both good or bad.
>
> There's such a vibe change for me when I just consider that exercise is made up garbage and if I simply DO more of the things instead of OUTSOURCE most of the things, then I will have movement naturally built into my day.
>
> I've been gardening (Virginia is in its sweet, Spring season), watering all of my flowers, cooking, tinkering with herbalism (taking a class locally at a farm), playing with the kids, staying on top of laundry and chores, and it's just a very active life. I pick up my 4 year old who loves to be held and tote her around. I'm up and down the stairs all day. I'm outside as often as possible and weeding the garden. I'll take a lunchtime stroll around my neighborhood.
>
> It's not complicated. We love to make it complicated. It's so elegant it's laughable. We waste so much precious energy trying to fix problems that aren't really problems that we're missing the whole point! There is so much abundance to be enjoyed if we can get out of our own way and connect with nature.
>
> Jack brought me in with the science but that's not what keeps me here. It's the essence of it. It's the return to nature - the wonder and awe that I feel watching a sunrise each morning while enjoying my tea and reading a book. It's the way the light hits the particles floating in the air during that magical evening hour when my kids are frolicking in the front yard and hanging on trees, while I enjoy my feet in the grass and my husband's company. All I did was go outside more, and everything changed.
>
> They want us to be too busy to notice that there is magic everywhere. That we have everything we need - we don't need bigger, faster, better, stronger. It makes me so angry. We are already new and improved. Why do we think we aren't intelligently designed? How much energy have I wasted over the years chasing exercise programs or other nonsense? I'll be 40 in a few months and it just hits differently. Time is so precious.
>
> Sending good vibes to all! I would apologize for the rant, but I know someone somewhere will resonate with me.

Rachel i absolutely love all of this. I have a small homestead in iowa and all of this is so true. Wish we could connect!

### Mark Copeland — 2025-11-24T01:33:09+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-you-tell-those-who-just-died-but-came-back-from-the-abyss-about-exercise.30723/post-363062>

Weight training/hypertrophy training is probably for sure pretty stupid- when did our ancestors have the ability to lift 100lb weights? In random positions until their muscles stopped working? Long term, cardio & bodyweight exercises are for sure the way to go if you want to add exercise into your routine.

Cardio (which is mostly what humans and animals have been doing for millions of years) mainly trains your heart, lungs, and energy systems, and thus improves the oxygen flow to your brain and organs. Weight training (strength & hypertrophy weight training indoors & no ground electrons) does the opposite, it builds large complex muscles and leaves your cardiovascular system the same, lowering your redox potential and ultimately starving your brain and other organs. How does this affect sleep apnea lol? This makes sense why almost all centralized studies favor this type of training, because it also favors disease & profit incentives. Weight training is also easier than cardio, and most people would not be able to work out more than 10-20 minutes of cardio.

Bodyweight exercises (squats pushups sit-ups etc.) are a combination of both, and ultimately it ends up improving the mitochondrial efficiency in your muscles, so it can work even more in your favor. Bodyweight exercises mainly train endurance instead of hypertrophy and strength. Unlike weight training, you are not just getting bigger & building size, you are improving your muscles _inward._ Combining cardio with light bodyweight exercises is the way to go.

# Thread: Surfer’s Ear: ear health tips?  - safest way for me to clean my ears?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surfer%E2%80%99s-ear-ear-health-tips-safest-way-for-me-to-clean-my-ears.30728/>

══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Johan Lindøm — 2025-04-21T12:31:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surfer%E2%80%99s-ear-ear-health-tips-safest-way-for-me-to-clean-my-ears.30728/post-351032>

Hi Dr. Kruse and community,
I’m dealing with surfer’s ear (exostosis) and a narrow ear canal, so stuff like saltwater or sweat gets stuck and irritates my ears, even though I use earplugs to keep water out (I don’t put my head under water). I try hard to avoid water in my ears and nnEMF close to my head (and mostly in life too - not that easy...). Sometimes it gets painful, and I have to see a doctor, but I really want to avoid that going forward. Based on your ideas, what’s the safest way for me to clean my ears at home to stop irritation or infections without needing an ENT?
Thanks!

(P.S. Yes, my environment plays a role, I am working on that, but even in a good environment, this problem persists due too mush excess bone growth.)

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-21T17:45:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/surfer%E2%80%99s-ear-ear-health-tips-safest-way-for-me-to-clean-my-ears.30728/post-351036>

safest way is to let ENT do it. That is what I do

# Thread: Dr. K. ....from June 17th, 2014
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-k-from-june-17th-2014.30732/>
**Subforum:** The Kruse Longevity Center
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### caroline — 2025-04-21T22:09:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dr-k-from-june-17th-2014.30732/post-351060>

an oldie but goodie.......

-   [Jun 17, 2014](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-use-almond-flour-the-paleo-darling-be-careful.11091/post-131164)

-   [Add bookmark](https://forum.jackkruse.com/posts/131164/bookmark)
-   [#7](https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/if-you-use-almond-flour-the-paleo-darling-be-careful.11091/post-131164)

Nature is always fundamental in health but no one wants to understand it is at is smallest levels to see why we are so off track. Paleo suffers from this as does allopathic medicine. This is why I tell people to still read my blog even if the do not understand quantum physics. What I write is important for all living things. The goal of the blog has always been to convince you not to turn away your attention because you do not understand the material. It is to draw your consciousness in to peer into how nature really works and light your curiosity on fire. As for Paleo, they have way too much wrong to get a pass from me. A doctor has a duty to point out where their message is dead wrong. Food is a very small part of the health equation. Paleo advocates paleofying food. I don’t "paleofy" any foods or Rx. This practice is one of the main reasons I want to be an outsider in this community. Sometimes you have to walk away from what you want to find what you deserve. When you fuel your life's journey on the opinions of others, you end up on a dark curvy road holding a gas can......... Understanding QED is why they continue to miss the big points for health reversals. Paleo is step in the correct direction from a SAD but there is so much poor info on basic physiologic functioning of circadian biology and food. Moreover, Their group and its leaders break all the circadian rules and thinks technology is a non issue and its all about grass fed meat and cross fit. Well it's all about electrons and paying attention to light cycles and food and exercise come after those. That is why I have a problem with them and I wont stop pointing it out until they get the message.

“It's a fact—_everyone_ is ignorant in some way or another because of their dopamine level.
Ignorance is our deepest secret. And it is one of the scariest things out there, because those of us who are most ignorant are also the ones who often don't know it or don't want to admit it. We don

# Thread: Kelly in Mexico
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kelly-in-mexico.30734/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Kel — 2025-04-22T01:14:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kelly-in-mexico.30734/post-351066>

Hello everyone. I just joined a couple weeks ago but have been following Uncle Jack for a few years. I was first told about him by a friend going through brain cancer. I was searching for info to try and help my dad who was dealing with stage 4 pancreatic cancer. We lost him in January of 2024. It was a highly stressful experience and a definite punch in the mouth as uncle Jack likes to say..
I am here picking up the pieces. I had a strange sort of spiritual awakening the end of 2019 and basically got the message the sun cures all from a singer who had passed over. curiously enough he has a song where he says “hemaglobin and chlorophyll same thing.” So when I found Uncle Jack the picture started to make more sense and I knew I was in the right place.
Background:
I grew up in the Bay area. No real health issues as a kid. I did receive a vaccine around 3 years old that cause me to drag one leg around for a few months. Only surgery I have even had was my wisdom teeth out around 10 years old. I took accutane in my early 20’s. Me and two of my three brothers all had bad acne as teens. I did work out that my mom had alot of dental procedures around that time, so guess that could have something to do with it.
My mom had stage 4 ovarian cancer in her early 40’s. It was removed and she is healthy and 76 now. She also had a benign tumor removed from her parada gland about 20 years ago. Says she remembers that spot in her neck bothering her since she was a kid. she grew up in upstate New York.
My dad had nephritis as a kid before penicillin was discovered. He spend a year around 9 years old in the hospital, watched all the kids die around him and then somehow survived. He was also born with a collapsed lung. violent childhood. became a life long pot addict. So alot of unresolved trauma and emotional stress for all of us growing up around him. He mostly grew up in Westport, Connecticut. He had stomach aches for last ten years or so which we now believe were the cancer and then got 2 covid vaccines… I think thats when the cancer went wild.

I traveled to India in 2008 and got 3 vaccines in one day. One was for hepatitis, then a tetunus shot and not sure what the 3rd was. Left for India a few days later and then started to feel like hell. Thought I was going to die on hotel room floor one night. Stomach and digestion hasn't been great since. I was hospitalized 5 weeks later when we got home after coming down with dengue fever. Started loosing consciousness and was rushed to emergency room where I was given Cipro and then had an allergic reaction to that.
No vaccines since!
I was a vegetarian for 20 years.
Had a healthy daughter in 2016. Home birth! All went well!

Fast forward to today. Main issues and reason I joined forum are
poor sleep, waking up tired
bags under eyes
lots of gas, burping and indigestion
pee at least a couple times thru night
no sex drive
stinky armpits
also my ears feel extra sensitive. especially during solar eclipses. the one last year had my ears ringing for a week straight. then there was one last month and ears rang again for 2 days..

I live near Puerto Vallarta Mexico. Have been here 20 plus years. Get a ton of sun. Definitly did over due the nnEMF and computer time during covid.Jumped down all the rabbit holes and then spent way too much time trying to figure it all out as I stared at the screen.
I am now grounding daily and have been watching sunrise for 2 months or so. Also trying to watch sunsets. We are outside tho most of our day here. Eating more local. getting used to oysters! breathe work and massage and walks in nature. I have a loving partner and alot of support. good community and strong family connections.
I am super sensitive to shitty light bulbs. wifi, perfume, soaps, cleaning products etc etc…
I feel extremely grateful for Uncle Jack. I told my partner they need to add another love language to the 5 love languages book. Tough love!! I so resonate with Uncle Jack’s straight talk and see why it is so necessary living in the world we are today to snap everyone out of their stupor!
I will stop there for now..

### AcuHealth — 2025-04-22T01:21:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kelly-in-mexico.30734/post-351067>

Hi Kel,

Where in the bay area?

### Kel — 2025-04-22T01:34:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kelly-in-mexico.30734/post-351068>

also forgot to mention I am 46.
and one more thing. ever since I was little when I get sick and need to throw up, I faint first. Was told it was some sort of vagus nerve issue?..

### Kel — 2025-04-22T01:34:16+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kelly-in-mexico.30734/post-351069>

> [AcuHealth said:](/goto/post?id=351067)
>
> Hi Kel,
>
> Where in the bay area?

Santa Cruz

### Kel — 2025-04-27T18:41:39+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/kelly-in-mexico.30734/post-351384>

One week in of morning sun, while grounded. Feeling so connected to my garden and finding all the different little sit spots! My hair feels thicker, I am sleeping, my digestion is better, and I think my wrinkles look softer! Also got two dear friends doing the same. Renewed attitude towards life. So magical and bewildering and also infuriating that this has been there all along. Contemplating where else in life simplicity has been overlooked!

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-01T15:36:52+0300

Same here, plus for some reason I can't access the q&a's now either. My password gets rejected and when I try to reset it, I get an invalid email error. But yet I could log in to this tablet using that same email. Just can't access the q&a page or patreon.

### Jacks — 2025-05-01T18:05:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-tell-me-the-difference-between-these-and-the-gold-membership.30735/post-351642>

Patreon is a different platform to the Optimal Klub Membership (which would be Gold or Silver or Platinum). You have to login to the [https://optimalklubs.com/](https://optimalklubs.com/) to access the blogs for all Klub membership levels - which are the exact same blogs as the Patreon Blogs. However, I have noted that the blogs sometimes are delayed being posted on the Optimal Klubs website compared to Patreon - you have to wait a few days. But you should have full access to the Q&A replays there from the dashboard, at least for the Gold and Platinum levels. Not sure about Silver - I think you cannot attend or view the Q&A's if you are Silver. You can also access other webinars at Optimal Klubs, though some of them come with an additional fee - like Kruse for Dummies.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-01T18:39:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/can-anyone-tell-me-the-difference-between-these-and-the-gold-membership.30735/post-351654>

You have to login to the [https://optimalklubs.com/](https://optimalklubs.com/) to access the blogs for all Klub membership levels - which are the exact same blogs as the Patreon Blogs. However, I have noted that the blogs sometimes are delayed being posted on the Optimal Klubs website compared to Patreon - you have to wait a few days. But you should have full access to the Q&A replays there from the dashboard, at least for the Gold and Platinum levels. Not sure about Silver - I think you cannot attend or view the Q&A's if you are Silver. You can also access other webinars at Optimal Klubs, though some of them come with an additional fee - like Kruse for Dummies.

The difficulty with logging into the q&a is a separate issue, though, but I got a response from Dr Jack on a separate thread that they're working on it.

# Thread: My beginning.
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-beginning.30740/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 19
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Tera Pfarr — 2025-04-22T21:17:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-beginning.30740/post-351094>

It has taken me 6 months to dive in and digest this information. The first time I heard Dr. Jack speak was in Dec. 2024 and it made me question every single thing I live and practiced in my clinic. Now…I wake up with such a curiosity to learn. It has sparked such an excitement in my life and I thank him for that.

I have always loved nature. As I child, we would tent camp in the northern woods of Wiscconsin and I would imagine myself as a Native American living with the earth. I was raised with a chiropractor mother and we always took a more natural approach when dealing with health issues and life. Now, I practice chirorpactic medicine with my mother in SE Wisconsin.

When I first became pregnant as an adult, my goal was to do it at home. Be in my own space and birth my children naturally as many women before me have done. I am very fortunate to have a very supportive husband who let me accomplish the birth I wanted as a women. I now have 3 beautiful children, that were all born at home and proud to say to this day (they are 5, 8 & 9) that they have never stepped foot in a hospital or centralized medicine. Through illness, broken bones and general health…I have used my knowledge of medicine and light (before I ever heard Dr. Jack speak) to heal them.

3 years ago…during the mist of Covid, my husband and I took our family on a trip to a remote cabin during the frigrid winter of Wisconsin to escape it all for a few days. I vividly remember one evening, we bundled our kids up and walked to the middle of a frozen lake we were staying at and layed on the ice looking up at the stars. In that moment of silence, nature was loud. Birds were chirping, deer were walking, critters were scurrying about. And in that moment…all the chaos of the world was gone. This was my ‘come to jesus’ moment in my life. Without all the news and media telling us what to think/feel/react…nature was still functioning.

We returned to civilization with a new outlook on life. What we wanted for our lives and our family had shifted dramatically. 3 months later, we purchased a 30 acre tree farm on a river. We bought a camper, set up some solar panels and lived ‘off grid’ with our children for the next 6 months. We still worked our jobs and Airbnb’d our city house for the season. We continue this cycle of 6 months at the farm and 6 months at our city house until we are able to build a structure where we can be there year round. We grow our own food, raise our own chickens and ducks…creating a homestead where we are not reliant on anyone but ourselves. We are moving back to the farm in a few short weeks to begin the build of our tiny house on the land and while hopefully never having to go back in our city home again. We have always wanted to homeschool but this knowledge has pushed us to begin that journey next. The kids are so excited!

Some family members are very supportive while others give us a lot of push back. I respect the rules of decentralized medicine to reduce friends/family who do not support the vision we have created. This has been a hard issue but recently have come to peace with the fact that I only need to surround my family with people who give us love and support.

Thank you Dr. Jack and for this forum to know we are not alone. Sometimes we feel like salmon swimming upstream, but there are others willing to take the journey (in many other parts of the world) along with us. I am ready to spread this knowledge in my practice and find other savages to do life with.

### Tera Pfarr — 2025-04-22T21:21:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-beginning.30740/post-351095>

Oh! I forgot to mention one thing…

2 weeks ago, as I was treating a patient in my room…my diploma on my wall shifted in the frame and was now very crooked. For 2 days I looked at my crooked piece of paper and it hit me like a brick. Everything I was taught and thought I knew was truth was no longer. I took my diploma down and put it away. It no longer defines who I am as a practitioner. I am ready to dive in, learn and heal this world as best I can in a decentralized way.

The universe works in mysterious ways! Lol

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-23T01:22:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-beginning.30740/post-351129>

I removed every certificate I had 20 yrs ago for the same reason. A title is a siren that youre part of the problem.

### Tera Pfarr — 2025-04-30T20:30:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-beginning.30740/post-351577>

Ever since December 2024, I have not missed the sun rise. Even in the frigid cold of Wisconsin, I have made it a commitment to my health. I can say that this has significantly improved my sleep and rejuvenation. I never really struggled with sleep issues but would have occasional restless nights depending on my stress levels of the day prior. Every night I sleep now…I am out like a rock and sleep restfully until about 5AM and naturally wake just before the sun rises. I listen to my body and circadian rhythm and am ready for sleep between 8-9PM because I physically cant keep my eyes open any longer. I have been working on cutting out all blue light after sunset, My children and I read a few books before bed by low incandescent light but now that the days are getting longer, we only use the light from the setting sun. I can say my curiosity to learn has amplified and absorption of knowledge is amazing. I feel like there was a light-switch in my brain that turned on again.

I am have been working out for the past 5 years about 3 days per week without significant changes in my body fat composition. I always thought, “well, this is the way my body is going to be after birthing 3 children.” I am not a big person but over the past 4 months I can see my body composition changing. My need for food has lessened as I get as much sun as I can on the days I am off while grounding. I am fortunate to have large windows in my treatment room at work and refuse to use any artificial light while I am working on patients. Sometimes it is a little dark at the beginning of my shift and patients will comment…but this gives me an opportunity to educate them and the choices I am making personally, to open their eyes to a new way of looking at health. I have gotten so tan with this practice, patients have been asking me where I went for spring break…and I reply “MY BACK YARD!” People are receptive and curious. I am planting seeds in my patients and will continue to share my journey and things I am learning from Dr. Jack. Next, I am going to have them remove their smart watches before I work on them because I personally don’t want to be anywhere near those things.

Over Easter, I had a very stressful event with a family member not agreeing with the life my husband and I have chosen for our family. I slept HORRIBLY that night and ended up getting sick 2 days after that. The rules of eliminating people who suck your energy are so true. So many people are asleep and you can‘t wake them up until they choose to waken themselves. I now an aware of large barriers I have to have up when conversing with these people…even when they are family.

I currently have 2 staff members who are on this journey with me. They got blue light glasses while working at the front desk, practice sunrise grounding and are changing their diet to include more DHA. We have a group chat going every time we are practicing this to encourage each other. We have talked about a weekly work event where we meet on the beach of lake Michigan, ground while watching the sun rise and drink DDW. LOL! I am fortunate to have them as my support team and part of my practice. They listen to every podcast I send them with Dr. Jack now and are learning and owning this message.

### Tera Pfarr — 2025-05-02T03:24:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-beginning.30740/post-351693>

Our solar panels are 50+ feet away from our structure of living (not on our roof). I would love more feedback, if any to improve this system. Let me know your thoughts.

### Mat.I — 2025-05-02T07:28:21+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-beginning.30740/post-351720>

> [Jack Kruse said:](/goto/post?id=351705)
>
> Not good.

When you have a free moment could you please elaborate on this.. My understanding is if the PV's are only serving the purpose of charging your battery bank (no inverter) and it is correctly designed with a decent charge controller (remote from living areas) and no AC is used what is the issue? To cancel out any Mag fields you would ensure you run decent twin/twisted pair cabling (even shielded where required). You would avoid any Boost converters and match your batt config with your setup in the house to match the required DC volts.. If any minor load ripples, these can be filtered (inductor capacitor) between charge controller and load (basic stuff) and you can go the extra mile and bond terminations / devices as necessary (in star config).

### Mat.I — 2025-05-02T07:30:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-beginning.30740/post-351721>

Harmonics are nasty in systems that use inverters.

we need technology to hear and learn these teachings. I don’t live next door to Dr. Jack to hear his teachings from his mouth (yet lol). The best we can do is be aware and respect the chronic effects and understand that we are gambling with our health being connected to them. I get where he is coming from with these comments. It is not out of judgement, but education.

Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

We are super excited about our new daylight computer! It’s AMAZING!! We have it functioning off ethernet so no WiFi around my children. Picture and clarity are great and I have so much less fear having my children using this device to learn while homeschooling next year.

### Tera Pfarr — 2025-07-27T16:59:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/my-beginning.30740/post-357034>

Update:

I have been obsessed with the sunrise. I wake up excited to drive 10 mins to Lake Michigan to watch it rise every morning. It sets my day and I never miss it. My kids often join me which is a beautiful thing. They don’t mind me walking them up at 5AM to go!

Since I started this practice, I feel calmer. Little things don’t get to me anymore. I feel like a better doctor because I’m more present in my head. I have been talking to patients a lot about what I’m learning and why I am doing this practice. I have many that have begun getting out daily in the sun and at sunrise. They have reported better sleep and improved vision!! I often recommend sunlight exposure to areas of complaint when they present. Patients are shocked but have been very receptive. My response when they look confused at my recommendation is…”it’s FREE, why not try it”!

Personally I have noticed big wins in my own health. I feel like my menstrual cycles are shorter and lighter, my hair and nails are growing more (I have been a chronic nail bitter and for the first time in my life I have long nails), my sex drive is way up and my sleep is phenomenal.

I don’t use artificial light in my treatment room when working (only light through windows), go out for sun breaks and use blue light blockers when doing notes (I have a red light panel I use also when doing notes).

My days off are spent outside. I am literally outside from 5AM-9PM on my days off. My home is becoming a bit of a mess because I don’t even want to go inside to clean!

My husband and I took a week off of work to begin our tiny house build. It’s amazing what you can accomplish in a week! Patients said to me “I hope your marriage survives that!” …That week off building and creating our home was one of the best weeks of my life. It only made us stronger as a unit. We are almost done with the exterior and designed a massive porch to live outside as much as we can and homeschool outdoors. We have 17 windows/doors that open in a 320sf home. We drove up to Green Bay and picked up an antique wood burning stove with cooktops to get us through the winter.

That’s my update! Enjoy the sun today!

> I was raised co-sleeping—born and raised in India. We co-slept with our kids. A pediatrician we went to worked in Africa (unsure where) for most of his career and said he can't "recommend" co-sleeping. However, everyone did it in Africa, and it was not a problem. I think the bed you sleep in means something, as well as whether the parents take medications to sleep, etc.

I expect for most of human history, most babies slept with their Mother (on the floor - grounded - with no nnEMF) Keep in mind most families historically all sept in one room. Where would you put the new born baby?

### NickD — 2025-04-24T05:52:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cosleeping-safety.30741/post-351186>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=351180)
>
> I expect for most of human history, most babies slept with their Mother (on the floor - grounded - with no nnEMF) Keep in mind most families historically all sept in one room. Where would you put the new born baby?

We were in one room, on the floor. Mum was on one side, Dad on the other... I mean this was the early 80s.. Now, I work work shifts, so my wife has one kid on either side.. They're 6 & 2 now. The bed we have is pretty hard, no real chance of rolling into anything. We also have railings on either side. Our first child was 2 months premature and would only sleep on my wife's chest. She was basically sleeping on an incline for months - solid effort. But you do what you can to have the baby sleep well.

### Alain Dwight — 2025-04-25T04:30:44+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cosleeping-safety.30741/post-351231>

Japan has an incredibly low infant mortality rate despite co-sleeping, so I don't think co-sleeping is a significant risk factor in itself... but if the mother is over weight or on meds, that's a different risk profile.

[@JD01](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/36109/) co-sleeps with our daughter, she found that floor sleeping removes a lot of the risk and anxiety about the baby falling down a crack or off a bed

### Anthony Smith — 2025-04-25T08:11:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cosleeping-safety.30741/post-351237>

If you lay on the floor it is hard to fall off . . .

### JD01 — 2025-04-26T16:52:59+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cosleeping-safety.30741/post-351324>

If you follow the safe sleep seven for cosleeping it's very safe and a lot of it is common sense.

I found cosleeping and nursing to sleep worked very well for me and my baby. She has her own shikibuton on the floor and I'm on a seperate Thai roll up mat or yoga mat. If you cosleep on a normal mattress I believe it would be best if there was a parent on either side of the baby.

In many countries around the world it's very common for babies to bed share and I do believe it has helped me and my baby sleep a lot better. Some babies seem to take to cribs more naturally than others and in the long run it could be easier if your baby sleeps well alone to have them in a crib or bassinet.

### AngieM — 2025-05-03T20:00:35+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cosleeping-safety.30741/post-351845>

Thank you all for your helpful responses! I have learned a lot from your answers.

### JD01 — 2025-05-04T18:20:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cosleeping-safety.30741/post-351897>

> [AngieM said:](/goto/post?id=351146)
>
> Thanks for telling me about the dock a tot!

I would never put a small baby into one of these for night time sleep or for any unsupervised sleep.

When cosleeping, it's much safer to have nothing there. This product is clearly a suffocation hazard

# Thread: Hola from Costa Rica!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-from-costa-rica.30742/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 2
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LizW — 2025-04-22T23:22:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hola-from-costa-rica.30742/post-351108>

Hi - just joined this group! Found Dr. Kruse via Bitcoin and Robert Breedlove podcast.
From Phoenix, AZ in the U.S. but have lived next to the beach in northwestern Costa Rica for over three years now.
Avid weight lifter, started when I was 51 years old. I lift five days a week in the early morning Costa Rica sun (6am - 8am). And I spend the day being an accountant, which I have always enjoyed. Worked my way up the Corporate America ladder for 30 plus years. Now I do bookkeeping for the fun of it in my partial retirement.
Very happy with where my body and health are right now and just want to continue to learn.

### India Rose Hopper — 2025-04-23T22:02:13+0300

# Thread: CJC-1295 / Ipamorelin
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cjc-1295-ipamorelin.30743/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### LizW — 2025-04-22T23:41:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cjc-1295-ipamorelin.30743/post-351111>

Hi, I have searched for anything on CJC-1295 / Ipamorelin combination on this site to see if there are any comments.

I am doing my **_second_** round of CJC-1295 / Ipamorelin.
\- First time I did it was seven years ago.
\- My visceral fat melted off within six weeks - fat I had no idea even existed since I was "skinny fat".

In week three of this current round.
\- I inject five days a week at bed time (Sunday - Thursday), fully fasted for at least three hours.

Has anyone else used CJC / Ipa lately? Not even sure it is legal to get anymore. My bottle is over two years old so I reconstituted and decided to use it up.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-23T00:58:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cjc-1295-ipamorelin.30743/post-351118>

This peptide was developed in the early 2000s to treat excess weight gain and obesity in people with comorbid Human Immunodeficiency Virus (HIV) and Acquired Immune Deficiency Syndrome (AIDS). It got a lot of bad side effects and then the FDA banned Ipamorelin because of aftermarket data and they knew the GLP drugs were coming and Big Harma wanted the deck cleared.

### LizW — 2025-04-23T22:07:43+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/cjc-1295-ipamorelin.30743/post-351169>

Huh!! Makes complete sense - good grief I never put that together until you said it. GLP drugs. I have some Melanotan II left over as well.
