# Thread: I got cocky with the no sunglasses and embarrassed myself
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-cocky-with-the-no-sunglasses-and-embarrassed-myself.30744/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 16
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### billytheadult — 2025-04-23T15:44:55+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-cocky-with-the-no-sunglasses-and-embarrassed-myself.30744/post-351145>

Just wanted to admit this and learn from it.
I didn't wear sunglasses on a boat day in Sanford, FL (29º) while Mito-preaching about light environment etc and well... I burned the shit out of my face and upper torso (and am peeling next day). While a couple friends are intrigued, it was embarrassing and I definitely got laughed at. It was just too much direct exposure and I did't get in the shade soon enough. And I feel like my credibility publicly took a hit.

I guess my solar callus has a long way to go.

### digital — 2025-04-23T16:20:13+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-cocky-with-the-no-sunglasses-and-embarrassed-myself.30744/post-351147>

> [billytheadult said:](/goto/post?id=351145)
>
> Just wanted to admit this and learn from it.
> I didn't wear sunglasses on a boat day in Sanford, FL (29º) while Mito-preaching about light environment etc and well... I burned the shit out of my face and upper torso (and am peeling next day). While a couple friends are intrigued, it was embarrassing and I definitely got laughed at. It was just too much direct exposure and I did't get in the shade soon enough. And I feel like my credibility publicly took a hit.
>
> I guess my solar callus has a long way to go.

From someone who was raised and grew up in NYC his entire life, made the sudden change to move down to Florida 8 years ago, we burnt for like 6-8 months before the skin was properly adjusted. Nowadays, we can be in the dead of the equator all day without a burn. Keep it up: tan daily, for as long as you can.
This profile pic serves as a reminder of my pale NYC self.

### Saichi — 2025-04-23T18:16:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-cocky-with-the-no-sunglasses-and-embarrassed-myself.30744/post-351152>

Wifi and bluetooth frequencies will make anyone burn in the sun. I learned the hard way myself even thinking the sun was responsible at first and it doesn't take six months to adjust to the sun only being free of nnemf assaults.

Don’t see how not wearing sun glasses had anything to do with getting sun burnt.
First light AM IR sun helps with sun UV exposure later in the day.
It can still take a few days to build up solar calluses depending on your haplotype.

### Brent Patrick — 2025-04-24T01:12:04+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-cocky-with-the-no-sunglasses-and-embarrassed-myself.30744/post-351176>

> [billytheadult said:](/goto/post?id=351145)
>

While a couple friends are intrigued, it was embarrassing and I definitely got laughed at. It was just too much direct exposure and I did't get in the shade soon enough. And I feel like my credibility publicly took a hit.
>
> I guess my solar callus has a long way to go.

It doesn't matter what others think,whats important is you keep progressing forward.

As a hack increase lycopene consumption via food and refer to Jacks Solar Callus blog it will take several weeks if not months to build a solid base so you can then develop a Tan, which depends on your Fitzpatrick skin color to start off with.

[https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36606553/](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/36606553/)

### [Infrared and Skin: Friend or Foe - PMC](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4745411/#:~:text=The%20process%20of%20exposing%20the,the%20spectrum%20\(figure%205\).)

In the last decade, it has been proposed that the sun's IR-A wavelengths might be deleterious to human skin and that sunscreens, in addition to their desired effect to protect against UV-B and UV-A, should also protect against IR-A (and perhaps even ...

 pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov

**"The process of exposing the skin to such radiation before its exposure to deleterious UVR wavelengths, closely emulates processes found in nature. This is understandable from an evolutionary standpoint since exposure to these early morning red and IR-A wavelengths in sunlight may ready the skin for the coming mid-day deleterious UVR.

Several _in vitro_ studies have shown that preconditioning fibroblasts with NIR could protect against upcoming UVB damage via p53 cell signaling-induced anti-apoptotic effects \[[45](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4745411/#R45), [46](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4745411/#R46)\]. In the first _in vivo_ study, prior to exposure to UVB, test subjects were pre-treated with 660 nm light as compared to controls who were not \[[30](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC4745411/#R30)\]. The progression in the development of erythema following UVB exposure was used as a measure of the deleterious reaction to UVB. The results of the study showed a reduction in the UVB-induced erythema reaction in a significant number of the pre-treated subjects. Also a SPF-15-like sun protection factor effect and a reduction in post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation were observed. Subsequent experiments (unpublished data) using multiple wavelengths showed superior reduction in erythema in favor of the NIR end of the spectrum"**

### ​

### Brent Patrick — 2025-04-25T09:09:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-cocky-with-the-no-sunglasses-and-embarrassed-myself.30744/post-351239>

> [billytheadult said:](/goto/post?id=351220)
>
> I did get AM IR sun in Florida the 3 prior days I was there. And I thought the non-sunglasses was at least partly responsible for my not burning/peeling during my 8 days in El Salvador

And for more context a post from Jack.........

**"People forget when sunlight hits the retina/skin 40-60% of our circulation increases in these capillary beds. They react the same way to sunlight. Their reaction to bright chronic blue light devoid of purple UV or red IR light is a different story.

What does full spectrum sunlight destroy naturally if your skin and eye are in the game of nature?

UVA and B light naturally lowers adrenalin (the stress hormone of the sympathetic nervous system) while its photons re-zip collagen that cortisol release causes at 4 AM that wakes us up by allowing water flows to occur between glial cells and neurons via the aquaporin 4 gates in the brain. AM sunrise light has no UVA or UVB present initially. It has blue, green, and red present. It has more blue than any other part of the spectrum and this is stimulatory to waking us up. Naturally, UVA light shows up later in the AM, depending upon your location in latitude, and this frequency of UVA light acts to begin to re-zip the collagen in our skin and eyes. That initial blue light stimulus from the sun’s rise is used to unwind our collagen to increase water flows to stretch the interspaces in neurons to wake our body and mind up at dawn from sleep. The aquaporin 4 gates are what is destroyed in Multiple sclerosis, which controls the flow of DDW from astrocytes to neurons, which is another photoelectric autoimmune condition. (Vitamin C is in that water transaction too)

When we are missing UV and/or IR light for any reason, these photoelectric and photochemical are not made. When full spectrum sunlight is absent in someone who is chronically stressed for any reason, sleep cannot be induced because the ocular melatonin cycle requires that these two frequencies of light be present to stimulate the regeneration processes in the eye during the daytime. If you think about your childhood, when you spent the day at the park of the beach, you might remember how easy it was to fall asleep after good solar exposure. If you had a lot AM light exposure you built your solar callus and this would mitigate a sunburn. If you did not you got burned early and had to take refuge, but either way, your sleep improved. The reason is simple, sunlight induces sleep because this light creates melatonin because the regeneration pathways that use melanopsin need daylight to regenerate.

When you did fall asleep, the redness of your skin did not come from the thermal burn, but it was from the increased blood flow due to the release of nitric oxide that acted to bring the arterioles of the dermis layers to the surface. This is a photochemical change induced by sunlight to allow the skin to absorb the UVA and UVB light at the surface. UVA and UVB light does not penetrate deep.

To absorb the UV light we need the circulatory system to come from the dermis and engorged the arterioles with RBC’s. This assumes there is no PEROXIREDOXIN damage in RBC's. (see my circadian RBC redox thread in the Redox forum)

The RBC’s are filled with hemoglobin and porphyrins that absorb both UV and IR frequencies. The sunburn is really an absorption of too much thermal IR energy. Deep sunburns can result from several factors: excessive sun, or thin skin, thick skin, or a poor adaptation to seasonal light due to chronic use of UV blocking makeup, clothing, or sunblock use in strong light cycles.

In fact, falling asleep in the strong daytime sun, is a key clinical sign to the astute clinician your normal daily light environment really is. UV and IR sunlight normally lowers endocrine steroids naturally(CT 7 blog) and lowers the nucleic acids in the surface keratinocytes without any liberation of heat on the skin’s surface = AUGER effect This last photochemical change shows you how UV and IR sun working together helps dissipate surface photonic power by exposing more nucleic acids to the incident rays of the sun."JK**

### MTA321 — 2025-04-25T18:33:54+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-got-cocky-with-the-no-sunglasses-and-embarrassed-myself.30744/post-351265>

# Thread: Dry Skin, Mosquitos, and timing of breakfast
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-skin-mosquitos-and-timing-of-breakfast.30748/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### michellejorge — 2025-04-24T16:16:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-skin-mosquitos-and-timing-of-breakfast.30748/post-351195>

Hi all, Due to the sun exposure my skin is dry and flaky. I understand we're not supposed to apply anything to our skin that would interfere with sun access. What about at night? Can we apply something, and if so, what?

Being outside, I'm getting attacked by mosquitoes. Any natural advice?

I'm thinking I have heard contradicting advice on when to eat breakfast. Before or after sunrise?

Thank you.

### digitald3 — 2025-04-24T16:30:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-skin-mosquitos-and-timing-of-breakfast.30748/post-351196>

> [michellejorge said:](/goto/post?id=351195)
>
> Hi all, Due to the sun exposure my skin is dry and flaky. I understand we're not supposed to apply anything to our skin that would interfere with sun access. What about at night? Can we apply something, and if so, what?
>
> Being outside, I'm getting attacked by mosquitoes. Any natural advice?
>
> I'm thinking I have heard contradicting advice on when to eat breakfast. Before or after sunrise?
>
> Thank you.

Hello. Typical advice around here is a loofa for dry skin.

Mosquitos are attracted to UV light. This is why Amazon sells uv light bug zappers. Your body is releasing uv light instead of using it for energy. This is a redox problem. So until you can fix that you will have a mosquito problem. So until then use cintronella candles or get some uv bug zappers for the yard.

And Dr Jacks leptin RX says to eat breakfast 30 min after sunrise so that you have eaten before uva rise.

### Dr Rohen Kapur — 2025-04-27T01:28:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dry-skin-mosquitos-and-timing-of-breakfast.30748/post-351354>

You can always apply coconut oil at night and wash it off before sunrise

# Thread: Thoughts about Jacobo Grinberg?
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-about-jacobo-grinberg.30749/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### leslievianett — 2025-04-24T17:02:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-about-jacobo-grinberg.30749/post-351197>

I looked everywhere (tweets, Patreon, here) for any comments about Jacobo Grinberg, a Mexican [neurophysiologist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neurophysiologist) and [psychologist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Psychologist), or his Syntergic theory, posing that human experience arises from the interaction between a "neuronal field" generated by the brain and a larger, energetic "space-time lattice" or "pre-space".

My partner has been watching documentaries about his "misterious disappearance" along with the disappearance of his computers, files and incomplete thesis back in 1994 (DARPA? CIA?).

Does Uncle Jack know about him?

Was he onto something? Half-truther? or just plain woo-woo?

[https://www.cia.gov/readingroom/docs/CIA-RDP96-00792R000700130002-5.pdf](https://www.cia.gov/readingroom/docs/CIA-RDP96-00792R000700130002-5.pdf)

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-24T20:22:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-about-jacobo-grinberg.30749/post-351205>

It is a theoretical framework that proposes a relationship between the brain, consciousness, and reality, suggesting that the brain creates a "neuronal field" that interacts with space-time, affecting perception and experience. While the theory has gained some attention within certain circles, it is not widely accepted within mainstream scientific communities and is often considered to be in the realm of pseudoscience.

### leslievianett — 2025-04-24T20:52:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-about-jacobo-grinberg.30749/post-351209>

We talk about quantum entanglement so I thought maybe there were some kernels of truth in his work. It looked suspicious that he went missing when trying to investigate and apply the scientific method to fields that seem to have always escaped it. Why would the CIA be interested in his work?

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-24T23:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/thoughts-about-jacobo-grinberg.30749/post-351215>

Searching for parts to add to MKULTRA

# Thread: ADHD, Bitcoin, and the Sudden Urge to Nuke My Old Life
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adhd-bitcoin-and-the-sudden-urge-to-nuke-my-old-life.30753/>

### Mark1969 — 2025-04-25T00:10:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adhd-bitcoin-and-the-sudden-urge-to-nuke-my-old-life.30753/post-351218>

Hey everyone,

I'm living in New Hampshire and seriously thinking about leaving—can't take another winter here. I was looking hard at New Mexico, but lately the whole Bitcoin and El Salvador vibe is hitting me hard. It's honestly overwhelming.

I’ve got OCD and ADHD, so when I fall into something, I _really_ fall into it. I’m already having dreams I missed the boat on everything—and that spiral is real.

Right now, I feel like getting a solid footing with Bitcoin should be my first priority before making any big moves. I know I need to soak up more knowledge from the community, but I also don’t want to get wrecked right out of the gate. A consult is definitely in my future once I build a little more armor so Jack doesn't knock me out with the first punch in the face.

I would love any advice on how to navigate this space smartly without burning out or making bad moves. I appreciate you all.

### Ivette88! — 2025-04-25T02:36:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adhd-bitcoin-and-the-sudden-urge-to-nuke-my-old-life.30753/post-351226>

I can’t advise much on bitcoin and moving. The only thing I would say is that I understand your comment about building an armor before talking to uncle Jack, but another way of looking at it is that the punch in the face is the way you build the armor.
Another way would be that he’s knocking your old beliefs out and giving you a totally new perspective.
Hope this encourages you to book that consult with him, I have mine Saturday.

Thanks for the feedback on New Mexico — I actually heard something similar in a recent pow-wow. I’ve been listening to as much as I can in the background while working. I visited NM last December and really loved the dry climate. I _want_ to be a snowbird, but just haven’t found the right place yet — anywhere humid just seems to set me off. I sweat like crazy, and with that comes higher blood pressure, irritability, and frustration. Maybe Jack has some insight into why I run like a leaky faucet all the time

The grounding stuff makes a lot of sense to me too.

I’ve never left the country and don’t even have a passport — just added that to my growing list (thanks Jack).

Not sure if I should ask this here, make a new post, or if it’s even okay to bring up Bitcoin stuff — but I really need to talk to someone about converting savings and metals into Bitcoin. One pow-wow mentioned Unchained Capital, but I also heard a warning in another not to let them hold your Roth IRA because “the Man” could sweep it.

I’m just trying to get my act together so I can have a productive consult. For now, I’m starting with the latest stuff on the club site and working backward. Appreciate any tips from those who’ve walked this path already.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-25T20:26:10+0300

Quick follow-up to my earlier post — I’ve been listening to the July 2024 Pow-Wow and Jack dives into **haplotypes** (great, another rabbit hole just opened up in the tunnel). Now I’m wondering if any of that shows up in the 23andMe test I regret taking 5 years ago. Pretty sure they’re tuned into my DNA frequency now and messing with me from space

But that part of the conversation really hit home — especially around **heat tolerance** and why I’ve always felt off in tropical or humid climates. I sweat like a busted faucet, and it’s not just annoying — it comes with higher blood pressure and this weird underlying irritability. Maybe my haplotype has something to do with that? Jack, if you’re out there, help a sweaty brother out. Or someone just stop me from spiraling further into the mitochondria matrix

For context — 40 years ago, fresh out of high school, I filled out an application to become a **car painter**... but my spelling sucked and they read it as **carpenter**. So here I am today, neck-deep in quantum biology and ancestral health, trying to connect the dots from a cosmic left turn I never saw coming. It’s all deep stuff, but I’m soaking it in one pow-wow at a time.

I’m still just trying to gather info and get my bearings. Working through the most recent content on the site and slowly backtracking. Appreciate all the insight from everyone — this community’s been a solid guidepost while I try to get my act together.

However, in the November 2023 Pow Wow, you said **“Not Unchained,”** which seemed to contradict that advice. So now I’m confused. Has your opinion changed since then?

I _am_ planning to book a full consult with you soon—mainly to go deeper on health and relocation—but I’m trying to move on this IRA rollover now before my account crosses $100K. Better late than never.

### MTA321 — 2025-05-01T21:25:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adhd-bitcoin-and-the-sudden-urge-to-nuke-my-old-life.30753/post-351664>

For BTC: I would just start drinking from the firehose. Read/listen to books and watch videos to truly understand how Bitcoin is immutable, scarce, decentralized, and allodial. Unchained is likely the best source when it comes to dealing with Roth/traditional IRAs as you have ownership over your keys. Set up and annual fees are kind of high but likely cheaper than if you had your IRA in actively managed funds with a brokerage. The only downsides to IRAs are capped by both income and contribution limits but that may not be an issue for you. Just something to keep in mind if you plan to keep contributing to those IRAs.

Any extra money you can not add to the IRAs I would just buy BTC at a Bitcoin only exchange and self custody. Strike and River are good options but it may be easier to do it all on Unchained depending on fees. Strike/River both offer no fees if you plan to DCA and set up recurring buys. Buy a hard wallet (many good brands coldcard, Blockstream, Trezor) and transfer the BTC from the exchange to cold storage in whatever amounts you are comfortable with. Need to be mindful of UTXOs as that dictates transaction fees down the road.

Bitcoin can be volatile, especially in early adoption so just learn to ride the wave.

For the other: Also start drinking from the firehose. You probably have some dysregulation from dysfunctional mitochondria and/or circadian/environmental mismatch. Go down the rabbit holes on circadian/environmental mismatches, blue/nnEMF and how it affects mitochondria and the hypothalamus/SCN by altering many things such as melatonin, dopamine/serotonin release, oxygen tension at IMM etc. which can contribute to energy dysregulation and ADHD/OCD. I am sure along the way you will learn about other things like leptin, POMC, DHA, Iodine and how all of this could play a potential a role in your heat tolerance issue. Improving redox and your light environment should fix things.

### Mark1969 — 2025-05-01T23:57:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adhd-bitcoin-and-the-sudden-urge-to-nuke-my-old-life.30753/post-351672>

Go down the rabbit holes on circadian/environmental mismatches, blue/nnEMF and how it affects mitochondria and the hypothalamus/SCN by altering many things such as melatonin, dopamine/serotonin release, oxygen tension at IMM etc. which can contribute to energy dysregulation and ADHD/OCD. I am sure along the way you will learn about other things like leptin, POMC, DHA, Iodine and how all of this could play a potential a role in your heat tolerance issue. Improving redox and your light environment should fix things.

Thanks so much for this thoughtful and detailed reply—it really helps, especially since you touched on both the Bitcoin and health aspects, which for me go hand in hand.

I get really anxious and fixated on things, and honestly, I struggle to fully comprehend a lot of what I read—especially when it gets heavy with medical or technical jargon. I'm not in the medical field, so I really appreciate how you broke things down. I’ll definitely search the forum for the topics you mentioned (like circadian mismatch, nnEMFs, leptin, etc.)—it feels like this hits right at the root of what I’ve been dealing with physically and mentally.

As for Bitcoin, I just wrapped up a call with a client manager at Unchained Capital and decided to sign up for their concierge service. They’ll help move my Roth IRA into Bitcoin and walk me through setting up two wallets—one for the IRA and one for personal funds. Total cost is $1,200 for the service, $150 for the two wallets, and a $250 manual processing fee (with a $2K minimum buy). I still have a Ledger I use with Coinbase, but it’s all been a steep learning curve. I feel like I’m drinking from five firehoses with my setup—especially since I spend my workday staring at five screens reviewing blueprints.

I had been seriously considering New Mexico as a place to relocate, but then I heard there’s no grounding and saw Uncle Jack’s comment saying it’s a no-go. El Salvador has definitely crossed my mind—I’d love to just go, but there’s so much to sort through and prepare for. Sometimes it feels like I’m late to the game.

Also, I panicked a bit after hearing Jack mention (for a second time in the November 2023 Pow Wow) the possibility of 40% confiscation—even with Unchained—so that shook me. But hearing your take, and doing my own research, I feel more confident I’m heading in the right direction.

Thanks again. You helped bring some calm to the chaos.

### Mark1969 — 2025-05-06T23:36:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adhd-bitcoin-and-the-sudden-urge-to-nuke-my-old-life.30753/post-352052>

**Subject: Unchained Bitcoin + ROTH Conversion — Seeking Advice from the Tribe**

Hey tribe —
I've only been here about two weeks, and while my brain feels like it’s been through a quantum blender, I’m making progress and wanted to share where I’m at — and get your thoughts.

Took some advice from the forum and set up an account with Unchained. I now have:

-   ✅ Two Trezor wallets in hand
-   ✅ Bank accounts linked
-   ✅ Just need to schedule the concierge call to walk through the ROTH conversion

But… now that I’m here, **I feel paralyzed**. Bitcoin's at record highs. I keep reading it can take 7+ days to move into cold storage. I'm stuck wondering:

Do I jump into the shallow end headfirst… or sit back and miss the move entirely?

I plan to hold long term — this is about sovereignty for me, not flipping coins. But I’d be lying if I said the timing doesn’t mess with my head.

Here's what I'm planning to ask on my concierge call:

1.  **What are the tax consequences of converting from my Traditional to ROTH?**
2.  **How fast can funds move — and can I "park" fiat while watching the market?**
3.  **If I lose a Trezor, what’s the recovery process for my multisig vault?**
4.  **Will Unchained help with documentation for IRS purposes?**

Would love to hear what others asked, especially if you've been through it already. Any lessons learned from the call?

I’m also making good progress elsewhere:

-   Got red-spectrum night lights and non-prescription blue blockers for evenings
-   Set my LEDs to red-only at night (shopping incandescent bulbs now)
-   Got an EMF meter and plan to do a rookie scan before shelling out for a building biologist

I know I’m not perfect — still waking up to pee at night (working on that), still overwhelmed — but I’m in motion.

Appreciate this group and all the insight so far. Truly.
—
_(PS: I feel like as soon as I buy, BTC will drop 30% just to test me )_

**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adhd-bitcoin-and-the-sudden-urge-to-nuke-my-old-life.30753/post-352059>

>
> What are the tax consequences of converting from my Traditional to ROTH?

Typically if all of your IRA is pre-tax, the entire amount will be taxed at ordinary income in the year you convert which could be a hefty tax bill. So if you had 100k in an IRA, that would be added to any income you currently make.

Example for easy math: if you make 100k, your total taxable income for the year (including Roth conversion) would be 200k - standard deduction. Just the conversion alone you would probably have to pay almost 24k in taxes (37k total if you include the other income).

Not financial advice blah blah blah.

### Mark1969 — 2025-05-07T02:09:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/adhd-bitcoin-and-the-sudden-urge-to-nuke-my-old-life.30753/post-352064>

Thanks MTA321, It is a Roth I'm just wigging out on the cost of going into bitcoin when it's so high and then paying a 1% commission to do so.

The post is about keeping cool without modern AC and Alain mentioned building a house.
My point was if you build right, the building would stay cool even on the hottest days and I used the ancient Ice Houses as an example.

### Alain Dwight — 2025-04-29T03:26:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-el-salvador-without-air-conditioning.30756/post-351458>

Thanks for all the options everyone, going to pass a combination of these along to the engineers for pricing, maybe some well placed vents, carefully chosen ceiling, and thicker cement walls will make AC pointless. Could run a fan off a battery pack in a pinch if needed.

Higher altitude isn't presently an option since we already have a verbal contract with some nice, rural land with really low EMF right in between San Salvador and the beaches, so we'll roll with that as long as it doesn't fall through.

Anytime we went to a higher altitude we did notice a lot of cloud cover, though.

### Woodstock — 2025-04-29T16:51:08+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-el-salvador-without-air-conditioning.30756/post-351478>

Is there a hill on your property or is it completely flat? I was just thinking that building inside of a hill, sometimes called an “earth ship” is really ideal. It’s kind of like a hobbit house completely covered by dirt on the sides,back and top and only the front is open for windows and doors. These keep very cool naturally. Lots of YouTube videos on this type of build. I would really like to build one but I also want to live very close to the beach. I haven’t found the right spot yet but maybe it will happen!

### JD01 — 2025-04-29T19:04:01+0300

There would be a lot of structural challenges with an earthship build on the plot of land. It is sloped but it is rocky. With the rainy season, dry season, and earthquakes, I'm not sure it would be cost effective to make that style of build safe and durable

### Woodstock — 2025-04-30T02:54:51+0300

> [JD01 said:](/goto/post?id=351502)
>

Maybe. But check out this guy. He is building inexpensive homes like this all over the world in different climates. I would love to take his workshop and build one myself.

### JD01 — 2025-04-30T03:55:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-el-salvador-without-air-conditioning.30756/post-351521>

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=351519)
>
> Maybe. But check out this guy. He is building inexpensive homes like this all over the world in different climates. I would love to take his workshop and build one myself.

Very neat! Building a house yourself does reduce labour costs but it's not something we'd like to do. Once you start hiring labour and getting professional assessments and designs for nonstandard homes in the area the costs ramp up. For the specific plot of land we have to consider proper drainage and reinforcement for long term stability. The long term maintenance also becomes another issue if you're not a do-it-yourselfer. With these building materials and high humidity, excessive mold also is a concern for me. At the end of the day, I think earthship homes are an interesting concept but smartly placed ventilation and passive solutions are probably the correct avenue for this specific project in this location.

### Alain Dwight — 2025-04-30T14:56:28+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-el-salvador-without-air-conditioning.30756/post-351543>

> [eugene1000 said:](/goto/post?id=351531)
>
> tldr: excuses
> "UPgrade on my $3K - 300 sq. ft. home" clickbait title or not, $10/sq.ft. cost basis +/- cheap guanaco labor is n0thing! plus, the risk-reward ratio for this ~$3K bet is a worthwhile gamble. earthship werks? sweet house for $3K. earthship doesnt werk? oh darn $3K gone ¯\\\_(ツ)\_/¯ .

You seem susceptible to marketing, so let me help:

The risk of doing it wrong is your house collapses and you and your family die in an earthquake. For us, it makes no sense just built a house to "see if it works," we have engineers, one can obtain multiple opinions and assessments if need be before hand.

Your cost assessment is wrong, there's a reason people in El Salvador, Florida etc. usually don't have basements (Earth Ships are partly underground). $3k is a marketing number used to sell people on purchasing courses, not anything to be taken seriously without first being validated by an engineer familiar with the plot of land in question.

### Woodstock — 2025-04-30T15:57:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-el-salvador-without-air-conditioning.30756/post-351547>

> [Alain Dwight said:](/goto/post?id=351543)

>

>
> Your cost assessment is wrong, there's a reason people in El Salvador, Florida etc. usually don't have basements (Earth Ships are partly underground). $3k is a marketing number used to sell people on purchasing courses, not anything to be taken seriously without first being validated by an engineer familiar with the plot of land in question.

I realize that was a marketing video but that wasn’t why I shared it. I have watched hundreds of videos from do it yourself builders with alternative methods. Obviously it would cost more than $3000 to build something like that. I only shared it because that guy has been to all different climates and countries and built many different kinds of structures, so I was just pointing out his channel using the video that dropped in my YouTube feed yesterday. He has a lot of cool examples on his channel of things he has built. So maybe I should have made that more clear. Im not an idiot. Obviously it would cost more than 3 grand.

My personal interest right now is in aircrete because you can make your own bricks out of cement and foam from dish soap. The aircrete does not mold (which is an huge concern for me) and you can make round shapes which are better for protecting from EMFs—also a huge factor in any building. I would love to take many workshops because it’s of interest to me, not because I am being pulled into a marketing scheme. So far I have only built a chicken coop. So I don’t really have much in the way of skills. But I did design it and figure out all the materials needed by myself by watching many videos, drawing out the design I wanted on graph paper, and using my brain.

Good luck to you.

### Woodstock — 2025-04-30T16:06:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-el-salvador-without-air-conditioning.30756/post-351552>

With these building materials and high humidity, excessive mold also is a concern for me. At the end of the day, I think earthship homes are an interesting concept but smartly placed ventilation and passive solutions are probably the correct avenue for this specific project in this location.

Mold is a huge concern for me as well. I don’t really know if an earth ship would work in El Salvador. I haven’t gotten that far in my research yet. I have just been imagining and brainstorming ideas until I find a piece of land myself and then hope that the lay of the land itself might dictate what is the best method. When I was planning to live in Colorado forever (before I found out it was Chernobyl) I wanted to build an aircrete dome house (or many small domes connected together) because domes naturally repel EMFs and aircrete is mold-free. I am not sure if thats the best method in El Salvador or what kind of permitting rules they have there. Best of luck with your home. I only posted the video just to share a source of ideas for alternative housing. I don’t have any affiliation with that. Just thought this guy has built all over in different climates so maybe he has found a good method for an environment similar to El Salvador.

### Woodstock — 2025-05-01T17:31:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-el-salvador-without-air-conditioning.30756/post-351637>

I can imagine aircrete might be difficult to make correctly. Especially in a humid environment. When I became interested in it, I was thinking somewhere in Colorado, probably near Crestone because that is an area that allows alternative building practices and is where a lot of cool structures have been built. But that area used to be the bottom of a lake bed. The soil is very depleted there. I was not getting a good vibe from the area. And then I found Jack! Glad I didn’t purchase property there!

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-01T18:38:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-el-salvador-without-air-conditioning.30756/post-351653>

It has been many years but I looked into dome homes. Basically you build an upside down swimming pool with an opening, lots of windows, facing south.

### JD01 — 2025-05-01T23:30:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-el-salvador-without-air-conditioning.30756/post-351669>

> [Woodstock said:](/goto/post?id=351637)
>
> I can imagine aircrete might be difficult to make correctly. Especially in a humid environment. When I became interested in it, I was thinking somewhere in Colorado, probably near Crestone because that is an area that allows alternative building practices and is where a lot of cool structures have been built. But that area used to be the bottom of a lake bed. The soil is very depleted there. I was not getting a good vibe from the area. And then I found Jack! Glad I didn’t purchase property there!

The thing about aircrete is that the cost savings are super appealing, but it doesn’t seem like it would hold up well down here.. too brittle and not great in humid or seismic environments. But now I’m looking more into things like ICF or SCIP panels. They're pricier upfront but much more durable, especially with the heat and earthquakes here. A high R value could lead to cost savings down the road but I'm not sure it'd work.
I feel like getting permits anywhere in USA seems like a pain, here I don't think it'd be that hard but we'll soon find out.

### JD01 — 2025-05-01T23:36:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-el-salvador-without-air-conditioning.30756/post-351671>

> [Anthony Smith said:](/goto/post?id=351653)
>
> It has been many years but I looked into dome homes. Basically you build an upside down swimming pool with an opening, lots of windows, facing south.

I am super interested in alternative homes also. I’ve researched domes, yurts, and geodesic designs pretty extensively. The biggest issue I’ve run into is finding people willing and able to work on them. Labour ends up being harder to find and the costs often seem to climb anyway.

After analysing it, it seems that the potentially most cost effective build is a small build using standard concrete for the bedrooms, and then doing large terrace areas and a few gazebos to keep things open and cool. And using proper vents. I'm looking into bamboo structures also but not sure how that'll go

### nz.adam — 2025-05-02T01:42:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-el-salvador-without-air-conditioning.30756/post-351680>

> [Alain Dwight said:](/goto/post?id=351229)
>
> Obviously would be better to not be running an AC grid in the bedroom during sleep, but are there other alternatives people use?
>
> Looking for options as I start to plan the small house build in a large piece of land.
>
> Am I just being soft and the best move is to just sleep in the heat?

If you sleep in an air conditioned room, you'll never adapt to the heat. Just get a fan and adapt. You'll sleep fine in under a week.

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-02T01:46:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sleeping-in-el-salvador-without-air-conditioning.30756/post-351681>

### Woodstock — 2025-05-03T10:13:09+0300

I noticed what looked like sawdust on the floor and the owner told me he would never build with bamboo again because of some kind of bug maybe termite or similar to it that eats through the bamboo!

### nz.adam — 2025-05-03T13:17:40+0300

I noticed what looked like sawdust on the floor and the owner told me he would never build with bamboo again because of some kind of bug maybe termite or similar to it that eats through the bamboo!

If you're interested in doing any "non-standard" or any other type of construction for that matter, my advise is to definitely make sure you get a local designer and builder that understands the environment.

Based on my research its been hard to find any local building codes (at least that are in English online) met a gal last time I was down that is a mito and knows Jack well that sent me over some information to review, but haven't had a chance to look at it yet. Do you have a designer/ architect already? If not, I think that would be a good place to start, I can ask if she'd be comfortable for me sharing her contact if you are interested?

As far as building materials, would recommend avoiding aircrete/ bamboo for any "structural" purposes. Both materials could be fine for auxiliary and non-structural items like other site walls or some type of gazebo or roof cover as you suggested. I think going the concrete frame route would be the cheapest and most effective ( you can have significantly more openings=more sunlight, without compromising on structural/ seismic integrity \[additional EMF blocking benefits as well\])

Just my two cents from a structural engineer!

# Thread: SH optimal journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sh-optimal-journal.30765/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 8
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### S Hudson — 2025-04-26T10:48:42+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sh-optimal-journal.30765/post-351313>

Okay, let's do this.

### Country of residence - UK​

### **Family History:**​

Father died of Non Hodgkin's Lymphoma and had type 2 diabetes.
Mother has thyroid issues and macular degeneration.
The joke on both sides of my family goes that we are "long lived stock, but never particularly healthy)

### Job History:​

Dental student from 1990-95.
Dental associate to 2000
Dental business owner 2000-2017
Retired 2017 and became a writer

### History of Present complaints​

I have a physiology that reacts poorly to stress, so in hindsight, dentistry was the wrong job for me.
**1994:** Developed Chronic Pelvic Pain syndrome (CPPS). Tests found no physiological cause.
**1996:** CPPS drifted into the background, replaced by IBS. Time I bought my first mobile phone, although with the awful battery, it was rarely used
**1997:** IBS drifted into the background, with occasional recurrences, replaced by Acid reflux. IBS eventually drifted off.

This was a period when I was pretty useless as a dentist. Like many dentists, I left dental school without the required skills. It was at the end of this period that I engaged in programme of self guided retraining. this and an interest in self improvement (NLP, TFT, exercise and meditation) seemed to ease things.

**1998-2006:** Relatively healthy symptom wise, although CPPS would flare up on occasion
**2007**: Developed palpitations, and no cause was found despite investigations. I declined the glorious offer to have a monitor implanted under my skin. Palpitations lasted about a year. Most likely stress related due to a changed working system in socialised dentistry at the time.

**2009**: midsternal chest pain. 3 diagnosis from 3 different specialists until I had a gastric camera which found nothing. Final diagnosis was muscle strain. This drifted into the background with occasional flareups.

**2012**: CPPS came back with a vengeance. Perianal pain, urinary urgency and frequency. Multiple specialists and "you'll just have to live with it). bad for 2 years and then settled with recurrences.

**2017**: Sold my practice and retired from dentistry. The month after was the best I'd felt since university. Started writing so massive increase in computer screen use.
**2018**: CPPS started to flare up again. Found I had to use a pillow to comfortably sit in an office chair.
**2019**: Mistakenly moved into a high nnEMF are and a high stress environment. CPPS flared to the worse it has ever done. The acid reflux and chest pain returned. Also, for the first time, I found I couldn't sit down due to back pain. Mental health went into the toilet. Put on a host of meds by my GP which I think triggered a mental health crisis (**antibiotics, gabapentin and** **proton-pump inhibitors).** MRI and other tests clear
**2020**: Moved again and symptoms eased. This was When COVID hit. CPPS eased by about 50%, and I started walking daily as I developed back pain.
**2021**: Moved again due to bad neighbours and bought a lodge on a lake in a low nnEMF area. Its presently a 5G dead zone, although there is WIFI pumped across the lodge park. This saw a marked improvement in my health... but this was temporary. Sitting became easier. Caught COVID end of that year which gave me a wicked case of constipation which lingered despite dietary changes.
**2022**: Started to notice discomfort lying on my sides (hips, bilateral). This began to worsen, creeping up on me. Mid-sternal pain returned as did CPPS. The lying on my back became a challenge.
**2023**: as if by magic, left sided abdominal pain appeared, linked to muscle trigger point and seemed to occur when constipation all but vanished. Arse camera, abdominal CT and ultrasound found nothing. This year saw the return of palpitations which was countered by prescribed folic acid.

### Low Redox lifestyle prior to discovering Dr Kruse:​

Discovered Jack end of last year
High computer monitor use. 4-5 hours most days.
High TV use most evenings.
Most exercise done in a gym
Fear of the sun.
Turns out, by putting a power cable under my sofa to the coffee table (which has phone charging cables) I turned said sofa into a nnEMF hotspot)
No seafood.

### Steps taken​

I've been implementing changes gradually so as not to revert back to old habits.
Blue light blocking glasses.
Red incandescent light bulbs.
Sleep in a room with the electricity isolated
Screen shields and reduced computer use.
Massive increase in sun exposure (Spring in UK has been favourable). for the first time I have a tan.
90% of exercise now done outside. Morning yoga in the sun.
Seafood everyday. Bulk of food is organic (meat grass fed or pasture raised) and locally sourced. I've never been a carb fiend.
Bedtime changed to 10pm, rather than the 1-2am previous. Wake up with the sun.
Cold and heat therapy. Sauna use followed by 5-10 minute cold shower. I live next to a lake so a daily dip in winter helped. Presently waiting on delivery of a plunge pool with water cooler to do extended cold immersion therapy for the warmer months.
Slowly abandoning all the supplements the food gurus recommended.
Stopped drinking tea.
Tap water replaced by bottled spring water with the lowest deuterium content available for a mainstream brand
Found a physio who understood the psychological nature of muscular pain.

### Presenting complaints:​

Intermittent CPPS which I know how to manage and mitigate.
Occasional palpitations that seems to be controlled by dietary folate.
Last two weeks has seen a recurrence in constipation, although mild in nature.
Left sided pain persists but manageable
Last week I've been suffering insomnia which I'm putting down to changing my sleep schedule. Might also be due to an increase in chronic pain
Sitting is becoming challenging again due to bilateral buttock tenderness. Seems to be a flare up
Lower back pain all but gone.
Lying on my sides is still a challenge, although lying on my back is easier... espcially when in the sun
Multi-site tooth ache which is referred pain from neck and jaw trigger points. Trigger pain massage deals with it,
The severity of each seems to fluctuate and rotate around the body. It's rare more than one symptom is severe at the same time.
I've learned that many of my complaints are linked to a condition known as TMS. Basically the belief is my own psychology is causing my body to manifest body pain. many of my muscle tenderness symptoms can be mitigated by focused breathing.

### Plan:​

Increase sun exposure.
Increase cold therapy
Fully implement the Epi-Paleo RX
Become religious with dusk/dawn sunlight
decrease blue screen use. Building a setup outside so that when I do use monitors, it will be in natural light.
Get healthy enough that i can get on a plane to visit the areas I have highlighted as replacements for the UK
Reach 1 BTC

As for sky dimming, it will take the muppets time to implement their plans (as an aside, I noticed a real drop-off of sky crisscrossing after USAID came under the hammer). I don't plan to be in the UK passed the end of this year. And if the criminals are doing such vile antics here, the rest of the west will suffer the same I fear.

### S Hudson — 2025-05-21T10:24:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sh-optimal-journal.30765/post-353056>

**Update:**
Been following the protocols pretty religiously, and building a nice tan (at least for me). Plenty of grounding and morning sun, and sitting out on the grass reading Jacks Epi-Paleo book...

**Improvement**
Palpitations and constipation all seem to have cleared up.
Heat rash no longer occurs.
Left sided pain better and insomnia mostly better although chronic pain waking me

**Challenges**
Multi-site body pain worsening and now appearing in my joints. Not severe, but several joints have a feeling of weakness about them.
It's getting more difficult to get comfortable enough to fall asleep. Feels like the body is becoming tender all over, except when out in the sun.

I can't help thinking something in my environment has changed (although there has been no visible ariel activity for close to 3 weeks now). As I'm still not really fit to fly, I'll be spending some time in a secluded retreat i know of.

### S Hudson — 2025-08-07T21:47:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sh-optimal-journal.30765/post-357800>

Okay, so it's obvious now that Britain isn't a country that Jack's teachings work in.
Yep. No surprises when I say Jack was right yet again.
The summers are dire and inconsistent (it is presently raining) so relocation plans are being made.
Even with the shabbiness of the British summer, I now have my first tan ever and have noticed an improvement of many of my symptoms. I'm now confident I can safely take a moderate length flight, having tested my body's reaction on a few short hauls (sitting down has been an issue for some time, but has eased considerably).

Tenerife will be my stopgap for now while I organise my finances.

I've been thinking for a while that my health has been a guide, forcing me away from the damaging aspects (it gave the nudge to sell my business, property and move out of the city to a remote location). Now, this and the failing British state is forcing me to abandon my home country.

I'm not sure I'll miss it.

One thing I've noticed specifically is that when I am in the sun, the area of my body that is presently afflicting me is the part of me that warms up the most, as if the sun is working harder in those areas. It's a fascinating feeling, and I'm looking forward to investigating these effects in a sunnier climate

### S Hudson — 2025-12-01T18:57:38+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sh-optimal-journal.30765/post-363458>

**Update.**
Just got back from Tenerife and showed I could 1) travel, and 2) Sleep away from my home (I have a problem with my hips that makes sleeping on a mattress that is remotely hard, but got around that by taking an inflatable camping mattress which worked).

If I can sit in cattle class on Ryanair for 5 hours, I reckon I can sit in business class to get to safe havens.
Have signed up for the retreat Jack has set up in ES and plan to do a month initially.
Also plan to spread my time so that I'm out of the UK as much as possible while I look for somewhere to relocate to. Might be ES, but I have a hankering for a PT lifestyle.
Just waiting on the Safe and Sound Pro II to be delivered to cover all the bases (the metre I thought was okay turns out to be sh\*t)

**New symptom:**
For some reason, my fibromyalgia like symptoms (yes, I'm aware that's a catch all diagnosis) now includes costochondritis like symptoms and intercostal muscle tenderness in multiple points on the ribcage. The discomfort floats about, and I feel it's nothing a good dose of sun won't get rid of.

# Thread: From artificial glow to natural radiance
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-artificial-glow-to-natural-radiance.30766/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### riksa — 2025-04-26T15:03:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-artificial-glow-to-natural-radiance.30766/post-351315>

Hello from the 60th northern latitude. I go by the name Matias.

It was light that pulled me from the darkness. I was on multiple heavy psychiatric medications without actual pathology, living and sleeping next to the elephants foot. My nights were illuminated by the high powered LED street lamp. Eating gutter oil for breakfast, smoking cigarettes made out of store receipt papers when I ran out of real papers. I was effectively lobotomized and doomed for an early demise. But I liked the sun, so I spent a lot of time in the summer outside. And this one summer, in an instant some switch flipped inside me for no apparent reason, that I must rebuke all of that. Stop all medications immediately, which I do not recommend, and became healthy. I believe it was the sun that gave me just enough clarity to see. Or God.

Took me two years to recover subjectively, with tremendous anguish and suffering. I was doing everything I can to become truly healthy. Peeling the layers month by month. Going from exercise and macros to supplements and fasting to cold exposure and nutritional composition nuance and so forth. Then I saw a video on Youtube. It was uncle Jack. Talking a bunch of crazy stuff with supreme confidence.. I've seen this before -- another false-guru. But there is something here. Then he starts to talk about light, blue light. And it triggered some animous compulsion inside me, because it matched a hundred precent what I've personally noticed of the effect of blue light. That made me watch and dig more and more and slowly my mind expanded. It was the light again that pulled me in.

I have had a fully strict light discipline for this year. Every morning when I get up I go straight to the forest for an walk for an hour. Bare head, bare chest and nasty looks from elders passing by in the -5C weather. This has truly recovered my self. Not just my health, but my self. I'm curious, now my curiosity runs so rampant that I constantly have to run to the computer to check some thesis out. My engines are purring again. I admit, I would have not figured this out by my self. I was 23 in the first passage, now turning 27.

I want to give thanks to you, Jack.

### riksa — 2025-04-26T16:12:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/from-artificial-glow-to-natural-radiance.30766/post-351316>

At the beginning, I slept with my head 15cm away from an mobile-data Wifi router. In a nnEMF hellhole. Coincidentally, one of the psychiatric medications prescribed to me was Gabapentin. Now why is that note worthy? Gabapentin acts on the voltage-gated calcium channels. By blocking it. The probable mechanism of action of the RF frequencies(Pall, 2018) by which it causes damage. It was prescribed for anxiety, and non-surprisingly, I had to take a high dose for it to work, and it did. Now, when I moved apartments and reduced the EMF to non existent levels as to that, the gabapentin works now _stronger with **15%** of the dose.

Edit: I don't believe in coincidences. It was one of the only medications I had to upkeep after the turning point, now I realize for a good reason._

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-27T02:50:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-sickness-nausea.30768/post-351360>

Morning sickness is a sign in utero oxygen problems.

Jaundice = Oxygen Holocaust at the mtDNA level.

Many people who develop skin and brain diseases of childhood will have been jaundiced at birth.

Morning sickness is Nature's warning shot.

**Neonatal Jaundice: Blue Light Therapy is a Human Disease Incubator**

Neonatal jaundice treatment shifted from full-spectrum light to blue light (425–475 nm) in the last 35 years, ignoring quantum biology. Centralized medicine, blind to neuropsin (UVA receptor) and melanopsin (435–465 nm), assumed blue light was safe for bilirubin photoisomerization (Oláh et al., 2013). Infants, with translucent skin and underdeveloped skeletons, allow deep blue light penetration, reflecting off bone to stimulate melanocytes. Long-term studies reveal increased dysplastic nevi in these children, a melanoma risk factor (Oláh et al., 2013).

Blue light’s mitotic effect mirrors uveal melanoma (UM) studies: human UM cells exposed to blue light (475 nm) show increased proliferation, an effect blocked by blue-light filters (Hu et al., 2014). In neonates, blue light decouples POMC signaling, overstimulating alpha-MSH and melanogenesis without UV’s protective feedback.

This drives mitochondrial heteroplasmy in melanocytes, as blue light suppresses cytochrome c oxidase, increasing ROS and mtDNA mutations (Godley et al., 2005). Mothers with circadian mismatches often from blue light exposure pass higher heteroplasmy rates to infants, amplifying the risk (Wallace, 2010).

This is how most childhood diseases transfer in the modern world. Light is the first injury children sustain in the germline that eventually becomes their body. They come into their lives with a higher heteroplasmy rate and this puts them closer to disease and death. Lowering their heteroplasmy post natally should be the goal fo the parents who harmed their germlines prenatally.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-27T03:02:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/morning-sickness-nausea.30768/post-351362>

Title: Neonatal Jaundice and Morning Sickness: Nature’s Quantum Warning—Blue Light Is Poisoning Our Kids

Authors: Nature in collaboration with Dr. Jack Kruse, CEO or Kruse Longevity Center, San Salavdor, El Salvador

**A Message to Parents**

Hey, moms and dads, this isn’t just another medical article. This is about your baby’s future, your morning sickness, and why the hospital’s “safe” blue light treatment for jaundice might be setting your child up for serious health problems, like melanoma, down the road. I’m here to break it down in a way that makes sense, using real science not the half-truths centralized medicine pushes. We’ll also connect the dots to the ancient Great Oxidation Event (GOE) and why jaundice is your baby’s oxygen alarm, screaming for sunlight, not blue light. Let’s dive in and uncover what nature’s been trying to tell us all along.

* * *

**What’s Happening with Neonatal Jaundice?**

If your newborn has jaundice, that yellow tint in their skin or eyes, it’s because their body is breaking down extra red blood cells (RBCs), producing bilirubin. Back in the day, doctors used full-spectrum light (like sunlight) to treat it, breaking down bilirubin so the baby could pee or poop it out. But about 35 years ago, centralized medicine switched to blue light (425–475 nm), thinking it was “safer” since it targets bilirubin directly. They stripped out ultraviolet (UV) and infrared (IR) light because they bought into the UV-causes-cancer myth. Big mistake.
Here’s the problem: blue light, without UV and IR, is toxic. Babies’ skin and eyes are super thin and let 80–90% of blue light through (Mainster, 2006). It reflects off their underdeveloped bones, hitting cells called melanocytes that make skin pigment (Image: Laser Penetration). Studies show this blue light makes melanocytes multiply too fast, just like it does in eye cancer cells (Hu et al., 2014). Worse, kids treated with blue light for jaundice have a higher chance of developing dysplastic nevi which are odd moles that can turn into melanoma, a deadly skin cancer (Oláh et al., 2013). Centralized medicine ignores this, but I won’t. Your baby’s first light exposure shouldn’t be a disease incubator.

* * *

**Morning Sickness: Your Baby’s SOS Signal in You**

Now, let’s talk about why you, and sometimes dad, felt so nauseous during pregnancy. Morning sickness isn’t random; it’s your baby’s quantum warning shot, telling you something’s off with your light environment. Here’s how it works: your baby inherits mitochondrial DNA (mtDNA) only from mom. Mitochondria are tiny power plants in cells, and they have to match perfectly with the nuclear DNA (half from mom, half from dad) to make a healthy kid. When they don’t match well—because mom’s been stuck indoors under blue light with no sun—your baby’s mitochondria end up with too many defects, a condition called heteroplasmy (Wallace, 2010).

Your baby senses this mismatch through its fetal RBCs, which still have mitochondria (unlike adult RBCs). It sends light signals,yes, cells emit light!, through your blood plasma to say, “Mom, my respiratory proteins don’t fit right!” See the picture below from a paper that shows this. Those proteins are how mitochondria make energy, and if they’re too “spread out,” your baby’s at risk for problems like jaundice, colic, or even childhood diseases (Image: Retinal Semiconductive Circuit). Nausea is nature’s way of making you eat less, triggering fasting in both you and the baby. Fasting shrinks those proteins through autophagy, lowering heteroplasmy and giving your baby a fighting chance (Wallace, 2010). { "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

Dads can feel this too, if their own light environment is good. Why? Because dad’s nuclear DNA is entangled with the baby’s through light signals (bio-photons). When dad’s mitochondria are healthy, thanks to sunlight, his cells can sense the baby’s distress signals (Popp, 1986). If dad’s under blue light all day, he might miss it. Morning sickness is your baby begging for sunlight to fix its mitochondria before birth.

* * *

**Jaundice as an Ancient Oxygen Sensor: The GOE Connection**

Let’s go back 2.4 billion years to the Great Oxidation Event (GOE), when oxygen levels on Earth spiked, and life had to adapt. Jaundice is an ancient oxygen sensor from that time. Bilirubin comes from heme (in hemoglobin), a molecule that evolved to handle oxygen. When bilirubin builds up, it’s a signal that oxygen levels in the baby’s cells aren’t right often because mom’s light environment messed up her mitochondria, passing that stress to the baby (Lane, 2015). Sunlight, with its full spectrum, regulates oxygen use in mitochondria by balancing heme breakdown with circadian signals like Rev-erb and NAD+/NADH.

Blue light throws this off. It suppresses cytochrome c oxidase, a key mitochondrial enzyme, creating too many reactive oxygen species (ROS) that damage mtDNA (Godley et al., 2005). Without UV and IR to balance it, blue light also overstimulates pigment production (melanogenesis) through a hormone called alpha-MSH, which comes from proopiomelanocortin (POMC). Sunlight normally keeps this in check, but blue light lets it run wild, increasing the risk of future cancers (Image: Proopiomelanocortin). Jaundice in your baby is a modern echo of the GOE, a cry for the right light to manage oxygen and energy. When the baby cyctochromes are not functioning well the message sent via the blood's biophoton spectra creates morning sickness in mom so that she won't eat to stress the cytochromes, but go out in the AM sunglight to renovate the heme proteins on the cytochromes called CCO. CCO controls the babies ability to repair its own mtDNA in the uterus. The baby signals you to fast to improve its own autophagy and apoptosis that relies on CCO functioning.

* * *

**Centralized Medicine’s Big Lie**

Centralized medicine, think big hospitals and drug companies, tells you sunlight is dangerous. They’ve pushed UV-blocking everything, from neonatal blue light therapy to sunscreen, because they’re stuck on old studies that used isolated UV light, not sunlight’s full spectrum (Elwood & Jopson, 1997). But sunlight isn’t just UV—it’s 49.4% IR, 42.3% visible (only 13–26% blue), and 8% UV, all working together. UV triggers nitric oxide to improve blood flow, IR boosts energy production, and blue light regulates rhythms, when balanced.

By stripping UV and IR, centralized medicine creates a “light hell” for your baby’s mitochondria. Blue light alone damages mtDNA, increases heteroplasmy, and sets the stage for diseases (Wallace, 2010). Meanwhile, they ignore quantum biology—like how cells emit light (bio-photons) to communicate, as discovered by Alexander Gurwitsch in 1923 and confirmed by Fritz Popp and Mae Wan Ho (Popp, 1986; Ho, 1998). This isn’t new science; it’s just science centralized medicine refuses to see because it doesn’t sell drugs. They also want your child to be a good future customer.

* * *

**My Decentralized Thesis: Light Is the Key to Health**

I’ve been saying this for years: when you eat matters more than what you eat, because light controls your body’s clock (Kruse, 2019). My Leptin Rx is built on this using sunlight to lower heteroplasmy in your mitochondria, slowing down bad gene expression. Every time your cells copy DNA to make proteins, they release light in the ELF-UV range. Too much copying (from a bad light environment) means more light leaks, speeding up disease (Ho, 1998). Sunlight, with UV and IR, keeps this in check by confining light in your DNA’s double helix, thanks to its high dielectric constant. Blue light? It unwinds DNA too fast, leaking light and driving problems like jaundice and melanoma risk.

The GOE taught life to use light and oxygen together. Mitochondria and chloroplasts—both stolen bacteria—evolved to capture sunlight’s energy using excitons, making energy production predictable (Lane, 2015). Your baby’s jaundice is a signal to get back to that ancient balance: full-spectrum sunlight, not blue light. Centralized medicine’s blue light therapy is a modern injury, raising your child’s heteroplasmy and disease risk right from the start.

* * *

**What You Can Do: Be a Decentralized Parent**

Parents, you’ve got the power to fix this. Morning sickness and jaundice are your baby’s way of saying, “Get me sunlight!” Here’s how to listen:

-   During Pregnancy: Spend time in morning sunlight without sunglasses and your skin exposed using UV and IR balance your mitochondria and your baby’s. Avoid blue light from screens at night; it messes up your circadian rhythm and increases heteroplasmy.
-   After Birth: If your baby has jaundice, push for full-spectrum light treatment, not blue light. Hospitals won’t like it because they’re stuck in their ways, but your baby’s health is worth the fight. Hold your baby in sunlight (safely, with no sunscreen) or while you’re grounded on the Earth to lower heteroplasmy (Kruse, 2019). Hospitals are also blue light factories so avoiding them when you have morning sickness is wise.
-   Long-Term: Keep your kid away from blue light-heavy environments (LEDs, screens) as much as possible. Sunlight is nature’s vaccine, it boosts vitamin D, controls genes like c-MYC to prevent cancer, and keeps mitochondria healthy (Kruse, 2017).

Your baby’s cells are talking to you through light signals. Morning sickness is their first warning; jaundice is the second. Don’t let centralized medicine’s blue light poison them. Reconnect with the sun because it’s what life has done since the GOE, and it’s what your family needs now.

* * *

**CITES**

-   Elwood, J. M., & Jopson, J. (1997). Melanoma and sun exposure: An overview of published studies. International Journal of Cancer, 73(2), 198–203.
-   Godley, B. F., et al. (2005). Blue light induces mitochondrial DNA damage and free radical production in epithelial cells. Journal of Biological Chemistry, 280(22), 21061–21066.
-   Ho, M. W. (1998). The Rainbow and the Worm: The Physics of Organisms. World Scientific Publishing.
-   Hu, D. N., et al. (2014). Blue light exposure enhances proliferation of uveal melanoma cells via mitochondrial ROS production. Photochemistry and Photobiology, 90(3), 567–573.
-   Kruse, J. (2017). Light, water, and magnetism: The quantum basis of human health. Quantum Health Press.
-   Kruse, J. (2019). The Leptin Rx: Resetting your body’s clock with light. Quantum Health Press.
-   Lane, N. (2015). The Vital Question: Energy, Evolution, and the Origins of Complex Life. W.W. Norton & Company.
-   Mainster, M. A. (2006). Violet and blue light blocking intraocular lenses: Photoprotection versus photoreception. British Journal of Ophthalmology, 90(6), 784–792.
-   Oláh, J., et al. (2013). Long-term hazards of neonatal blue-light phototherapy. British Journal of Dermatology, 169(2), 243–249.
-   Popp, F. A. (1986). On the coherence of ultraweak photoemission from living tissue. In Disequilibrium and Self-Organization (C.W. Kilmister, Ed.), pp. 207–230. Reidel, Dordrecht.
-   Wallace, D. C. (2010). Mitochondrial DNA mutations in disease and aging. Environmental and Molecular Mutagenesis, 51(5), 440–450.

**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/subject-trading-fluorescents-for-volcanoes-%E2%80%94-help-me-crowdsource-a-mitohacker-landing-pad-in-el-salvador.30769/>

**Posts:** 1

### Mark1969 — 2025-04-26T23:01:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/subject-trading-fluorescents-for-volcanoes-%E2%80%94-help-me-crowdsource-a-mitohacker-landing-pad-in-el-salvador.30769/post-351341>

**Subject: Trading Fluorescents for Volcanoes — Help Me Crowdsource a Mitohacker Landing Pad in El Salvador**

Hey tribe,

I’m neck-deep in blue light and construction bids, trying to map out my escape route to a nature-first lifestyle — and I’m turning to you all to help me zoom out and see El Salvador from 30,000 feet.

### The dream:​

-   **Timeline**: Fall 2025 move — ideally November through April at first.
-   **Budget**: <$250K to buy land or a modest home. Would build if the terrain and vibe are right.
-   **Work**: I’m a **master carpenter turned remote commercial construction estimator**. I’ve put down the tools (for now), but I still live in CAD files, cost breakdowns, and Zoom fatigue.
-   This **job and screen time are frying my brain**, but I need to keep it going until I figure out the next move. I’ve probably got another 8–10 years before I can officially pull the plug… unless I figure out how to live on sardines and sats.

### My situation:​

-   Never left the country.
-   Don’t speak Spanish.
-   Still need to get a passport. (Yeah, yeah, I know.)
-   But I’m ready to **do hard things** if it means trading fluorescent lights and HVAC for sunlight, seafood, and grounding.

### What I’m looking for:​

-   **Cooler, elevated climates** — Comasagua or “Little Switzerland” area has me intrigued. I cannot stand humidity (I’m not a frog).
-   A **gated community if necessary**, but ideally something secure and local, not a Miami expat bubble.
-   I’d love access to a **pool, river, or lake** — I’m CT-friendly and looking to stay that way.
-   **Remote work needs:** I know this is a big one… what’s the _real_ internet situation? Anyone rocking Starlink? How painful is it to stay connected from up in the hills?

### What I need help with:​

1.  **Trusted realtors, lawyers, or fixers** you’ve worked with?
2.  Is Comasagua overhyped or a hidden gem?
3.  **Internet realities** — Starlink, fiber, LTE, carrier pigeons?
4.  What’s the starter kit for an American building or buying in ES who has never owned land abroad?
5.  Would love tips on land buying, utility hookups, dealing with local contractors (especially from a builder’s perspective).

### Listings I’m eyeing:​

I’ve found a few from:

-   [RE/MAX El Salvador](https://www.remax-centralamerica.com/el-salvador/comasagua/land-in-co-002282623003)
-   [Bienes Raíces en El Salvador](https://www.bienesraicesenelsalvador.com/property/terrenos-con-vista-5000vr2-calle-a-comasagua-para-vivir-o-negocio-jda-cea-c?agent=bienes-raices-en-el-salvador)
-   [Elsalvadorsurfrealestate.com](https://elsalvadorsurfrealestate.com/)
-   [Oceansideelsalvador.com](https://oceansideelsalvador.com/)

Any leads, locals, or mitohackers already living this life — I’d love to connect.

I’m not looking for a Whole Foods and good WiFi. I’m looking for **a place to rebuild mitochondrial health**, eat like a fisherman, and start prepping for phase two of life before I get too crusty.

Appreciate any guidance from those ahead of me on the trail.
Let’s quantum leap this thing.

# Thread: JAUNDICE = OXYGEN HOLOCAUST THAT = HIGHER DISEASE RISK FOR BABY

**Subforum:** Female Quantum Biology
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-27T02:13:14+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jaundice-oxygen-holocaust-that-higher-disease-risk-for-baby.30774/post-351357>

**Neonatal Jaundice: Blue Light Therapy is a Human Disease Incubator**

Neonatal jaundice treatment shifted from full-spectrum light to blue light (425–475 nm) in the last 35 years, ignoring quantum biology. Centralized medicine, blind to neuropsin (UVA receptor) and melanopsin (435–465 nm), assumed blue light was safe for bilirubin photoisomerization (Oláh et al., 2013). Infants, with translucent skin and underdeveloped skeletons, allow deep blue light penetration, reflecting off bone to stimulate melanocytes. Long-term studies reveal increased dysplastic nevi in these children, a melanoma risk factor (Oláh et al., 2013).

Blue light’s mitotic effect mirrors uveal melanoma (UM) studies: human UM cells exposed to blue light (475 nm) show increased proliferation, an effect blocked by blue-light filters (Hu et al., 2014). In neonates, blue light decouples POMC signaling, overstimulating alpha-MSH and melanogenesis without UV’s protective feedback.

This drives mitochondrial heteroplasmy in melanocytes, as blue light suppresses cytochrome c oxidase, increasing ROS and mtDNA mutations (Godley et al., 2005). Mothers with circadian mismatches often from blue light exposure pass higher heteroplasmy rates to infants, amplifying the risk (Wallace, 2010).

This is how most childhood diseases transfer in the modern world. Light is the first injury children sustain in the germline that eventually becomes their body. They come into their lives with a higher heteroplasmy rate and this puts them closer to disease and death. Lowering their heteroplasmy post natally should be the goal fo the parents who harmed their germlines prenatally. Today's 50% infertility rate in zip codes is Nature's warning that maybe most of the world is not fit to be parents due to the light stress they allow.

In women with PCOS, endometriosis, and men with sperm motility issues due to blue light stress infertility rates are quoted between 30% to 50% in the infertility literature. Additionally, older women may have even higher infertility rates with lower chances of conception each cycle. Younger women on BCPs over 15 years are also experience epic infertility rates. The data is everywhere.

# Thread: Brook’s optimal health journal
