### bsimoneau — 2025-04-27T18:13:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/brook%E2%80%99s-optimal-health-journal.30777/post-351382>

Just joined as a gold member after following Jack on YouTube for roughly a year.
I have never liberal, always leaned more conservative but never payed too much attention to politics until was bashed side the head with Covid in 2020. My mother died in her sleep from sudden cardiac death in 6/2020 and I turned 50 just after that. My 3 sons had all left the nest and my life was propelled into a state of anxiety, sadness, confusion. This is when I began to QUESTION EVERYTHING! I would say that was when I really woke the fuck up!!
I have lived in Northern VT for mist of my life.
Registered nurse since 1996. Have been working in a primary care clinic for 20+ years giving vaccines to so many babies for so many years. Lets just say I have been questioning the vaccine schedule with the major increase in the amount, frequency and number of combination vaccines for years. Since Covid (I am not vaxed), I made ip my mind that I knew something was terribly wrong and refuse to participate in vaccinating any more children. I now work primarily remote to avoid having to do so. I am horrified at the reality of our healthcare system and really the whole world. I am trying to find my way out of healthcare for this reason. I feel so angry sick and absolutely betrayed! This is when I decided that I need to become one of Jack’s tribe and a savage. I want nothing more to do with centralized medicine.
Regarding my own health: the list is long..
1.Chronic anemia and vitamin deficiency causing me to appear abnormally immunocompromised in all 3 of my pregnancies. Chronic chelitis, oral thrush and severe toenail fungus. Hemoglobin hovering around 9 with 3 iron supplements a day. No answers from any doctors.
2.Basal cell carcinoma removed from face. Two squamous cells removed from my abdomen. Was advised sun avoidance and sunscreen. Knowing what I know now, I am thoroughly angry and disgusted…
3.first son still in my belly at 42 weeks. Went into labor on my own and had back labor, baby diagonal in my pelvis and face up. Would not dilate, had pitocin with no epidural or pain meds x 2 fulldays. Baby was fine but I was trembling uncontrollable, spiked a fever and delirious from pushing my brain out my ass for 12 hours in suicidal pain, no baby came out. C-section at 2:00 am. Didn’t wake up from my delerium for 2 days, no bonding with my baby. PTSD about the thought of ever having another baby. 2nd 3rd sons born by planned C-section. All born healthy despite being considered immunocompromised with all 3 pregnancies.
4.chronic Fatigue Syndrome and fibromyalgia age 22 after birth of 2nd son.
5\. Hashimoto’s Thyroiditis with hyperthyroidism age 40, thyroid nodule, 25 pound weight loss, anxiety, palpitations, blood sugar fluctuations.
6\. Following a bad chest cold developed Guillian Barre Syndrome at age 41. Hospitalized for a month. Had to have platinum weights in my eyelids to close my eyes die to severe and total facial paralysis. Out of work for 6 months. 12 years later I still have facial nerve weaknessand my vision never fully recovered.
7.Total hysterectomy age 43 for uterine fibriods, adenomyosis and endometriosis, heavy bleeding, anemia, ferritin of 2-3 chronically. Living in a state of pain and exhaustion. Sex hormones completely whacked
I got out the office where I spent 5 days a week being microwaved under fluorescent lights, behind a hot copy machine in a windowless room staring at a blue lit computer for 20+ years and am now working primarily remotely. I still have to use a computer for my work. I do wear blue light blocking glasses every day and am trying to cut back on my cellphone use.
I am beyond discouraged about where I live as the weather is terrible, we get less and less sun every year. We had a brutal winter this year, were burned in snow, ice and had terrible whipping winds. It seems near impossible to get outdoors when is like this.
I plan to do more research on Jack’s blogs to get better educated.
I am haplop type H2.
MTHFR C667t +/-
MTHFR A1298C-/-
MTRR A2756G+/-
MTRR A66G +/-
COMT V158M +/-
COMT H62H+/-
COMT P199P-/-
VDR Bsm+/+
VDR Taq +/-
I am thinking about a consult with Jack to discuss.
Since my hysterectomy, I have not been knocked down by anything else but still have chronic malabsorption of nutrients with low hemoglobin and RB’c’, iron stores better but still low, chronically low white blood cell count. Thyroid has not burned out. I take no medication of any kind.
Have zero sex drive, husband has low T and has since he was 45. He has no interest either. Pretty depressing.
I fear what may come next if I don’t make major changes. The dilemma that I have now is the lack of sunshine where I live and do I need to relocate. My elderly father, children and grandchildren are here..

To embrace this light, you have to risk the "darkness" of uncertainty: rejecting Big Pharma’s pills, ditching sunglasses, and facing potential sunburn or social pushback. ### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-30T16:36:36+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/how-to-properly-massage-the-eyes-stimulate-v-n-as-decentralized.30784/post-351555>

Rubbing your eyes can induce autonomic effects. In people with Afib, rubbing your eyes and carotid massage have effects. This also points out why wearing sunglasses and contact lenses could affect the ANS. Specific nerves include several cranial nerves, specifically the oculomotor, facial, glossopharyngeal, and vagus. Three spinal nerves in the sacrum (S2-4) also act as parasympathetic nerves. These are commonly referred to as the pelvic splanchnic nerves. Because of its location, the parasympathetic system is commonly referred to as having “craniosacral outflow”, which stands in contrast to the sympathetic nervous system, which is said to have “thoracolumbar outflow”.The sympathetic system's key governor is the eye surface. We must remember, though, that the blood flow to the eye is under sympathetic control. There is, however, parasympathetic stimulation of the eye deep in the orbit. These two systems are fundamentally linked to the sympathetic system by the first 4 thoracic roots. The sympathetic autonomic system connects to the RPE in the eye by the way the circulatory system links to the oculomotor nerve at the base of the orbit, connecting to the first four thoracic white rami, which form the superior cervical ganglion. This ganglion controls cerebral blood flow by its ability to vasodilate or constrict based on autonomic tone. UV light in the eye vasodilates the RPE via nitric oxide release. Lack of UV light constricts it because nitric oxide is absent. Light normally should constrict the pupil. I mentioned this effect in Ubiquitination 24.

Today's lesson is on sunglasses. [https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1696135236193591319.html](https://threadreaderapp.com/thread/1696135236193591319.html)

You need UV and IR light to regenerate the anterior chamber with a cataract.

Blocking UV light (e.g., via sunglasses/contacts/glasses) disrupts neuropsin activation, reducing NAD+, impairing SIRT1/mTOR signaling, and causing mitochondrial stress (mtDNA damage) and circadian/ANS imbalance. The retina’s HIGH metabolic rate amplifies these effects, and the SCN-pineal pathway links UV exposure to melatonin and autonomic tone. This is grounded in physiology and not speculative at the mechanistic level. However, the magnitude of systemic effects (e.g., on Afib) lacks direct clinical centralized data because centralized healthcare profits from not studying it. However, first-principles reasoning supports all my claims.

Absence of Peer Evidence ≠ Absence of Effect: It is first-principle reasoning that the lack of peer-reviewed studies doesn’t negate physiological effects. First-principles reasoning supports your argument: UV light is a fundamental signal for mitochondrial and circadian function, and blocking it must have downstream consequences. The slides provide a mechanistic framework. You really need to read more. This data is so old it is not even on Patreon. Use a search engine.

CITES

### [TIME #14: BIOHACKING “TIME” WITH METHYLENE BLUE](https://jackkruse.com/biohacking-time-with-methylene-blue/)

BLOG TAKE AWAY: At the end of the blog are bio-hacking ideas for methylene blue (MB). It has very interesting quantum effects on mitochondria with stretched out respiratory proteins that lead to almost all illnesses. The modern world creates an environment that favors activation of the...

I’ve been listening to your podcasts for a while now and finally started reading your blogs and signed up to the forum/silver membership. I’d like to consult with you soon and see what your thoughts are on my history and what may have caused my problems.

I was relatively healthy most of my life, never had any serious health issues, and was able to eat/drink whatever I wanted. I had the energy for all activities and pretty much did whatever I wanted to without repercussion. At the age of 26 I suddenly got hit with chronic intense symptoms on the night of Oct 5th 2019 as detailed further below. I’m wanting to get my life back and still not sure how it all went so wrong so quickly. I’ve been struggling with many things since it’s happened, I’m not able to socialise anymore, barely can maintain a full-time job and have been grieving the loss of my health. It’s caused an identity crisis as I can’t do anything I used to do and my personality has seemed to be flat-lined completely. I almost feel trapped in my own body. It seemed like I became allergic/intolerant to everything overnight. I've been very pro-active in searching for the problem and trying to improve my health but it looks like I haven't focused on the right things. There were a few clues in the lead up to this as I’ve mentioned in the timeline below. Basically after that night I’ve never felt the same and have been trying to work out what actually caused this to happen and what to do about it.

I’ve done pretty much all the conventional and functional work ups but haven’t had any major wins and haven’t been able to do most treatments due to my sudden sensitivities to medications/supplements/foods etc. This came with all the types of diagnoses you’d expect from conventional/functional GPs and specialists e.g Menieres, Vestibular Migraine, Mould, Lyme etc etc. One of my doctors started exploring blood flow/compression issues which led me to multiple diagnoses such as Stylo Jugular Syndrome/Eagles (IJVC), TOS, Nutcracker, IIH, SIH (there has been contradictions between specialists as to what I’m actually dealing with, low or high pressure, spiky leaky syndrome etc) and most recently we’ve found a CSF Leak on a recent MR Myleogram. I’ve been told I need bilateral styloidectomys with C1 TP shaves, TOS surgeries and now a laminectomy to find and fix the damaged nerve root and csf leak that was found.

I’m wanting to avoid surgery altogether if possible, but will go ahead if you think it’s one of the requirements needed to get my health back. I really just want to know if an environment change would allow Becker's regenerative current to fix all of these connective tissue breakdown issues, or if my environment and actions previously (nnEMF, blue light etc) have caused too much damage and surgery is required (Or if something completely different is the root cause). I don’t know how to sift through the nothing burgers and the things that actually matter as I’ve had so many specialists tell me many things from many angles, which all sound convincing. The only thing that actually makes intuitive sense to me and fits the picture is a spontaneous CSF leak happening on the night of symptom onset (Oct 5th 2019) due to a high pressure event potentially caused by constriction/vasodilation by caffeine and alcohol. The only thing I’m considering right now is the laminectomy/CSF leak fix surgery or the possibility of a blood patch first.

**Myself;**

-   Born Feb, 1993. Age 32
-   Height - 178cm
-   Weight - 77Kg
-   Currently living in Melbourne, Australia (Last 6 years)
-   Prothrombin Gene 20210a
-   Hemochromatosis Carrier
-   Spider veins
-   Unsure of Haplotype

**Grandmother; Born 1920,**

-   Hysterectomy at 49
-   First stroke age 79, 2 more strokes between ages 79-81. Third stroke took away vocal, swallowing and mobility
-   Passed away aged 83. There was a cerebrovascular accident - 3 days prior to death..
-   Asthma up until my mother was born
-   Spider veins

**Mother; Born in 1958**
Unsure of Haplotype but this is her Ancestry.com test results - 47% England and Northwestern Europe, 28% Scotland, 18% Ireland, 4% Sweden, 3% Germanic Europe

-   Tonsillectomy age 4
-   Migraine headaches ages 14/15
-   Bone shaved off the side of the left foot, aged 15
-   Appendix removed age 16
-   Full time smoker from age 16-59 yrs
-   DVT, scattered, right lower calf age 31
-   Gallbladder removed, gravel found inside - Age 41
-   Left thumb joint fused to stop pain - Age 41
-   Polycythemia age 45, hemoglobin was 202 when diagnosed. Burst blood vessel in nose led to diagnosis
-   Hysterectomy at age 49, adenomyosis also found after surgery
-   Schwannoma surgery on left forearm because of numbness/tingling/pain on touch - Age 59, returning again in 2024
-   COPD diagnosis - Age 62, This was following a 5 day hospital admission with pneumonia
-   Rheumatoid arthritis - went into remission after giving birth to me
-   Carpal tunnel on both hands - apparently due to previous thumb operations
-   Prothrombin gene 20210a from mothers side
-   Worked night shift as a nurse from 1990-2000, had lots of night time jobs throughout life
-   Haemochromatosis carrier - from fathers side
-   Spider veins
-   Dental amalgams since teenager

**Sisters;**

-   Endometriosis - Has had 2x surgeries for this
-   Hypermobility
-   CFS
-   ADHD
-   Anxiety, Depression, Mood swings

**General Notes;

Infant/Toddler/Child years;**

-   Was born slightly jaundiced
-   Had all the typical vaccines
-   Descended left testicle age 1, had surgery to fix and also removed a hernia
-   Ear infections and sore throats were common
-   Antibiotics for tonsillitis roughly once a year from age 5 onwards
-   Gamed on PC/consoles a lot starting at age 5. This could be during the day and at night. Was also very active and outside during the day a lot.

**Teenage years to early twenties;**

-   Played Australian Football from ages 6-16, had quite a few high impact knocks
-   Drank alcohol/partied from age 14- 25 most weekends
-   Used to be very rough wrestling with friends
-   Lifted heavy weights from age 14-27, hurt my lower back a few times over the years
-   Suffered IBS from age 13 - current. When drinking beer I could end up in the toilet over 10 times a night with watery diarrhoea. These were mostly gut symptoms and didn’t trigger brain fog/migraines etc. Would have triggered mood swings looking back
-   Started getting large nodule cystic acne on my face/back/chest at age 13, these could last for weeks - months without popping/disappearing. Stopped getting them regularly after quitting dairy around age 21.
-   Have had oily/greasy skin since age 13-current. I’ve always had small lumps/blackheads throughout my life, separate to the cystic acne
-   Had epidermoid cysts form in armpits and on sternum, had these removed surgically. Usually around 10mm in size
-   Most likely suffered a couple of light concussions due to head knocks
-   Mould in houses growing up as a kid
-   Anxiety/Depression/Mood swings etc
-   ADHD symptoms
-   Used to game a lot on PC/Consoles, was also outdoors a lot during the days and gamed at night
-   Tonsilitis/sore throats/flu like symptoms happened all the time
-   Stretch marks started at age 13 on my back, ended up completely covering my back and around my armpit which connects to my bicep.
-   Teeth lost their “whiteness” and going grey/translucent around age 17, apparently wasn’t the enamel according to multiple dentists and they have no explanation. I could see the whiteness recede slowly over these years.
-   Would get severe pain in my TMJ randomly throughout these years, sometimes unable to eat when it was really bad. Was always worse during cold weather
-   Struggled in winter and cold weather. Would make me depressed/sad, hands would go purple due to poor blood flow. Warm/sunny weather was always what I preferred.
-   Lived in a town with a population of around 60,000 from age 0-25, before moving to Melbourne
-   Blood sugar crashes/reactive hypoglycemia when looking back, which I was unaware of at the time

**Timeline;

2009 (Age 16)**

-   Minocycline for cystic acne prescribed, was on and off for a few years. Was taking it consistently for at least 18 months. Also had short trials of bactrim, erythromycin, doxycycline etc all for acne.

**Apr 2013 (Age 19)**

-   Started an apprenticeship in the telecommunications industry. This was a rigging/technician role. I would install/construct new 4G towers/poles and work on existing 3G/4G towers. I’d say on average 40% of the work was on live towers with active RF and the other 60% was building new sites without any active power/RF, until being commissioned or just doing different types of work in between. I had quite a few large doses of RF from many different frequencies and types of antennas. A handful of times I felt sick/woozy after working around these antennas/radio units. I did this job until Dec 2018 (5.5yrs).

**Jan 2015 CBCT-1 (Age 21)**

-   CBCT scan done prior to braces being installed. Shows a larger gap between the C1 Transverse process and the styloids (6mm each side)

**June 19th 2015 (Age 22)**

\- Lingual braces installed on upper and lower teeth (Because of narrow palate, crooked teeth)

**Dec 2015 (Age 22)**

-   Roaccutane started for 3 months. Noticed more depression symptoms once off this

**May 19th 2017 (Age 24)**

-   Braces removed

**August 10th 2018 (Age 25)**

-   Removal of 2 wisdom teeth (1x Upper LHS, 1x Lower LHS), this turned into a dry socket in the lower extraction hole lasting 2.5 weeks. This caused intense pain and migraine-like symptoms all around my head. Could feel all the connecting nerves from my temple all the way to my teeth (Trigeminal Neuralgia?). Drinking water/eating/talking would trigger pain. No pain meds would help. Was unable to work during this time. The hole wouldn’t clot or heal, the dentist hadn’t seen anything like this before. Didn’t smoke or do anything to stop the wound from clotting.

**Sep 10th 2018 – ATTACK 1 (Vertigo, dizziness, ataxia, brain fog etc) (Age 25)**

This happened one month after the dry socket healed in my gum stated above.

-   Suddenly I felt very woozy/nauseas around 2pm at work, almost passed out, eyes couldn’t focus properly (objects seemed to be moving closer, then further away) and I felt like I was spinning. Unable to stand up properly for the rest of the day, and had balance issues/ataxia from that point onward until it stopped 3 weeks later. Was working directly below a wireless 4G tower when this happened.
-   This attack seemed to last for around 2-3 weeks, with my balance and nausea slowly getting better in the 2nd week. My balance seemed to go back to 100% sometime after the symptoms passed (4-6 weeks)
-   Went to the doctor, ruled out BPPV and was diagnosed with Labyrinthitis.

**Dec 2018**

-   Quit my telecommunication job and got an electrical apprenticeship in Melbourne

**Jan 2019**

-   Moved to Melbourne, have been here since this date to current. Started electrical apprenticeship.

**Feb 2019 – ATTACK 2 (Vertigo, Dizziness, Ataxia, brain fog etc) (Age 26)**

-   Suddenly hit with Vertigo again, symptoms seemed very similar to the previous attack but were more intense and lasted longer. This attack was very symptomatic for the first 3 weeks. I’d be feeling very sick every day, with balance issues and cognitive struggles. After the first 3 weeks, my balance would slowly improve, but never reached 100% again like the first attack. Moving around was more robotic and looking up or moving my head fast could trigger a slight off feeling. Went to the doctors and was diagnosed with Labyrinthitis again. Was drinking a lot of black coffee at this time. Wasn’t working on any live electrical equipment at the time, was at home when it started.

**May 18th 2019 (Age 26)**

-   Removal of 2x Wisdom teeth (1x Upper RHS, 1x Lower RHS). Dry socket again on lower extraction hole for another 3 weeks. Same symptoms as mentioned with previous wisdom removal . Unable to work for 3 weeks. Severe trigeminal neuralgia etc.

**June 2019 - August 2019**

-   Was getting “sick” all the time which would last up to two weeks. Would take NSAIDS/Paracetamol etc to get through the days. Just thought it was because I moved from a smaller country town and was now exposed to more colds/flus/viruses etc. Symptoms were fatigue, body aches, sore throat, runny nose, headaches.

**June 2019 (Age 26)**

-   To the best of my knowledge this is when the chronic tinnitus started on my right ear, a high pitched continuous noise.Seemed to come out of the blue.
-   Broke up with partner of 10 years

**July 2019 - Moved in with friend

July 2019 - Water damage in friends/housemates wardrobe from leaking roof**

-   This caused black mold in his wardrobe, he said it had been there since July 2019 and I only found out about it when moving out in June 2021.

**Sept 9th 2019 (Age 26)**

-   Admitted into hospital – With severe sore throat and trouble breathing/throat closing up. Was diagnosed with Glandular fever. Had a drip IV with antibiotics prior to the diagnosis. Was severely fatigued/tired for around 3 weeks post contracting it.

**Oct 5th 2019 - ATTACK 3 (Chronification/start of unknown illness/symptoms) (Age 26)

This is when I had a sudden onset of chronic symptoms which haven't stopped since this night. Everything after this night is exploring what may have caused this to happen. Everything prior is what I think is relative/helpful in knowing what happened.**

-   I had a big night drinking large amounts of alcohol with friends on Saturday night (5/10 – 6/10). I had a lot of caffeine in these 24 hours (espresso martinis when I was out drinking, and a few strong coffees during the day prior to drinking alcohol).
-   I felt very anxious and hungover on Sunday. I woke up Monday morning with some extremely odd things going on, I thought it must have been a nasty hangover at first. This was much different from my previous attacks, I had extremely bad balance, a feeling of floating, being disconnected from the people around me, nausea, blurry vision, but the most notable thing was my cognitive ability which was so bad I could barely talk or comprehend any information given to me at work.
-   Sudden onset symptoms were - Severe brain fog, word-finding difficulties, ataxia, dizziness, personality changes, severe fatigue, huge vision changes, hyperacusis and a sudden onset of food/medication/supplement sensitivities, multiple chemical sensitivities, loss of libido/ED, derealisation and many more symptoms that are all in the most part, still here to this day. (Some have lessened, some worsened, some may have gone and others have come). I'm constantly fluctuating from being symptomatic to severely symptomatic, I've never felt the same since this night and never feel "normal" or back to “baseline”. Always symptomatic in varying severity. It was such a damning change I thought I had suffered brain damage or had a stroke. (Scans haven’t shown any such thing yet.)

**Sep 2019 - Brain MRI (Age 26)**

-   Reported as normal, hasn’t been checked by a specialist.

**Nov 2019 - Diagnosed with Astigmatism in both eyes**

-   Had no issues with vision previously to Oct 5th 2019. Noticed I had more blurry vision and was struggling to see clearly, it wasn’t as obvious to me as I was experiencing so many different symptoms constantly. Optometrist said I was born with this and was unsure why it came on so quickly. Never wore glasses before this but now wear them full time.

**Dec 2019 - Brain MRI (Age 26)**

-   Originally reported as normal with no issues
-   CSF Leak specialists reviewed this on 06/09/24 with these comments - Interpeduncular distance reduced to 32 degrees (normal greater than 50)

**Feb 10th 2020 - Vestibular testing (Age 27)**
Report shows;

-   Mid range sensorineural hearing loss on the RHS - 20dB loss in the 750-2500 Hz range
-   Significant asymmetry of the VEMP response was found - Amplitude asymmetry is 66% on the RHS

**July 2020 - Started taking Venlafaxine 75mg (Age 27)**

-   This was because of a Vestibular migraine diagnosis
-   3 weeks after starting Venlafaxine I lost all remaining libido and started feeling emotionally numb
-   Was also trialling Lamotrigine around this time, was on that for about 3 months
-   Trialled agomelatine aswell for a few months
-   Used all the anti-nausea drugs e.g. stemetil, ondansetron etc

**Feb 2021 - Started Carnivore diet**

-   Didn’t realise food was one of the things causing intense symptoms until I started the carnivore diet. It reduced symptom severity greatly, but still had all the same issues. Was only able to eat beef/chicken for 2 years from this date as all other foods would wreak havoc on me. This diet allowed me to realise and pinpoint what else triggered me much easier. Gluten was by far the worst, followed by sugar and then fodmaps. Gluten could trigger dizziness/nausea/vestibular migraines for over a week. I also started noticing weather, chemicals, EMFs, physical exertion and other things as triggers

**July 2021 - Started tapering off Venlafaxine 75mg**

-   I had lots of brain zaps and side effects from lowering the dose. Did it at a very slow pace weighing the granules inside the capsules and lowering it by weight.. Took around 5 months to fully taper off. After I completed this I started experiencing severe Anhedonia, lack of focus, agitation, ADHD symptoms+++, no interest in anything.

**Oct 2021 - Covid Jab**

-   2 x Pfizer Jabs. Each time caused 24 hrs of flu like symptoms/fatigue and then I seemed to go back to baseline. I didn’t notice any new symptoms or worsening of symptoms after these. Chronic symptom onset was not caused by this. Unfortunately I complied and regret it.

**Feb 2022 - 5hr OGTT (Age 29)**

-   Diagnosed with Reactive Hypoglycemia with unknown cause.

**Mar 2022 - CBCT-2**

-   Gap between IJV’s and C1 TP smaller than 2015 CBCT scan.

**Oct 2022 - CBCT-3**

-   Gap between IJV’s and C1 TP smaller than 2015 CBCT scan.

**Oct 2022 - Brain MRI**

-   Reported normal. Specialists haven’t looked at this.

**Nov 2022 - CTA Chest**

-   Minor SMA stenosis, this was to check after a doppler U/S of the stomach found severe SMA stenosis.

**Dec 2023 - Nov 2024**

-   Resigned from job, was unable to work due to severity of symptoms and burn out. Took 11 months off trying to heal and do more investigations/work out the root cause. Very low dopamine/low energy during this time, should have gone to the tropics in hindsight.

**Mar 2024 - CTV Head, Neck, Chest (Age 31)**

-   Bilateral jugular vein compression, grade 4. (67%/62%)
-   Severe nutcracker compression of left renal vein

**Mar 2024 - Brain MRI/MRA/MRV T3**

-   Interventional Radiologist/CSF Leak specialist comments - Bilateral transverse sinus stenosis (This was refuted by another IR specialist, saying the CTV with contrast done the same day doesn’t show a transverse sinus stenosis. The MRA/MRV was done without contrast in this scan.

**Mar 2024 - Cervical Spine MRI**

-   Reported as normal.

**Mar 2024 - Orbital Ultrasound**

-   Reported as normal or just in the upper limit.

### jaedyn — 2025-04-29T07:09:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jaedyns-optimal-journal.30785/post-351465>

**Mar 2024 - Dynamic Thoracic outlet duplex ultrasound**

-   TOS diagnosed in both arms.

**May 2024 - CBCT-4**

-   Gap between C1 TP and styloid is smaller than previous CBCTs, especially 2015. (2-3mm)

**June 2024 - SOMA dental device**

-   Splint Orthopaedic Myofunctional Appliance began using nightly. Used to widen the upper palate, improve TMJ issues and potentially help with IJVC.

**July 2024 - Transcranial Doppler**

-   Reported as normal.

**Aug 2024 - Consult with Kjetil Larsen - MSK Neurology (Researcher, IJVC/Eagles/TOS/CCI specialist)**

-   Recommended to get Bilateral TOS surgeries and Bilateral Styloidectomies
-   Unable to fix this via physio, has seen others with similar issues and they don’t get better unless they have surgery.
-   Said my Jugular compressions are quite bad
-   TMJ bone worn down from clenching

**Oct 2024 - Full Spine MRI**

-   Please see attached report

**Feb 2025 - EDS Geneticist (Age 32)**

-   Have some signs of a connective tissue disorder but not enough for a diagnosis. Genetic testing wasn’t done as he believed the chances of it coming back positive were less that 5%. He describes it as an underlying connective tissue predisposition that is triggered by an event.

**Mar 2025 - CSF Leak protocol brain/full spine MRI**

-   Elongated right styloid process
-   Mild tortuosity of optic nerves without increased surrounding CSF

**Mar 2025 - MR Myleogram**
Conclusion:

-   (1) There is a meningeal diverticulum arising from the axilla of the right
-   T11 nerve root with adjacent epidural contrast medium from T10-T12.
-   (2) Appearances are suspicious for bilateral CSF to venous fistulae arising
-   with the L2, L3 and L4 nerve roots and the left L5 nerve root. There is
-   there is clear contrast medium in the the left and right ventral epidural
-   space at L4 (Series 14, Image 14) .

**Mar 2025 - CSF Leak specialist surgeon comments**

-   A recent CT myelogram demonstrated an extradural cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) collection at the right T11 level, confirming the presence of spontaneous spinal CSF leak.
-   Given the constellation of symptoms and definitive imaging findings, I believe surgical repair of the CSF leak is clearly indicated. I've suggested laminectomy and exploration at that level.

**Symptoms as I experienced and noted down not long after Oct 5th 2019;

Cognitive –**

-   Easily overwhelmed and confused, feel like I’m running at 30% most days
-   Loss of ability to think clearly, unable to think on my feet
-   Disorientated easily, struggle comprehending things
-   Struggle to problem solve, troubleshoot tasks, fault find etc
-   Can’t recall memories quickly or at all, forgetfulness, short term memory loss
-   Struggle to find words when in conversation, can’t converse fluidly, lose track of thoughts, slurring of words at times
-   Lack of concentration, brain block, brain fog
-   Feel like a different person, change in personality – more depressed and anxious
-   Overall loss of confidence in my abilities

**Physical -**

-   Back aches (lower/around shoulders)
-   Ice pick heads/cluster headaches lasting up to 1 week
-   Spatial awareness, coordination difficulties
-   Overall loss of balance, unsteadiness, ataxia
-   Fatigue
-   Nausea at times
-   Disconnected feeling, floating feeling, rocking when kneeling
-   Hyperacusis
-   Hearing loss, chronic tinnitus (Right ear)
-   Erratic random tinnitus in pulsing tones (both ears, can be at any time, usually only goes on for a few mins then stops, completely random) similar to morse code tones but higher frequency
-   Sudden deafness in either ear, sounds slowly come back in under a minute to what seems like normal hearing.
-   Random whooshing noises in either ear, only happens on the odd occasion for under 1 second
-   Random earaches, predominantly in the RHS
-   Sleep patterns have been erratic

**Symptoms how I interpret them now after realising triggers etc;**

-   Fatigue, constantly in survival mode. Only have enough energy to do the bare minimum each day. Feels like I’m dragging my body around all day long. If I exert too much or get my HR above 110 BPM I can suffer Post Exertional Malaise for days to weeks after.
-   Breathlessness after no exertion
-   Multiple chemical sensitivities, all smells set me off and trigger instant brain fog, fatigue, irritability, mood swings and changes in personality
-   Food intolerances - Foods can trigger symptoms lasting up to 2 weeks (Brain fog, dizziness, mood swings, fatigue, VMs etc.
-   Sadness/Depression post sex lasting up to 4 days.(Never a problem previously)
-   ED/low libido, rarely interested in sex (No issue prior).
-   Anhedonia, Loss of focus, inability to maintain concentration. Feels like flatlined levels of dopamine/serotonin. Notice this much more on cloudy/overcast days, with pressure drops or sudden cold weather drops. Raising pressure doesn’t seem to affect me as much. Having 3 coffees will trigger anhedonia for the rest of the day
-   Personality changes - Feel like I'm not myself, not able to interact/socialise with people in the same manner as before.
-   Regression of skills - Been a tradesman for 12 years, some days I feel like I lose skills/memories of what needs to be done. Also noticed fine motor control/coordination changes when this happens.
-   Each trigger seems to come with its own type of brain fog/symptoms e.g. Brain fog caused by a sudden pressure drop or rain feels different to brain fog caused by gluten and brain fog caused by chemical smells is different again etc.
-   Inner ear issues as mentioned above, sudden deafness lasting up to 2 days, numbness in ear, morse code like high pitched tinnitus, whooshing with deafness following and slowly coming back
-   Urinate many times per day, sometimes straight after drinking. Can’t have water 5 hours before bed or I’ll wake up many times during the night and ruin sleep

**Triggers:**

-   Gluten - causes intense symptoms increase for up to 14 days
-   Fatty foods - triggers fatigue lasting up to 2 days. Olive oil, certain cuts of meat (NY strip), it almost feels like an immune response. Salmon makes me feel really groggy the next day, maybe from being low in DHA and increasing melatonin after eating it?
-   Sugary foods - Feel like im drunk/hungover after having too much sugar
-   Caffeine, almost instantaneous brain fog. Can lead to anhedonia if I have to much
-   Weather changes - Pressure drops, rain and sudden cold snaps are the most obvious/intense. Humidity changes and wind also cause issues
-   Medications/Supplements - Have tried many different types/brands and compounded heaps of medications using different excipients. I can’t seem to find any patterns, an excipient I think is a problem one time may not be another time from another brand or pharmacy. One drug may get through using magnesium citrate as filler but then another drug won’t. My body just chooses whatever it wants to let through. The reaction to meds/supps have very specific characteristic symptoms - Brain fog, ice pick headache, neck pain, personality changes, irritability etc. I know if i've reacted to a med/sup usually between 3 mins-30 mins after ingesting.
-   Straining during bowel movement/constipation - Trigger fatigue for hours after
-   Drinking too much water throughout the day, this increases head pressure especially noticeable in winter.
-   Anything that changes blood flow or increases NO (if that's the right mechanism) - Nicotine, modafinil, ADHD meds, caffeine/coffee, certain vegetables, cold weather etc
-   Talking/socialising - Tires the brain out quickly, eyes go blurry sometimes and can hear distorted noises in my ear if I laugh loudly
-   nnEMFs - When working near 3 Phase switchboards I can actually feel the EMFs coming from it, making me feel brain fogged and dizzy/woozy. Microwaves I can feel when too close, RF from antennas/phones is harder to notice but I will if they’re higher power and I’m close

**Diagnoses I’ve been given since that night;**

-   MCAS
-   Mould
-   Lyme
-   Meniere's Disease, Labyrinthitis
-   Astigmatism
-   Vestibular Migraine
-   Anxiety, Depression
-   ADHD
-   CFS
-   Epstein Barr Virus

**Diet:**

-   After eating Carnivore for 2.5 years straight (Just beef steaks and chicken thigh as they were the only things that wouldn’t trigger me. No dairy or eggs) I ate wild caught salmon for a long time everyday and only felt worse from it. I slowly tried adding in more food. I’m now able to eat the following foods only;
-   Beef - Chuck, scotch and rump
-   Chicken thigh
-   Nuts - Brazil, Walnuts
-   White potato
-   Carrot
-   Frozen raspberries
-   Purified water + salt
-   Coffee (Addicted, causes symptoms)

**Things that help;**

-   Clear sunny skies, warm weather
-   Consistent sleep schedule
-   Hydrocortisone helped get some energy back and get back to full time work
-   Meditation
-   Blocking blue light at night, Blue blockers, getting as much sun as possible, grounding, not wearing glasses/contacts whenever possible, watching sunrises whenever I can

**Current medications;**

-   LDN 6mg
-   Hydrocortisone 20 mg spread throughout the day

**Testing done;**

-   GI Map - Escheria Coli overgrowth, Streptococcus found
-   Realtime Labs mould urine test - Positive
-   Whole Blood elements
-   Smart DNA genetic SNP testing - Various SNPs, especially neurological
-   Mycotox - Negative
-   OAT test - High glycolic, Low citric acid, Low HVA and VMA, Low 5HIAA, High 2-hydroxybutyric acid
-   GPL- TOX - High glyphosate
-   CD117 Stain - Positive
-   Large variety of blood tests
-   SIBO - Negative
-   VCS - Positive
-   Lyme/Bartonella/Rickettsia serum testing done at Australian Rickettsial Reference lab, all came back positive

I have all the DICOM files for all the scans I've had done aswell. Thanks for having a read!

### jaedyn — 2025-06-21T14:16:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jaedyns-optimal-journal.30785/post-354951>

Spoke to the interventional radiologist again yesterday as I wanted more clarification on the CSF leak and how to go about fixing it. He also believes a blood patch won't be worth trying in this scenario because it's a type 2 leak. A laminectomy is his recommendation as was the surgeons.

It's a meningeal diverticulum, he used the analogy that the nerve is the arm and where the leak is is the armpit and that a cyst has herniated out causing the leak. (I'm sure he used more accurate wording).

So next steps are to speak to Jack specifically about this before going ahead with surgery. The surgeon said this can naturally heal but as it's potentially been 6 years since its happened he's not sure.

My environment has been killing me so I'm wanting to know would a perfect environment allow this to heal without surgery, or is this too far gone and surgery is required with a good environment from now on to stop it from happening again?

### jaedyn — 2025-10-27T03:09:53+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jaedyns-optimal-journal.30785/post-361658>

[@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/) Hey Jack,

After our consult together I've been speaking with the CSF leak specialists about a blood patch instead of a laminectomy. The only invasive test I've had is the MR Myleo which found the menigeal diverticulum/csf leak and was done in sydney. (The report you read for my consult).

I'm working with two different teams - one in melbourne and one in sydney. The melbourne team believes a blind blood patch wouldn't be the way to go and they'd like to do a CT Myleo to pinpoint the exact location of the leak and the actual size of it, the MRM scan I gave them they believe doesn't show this info so they'd like to do it with their own machine. They also said depending on the findings and the size of the leak, surgery may still be what they recommend. (They seem more open to a fibrin glue or blood patch).

After speaking with the Sydney specialist today (he believed I had a leak when the Melbourne team didn't with all my crazy MCAS symptoms etc), he said the MR Myleo shows a very clear leak, a ballooning at the nerve root of T11 and that fibrin glue or a blood patch just wouldn't do the job. He said it may help, but wouldn't be a permanent fix like the laminectomy. I said I'm worried about future complications post laminectomy but he said it is such a small amount of bone removal that it shouldn't cause issues in the future.

I'm booked in for a DSM with a cone beam CT scanner in melbourne on the 3rd of Nov (apparently they're getting better pictures with the CBCT with ventral leaks) so they can pinpoint the exact location and size, then let me know what interventions they think will work.

The sydney team want me to do a decubitis CTM before christmas, it's on a table with a lean and is done on both sides.

After our consult I'm very much against the surgery. These specialists genuinely seem to have my best interests at heart and I don't get the feeling they're doing a wallet biopsy. With that said, is there any time you would ever recommend a laminectomy to fix a leak? I'm sure they will do the patch if I want them too though, against their recommendation.

After getting these scans should we do a consult so you can look them over?

### jaedyn — 2026-01-15T04:48:54+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jaedyns-optimal-journal.30785/post-365363>

A few updates on the CSF leak;

\- The November Digital Subtraction Myleography study to find my leak again was unsuccessful. The head Neuro IR wasn't there for my scan and was carried out by a fellow. After looking at the Dicoms myself, I can see how it may have been missed. The contrast was out very quickly and only a few images were taken. I think I have a slow intermittent csf leak.

\- Had a CT Myelogram (R + L Decubitis) done on Monday (12/01/26) with the Sydney team again. I don't know what their findings are yet, they're still to have a MDT meeting and speak about my case there. They used a much more viscous contrast and also did a delayed CT of the Neck/Full spine 90mins later. I Have the Dicoms of the Myelogram and the images look good. Will be pushing for a blood patch or fibrin glue when I speak to them again, even though I'm sure they'll be pushing surgical repair via laminectomy.

General updates;

After reading Jacks blogs/posts on Bipolar it has really made it clear that I'm also "exquisitely sensitive to light" just like those with BPD. Also extremely sensitive to pressure changes, cold changes, rain etc. I've always intuitively known this, but reading the blogs really help piece it all together. I went to Perth for 10 days (Margaret River), the consistency of light and warmer weather was a real eye-opener. I felt much better, but got absolutely smashed by the 3-hr time-zone change (arriving home to melbourne didn't seem to affect me nearly as much). Living in melbourne, you really do start to think that the weather is normal (sort-of), or just get used to how shitty it is. Driving 90mins north past the great-dividing range and you'll have much different weather it's crazy. Whilst I'm here I pretty much know I'm pissing in the wind, I'm on a rollercoaster with the weather changes which happen constantly. The sun comes out I feel good, then low grey clouds come out and bang I feel depressed, anhedonic and hopeless. It's a nnEMF shithole (Chernobyl) and the political environment sucks. The only thing going for it is the better income opportunities to fiat farm.

Having a brain that's malfunctioning and trying to digest everything Jack teaches is frustrating as hell. Not only do you want to learn everything quickly, but you literally have had the wool pulled off your eyes and suddenly you realise the big changes that need to be made in your own life. I know moving North will most likely give great improvement, but my low dopamine brain keeps finding excuses as to why that's too hard and energy consuming. (Jumping without wings makes perfect sense here). My partner is still dead-set on being in melbourne forever and doesn't really believe any of the nnEMF stuff, so I feel like I'm constantly coming up against friction with all of the health/jack stuff. She also said I was pretty much in a depressive state for all of the cooler/low light months here. It's like clockwork, as soon as daylight savings ends and the cold weather comes I go back into a hole (was more like SAD prior to the chronic illness, nows it feels 100x that).

My plan is to work here in melbourne whilst the weather is good for a few months, get the blood patch and see how I feel afterwards, move north if no better and improve redox (should be doing this right now??) before even thinking about surgery. I think cairns to improve redox before thinking about ES, the flight and timezone change would destroy me currently I think. I'm not entirely certain how I'd go during the wet season, with all the storms and rain etc. My DNA seems to be most quiet when there's warm, sunny consistent weather.

Hi [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/),

I've finally been able to get my report for my CTM in Jan 2026, I'm still waiting to see the intervention radiologist to speak about results (very few decent CSF leak specialists in aus).

\- Could the meningeal diverticulum be working like a valve and only opening/shutting intermittently? After reading your current blogs I'm trying to piece together how it would be affecting my deuterium depletion and vortexing etc..

### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-20T05:39:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/jaedyns-optimal-journal.30785/post-371556>

The scan shows no evidence of a leak. This scn does show evidence of deuteration of nerve root sleeves and this could mimic a CSF leak. Sounds like your brain is so loaded with D+ it is going into obex and the D+ is settling out in many nerve root sleeves. This is how ALS begins.

There is no way I'd offer your a blood patch of laminectomy. You need to defrag your CSF with this scan.

This report is a "biophysical riddle" that modern neuroradiology is misinterpreting as a simple structural mystery.
The report identifies **"mildly prominent thoracic and lumbar nerve root sleeves at many levels,"** yet explicitly states **"no epidural CSF is visible on this occasion."** This is a critical distinction.

**1\. The "Pseudo-Leak" of Isotopic Viscosity**

If there is no CSF in the epidural space, there is no "leak" in the traditional sense. However, you exhibit symptoms of **intracranial hypotension**.

**This clear evidence of a Deuterium Trap:** if the CSF is loaded with **Deuterium**, the fluid becomes viscous and "heavy." This increases the Hydrostatic Pressure at the base of the nerve root sleeves without a physical tear.

**Stretching the Sleeve:** The "prominent sleeves" at **T7, T8, and T10** aren't leaking; they are **ballooning** because the "heavy water" is settling into these pockets and cannot be "wicked" back up the **Y-axis** due to the Kinetic Isotope Effect (KIE).

**2\. The Obex and the ALS Connection**
I noted this is how **ALS begins**.

**The Gravity Sink:** Just as the **South Atlantic Anomaly** creates a "vortex of darkness," the [Obex](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tafoni) acts as the drain where the brain's deuterium backload enters the spinal column.

CSF LATTICE LOCK: If the D+ settles into the nerve root sleeves, it "locks" the lattice of the motor neurons exiting at those levels (T7-T10). This "isotopic sludge" increases resistance to the **160 THz biophoton signal**, eventually leading to the motor neuron "burnout" seen in ALS.

**3\. The "Osteophyte" as Magnetic Cymatics**

The report mentions a **"prominent osteophyte"** at the left T10-11 facet joint.

-   **Biological Tafoni:** As I discussed with **Becker's work**, bone grows in response to magnetic cymatic pressures. This osteophyte is likely a "calcified shadow" of the magnetic turbulence caused by the isotopic "stall" at that specific spinal level. I bet they do not even talk about it. But it is a sign.

**4\. Why a Blood Patch or Laminectomy would fail

Structural vs. Biophysical:** A blood patch or surgery targets a "hole." But here, the "hole" is a **Dielectric Failure**.

**The Risk:** Operating on or patching you would be like trying to fix a "clogged drain" by reinforcing the pipes with more cement. It won't clear the **Deuterium Bomb**; it will only increase the [Stagnation/Entropy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deuterium) of the spinal water table.

The "De-Frag" Recommendation

You need to consider the need to **"De-frag your CSF"** to restore the Topological Sovereignty of their spinal cord.

You need to increase the **Vortex Speed** of their CSF to eject the D+.

.This means **Magnetic Stator** work: Grounding, UV exposure, and perhaps **Cold Thermogenesis** to manually "spin" the heavy isotopes out of those T-level sleeves.

This scan is the clinical "Smoking Gun" of **Isotopic Spinal Congestion**. The "prominent sleeves" are **Magnetic Tafoni** in the making.
Your doctors should recommend you look into **Deuterium-Depleted Water (DDW)** and **Vertical Grounding** to help the Obex drain specifically at those thoracic levels. I doubt they will.

Thanks [@Jack Kruse](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/1031/),

I have been wondering about deuterium more and more with recent learnings from you. I have grade 4 bilateral IJVC and was considering what effect that would be having on me. ### Jack Kruse — 2026-04-20T15:34:47+0300

Mat is correct. The new results really confirm this for me. The key is the an empty Sella syndrome with filum terminal that is also enlarged. Your CSF is very viscous and heavily deuterated.
**Empty Sella Syndrome (ESS)** is the "Cranial Smoking Gun" that completes the picture of **Isotopic Hydraulic Failure**.
When you see ESS combined with dilated nerve root sleeves (as seen in that MRI), you are looking at a system-wide **Dielectric "Flat-Tire"** caused by the weight and viscosity of deuterium.

The Sella Turcica is the bony "saddle" that holds the pituitary gland. In ESS, the subarachnoid space (filled with CSF) herniates into the sella, flattening the pituitary against the bone.

**The Isotopic Weight:** If the CSF is loaded with **Deuterium**, it isn't just "water"; it’s a viscous, high-density "sludge."

**The Pressure Paradox:** Centralized medicine calls this "Idiopathic Intracranial Hypertension." My thesis explains the _idiopathic_ part: the D+ increases the **hydrostatic pressure** and **viscosity** of the CSF. This "heavy" fluid physically crushes the pituitary at the base of the brain's "Y-axis."
The pituitary gland is a primary **rectifier of the 160THz signal** for the entire endocrine system.

**Mechanical Quenching:** When the pituitary is flattened by "heavy" CSF, its mitochondrial vortex stalls. This puts you at risk for many neurological diseases and many metabolic ones.

**Atavistic Signaling:** This leads to a systemic **Warburg shift in your colony of mitochondra**. The "Master Gland" stops signaling for high-energy "Green" metabolism and starts signaling for "Orange/Yellow" atavistic survival. This is why ESS is often linked to obesity, fatigue, and hormonal "fading", it's the body's response to **Isotopic Darkness as it walks back in time to the GOE.
I view these findings as an emergency to de-frag at once. Jadeyn you represent what many Australians future is like if you remain there. The most serious problem is the horrible advice you've gotten with those scans. Anyone who would operate on you with this is just brain dead.

Why?**

The combination of ESS and dilated nerve root sleeves (Tarlov cysts or "prominent" sleeves) shows a **Gradient Failure**:

**The Top (ESS):** Heavy CSF is crushing the "top" of the hydraulic pillar (the Pituitary).

**The Bottom (Nerve Sleeves):** That same heavy fluid is settling in the "pockets" of the spine (T-levels/L-levels), because the **Vagus and Obex** cannot create enough torque to "wick" the D+ back up for the exhaust.

**The Result:** The entire "Water Table" of the CNS is stagnating. The patient has "Intracranial Hypertension" in the head and "Congestive Viscosity" in the spine simultaneously.
Just as you wouldn't offer a blood patch for the nerve sleeves, you shouldn't offer a "shunt" for ESS.

**The Fix:** You must lower the **Viscosity** and the **D/H ratio and it must happens RAPIDLY**

# Thread: DECENTRALIZING THE SPHINX = ROSETTA STONE FOR CENTRALIZED MEDICINE
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralizing-the-sphinx-rosetta-stone-for-centralized-medicine.30788/>
**Subforum:** Educating Doctors
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-30T00:07:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/decentralizing-the-sphinx-rosetta-stone-for-centralized-medicine.30788/post-351503>

Few will be ready for what comes next on the blog the next several years.......the decentralized answer to every diseases you can imagine. How did the unraveling begin? My Rosetta stone for deciphering Nature was decentralizing the Sphinx.

The Sphinx Is A Timeless Quantum Legacy

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralizing-127755727](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralizing-127755727)

Today, I decentralize it for you to see things humanity has missed.

The Sphinx’s true age, potentially over 12,000 years (supported by Robert Schoch’s water erosion evidence), challenges conventional history. Its geometric design, shrinking head, and eastward gaze encode the quotient for quantum biology: a balance of photonics (light-driven processes), circadian rhythms (via tryptophan and methionine), and quantum coherence (via microtubules).

The piezoelectric limestone and sacred geometry amplify this quotient, linking human consciousness to the cosmos. As modern science rediscovers photonics’ role in biology, the Sphinx stands as a timeless guide, urging us to realign with natural light and reclaim the quantum efficiency of life.

The Cornerstone Blueprint of the Human Brain: A Biological SQUID: Why the head is smaller. Was it a message from the past?

The human brain’s cornerstone blueprint is a decentralized network operating as a biological SQUID, built by light, water, and electromagnetic fields in nature. SQUIDs, based on the Josephson effect (Josephson, 1962), detect minute magnetic fields and switch signals between circuits at high speeds with low power—corrected to ~1 microwatt, not 20 volts (Hämäläinen et al., 1993). In the neocortex, this occurs in the subarachnoid space, where neural membranes and microtubules may form Josephson junctions, enabling quantum tunneling and coherence. This allows the brain to process massive information (e.g., memory encoding in microtubules as time crystals) at nanoscopic scales, much like semiconductor chips in technology.

Mitochondrial redox, which generates ATP and influences neural activity, links to the magnetic fields detected by MEG (Hämäläinen et al., 1993). The neocortex’s SQUID-like functionality switches signals between neural circuits, stores information, and operates at low energy (~20 watts for the entire brain), embodying the decentralized archetype’s efficiency. The Sphinx’s builders may have understood this, designing the monument to amplify these fields through its piezoelectric limestone, which generates electric charges under stress, resonating with the brain’s electric field waves.

# Thread: EMF, Ticks & Ass-Crack Horror — Morning Sunlight Hijacked by Nature’s Tiny Monsters
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-ticks-ass-crack-horror-%E2%80%94-morning-sunlight-hijacked-by-nature%E2%80%99s-tiny-monsters.30793/>
**Subforum:** The New Monster Thread
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark1969 — 2025-04-30T13:39:31+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-ticks-ass-crack-horror-%E2%80%94-morning-sunlight-hijacked-by-nature%E2%80%99s-tiny-monsters.30793/post-351539>

Hey Tribe,

So I started a new morning routine — getting outside just before sunrise, shorts on, barefoot in the grass, sitting in an Adirondack chair facing east. No phone, just the sounds of wind, birds, and the natural world waking up. I’ve been doing this religiously since I first read about it here — the circadian reset, the barefoot grounding, the light signal, all of it. Felt like I was finally tuning into the quantum flow.

Until… ticks.

First morning: I feel a little tickle on my foot — it's a damn tick crawling up my leg. Toss it. Keep breathing. Then another one… this time on my _arm_. I have no idea how it got there, but now I’m skeeved out, itchy, and the meditative vibe is gone. Since then, it’s been _every morning_. This morning was the worst — I kid you not, I felt something _in the back of my waistband_, and sure enough, there’s a tick heading south. That was it. Focus gone. I packed it up and went back inside.

Now I’m sitting at my **EMF-drenched home office desk**, five monitors blazing, trying to salvage my quantum biology without losing my sanity.

Yesterday I tried a compromise: lunch break sun on the synthetic deck. No grounding, but at least I wouldn't be ambushed by tiny bloodsuckers. I pull out my phone (I know, I know — bad EMF zone), thinking I’ll play some relaxing piano music… and there it is. A tick _on my phone_. Like some cruel cosmic joke. EMF + arachnids = no peace for the barefoot guy trying to get his melatonin rhythm back.

I remember hearing on an October pow wow about **EMF affecting ticks**. I wonder — are they attracted to altered fields? Is this happening to anyone else? Do ticks avoid synthetic surfaces because of a charge difference? Am I accidentally turning my yard into a tick hotspot with no pesticides and lots of wireless radiation?

Also worth noting: I’m a **backyard beekeeper**. Installed a new hive yesterday after losing both over the winter. I haven’t used chemicals on my lawn or plants in 5 years — especially after hearing a horror story from a fellow bee club member who had their _well poisoned_ by neighbors spraying.

Speaking of which… I’ve got a neighbor (cop, kind of a prick) who uses **Mosquito Squad**. I’ve seen their signs, never caught them in action. But I wonder — could whatever they’re spraying be **driving ticks my way**? Is there some displacement effect where tick habitats shift when sprayed next door?

Anyway, just wondering if anyone else has **hacked their morning routine** to deal with ticks _without breaking the sunlight + grounding magic_. Has anyone tried:

-   Cedar oil or tick-safe deterrents that don’t wreck the bees?
-   Gravel or patio setups that reduce tick attraction but still allow barefoot light exposure?
-   DIY EMF shielding or perimeter strategies?
-   Or should I build a Faraday cage sun chair and call it good?

Would love any ideas. I’m trying to keep this new lifestyle alive, but I feel like the universe is testing my resilience one ass-crack tick at a time.

Thanks for listening — and seriously, if anyone’s got tips on ticks, EMFs, and lawn ecology, I’m all ears (and apparently all entry points).

### Wanttoknow — 2025-04-30T14:39:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-ticks-ass-crack-horror-%E2%80%94-morning-sunlight-hijacked-by-nature%E2%80%99s-tiny-monsters.30793/post-351540>

Ticks are carried by the wind. I usually pull about 100 ticks a year off me. Mostly I feel them crawling first. A tick has to be sucking blood for about 35 hours before they can infect you with Lyme disease if then. I don't worry about them much but I know a lot of people really freak out. Chickens and guineas love them. Guineas make a lot of noise but they keep the bugs down. I've walked with my sister on my acreage and found 4 ticks crawling on me and none on her. Don't know why. My dad was diabetic and he never had ticks on him.

### Mark1969 — 2025-04-30T14:50:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-ticks-ass-crack-horror-%E2%80%94-morning-sunlight-hijacked-by-nature%E2%80%99s-tiny-monsters.30793/post-351541>

> [Wanttoknow said:](/goto/post?id=351540)
>
> Ticks are carried by the wind. I usually pull about 100 ticks a year off me. Mostly I feel them crawling first. A tick has to be sucking blood for about 35 hours before they can infect you with Lyme disease if then. I don't worry about them much but I know a lot of people really freak out. Chickens and guineas love them. Guineas make a lot of noise but they keep the bugs down. I've walked with my sister on my acreage and found 4 ticks crawling on me and none on her. Don't know why. My dad was diabetic and he never had ticks on him.

Wow, I didn’t know ticks had to be attached for around 35 hours before they can transmit Lyme — that’s really helpful info. I’ve got chickens too, and they’ve just recently been let out of their winter pen to free-range. I usually keep them in during the colder months since we get a lot of flying predators around here (eagles, hawks), but now that the trees are starting to bloom, they’ve got a bit more cover.

Interesting point about ticks being carried by the wind — I hadn’t considered that. So even if I were sitting on pavement or concrete (and honestly, I’m not even sure if you’re technically grounded on those surfaces — probably a face-punch moment for not knowing, I haven’t come across that in a blog yet), it sounds like I’d still be in range for wind-blown hitchhikers.

Thanks again for the insight — it helps me feel a little less freaked out and a bit more strategic.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-04-30T16:00:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-ticks-ass-crack-horror-%E2%80%94-morning-sunlight-hijacked-by-nature%E2%80%99s-tiny-monsters.30793/post-351549>

### BiohackersTO — 2025-05-21T22:51:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-ticks-ass-crack-horror-%E2%80%94-morning-sunlight-hijacked-by-nature%E2%80%99s-tiny-monsters.30793/post-353087>

I found a blood sucking tick on me for the first time after a hike two days ago. It definitely was not on me for 8 hours (let alone 24) but I did freak out because Lyme disease is quite active here and once we identified the variety, it was a deer tick, the kind that can carry Lyme. I disinfected the area. It was a good warning and a good motivator to get the eff out of Canada.

### JulieBlueSky — 2025-05-22T09:32:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-ticks-ass-crack-horror-%E2%80%94-morning-sunlight-hijacked-by-nature%E2%80%99s-tiny-monsters.30793/post-353108>

Lovely sunrise and views, can see why you picked that location. Lovely deck there too Jack. Ours gets sun from about 7.30 to 7.30 in the UK, IF the sun is out.

Have to go out front for Sunrise though. Best investment we ever made. We also had a covered section so we can sit sheltered from the rain and for the first 5 years I said to hubbie I want sides put on it, it's too cold. Having found your wisdom, really glad never got around to doing that and closing it in!! Patch of lawn for grounding too, morning tea sat in Sun with barefeet.

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-05-22T15:37:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-ticks-ass-crack-horror-%E2%80%94-morning-sunlight-hijacked-by-nature%E2%80%99s-tiny-monsters.30793/post-353116>

We have spent over 4 years building our new home, just a mile, or so, from our present one, but on 20 acres , separate from other homes, with many acres of forest behind, and our patio overlooks a 200 acre lake that faces East, affording a great sunrise each day, and less nnEMF. We move in next week. Never as grand as the view Jack has, nor as well situated geographically, but will have to do for now. 32.5130° N, 93.7515° W (Shreveport, LA)

### Stuart Briscoe — 2025-05-22T17:37:11+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-ticks-ass-crack-horror-%E2%80%94-morning-sunlight-hijacked-by-nature%E2%80%99s-tiny-monsters.30793/post-353125>

To naturally rid land of ticks, focus on habitat modification and natural repellents. Maintain a well-manicured yard by mowing grass and pruning shrubs, and create barriers like wood chips or gravel between lawns and wooded areas. Plant tick-repellent plants like [garlic](https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&cs=0&sca_esv=ad43a62fd8d4007c&q=garlic&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwj_nP3kpbeNAxV9QjABHf7JM6AQxccNegQIAxAB&mstk=AUtExfDQfLmSbckSC4SXN-r_xnTA-saWrnJ_FZY9dS7ZmFfC-c5HApQTmFxvrW0mq7CQnrVe7cQHOVIbFxlNPgVXGDcFK6Bg6PIpISlP4Np6S4P0r12nBmz4820Y9PKdUcuhB-U&csui=3), [sage](https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&cs=0&sca_esv=ad43a62fd8d4007c&q=sage&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwj_nP3kpbeNAxV9QjABHf7JM6AQxccNegQIAxAC&mstk=AUtExfDQfLmSbckSC4SXN-r_xnTA-saWrnJ_FZY9dS7ZmFfC-c5HApQTmFxvrW0mq7CQnrVe7cQHOVIbFxlNPgVXGDcFK6Bg6PIpISlP4Np6S4P0r12nBmz4820Y9PKdUcuhB-U&csui=3), [mint](https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&cs=0&sca_esv=ad43a62fd8d4007c&q=mint&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwj_nP3kpbeNAxV9QjABHf7JM6AQxccNegQIAxAD&mstk=AUtExfDQfLmSbckSC4SXN-r_xnTA-saWrnJ_FZY9dS7ZmFfC-c5HApQTmFxvrW0mq7CQnrVe7cQHOVIbFxlNPgVXGDcFK6Bg6PIpISlP4Np6S4P0r12nBmz4820Y9PKdUcuhB-U&csui=3), [lavender](https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&cs=0&sca_esv=ad43a62fd8d4007c&q=lavender&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwj_nP3kpbeNAxV9QjABHf7JM6AQxccNegQIAxAE&mstk=AUtExfDQfLmSbckSC4SXN-r_xnTA-saWrnJ_FZY9dS7ZmFfC-c5HApQTmFxvrW0mq7CQnrVe7cQHOVIbFxlNPgVXGDcFK6Bg6PIpISlP4Np6S4P0r12nBmz4820Y9PKdUcuhB-U&csui=3), and [marigolds](https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&cs=0&sca_esv=ad43a62fd8d4007c&q=marigolds&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwj_nP3kpbeNAxV9QjABHf7JM6AQxccNegQIAxAF&mstk=AUtExfDQfLmSbckSC4SXN-r_xnTA-saWrnJ_FZY9dS7ZmFfC-c5HApQTmFxvrW0mq7CQnrVe7cQHOVIbFxlNPgVXGDcFK6Bg6PIpISlP4Np6S4P0r12nBmz4820Y9PKdUcuhB-U&csui=3). Consider using natural repellents like [cedar oil](https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&cs=0&sca_esv=ad43a62fd8d4007c&q=cedar+oil&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwj_nP3kpbeNAxV9QjABHf7JM6AQxccNegQIChAB&mstk=AUtExfDQfLmSbckSC4SXN-r_xnTA-saWrnJ_FZY9dS7ZmFfC-c5HApQTmFxvrW0mq7CQnrVe7cQHOVIbFxlNPgVXGDcFK6Bg6PIpISlP4Np6S4P0r12nBmz4820Y9PKdUcuhB-U&csui=3), [eucalyptus oil](https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&cs=0&sca_esv=ad43a62fd8d4007c&q=eucalyptus+oil&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwj_nP3kpbeNAxV9QjABHf7JM6AQxccNegQIChAC&mstk=AUtExfDQfLmSbckSC4SXN-r_xnTA-saWrnJ_FZY9dS7ZmFfC-c5HApQTmFxvrW0mq7CQnrVe7cQHOVIbFxlNPgVXGDcFK6Bg6PIpISlP4Np6S4P0r12nBmz4820Y9PKdUcuhB-U&csui=3), or [diatomaceous earth](https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&cs=0&sca_esv=ad43a62fd8d4007c&q=diatomaceous+earth&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwj_nP3kpbeNAxV9QjABHf7JM6AQxccNegQIChAD&mstk=AUtExfDQfLmSbckSC4SXN-r_xnTA-saWrnJ_FZY9dS7ZmFfC-c5HApQTmFxvrW0mq7CQnrVe7cQHOVIbFxlNPgVXGDcFK6Bg6PIpISlP4Np6S4P0r12nBmz4820Y9PKdUcuhB-U&csui=3).

Elaboration:

1.  **1\. Habitat Modification:**
    Ticks prefer shady, humid areas with tall grass and brush. By keeping your lawn mowed, pruning shrubs, and removing debris, you create a less hospitable environment for ticks.

2.  **2\. Barriers:**
    A 3-foot wide barrier of wood chips or gravel can act as a physical barrier, making it difficult for ticks to move from wooded areas into your yard.

3.  **3\. Repellent Plants:**
    Planting tick-repellent plants like garlic, sage, mint, lavender, and marigolds can help deter ticks from entering your yard.

4.  **4\. Natural Repellents:**
    -   **[Cedar oil](https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&cs=0&sca_esv=ad43a62fd8d4007c&q=Cedar+oil&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwj_nP3kpbeNAxV9QjABHf7JM6AQxccNegQIExAB&mstk=AUtExfDQfLmSbckSC4SXN-r_xnTA-saWrnJ_FZY9dS7ZmFfC-c5HApQTmFxvrW0mq7CQnrVe7cQHOVIbFxlNPgVXGDcFK6Bg6PIpISlP4Np6S4P0r12nBmz4820Y9PKdUcuhB-U&csui=3):** Kills ticks by causing dehydration and disrupting their systems.

    -   **[Eucalyptus oil](https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&cs=0&sca_esv=ad43a62fd8d4007c&q=Eucalyptus+oil&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwj_nP3kpbeNAxV9QjABHf7JM6AQxccNegQIGxAB&mstk=AUtExfDQfLmSbckSC4SXN-r_xnTA-saWrnJ_FZY9dS7ZmFfC-c5HApQTmFxvrW0mq7CQnrVe7cQHOVIbFxlNPgVXGDcFK6Bg6PIpISlP4Np6S4P0r12nBmz4820Y9PKdUcuhB-U&csui=3):** Considered a strong tick repellent due to its scent and potential to disrupt tick activity.

    -   **[Diatomaceous earth](https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&cs=0&sca_esv=ad43a62fd8d4007c&q=Diatomaceous+earth&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwj_nP3kpbeNAxV9QjABHf7JM6AQxccNegQIMhAB&mstk=AUtExfDQfLmSbckSC4SXN-r_xnTA-saWrnJ_FZY9dS7ZmFfC-c5HApQTmFxvrW0mq7CQnrVe7cQHOVIbFxlNPgVXGDcFK6Bg6PIpISlP4Np6S4P0r12nBmz4820Y9PKdUcuhB-U&csui=3):** A natural powder that dehydrates ticks and can be sprinkled around your yard.

5.  **5\. Other Natural Methods:**

    -   **Salt:** Can kill tick larvae and eggs by dehydrating them.

    -   **[Neem oil](https://www.google.com/search?client=safari&cs=0&sca_esv=ad43a62fd8d4007c&q=Neem+oil&sa=X&ved=2ahUKEwj_nP3kpbeNAxV9QjABHf7JM6AQxccNegQIFxAB&mstk=AUtExfDQfLmSbckSC4SXN-r_xnTA-saWrnJ_FZY9dS7ZmFfC-c5HApQTmFxvrW0mq7CQnrVe7cQHOVIbFxlNPgVXGDcFK6Bg6PIpISlP4Np6S4P0r12nBmz4820Y9PKdUcuhB-U&csui=3):** Used as a natural insecticide and tick repellent.

    -   **Essential oils:** Many essential oils, like lemon, orange, cinnamon, and lavender, are thought to be unpleasant to ticks.

    ### [How to Get Rid of Ticks in Your Yard without Pesticides](https://mandmpestcontrol.com/how-to-get-rid-of-ticks-in-your-yard-without-pesticides/)

    Here are some natural and eco-friendly ways to keep your property tick-free this season without resorting to using chemicals.

     mandmpestcontrol.com

### markthesunbather — 2025-05-22T18:49:22+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/emf-ticks-ass-crack-horror-%E2%80%94-morning-sunlight-hijacked-by-nature%E2%80%99s-tiny-monsters.30793/post-353128>

I used to say -I'm not a chick magnet -I'm a tick magnet.

I was told I had chronic Lyme back in 2014. Kept chipping away it at for years; finally took something in 2021 that knocked it out completely, to the point where I'd say I was cured. One month later, I was standing in the grass with my shirt off, trying to get some sun, when I felt something and one bit me on my groin area. Had a rash and everything. I basically had one month of total relief and then BAM, back to square one it felt like. Only I got on top of it quick that second time....so not a big deal in the end. I'm good now I think.

They're not just a Midwest/northeast pestilence. We got 'em in Florida too.

### Chlo_e — 2025-04-30T19:05:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-351570>

Curious to hear what people on the forum think of the Telepathy Tapes docuseries?
I haven't seen much chat on here about it, thought there would be a lot more discussion.

On a related note, this interview is also fascinating, I've screenshotted what one of the Autistic kids has said below at minute 46.

### brianalfredeneuman — 2025-05-06T16:22:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352035>

I have certainly experienced telepathy. I am surprized it's even a question sometimes. I like to think that love is telepathic, which explains why things happen when you think of a friend or whatever and they call you out of the blue.

Maybe Uncle Jack would say it's quantum entanglement, which I never really understood the full meaning but seems totally accurate from what little I understand of his meaning.

I strongly believe some people are born with very different talents and purposes. Autistic children sometime have obvious 'specialties'. Perhaps they are excellent at Math or some other accepted skill but it makes sense that they might also have other unknow human abilities and have a focused version of that as well. I'm not autistic for the record.

Animals have senses before Earthquakes and other events that threaten their time. We probably have the same thing.

I don't believe much in the type of 'psychics' that people go to for advice but I do believe we all have some puzzle pieces that cross our path and with the correct pattern recognition, we can put together for our selves to benefit our life.

One time for example, I was once kept up all night by a type of anxiety. I kept going through my life and problems in my mind, but kept coming back to this repedative thought, "those are small problems."

That night instead of sleeping, I went to the beach stayed up all night doing some Thai Chi/Chi gong. The only thing that bothered me during my experience were the planes taking off from the airport that I could see taking off over the harbor, it interupted me and the flight path was right over my head. I had a weird symbol I noticed in the clouds going over the sliver of the moon (crecent moon). The symbol made a sort of dragon shape from a book I remembered from my childhood. At that moment I thought about a moon on the top of the minarets (Islam). At the same time I thought out of the blue about why we "America" will bomb the Middle East. Osama bin Laden popped into my head. I knew who he was as I followed world politics closely. Anyway I saw the sun rise and then went back to a Marina and saw this old man watching a little TV in his security booth with some guy staring in. It was the morning of 9/11 and the planes I had watched were two of the planes that left Boston that morning and collided with the World Trade Center. It took a long time to deal with that event for me, for so many reasons it's hard to explain. I had massive quantum entanglement you'd say, that kept on for years. Too many things to deny.

### eve001 — 2025-05-06T18:03:49+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352038>

I think it's really fascinating and I wonder how it 'works' and how it links to nnEMF poisoning and to embodiment.
Last week I came across some testimonies of people about severe autistic kids having trouble speaking.
Then they reduced EMFs and one young autistic kid made huge improvements with speaking.

In the series they mention experiments, showing that also when shielded from electromegnetic waves in a bunker still telepathy was possible...
Suggesting it must go through some other medium than EMFs. Scalar waves - of which I know even less?

I also recall them speaking of people receiving radio signals (songs and speech) with their teeth...
It makes me wonder how many poor people ended up in psychiatry for hearing voices, while simply having a dental issue and literally hearing radio voices.

Also, when reduction of nnEMFs improves autism - would that reduce their telepathic skills?
It reminds me of the Rick Rubin podcast where they speak about his surgery and methylene blue.

As I understand the idea: people get poisoned by EMFs and chemicals to the point of kids not embodying well anymore. The poor embodiment however seems to open them up to a supernatural realm. As if bc of incomplete embodiment some gate were kept open. Divinity leaks into our world and spreads and cancels the evil plans of the greedy and power-hungry...

I once knew a person who after a concussion said she got some psychic abilities, talking with the dead. Another man I knew said he received biographical personal info on total strangers he crossed in the city. He would then start talking to these stranger. I heard more anecdotes like this. The theosophical society in the 19th centruy was into clairvoyance and into science. Some thesosophical scientist looked into the structure of atoms with some sort of clairvoyance.

So I do not dismiss the possibility of things discussed on these telepathy tapes, it's very fascinating, but I cannot assess its authenticity.

### Chlo_e — 2025-05-13T20:35:05+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352444>

Fascinating to read your comments above.

I did pay to access their website videos (and to support the making of their documentary) and whilst I agree with Dr Powell that their footage wouldn't satisfy heavily sceptical scientists (especially where the parents are more involved) some of the footage is extremely convincing. I do not think facilitated communication nor the ideomotor effect can explain all of what is being shown and described. Even the choice of words from the Autists is completely unique, not to mention all of the circumferential discussions that take place in the talk tracks. I also read several books written by individuals featured in the series and it certainly helps feed in to a larger picture of what's unfolding.

There are now so many accounts (NDEs, OBEs, intuitive experiences/dreams, savant examples, genuine whistle blowers from military / intelligence / governmental organisations, SRA survivor accounts, interviews with staff at the department of perceptual studies (DOPS), and also other clues scattered throughout nature, archaeology. ancient teachings, literature and the arts) that it's hard to deny we are experiencing only a small proportion of reality, and perhaps of our true potential.

In circles where the focus is on the metaphysical/spiritual, it is said that we are supposed to have all of these abilities without loss of motor function or neurological injury. That we have been dumbed down and programmed to believe otherwise, and to actively suppress these abilities from developing through a variety of ways. I also have had experiences that science cannot explain, so I am inclined to take this seriously ..

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-14T02:16:25+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352454>

I grew up in a family where these things were considered normal. In a Christian church setting - ”words of knowledge”, in the Spiritualist setting clairvoyance and/or clairaudience. I know many people who experience telepathy - mind to mind communication - to a greater or lesser extent.

An old family friend could read the cards I was looking at. He was a consultant to Interpol in Europe to find people. He could tell them ahead of time if they were still alive or had died. Another friend would “visit“ us and would flash the lights to let us know he was there. The next day he could tell us what we were doing or watching.

This is not woo but science. I believe the biology behind these abilities involves the pineal gland which when “open” acts at a “crystal radio”. nnEMF and fluoride are known to calcify the pineal glad so it stops functioning in this way.

Another friend would “visit“ us and would flash the lights to let us know he was there. The next day he could tell us what we were doing or watching.
