Yes, this is what I am hearing more and more. There is also peer-reviewed literature (for what that's worth) showing higher melatonin levels in people who meditate. As well as all the chemical and electromagnetic stressors that would damage the pineal or deplete melatonin (photoreceptor damage), I am also pondering on the thought-based intellectual education system and the impact this has on pineal gland function, particularly if it is a quiet mind that primes the nervous system and allows access to these abilities and knowledge...

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-05-14T21:20:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352499>

I listened to the Telepathy Tapes twice. I truly believe we are only beginning to understand consciousness and the soul, and I am not convinced either reside in the body. If DNA has memory then when I think of Dr. Emoto's (sp?) and Luc Montagnier's work with water / DNA...and now telepathic thought, it changes my belief system. Not my faith, but my belief. There are bonus episodes including the cameraman (or producer, I forget) interview, who eventually was able to visit The Hill. I lost my husband young, to cancer. And I will tell you this...he appeared in dreams to three different unrelated people, each time wearing the same clothes, and doled out advice. One night he appeared to me, in voice only, telling me I was doing great raising our daughter, and not to listen to well-meaning family. Another time, he told me it was okay to downsize and sell our albatross of a home...in fact I could hear his voice say if you love me let it go...the memories carry with you, while I was raking leaves. This was 23 years ago. I'm serious. When he talked to me in my sleep, I actually sat up and thought I could grab him, touch him but only heard his voice. I had goosebumps. Way back, my mom died of cancer. I was 16 years old. I sat up in bed at 3:30am and said, she's gone; she had died at 3:15am at the hospital. My dad had not come home to tell us yet. I believe we can do what the kids do in the Telepathy Tapes, but it requires quieting the mind -- silencing your voice. The closest I have ever come is using my red light box behind my head and saying a rosary some nights. It takes 8 minutes for the box to quiet my thoughts --after that, it's like I can manifest things for my future, see things clearly that I want for myself or others, pray in earnest for others etc. When thinking about the Telepathy Tapes we have to remember, these kids, most of them, have no "feeling" of body. They have to be reminded they have limbs -- legs, arms etc. If you were free in your mind, unencumbered by your physical nature, I wonder what it would feel like. Even when I am lying awake on a bed, I am conscious of clothing, the feel of sheets, noises in the yard etc. These kids seem to eliminate all that -- and focus intently on one thing, or at least very few things. I've known people who mediate very deeply and claim to have out of body experiences and see things far away or talk to people in thought. I never really believed it...until the Telepathy Tapes. God bless.

One night he appeared to me, in voice only, telling me I was doing great raising our daughter, and not to listen to well-meaning family. Another time, he told me it was okay to downsize and sell our albatross of a home...in fact I could hear his voice say if you love me let it go...the memories carry with you, while I was raking leaves. This was 23 years ago. I'm serious. When he talked to me in my sleep, I actually sat up and thought I could grab him, touch him but only heard his voice. I had goosebumps. Way back, my mom died of cancer. I was 16 years old. I sat up in bed at 3:30am and said, she's gone; she had died at 3:15am at the hospital. My dad had not come home to tell us yet. I believe we can do what the kids do in the Telepathy Tapes, but it requires quieting the mind -- silencing your voice. The closest I have ever come is using my red light box behind my head and saying a rosary some nights. It takes 8 minutes for the box to quiet my thoughts --after that, it's like I can manifest things for my future, see things clearly that I want for myself or others, pray in earnest for others etc. When thinking about the Telepathy Tapes we have to remember, these kids, most of them, have no "feeling" of body. They have to be reminded they have limbs -- legs, arms etc. If you were free in your mind, unencumbered by your physical nature, I wonder what it would feel like. Even when I am lying awake on a bed, I am conscious of clothing, the feel of sheets, noises in the yard etc. These kids seem to eliminate all that -- and focus intently on one thing, or at least very few things. I've known people who mediate very deeply and claim to have out of body experiences and see things far away or talk to people in thought. I never really believed it...until the Telepathy Tapes. God bless

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-05-14T21:36:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352501>

> [pfedorow said:](/goto/post?id=352500)

Thanks for sending this -- I understand what you are saying -- one concern I had in Telepathy Tapes was the researcher family line - the doc whose name escapes me had a dad who worked for Battelle Systems or something like that -- a fed contractor. For me it was a red flag. That said, I truly believe the mind is capable of so much, when the noise is quieted, and removed.

### eve001 — 2025-05-16T22:54:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352668>

> [Mightygumshoe said:](/goto/post?id=352501)
>
> Thanks for sending this -- I understand what you are saying -- one concern I had in Telepathy Tapes was the researcher family line - the doc whose name escapes me had a dad who worked for Battelle Systems or something like that -- a fed contractor. For me it was a red flag. That said, I truly believe the mind is capable of so much, when the noise is quieted, and removed.

Interesting regarding the ELFs... These are in DE/EMI also. Other LF but les extreme LF are also said to impact humans a lot and Brian Hoyer speaks of this and how meters to measure these are hard to find.

It reminds me again of how Carl Jung didn't want light/electricity/radio at his lake house so that he could connect with other forces.

I am not convinced all humans were used to be able of these skills. Shamans, mystics etc.. enter into great discipline as I understood to aquire the powers and skills they have. But if the magnetic field of earth was much stronger then maybe metabolism and detox required less energy and maybe it was easier... also there were less toxins.

This being said it is remarkable that these people with severe autism developed these skills - since autism is so often linked to nnEMF exposure and to other toxins...

My reserve about the podcast is similar to my reserve towards Greta Thunberg being turned into this child icon of climate change activism --- young and vulnerable people --- it feels like not right to me to put them into such a role of heroes and saviors bringing the much needed change.

Of course the telepathy tapes incites sympathy and wonder and amazement and opens the mind to all that's possible... away from the old paradigm.

I do hope these spellers get to have the educational system changed so that they can participate with their gifts. But society/governments do not yet respect unjabbed/jab-injured/microwaved... And the unjabbed, jab-injured and microwaved are not even yet truly helpful to eachother either, so how do we imagine spellers can be of help? Their caregivers are probably exhausted and in need of help also.

But I have not watched the interview with Veda Austin from which you quoted [@Chlo\_e](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29446/)

In the world of healing and meditation there are these two currents: up and down.
Those who say to feel the feels and see the way through pain as the way past pain: they promote enhanced embodiment; the roots.
Then there are those who promote a detachment and abstraction from the body. Projecting ourselves in imagined realities to manifest their realization - generating feelings of excitement and gratefulness of things we want in life.

To me the turn towards a new paradigm away from newtonian mecahnistic thinking is exciting - but I feel like we have never really respected 'matter' and the materialism we see today is actually an idealism. Materialism in pop terms often stands for a love of brands andof the possession of goods. Respect for matter and for the wonder of the elements that the things in our perceptible universe are made of is rather rare. Attention for the structures and forms. How often do not people embrace quantum physics as a shortcut to reality-despising spirituality? It reminds so very much of how christianism has often despised nature, women, the body, matter, sexuality, the senses. Which is to me what vaccines, nanorobiots etc are also about: total disrespect for the wonder and beauty of the reality we are in and that we are more or less sure of.

There is now this recent idea that our reality is a simulation. Again I feel like it's from people unable to love our reality. I might be wrong but to me it sounds like total BS. What is it a simulation of? Of nothing? Then in what way is it a simulation etc... It sounds like a copy of the homunculus - before people thought there was this little man in our brain steering us. That was an explanation... The one interesting thing about it is how ot leads to the idea of fractality.

I kind of went in a tangent maybe - but it feels related.  I feel like most people have forgotten/un-learned to enjoy their embodiment. Myself incl. often. Bc of nnEMFs and co.

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-05-17T21:35:32+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-352763>

> [eve001 said:](/goto/post?id=352662)
>
> What kind of red light do you use?

Bestqool BQ60 from Amazon, got it on a Prime Day. I like the way they test them. I have a mat too, same brand, but foldables with LED/NIR not as strong. The “box” lets you turn on/off NIR/LED independently. I love my units. I pulled a muscle in neck and had ear infection. I put garlic oil in my ear and put the box right up to side of my neck and head, 2” away. 20 min every day for a week. Ear infection gone. At 2 weeks neck healed. But what it does to calm my mind is what blew me away. Takes just about 8 minutes. One night I did 60 min back of skull. Felt wonderful! Like I had been floating in a pool. I also use an earthing mat by Earthings. Love it too. My cats (sadly now have just one) love(d) them too, naturally going to them, and sleeping on them. Especially in winter when their fur is electric.

>

Materialism in pop terms often stands for a love of brands andof the possession of goods. >
> There is now this recent idea that our reality is a simulation. Again I feel like it's from people unable to love our reality. I might be wrong but to me it sounds like total BS. What is it a simulation of? Of nothing? Then in what way is it a simulation etc... It sounds like a copy of the homunculus - before people thought there was this little man in our brain steering us. That was an explanation... The one interesting thing about it is how ot leads to the idea of fractality.
>
> I kind of went in a tangent maybe - but it feels related.  I feel like most people have forgotten/un-learned to enjoy their embodiment. Myself incl. often. Bc of nnEMFs and co.

I've had lots of similar thought processes.

I believe many of us are searching for discernment without paranoia. We are reaching (already have reached?) a point where we can be quite easily deceived by information obtained through our 5 senses, particularly as AI advances. I suppose it's up to each individual what they do with the information presented in the Telepathy Tapes. In my view, this is something to be (re)learned and experienced, starting with a strong cultivation of intuition. No amount of descriptive words could capture the taste of a strawberry- it needs to be experienced.

Some say we are here to experience extremes and opposites. That we can learn the most and the fastest when under extreme discomfort and backed into a corner. The concept of us having such innate abilities depends on one's view of history, human origins and reality in general. I am also struck by how damaged the nervous system can be and yet, these individuals have seemingly tapped into other channels of reality. A recurring theme in my research is the degree to which we identify with the physical body- which as you mention- these kids/young adults need to be reminded that they have limbs. Perhaps this is an important factor. 'Be in the world but not of it' etc.

There are countless interviews with people who've had NDEs or OBEs who also say this is a 'simulation' of sorts- i.e not the totality of reality. However, many of them live their lives much more fully, engaged, present and without fear after these events- they expand, rather than shrink, after experiencing reality from a different perspective.

A final point that I find intriguing which overlaps with JK (this is not mentioned in the TTs but keeps cropping up in my readings and research) is that the amount of light the body can hold is also linked with these abilities- however there may be other inputs which increase this capacity beyond sunlight and environmental mitigation- still an area I am exploring further. In any case... what a time to be alive!

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-05-22T20:25:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-353143>

> [eve001 said:](/goto/post?id=352814)
>
> Great! Thanks! It's a panel, right? I was wondering if a box was different from a panel. I will try one on batteries then, as I am still pretty sensitive to EMFs.

If you buy a "soft" pad also sold by Bestqool or another brand, you will never get the level of power that you get from a "box" with LEDs -- I mean a hard box. I have a smaller unit that can be pointed at a small area on a face and it runs on batteries. Different brand. Seldom use. That said, NOTHING beats the box. The soft wrap is nice because you can roll it around a sore knee or put it on your lap/belly. But the "boxes" are really powerful. I love mine. I use it every other day, minimum. There are bigger options too. The reason I like Bestqool (again other brands probably offer the same) -- is that their hard box allows you to turn on/off LED and near infrared independently (their pads do not) but I like the option of just using the red light vs both together. Also, I appreciate the way they test their devices. Hooga or something like that, cannot remember the name, also sells boxes. When Amazon Prime days come around you can get them on a discount. My BQ60 was marked "way" down. Best purchase ever. On "full power" if the pad we have is put behind a skull/neck it does the same relaxation thing as the box, but it takes longer and has to be put directly on skin to see the benefit.The box, I can set about 4-6" from the back of my head and in 8 minutes on average, I am in a very special "chill out" as I call it. I have done it for an hour at a time on occasion, and feel like I slept for three hours -- that relaxed. Before you buy anything, look at Amazon reviews. And research. I have a family member extremely affected by EMF, cell phones etc (body rash, skull pain, anxiety, headaches etc....and more) and there has never been a problem using the LED/Near Infrared box. I own both these, Redot and BQ60.

### Mightygumshoe — 2025-05-23T17:53:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what-do-people-think-of-the-telepathy-tapes.30796/post-353216>

> [eve001 said:](/goto/post?id=353191)
>
> Thanks so much for sharing the details! It is very interesting for me to know it is so helpful for you. I may try it at some point and will keep your recommendations. My concern is that we have lots of dirty electricity (DE) here - it seems to affect me a lot, (esp. on sunny days) which is why I am mindful of using plugged-in devices. I do have an IR/red incandescent 300Watt lamp and don't use it often. All houses/neighborhoods are different so maybe DE is not an issue for others bc there is little of it or bc they are less affected bc of better redox etc.

1) I have a family member VERY affected by non-native EMF. Checks almost every box. Skull pain with microwave running (if behind it in another room because not protected with faraday cage),rashes, pain in head if holds a cell phone nearby, leg pain with laptop on lap; I purchased faraday fabric and want to sew a nice work jacket for protection for this person; This is one of the main reasons why I became a gold member and am hoping to steer her to that as well -- a family membership.

2) Red light has helped a lot for anxiety, sleep...but is not all we did. We also:
\- Eat organic grass fed meat/good shrimp etc, organic veggies.
3) This AM put BQ60 red light box on myself this morning. 20 minutes. 10 upper body, 10 minutes holding it to my face and skull top about 6" away; and maybe added another 5 for a lower arm (radius / ulna- I pulled up big pieces of grass this week).
\- I get such peace afterwards. I do not know much about red light except what I read in like 40 papers (data miner) and this was almost 2 years ago, ditto grounding, which I do -- and funny thing, the cat(s) do too. These items are not cheap, but do some reading; and check Amazon for Prime days. Your health is your wealth. Mine was marked down, way down -- like to $110 or $120 or so I grabbed it.
\- The earthings mat I ordered comes with 2. They also sent me instructions for testing e-field etc...see below.
\- Do some research. Bestqool and others make pads some that you can interconnect if you later buy a second one (like a mattress set up - to make it longer).

As for dirty electricity,

1) I had an antique lamp next to my bed -- 100 yr old or close to it. Not grounded, metal frame, common in the day.
2) 40 volt E field off of it (used voltmeter on AC with one plug in a ground port of outlet, and other in my fingertips -- thumb/forefinger of one hand, as I moved around illuminated fixtures etc). YIKES.
3) To resolve, I got rid of almost all crap in my room. Also, the method I mention to measure, is a great way to be sure your grounding mat works.

PROCEDURE:
1) Plug the voltmeter into the same outlet the grounding mat is plugged into (the ground plug);
2) and touch the pad with a free hand,
3) Hold the other cable end of the voltmeter cord in the other hand's thumb/forefinger finger tips.
4) Be sure meter is set to AC volts.
RESULT: should go to zero on your multimeter/voltmeter if you touch your pad and it works (is grounded).

I do use an old style alarm clock/radio. But I have a wind up one (no kidding) am thinking of using instead.

We cut internet at night, use a grounding pad on bed, lights off, blue light blocking etc, morning sun. I sleep like a BABY. I am 62, and have not felt this good since I was in my early 20s. My skin looks so good now. A neighbor even asked what I was doing -- and I pointed up. For the record, I love frankincense oil too, mixed with castor or fractionated coconut. And now at end of shower I turn it to cold and try to stand there for however long I can stand. Not quite ready for thermogenesis... we have all but ceased using the microwave. No more unstructured water for us.....

Have you ever measured the field (diff than EMF) around your rooms at night / during the day near extension cord outlets, lights when on / off, table lamps, etc. Consider what I mentioned above \[numbered 1-5\] with voltmeter (there are YT videos out there);

Red light helped me relax but getting rid of E field devices near my bedside made a huge difference! Some places in my room I picked up 10 volts AC, other places like lamp 20 to 40; and we found some dead spots in our living room which explains my favorite reading chair where I always say I feel so relaxed.... God bless.

edited to put bullets on recommendation .

The discovery of hemoglobin in hair follicle keratinocytes suggests a potential specialized function in these cells. Hb alpha emits biophotons. Heme in the +3 state makes your hair fall out. You live in a +3 world. When you do you go bald because +3 state = your hair falls out due to the poor UPE spectra. Hair follicles utilize the Tricarboxylic Acid (TCA) cycle for energy production to keep you healthy and flush. When you go to Warburg, you go bald = topological effect of blue and nnEMF. Sunlight is best not a laser.

### [Keratinocytes of the Upper Epidermis and Isthmus of Hair Follicles Express Hemoglobin mRNA and Protein - PubMed](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/37981423/)

# Thread: Dramatic decline/seeking connection
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dramatic-decline-seeking-connection.30803/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 15
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### samantha_jordan — 2025-05-01T17:42:07+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dramatic-decline-seeking-connection.30803/post-351640>

I humbly submit myself to this community as someone deeply flawed who is ready to face reality and heal myself. I am looking for advice from the only people I can trust.
I've made some horrible mistakes, and I'm paying a steep price now.

Mistakes: I discovered Dr. Kruse's work in October, but it did not create dramatic enough changes. After a great reduction of symptoms, I have now had a steep decline.

Current symptoms: massive anxiety and inability to sleep, heart palpitations, mild hypoxia symptoms returned.

I stupidly believed that the changes I made were enough, because after implementing, I had relief of hypoxia symptoms, good sleep, hair regrowth all over my head, and disappearance of chronic nausea/vomiting/stomach pain.

Confessions of wrong:

\* did not immediately replace blue light blocking glasses after puppy destroyed them. Engaged in blue light addiction, an average of 4 hours per day, for the last few months. Receiving a new pair tomorrow.

\* Thought waking up at 730 after daylight saving was good enough for about a month, and started noticing anxiety/sleep symptoms creeping in. Have corrected this.

\* I did not immediately order my passport to leave this area (rural southern Oregon). I thought the positive changes were evidence that I could heal here.

\* did not mitigate the current environment EMF

I'm here to be honest. I've engaged in toxic positivity that I now see was delusional. I was afraid that if I acknowledged my illness after receiving mitigation of symptoms, it would perpetuate it. Instead of accepting my severe illness, I engaged in delusional thinking to manifest my way out of it. Realize now that I can not imagine I'm not sick when I already am. Believing I'm healthy is delusional and will not change my reality.

Today I will order my expedited passport to travel to El Salvador for my best chance at healing.

Is there anyone out there who can talk to me through this process? I know I will continue to make the changes alone, but I crave a community to bolster me through this. My mind is fighting darkness with every fiber of my being, trying not to drown in shame and panic. I desire words of hope and encouragement that I'm on the right track.

Where can I safely get nude in the sun in El Salvador besides just finding some yard to stay in? Is there any communities engaging in this I can join as I want to be as smart about physical safety for myself and my daughter as possible.

Is there anyone who has children in El Salvador who would be willing to offer time to me and my 3-year-old daughter, whom I'm bringing with me? My daughter is extremely intelligent, active, and joyful. Any references on the community for my daughter and me would be greatly appreciated. I desire to be with others who understand these truths. Obviously, I'm currently ill, but I can contribute money, hard work, cooking, gardening skills, and a passion for wellness. I've been described as a healer all my life and a person of light by many others. Even though my light is dimmed, it will return, and I can contribute even more.

Thank you for reading and letting me know I'm not alone in my desire for true wellness. Thank you, Dr. Kruse, for your incredible work and this forum. Any advice or connection is deeply appreciated.

I can relate to trying to think my way or rather manifest my way out of my health problems.

And while I haven't given up my meditation practice (ever hear of Dr Joe Dispensa?), I've decided that I'm taking the matter - bull by the horns at the same time that I'm working on changing my reality via meditation, manifestation, etc.

So yeah, you're not alone. I can't help you with the practicalities of moving to ES, since I'm exactly in that newbie boat myself, but I'm sure you'll find plenty of help from others here. Cheers!

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-01T19:38:52+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dramatic-decline-seeking-connection.30803/post-351660>

You are not down in a crevice. You've just lost some temporary control of your agency.

Some wear a crown of filth; in apathy, they drown, and the end is permanent. These ideas were codified in my past by a friend. It reflects the older version of man who erred, so the crown of filth symbolizes self-loathing and apathy. You need that pain to regenerate. It also imbues you with the wisdom that time is relative. Where you were then is not where you are now. This ties to a state of mitochondrial decline due to neglecting Nature’s laws; vitality dims in a misaligned life.

But you've woken up..........

This is your Lazarus moment.

Your age of wisdom now reflects well, because you're owning your self-inflicted scars before the final dawn. Embrace the suck because it captures your new reflective tone, because you're owning up to a lifetime of mistakes as you face mortality in your solitude of thought. This is the profound lesson for the mitochondriacs to self-correct. This is what pain teaches us.

You might see yourself as a fragile flame, in Nature’s grace, but I see you reborn, and not yet gone. Because you put Windex on your dirty eyes, I can see a hopeful ending, because even in your decline, you still have enough moxy to re-align with Nature to rekindle your vitality. This is a call for the tribe to find redemption. This lesson is beautiful and not often crafted on these forums. Many more need to reflect as you have here. You have used your journal wisely.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-01T19:43:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/dramatic-decline-seeking-connection.30803/post-351661>

**Starfire Resurrected: Nature's Provides Us an Eternal Dawn**

We are stardust, forged in Nature’s celestial forge,
A symphony of light where vibrations surge and soar.
As above, so below, through galaxies’ breath we rise,
Mitochondria blaze, a cosmic fire in Earth’s guise.
Once we wore a crown of filth, in apathy we sank,
Vitality dimmed, mitochondria starved, a life so blank—
Self-loathing chained us, neglecting Nature’s sacred law,
A misaligned existence, where time’s relativity we saw.
But behold, a Lazarus moment, from the abyss we wake,
Pain, our crucible, regenerates what apathy did break—
Wisdom dawns, for where we were is not where we stand,
Scars self-inflicted, now owned, in solitude’s grand expand.
Embrace the suck, mitochondriacs, for pain’s lesson is clear,
Reflect, correct, before the final dawn draws near
No fragile flame are you, but a phoenix, reborn, alive,
Windex is on your eyes, you see, and in Nature’s grace, you thrive.
Angels on the sidelines, once puzzled by our fall,
Now marvel at our rise, a tribe answering Nature’s call
Eden’s abundance flows, no longer split, right in two,
We unite in Pneuma’s breath, her laws we now pursue.
Defy her no more, or mitochondria will cease to roar,
Health will wither, a plague of your own making will soar
Align swiftly, for Nature’s wrath is a primal, endless tide,
Her rivers run with blood, where the unaligned reside.
We are will and wonder, in her majestic embrace we glow,
Bound to recall, remember, her eternal currents flow
Born of one breath, one word, in oceans’ hymn we mend,
We are one spark, sun ascending, a radiance without end.
Through the mind’s vast cosmos, with mountains’ might as guide,
Infinite vitality surges, in Nature’s dawn we ride
No longer repugnant, we lift our eyes to heaven’s throne,
Reborn in Moxy’s fire, our mitochondrial starfire shown.

# Thread: Ilma's Optimal Journal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 116
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-01T18:27:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-351648>

This is where I currently live:

{ "lightbox\_close": "Close", "lightbox\_next": "Next", "lightbox\_previous": "Previous", "lightbox\_error": "The requested content cannot be loaded. Please try again later.", "lightbox\_start\_slideshow": "Start slideshow", "lightbox\_stop\_slideshow": "Stop slideshow", "lightbox\_full\_screen": "Full screen", "lightbox\_thumbnails": "Thumbnails", "lightbox\_download": "Download", "lightbox\_share": "Share", "lightbox\_zoom": "Zoom", "lightbox\_new\_window": "New window", "lightbox\_toggle\_sidebar": "Toggle sidebar" }

That was a rare morning maybe a month ago when you could actually see the sun rising (a good hour and change after it actually rose) behind the mountains where I live.

Needless to say, I've got health issues. To be fair though, my problems didn't start as a consequence of my moving here 21 years ago. I'm sure they started in Florida (where I was born and grew up) because of the night-owl/blue-lit lifestyle I led while down there. I actively avoided the Sun, and never had a very good tan.

My prolonged schooling (k through grad school) ensured that I got plenty of artificial light during the day, and then after graduation my work in hospitals and clinics finished the job.

On top of that, I routinely used to stay up till past midnight, and slept as late into to the day as I could to try to compensate for it. My sleep was craptastic though, and no surprise.

And let's not forget about the screen addiction, which was exacerbated by my husband's suffering a TBI in January of 2017. Before that I only ever had a flip phone and couldn't see what the big deal was with smartphones.

Unfortunately after his accident, my need to feel available to his providers night and day throughout the eight month ordeal that followed ensured that I too got hooked on the damned phone.

My husband survived that TBI, but was left with significant limitations and a serious seizure disorder. He has had two life threatening dances with status epilepticus, the first being in mid 2019, and the second in 2023. We never know when the next one might be, and the frequency of his seizures has increased in the last 9 months.

I developed AFib in late 2019, and it's only been getting worse. I also have osteoporosis and a list of other issues which I'll get into at another time.

For now the AFib is my main concern, with the osteoporosis a close second.

At any rate, after reading Dr Jack's book and inhaling everything I could about the mitochondriac lifestyle, I mended my ways and started changing my life on February 20th of this year. I can't tell you why but that was the day I decided to start getting up to watch the sunrise. And I haven't missed one since.

Had my first consult with Uncle Jack earlier this week and it's decided: I'm moving to ES for next winter, with or without my husband. After speaking to Dr K, my husband was interested in the idea, and I believe it would do great things to improve his condition, but now he says he won't go. I'll need to find someone who can care for him here at home or else he will have to go into a facility for the 6 months that I'm going to be away. I don't know how I'm going to pull it off, but I will.

Thanks for reading peeps, it's at least 50° now so I'm going to take off my clothes and go in the sun. Honk your horn as you're coming up the driveway unless you want to get an eyeful.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-01T18:29:18+0300

Ha! Thanks.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-03T12:45:48+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-351823>

My sunrise this morning. I'd like to take a moment to give a shout out to the brief opening between the clouds which allowed me to get a glimpse of some golden pink sky. Much appreciated, clouds.

But at least it's 50° out, so I'm sitting outside with my bare feet on the cobblestoned patio behind my house, trying to get as much reflected rays as I can. I am shivering under my hoodie and PJ's though. I read somewhere that if you stay outside in the cold for at least 45 minutes that it's equivalent to a 3 minute dip in cold water.

I was able to get some sun on Thursday when I started this journal, too much as it turns out. I'm rather crispy now. But it felt really good to lay in the Sun. I just have no experience with how much is too much. Kind of an odd thing for a native Floridian to say, but there you have it.

I was happy that my husband joined me for naked sunbathing outside on Thursday. The funny thing is that he gets pink fairly quickly and that tells him it's enough, but I had zero indication that I was overdoing it. I kept checking my skin and it still looked fine. The redness didn't seem to kick in until HOURS later. And the discomfort didn't actually hit until last night, when I felt really itchy and tender in the red areas. I don't know if I'm going to peel, it doesn't look that bad, but it feels pretty bad. Ah well, live and learn.

Speaking of my husband, I think he's turned a corner and now says he WILL come to El Salvador with me in September. The current plan is for me to come down for week or two to do a scout sometime this summer. So we're going to purchase a "Visit El Salvador" tourist guide and start learning about the areas.

My proclivity is to be near the beach, but coming from a part of the world that experienced a minus 30 winter which only just ended, I think we might do better if we're at a higher altitude where it's cooler. It'll help a little bit in that we're leaving here after the summer, but I don't think that's going to be enough to acclimate us completely.

I've lived in a third world country (Paraguay), so I'm mentally prepared for the heat, but having lived up here in the Frozen North for 20 plus years, I'm also prepared for the reality that I'm no longer used to it.

Another advantage of having lived there is that I know how crazy the driving is in South America. I'm assuming that drivers in ES have the same talent for carving out five lanes of traffic in a two lane road as do the Paraguayan drivers. So tell me folks, how many of you have your own vehicle versus just getting an Uber when you need to go somewhere? Would it be feasible to rent a vehicle for 6 months? Maybe I should drive down. Do you see RVs on the road there?

Any input or suggestions would be much appreciated. Have a great day all. I'm starting to lose feeling in the tips of my fingers, so I think I'm going to go inside now.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-04T14:02:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-351875>

Aaaaand it's raining this morning. But at least it's not freezing out. I'm guessing it's in the 50s right now. My sliding door is open to let whatever rays are out there come inside.

I've been "AFibbing" for more than 24 hours now. The episode started day before yesterday at around 6:00 p.m. it's in the mild to moderate discomfort range, but still. I think I'm going to start using this journal to track the duration and intensity of the episodes compared to the amount of sun exposure I get. I've previously tracked it relation to what foods I ate, how much sleep I got, etc. But never in relation to how much sun I got, so this should be interesting.

I tend to go all in with things, and my current thing is El Salvador. I've been researching about the country, living expenses, geography, etc. And truth be told, I spent the day of the evening my afib kicked in watching ES YouTube videos on the Roku TV. I've never been much into TV. I feel all gross after watching TV for an extended period and the other day was no different.

So my quandary is how to both protect myself from nnEMF and also research because I've got a big move ahead of me.

> Any input or suggestions would be much appreciated. Have a great day all. I'm starting to lose feeling in the tips of my fingers, so I think I'm going to go inside now.

Hello, the driving in ES is really manageable and civil. There are some very slow trucks and vehicles and buses, but not the aggressive driving that you describe that seems much more common in other Latin American countries. I definitely wouldn’t drive your own vehicle down here - a friend drove her car here from Panama and it was extremely cumbersome at each border (both exit and entry border). ES is extremely safe, but your route down here would have some dangers. Renting a car cheaply and long term is easy here. Good luck!

ES is extremely safe, but your route down here would have some dangers. Renting a car cheaply and long term is easy here. Good luck!

Hi Kirsten, thanks for your input. I see that you're an expat from Canada. I'm assuming there's some kind of driver's license required to drive in ES. Did you get an international driver's license in Canada before you left or were there some hoops to jump through once you got to El Salvador to get a proper Salvadoran driver's license?

### harezlak19 — 2025-05-05T04:12:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-351914>

On both my last trips, I have taken an international license with me but no one ever asked or looked at it when I booked my rental car. Probably good to have in case there is an accident just to be covered liability wise.

### Kirsten F — 2025-05-05T15:09:37+0300

Did you get an international driver's license in Canada before you left or were there some hoops to jump through once you got to El Salvador to get a proper Salvadoran driver's license?

You would need to double check, but I believe your Canadian license is valid for 90 days after you enter ES and the clock would reset each time you leave and reenter. You can get a Salvadoran license, without a test and just based on your Canadian license, but that is only available to official residents of El Salvador.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-05T19:55:50+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-351959>

My afib finally broke at 4:00 p.m. yesterday, so this episode was 46 hours long. Ugh.

At least I slept well last night. Amazing what getting up early and catching the sunrise will do for you huh.

I need to stop binge-watching El Salvador YT videos and actually DO something.

Today's plan: go sit outside and start an action list.

First item on list will be to set up a new consult with Dr. K to address DH's medical needs while in ES.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-06T16:37:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-352036>

Ok, I've made my list and scheduled a new consult.

Yesterday we ate breakfast and dinner outside between rain showers in addition to morning sunrise.

That said, from what I read yesterday I need to spend more time in the morning and evening in the sun. 10 minutes ain't going to cut it, especially in this latitude, duh.

So this AM, DH and I went outside and grounded for the first 10 minutes under an umbrella in the cold 50°F rain. And then I saw lightning. So DH took off his winter beanie and I took off my top and we both did obeisance to the sun with our skin for a half hour...next to a wide open sliding glass door but inside the house.

But I think that "counts", yes? To be next to an open window or door?

Of note, it doesn't take me long at all to start shivering outside. 24 years living here and my cold tolerance is still very low. I've been trying to build up my tolerance with cold showers but it's slow going. My legs will literally start shivering within a minute. And the water isn't even THAT cold. It's about 54° being as it comes out of a well deep down into the ground.

Anyway, after paying homage to the Sun, we had something to eat, and then I came back to bed. I had had another AFib episode start last night around 7PM, and it kept me up half the night so I was wiped out. I wasn't able to fall asleep but something interesting happened: my heart went back into rhythm! So this episode only lasted 12 hours. A significant improvement over the usual 36+ hours. I'm going to give the Sun credit for this one. Thank you, Sun.

(For documentation, the afib-free time between events was about 25 hours.)

Plan for the rest of the day is cross off more items on my to-do list and catch more rays between rain showers.

And clean the house. We're entertaining a couple that is interested in taking over our business. That's a huge hurdle between us and ES right now, is the business. It'll be a relief to pass it on to the next caretakers so we can be properly retired.

Enjoy your day, peeps!

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-07T13:14:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-352091>

Up at 4:15 AM and meditated. Caught the sunrise during a .5 mile long walk with DH. It's still raining, so I'm sitting next to the open slider, skin in the game

Woke up with mild AFib, so I'm hopeful that I'll get a repeat of yesterday and that my heart will go back into coherence in short order.

Time between episodes: maybe 21 hours.

Business meeting went well last night. I'm hopeful that we'll be able to negotiate a smooth transition.

Crossed a few more items off my ES to-do list, so that's all good. Things are falling into place.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-08T13:53:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-352150>

Poor sleep; AFib kept me up. This episode is going on more than 24 hours. Finally got out of bed at 4:15 a.m., meditated for an hour, then sat outside with DH to catch the morning sun behind the thick clouds that have been around for about a week. Had some breakfast and I think I'm going to go back to bed now. I'm exhausted.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-09T13:37:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-352192>

Sitting outside in 45° temps, topless underneath a winter house coat which I have opened to catch whatever rays are available behind these thick clouds. I'm pretty cold, but it's what I gotta do right, so I'm doing it. Every so often I'll catch a glimpse of blue skies and pink tinged high clouds beyond the heavy low-laying gray clouds which have been around for about a week. It's been raining most of that time so at least I'm thankful that I can sit outside today.

It occurs to me that that description is a good metaphor for my immediate future. I'm living under the gray clouds, but there are blue skies beyond. I just have to do what I have to do to get to the blue skies in ES.

I didn't have much energy yesterday. My afib didn't break until close to 5:00 p.m., a 37-hour marathon this time, but I did do some research on plane tickets and other things related to moving to ES for the winter.

I've been using the daylight computer as much as possible instead of the phone, which unfortunately is on hotspot much of the day because there's physically no way to plug in at this house where I live in the country in the mountains. When we bought this house seven years ago, The neighbors all complained of the poor quality and high cost of their internet connection. I tried getting a landline for the phone at least, but it was literally down half the time. It seems that the infrastructure has been neglected since everyone else has cell phones, so after about a year I canceled that account and have been using the cell phone as our main phone and business number since. I purchased a plan with ample data so that we use the hotspot to get online.

I'm going to purchase my own meter I think. I'd rather get a building biologist to come out and do some readings but apparently there are no building biologists in New Hampshire. The closest one I've been able to find is in Massachusetts, about 3 hours away.

Plan for the day is to continue to cross off items on my list, deal with some work things, and have a productive day.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-10T13:26:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-352258>

It's cloudy this morning, again. And drizzling. But at least it's warmer than it was yesterday. It feels like it's in the 50s. Until the wind kicks up, and then it feels like it's in the mid 40s.

So here I am sitting outside facing what I know to be East in bare-chested defiance of the modern world.

It's beautiful here though. The trees are now starting to leaf out and the hills and mountains are speckled with blotches of color from light greens rusty reds and everything in between. It's like a soft pastel version of Fall foliage. Spring lasts about a week or two here. And then winter and summer duke it out for domination. It's supposed to be sunny and in the 80s next week, but we can have snow into June.

Man, this wind is cold. I'm grateful to the voice typing feature on this tablet as it keeps my hands tucked into my sleeves.

Good thing about the wind though is that it keeps the black flies away. Between mother's Day and Father's Day swarms of the little blood sucking bastards do their best to get a year's worth of eating into a month so they can complete their cycle. They stick around all summer mind, but for some reason that month between those two days are really intense. Then, we actually pray for a frost as that seems to knock them off. Mosquitoes don't like me so much, but black flies do. I won't miss them for one moment.

Made a good connection yesterday which will help me transition to life in ES, but also received bad news. The couple that I was hopeful would be interested in taking over the business have decided to not take it on. So I think my only other option is to liquidate it. Have a retirement sale, sell off the stock, and be done with it...and buy BTC with the proceeds.

I think that's a better retirement plan in the long run.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-25T12:49:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-353294>

Thick clouds this morning. Again. I can't even see the mountain right behind me even though I'm on its shoulders. I think I've seen the sun twice maybe, since my last entry. Ah, well. My view will change soon.

Lots of movement forward in terms of our stay in ES. My scouting trip is now planned for the first week in July; tickets are purchased, stay and transportation are both arranged, and now the "insides" need to be figured out.

The insides being the work of the business. I need to meet with providers for DH, get an understanding of the local healthcare system / hospitals and how they work, decide on long-term lodging to start in the fall, figure out where I'm going to get my raw milk etc.

I'm very grateful to have help from [@Laudy Cincotta](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/24082/) and [@Jeremyfranco](https://forum.jackkruse.com/members/29635/) in figuring out all of the above. THANK YOU.

Another news, DH had a surgical procedure two weeks ago which will hopefully decrease the amount of his seizure activity. He has healed well from the surgery. It was a rough ride for him at first poor guy. He refused all pain killers and was feeling it for a bit, but now he says he's much better. The procedure involved the placement of a device which will send pulses of stimulation to his vagus nerve. Kind of like a pacemaker for the brain if you will. I hope that once the device is turned on next week that his neurologist will be willing to consider decreasing some of the medications that were increased in tandem with the increasing seizure activity he had been experiencing. His gait is really affected by all the pillage.

I think I need to wrap this up for now. It's 40°, feels like 36 degrees according to the weather app, damp, cloudy, drizzly and I'm starting to lose feeling in my hands and feet. I'm going to go inside and thaw out next to the wood stove.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-05-25T20:08:39+0300

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-28T12:36:57+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-353450>

Another cold morning here in the Great North Woods. 39° F and I'm sitting outside in my birthday suit, though not completely. I'm cheating in so far as I've also got on a winter house robe over my arms and protecting me from this cold patio chair.

Later today, I'm having a business meeting with yet another group of people who are interested in taking over the company. I think that if this one doesn't pan out I'm just going to have to liquidate and be done with it. I'd like to be retired.

Yesterday was an interesting day. It was warm, relatively speaking, in the a.m. and I stayed out here at daybreak for a good 30 minutes before going inside for a nap. The AFib had kept me up for part of the night and I was very tired. So I went back to bed for maybe an hour and a half ish. I got up at 7:00, made breakfast, and brought it outside here to eat. The sun was very strong by then, and I'm not used to it at all. So I didn't want to get crispy and I was mindful that I have no feedback from my skin when I'm overdoing it, which meant that the only way to prevent sunburn was by timing myself. So that's what I did. 15 minutes on each side more or less. And the afib broke. Sunshine for the win!

The rest of the day was spent outside doing gardening work though with frequent breaks to escape the swarms of blood sucking black flies which are trying to make up for the past two weeks of rain we've had. I ended up getting bit three times, which wasn't that bad. I use a suction device over the bite to draw out the venom so that it stops itching and doesn't become an angry welt. If I don't use that device, the welts swell up to about the size of an American quarter and they itch for a good week and a half.

Today's plan: go to the warehouse and get the orders out after breakfast in time to take DH to therapy appointment late morning, then back here to prep for the business meeting. It's going to be a busy day.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-06-06T12:46:45+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-354083>

June 6th, 2025.

We had two days of hot ( for us ) temps and now it looks like another week of rain. But at least I hope that temperatures in the 30s are behind us for a few months. We might still get a frost, but the chances of that are now significantly lower. The other good news is that the numbers of blood sucking black flies will soon decrease. They're always the most intense between Mother's Day and Father's Day.

In other news, things are moving forward regarding our relocation to ES in the fall. There's a neurologist with whom I will meet when I go down for my scout in less than a month now. I hope that he has the equipment necessary to monitor the vagus nerve stimulation device (VNS) that DH recently had implanted.

DH underwent a minor surgical procedure 3 weeks ago to have it placed, and we went down to Dartmouth last Friday for his current neurologist to turn the device on. I was told at that time that DH would need to come in every 2 months for the next 6 months to make adjustments. I was able to procure appointments for July and September, but then I got a phone call that the MD was double booked and we needed to move the September appointment into October. So if the neurologist in El Salvador does not have the equipment to make those adjustments, we will have to delay our trip by a week or two and possibly have to come back up here every 2 months to have those adjustments made. It's not clear to me if this is an initial 6 month adjustment period if this is how it's going to be.

I have family in Florida, though. It just occurred to me that I might be able to find a neurologist in the Miami area who could make those adjustments. A flight from ES to Miami is preferable than a flight from ES to up here in December in every way.

The VNS seems to be making DH very very tired though. He's been sleeping on average 18 hours a day since it was turned on. I suppose that if the point is to stimulate his vagus nerve into telling his brain to relax that it's doing its job, and that sleeping is preferable to having a seizure, but I don't think that 18 hours of sleep a day is sustainable in the long term. If it doesn't seem to improve within another couple of weeks I'll call the neurologist and see what he says.

Not that he's very happy with me mind. We seem to have gotten into a pattern lately him and me. I ask him a question that challenges his worldview. He gets very ruffled and upset and tells me that the point I'm trying to make is either crazy or else that I'm asking for something unreasonable. I stand my ground in a very gentle but firm manner, and he gets very quiet and focused on his EMR. I can see his wheels slogging through mud. We then move on to the business of the meeting, and by the time it's time to wrap up he concedes as far as he can and gives me the green light to do what I wanted to do in the first place, with his own tweaks and warnings.

He's an interesting character that guy. Very proper and old school but also I think very insecure. We don't have many choices up here unfortunately. As it is we travel 2 hours to get to Dartmouth to see him. I've tried to find other neurologists but they're all full and not taking new patients. So we do this dance in the meantime. I have not yet told him that we're moving to ES for the winter. THAT's going to be interesting conversation.

Shifting gears to our business: I found someone to take over while we're gone. It's a family of someones actually, so I hope that between them they'll be able to figure it out. There are many hurdles between them and success though, not least of which is the current "trade war" between the US and China. If it were possible to obtain the products that we sell from the US I'd be all over that. But they're simply not available and will not be due to the regulations here that prevent us from pissing in the pool that we drink from. The Chinese don't seem to mind urine water. So we hypocritically allow imports of products whose manufacture would never be allowed in the US. Actually, it's beyond "allow". We're DEPENDENT on products whose manufacturer would never be allowed in the US. Anyway, I hope they can make it work, but if they can't the plan is to liquidate the business when we get back in the spring next year and call it good. They're willing to take it on for which I'm very grateful.

And I think that's all I've got for right now. We're going to be driving down state for a family visit in a few hours, and I still haven't packed.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-06T15:42:27+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-354090>

You said, The VNS seems to be making DH very very tired though. He's been sleeping on average 18 hours a day since it was turned on. I suppose that if the point is to stimulate his vagus nerve into telling his brain to relax that it's doing its job, and that sleeping is preferable to having a seizure, but I don't think that 18 hours of sleep a day is sustainable in the long term. If it doesn't seem to improve within another couple of weeks I'll call the neurologist and see what he says. the blogs told you in the demyelination series that stimulating the VNS with a DC electric current would remyelinate a CNS that has short circuited itself due to light. The 18 sleep shows just how bad his redox is. He needs every bit of that 18 hours to remyelinate. As the myelin comes back he'll sleep less. This is how the body heals.

What is the story of myelin?

My photo-bioelectric take became a big story 425 million years ago with fishes with jaws. These are the same fish where opsin expansion was done. This is why the modern human brain is filled with melanopsin, encephalopsin, and neuropsin. This means myelin biology and its evolution are linked to a powerful light story mixed with oxygen. I shared that light story with Nick Jikomes in his pod.

The default state of life was sleep and we evolved wakefulness. Your seizing husband was sliding back to backward in time. His failing physiology could not support his CNS due to a lack of UV-IR and oxygen at the mtDNA level so he began to short circuit = seizures.

Myelin biology occurs post Cambrian explosion by about 200 million years after our G class star starts kicking out more UV light. Physics says it was between 10-20% for our star. So it took life about 200 million years to make use of that light to help sleep. As more Ultraweak biophotons could be created between mtDNA, heme proteins, and melanin this fueled the evolution of myelin so that we could reduce our need for sleep so that we could become more complex. Encephalization in mammals really stresses this situation.

Myelin has two major purposes. One is optimizing membrane function in the CNS and PNS. The second one is reducing the amount of time one needs to sleep tp explore the world. No one should deny this, but the second purpose is very murky for centralized biology and biophysics is how white matter links to UV light and oxygen use. Myelin innovation in my view comes out of the GOE. The GOE begins as an oxygen holocaust but then the later spike of UV that happens changes the biophysical mix.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-06T15:44:01+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-354091>

This paper is the first of its kind to begin to unravel what I have already unraveled in the demyelination blogs. [https://royalsocietypublishing.org/doi/full/10.1098/rsob.210177](https://royalsocietypublishing.org/doi/full/10.1098/rsob.210177)

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-06T15:46:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-354092>

**Myelin Evolved in Response to UV Light** as O2 tension began to rise.

1\. Evolutionary Pressure from UV Light Post-Cambrian Explosion: The Cambrian explosion marked a period of rapid diversification of life, driven in part by increased oxygen levels and UV radiation. I’ve noted that 200 million years later (around 338 million years ago), a UV surge occurred as our G-class star increased its UV output by 10–20%. This aligns with the Devonian period, a time when jawed fishes (the first vertebrates with myelin) emerged, around 425 million years ago. Myelin’s evolution in jawed fishes coincided with opsin expansion (melanopsin, encephalopsin, neuropsin), as you’ve pointed out. Opsins are photoreceptor proteins that absorb UV and visible light, suggesting that light played a pivotal role in the evolution of the nervous system. This should not surprise anyone because MS has a UV light link via its latitide etiology.

Myelin, as a lipid-rich structure, evolved to optimize bioelectric signaling in this light-rich environment, supporting the rapid nerve conduction needed for more complex behaviors in vertebrates. From first principles, increased UV light would have amplified ultraweak biophoton production in cells, particularly from mtDNA, heme proteins, and melanin (as I’ve described in my thesis I shared with Nick). Biophotons, emitted in the 400–700 nm range, are a byproduct of oxidative processes in mtDNA and can influence cellular signaling epigenetically. The spectra of UPE is the light from the semiconductor that brings myelin back to life.

The VNS is doing this to your spouse right now. THE VNS IS TURNING ON THIS LIGHT SHOW at the NANOSCALE. Its DC power is replaceing the light he never got from the sun.

Myelin, with its high lipid content, had to have evolved to harness these biophotons, either by absorbing UV light directly (200–350 nm, as inferred in my thesis) The other possibility is that it could have occurred by interacting with biophotons emitted by nearby melanin or heme proteins in RBCs. This photo-bioelectric role would have reduced the need for prolonged sleep by enhancing energy efficiency in the nervous system, allowing for greater wakefulness and further encephalization.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-06T15:48:23+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-354093>

The paper by [Felix Scholkmann](https://x.com/FScholkmann) does not connect myelin’s function to UV light or oxygen dynamics, nor considers how myelin biosynthesis might adapt to environmental stressors like ultraweak photon emission (UPE) spectra and oxygen tensions, but it talks about improving proton dynamics for the ATPase.

According to my decentralized thesis, this is a critical omission, as these factors influence metabolic pathways, including the citric acid cycle, which can operate in opposing directions under hypoxia to support energy production. This was big in the GOE and Cambrian transition before jaw-hinged fish became myelin and opsin experts on Earth.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-06T15:52:51+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-354094>

My Explanation for the Gap: The paper’s focus on modern myelin function misses the evolutionary context I have provided: the UV surge, which happened 600 million to 200 million years ago, to the post-Cambrian explosion, increased biophoton production and drove myelin’s evolution.

This light-driven pressure likely reduced sleep dependency by enhancing energy efficiency in the nervous system, allowing for prolonged wakefulness.

Myelin’s role in oxygen utilization (which I note is “murky” in centralized biology) should be tied to its proton capacitor function: proton discharge during wakefulness might enhance mitochondrial ATP production, which clearly relies on oxygen biophysics.

This connection warrants further biophysical research on how myelin’s lipid composition interacts with UV light and oxygen. Its absorption and emission spectra hold many clues for biophysicists in labs. Cholesterol, Sphingolipids, and Plasmalogens that make up myelin have told me for a long time why MS has an equatorial latitude linkage. They still have not figured it out.

Sooner or later, they will listen to Uncle Jack, or I will have to keep perturbing them with first-principle thinking. MS and T1D have a similar white matter loss when you look at their autopsied brains. I have. MD and T1D also have something in common a loss of cognitive abilities.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-06T16:08:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-354095>

The brain is loaded normally with cholesterol, but the injured brain lacks it and this lowers its electrical its electrical network seizures. Pure cholesterol exhibits weak fluorescence, typically emitting in the 350–400 nm range when excited by UV light (~240 nm). Glycosphingolipids, especially if oxidized, can fluoresce in the 400–500 nm range, as lipid peroxidation products (e.g., lipofuscin-like compounds) often emit blue-green light.

When exposed to oxidative stress (e.g., from light-induced reactive oxygen species), these lipids in myelin can form fluorescent products that emit in the 450–550 nm range, similar to lipid peroxidation byproducts, which note ultraweak light emission resembling chemiluminescence from lipid peroxidation. I gave you a blog already on lipofuscin.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-06T16:12:56+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-354096>

Protein Fluorescence of MBS & PLP also support Uncle Jack: Aromatic amino acids in myelin proteins (tyrosine, tryptophan) fluoresce when excited at their absorption peaks. Tyrosine emits around 300–350 nm, and tryptophan emits around 330–380 nm. However, protein fluorescence is likely quenched in myelin due to energy transfer to lipids or water. That water inside the membrane is actually what makes it white. What does white mean for semiconductors concerning colors? Another big clue for you.

In semiconductors, "white" color is typically achieved by combining light of multiple wavelengths, or by using a material that absorbs light in the UV region and emits light across the visible spectrum. This can be done using specific combinations of semiconductor materials or by employing a phosphor that converts light from a short-wavelength semiconductor into longer wavelengths, creating the perception of white light.

When UV light is scarce in a human's environment, reduced biophoton emission impairs myelin’s proton capacitor function, necessitating alternative metabolic adaptations to offset the loss. As a result, sleep and regeneration are poor in T1D and in MS folks. That is why it happens. Has zero to do with the food. This is biophysics of semiconductors 101. Look at the picture to understand how they work.

Even some biophysicists get caught in the food guru and biochemist web of deceit.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-06-06T16:15:12+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-354097>

Even the biochemists are not experts at their own game. Myelin biosynthesis, primarily involving lipid synthesis (e.g., cholesterol and sphingolipids), relies on acetyl-CoA, a key citric acid cycle intermediate.

Under normoxic conditions, acetyl-CoA is generated through pyruvate oxidation, supporting lipid production for myelin sheath formation. However, under hypoxic conditions (e.g., post-GOE or post-K-T extinction), alternative pathways emerge. This means when Fe is +3 shifted due to light interaction on heme proteins, things change.

When iron is +3 you cannot use oxygen or the TCA cycle as it was designed. The citric acid cycle’s reverse operation (e.g., conversion of α-ketoglutarate to isocitrate) generates succinyl-CoA, which can feed into lipid synthesis via the mevalonate pathway for cholesterol production. This adaptation ensures myelin maintenance despite low oxygen, as seen in my decentralized thesis’s discussion of Warburg metabolism in early eukaryotes. That is what his VNS is working against now.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-06-11T12:47:06+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-354481>

It's a beautiful morning here the Great North Woods. The sky is clear with only a couple of small poofy clouds for accent. It's amazing that I never fully realized just how cloudy it is here, all the time, until I started coming out every morning to greet the Sun. Or at least to greet the direction that I know the sun is in. It's going on four months now since I've started this new habit. My sleep has improved a thousand fold, my afib not so much.

I started listening to the pow wow yesterday and I looked into places nearby that would offer the test that Dr Jack recommends for AFib but found out that it's only available with a doctor's order. I'll reach out to my PCP just to say I did, but in the past she's been unwilling to order any tests that she thinks are inappropriate. Hopefully I can convince her of the appropriateness of this one.

Oh! I just thought of something: I wonder if I can get one of those in El Salvador? I'll be there in 3 weeks. Something to consider and look into further.

In other news, I've got a friend coming to stay for a few days and then I'll be involved in a week-long libertarian freedom festival which is held in a local campground. I run a very popular, arguably THE busiest event venue there, which keeps me going nonstop from this Sunday coming to the next one.

But I've already connected with a few Mitos there, and will be airing a replay of one of Dr Jack's Vermont talks that he recommended specifically to me as a good way to introduce people to his work. We're also going to have a meet up and do a few early morning G2Gs.

Plan for today is to finish getting the house tidied up, take DH to a therapy appointment he has, get my house guest settled in, make some more edits in the event schedule for my site, and call it a good day.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-07-06T14:08:47+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-355847>

I'm in El Salvador. I arrived last Wednesday, so today is my 5th day in the country. I'll be here until Wednesday next week when I fly back to New Hampshire.

It feels very surreal being here. And even more surreal that I'll be moving here in less than 3 months.

The time difference isn't helping either. ES is 2 hours behind NH. So I've been waking up at 3:00 a.m. El Salvador time and today it was 2:30.

I even woke up before the rooster next door by about 15 minutes. Good thing I was already awake, because I realized something about roosters. The first one to wake up wakes all the other ones up. That pause between crows? That's just to listen to hear if any of the other feathered dudes have woken up yet too.

Since I had the good fortune of having a fellow early riser for a neighbor, I was able to observe that the first three crows were followed by silence of about 20-30 minutes. Then, after the second or third crow in the next series, I could vaguely, faintly, hear the sounds of a rooster across town responding to my guy. And that's all it took. Now there's at least six or seven roosters all catching each other up on the morning's news.

I don't know how my cousin in the room next door is able to sleep through the serenade.

But anyway, tomorrow night and for the rest of the evenings of this trip I'll be staying in the capital so I can continue the work of this scout. I have an appointment with a neurologist on Monday and I still have a lot of questions that I need to figure out the answer to.

So for now I'll sign off since I think it's time to wake up my cousin so we can find a place to watch the Sun.

Good morning from El Tunco in El Salvador.

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-07-20T15:09:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-356653>

I've been back in New Hampshire for about 10 days now. It feels like forever ago that I was in El Salvador.

The neurologist in the capital does not have the equipment necessary to manage the VNS. He said he doubted that any other neurologists in the country had it either, but he gave me his card and said for me to send him an email and that he would check around with his colleagues to make sure. He did at least agree to monitor/manage DH's "pillage" like I call it. He said that pretty much everything that is prescribed to him currently is also available in ES so that's something. I think I can work with this guy. He seems open-minded at least.

As to my own medical issues, I feel like I'm in a bog with no sure footing. I have to make some decisions about where to live in ES which take into account my own healing as well as DH's and I just don't have enough knowledge to make a good judgment call.

So for example, I really liked the Lake Coatepeque area. The terrain is varied and the evenings are cool. Not to mention that the lake itself is spectacular. The downside is the slope going down to the water's edge. It's steep, and DH would have to negotiate many many steps to get down to the water. The other downside is the road that borders the lake is a nightmare. I understood from the caretakers that if you hang a left instead of a right where the main road leading to the lake intersects the perimeter lake road, that the left side (going clockwise) is paved. Our Airbnb's location required us to go right, so we went counterclockwise around the lake. And the dirt road was rutted and full of potholes. I think it took us close to an hour to go from one end of the lake to the other end. So if I end up having a medical emergency with DH that road would complicate things significantly. We never did go in the other direction, so it may be not be an issue should we find lodgings on that side.

Nonetheless, after that experience, I thought I'd focus my efforts on determining where along the coast felt like a good place to stay. We had already been to Costa del Sol, and I found that area to be quite a viable option in so far as the beaches are clear of rocks and mostly flat. The downside of that area is the distance from the medical centers and capital.

And then I met San Blas. What a cute little area! It's close to the hospital, close to the PCP's office, close to shopping etc. it's a little more urban than I would have liked, but it was still doable. I was shown a few options that are for rent in a development that has private beach access at three blocks' distance. Unfortunately that area is very steep too, and because of this I would have to pack DH in the car and drive to the closest spot to the beach (about a block away) where I can park the vehicle and then walk to the beach with him. And the beach itself is a bit steep and somewhat rocky too. But I thought we could make it work.

And then I came back home. And the first day back DH fell while trying to pick up something heavy from the ground. And then he almost fell again a day later. I don't know if being away made me notice what's been there all along: the significance of his instability, or if he's deteriorated that much in the week that I was gone. I also noticed that he's carrying one of his arms somewhat limply, as if he might have had a little stroke. He's had that before though, after a seizure it's not uncommon for his right side to be much more affected than his left. So maybe he had an event while I was gone that was missed by his caretakers. He says he doesn't think he had a seizure but his reliability is questionable.

In either case, I decided against San Blas just due to his mobility issues. And now in the timeline of my mind I'm projecting forward to a not so distant future where he might be wheelchair bound and because it's just the two of us, that means that I need to take into account my own limitations so to speak. Limitations of energy mostly, though limitations of strength are also an important consideration.

So.

As I understand it, getting our grounding by the seashore would be optimal.

But so would dipping our toes in the waters of Lake Coatepeque.

And because I learned from last month's powwow that water magnifies absorption of UV light which is what I need, what we both need really, and you can't really swim in the ocean there, and the waters of the lake are sometimes polluted and toxic, wouldn't having a saltwater pool that would be available to us anytime also be optimal? Or at least an optimal backup plan?

Because if that's the case, then I need to be looking for a house that has a small pool that can be converted into a saltwater pool for days when it's just not viable for me to help DH to the shore.

And beyond what's optimal from a MITO perspective, what would be optimal for ME would be to have some land around me, some nice trees, some nice fruit trees even. Which I don't think I can get right along the edge of the ocean. Coconut palms will grow but I don't know what else can tolerate that level of salinity and the poverty of sand.

So now I'm wondering if maybe I shouldn't be considering a piece of property that's a few blocks in from the shore so that I can have more land, and my plants/trees, AND my saltwater pool. Bonus points if said property is reasonably close to public beach access...

Because even though the plan is to come for 6 months, I've had it all along in the back of my mind that we might just end up staying there. And DH has said the same.

And so as long as our healing can happen from the shore of a saltwater pool as much as from the actual seashore or lakeshore, then my options open up and I can reconsider the lake area or some other location with a cooler climate. I just don't know if I'm picking from a number of plan As or if there are other variables of which I am ignorant that would make some of these options plan Bs.

I have many things to consider.

### Jack Kruse — 2025-10-28T04:23:04+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-361732>

Light at night and heart disease links are strong! This was just published this today in JAMA Network Open.

Using 13 million hours of personal light exposure data in >88,000 people, they found that those with the brightest nights had significantly higher risks of developing coronary artery disease, myocardial infarction, heart failure, **atrial fibrillation,** and stroke.

These associations were robust after adjusting for established cardiovascular risk factors, including physical activity, smoking, alcohol, diet, sleep duration, socioeconomic status, and polygenic risk. Keep your homes dark at night and get in the sun for photorepair of your arteries and heart!

[https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489](https://jamanetwork.com/journals/jamanetworkopen/fullarticle/2840489)

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-11-05T15:21:25+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ilmas-optimal-journal.30804/post-362198>

I had never seen a moon-set before this morning.

The morning started unusually in that DH got up before me. Usually I'm the one that wakes him up. But the afib kicked in last night and kept me up so I was tired. I was awake when he woke up, just still in bed.

He was up to walking towards the shore, so I followed but quite honestly I would have been happy to just sit on the front porch because I was that tired.

So we walk the couple hundred meters towards the ocean and I happen to look to my right. What I saw took my breath away: a full moon setting with just a few wisps of clouds to give it interest.

I ran back inside, grabbed the phone, turned it on while I was running back out and made it just in time to take these pictures.
