### Wanttoknow — 2025-05-04T17:56:24+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/47-on-patreon-who-wrote-the-poem.30828/post-351894>

Talking about decentralization the music industry has been. A website called Suno.com has made it possible to anyone who can write a poem to turn it into a song. Just take your poem and put a chorus in it and then go to the suno website and click create and put your lyrics in. It is free to use but you will not own the copyright but if you pay $10 a month you will own the song. $10 will let you do 25 songs a month. This company is making money hands over fists with around 40 employees or so. You can choose what type of music you like (country, rock, rhythm and blues .....) the voice either male or female the pace as well as other things. It will even write a song for you if you give some details in what you want the song to be like. The quality is getting better each month. It is hard to tell if it a real human singing or AI. It costs around $900 to have a song professionally done if I sent it in to a demo recording studio, but the music world is closed to new artists as they will not listen to any demo's sent to then, so I think they will not control what is sung on the radio waves anymore.

Here is what I did with Jacks poem.

A Poem for the Sovereign Soul

Battle Cry, male voice, strings intro

In the cradle of my bones, a fire burns,
Mitochondrial sparks where the cosmos turns.
Pneuma breathes, a light unseen,
A sovereign soul, fierce and clean.

Chorus
Sovereignty's price? Your heart, your fight.
Will you stand, or fade into the night?

Central chains, they creep, they bind,
Steal the body, cage the mind.
They track my coin, they script my care,
They choke the light in the open air.

Chorus
Sovereignty's price? Your heart, your fight.
Will you stand, or fade into the night?

But I am no tenant, no pawn, no slave,
I'll carve my path to the freedom I crave.
In El Salvador's sun, I stake my claim,
Bitcoin my shield, health my flame.

Chorus
Sovereignty's price? Your heart, your fight.
Will you stand, or fade into the night?

The breath of ages is yours to claim,
A photon's pulse, a soul's bright flame
Sovereignty's price? Your heart, your fight.
Will you stand, or fade into the night?
will you stand, or fade into the night.....................................

### Wanttoknow — 2025-05-05T00:00:00+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/47-on-patreon-who-wrote-the-poem.30828/post-351904>

Kiera is but one individual in the machine of copy-cats that pretend to understand what they’re talking about and actually don’t (and profit from Jack’s work).

Episode 126 of the “quantum” biology collective podcast: [https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podca...llective-podcast/id1657777911?i=1000704695617](https://podcasts.apple.com/us/podcast/the-quantum-biology-collective-podcast/id1657777911?i=1000704695617)

I was intrigued to listen to a derm claiming to have a “quantum approach” to healing, but I was not optimistic as the QBC podcast has gotten it wrong before. The episode was an absolute joke with the derm advocating for sunblock use whenever UV is present. Same old, same old.

### AlbertD — 2025-05-30T19:31:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/a-word-of-caution-on-%E2%80%9Cquantum%E2%80%9D.30829/post-353583>

New to what happened to Kiera. My takeaways are not to steal and plagiarize someone else's work. But there is a line between plagiarism and fair use, no? Social media outlets/influencers copy each other for commercial use all the time sometimes without giving attribution.

I've read/heard Jack say "his only ask is to share this message". Sharing this message means one can profit "monetarily" and/or "status-wise". We share the products/ideas we like for status and even "profit" (via affiliate links/other monetary strategies) among the people we are care about.

I imagine clinicians in this community learn about these pillars to better their patients (they also profit, no?) And shouldn't they be properly compensated proportional to the value they bring? For bettering themselves and improve the quality of care they provide?

It seems to me that based on fair use laws that one does not have to provide attribution for limited use of the original author's work. I notice that the blogs clearly delineate between Jack's work vs someone else's work (Gilbert Ling, Feynman, Mae-wan Ho, etc.).

My impression is: Fair use exists. But to avoid Jack's wrath, just provide credit when his ideas are being used outside this community.
Anyone disagree?

# Thread: Introduction: Daniel, Seeker of Truth, Health, and Wealth
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-daniel-seeker-of-truth-health-and-wealth.30830/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nitton76 — 2025-05-04T16:59:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/introduction-daniel-seeker-of-truth-health-and-wealth.30830/post-351889>

**Greetings, Kruse community!** I’m Daniel, a fervent seeker of truth, health, and wealth, and I’m profoundly honored to join this exceptional forum, inspired by your audacious, paradigm-shifting discussions. Your deep dives into biological optimization, fearless questioning of mainstream narratives, and commitment to thriving through nature’s truths have compelled me to craft this introduction—a candid, expansive exploration of my journey through health struggles, relentless truth-seeking, and a visionary drive to empower humanity. This is a comprehensive narrative of who I am, why I’m here, and how I aspire to contribute to our shared mission of living with unparalleled vitality. If my story resonates, I invite you to read to the end and join me in forging connections to spark transformative ideas that can reshape our world. Full transparency: I’ve spent the last 90 minutes collaborating with AI to craft this message. While AI shaped the words, this is unequivocally my voice, my heart, my message.

**How I Found Kruse’s Work**
My journey to Kruse began in December 2024, ignited by an encounter with Robert Breedlove in Mexico, shortly after their podcast interview. Robert’s infectious passion for Kruse’s insights—circadian rhythms, light as a therapeutic force, and ancestral health—seized my attention, propelling me to immerse myself in Epi-paleo Rx and the podcast. Initially, the book seemed like an echo of my existing practices; I was already dedicated to natural light exposure and sleep hygiene. But I live by the ethos, “I don’t do anything that ain’t worth overdoing.” 2 weeks ago, I escalated my morning and evening routines to an extreme—uncompromising sunrise exposure, grounding barefoot on grass, and a strict ban on evening screens, post 5pm. The impact was profound: my energy soared, my focus sharpened, and I rediscovered a vitality dormant for years. My wife, grappling with mental well-being challenges and hormone imbalances managed by pharmaceuticals, has seen progress with these changes—her sleep is improving, her mood is lifting—but we both face a long road ahead. This transformation is a clarion call, and I’m here to deepen my understanding with your collective expertise.

**Health: Extreme Routines, Real Food, and Mental Struggles**
Health is the bedrock of my existence—without it, all else falters. My physical health is formidable, and my appearance reflects years of disciplined care, but my mental capacity falls short of my peak a decade ago, when I thrived in the frenetic chaos of emergency management. Back then, my brain was a dynamo, wired for rapid, multitasking decisions—crisis mode was my forte. Today, my work is strategic and long-term, building ventures like Cowbolt and FYSIGO, requiring sustained focus and discipline that often clash with my brain’s current wiring, exacerbated by health challenges. I’ve always possessed an extraordinary capacity to work, performing at levels that mask my struggles from others—most wouldn’t suspect anything’s wrong. Yet, I know there’s a vast reservoir of untapped potential within me, waiting to be unleashed if I can optimize my brain. Only three months ago did I realize I’d been grinding for years—five, perhaps eight—immersed in client work with global giants, pushing without pause. Recent changes are steering me in the right direction, but I’m far from my full capacity.

Overtraining was and is my Achilles’ heel; every 6-10 months, I’d pursue grueling workouts, chasing performance, only to crash with burnout or injuries. Fitness trackers provided heart rate variability data, but I ignored my body’s signals, driven by an insatiable need to excel. Years of poor sleep cloaked my mind in fog, blunting my cognitive edge. I’m unvaccinated, a choice rooted in my unyielding commitment to individual sovereignty—a principle I’d defend to the end.

Eight weeks ago, Epi-paleo Rx catalyzed a revolution, pushing me to amplify my morning and evening routines to an extreme: sunrise walks without sunglasses, grounding on dew-soaked grass, and a complete halt to blue light after dusk. This approach has worked wonders—my sleep is restorative, and my mental sharpness is slicing through tasks, though not yet at my decade-ago zenith.

At the same time, the past two months have been demanding. I rely on ADHD medications a few days a week to sharpen my focus for critical tasks—strategy sessions, pitches, deep work. I’m adamantly opposed to pharmaceuticals; they’re a crutch that clashes with my principles, and I resent their necessity. Once or twice a month, when creativity eludes me, I’ll take an 8-centiliter gin and tonic, always early morning, never after 2 PM to safeguard my sleep. The creativity boost can be the edge I need to deliver on commitments, but I’m not proud of it—I want to thrive without these aids. My goal is to eliminate both entirely, and I’m relying on this community’s expertise for natural alternatives—circadian optimizations, dietary adjustments, or biohacks to rewire my brain. More concerning are symptoms possibly linked to a stroke or poor brain blood flow: tingling in my limbs, occasional brain fog, and sluggish thoughts, flagged as bad blood flow to parts of the brain on the scan.

Recent sleep improvements have reduced fog and tingles, but I remain worried and am eager for Kruse-inspired strategies, such as mitochondrial enhancement or vascular health optimization.

I’ve been using LiveO2 contrast therapy ([https://liveo2.com](https://liveo2.com)), and it’s proving transformative. Its Adaptive Contrast technology—cycling oxygen-rich and oxygen-depleted air during HIIT exercise—feels like it’s supercharging my circulation, flooding my brain with oxygen, and boosting energy, directly supporting my goals for mental clarity and blood flow.

My blood markers are exceptional, though some doctors raise concerns about my 350+ cholesterol levels. Holistic practitioners would likely endorse my overall data, reflecting a body in balance. I’d never consume the processed junk Liquid supports our customers to produce. I source raw, unprocessed A2 cow dairy and meat from an Amish farm or Wild Idea Buffalo—their bison is phenomenal, you’ve got to try it!

**Truth: Courage in the Face of Dogma**
Truth is my guiding star—a dogged pursuit of unfiltered facts, no matter how controversial. I’m inspired by those who sacrifice to speak out, staking their reputations, careers, or livelihoods for what’s right. During the COVID-19 turmoil, Dr. Mary Talley Bowden was a lighthouse, suspended from Houston Methodist Hospital for championing early treatments like ivermectin and challenging vaccine mandates, prioritizing patients over institutional pressures. Anders Tegnell, Sweden’s state epidemiologist, was another pillar, standing resolute with a data-driven, no-lockdown approach that defied global hysteria. His response to a reporter’s query—“The whole world is wondering which data you are using”—is my all-time favorite quote: “The question you should be asking is what is the rest of the world using because I haven’t seen any data at all.” That piercing clarity crystallized my commitment to grounding every belief in rigorous evidence.

My own truth-seeking led me to an Israeli study demonstrating natural immunity’s superiority, with a 6.72 times lower reinfection risk compared to vaccination, reinforcing my unvaccinated stance and spurring deeper research into immunity. In business, I embodied this truth: one of my CEOs departed when I refused a vaccine mandate, a decision rooted in my unshakeable belief in individual sovereignty. This wasn’t merely a stand—it was an expression of my core identity. I’m always open to those who risk everything to share their message, and I bring that same openness to this community, eager to engage with your perspectives, challenge my assumptions, and build on data-driven insights that pierce the veil of misinformation.

Health and Wealth: From Industry Contradictions to Freedom’s Horizon
Health is the bedrock of my life’s mission, and I’m building FYSIGO, a decentralized health marketplace to empower individuals with tools for wellness. I’d never touch the processed liquid foods Liquid supports—I call them “poison” for a reason, and I stand by that conviction. For 19 years, I’ve led Liquid, one of the world’s few specialists assisting global giants in producing the sugary drinks and processed junk I believe are undermining our health. The irony is sharp; this work has sustained my livelihood and continues to do so, but it contradicts my values. I built Oatly’s first U.S. factory, amplifying their “poisonous sugar water,” and if asked to build another, I’d do it—business is my reality—but I’d proclaim their products’ harm from every platform. Truth is non-negotiable, even when it’s tied to my income. Wealth, to me, is freedom—self-sufficiency and providing for my family, not merely financial gain. Bitcoin revolutionized my perspective in 2018, unveiling decentralization as the path to true empowerment. Now, Cowbolt.com is my top priority, transforming Bitcoin into a practical, everyday currency for peer-to-peer transactions—splitting bills, sharing meals, or settling group expenses—using Bitcoin, Liquid Bitcoin, and Tether on the Liquid Network. It’s private, non-custodial, and fosters freer communities, enabling people to onboard friends and family through real-world utility, not speculation. I’m calling on Bitcoiners to join as Founding Partners to shape this mission, building a financial system that supports health, sovereignty, and freedom. Cowbolt and FYSIGO embody the ethos of seizing control, breaking free from restrictive systems, and forging a future where health and autonomy prevail. I don’t need to work—I choose to, because I love it, just as I loved the adrenaline of emergency management. Yet, my current strategic work demands a mental capacity I’m striving to reclaim, making sleep and cognitive clarity paramount.

**My Mantra: Truth, Health, Wealth**
“Truth, health, wealth” is my daily compass, anchoring me amidst a sea of noise. Truth demands unfiltered facts, no matter the cost or controversy. Health fuels vitality, empowering me to pursue my mission with unrelenting drive. Wealth unlocks freedom to live authentically and support those I cherish. Their order is deliberate—prioritizing money over well-being or honesty leads to imbalance.

**Current Focus: Unleashing Mental Capacity for Global Impact**
I’m here to sharpen my mental effort, the critical lever to unlock my vast, untapped potential when time and physical effort are stretched to their limits. My formula—Results = Time × Physical Effort × Mental Effort²—reveals that clarity, focus, and strategy are the true catalysts for transformative impact. I’m channeling this into Cowbolt.com, liberating people financially to prioritize health by making Bitcoin a seamless, everyday currency, and FYSIGO, cultivating wellness communities grounded in biological truths like circadian alignment, nutrient-dense nutrition, and mitochondrial vitality. My current strategic work—unlike the crisis-driven emergency management of my past—clashes with my brain’s wiring, particularly given my health challenges, making enhanced sleep performance and cognitive capacity non-negotiable. I choose this work because I love it, driven by passion just as I was in my crisis management days, but I’m desperate to elevate my life and unleash the full potential I know lies within me.

**Let’s Connect**
If you’ve journeyed through this entire post, you’re my kind of truth-seeker—thank you for staying with me! I’m here to learn, share, and contribute to our shared mission of living better through biology. Want to dive deeper? Find me on LinkedIn (http://www.linkedin.com/in/daniel₿) for updates on Cowbolt.com and FYSIGO, and my reflections on truth, health, and wealth. I’m passionate about meeting new people, so reach out for a FaceTime or Zoom to brainstorm health, truth, or world-changing ideas. Thank you for inspiring this introduction—let’s uncover the truth and build a brighter future together!

From Florida With Love!
Daniel

When we enter a commercial store like a supermarket, we are forced to walk in between two EAS devices that undoubtedly blast us with high amounts of EMF. I can't find any actual EMF measures, so how bad is it really? It seems suspect as fuck because the store is obviously meticulously designed to alter behaviour trough the lighting. All of the peasants have go to these super/hyper-markets. So I'm starting to get a feeling that is a place to get our bi-weekly mega-dose of mind altering radiation. Obviously these places have high amounts of EMF by default, but I'm thinking there is a more intricate and deliberate design to it. What do you think about this?

# Thread: Colonoscopy Question
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/colonoscopy-question.30832/>
**Subforum:** Optimal Labs
**Posts:** 3
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Christopher C — 2025-05-04T22:39:46+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/colonoscopy-question.30832/post-351902>

Hi everyone!
Jack recently mentioned plans to challenge a well known gastroenterologist about the usefulness of colonoscopies. I did the read paper below. I have a family history of this disease. My father died from cancer that began on his liver and spread to his colon. Nevertheless, I’ve been very weary of getting a colonoscopy for years now, after reading the work of Dr Sherri Tenpenny (The Truth About Colonoscopies) and learning about how many mistakes are made, how dangerous the procedure can be and how it often does not make a difference as far as cancer mortality. My question for this group of people is: Is there any other lab or test that’s recommended for someone like me to gauge their colon health? I’m really not thrilled with the idea of a tube being put up my butt… Note: I am 47 years old, I do get a lot of Sun, I limit my exposure to blue light/nnEMF. I ground everyday at sunrise and usually also at sunset. I wear blue blockers at night and really stay away from screens before bedtime. I use a red light sauna from Therasage as well. My vitamin D is due to be tested but last time I checked it was 82. Any tips are appreciated. Thanks so much!

PAPER: [https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5518954/](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5518954/)

# Thread: Hello ALL!
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-all.30833/>
**Subforum:** Meet and Greet
**Posts:** 5
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### BradyMartinik — 2025-05-05T03:48:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-all.30833/post-351910>

Hey everybody,

My name is Brady Martinik. I just turned 19 a little over a week ago and I currently reside in suburban Northford, Connecticut, USA. My health journey started out after COVID when all my environmental polluters caught up to my young mitochondria and I was left obese, sad and hungry to feel how a wild human should. I grew up labeled a “Talented and Gifted” child, within Rockefeller’s indoctrination camp and traditional schooling came very easy to me. I loved the appraisal from parents, peers, teachers, family and friends. A prospectively good slave in training, ripe to spin the wheels of the NGO’s ideal paradigm. I loved sports, hockey, lacrosse, baseball, football, tag, bikes, it didn’t really matter, I’m always up for a competition.

However, some of my earliest memories are my parents growing frustrated. Frustrated with my inquisitive mind. Always asking ”Why?” Or “How?”, never satisfied, sticking my nose into any conversation, task, trial and tribulation I could weasel my way into (Frequently averse to the liking of whom I’d relentlessly question. ). It arose to the point that they’d submit a question cooldown. Precisely at this time I received my first digital device and these devices never grew tired of me. Access to the internet and an infinite library of videos, games, and answers to my questions never ceased! I grew very fond of these devices but kept a natural balance due to my mind, body and spirits intuitive love for activity and REAL dopamine that my body craved just the same. This teetering of the seesaw hadn’t shot me in to the sky or slammed me on my ass yet… But surely, as you can presume, in due time, it did, which I’ll get into later.

Fast forward to middle school and everyone else was getting bigger, taller, stronger, but I lulled behind. Deemed a late bloomer, everyone else growing in height and bicep peaks, and me waist circumference and weight. Staying up late nights on bright blue lit devices, gulping down glyphosate, corn syrup and a laundry list of other toxins from big box foods religiously, culminating a slow but steady trek toward low dopamine device dependence just to stimulate a fleeting spark to continue on. I can remember my first thoughts of ending my suffering, not deep rumination, not imminent, but an overcast whisper that shadowed my mind being lit up by blue light from a tablet, or DS in the darkness of night. Still social, still making my parents proud with A’s in school, still playing the sports I do dearly loved, but dragging along instead of fiercely attacking life with vigor and vibrance as Nature permits.

Around this time as well, 7th-8th grade, 2018-2019, kids began to talk about masturbation. “What is this I thought?” A flex of pride and masculinity at the time. Encapsulating these conversations is a vivid memory of a lunch table conversation “Some kid told me on Xbox that he never masturbated in his life and he’s a junior in high school!” \*The table bust out laughing.\* “Seriously!?” “What a loser!” I chimed, trying to protect my image of pride and masculinity myself. I had never masturbated a day in my life, didn’t even know what It was or even had the testosterone too due to the circumstances I described. But with my handy dandy device my questions were answered. Every website I came across answered the same. .Org .Edu .Com .NIH .MED had all said the same. “Masturbation and Pornography are healthy and normal and should be experimented with!” So, I obviously followed suit to the digital GOD, how could I not! All signs pointed forward. Explicit, vivid, graphic imagery, a SWORD of dopamine in my pocket any and every time I desired.

Fast forward again, January 2020, 8TH Grade, my friends and I began to experiment with extremely high THC street marijuana carts and again I followed the crowd. Another dopamine sword in my armory. A week after my first time smoking marijuana we got sent home from school indefinitely for a little virus called the “Coronavirus”. Amazing! No school! Just me and an internet connection. The pornography and masturbation lie relatively dormant and in control, a weekly or monthly thing. Marijuana became a weekly endulgement as well. Smoking weed and stuffing our faces with pizza, cookies and cakes, and funny movies, filled with the thrill of hiding out secret from our unknowing parents was the weekly routine with my buddies and I. My dopamine receptors melting with delight. An innocent endulgement. Yet the devil seethed at the path to come, as I traversed mindlessly down this unknown dark road. I felt guilty about smoking, lying to my parents, masturbating, but it wasn’t out of hand just yet. Still dragging along.

During this time I became immersed in a virtual no restriction, no filtration, world of gaming as well. My parents resisted the excessive gaming but I tied their hands claiming that It was a socialization aspect as we were locked down still due to the control experiment we now called the “COVID” pandemic. 12 Hours Daily I endulged in this digital world. Playing video games, bonding with others from 13-25 doing the same things as me, gaming, smoking weed, masturbating, ALL in more and more excess slowly but steadily as I attempted to fill the endless dopamine void unknowingly. I competed in gaming competitions and treated it like a job, streaming on twitch, making YouTube videos, playing wagers (Let’s throw in a gambling addiction and the stimulus of bank deposits and transactions larger than I he ever seen before while we’re in this dark dopamine storm while we’re at it right?), new weaponized content machines like TikTok, YouTube Shorts and Instagram Reels, High Defintion Pornography, etc, being optimized at Light speed, in order to fuel this global dopamine fire. I was the moth to the flame. Burning my hands on this piping stove repeatedly, numb to it’s burning sensation.

Fast forward again, 2 or 3 years this time. This storm approached MACH 10 and I became insufferable. Whenever I stepped out of my room, looked in the mirror, etc, I was ANGRY, depressed, less and less social, less and less active, less and less pleasant, the mask and facade I was able to upkeep out of the virtual world was too small to shelter what I had become. My health and spirit slipping away before my eyes. One day my parents called me into the living room and showed me my pictures from my hockey portraits and told me they couldn’t send these out to any family members. I was broken. I stormed away and balled. I couldn’t stand to look myself in the mirror but with the confrontation of my parents I knew I wasn’t hiding my reality from anyone or anything, any more. It was real, it was here, and it had to be dealt with. That overcast shadow of hopelessness and the eternal escape of death engulfed my soul now.

Nevertheless, my soul’s flame although diminished, lay eternal, with hot embers burning in my core. The very weapon that insidiously and mercilessly tormented me would be my tool. The light in the darkness was there I just had to access it. I used the internet now to fuel and educate myself on how I could fix what I had destroyed. Not a little boy anymore, no one there to save me but me, I did what I had to do, slowly, still these demons slithering and striking me at the onset of any weakness of my spirit, I fought and I fought and I fought for my indomitable spirit and birthright back.

As I weaponized my mind with knowledge about these demons, asked “Why?”, asked “How?” and asked “What?” I could do to fix myself these addictive dopaminergic pathways I was coerced into were weaponized against the Evil dark paradigm with the very Evil they used to do so. They didn’t realize it but they had just battle hardened and weaponized their worst nightmare. The coin they carved was flipped and now I sit here typing this message with a Fire in my Soul that will Light up an Army. An army of hungry, broken, corrupted, violated spirits the same as me. Tirelessly searching for the fuel to the embers of their hearts that won’t fleet. That fuel is the sun. I know their tricks, I know who they are, and I know their game inside and out.

I am here now for all the little boys and girls doomed to drown in the darkness I was swallowed by for so long. Every day I grow increasingly and exponentially more and more aware of the mechanisms used by the controllers that tainted my soul and consciousness. Stripping away, piece by piece, with every sunrise and sunset, the lead these Evil, transhumanist, globalist, technocratic, pieces of plastic. They don’t know it but I know my story is one of the masses. Discovered, infinitely and abundantly more and more every single day as we all fight the good fight and find the true light!

These motherfuckers chose the wrong side of the coin. No matter how much filth and darkness they sputter they are trumped by the most abundant and infinite force in our possession. This force is one everyone in this forum knows and will know more deeply and dearly every single sunrise.

I love you all, I feel you all, never lose hope, never stop fighting, your fuel is infinite and tireless. Choose this fuel, now and forever, it is the only fuel that can and will satisfy your souls hunger. The cavalry is here, march onward, wielding your indisputable, undeniable, indomitable flame that GOD gifted us all. Do it for the sick man and women you once were and the countless, innocent, sick little boys and girls crying, hoping, pleading, for help, that there still are.

LET THERE BE LIGHT ☀️

### Wilma Without a W — 2025-05-05T23:20:43+0300

Welcome, Brady. I hear the fire in your ancient soul through your young voice. How did you find us? You skipped that part. I'd like to know that part of the story.

### BradyMartinik — 2025-05-06T01:37:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/hello-all.30833/post-352000>

> [Wilma Without a W said:](/goto/post?id=351976)
>
> Welcome, Brady. I hear the fire in your ancient soul through your young voice. How did you find us? You skipped that part. I'd like to know that part of the story.

Hello Ilma! I traveled from TikTok gym bros, to your average dogmatic diet guru, praising each one as a Saint with each new practice I found over time. Saladino, Huberman, Dr Berg, etc. Then I began watching UFC fighters Sean O Malley and Tim Welch’s podcasts. Two young guys preaching a balanced lifestyle, living out their dreams, helping others with their gifts without being tied down to a timesheet, just as I strongly desired. Their teacher was Paul Chek and when I found him, he encapsulated so much in his practices. He embodies his practice and on his YouTube channel and podcasts I could find a practical answer that he himself practiced and shared with all his other CHEK practitioners with the results to show in his and his students clarity of mind, body and spirit and I clinged to this. A clear way to help people achieve true health. Recently, Paul had Dr Alexis and Dr Lieberman on and I had heard and seen through the digital grapevine some of Dr Kruse before. Dr Alexis and Dr Lieberman’s eloquence led me to dive deeply into all their work leading me right to the source. Uncle Jack. Experiencing the pains of a digital, indoor and inverted circadian lifestyle this past Connecticut winter in my personal health, the picture became undeniably clear and intuitive. The rest is history. The past month has been an infatuation of our human nature and evolution through light, water and magnetism and the deceptive weaponization used by the machine against the masses, that no one seemed to talk about! With the my first sunrises, barefoot sunbaths and sunsets at the onset of a Connecticut spring the proof was in the pudding and now I’m here! A raging mitochondriac who finally had ANSWERS and a Picasso of a picture to paint and provide answers for others… Now, with a raging tribe of fed up wild humans beside me!

My health journey started out after COVID when all my environmental polluters caught up to my young mitochondria and I was left obese, sad and hungry to feel how a wild human should. I grew up labeled a “Talented and Gifted” child, within Rockefeller’s indoctrination camp and traditional schooling came very easy to me. I loved the appraisal from parents, peers, teachers, family and friends. A prospectively good slave in training, ripe to spin the wheels of the NGO’s ideal paradigm. > However, some of my earliest memories are my parents growing frustrated. Frustrated with my inquisitive mind. It arose to the point that they’d submit a question cooldown. Precisely at this time I received my first digital device and these devices never grew tired of me. Access to the internet and an infinite library of videos, games, and answers to my questions never ceased! I grew very fond of these devices but kept a natural balance due to my mind, body and spirits intuitive love for activity and REAL dopamine that my body craved just the same. This teetering of the seesaw hadn’t shot me in to the sky or slammed me on my ass yet… But surely, as you can presume, in due time, it did, which I’ll get into later.
>
> Fast forward to middle school and everyone else was getting bigger, taller, stronger, but I lulled behind. ### Mark1969 — 2025-05-06T02:51:33+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/volcanoes-lightning-mitochondria-the-only-thing-that-finally-clicked.30840/post-352007>

I just dropped a video inspired by **Decentralized Medicine #44** — and this might be the _only_ part of Jack’s blogs I’ve been able to fully wrap my head around. The image of Earth as a giant lightning rod lit up by volcanic sulfur? Finally, something I could _see_, not just stare at blankly while my mitochondria cried for help.

Honestly, maybe I should’ve stuck to Patreon — I feel like I accidentally enrolled in **Quantum Biology 401** when I was just looking for **"Sunlight for Dummies."**

That said, I’m here for it. I’m trying to suck this stuff up, even if it feels like I’m reading theoretical physics in a foreign language — upside down — on fire.

Shoutout to Jack. The man’s brain operates on a different frequency, but when the gears _do_ connect, it’s like flipping on a light switch you didn’t know you had.

# Thread: An Optimal Journey across Latitudes - Tanya
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-optimal-journey-across-latitudes-tanya.30841/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 4
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### MillValley22 — 2025-05-06T05:10:53+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-optimal-journey-across-latitudes-tanya.30841/post-352016>

Well, it's been a year, and I'm going to commit to my Optimal Journal by putting it out there.

I'm 54.

I moved 27 times times between when I was born until I graduated from high school. We lived in Northern Michigan, Alaska, Japan, South Korea, Idaho. Then Washington DC, Amsterdam, New York, Tulsa, London, Amsterdam, Singapore, Boulder, Mill Valley, California. I recognized as a kid I felt different in different places. Singapore was probably the place I felt the healthiest, although I hated the humidity and the heat, now being back to the states in a 5G environment, I recognize this was much healthier for me.
Snapshot: Very sporty, healthy, until I wasn't. It happened at age 27. Diagnosed with different things but Fibromyalgia was what the eventual diagnosis. I now know my nervous system was shot, and I had bad heteroplasmy, probably since birth as I was jaundiced. This happened after an epic heartbreak, then a move to London, where I didn't get any sun. Strangely, I felt better as I got pregnant, and after having my children. However, all of My three children were all born with jaundice as well. I'm not sure what made me better, but I think it was a mix of naturopathy, osteopathy, Chinese Medicine, then eventually the move to Singapore, taking me to 3 degrees latitude, where I was for 15 years. My stats were 5 feet eight inches tall, 118 pounds, generally healthy, eating healthy and a supplement cult follower in the realms of Ben Greenfield and Dave Asprey (until I worked with both of them on projects and became disenchanted). I was doing a lot of the 'right' things, but still very blue light toxic from working all hours and international travel every week.

I moved during Covid from Singapore to Boulder, and I felt very off there. The altitude, the puffiness and inflammation. I went very pale, and never acclimated. Both of my daughters experienced extreme depression. We moved to California and things greatly improved. I know California isn't perfect but I felt dizzy in Colorado, and hearing some of Jack's talks on our monthly calls, I realize now it must be from the various army bases.

Since my move 3.5 years ago fro the tropics, although it may also be my age, my hormones have gone wild, and I've gained 20 pounds. On top of the weight, I am puffy and my nervous system is trigger happy, especially under stress (like being in a car, especially)

I found Dr. Jack a little over a year ago, and have started to look after my circadian health. I'm the tannest I've ever been, but I'm concerned my redox is shot. I spend a minimum of of five hours outside every day. I've been to El Salvador now twice (all less than a week) with cherished friends I've met through this community. I noticed on my last trip, my puffiness reduced, my tummy was starting to normalize, and as I look at photos from that trip, I feel like the old me was shining through.
I wil be getting my vitals/bloods redone soon. I travel a lot for work, not ideal. I am juggling a lot, including three children. But my brain is working so much better since I started my circadian health. I wake up with the sun, and in bed by 8pm, usually. I ordered a daylight computer. I use blue blockers. I use tech way too much but Ive reduced usage. . I sold my home and converted it to bitcoin. Things are great (overall), especially I feel my mental capacity. I noticed in Boulder, although I didn't get the jab, I did get covid (mild symptoms) however there was a brain fog for six months, until I moved to California.

My ancestral lineage is Japan and Finland - both love geothermal/hot therapies (onsen and sauna) and cold therapy. I actually love the cold and feel if I can find a mix of cold and sunlight, I would be a very happy Mito Mama.

I have three children, one with severe issues. She has the palest skin and her vitamin D level is 5%. This is improving but I believe her mental health issues is related to low redox, she is 22 and she's my biggest concern at the moment.
I will be creating more of a plan and considering another consult with Jack after I get my bloodwork. Hormones seem to be fluctuating, and I want to truly optimize as much as possible for the next year until my youngest graduates and I will be mobile. I look forward to learning more through this forum, taking more action on what I've learned from Dr. Jack (I feel I understand around 60%, and learning how to speak Mito), but probably more importantly buying back some of my time through bitcoin so I can make better choices, especially freeing myself from the laptop and endless zoom calls! I am considering as a big gift to myself becoming a Farm member but I'd like to see how much I can improve over the next six months with regular trips to El S., more discipline with blue light, and increasing my cold therapy as summer approaches.
The biggest issue I have at the moment is the start of hot flashes - it's a thing! Just got some Chinese medicine herbal tinctures which seems to have helped a bit. Love this community. Tanya

### MillValley22 — 2025-11-09T17:40:47+0200
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/an-optimal-journey-across-latitudes-tanya.30841/post-362422>

Hi Jack, thank you for these links, I studied them over the summer and into the fall. I also listened to around 30 of your past webinars in the 2015 - 2020 range which have been so helpful and 2-3 podcasts a week that you are on. I'm a bitcoin maxi now. I wake up with the sun everyday and watch the sunset at night. At least 4-5 hours outdoors a day and this has been a game changer. I feel healthier on some levels but I also know I have EMF challenges as I'm on a laptop, although I've mitigated some things through an EMF consult. I started HRT (estrogen, progesterone and testosterone) in July - as my night sweats were so bad I couldn't sleep - this was after six months of constant waking up - and I finally bit the bullet. I immediately felt better and also less anxious. I had a lot of business travel for a couple of months and felt the impact of this. What I have noticed though is I seem very very susceptible to histamine, and have lowered histamine foods and I know histadine/histamine is related to good sunlight. I plan another trip to El Salvador in either December or January. I saw an older post on FB that you mention onions and cooking them will release more Quercetin which I believe you wrote also helps with Histamine issues. I have a lot of facial and stomach swelling and considering a consult. Jack, I hope you know you are doing such a service to humanity, and your genius is so appreciated. I have a dear friend who works with RR (a musician) that I also want to send you to for a consult. Bless you. Tanya

Any recommendations would be greatly appreciated

# Thread: Nick's Optimal Journal: My Journey to Health and Decentralized Psychotherapy
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-optimal-journal-my-journey-to-health-and-decentralized-psychotherapy.30846/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Nick-Rolser — 2025-05-07T17:12:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-optimal-journal-my-journey-to-health-and-decentralized-psychotherapy.30846/post-352098>

New member here. 27-year-old male, living in New Hampshire on the Seacoast. My reasons for being here are two-fold:

First, I’m a songwriter and psychology student.

My interest in psychology started with Carl Jung, whose work I began reading when I first dropped out of school at 19 to pursue music. I attribute reading Jung with learning how to think properly, based off of empirical observation. He also gave me a map to better understand the unconscious mind which I found helped channel my creativity. As satisfying as it is to make and perform music, I found that it wasn't something I could make a living doing. So my passion for psychology led me back to school. However, the psychology taught in academia is highly centralized and ineffective. They've completely lost touch with the history of dynamic psychiatry.

Later I found Rupert Sheldrake, whose morphic resonance theory gave a potential basis for Jung’s archetypal hypothesis. Then when I read Becker the whole thing clicked: The “morphogenetic field” from Sheldrake's hypothesis, which seemed so abstract, had a potential basis in the electromagnetic field, acting as the mechanism behind information transfer and species-level inheritance of instincts (Jung's "collective unconscious"). I'm far from having it all worked out yet, but it feels like the right direction to take. I haven't looked into Levin's research yet, but I'm glad I found this community first to understand where his limitations will be.

Psychodynamic theory at its best, despite it’s criticism as being “subjective”, goes much deeper than the cognitive behavioral theory which currently monopolizes the field, in the same way molecular biology has monopolized medicine. Poppers rejection of Freud's theories as "psuedoscience" were premature, since neuroscience had yet to catch up to provide any objective basis for the subjective experience. By updating it with modern neuroscience and quantum physics, I believe it could become a truly decentralized approach to psychotherapy. So I’ve gone back to school in the hopes of contributing to that. That’s one reason for my quantum entanglement within this community.

I start with the above because that describes who I am and what I'm passionate about far more than my second reason for being here, which is merely a symptom and not something I intend to define myself by.

That reason is a health issue that's persisted for about three years now. Dermatitis/rosacea of some kind. Exclusively on my face. Last year I went down the diet guru route—primarily carnivore/animal based. I followed a protocol of customizing the diet, “gentle cleansing”, and exercising from an online guru who claimed to heal skin diseases. After a year of dedicating myself to that, I wound up somewhat jacked with better redox in my gut, although my skin didn’t budge.

Fast-forward to January of this year when a friend of mine introduced me to Dr. Kruse. I started with the Huberman podcast and it clicked immediately. I didn’t understand most of it, but my intuition told me everything I needed to know. Since then, I’ve been deep diving—I read Becker’s _The Body Electric_ and am halfway through _Cross Currents_ right now.

I’ve also read the Solar Callus blog and have started making shrimp broth. I’ve been getting at least an hour of AM sunlight most mornings, as well as tanning frequently in the afternoon. Pretty much every area of my body can tan with the exception of my face which burns up pretty quickly.

I’ve been desperately trying to put an end to my caffeine addiction, which I know is a consequence of my low-redox environment. I usually drink one or two cups of coffee a day so my brain can function optimally. But it simultaneously produces anxiety and is too much of a stressor on top of all the light pollution of modern living.

I’m planning on moving to a better location in December once I finish my bachelors. Obviously I’m aware that my light environment sucks here, and even with the arrival of spring they’ve been going berserk with the chemtrails. I’ve been thinking about Florida, but leaving the country is not out of the question some time down the road. My mitochondrial haplotype is V. I’m not aware of any relevant SNPS. Perhaps someone can recommend where I might send my data to find these out?

As far as family history goes, my mother has had kidney stones in the past. My maternal grandmother has lost her hearing and is showing signs of memory impairment (which is no surprise to me given that my family recently got her on Bluetooth hearing aids). If I'm not mistaken my great-grandmother also had both of these issues. I'm aware of the skin-brain neuroectoderm connection so I'm assuming that's how that ties in to my own situation. Curious to hear if anyone has additional insights to offer here.

Thanks for reading. Excited to be here and to learn from this community.


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Optimal Journal - From Nassau Bahamas to Piha NZ, addictions distractions and lifelong sun obsession
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-from-nassau-bahamas-to-piha-nz-addictions-distractions-and-lifelong-sun-obsession.30849/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### The.Naked.Doctor — 2025-05-08T02:59:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/optimal-journal-from-nassau-bahamas-to-piha-nz-addictions-distractions-and-lifelong-sun-obsession.30849/post-352122>

**Warning LONG and not expecting anyone to read, this is more for me to keep track and see patterns etc.**

41/f no health concerns except, lifelong um sometime problematic enthusiasm (ahaem addiction) with anything buzzy!

**History**:

Born and grew up in the Bahamas from Bahamian dad and German mum
Dad is a doc, and after my one and only vax at 3 months (and life threatening reaction) went quantum/holistic and never looked back
The bad: because of this and shunning of conventional medicine that almost killed his daughter, dad has too much focus on botanical medicine/sups/intravenous vitamins/ozone functional BS MUM opened a chain of health shops that funded my education and life for 30 years.
The good: Dad always had us up in the morning barefoot, shuffling our feet on the damp grass or sand, mum was obsessed with sun when pregnant, never wore sunscreen, grew up on the beach, not allowed cell phone etc until I left Bahamas for university. Ate a macrobiotic type diet rich in fresh seafood.
Went to university in Florida, high EMF but great sun... BUT then to Canada to become a naturopathic doctor at 21- problems started. Started smoking weed to sleep, lived in 11floor downtown condo, continued for almost 15 years. Became quite dependant on cannabis to sleep
Moved back to Nassau to practice with my dad in 2013, began drinking a fuck lot. Worked in blue lit office.
2014 Visisted AUckland NZ- fell in love, left my husband and clinic behind for new relationship also addict, toxic relationship serious, class A, cannabis, alcohol, psycadelics, u name it, I probably did it weekly at least.
Discovered JK in 2016. Quit drinking to be able to wake up for sunrise, red lights in house, blue blockers, more seafood daily. Started keto diet.
Still weed dependant to sleep, then discovered phenibut and used this for another 5 years 3x per week
2020 Dumped ex- who was the driver and supplier of all of the substances. Moved rurally to black sand titanomagnetic beach in west Auckland

Started CT, began to change my life, gave up weed, and phenibut. Started sleeping properly and DREAMING for the first time in 15+ years!!

**Where I'm at now:**

I live in an open plan tiny house/studio with no internal walls and no artificial light. Switched from red lights to only a few candles in winter (dark at 6:30)
I work outside on online biz naked in the sun (though for new clients I do wear a shirt haha until they get to know me
I'm outside ALL day. I do Kundalini yoga from 5:30-6:30am then go barefoot walking in the hills (or rucking with 18kg bag) from 7:30-8:30
Work on my daylight (when the fucker will accept my ethenet connecter!!) outside. But do use a BT keyboard as its on a stand. Use phone hardwired for video calls.
Swim in the ocean or waterfall daily at lunch break
Sunset walk barefoot every evening rain or shine.
Barefoot HITT workouts in the sun 2-3x per week

EMF testing/mitigation last year - kill switch on circuits near bed, hardwired internet, removed smart meter, fridge on timer at night, aluminum shielding on deck where I work.
EMF testing 3 months ago dt new tower in area- working on new EMF aluminum screen for above our deck, and already bought the shielding paint - but have yet to get the electrician to put the grounding plate in for the painted wall.
Eat local seasonal raw meat/fish diet (body craves the rae meat ha) . I do poach my eggs still. I grow microgreens so most of my veggies are in the form of brassica etc micros homegrown in the sun.

**Current over enthusiasms - trying to let go of.:**

Walnut, pistachio and macadamia over indulgence- there is NO off switch if these are in the house!
NICOTINE!!! After stressful period with my parents here about 3 months ago, started vaping. Always outside, all day in the sun, but this sucker is pretty sticky!!!!
I fucking LOVE my sauna, been doing infrared sauna since 17, my parents had one, and when I got my first place I bought one. I have one now (Building biologist verified very low EMF) but man if I miss a few days I get quite antsy!!

Labs good, but doc always freaked out about my total cholesterol is over 9mmol
Fasting insulin 6
HBAic bit high at 34
I'm skinny but strong as fuck. 48kg 165cm fitter than I've ever been.
Never injured anymore after years of gym workouts herniated disks, issues with knees etc from 17-35

**Questions/Opinions**?

Our water is rain water, collected from roof, carbon block filtered, then refiltered in Berkey. Then "structured/vortexted in Mayu jug in natural light-
Due to high supplement use, (just sent away salivary samples for testing) wondering if its wort doing DDW to help with addictions?

As a lifelong sun worshipper and melanin-obsessive (was teased so much for being a white kid in the Bahamas so get a bit freaked out when I'm not super brown ha ha ), wondering about spending half the year June-Oct in either Pacific islands (shitty water, DE/Shitty light pollution, awesome sun and seafood) or NE AUstralia (shitty water, shitty magnetism, awesome winter sun). OR would it be more ideal to just stay put and do more CT as I'm pretty healthy/young etc, do some indoor tanning (shitty nnEMF!) but beds here have red light/UVA UVB bulbs?

Given everything else is pretty good/optimal, I mean, can I just relax and enjoy a few more months of vaping

I know JK thinks Ir sauna are underpowered/not worth it but I love the relaxation/sweat, is there a downside if I have time and enjoy it (confirmed low EMF, after we removed the stupid BT thing it had in the roof)? Because I'm pretty skinny in the winter after swims it takes me a while to warm up if I DONT have a short sauna after...

# Thread: Still Skeptical, Still Curious: My Road to Optimal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-skeptical-still-curious-my-road-to-optimal.30850/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark1969 — 2025-05-08T03:46:30+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/still-skeptical-still-curious-my-road-to-optimal.30850/post-352125>

Hey all,

I’m starting this as part of my **“Optimal Journal”**—something I’ve been hesitant to do, but I know it’s time. I need to stop holding things in (literally and metaphorically) and start telling my story, piece by piece. Not just to vent, but to track, reflect, and grow.

I didn’t want to clutter up the **"13 Years with Sleep Apnea"** thread—so I made this new post. That thread, plus Jack’s quote...

> **“Sleep apnea is the brain protecting itself from nnEMF.”**

...got my wheels turning. Then I read DM #37–#40, Hypoxia #16, the Leptin Rx, and it started to click.

* * *

### ‍♂️ My Background​

-   I’ve got **type 2 diabetes**
-   I’ve had a **total colectomy** (yes, sleeping is a logistical challenge)
-   I use a CPAP machine every night
-   And I’ve had a long, stressful relationship with **not feeling safe in my body** or my environment

Sleep is shallow. The CPAP mask feels like a punishment. I tried skipping it one night after hearing Jack’s quote… woke up gasping. So no, I’m not unplugging it yet. But I am working on **earning the ability to not need it anymore.**

* * *

### Why I’m Doing This​

I’m starting to see things differently now. Like maybe apnea isn’t a disease—it’s a signal. Maybe my cells can’t use oxygen because my mitochondria are stuck in a broken loop. Maybe my whole system is out of sync because I’ve lived under LED lights and stress, disconnected from the sun and Earth, for decades.

Jack says, _put yourself first_. And that’s what I’m trying to do.

> I'm slowly turning down the LED + EMF fryalator I’ve been baking in.
> I’m watching sunrises.
> I'm listening to Club calls and pow wows.
> I’m using my own personal GPT assistant to help me learn and break this stuff down.
> And I’m hoping to someday teach what I learn through videos—if I can carve out the time and clarity to do it right.

* * *

## ✅ CPAP Step-Down Plan (Short Summary)​

This is the simple version of what I’m starting:

* * *

### **PHASE 1: Use the CPAP Safer While I Rebuild**​

-   **Extend the hose** (12 ft total using two 6-ft hoses)
-   **Plug it into another room**, run an extension cord
-   **Turn off bedroom circuit breaker** at night
-   **Add a metal shield** (like foil + cardboard) between me and the machine
-   **Remove heated hose and humidifier** (EMF goes straight to the airway)

* * *

### **PHASE 2: Repair My Circadian System**​

-   **AM sunlight to eyes and skin daily**, before food or screens
-   **Leptin Rx breakfast**: 50g+ protein, low carb, within 30 min of waking
-   No snacking. No food after sunset. No workouts in the morning.
-   **Red light only at night**, screens off, WiFi off, phone out of the room
-   **Cold exposure** (showers or walks) + **grounding** daily
-   Working to **retrain mitochondria** to use oxygen again

* * *

### **PHASE 3: Slowly Wean Off CPAP (When Ready)**​

-   Start with **every other night**
-   Then try **half-nights only**
-   Use **pulse oximeter or sleep tracker** to monitor oxygen, REM, and HRV
-   Only go fully off when data and symptoms show I’m ready

* * *

### Final Thoughts​

I’m not trying to “biohack” my way out of this. I’m trying to **listen to my body**, repair my environment, and finally understand what the hell has been going on inside me.

I don’t expect perfection. I just want progress.

Thanks for reading this—and thanks to folks like **tooth Driller**, **Anthony Smith**, and everyone here who’s laid the groundwork and shared openly. I’ve felt less alone because of it.

Would love any feedback from those who’ve been through something similar.

— _Still skeptical. Still curious. Still here._
(_And finally putting myself first._)


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
# Thread: MS, Big Pharma, and the Moment I Woke the F*** Up
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ms-big-pharma-and-the-moment-i-woke-the-f-up.30852/>
**Subforum:** Beginners Area
**Posts:** 1
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### Mark1969 — 2025-05-08T04:19:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/ms-big-pharma-and-the-moment-i-woke-the-f-up.30852/post-352131>

My daughter—31—started having weird eye issues **last summer**. Blurriness, trouble focusing—nothing made sense. She saw an eye doc, got referred around, and by **October**, they gave her the diagnosis: **Multiple Sclerosis**.

That alone was a heavy one.

She’s been going through the system ever since. Then in **March**, her neurologist mentioned she’d be **leaving the current hospital**, but told my daughter she could follow her to her new practice if she wanted.

Around that time, they also started her on a drug—**Zeposia**, I think—about **8 weeks ago**.

So earlier this **May**, my daughter logs into the **new hospital’s portal** to message the doc about a new symptom…

And **boom**, first thing that pops up:

> A disclaimer saying the doctor is a **paid representative for the same drug company** that makes the med she just prescribed.

Dauger told me right after she logged in to the new hospital—and that was it.

> This is _exactly_ what Jack Kruse has been warning about for years.

I didn’t get the full picture until I got behind the **Klub paywall**. Before that, I was just getting the media-safe, ad-friendly YouTube version that keeps it palatable and politically correct.

This isn’t about healing. It’s about **pushing product**. The doctor pipeline is wired into pharma. And they don't even hide it—they put it right in the portal now.

> You f\*\*\* with my kid’s health to move product—**Papa Bear wakes up.**

I’m learning now. And yeah, I’m still skeptical—but I’m **wide awake**.

Not staying quiet anymore.

— _One sunrise at a time. For her and for me._


══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

# Thread: Presbyopia healing eyes and light
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/presbyopia-healing-eyes-and-light.30854/>
**Subforum:** Biohacking 101
**Posts:** 14
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### mangoqueen — 2025-05-08T23:44:03+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/presbyopia-healing-eyes-and-light.30854/post-352163>

Im asking to find definitive info about how IOL lenses that block UV below 380nm will potentially affect my health, please.

Ive booked an elective RLE op for mid june, and just recently found you ( thanks to a podcast).
Im 65, I live at 17th latitude S for 30 of my years.
So Im now second guessing my decision to go ahead.

Im relatively healthy, never taken meds and now never missing a sunrise. Clean diet, clean air and water, and I spend 2 hours walking and swimming in ocean daily at sunrise.

### Brent Patrick — 2025-05-09T00:30:17+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/presbyopia-healing-eyes-and-light.30854/post-352166>

Welcome.

If you go ahead with procedure you will have to try and make up the deficit by having majority of skin exposed to full spectrum SUNLight for longer periods of time.

A post from Jack on FB 6TH DEC 2016 and link to study you may find interesting.

**One very exciting possibility mentioned in this paper is that they believe there might be a future production of an intraocular lens where solar UV light is converted to IR-A to repair mitochondria. Guess what my December 2016 webinar was about? How that "lens" like photochemical was built into our mitochondria by nature to increase our yield of this regenerative light for mitochondria. Glaucoma is another disease of the eye tied to myopia and a lack of sunlight in the human environment. Sunlight has UV and IR light buried within its rays and both can help heal a failing eye. It's been shown in recent large meta-analysis that Increased UVB environmental exposure is associated with reduced myopia, particularly in adolescence and young adulthood. If myopia is diminshed we can expect glaucoma incidence to drop as well. Why? The link between myopia and glaucoma has been explored for nearly a century by eye docs. A number of studies, including the Blue Mountains Eye Study and the Beijing Eye Study, have found that the risk of glaucoma appears to increase in persons with high myopia (6 diopters or more). Recently, Kuldev Singh, MD, MPH, at Stanford University, and Shan C. Lin, MD, at the University of California, San Francisco, reported a similar association in a U.S. population. This myopia trend globally is due to our blue lit microwaved world and the eye docs are slowly catching on. The Japanese eye docs are the furthest along in understanding this linkage to our poorly constructed modern world of light engineering. Dr. Law has been quoted as saying that patients with mild myopia appear to follow a course similar to those without myopia. This should concern us all. It means our eye clock is being destroyed by our neighbors choices.** [http://www.sciencedirect.com/.../pii/S1567724916302586](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1567724916302586) **JK**

"The positive effects of red light includes promoting wound healing, cause complement propagation, and reducing oxidative stress, inflammation and cell death (Del Olmo-Aguado et al., 2012, Liang et al., 2006, Wong-Riley et al., 2005, Ying et al., 2008)) as well as improving recovery rates of soft tissue injuries and myocardial infarction (Oron et al., 2001, Simunovic et al., 2000). **In the case of the eye, red light stimulates COX and ATP synthesis in the retina (Begum et al., 2013, Gkotsi et al., 2014) and protects against photoreceptor death in situ"**

### mangoqueen — 2025-05-09T11:12:57+0300

> [Jacks said:](/goto/post?id=352239)
>
> I always thought the IOL were just for cataracts! I have presbyopia, but I guess it is fairly mild as I just wear basic drug store glasses with some magnification for reading in dim light or for detail work only, and I have blue blocking ones with magnification for nightime reading/computer use from Lucia Eyes.

Lol. Me too originally, but my sight with drugstore strong reader glasses impacts my life daily. Im a beekeeper and , well, it gets very messy constantly pushing glasses up my honey smeared nose. And try fixing something when your head is upside down and you need 2 hands free. Haha! Plus the gremlins are always hiding them from.me....

# Thread: I told my Optometrist Uncle Jack's thoughts on keratoconus & why I wanted no UV block in lens, this is her reply...
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-told-my-optometrist-uncle-jacks-thoughts-on-keratoconus-why-i-wanted-no-uv-block-in-lens-this-is-her-reply.30856/>
**Subforum:** The Cave
**Posts:** 21
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### billytheadult — 2025-05-09T00:58:10+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-told-my-optometrist-uncle-jacks-thoughts-on-keratoconus-why-i-wanted-no-uv-block-in-lens-this-is-her-reply.30856/post-352167>

So I transcribed Uncle Jack from a podcast and sent her this:
**ME:** For context on my request for non UV blocking lens... around the same time I was diagnosed with keratoconus I was also diagnosed with vitiligo. Both of which my doctors had no answers for. I've recently come across a retired neurosurgeon who suggested light environment could be a factor and when I retraced my steps since I moved to NYC, and realized I went 20 years without daily sunlight on my retinas and was tech abused and getting artificial blue light every night -- needless to say I took notice. I’ve been reading about Dr. Jack Kruse’s thoughts on keratoconus and wanted to share for your perspective.

From notes I took from a podcast:
_The cornea can develop Fleischer rings, which are composed of hemosiderin — a breakdown product of hemoglobin that is paramagnetic.
This may alter the electrical resistance of the anterior portion of the eye.
The implication is that disrupted hemoglobin metabolism and iron deposition may play a role in the pathophysiology of keratoconus.
This line of thinking also connects to Dr. Gilbert Ling and Dr. Becker’s work on cellular bioelectricity and magnetic properties in tissues._
I’d love to hear your thoughts on whether this has any clinical relevance or if you’ve come across similar theories in your practice.

**Dr. H:** (after reading it front of me) Well that IS a little out there...

(but she was nice and understood my request, and got back to me with this:

**Dr. H:**
It was very nice to meet you today. Just wanted to follow up on the UV filter question. The lab does not use materials that don't filter UV. All of their highly oxygen permeable materials incorporate a filter that cuts out shorter than 400 nm wavelengths (the rays that damage the macula). They do still allow blue light (generally 400-500nm) transmission which is important for the circadian rhythm. The UV filter actually blocks less UV than the natural lens of the eye, so if you had no UV filter in the contact, your natural lens would absorb even longer wavelengths into the blue range than the UV filter in the contact does (which is why people develop cataracts as they age). So I think we will both get what we want: I am happy to protect your maculas and you will still benefit from blue light while wearing the contact lens.

Looking forward to better vision!
\---
Thoughts?!

### Mat.I — 2025-05-09T04:46:40+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-told-my-optometrist-uncle-jacks-thoughts-on-keratoconus-why-i-wanted-no-uv-block-in-lens-this-is-her-reply.30856/post-352182>

Why blocking UV to the anterior eye is also a problem - impair redox signalling, disrupt EZ water structuring, reduces natural collagen crosslinking, impairs neuropsin based circadian entrainment, & blocks endothelial ionic pump tuning etc..

Also, aging and lens stiffening would be a result of poor redox and poor light environment.

Any lens that blocks UV would = photonic malnutrition, which will make this condition worst. Your eye is a quantum sensor.

### Pete Collins — 2025-05-27T08:06:02+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-told-my-optometrist-uncle-jacks-thoughts-on-keratoconus-why-i-wanted-no-uv-block-in-lens-this-is-her-reply.30856/post-353405>

I am +22 contact lens prescription & +17 spectacles (very thick heavy lenses)
Below are the specs of the best contacts I can find for my personal wear (I never wear at sunrise + UVA rise)
I pushed my 'waking up' ophthalmologist hard on this one.
For your reference, here are the properties of Boston XO® lenses:
Material Properties of Boston XO® (hexafocon A) Contact Lenses: RGP (hard)

-   Specific Gravity: 1.27
-   Refractive Index: 1.415
-   Light Absorbance:
    -   640 nm (Blue): 7.8
    -   640 nm (Ice Blue): 5.4
    -   585 nm (Violet): 5.4
    -   640 nm (Green): 4.9
        (Absorbance units per inch)
-   Light Transmittance: 92% (Average %T from 400-800 nm)
-   Surface Character: Hydrophobic
-   Wetting Angle: 49°
-   Wetting Angle with Hydra-PEG: 10°
-   Water Content: Less than 1%
-   Oxygen Permeability: 140\* (100\*\*)
    Measured by gas to gas method
    Measured using the polarographic method (ISO/Fatt)

### Saichi — 2025-05-27T14:14:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-told-my-optometrist-uncle-jacks-thoughts-on-keratoconus-why-i-wanted-no-uv-block-in-lens-this-is-her-reply.30856/post-353411>

### Jack Kruse — 2025-08-05T17:23:41+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/i-told-my-optometrist-uncle-jacks-thoughts-on-keratoconus-why-i-wanted-no-uv-block-in-lens-this-is-her-reply.30856/post-357598>

What Decentralized Medicine Insights on CATARACT formation are included in this new masterpiece blog for Savage consumption?

[https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-65-124070324](https://www.patreon.com/posts/decentralized-65-124070324)

Changes in light input via the lens predict epigenetic programming. Blocked sunlight disrupts histone modifications and DNA methylation, contributing to modern diseases MAHA could never solve because of their myopic focus on food.

The creeping blindness of cataract formation reflects a broader metaphor in modern life: modern comforts (e.g., technology abuse, centralized medicine) suppress the discomfort of poor thinking; poor thinking and poor outcomes should lead to fostering wisdom and identity. Avoiding this decentralized struggle (e.g., via opioids over sunlight) poisons resilience and creativity.

Melanin, Melatonin, and Vitamin D Deficiency: Hypothyroidism reduces Coulomb charge in the skin and lens due to low melanin, melatonin, an an altered VDR, acting in unison to alter how Gauss law can be used and this impairs charge collection in cells leading to disease. This process opacifies the lens, causing cataracts.

Stars require darkness to shine, just as human biology needs light-dark cycles for optimal function. An opaque lens diminishes this balance, reducing the relative power of light to drive epigenetic programming and cellular evolution. Modern artificial lighting (e.g., blue light overuse) exacerbates this, further distorting the light-dark tension essential for health.

Leptin-Melanocortin Pathway: Leptin stimulates TRH via the leptin-melanocortin pathway, boosting α-MSH and POMC, which are essential for melanin production. In hypothyroidism, this pathway is disrupted, leading to pale skin, lens opacification, and increased sunburn risk. A cataract gives you a ciliary ganglionectomy just like sunglasses do and this leads to many diseases in modern humans.

Thermoregulation and TRH: TRH acts as a neurotransmitter, inducing hyperthermia when injected intracerebroventricularly. This heat stress, combined with a diminished heat sink (e.g., in the retina and lens), heightens cataract risk because it alters the production of high signal UPEs in the eyes and central retinal pathways leading to more diseases you never saw coming.

# Thread: sick looking kids

### Wanttoknow — 2025-05-09T06:03:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/sick-looking-kids.30857/post-352184>

I was sitting in the park today eating lunch and watched a group of high school kids walk by. They were walking slow, heads hung down, no life in them no joy. I thought these kids are just like growing a tomato plant from seed. One seed under the sun and one seed inside the house under florescent light. The kids are like the tomato plant grown inside under artificial light straggly, thin, barely growing. Its obvious people are not getting the sunlight needed for health. Very sad and no way to stop this trend from getting worse. I am very thankful I understand how important the sun is for health

### Steve Z — 2025-05-13T20:41:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/what%E2%80%99s-better.30865/post-352445>

There are 2 main issues with indoor light, as I understand it.

Artificial lighting sources have the wrong spectrum (some worse, some better) and it is nowhere near the light intensity (LUX) of sunlight even when filtered through the clouds. Those are the big issues.

Beyond that are issues like polarized (man's light) vs unpolarized, lights that flicker with the power grid vs Fraunhofer lines of sunlight, etc.

These are why Dr. Kruse says TINA - there is no alternative to sunlight.

# Thread: Nick's Journey - On the road to optimal
**Thread URL:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-journey-on-the-road-to-optimal.30873/>
**Subforum:** My Optimal Journal
**Posts:** 7
══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════
### NickD — 2025-05-10T16:42:09+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-journey-on-the-road-to-optimal.30873/post-352265>

Hey all,

I've been lurking around on the forum for a while now, and have been hesitant to post a journal of any sort. Mainly because if I do something, I want to not give it a half-arsed go. So what I thought was to make a start, then keep adding as I log into the Forum. So with that little preface, here we go...

My name is Nick, I'm 44 years old. I live in NSW, Australia, with my wife and 2 girls, 6 & 2. I was born in India (Bombay - Mumbai now). My wife has a Scottish and English heritage (white as a ghost LOL).

I work for an electrical distribution company and have been in that field for my career. I started, as most people do, with an apprenticeship. We couldn't afford the engineering course at university. I started building switchboards for power stations. We had the whole process, from sheet metal to a fully working product. I then switched to domestic and commercial electrical work. Then into the power distribution field (climbing poles etc). I'm currently in the control room... This is where I guess we'd start...

So I work a 24/7 roster in an electrical distribution control room. We handle all faults and emergencies, and all high-voltage maintenance and construction work. Basically, from your 240V domestic to 132kV transmission systems.

I used to exercise all the time, and working shifts, I was just starting to go backwards, no matter what I did.

I ended up meeting a guy named David Herrera and booked a consultation. Through that and further works, I was introduced to Uncle Jack. David tweaked my lifestyle and also pointed me to Jack and his work. From there, I've been "drinking from a fire hose", so to speak.

The current protocol would be as follows:

No matter what shift I'm on, I see the sunrise. This works for afternoon and night shifts. For day shifts, I'm at work at 05:30 and totally miss the sunrise. I have an EMR-TEK redlight panel, which I use to supplement where needed. When I bought the panel, I bought 2. One for home called the Ultron, and another for night shifts at work called the Fire Dragon. They gave me a code, CDNICK81, which, if you venture down the red light panel path, you can use for a discount. A few on this forum have used it, which is nice to hear that the discount worked.

I also have a cold shower in the morning and night - bookending each day. I aim to get a cold plunge unit, but there's a lot of landscaping work that needs doing at home.

I eat an all-protein and fat breakfast, then a little meal in the afternoon, then dinner at night.

As I've been learning, I've been able to get my wife to stop with the sunscreen and not burn. She can't believe it!

I've switched out our lights at home to the Block Bluelight yellow for the living areas and red for the bedrooms. Having red in the bedroom has been great on the romance/sexual front - highly recommend that

I've even started an Instagram account called "circadian\_defence" where I've been posting bits and pieces I've been learning. My friends have been curious, so spreading the word has been well received. I also have my personal account, nick\_dickinson81, where it's mainly family pics and books I've read. I love reading, but finding time is hard, so I hammer Audible.

So that's about all for now. Thank you, Jack, for changing my life and giving me something to dive into! It's been a hell of a ride with a lot more to go!

### NickD — 2025-05-11T15:44:26+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-journey-on-the-road-to-optimal.30873/post-352314>

I finished reading a book called Red Light Therapy by Mark Sloan. Since I use a panel, I thought it best to gain some info. It's an easy read that just lays out its benefits. As Jack says TINA, my schedule kind of demands I have to supplement it. In any case, it just proved what everyone here already knows
Here's the link if anyone wants to pick it up.
