### NickD — 2025-05-11T15:50:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-journey-on-the-road-to-optimal.30873/post-352315>

Oh, I'm almost finished The Body Electric by Robert O. Becker & Gary Seldon. The guy is brilliant. You can see his curiosity evolve as the story unfolds. His thoughts about what gets incentivised gets published and how that strays away from real science - letting the research guide you, not the funding.

At the start of chapter 13, there was this little gem that resonated with me:
"_The surgeon can cut, remove, or rearrange the tissues, and sew up the wound, but only the patient can do the healing. Surgeons must always be humble before this miracle. We must treat the tissues with sure, deft gentleness, and above all we must do no harm, for we are nothing more than nature's assistants_".

How cool is that!

Here's the link if anyone wants to pick it up.

[https://www.amazon.com.au/Body-Electric-Robert-Becker/dp/0688069711/ref=sr\_1\_1?crid=1A15MB7XBRIQT&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.QGSr85cIxV-WnVN6wnOpNdR6mWG9uqX8kwKS2fpGm9ckoDHsV9ns5CeGaG6776bOeVIEVjlKGfFnkuNHFqBDHPnAPLc0v3P7FayDJa01ikEsi3bb6xRjQsae6QuVPwLQFOQSOvxkN0QWxD-wAi1kSUx\_3mPwEyDWqRb5Ng3RsXbHkGPl14AsX\_cLZ-AjRou1QcOLX4NnjxR2A20EIXoqXv52eskJieVr8Zf\_UJy5R88.D\_BpXk4J7iTMyB\_JlnhAlYCoCbxpMbW8Ib2NnbDXklk&dib\_tag=se&keywords=the+body+electric&qid=1746967879&s=books&sprefix=The+body+electri%2Cstripbooks%2C1106&sr=1-1](https://www.amazon.com.au/Body-Electric-Robert-Becker/dp/0688069711/ref=sr_1_1?crid=1A15MB7XBRIQT&dib=eyJ2IjoiMSJ9.QGSr85cIxV-WnVN6wnOpNdR6mWG9uqX8kwKS2fpGm9ckoDHsV9ns5CeGaG6776bOeVIEVjlKGfFnkuNHFqBDHPnAPLc0v3P7FayDJa01ikEsi3bb6xRjQsae6QuVPwLQFOQSOvxkN0QWxD-wAi1kSUx_3mPwEyDWqRb5Ng3RsXbHkGPl14AsX_cLZ-AjRou1QcOLX4NnjxR2A20EIXoqXv52eskJieVr8Zf_UJy5R88.D_BpXk4J7iTMyB_JlnhAlYCoCbxpMbW8Ib2NnbDXklk&dib_tag=se&keywords=the+body+electric&qid=1746967879&s=books&sprefix=The+body+electri%2Cstripbooks%2C1106&sr=1-1)

### NickD — 2025-05-24T19:42:58+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-journey-on-the-road-to-optimal.30873/post-353267>

Continuing with Body Electric, I'm up to the part where he discusses our planet's magnetic field and the effects of the solar and lunar cycles.

\- 28K patients across 8 different hospitals had psychiatric issues directly correlated to magnetic disturbances.
\- Oysters, totally isolated from natural light, still opened and closed with the tide (lunar cycle). Even when moved to another location, they synced up with the lunar cycle.
\- Animals, totally isolated from light, moved from their original place yet managed to sync in with the earth's rhythm.

At work, we definitely notice an increase in incidents when there's a full moon. So much so that we joke that it'll be a busy night when the full moon is out. Crazy how an anecdote at work actually rings true!

The more I read/research, the more I'm amazed!

### Anthony Smith — 2025-05-24T21:34:37+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-journey-on-the-road-to-optimal.30873/post-353279>

I have always understood the best time to do a fast (3 days) is the dark of the moon. If you break the habit of always eating you may find you are not even hungry at this time.

### NickD — 2025-05-28T18:59:18+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-journey-on-the-road-to-optimal.30873/post-353472>

A few months ago, we also had our water filtered. There are three filters on the main line and another three for our drinking water (reverse osmosis). So far, we've changed one filter - they schedule replacements and automatically send them out when they're due. So far so good. The company is called Water Analytics if anyone is interested in Oz.

### NickD — 2025-06-07T17:52:34+0300
**Source:** <https://forum.jackkruse.com/threads/nicks-journey-on-the-road-to-optimal.30873/post-354201>

I wanted to share an update. Before joining this group, I was taking 5,000 IU of Vitamin D daily, and my levels were at 179 nmol/L. After consulting with David Herrera, I stopped the supplements and focused on spending more time outdoors. I recently had my levels rechecked, and they were 175 nmol/L—this was just after summer here in Australia. Pretty impressive without any Vitamin D supplements! Tuning into natural sunlight really does work.
Another step on the road to optimal
